proof_authority: Lean source checked by the pinned Lean kernel

declarations.name: residueOffset
declarations.docstring: The least positive shift sending `N` to a multiple of `d`.

declarations.name: residueOffset_eq_sub_of_lt
declarations.signature: theorem residueOffset_eq_sub_of_lt {d N : ℕ} (hN : N < d) : residueOffset d N = d - N
declarations.docstring: Above the vertex index, the residue offset is the literal gap to `d`.

declarations.name: foreignResidueKernel
declarations.signature: noncomputable def foreignResidueKernel (d N : ℕ) : ℝ
declarations.docstring: The exact Möbius residue channel whose sum is the local totient tail.

declarations.name: positiveForeignResidueKernel
declarations.signature: noncomputable def positiveForeignResidueKernel (d : ℕ+) (N : ℕ) : ℝ
declarations.docstring: The analytic residue state is indexed by positive channels. Keeping this wrapper separate from the finite natural-indexed kernel excludes the totalized `d = 0` denominator from every future `tsum` theorem.

declarations.name: positiveResidueIncrement
declarations.signature: noncomputable def positiveResidueIncrement (d : ℕ+) (H : ℕ) : ℝ
declarations.docstring: The diagonal increment on the positive analytic channel space.

declarations.name: stableResidueFactor
declarations.docstring: The common stable factor carried by every channel above the vertex.

declarations.name: foreignResidueKernel_of_lt
declarations.signature: theorem foreignResidueKernel_of_lt {d N : ℕ} (hd : 0 < d) (hN : N < d) : foreignResidueKernel d N = ((ArithmeticFunction.moebius d : ℤ) : ℝ) * (2 : ℝ) ^ N * stableResidueFactor d
declarations.docstring: Once `N < d`, the residue kernel has no remaining dependence on `N mod d`: it is `mu(d) * 2^N` times a fixed Mersenne factor.

declarations.name: residueIncrement
declarations.signature: noncomputable def residueIncrement (d H : ℕ) : ℝ
declarations.docstring: Contribution of channel `d` to the diagonal `R_(2H) - R_H`.

declarations.name: positiveResidueIncrement_coe
declarations.signature: theorem positiveResidueIncrement_coe (d : ℕ+) (H : ℕ) : positiveResidueIncrement d H = residueIncrement (d : ℕ) H
declarations.docstring: Positive-channel increments agree definitionally with the finite kernel on the coerced natural index.

declarations.name: residueIncrement_of_dvd
declarations.signature: theorem residueIncrement_of_dvd {d H : ℕ} (hd : 0 < d) (hdvd : d ∣ H) : residueIncrement d H = ((ArithmeticFunction.moebius d : ℤ) : ℝ) * (H : ℝ) / ((d : ℝ) * ((2 : ℝ) ^ d - 1))
declarations.docstring: A divisor channel is exactly one summand of the finite explicit shadow. This is the finite-side bridge behind the decomposition into divisor and foreign channels; the global divisor-sum identity remains a separate Möbius-expansion obligation.

declarations.name: residueIncrement_of_twice_lt
declarations.signature: theorem residueIncrement_of_twice_lt {d H : ℕ} (hd : 0 < d) (hstable : 2 * H < d) : residueIncrement d H = ((ArithmeticFunction.moebius d : ℤ) : ℝ) * (diagonalCoefficient H : ℝ) * stableResidueFactor d
declarations.docstring: In the stable range `2H < d`, the diagonal channel is the explicit coefficient `C_H` times one fixed Mersenne factor.

declarations.name: stableResidueFactor_nonneg
declarations.docstring: The stable Mersenne factor is nonnegative.

declarations.name: stableResidueFactor_le_geometric
declarations.signature: theorem stableResidueFactor_le_geometric {d : ℕ} (hd : 0 < d) : stableResidueFactor d ≤ 2 / (2 : ℝ) ^ d + 4 / (4 : ℝ) ^ d
declarations.docstring: Pointwise geometric domination used by the closed complement budget.

declarations.name: abs_residueIncrement_le_geometric
declarations.signature: theorem abs_residueIncrement_le_geometric {d H : ℕ} (hstable : 2 * H < d) : |residueIncrement d H| ≤ (diagonalCoefficient H : ℝ) * (2 / (2 : ℝ) ^ d + 4 / (4 : ℝ) ^ d)
declarations.docstring: Every omitted stable channel is dominated by the geometric summand whose tail is `foreignComplementBound`.

declarations.name: foreignTailWindow
declarations.signature: noncomputable def foreignTailWindow (H D L : ℕ) : ℝ
declarations.docstring: A finite window of omitted channels above cutoff `D`. This is the finite precursor of the analytic complement `G_H - P_(H,D)`.

declarations.name: abs_foreignTailWindow_le_geometric_sum
declarations.signature: theorem abs_foreignTailWindow_le_geometric_sum {H D L : ℕ} (hcutoff : 2 * H ≤ D) : |foreignTailWindow H D L| ≤ (diagonalCoefficient H : ℝ) * ∑ d ∈ Finset.Icc (D + 1) L, (2 / (2 : ℝ) ^ d + 4 / (4 : ℝ) ^ d)
declarations.docstring: Finite tail windows obey the same geometric majorant as the intended infinite complement. Passing this estimate to the `tsum` limit is now a separate summability step.

declarations.name: geometric_window_le_closed_complement
declarations.signature: theorem geometric_window_le_closed_complement (D L : ℕ) : ∑ d ∈ Finset.Icc (D + 1) L, (2 / (2 : ℝ) ^ d + 4 / (4 : ℝ) ^ d) ≤ 2 / (2 : ℝ) ^ D + 4 / (3 * (4 : ℝ) ^ D)
declarations.docstring: The finite geometric majorant of every window past `D` is already inside the closed complement budget. This is the finite, validation-friendly part of the eventual `tsum` estimate.

declarations.name: foreignComplementBound
declarations.signature: noncomputable def foreignComplementBound (H D : ℕ) : ℝ
declarations.docstring: Closed geometric budget for every omitted channel when `2H ≤ D`.

declarations.name: abs_foreignTailWindow_le_foreignComplementBound
declarations.signature: theorem abs_foreignTailWindow_le_foreignComplementBound {H D L : ℕ} (hcutoff : 2 * H ≤ D) : |foreignTailWindow H D L| ≤ foreignComplementBound H D
declarations.docstring: The finite foreign tail is bounded by the closed complement budget. The only remaining analytic obligation is to identify the actual complement with the limit of these windows.

declarations.name: projectedForeignDefect
declarations.signature: noncomputable def projectedForeignDefect (H D : ℕ) : ℝ
declarations.docstring: The actual foreign channels up to cutoff `D`; divisor channels are the explicit shadow and are deliberately excluded.

declarations.name: projectedDivisorChannels
declarations.signature: noncomputable def projectedDivisorChannels (H D : ℕ) : ℝ
declarations.docstring: The complementary finite state consisting of precisely the divisor channels. Its explicit Möbius form is supplied below.

declarations.name: finiteResidueDiagonal
declarations.signature: noncomputable def finiteResidueDiagonal (H D : ℕ) : ℝ
declarations.docstring: The unprojected finite residue state before splitting at the divisor boundary.

declarations.name: finiteResidueDiagonal_eq_projectedForeign_add_divisor
declarations.signature: theorem finiteResidueDiagonal_eq_projectedForeign_add_divisor (H D : ℕ) : finiteResidueDiagonal H D = projectedForeignDefect H D + projectedDivisorChannels H D
declarations.docstring: The finite residue state splits exactly into the retained foreign projection and the complementary divisor state.

declarations.name: projectedDivisorChannels_eq_truncated_mobius
declarations.signature: theorem projectedDivisorChannels_eq_truncated_mobius (H D : ℕ) : projectedDivisorChannels H D = ∑ d ∈ Finset.Icc 1 D, if d ∣ H then ((ArithmeticFunction.moebius d : ℤ) : ℝ) * (H : ℝ) / ((d : ℝ) * ((2 : ℝ) ^ d - 1)) else 0
declarations.docstring: The finite divisor complement is the literal truncated Möbius shadow. Consequently, the only missing comparison with `scaleExplicitShadow` is the global divisor expansion supplied by the radical-shadow owner.

declarations.name: ControlledForeignProjection
declarations.docstring: The projection is controlled when its difference from the actual foreign defect lies inside the explicit geometric budget.

declarations.name: ProjectedFullTargetSeparation
declarations.docstring: Exact finite separation of the projected diagonal from every integer.

declarations.name: scaleFullTarget_miss_of_projected_separation
declarations.signature: theorem scaleFullTarget_miss_of_projected_separation {H D : ℕ} (hcontrol : ControlledForeignProjection H D) (hseparation : ProjectedFullTargetSeparation H D) : ¬ScaleFullTargetHit H
declarations.docstring: **Complement noncancellation consumer.** A controlled projection whose finite rational state is farther from every integer than the whole omitted complement can move it forces a genuine full-target miss.

declarations.name: scaleFullTarget_miss_of_abs_sub_le_of_forall_int
declarations.signature: theorem scaleFullTarget_miss_of_abs_sub_le_of_forall_int {H D : ℕ} (hcontrol : |scaleForeignDefect H - projectedForeignDefect H D| ≤ foreignComplementBound H D) (hseparation : ∀ z : ℤ, foreignComplementBound H D < |scaleExplicitShadow H + projectedForeignDefect H D - (z : ℝ)|) : ¬ScaleFullTargetHit H
declarations.docstring: Expanded hypothesis form for downstream certificate generators.

declarations.name: firstLambertTailCorrectionRat
declarations.docstring: Exact rational correction which spends the first-order Möbius-Lambert tail instead of bounding it absolutely.

declarations.name: renormalizedResidueProjection
declarations.signature: noncomputable def renormalizedResidueProjection (H D : ℕ) : ℝ
declarations.docstring: The corrected residue presentation of the projected diagonal.

declarations.name: RenormalizedResidueAgreement
declarations.docstring: Agreement of the corrected residue presentation with the canonical squared-Mersenne centre. The exact checker proves this finite identity for fixtures; the remaining general Lean proof is the global divisor/kernel bridge owned by this residue lane.

declarations.name: scaleFullTarget_miss_of_renormalized_residue_separation
declarations.signature: theorem scaleFullTarget_miss_of_renormalized_residue_separation {H D : ℕ} (hagrees : RenormalizedResidueAgreement H D) (hsep : ∀ z : ℤ, lambertSquareComplementBound H D < |renormalizedResidueProjection H D - (z : ℝ)|) : ¬ScaleFullTargetHit H
declarations.docstring: Once the finite residue/kernel bridge supplies agreement, the canonical sharp square-tail enclosure immediately turns corrected residue separation into an actual full-target miss.

declarations.name: scalarLocalization_complement_dvd
declarations.signature: theorem scalarLocalization_complement_dvd (x : ℚ) (c : ℤ) {H : ℕ} (hH : H ∣ x.den) (hscaled : ((c : ℚ) * x).den ∣ H) : x.den / H ∣ c.natAbs
declarations.docstring: If a scalar multiple of a reduced rational has denominator contained in `H`, then the complementary denominator `x.den / H` divides the scalar. This is the exact scalar-localisation tax: shrinking the displayed denominator to a surviving channel does not discard the complement; it moves that complement into the coefficient.

declarations.name: scalarLocalization_divisor_dvd_coefficient
declarations.signature: theorem scalarLocalization_divisor_dvd_coefficient (x : ℚ) (c : ℤ) {H R : ℕ} (hH : H ∣ x.den) (hscaled : ((c : ℚ) * x).den ∣ H) (hR : R ∣ x.den / H) : R ∣ c.natAbs
declarations.docstring: Any prescribed divisor of the complementary denominator must already divide the scalar coefficient. This is the form used to compare a desired 2-adic gain with the Archimedean size paid by the localiser.

declarations.name: scalarLocalization_integer_eq_mul_num
declarations.signature: theorem scalarLocalization_integer_eq_mul_num (x : ℚ) (c : ℤ) {H : ℕ} (hH : H ∣ x.den) (hscaled : ((c : ℚ) * x).den ∣ H) : ∃ t : ℤ, (H : ℚ) * (c : ℚ) * x = (t : ℚ) * x.num
declarations.docstring: After scalar localisation, the resulting channel-cleared rational is an integer multiple of the *original reduced numerator*. This is the equality form of height conservation: the omitted denominator has been transferred to the coefficient exactly, not erased.

declarations.name: positiveRat_numDivisor_mul_lt_two_mul_den
declarations.signature: theorem positiveRat_numDivisor_mul_lt_two_mul_den (x : ℚ) {N M : ℕ} (hx : 0 < x) (hM : 0 < M) (hNnum : N ∣ x.num.natAbs) (hlt : x < (2 : ℚ) / M) : N * M < 2 * x.den
declarations.docstring: Elementary real/2-adic height conservation in an integral form. If a positive rational `x` is smaller than `2 / M` and `N` divides its reduced numerator, then `N * M < 2 * x.den`. Taking `N = 2^r` gives the denominator lower bound needed for synchronized tails, without introducing a global prefix LCM.

declarations.name: positiveRat_twoPow_mul_lt_two_mul_den
declarations.signature: theorem positiveRat_twoPow_mul_lt_two_mul_den (x : ℚ) {r M : ℕ} (hx : 0 < x) (hM : 0 < M) (hpow : 2 ^ r ∣ x.num.natAbs) (hlt : x < (2 : ℚ) / M) : 2 ^ r * M < 2 * x.den
declarations.docstring: The 2-power specialization used by a synchronized rational tail.

declarations.name: positiveRat_mersenne_height
declarations.signature: theorem positiveRat_mersenne_height (x : ℚ) {r n : ℕ} (hx : 0 < x) (hn : 0 < n) (hpow : 2 ^ r ∣ x.num.natAbs) (hlt : x < (2 : ℚ) / (2 ^ n - 1 : ℕ)) : 2 ^ r * (2 ^ n - 1) < 2 * x.den
declarations.docstring: Mersenne-denominator form of the elementary adelic height bound.

declarations.name: linearDescender_eq_smul_eval
declarations.signature: theorem linearDescender_eq_smul_eval {V W : Type*} [AddCommGroup V] [Module ℚ V] [AddCommGroup W] [Module ℚ W] (ev : V →ₗ[ℚ] ℚ) (Λ : V →ₗ[ℚ] W) (hker : LinearMap.ker ev ≤ LinearMap.ker Λ) (he : ∃ e : V, ev e = 1) : ∃ w₀ : W, ∀ v : V, Λ v = ev v • w₀
declarations.docstring: A linear channel that kills every scalar relation is only the original evaluation followed by multiplication by one fixed vector. Thus a rational-linear cyclotomic or residue channel cannot descend from equality of scalar values while retaining independent denominator information. The explicit preimage of `1` is the minimal surjectivity hypothesis needed for the one-dimensional quotient argument.

declarations.name: AdjacentRobustFailureAt
declarations.signature: def AdjacentRobustFailureAt (h N L : ℕ) : Prop
declarations.docstring: Failure of the robust adjacent certificate arc.

declarations.name: adjacentSignedLift
declarations.signature: def adjacentSignedLift (h N L : ℕ) : ℤ
declarations.docstring: The unique short signed representative selected by a robust adjacent failure: the low residue itself, or the high residue translated by `-2^L`.

declarations.name: adjacentWindowLift
declarations.signature: def adjacentWindowLift (h N L : ℕ) : ℤ
declarations.docstring: The adjacent signed lift translated back to the original window coordinate.

declarations.name: adjacentCarryState
declarations.signature: def adjacentCarryState (h N L : ℕ) : ℤ
declarations.docstring: The carry coordinate conjugate to the adjacent signed state.

declarations.name: eq_of_modEq_of_strict_sub_bounds
declarations.signature: private lemma eq_of_modEq_of_strict_sub_bounds {P a b : ℤ} (hP : 0 < P) (hmod : a ≡ b [ZMOD P]) (hlo : -P < b - a) (hhi : b - a < P) : a = b

declarations.name: adjacentStepResidue_nonneg
declarations.signature: lemma adjacentStepResidue_nonneg (h N L : ℕ) : 0 ≤ adjacentStepResidue h N L

declarations.name: adjacentStepResidue_lt
declarations.signature: lemma adjacentStepResidue_lt (h N L : ℕ) : adjacentStepResidue h N L < (2 ^ L : ℤ)

declarations.name: adjacentFailure_partition
declarations.signature: lemma adjacentFailure_partition {h N L : ℕ} (hfail : AdjacentRobustFailureAt h N L) : adjacentStepResidue h N L < 2 * (N + h + L + 2 : ℤ) ∨ (2 ^ L : ℤ) - 2 * (N + h + L + 2 : ℤ) < adjacentStepResidue h N L
declarations.docstring: A robust failure lies on exactly one side of the closed robust arc.

declarations.name: adjacentSignedLift_mod
declarations.signature: lemma adjacentSignedLift_mod (h N L : ℕ) : adjacentSignedLift h N L ≡ adjacentStepResidue h N L [ZMOD (2 ^ L : ℤ)]
declarations.docstring: The signed lift represents the adjacent residue modulo `2^L`.

declarations.name: adjacentSignedLift_abs_lt
declarations.signature: lemma adjacentSignedLift_abs_lt {h N L : ℕ} (hfail : AdjacentRobustFailureAt h N L) : |adjacentSignedLift h N L| < 2 * (N + h + L + 2 : ℤ)
declarations.docstring: Every robust failure has a canonical representative in the strict `2W` signed interval.

declarations.name: adjacentSignedLift_exists_unique
declarations.signature: lemma adjacentSignedLift_exists_unique {h N L : ℕ} (hfail : AdjacentRobustFailureAt h N L) (hroom : 4 * (N + h + L + 2 : ℤ) < (2 ^ L : ℤ)) : ∀ t : ℤ, t ≡ adjacentStepResidue h N L [ZMOD (2 ^ L : ℤ)] → |t| < 2 * (N + h + L + 2 : ℤ) → t = adjacentSignedLift h N L
declarations.docstring: Under `4W < 2^L`, the short representative is unique.

declarations.name: adjacentStepResidue_succ_mod
declarations.signature: lemma adjacentStepResidue_succ_mod (h N L : ℕ) : adjacentStepResidue h (N + 1) L = (2 * adjacentStepResidue h N L - deltaTotient h (N + L + 1) + deltaTotient h (N + L + 2)) % (2 ^ L : ℤ)
declarations.docstring: The spatial slide induces the exact adjacent-residue recurrence modulo `2^L`.

declarations.name: adjacentSignedLift_succ_of_two_failures
declarations.signature: lemma adjacentSignedLift_succ_of_two_failures {h N L : ℕ} (hfail : AdjacentRobustFailureAt h N L) (hfail' : AdjacentRobustFailureAt h (N + 1) L) (hroom : 8 * (N + h + L + 3 : ℤ) < (2 ^ L : ℤ)) : adjacentSignedLift h (N + 1) L = 2 * adjacentSignedLift h N L - deltaTotient h (N + L + 1) + deltaTotient h (N + L + 2)
declarations.docstring: Two consecutive robust failures cannot wrap when the next endpoint has `8W` dyadic room.

declarations.name: adjacentWindowLift_mod_windowResidue
declarations.signature: lemma adjacentWindowLift_mod_windowResidue (h N L : ℕ) : adjacentWindowLift h N L ≡ windowDiscrepancy h N L [ZMOD (2 ^ L : ℤ)]
declarations.docstring: The original-window lift has the same residue as `windowDiscrepancy`.

declarations.name: adjacentWindowLift_branch_bounds
declarations.signature: lemma adjacentWindowLift_branch_bounds {h N L : ℕ} (hfail : AdjacentRobustFailureAt h N L) : (-(N + h + L + 2 : ℤ) < adjacentWindowLift h N L ∧ adjacentWindowLift h N L < 3 * (N + h + L + 2 : ℤ)) ∨ (-3 * (N + h + L + 2 : ℤ) < adjacentWindowLift h N L ∧ adjacentWindowLift h N L < (N + h + L + 2 : ℤ))
declarations.docstring: The low and high signed branches give asymmetric `3W` bounds for the window lift.

declarations.name: adjacentWindowLift_abs_lt_three
declarations.signature: lemma adjacentWindowLift_abs_lt_three {h N L : ℕ} (hfail : AdjacentRobustFailureAt h N L) : |adjacentWindowLift h N L| < 3 * (N + h + L + 2 : ℤ)

declarations.name: certifiedKill_of_adjacentWindowLift_abs_gt
declarations.signature: theorem certifiedKill_of_adjacentWindowLift_abs_gt {h N L : ℕ} (hfail : AdjacentRobustFailureAt h N L) (hroom : 4 * (N + h + L + 2 : ℤ) < (2 ^ L : ℤ)) (hout : (N + h + L + 2 : ℤ) < |adjacentWindowLift h N L|) : certifiedKill h N L
declarations.docstring: If the centred original-window lift lies outside the endpoint strip, the same-`N` endpoint certificate already fires.

declarations.name: tail_diff_notMem_int_of_adjacentWindowLift_abs_eq
declarations.signature: theorem tail_diff_notMem_int_of_adjacentWindowLift_abs_eq {h N L : ℕ} (hroom : 2 * (N + h + L + 2 : ℤ) < (2 ^ L : ℤ)) (heq : |adjacentWindowLift h N L| = (N + h + L + 2 : ℤ)) : totientTail (N + h) - totientTail N ∉ Set.range ((↑) : ℤ → ℝ)
declarations.docstring: Equality with the endpoint radius is itself a non-integrality certificate, despite not satisfying the strict `certifiedKill` predicate.

declarations.name: adjacentCarryState_abs_le_of_windowLift_abs_lt
declarations.signature: lemma adjacentCarryState_abs_le_of_windowLift_abs_lt {h N L : ℕ} (hin : |adjacentWindowLift h N L| < (N + h + L + 2 : ℤ)) : |adjacentCarryState h N L| ≤ (N + L + h + 1 : ℤ)
declarations.docstring: The third branch is the strict initial candidate box of `survivorKill`.

declarations.name: adjacentFailure_kill_or_boundaryKill_or_strictCarry
declarations.signature: theorem adjacentFailure_kill_or_boundaryKill_or_strictCarry {h N L : ℕ} (hfail : AdjacentRobustFailureAt h N L) (hroom : 4 * (N + h + L + 2 : ℤ) < (2 ^ L : ℤ)) : certifiedKill h N L ∨ (totientTail (N + h) - totientTail N ∉ Set.range ((↑) : ℤ → ℝ)) ∨ |adjacentCarryState h N L| ≤ (N + L + h + 1 : ℤ)
declarations.docstring: Correct pointwise interpretation of robust-arc failure.

declarations.name: adjacentCarryState_succ
declarations.signature: lemma adjacentCarryState_succ {h N L : ℕ} (hfail : AdjacentRobustFailureAt h N L) (hfail' : AdjacentRobustFailureAt h (N + 1) L) (hroom : 8 * (N + h + L + 3 : ℤ) < (2 ^ L : ℤ)) : adjacentCarryState h (N + 1) L = 2 * adjacentCarryState h N L - deltaTotient h (N + L + 1)
declarations.docstring: In the strict robust-failure tube, the adjacent carry state obeys the existing carry recurrence.

declarations.name: adjacentCarryState_eq_carryOrbit
declarations.signature: theorem adjacentCarryState_eq_carryOrbit {h N L i : ℕ} (hfail : ∀ j, j ≤ i → AdjacentRobustFailureAt h (N + j) L) (hroom : ∀ j, j < i → 8 * (N + j + h + L + 3 : ℤ) < (2 ^ L : ℤ)) : adjacentCarryState h (N + i) L = carryOrbit h (N + L) (adjacentCarryState h N L) i
declarations.docstring: Exact affine conjugacy with `carryOrbit` over any robust-failure run.

declarations.name: exists_adjacent_exit_of_survivorKill
declarations.signature: theorem exists_adjacent_exit_of_survivorKill {h N L K : ℕ} (hroom : ∀ i, i ≤ K → 8 * (N + i + h + L + 3 : ℤ) < (2 ^ L : ℤ)) (hkill : survivorKill h (N + L) K) : ∃ i ∈ Finset.range (K + 1), certifiedKill h (N + i) L ∨ (totientTail (N + i + h) - totientTail (N + i) ∉ Set.range ((↑) : ℤ → ℝ))
declarations.docstring: The existing finite carry certificate forces an adjacent endpoint or boundary exit.

declarations.name: adjacentStepResidue
declarations.signature: def adjacentStepResidue (h N L : ℕ) : ℤ
declarations.docstring: Canonical residue of the displacement between adjacent fixed-depth window discrepancies.

declarations.name: AdjacentMiddleThirdAt
declarations.signature: def AdjacentMiddleThirdAt (h N L : ℕ) : Prop
declarations.docstring: The adjacent residue lies in the closed middle third of the dyadic circle.

declarations.name: AdjacentCertificateArcAt
declarations.signature: def AdjacentCertificateArcAt (h N L : ℕ) : Prop
declarations.docstring: A wider adjacent arc that already forces the original endpoint certificate.

declarations.name: DTWAdjacentMiddleThirdSeparation
declarations.docstring: Cofinal adjacent middle-third separation. This is a normal form for a possible certificate supply, not an unconditional theorem.

declarations.name: DTWAdjacentPhaseSeparation
declarations.docstring: Cofinal separation of an adjacent pair by the norm-square threshold consumed by the two-point first-harmonic argument.

declarations.name: adjacentStepResidue_eq_translated_windowResidue
declarations.signature: theorem adjacentStepResidue_eq_translated_windowResidue (h N L : ℕ) : adjacentStepResidue h N L = (windowDiscrepancy h N L + deltaTotient h (N + L + 1)) % (2 ^ L : ℤ)
declarations.docstring: Sliding by one base point translates the old residue by exactly the fresh endpoint letter.

declarations.name: windowResidue_eq_adjacentStepResidue_sub_endpoint
declarations.signature: theorem windowResidue_eq_adjacentStepResidue_sub_endpoint (h N L : ℕ) : windowDiscrepancy h N L % (2 ^ L : ℤ) = (adjacentStepResidue h N L - deltaTotient h (N + L + 1)) % (2 ^ L : ℤ)
declarations.docstring: Exact inverse translation: the adjacent datum together with the known endpoint letter reconstructs the original window residue.

declarations.name: certifiedKill_of_adjacentCertificateArc
declarations.signature: theorem certifiedKill_of_adjacentCertificateArc {h N L : ℕ} (harc : AdjacentCertificateArcAt h N L) : certifiedKill h N L
declarations.docstring: The robust adjacent arc forces the original certificate at the same base `N`.

declarations.name: adjacentMiddleThird_implies_certificateArc
declarations.signature: theorem adjacentMiddleThird_implies_certificateArc {h X N L : ℕ} (hNlt : N < 2 * X) (hroom : 16 * (2 * X + h + L + 2) ≤ 2 ^ L) (hmid : AdjacentMiddleThirdAt h N L) : AdjacentCertificateArcAt h N L
declarations.docstring: Under the standard dyadic room bound, the middle third is contained in the robust certificate arc.

declarations.name: certifiedKill_of_adjacentMiddleThird
declarations.signature: theorem certifiedKill_of_adjacentMiddleThird {h X N L : ℕ} (hNlt : N < 2 * X) (hroom : 16 * (2 * X + h + L + 2) ≤ 2 ^ L) (hmid : AdjacentMiddleThirdAt h N L) : certifiedKill h N L
declarations.docstring: The middle-third event certifies the original base `N`, not merely one member of the adjacent pair.

declarations.name: tail_diff_eq_windowDiscrepancy_div_add_shifted
declarations.signature: theorem tail_diff_eq_windowDiscrepancy_div_add_shifted (h N L : ℕ) : totientTail (N + h) - totientTail N = ((windowDiscrepancy h N L : ℤ) : ℝ) / 2 ^ L + (totientTail (N + L + h) - totientTail (N + L)) / 2 ^ L
declarations.docstring: The finite discrepancy is the exact depth-`L` prefix of the tail difference; the only omitted term is the translated tail difference itself.

declarations.name: adjacent_step_div_eq_tail_diff_add_error
declarations.signature: theorem adjacent_step_div_eq_tail_diff_add_error (h N L : ℕ) : (((windowDiscrepancy h (N + 1) L - windowDiscrepancy h N L : ℤ) : ℝ) / 2 ^ L) = (totientTail (N + h) - totientTail N) - (deltaTotient h (N + 1) : ℝ) + ((totientTail (N + L + h) - totientTail (N + L)) - (totientTail (N + L + 1 + h) - totientTail (N + L + 1))) / 2 ^ L
declarations.docstring: Exact adjacent-step approximation to the tail class. Modulo integers, the adjacent displacement is the current tail difference plus a translated tail error; hence adjacency exposes no independent arithmetic coordinate.

declarations.name: abs_adjacent_step_tail_error_lt
declarations.signature: theorem abs_adjacent_step_tail_error_lt (h N L : ℕ) : |((totientTail (N + L + h) - totientTail (N + L)) - (totientTail (N + L + 1 + h) - totientTail (N + L + 1))) / 2 ^ L| < (2 * (N : ℝ) + 2 * L + 2 * h + 5) / 2 ^ L
declarations.docstring: The exact adjacent-step error is exponentially small at fixed base: its numerator is bounded by the two adjacent tail strips.

declarations.name: re_add_le_nine_fifths_of_unit_sq_chord
declarations.signature: theorem re_add_le_nine_fifths_of_unit_sq_chord {z w : ℂ} (hz : ‖z‖ = 1) (hw : ‖w‖ = 1) (hsep : (19 / 25 : ℝ) ≤ ‖z - w‖ ^ 2) : (z + w).re ≤ 9 / 5
declarations.docstring: Sharp norm-only conversion from squared chord separation to the `9/10` average consumed by the first-harmonic certificate theorem.

declarations.name: exists_certifiedKill_of_phasePairSeparation
declarations.signature: theorem exists_certifiedKill_of_phasePairSeparation {h X L N M : ℕ} (hNM : N ≠ M) (hNlt : N < 2 * X) (hMlt : M < 2 * X) (hroom : 16 * (2 * X + h + L + 2) ≤ 2 ^ L) (hsep : (19 / 25 : ℝ) ≤ ‖windowFirstExp h N L - windowFirstExp h M L‖ ^ 2) : ∃ K ∈ ({N, M} : Finset ℕ), certifiedKill h K L
declarations.docstring: Separation of any two distinct phases in the standard dyadic block forces a certificate at one member of the pair.

declarations.name: irrational_totient_series_of_adjacentMiddleThirdSeparation
declarations.signature: theorem irrational_totient_series_of_adjacentMiddleThirdSeparation (hsep : DTWAdjacentMiddleThirdSeparation) : Irrational (∑' n : ℕ, (Nat.totient n : ℝ) / 2 ^ n)
declarations.docstring: A cofinal adjacent middle-third supply proves irrationality through the existing finite certificate consumer.

declarations.name: irrational_totient_series_of_adjacentPhaseSeparation
declarations.signature: theorem irrational_totient_series_of_adjacentPhaseSeparation (hsep : DTWAdjacentPhaseSeparation) : Irrational (∑' n : ℕ, (Nat.totient n : ℝ) / 2 ^ n)
declarations.docstring: A cofinal adjacent chord-separation supply also proves irrationality, via the two-element first-harmonic consumer.

declarations.name: CoeffZeroWindow
declarations.signature: def CoeffZeroWindow (f : ℕ → ℕ) (N h : ℕ) : Prop
declarations.docstring: `f` vanishes at the `h` positive offsets after `N`, namely `N + 1, ..., N + h`.

declarations.name: coeffZeroWindow_zero
declarations.signature: @[simp] theorem coeffZeroWindow_zero (f : ℕ → ℕ) (N : ℕ) : CoeffZeroWindow f N 0
declarations.docstring: An empty coefficient window is vacuous.

declarations.name: coeffZeroWindow_one_iff
declarations.signature: @[simp] theorem coeffZeroWindow_one_iff (f : ℕ → ℕ) (N : ℕ) : CoeffZeroWindow f N 1 ↔ f (N + 1) = 0
declarations.docstring: A length-one window consumes exactly the coefficient at `N + 1`.

declarations.name: BinaryTailWindowLowerBound
declarations.signature: def BinaryTailWindowLowerBound (f : ℕ → ℕ) (scale : ℝ) (start h : ℕ) : Prop
declarations.docstring: A domain-produced lower certificate for a binary coefficient tail.

declarations.name: BinaryCoeffTailEnvelope
declarations.signature: def BinaryCoeffTailEnvelope (f : ℕ → ℕ) (envelope : ℕ → ℝ) : Prop
declarations.docstring: A global real-valued upper envelope for a binary coefficient tail.

declarations.name: state_eq_pow_mul_of_coeffZeroWindow
declarations.signature: theorem state_eq_pow_mul_of_coeffZeroWindow (f : ℕ → ℕ) (offset v N h : ℕ) (u : ℕ → ℕ) (hrec : ∀ n : ℕ, u (n + 1) + v * f (offset + n + 1) = 2 * u n) (hzero : CoeffZeroWindow f (offset + N) h) : u (N + h) = 2 ^ h * u N
declarations.docstring: A natural state doubles exactly across a coefficient-zero window.

declarations.name: binaryTailWindowLowerBound_of_natState
declarations.signature: theorem binaryTailWindowLowerBound_of_natState (f : ℕ → ℕ) (offset v N h : ℕ) (u : ℕ → ℕ) (huN : 0 < u N) (hrec : ∀ n : ℕ, u (n + 1) + v * f (offset + n + 1) = 2 * u n) (htail : ∀ n : ℕ, (u n : ℝ) = (v : ℝ) * binaryCoeffTail f (offset + n)) (hzero : CoeffZeroWindow f (offset + N) h) : BinaryTailWindowLowerBound f (v : ℝ) (offset + N) h
declarations.docstring: A positive natural state realizing a scaled tail produces the reusable lower-tail certificate. Only positivity at the start of the window is used.

declarations.name: summable_succ_div_two_pow
declarations.signature: theorem summable_succ_div_two_pow : Summable (fun j : ℕ => ((j + 1 : ℕ) : ℝ) / (2 : ℝ) ^ (j + 1))
declarations.docstring: The weighted geometric majorant used by every subpower tail envelope is summable.

declarations.name: tsum_succ_div_two_pow
declarations.signature: theorem tsum_succ_div_two_pow : (∑' j : ℕ, ((j + 1 : ℕ) : ℝ) / (2 : ℝ) ^ (j + 1)) = 2
declarations.docstring: Exact value of the weighted geometric majorant.

declarations.name: binaryCoeffTail_le_two_mul_rpow
declarations.signature: theorem binaryCoeffTail_le_two_mul_rpow (f : ℕ → ℕ) (C δ : ℝ) (hδ0 : 0 ≤ δ) (hδ1 : δ ≤ 1) (hf : ∀ n : ℕ, (f n : ℝ) ≤ C * (n : ℝ) ^ δ) (M : ℕ) : binaryCoeffTail f M ≤ 2 * C * (((M + 1 : ℕ) : ℝ) ^ δ)
declarations.docstring: A coefficient bound `f(n) ≤ C n^δ`, with `0 ≤ δ ≤ 1`, gives the explicit binary-tail bound `2 C (M+1)^δ`.

declarations.name: binaryCoeffTailEnvelope_two_mul_rpow
declarations.signature: theorem binaryCoeffTailEnvelope_two_mul_rpow (f : ℕ → ℕ) (C δ : ℝ) (hδ0 : 0 ≤ δ) (hδ1 : δ ≤ 1) (hf : ∀ n : ℕ, (f n : ℝ) ≤ C * (n : ℝ) ^ δ) : BinaryCoeffTailEnvelope f (fun M : ℕ => 2 * C * (((M + 1 : ℕ) : ℝ) ^ δ))
declarations.docstring: Predicate-shaped constructor for the standard subpower envelope.

declarations.name: binaryTailWindowLowerBound_le_mul_envelope
declarations.signature: theorem binaryTailWindowLowerBound_le_mul_envelope {f : ℕ → ℕ} {scale : ℝ} {start h : ℕ} {envelope : ℕ → ℝ} (hlower : BinaryTailWindowLowerBound f scale start h) (hscale : 0 ≤ scale) (henvelope : BinaryCoeffTailEnvelope f envelope) : (2 : ℝ) ^ h ≤ scale * envelope (start + h)
declarations.docstring: Compose a lower-tail certificate with an independent upper envelope.

declarations.name: nat_add_one_le_two_pow
declarations.docstring: The elementary exponential bound used in the absorption step.

declarations.name: pow_sub_one_mul_le_of_pow_le_mul_add_invNat_rpow
declarations.signature: theorem pow_sub_one_mul_le_of_pow_le_mul_add_invNat_rpow (k Y h : ℕ) (hk : 2 ≤ k) (hY : 1 ≤ Y) (L : ℝ) (hL : 1 ≤ L) (hpow : (2 : ℝ) ^ h ≤ L * (((Y + h : ℕ) : ℝ) ^ ((k : ℝ)⁻¹))) : (2 : ℝ) ^ ((k - 1) * h) ≤ L ^ k * (Y : ℝ)
declarations.docstring: Raising the self-referential inequality to the `k`-th power and using `Y+h ≤ Y*2^h` consumes exactly one of the `k` binary factors.

declarations.name: length_le_logb_of_pow_le_mul_add_invNat_rpow
declarations.signature: theorem length_le_logb_of_pow_le_mul_add_invNat_rpow (k Y h : ℕ) (hk : 2 ≤ k) (hY : 1 ≤ Y) (L : ℝ) (hL : 1 ≤ L) (hpow : (2 : ℝ) ^ h ≤ L * (((Y + h : ℕ) : ℝ) ^ ((k : ℝ)⁻¹))) : (h : ℝ) ≤ (Real.logb 2 (Y : ℝ) + Real.logb 2 (L ^ k)) / ((k - 1 : ℕ) : ℝ)
declarations.docstring: Logarithmic form of the absorption theorem.

declarations.name: length_le_logb_of_binaryTailWindowLowerBound
declarations.signature: theorem length_le_logb_of_binaryTailWindowLowerBound (f : ℕ → ℕ) (scale C : ℝ) (start h k : ℕ) (hlower : BinaryTailWindowLowerBound f scale start h) (hscale : 0 ≤ scale) (henvelope : BinaryCoeffTailEnvelope f (fun M : ℕ => C * (((M + 1 : ℕ) : ℝ) ^ ((k : ℝ)⁻¹)))) (hk : 2 ≤ k) (hLC : 1 ≤ scale * C) : (h : ℝ) ≤ (Real.logb 2 (((start + 1 : ℕ) : ℝ)) + Real.logb 2 ((scale * C) ^ k)) / ((k - 1 : ℕ) : ℝ)
declarations.docstring: Fixed-`k` analytic elimination from the certificate-first boundary.

declarations.name: exists_nat_two_le_inv_sub_one_le
declarations.signature: theorem exists_nat_two_le_inv_sub_one_le {ε : ℝ} (hε : 0 < ε) : ∃ k : ℕ, 2 ≤ k ∧ (((k - 1 : ℕ) : ℝ))⁻¹ ≤ ε
declarations.docstring: Archimedean selector in exactly the exponent produced by absorption.

declarations.name: logb_nat_affine_le_add
declarations.signature: theorem logb_nat_affine_le_add (c N : ℕ) : Real.logb 2 (c + N + 1 : ℝ) ≤ Real.logb 2 (c + 1 : ℝ) + Real.logb 2 (N + 1 : ℝ)
declarations.docstring: Separate a fixed affine shift from the growing index inside `log₂`.

declarations.name: le_epsilon_mul_logb_add_constant_div_of_affine_log_bound
declarations.signature: theorem le_epsilon_mul_logb_add_constant_div_of_affine_log_bound {ε D x : ℝ} {c N k : ℕ} (hbound : x ≤ (Real.logb 2 (c + N + 1 : ℝ) + D) / ((k - 1 : ℕ) : ℝ)) (hinv : (((k - 1 : ℕ) : ℝ))⁻¹ ≤ ε) : x ≤ ε * Real.logb 2 (N + 1 : ℝ) + (Real.logb 2 (c + 1 : ℝ) + D) / ((k - 1 : ℕ) : ℝ)
declarations.docstring: Convert a parameterized `1/(k-1)` affine-log estimate to an `ε * log₂(N+1)` estimate while keeping the remaining term explicit.

declarations.name: logb_nat_succ_le_one_add
declarations.signature: theorem logb_nat_succ_le_one_add (N : ℕ) (hN : 1 ≤ N) : Real.logb 2 (N + 1 : ℝ) ≤ 1 + Real.logb 2 (N : ℝ)
declarations.docstring: For positive `N`, replace `log₂(N+1)` by `1 + log₂ N`.

declarations.name: positiveSupportBit
declarations.signature: noncomputable def positiveSupportBit (A : Set ℕ) (n : ℕ) : ℤ
declarations.docstring: The positive-index integer indicator of a support. Arithmetic functions must vanish at zero, which is also the correct normalization for the Lambert coefficient calculus.

declarations.name: positiveSupportBitAF
declarations.signature: noncomputable def positiveSupportBitAF (A : Set ℕ) : ArithmeticFunction ℤ
declarations.docstring: `positiveSupportBit` packaged as an integer-valued arithmetic function.

declarations.name: supportCoeffAF
declarations.signature: noncomputable def supportCoeffAF (A : Set ℕ) : ArithmeticFunction ℤ
declarations.docstring: The support divisor-count coefficient, cast to an integer-valued arithmetic function.

declarations.name: positiveSupportBit_zero
declarations.signature: @[simp] theorem positiveSupportBit_zero (A : Set ℕ) : positiveSupportBit A 0 = 0

declarations.name: positiveSupportBit_eq_one_iff
declarations.signature: theorem positiveSupportBit_eq_one_iff {A : Set ℕ} {n : ℕ} (hn : 0 < n) : positiveSupportBit A n = 1 ↔ n ∈ A

declarations.name: positiveSupportBitAF_apply
declarations.signature: @[simp] theorem positiveSupportBitAF_apply (A : Set ℕ) (n : ℕ) : positiveSupportBitAF A n = positiveSupportBit A n

declarations.name: supportCoeffAF_apply
declarations.signature: @[simp] theorem supportCoeffAF_apply (A : Set ℕ) (n : ℕ) : supportCoeffAF A n = (supportCoeff A n : ℤ)

declarations.name: positiveSupportBitAF_mul_zeta
declarations.signature: theorem positiveSupportBitAF_mul_zeta (A : Set ℕ) : positiveSupportBitAF A * (↑(ArithmeticFunction.zeta : ArithmeticFunction ℕ) : ArithmeticFunction ℤ) = supportCoeffAF A
declarations.docstring: The divisor-count coefficient is exactly `1_A * ζ`, with the support indicator normalized at zero.

declarations.name: moebius_mul_supportCoeffAF
declarations.signature: theorem moebius_mul_supportCoeffAF (A : Set ℕ) : ArithmeticFunction.moebius * supportCoeffAF A = positiveSupportBitAF A
declarations.docstring: Exact Möbius recovery: `μ * f_A = 1_A` as arithmetic functions.

declarations.name: mobius_supportCoeff_eq_one_iff
declarations.signature: theorem mobius_supportCoeff_eq_one_iff (A : Set ℕ) {n : ℕ} (hn : 0 < n) : (ArithmeticFunction.moebius * supportCoeffAF A) n = 1 ↔ n ∈ A
declarations.docstring: Pointwise positive-index form of Möbius recovery.

declarations.name: mobius_supportCoeff_boolean
declarations.signature: theorem mobius_supportCoeff_boolean (A : Set ℕ) (n : ℕ) : (ArithmeticFunction.moebius * supportCoeffAF A) n = 0 ∨ (ArithmeticFunction.moebius * supportCoeffAF A) n = 1
declarations.docstring: The Möbius transform of every support coefficient is Boolean.

declarations.name: mobius_supportCoeff_recovers_support
declarations.signature: theorem mobius_supportCoeff_recovers_support (A : Set ℕ) (hzero : 0 ∉ A) : {n : ℕ | (ArithmeticFunction.moebius * supportCoeffAF A) n = 1} = A
declarations.docstring: Under the honest zero normalization, the Boolean Möbius transform recovers the entire support set, not merely its positive part.

declarations.name: booleanMobiusSupport
declarations.signature: noncomputable def booleanMobiusSupport (f : ArithmeticFunction ℤ) : Set ℕ
declarations.docstring: The positive support selected by the Boolean Möbius transform of `f`.

declarations.name: zero_not_mem_booleanMobiusSupport
declarations.signature: @[simp] theorem zero_not_mem_booleanMobiusSupport (f : ArithmeticFunction ℤ) : 0 ∉ booleanMobiusSupport f

declarations.name: moebius_mul_eq_positiveSupportBitAF_of_boolean
declarations.signature: theorem moebius_mul_eq_positiveSupportBitAF_of_boolean (f : ArithmeticFunction ℤ) (hbool : ∀ n : ℕ, 0 < n → (ArithmeticFunction.moebius * f) n = 0 ∨ (ArithmeticFunction.moebius * f) n = 1) : ArithmeticFunction.moebius * f = positiveSupportBitAF (booleanMobiusSupport f)
declarations.docstring: A Boolean Möbius transform is exactly the normalized indicator of the support that it selects.

declarations.name: eq_supportCoeffAF_booleanMobiusSupport_of_boolean
declarations.signature: theorem eq_supportCoeffAF_booleanMobiusSupport_of_boolean (f : ArithmeticFunction ℤ) (hbool : ∀ n : ℕ, 0 < n → (ArithmeticFunction.moebius * f) n = 0 ∨ (ArithmeticFunction.moebius * f) n = 1) : f = supportCoeffAF (booleanMobiusSupport f)
declarations.docstring: **Boolean Möbius inversion, converse direction.** Every integer-valued arithmetic function with Boolean Möbius transform is the support coefficient of the positive set selected by that transform. No search or finiteness hypothesis appears.

declarations.name: card_divisors_le_two_mul_sqrt
declarations.signature: theorem card_divisors_le_two_mul_sqrt (n : ℕ) : n.divisors.card ≤ 2 * Nat.sqrt n
declarations.docstring: Elementary divisor-pair estimate `τ(n) ≤ 2⌊√n⌋`. Mathlib currently supplies only the coarser `τ(n) ≤ n`; the partition below pairs every divisor above the square root with its complementary divisor below it.

declarations.name: supportCoeff_le_two_mul_sqrt
declarations.signature: theorem supportCoeff_le_two_mul_sqrt (A : Set ℕ) (n : ℕ) : supportCoeff A n ≤ 2 * Nat.sqrt n
declarations.docstring: The support coefficient inherits the sharp divisor-pair envelope.

declarations.name: sqrt_shift_le_sqrt_add_index
declarations.signature: theorem sqrt_shift_le_sqrt_add_index (N j : ℕ) : Nat.sqrt (N + j + 1) ≤ Nat.sqrt N + j + 1
declarations.docstring: The floor-square-root of a shifted index is controlled by the fixed root plus the shift.

declarations.name: binaryCoeffTail_supportCoeff_le_two_sqrt_add_four
declarations.signature: theorem binaryCoeffTail_supportCoeff_le_two_sqrt_add_four (A : Set ℕ) (N : ℕ) : binaryCoeffTail (supportCoeff A) N ≤ 2 * Real.sqrt (N : ℝ) + 4
declarations.docstring: **Sharp support-tail strip.** The scaled binary support-coefficient tail grows at most like `2√N + 4`, exactly the envelope used by the T8 Boolean-carry formulation.

declarations.name: binaryCoeffTail_supportCoeff_pos_of_exists_pos_mem
declarations.signature: theorem binaryCoeffTail_supportCoeff_pos_of_exists_pos_mem (A : Set ℕ) (hA : ∃ a : ℕ, 0 < a ∧ a ∈ A) (N : ℕ) : 0 < binaryCoeffTail (supportCoeff A) N
declarations.docstring: A single positive support element forces every scaled coefficient tail to be strictly positive.

declarations.name: erdosSupportSeries_two_eq_binaryCoeffSeries
declarations.signature: theorem erdosSupportSeries_two_eq_binaryCoeffSeries (A : Set ℕ) : erdosSupportSeries 2 A = binaryCoeffSeries (supportCoeff A)
declarations.docstring: At base two, the support Lambert series is literally the generic binary coefficient series of `supportCoeff A`.

declarations.name: erdosSupportSeries_rational_iff_exists_temperedCarry
declarations.signature: theorem erdosSupportSeries_rational_iff_exists_temperedCarry (A : Set ℕ) : HasRationalValue (erdosSupportSeries 2 A) ↔ ∃ q : ℕ, 0 < q ∧ ∃ U : ℕ → ℤ, IsTemperedBinaryOrbit (supportCoeff A) q U
declarations.docstring: Rationality of the support series is exactly existence of a tempered integer carry orbit for its support coefficient.

declarations.name: exists_temperedCarry_of_support_fraction
declarations.signature: theorem exists_temperedCarry_of_support_fraction (A : Set ℕ) (p : ℤ) (q : ℕ) (hq : 0 < q) (hvalue : erdosSupportSeries 2 A = (p : ℝ) / (q : ℝ)) : ∃ U : ℕ → ℤ, U 0 = p ∧ IsTemperedBinaryOrbit (supportCoeff A) q U
declarations.docstring: A displayed fraction produces an orbit with that exact denominator. The proof constructs the integer prefix orbit and identifies it with the scaled analytic tail.

declarations.name: support_fraction_of_temperedCarry
declarations.signature: theorem support_fraction_of_temperedCarry (A : Set ℕ) (p : ℤ) (q : ℕ) (hq : 0 < q) (U : ℕ → ℤ) (hU0 : U 0 = p) (horbit : IsTemperedBinaryOrbit (supportCoeff A) q U) : erdosSupportSeries 2 A = (p : ℝ) / (q : ℝ)
declarations.docstring: Every tempered carry orbit with initial numerator `p` certifies the displayed fraction `p/q`.

declarations.name: support_fraction_iff_exists_temperedCarry
declarations.signature: theorem support_fraction_iff_exists_temperedCarry (A : Set ℕ) (p : ℤ) (q : ℕ) (hq : 0 < q) : erdosSupportSeries 2 A = (p : ℝ) / (q : ℝ) ↔ ∃ U : ℕ → ℤ, U 0 = p ∧ IsTemperedBinaryOrbit (supportCoeff A) q U
declarations.docstring: Fixed-fraction equivalence form of the Boolean carry trunk.

declarations.name: correctionCarry_add_one_recurrence_iff
declarations.signature: theorem correctionCarry_add_one_recurrence_iff (A : Set ℕ) (K : ℕ → ℤ) (N : ℕ) : K (N + 1) = 2 * K N + 1 - (supportCoeff A (N + 1) : ℤ) ↔ K (N + 1) + 1 = 2 * (K N + 1) - (supportCoeff A (N + 1) : ℤ)
declarations.docstring: Adding one to the correction recurrence turns its affine input `1 - f_A` into the existing unit support-carry input `f_A`.

declarations.name: correctionCarry_two_mul_add_one_recurrence_iff
declarations.signature: theorem correctionCarry_two_mul_add_one_recurrence_iff (A : Set ℕ) (K : ℕ → ℤ) (N : ℕ) : K (N + 1) = 2 * K N + 1 - (supportCoeff A (N + 1) : ℤ) ↔ 2 * (K (N + 1) + 1) = 2 * (2 * (K N + 1)) - ((2 * supportCoeff A (N + 1) : ℕ) : ℤ)
declarations.docstring: Doubling the add-one correction state gives exactly the denominator-two support-carry recurrence.

declarations.name: halfCarry_eq_affineBinaryOrbit
declarations.signature: theorem halfCarry_eq_affineBinaryOrbit (A : Set ℕ) (U : ℕ → ℤ) (hU0 : U 0 = 1) (hrec : ∀ N : ℕ, U (N + 1) = 2 * U N - ((2 * supportCoeff A (N + 1) : ℕ) : ℤ)) : ∀ N : ℕ, U N = affineBinaryOrbit (fun n : ℕ ↦ ((2 * supportCoeff A n : ℕ) : ℤ)) 1 N
declarations.docstring: The initial value and recurrence determine the denominator-two support carry uniquely, and identify it with the existing affine orbit.

declarations.name: support_half_iff_affineBinaryOrbit_tempered
declarations.signature: theorem support_half_iff_affineBinaryOrbit_tempered (A : Set ℕ) : erdosSupportSeries 2 A = (1 : ℝ) / 2 ↔ IsTemperedBinaryOrbit (supportCoeff A) 2 (affineBinaryOrbit (fun n : ℕ ↦ ((2 * supportCoeff A n : ℕ) : ℤ)) 1)
declarations.docstring: A support has displayed value `1/2` exactly when its canonical affine denominator-two carry is tempered. This is the fixed-fraction carry theorem with the existential orbit removed by recurrence uniqueness.

declarations.name: halfSupportSeries_residual_scaled_eq_affineCarry_sub_tail
declarations.signature: theorem halfSupportSeries_residual_scaled_eq_affineCarry_sub_tail (A : Set ℕ) (N : ℕ) : (2 : ℝ) ^ N * ((1 : ℝ) / 2 - erdosSupportSeries 2 A) = (affineBinaryOrbit (fun n : ℕ ↦ ((2 * supportCoeff A n : ℕ) : ℤ)) 1 N : ℤ) / 2 - binaryCoeffTail (supportCoeff A) N
declarations.docstring: Exact residual identity for the canonical half carry. It is the prefix-plus-scaled-tail decomposition expressed directly in the existing affine-orbit coordinate.

declarations.name: supportCoeff_inter_Iic_eq_of_le
declarations.signature: theorem supportCoeff_inter_Iic_eq_of_le (A : Set ℕ) {n M : ℕ} (hnM : n ≤ M) : supportCoeff (A ∩ Set.Iic M) n = supportCoeff A n
declarations.docstring: Truncating a support above `M` does not change its divisor-count coefficient through level `M`.

declarations.name: affineHalfCarry_inter_Iic_eq
declarations.signature: theorem affineHalfCarry_inter_Iic_eq (A : Set ℕ) (M : ℕ) : affineBinaryOrbit (fun n : ℕ ↦ ((2 * supportCoeff (A ∩ Set.Iic M) n : ℕ) : ℤ)) 1 M = affineBinaryOrbit (fun n : ℕ ↦ ((2 * supportCoeff A n : ℕ) : ℤ)) 1 M
declarations.docstring: Consequently, the canonical half carry at depth `M` can be computed from the full support or from its finite prefix through `M`.

declarations.name: halfDenominatorCarry_eq_two_mul_integerCarry
declarations.signature: theorem halfDenominatorCarry_eq_two_mul_integerCarry (A : Set ℕ) (hone : 1 ∉ A) (N : ℕ) : affineBinaryOrbit (fun n : ℕ ↦ ((2 * supportCoeff A n : ℕ) : ℤ)) 1 (N + 1) = 2 * affineBinaryOrbit (fun n : ℕ ↦ (supportCoeff A (n + 1) : ℤ)) 1 N
declarations.docstring: If exponent one is absent, the denominator-two carry factors after its first step as twice an integer affine carry with unit coefficient input. This is the precise `U = 2V` identification from the corrected development.

declarations.name: halfFinitePrefix_residual_eq_pow_mul_integerCarry_sub_finiteTail
declarations.signature: theorem halfFinitePrefix_residual_eq_pow_mul_integerCarry_sub_finiteTail (A : Set ℕ) (hone : 1 ∉ A) (N : ℕ) : (1 : ℝ) / 2 - erdosSupportSeries 2 (A ∩ Set.Iic (N + 1)) = ((affineBinaryOrbit (fun n : ℕ ↦ (supportCoeff A (n + 1) : ℤ)) 1 N : ℤ) - binaryCoeffTail (supportCoeff (A ∩ Set.Iic (N + 1))) (N + 1)) / (2 : ℝ) ^ (N + 1)
declarations.docstring: **Finite-prefix carry-tail residual formula (the formal development.s equation (18)).** The integer term is the existing affine support carry, while the analytic term is the future-multiple tail generated only by the finite support `A ∩ Iic (N+1)`.

declarations.name: temperedCarry_difference
declarations.signature: theorem temperedCarry_difference (A : Set ℕ) (q : ℕ) (U : ℕ → ℤ) (horbit : IsTemperedBinaryOrbit (supportCoeff A) q U) (N : ℕ) : 2 * U N - U (N + 1) = (q : ℤ) * (supportCoeff A (N + 1) : ℤ)
declarations.docstring: The exact carry difference in a support orbit.

declarations.name: denominator_dvd_temperedCarry_difference
declarations.signature: theorem denominator_dvd_temperedCarry_difference (A : Set ℕ) (q : ℕ) (U : ℕ → ℤ) (horbit : IsTemperedBinaryOrbit (supportCoeff A) q U) (N : ℕ) : (q : ℤ) ∣ 2 * U N - U (N + 1)
declarations.docstring: Divisibility of every carry difference by the displayed denominator.

declarations.name: temperedCarry_quotient_eq_supportCoeff
declarations.signature: theorem temperedCarry_quotient_eq_supportCoeff (A : Set ℕ) (q : ℕ) (hq : 0 < q) (U : ℕ → ℤ) (horbit : IsTemperedBinaryOrbit (supportCoeff A) q U) (N : ℕ) : (2 * U N - U (N + 1)) / (q : ℤ) = (supportCoeff A (N + 1) : ℤ)
declarations.docstring: After exact division, the carry quotient is precisely `f_A(N+1)`.

declarations.name: temperedCarry_pos_of_exists_pos_mem
declarations.signature: theorem temperedCarry_pos_of_exists_pos_mem (A : Set ℕ) (hA : ∃ a : ℕ, 0 < a ∧ a ∈ A) (q : ℕ) (hq : 0 < q) (U : ℕ → ℤ) (horbit : IsTemperedBinaryOrbit (supportCoeff A) q U) (N : ℕ) : 0 < U N
declarations.docstring: A positive support and positive multiplier force every state of a tempered support carry to be a positive integer.

declarations.name: temperedCarry_le_denominator_mul_two_sqrt_add_four
declarations.signature: theorem temperedCarry_le_denominator_mul_two_sqrt_add_four (A : Set ℕ) (q : ℕ) (U : ℕ → ℤ) (horbit : IsTemperedBinaryOrbit (supportCoeff A) q U) (N : ℕ) : (U N : ℝ) ≤ (q : ℝ) * (2 * Real.sqrt (N : ℝ) + 4)
declarations.docstring: Every tempered support carry lies in the formal development.s sharp square-root strip.

declarations.name: tendsto_div_pow_zero_of_nonnegative_sqrt_bound
declarations.signature: theorem tendsto_div_pow_zero_of_nonnegative_sqrt_bound (q : ℕ) (U : ℕ → ℤ) (hnonneg : ∀ N : ℕ, 0 ≤ U N) (hbound : ∀ N : ℕ, (U N : ℝ) ≤ (q : ℝ) * (2 * Real.sqrt (N : ℝ) + 4)) : Tendsto (fun N : ℕ ↦ (U N : ℝ) / (2 : ℝ) ^ N) atTop (nhds 0)
declarations.docstring: The square-root strip is strong enough to enforce the tempered boundary; the proof deliberately relaxes `√N` to `N+1` and uses the already checked linear-over-exponential limit.

declarations.name: carryQuotient
declarations.signature: def carryQuotient (q : ℕ) (U : ℕ → ℤ) (n : ℕ) : ℤ
declarations.docstring: The normalized integer carry quotient. Index zero is forced to zero; at positive index `n` this is `(2U(n-1)-U(n))/q`.

declarations.name: carryQuotientAF
declarations.signature: def carryQuotientAF (q : ℕ) (U : ℕ → ℤ) : ArithmeticFunction ℤ
declarations.docstring: The carry quotient packaged as an arithmetic function.

declarations.name: BooleanMobiusCarryCertificate
declarations.signature: structure BooleanMobiusCarryCertificate (p : ℤ) (q : ℕ) (U : ℕ → ℤ) : Prop
declarations.docstring: The quotient-only T8 certificate. It mentions no support set: Boolean Möbius inversion reconstructs the unique normalized support afterwards.

declarations.name: carryQuotient_zero
declarations.signature: @[simp] theorem carryQuotient_zero (q : ℕ) (U : ℕ → ℤ) : carryQuotient q U 0 = 0

declarations.name: carryQuotientAF_eq_supportCoeffAF
declarations.signature: theorem carryQuotientAF_eq_supportCoeffAF (A : Set ℕ) (q : ℕ) (hq : 0 < q) (U : ℕ → ℤ) (horbit : IsTemperedBinaryOrbit (supportCoeff A) q U) : carryQuotientAF q U = supportCoeffAF A
declarations.docstring: A tempered support carry recovers the whole divisor-count coefficient through exact quotient extraction.

declarations.name: moebius_mul_carryQuotientAF
declarations.signature: theorem moebius_mul_carryQuotientAF (A : Set ℕ) (q : ℕ) (hq : 0 < q) (U : ℕ → ℤ) (horbit : IsTemperedBinaryOrbit (supportCoeff A) q U) : ArithmeticFunction.moebius * carryQuotientAF q U = positiveSupportBitAF A
declarations.docstring: The Möbius transform of the carry quotient is the normalized support bit. This is the inversion formula `μ * f_U = 1_A`.

declarations.name: mobius_carryQuotient_boolean
declarations.signature: theorem mobius_carryQuotient_boolean (A : Set ℕ) (q : ℕ) (hq : 0 < q) (U : ℕ → ℤ) (horbit : IsTemperedBinaryOrbit (supportCoeff A) q U) (n : ℕ) : (ArithmeticFunction.moebius * carryQuotientAF q U) n = 0 ∨ (ArithmeticFunction.moebius * carryQuotientAF q U) n = 1
declarations.docstring: Hence the Möbius transform of every exact carry quotient is Boolean.

declarations.name: mobius_carryQuotient_recovers_support
declarations.signature: theorem mobius_carryQuotient_recovers_support (A : Set ℕ) (hzero : 0 ∉ A) (q : ℕ) (hq : 0 < q) (U : ℕ → ℤ) (horbit : IsTemperedBinaryOrbit (supportCoeff A) q U) : {n : ℕ | (ArithmeticFunction.moebius * carryQuotientAF q U) n = 1} = A
declarations.docstring: With `0 ∉ A`, the carry quotient's Boolean Möbius transform recovers the support literally as a set.

declarations.name: exists_booleanMobiusCarry_of_support_fraction
declarations.signature: theorem exists_booleanMobiusCarry_of_support_fraction (A : Set ℕ) (hzero : 0 ∉ A) (hpos : ∃ a : ℕ, 0 < a ∧ a ∈ A) (p : ℤ) (q : ℕ) (hq : 0 < q) (hvalue : erdosSupportSeries 2 A = (p : ℝ) / (q : ℝ)) : ∃ U : ℕ → ℤ, BooleanMobiusCarryCertificate p q U ∧ {n : ℕ | (ArithmeticFunction.moebius * carryQuotientAF q U) n = 1} = A
declarations.docstring: **Forward T8 direction.** A normalized nonempty support with value `p/q` produces a positive square-root-bounded integer orbit whose differences are divisible by `q`, whose carry quotient has Boolean Möbius transform, and whose selected support is exactly the original `A`.

declarations.name: support_fraction_of_booleanMobiusCarry
declarations.signature: theorem support_fraction_of_booleanMobiusCarry (p : ℤ) (q : ℕ) (hq : 0 < q) (U : ℕ → ℤ) (cert : BooleanMobiusCarryCertificate p q U) : let A
declarations.docstring: **Converse T8 direction.** A quotient-only Boolean carry certificate reconstructs a normalized support `A`, becomes a tempered support orbit for that `A`, and certifies the exact value `p/q`.

declarations.name: BooleanMobiusCarryCertificate.reconstructsSupport
declarations.signature: theorem BooleanMobiusCarryCertificate.reconstructsSupport {p : ℤ} {q : ℕ} {U : ℕ → ℤ} (hq : 0 < q) (cert : BooleanMobiusCarryCertificate p q U) : let A
declarations.docstring: A quotient-only Boolean carry certificate exposes every reconstructed object used by the converse, not only the final scalar identity.

declarations.name: exists_normalized_support_fraction_iff_exists_booleanMobiusCarry
declarations.signature: theorem exists_normalized_support_fraction_iff_exists_booleanMobiusCarry (p : ℤ) (q : ℕ) (hq : 0 < q) : (∃ A : Set ℕ, 0 ∉ A ∧ (∃ a : ℕ, 0 < a ∧ a ∈ A) ∧ erdosSupportSeries 2 A = (p : ℝ) / (q : ℝ)) ↔ ∃ U : ℕ → ℤ, BooleanMobiusCarryCertificate p q U
declarations.docstring: **Certificate T8 equivalence.** Positive normalized supports with value `p/q` are in exact correspondence at the existence level with quotient-only Boolean Möbius carry certificates. The converse support is not guessed: it is reconstructed as `booleanMobiusSupport` of the carry quotient.

declarations.name: support23
declarations.docstring: The two-point support used in the formal development.s explicit orbit check.

declarations.name: supportCoeff23Residue
declarations.docstring: The positive-index coefficient `f_{\{2,3\}}`, represented by its six residue values. Its value at zero is a table convenience and is never used as an arithmetic-function value.

declarations.name: carryOrbit23
declarations.docstring: The formal development.s pure period-six numerator orbit.

declarations.name: mod_six_cases
declarations.signature: private theorem mod_six_cases (n : ℕ) : n % 6 = 0 ∨ n % 6 = 1 ∨ n % 6 = 2 ∨ n % 6 = 3 ∨ n % 6 = 4 ∨ n % 6 = 5

declarations.name: supportCoeff23Residue_eq_divisibility
declarations.signature: theorem supportCoeff23Residue_eq_divisibility (n : ℕ) : supportCoeff23Residue n = (if 2 ∣ n then 1 else 0) + (if 3 ∣ n then 1 else 0)
declarations.docstring: Residue form equals the expected pair of divisibility indicators.

declarations.name: supportCoeff_support23_eq_residue
declarations.signature: theorem supportCoeff_support23_eq_residue {n : ℕ} (hn : 0 < n) : supportCoeff support23 n = supportCoeff23Residue n
declarations.docstring: The residue table is not an auxiliary model: on positive integers it is exactly the kernel's `supportCoeff` for `{2,3}`.

declarations.name: erdosSupportSeries_support23_eq_ten_div_twenty_one
declarations.docstring: Exact value of the support series: `1/3 + 1/7 = 10/21`.

declarations.name: carryOrbit23_initial_cycle
declarations.signature: theorem carryOrbit23_initial_cycle : carryOrbit23 0 = 10 ∧ carryOrbit23 1 = 20 ∧ carryOrbit23 2 = 19 ∧ carryOrbit23 3 = 17 ∧ carryOrbit23 4 = 13 ∧ carryOrbit23 5 = 26
declarations.docstring: The six listed orbit values, in their unambiguous order `U₀,...,U₅`.

declarations.name: carryOrbit23_periodic
declarations.signature: theorem carryOrbit23_periodic (N : ℕ) : carryOrbit23 (N + 6) = carryOrbit23 N
declarations.docstring: The orbit is purely periodic with period six.

declarations.name: carryOrbit23_bounds
declarations.signature: theorem carryOrbit23_bounds (N : ℕ) : 10 ≤ carryOrbit23 N ∧ carryOrbit23 N ≤ 26
declarations.docstring: Uniform positive bounds for the explicit orbit.

declarations.name: carryOrbit23_recurrence
declarations.signature: theorem carryOrbit23_recurrence (N : ℕ) : carryOrbit23 (N + 1) = 2 * carryOrbit23 N - ((21 * supportCoeff support23 (N + 1) : ℕ) : ℤ)
declarations.docstring: Exact carry recurrence for the explicit orbit.

declarations.name: carryOrbit23_isTempered
declarations.docstring: The explicit six-cycle is a genuine tempered integer carry orbit, not merely a finite recurrence check.

declarations.name: ten_coprime_twenty_one
declarations.docstring: The displayed fraction is reduced.

declarations.name: twenty_one_dvd_carryOrbit23_difference
declarations.signature: theorem twenty_one_dvd_carryOrbit23_difference (N : ℕ) : (21 : ℤ) ∣ 2 * carryOrbit23 N - carryOrbit23 (N + 1)
declarations.docstring: Exact divisibility of every difference in the explicit six-cycle.

declarations.name: carryOrbit23_quotient_eq_supportCoeff
declarations.signature: theorem carryOrbit23_quotient_eq_supportCoeff (N : ℕ) : (2 * carryOrbit23 N - carryOrbit23 (N + 1)) / (21 : ℤ) = (supportCoeff support23 (N + 1) : ℤ)
declarations.docstring: Exact quotient recovery in the explicit six-cycle.

declarations.name: mobius_carryOrbit23_recovers_support
declarations.signature: theorem mobius_carryOrbit23_recovers_support : {n : ℕ | (ArithmeticFunction.moebius * carryQuotientAF 21 carryOrbit23) n = 1} = support23
declarations.docstring: The explicit quotient's Möbius transform recovers exactly `{2,3}`.

declarations.name: carryOrbit23_eq_twenty_one_mul_tail
declarations.signature: theorem carryOrbit23_eq_twenty_one_mul_tail (N : ℕ) : (carryOrbit23 N : ℝ) = 21 * binaryCoeffTail (supportCoeff support23) N
declarations.docstring: The analytic scaled-tail identity behind the six-cycle.

declarations.name: carryOrbit23_positive_and_sqrt_bounded
declarations.signature: theorem carryOrbit23_positive_and_sqrt_bounded (N : ℕ) : 0 < carryOrbit23 N ∧ (carryOrbit23 N : ℝ) ≤ 21 * (2 * Real.sqrt (N : ℝ) + 4)
declarations.docstring: The explicit orbit satisfies the sharp positive square-root strip.

declarations.name: halfSkippedPrefix
declarations.signature: noncomputable def halfSkippedPrefix (M : ℕ) : Finset ℕ
declarations.docstring: The skipped exponents visible through level `M`.

declarations.name: finiteMersenneShift
declarations.signature: noncomputable def finiteMersenneShift (F : Finset ℕ) : ℝ
declarations.docstring: The Mersenne-rational shift carried by a finite set of exponents.

declarations.name: IsLastHalfGreedySkip
declarations.docstring: `M` is the final exponent skipped by the actual greedy half orbit.

declarations.name: isLastHalfGreedySkip_pos
declarations.signature: theorem isLastHalfGreedySkip_pos {M : ℕ} (hlast : IsLastHalfGreedySkip M) : 0 < M

declarations.name: coe_halfSkippedPrefix_eq_skippedSupport
declarations.signature: theorem coe_halfSkippedPrefix_eq_skippedSupport {M : ℕ} (hlast : IsLastHalfGreedySkip M) : (↑(halfSkippedPrefix M) : Set ℕ) = greedyMersenneSkippedSupport (1 / 2 : ℝ)
declarations.docstring: Under a last-skip hypothesis, the finite prefix is the complete skipped support.

declarations.name: finiteMersenneShift_eq_cast_finiteErdosSum
declarations.signature: theorem finiteMersenneShift_eq_cast_finiteErdosSum (F : Finset ℕ) : finiteMersenneShift F = ((finiteErdosSum F 2 : ℚ) : ℝ)
declarations.docstring: A finite Mersenne shift is visibly rational.

declarations.name: halfGreedySupportSuffix_eq_tail_of_lastSkip
declarations.signature: theorem halfGreedySupportSuffix_eq_tail_of_lastSkip {M : ℕ} (hlast : IsLastHalfGreedySkip M) : positiveMersenneSupportSuffix (greedyMersenneSupport (1 / 2 : ℝ)) M = mersenneTail M
declarations.docstring: After a last skip, every term in the remaining suffix is selected.

declarations.name: halfGreedySupportValue_eq_prefix_add_tail_of_lastSkip
declarations.signature: theorem halfGreedySupportValue_eq_prefix_add_tail_of_lastSkip {M : ℕ} (hlast : IsLastHalfGreedySkip M) : positiveMersenneSupportValue (greedyMersenneSupport (1 / 2 : ℝ)) = halfGreedyMersennePrefix M + mersenneTail M
declarations.docstring: The full selected value is the finite selected prefix plus the complete tail after a last skip.

declarations.name: lastHalfSkip_shiftDelta_eq_remainder_sub_tail
declarations.signature: theorem lastHalfSkip_shiftDelta_eq_remainder_sub_tail {M : ℕ} (hlast : IsLastHalfGreedySkip M) : (1 / 2 : ℝ) + finiteMersenneShift (halfSkippedPrefix M) - erdosBorweinMersenneConstant = greedyMersenneRemainder (1 / 2 : ℝ) M - mersenneTail M
declarations.docstring: Exact fatal coordinate: the finite-shift displacement is the residual excess above the remaining tail.

declarations.name: lastHalfSkip_remainder_lt_weight
declarations.signature: theorem lastHalfSkip_remainder_lt_weight {M : ℕ} (hlast : IsLastHalfGreedySkip M) : greedyMersenneRemainder (1 / 2 : ℝ) M < mersenneWeight M
declarations.docstring: The last skipped branch leaves the level-`M` residual below `w_M`.

declarations.name: lastHalfSkip_tail_le_remainder
declarations.signature: theorem lastHalfSkip_tail_le_remainder {M : ℕ} (hlast : IsLastHalfGreedySkip M) : mersenneTail M ≤ greedyMersenneRemainder (1 / 2 : ℝ) M
declarations.docstring: After a last skip the greedy orbit selects every later exponent, so the partial tail sums telescope inside the level-`M` residual: the complete tail is at most the residual.

declarations.name: lastHalfSkip_shiftDelta_nonneg
declarations.signature: theorem lastHalfSkip_shiftDelta_nonneg {M : ℕ} (hlast : IsLastHalfGreedySkip M) : 0 ≤ (1 / 2 : ℝ) + finiteMersenneShift (halfSkippedPrefix M) - erdosBorweinMersenneConstant
declarations.docstring: The finite-shift displacement is nonnegative for a last skip.

declarations.name: lastHalfSkip_shiftDelta_ne_zero
declarations.signature: theorem lastHalfSkip_shiftDelta_ne_zero {M : ℕ} (_hlast : IsLastHalfGreedySkip M) : (1 / 2 : ℝ) + finiteMersenneShift (halfSkippedPrefix M) - erdosBorweinMersenneConstant ≠ 0
declarations.docstring: Irrationality excludes equality at the rational centre of the shifted fatal interval.

declarations.name: lastHalfSkip_shift_fatalInterval
declarations.signature: theorem lastHalfSkip_shift_fatalInterval {M : ℕ} (hlast : IsLastHalfGreedySkip M) : 0 < (1 / 2 : ℝ) + finiteMersenneShift (halfSkippedPrefix M) - erdosBorweinMersenneConstant ∧ (1 / 2 : ℝ) + finiteMersenneShift (halfSkippedPrefix M) - erdosBorweinMersenneConstant < mersenneGap M
declarations.docstring: Exact one-sided fatal interval around the rational shift determined by a putative last skip.

declarations.name: lastHalfSkip_shadow_mem_gap
declarations.signature: theorem lastHalfSkip_shadow_mem_gap {M : ℕ} (hlast : IsLastHalfGreedySkip M) : |halfGreedyMersenneShadow (M - 1) - erdosBorweinMersenneConstant| < mersenneGap M
declarations.docstring: The `M-1` shadow uses the same gap as the last skipped exponent `M`. This theorem is the exact index reconciliation for the death-hole picture.

declarations.name: mersenneGap_lt_quarterPow
declarations.signature: theorem mersenneGap_lt_quarterPow {M : ℕ} (hM : 3 ≤ M) : mersenneGap M < ((1 : ℝ) / 4) ^ M
declarations.docstring: The Mersenne gap is already smaller than the depth-`2M` dyadic scale for `M ≥ 3`.

declarations.name: lastHalfSkip_shifted_mem_dyadicCylinder
declarations.signature: theorem lastHalfSkip_shifted_mem_dyadicCylinder {M : ℕ} (hM : 3 ≤ M) (hlast : IsLastHalfGreedySkip M) : (1 / 2 : ℝ) - ((1 : ℝ) / 4) ^ M < erdosBorweinMersenneConstant - finiteMersenneShift (halfSkippedPrefix M) ∧ erdosBorweinMersenneConstant - finiteMersenneShift (halfSkippedPrefix M) < (1 / 2 : ℝ)
declarations.docstring: A last skip puts the shifted constant inside the depth-`2M` cylinder immediately below `1 / 2`.

declarations.name: campbellQuarterExponent_periodFreeze_noSync
declarations.signature: theorem campbellQuarterExponent_periodFreeze_noSync {d t P : ℕ} (hd : 2 ≤ d) (htd : t ≤ d) (hP : P ≤ 4 * t) (hAGP : d ^ 4 ≤ P) : False
declarations.docstring: If `d` is the combined prime-progression modulus, `t` is the unavoidable phase factor, and `P` is the prime cap forced by the fatal window, Campbell's quarter-exponent applicability condition is impossible.

declarations.name: campbellPhaseFreeze_parameters_impossible
declarations.signature: theorem campbellPhaseFreeze_parameters_impossible {M q0sq t d P : ℕ} (hq : 0 < q0sq) (htpos : 0 < t) (hd : 2 ≤ d) (ht : M ≤ t * q0sq) (htd : t ≤ d) (hwindow : P * q0sq ≤ 2 * M + 2) (hAGP : d ^ 4 ≤ P) : False
declarations.docstring: Substrate-native parameter map. The hypotheses expose exactly where a future analytic construction must improve: the phase-period factor `t`, the fatal-window cap `P`, or the sublinear modulus exponent.

declarations.name: four_mem_greedyMersenneSkippedSupport_half
declarations.docstring: Exponent four is already skipped by the exact greedy recursion. This small evaluator fixes the positive-index convention used by the last-skip endpoint below.

declarations.name: exists_isLastHalfGreedySkip_iff_skippedSupport_finite
declarations.signature: theorem exists_isLastHalfGreedySkip_iff_skippedSupport_finite : (∃ M : ℕ, IsLastHalfGreedySkip M) ↔ (greedyMersenneSkippedSupport (1 / 2 : ℝ)).Finite
declarations.docstring: A final skipped exponent exists exactly when the actual skipped support is finite. Nonemptiness is unconditional because exponent four is skipped.

declarations.name: existsFatalHalfGap_iff_exists_isLastHalfGreedySkip
declarations.signature: theorem existsFatalHalfGap_iff_exists_isLastHalfGreedySkip : ExistsFatalHalfGap ↔ ∃ M : ℕ, IsLastHalfGreedySkip M
declarations.docstring: The cut-locator's arbitrary-word fatal branch is now identified with the canonical dynamical endpoint: it occurs exactly when the actual greedy orbit has a final skipped exponent.

declarations.name: GreedyShiftWindowZero
declarations.docstring: The last-skip-guarded shifted-window interface. Under a putative last skip `M ≥ 3`, the complete finite shift is required to lie outside the all-one depth-`2M` cylinder. The equivalence theorem below shows that this guarded formulation is an endpoint restatement, not an independent arithmetic producer.

declarations.name: not_isLastHalfGreedySkip_of_shiftWindowZero
declarations.signature: theorem not_isLastHalfGreedySkip_of_shiftWindowZero (hzero : GreedyShiftWindowZero) {M : ℕ} (hM : 3 ≤ M) : ¬ IsLastHalfGreedySkip M
declarations.docstring: The shifted-zero producer rules out a final skipped exponent.

declarations.name: greedyMersenneSkippedSupport_infinite_of_shiftWindowZero
declarations.docstring: The shifted-zero producer forces infinitely many actual skips.

declarations.name: shiftWindowZero_of_greedyMersenneSkippedSupport_infinite
declarations.docstring: Infinitely many skipped exponents make the last-skip hypothesis in the shifted-window producer impossible. This closes the converse logical direction without adding any analytic input: the genuine arithmetic content of `GreedyShiftWindowZero` is exactly the exclusion of a final skip.

declarations.name: shiftWindowZero_iff_greedyMersenneSkippedSupport_infinite
declarations.docstring: Exact endpoint classification of the Campbell-shift interface. It is equivalent to infinitude of the actual greedy skipped support, rather than a strictly weaker producer waiting for a separate composition theorem.

declarations.name: half_mem_mersenneAchievementSet_of_shiftWindowZero
declarations.docstring: Composition with the checked greedy endpoint yields exact half membership once the shifted-zero producer is supplied.

declarations.name: shiftWindowZero_iff_half_mem_mersenneAchievementSet
declarations.docstring: The shifted-window formulation is exactly equivalent to the concrete half-achievement endpoint.

declarations.name: totientTail_pos
declarations.docstring: The local totient tail is strictly positive.

declarations.name: totientTail_le
declarations.signature: lemma totientTail_le (M : ℕ) : totientTail M ≤ (M : ℝ) + 2
declarations.docstring: `R_M ≤ M + 2`: the window bound at depth `0`.

declarations.name: abs_tail_diff_lt
declarations.signature: lemma abs_tail_diff_lt (h N : ℕ) : |totientTail (N + h) - totientTail N| < (N : ℝ) + h + 2
declarations.docstring: The tail difference `D_h(N) = R_{N+h} - R_N` is trapped in the open strip `|D_h(N)| < N + h + 2`.

declarations.name: totientTail_succ
declarations.signature: lemma totientTail_succ (M : ℕ) : totientTail (M + 1) = 2 * totientTail M - (Nat.totient (M + 1) : ℝ)
declarations.docstring: **Carry recurrence.** `R_{M+1} = 2·R_M - φ(M+1)`: doubling the tail shifts the window by one and expels the head totient.

declarations.name: totientTail_eq_partial_add_shifted
declarations.signature: lemma totientTail_eq_partial_add_shifted (M L : ℕ) : totientTail M = (∑ j ∈ Finset.range L, (Nat.totient (M + 1 + j) : ℝ) / 2 ^ (j + 1)) + totientTail (M + L) / 2 ^ L
declarations.docstring: **Exact tail-window split.** The first `L` binary digits of `R_M` are the finite window already used by the certificate kernel; the remaining endpoint is exactly `2⁻ᴸ R_{M+L}`. This is the general coboundary identity behind every later sliding-suffix specialization.

declarations.name: deltaTotient
declarations.docstring: The window step `a_n = φ(n+h) - φ(n)` driving the carry recurrence.

declarations.name: abs_deltaTotient_le
declarations.signature: lemma abs_deltaTotient_le (h n : ℕ) : |deltaTotient h n| ≤ (n + h : ℕ)
declarations.docstring: The fresh totient forcing letter is bounded by its later argument. This is the arithmetic input that makes the depth barrier strict: at depth `L` the new letter has size at most `N + h + L + 1`, one less than the endpoint corridor radius.

declarations.name: windowDiscrepancy_succ
declarations.signature: lemma windowDiscrepancy_succ (h N L : ℕ) : windowDiscrepancy h N (L + 1) = 2 * windowDiscrepancy h N L + deltaTotient h (N + L + 1)
declarations.docstring: **Exact depth recurrence for the endpoint word.** Increasing certificate depth doubles the existing signed window and appends exactly one fresh totient-difference letter. This is a depth recurrence with explicit input, not the autonomous temporal recurrence ruled out by the finite-state no-go.

declarations.name: windowDiscrepancy_slide
declarations.signature: theorem windowDiscrepancy_slide (h N L : ℕ) : windowDiscrepancy h (N + 1) L = 2 * windowDiscrepancy h N L - deltaTotient h (N + 1) * 2 ^ L + deltaTotient h (N + L + 1)
declarations.docstring: **Exact spatial recurrence for a fixed-depth window.** Sliding the base from `N` to `N+1` expels the head letter with weight `2^L`, doubles the old window, and appends one fresh endpoint letter. Modulo `2^L` only the doubled residue and the fresh endpoint remain. The natural bound `abs_deltaTotient_le` already places that endpoint inside the certificate corridor, so this identity alone is not a one-step anti-trapping theorem.

declarations.name: certifiedKill_succ
declarations.signature: theorem certifiedKill_succ {h N L : ℕ} (hcert : certifiedKill h N L) : certifiedKill h N (L + 1)
declarations.docstring: **Depth barrier.** Once an endpoint certificate fires, every deeper window fires as well. The proof uses the exact input-driven recurrence and the strict totient bound; no finite state or empirical residue table enters. Equivalently, endpoint survival is prefix-closed in certificate depth.

declarations.name: certifiedKill_add
declarations.signature: theorem certifiedKill_add {h N L : ℕ} (hcert : certifiedKill h N L) : ∀ k : ℕ, certifiedKill h N (L + k)
declarations.docstring: A successful depth is an absorbing proof state: appending any finite suffix of the totient forcing word cannot recreate an endpoint survivor.

declarations.name: endpointSurvivor
declarations.signature: def endpointSurvivor (h N L : ℕ) (z : ℤ) : Prop
declarations.docstring: The signed endpoint-survivor fibre at depth `L`. Its elements are the integer representatives of the exact discrepancy residue that still fit in the analytic endpoint corridor.

declarations.name: no_endpointSurvivor_of_certifiedKill
declarations.signature: theorem no_endpointSurvivor_of_certifiedKill {h N L : ℕ} (hcert : certifiedKill h N L) : ∀ z : ℤ, ¬ endpointSurvivor h N L z
declarations.docstring: A central-arc certificate empties the signed endpoint-survivor fibre.

declarations.name: certifiedKill_of_no_endpointSurvivor
declarations.signature: theorem certifiedKill_of_no_endpointSurvivor {h N L : ℕ} (hroom : (2 * (N + h + L + 2) : ℤ) < 2 ^ L) (hempty : ∀ z : ℤ, ¬ endpointSurvivor h N L z) : certifiedKill h N L
declarations.docstring: Under the natural dyadic-room condition, fibre emptiness is not merely a consequence of `certifiedKill`: it is exactly the central-arc certificate.

declarations.name: certifiedKill_iff_endpointSurvivor_empty
declarations.signature: theorem certifiedKill_iff_endpointSurvivor_empty {h N L : ℕ} (hroom : (2 * (N + h + L + 2) : ℤ) < 2 ^ L) : certifiedKill h N L ↔ ∀ z : ℤ, ¬ endpointSurvivor h N L z
declarations.docstring: Paper-facing form of the survivor-kernel bridge: once the depth floor is available, `certifiedKill` is equivalent to exact emptiness of the signed endpoint fibre.

declarations.name: tail_diff_succ
declarations.signature: lemma tail_diff_succ (h M : ℕ) : totientTail (M + 1 + h) - totientTail (M + 1) = 2 * (totientTail (M + h) - totientTail M) - (deltaTotient h (M + 1) : ℝ)
declarations.docstring: The tail difference obeys the exact carry recurrence `D_h(M+1) = 2·D_h(M) - a_{M+1}`.

declarations.name: tail_diff_eq_increment_prefix_add_shifted
declarations.signature: lemma tail_diff_eq_increment_prefix_add_shifted (h N L : ℕ) : totientTail (N + h) - totientTail N = (∑ j ∈ Finset.range L, (deltaTotient h (N + 1 + j) : ℝ) / 2 ^ (j + 1)) + (totientTail (N + L + h) - totientTail (N + L)) / 2 ^ L
declarations.docstring: **Exact endpoint telescoping for every tail difference.** The first `L` dyadic digits are the signed increment prefix, and the only remainder is the translated tail difference scaled by `2⁻ᴸ`. On the LCM diagonal this is the dyadic-cocycle collapse: sliding a suffix exposes no independent arithmetic sample beyond the original carry orbit.

declarations.name: carryOrbit
declarations.signature: def carryOrbit (h N : ℕ) (d : ℤ) : ℕ → ℤ | 0 => d | i + 1 => 2 * carryOrbit h N d i - deltaTotient h (N + i + 1) /-- **Orbit tracking.** If the tail difference at `N` is the integer `d`, the carry orbit computes the exact tail-difference sequence: `orbit i = D_h(N+i)`. -/ lemma carryOrbit_eq_tail_diff {h N : ℕ} {d : ℤ} (hd : (d : ℝ) = totientTail (N + h) - totientTail N) (i : ℕ) : (carryOrbit h N d i : ℝ) = totientTail (N + i + h) - totientTail (N + i)
declarations.docstring: The integer carry orbit launched from candidate `d` at position `N`: `orbit 0 = d`, `orbit (i+1) = 2·orbit i - a_{N+i+1}`. If `D_h(N)` is the integer `d`, this orbit equals `D_h(N+i)` forever.

declarations.name: carryOrbit_eq_tail_diff
declarations.signature: lemma carryOrbit_eq_tail_diff {h N : ℕ} {d : ℤ} (hd : (d : ℝ) = totientTail (N + h) - totientTail N) (i : ℕ) : (carryOrbit h N d i : ℝ) = totientTail (N + i + h) - totientTail (N + i)
declarations.docstring: **Orbit tracking.** If the tail difference at `N` is the integer `d`, the carry orbit computes the exact tail-difference sequence: `orbit i = D_h(N+i)`.

declarations.name: survivorKill
declarations.signature: def survivorKill (h N K : ℕ) : Prop
declarations.docstring: The **carry-survivor certificate**: every integer candidate in the initial box `|d| ≤ N+h+1` — enumerated as `d = j - (N+h+1)` for `j < 2·(N+h+1)+1` — provably escapes the strip `|·| ≤ N+i+h+1` within `K` steps (escape stated as a two-sided disjunction). Finitely many candidates, finitely many steps: decidable.

declarations.name: tail_diff_notMem_int_of_survivorKill
declarations.signature: theorem tail_diff_notMem_int_of_survivorKill {h N K : ℕ} (hkill : survivorKill h N K) : totientTail (N + h) - totientTail N ∉ Set.range ((↑) : ℤ → ℝ)
declarations.docstring: **Survivor soundness.** A survivor kill proves the tail difference is not an integer: an integer value `d` would launch an integer carry orbit equal to the true tail-difference sequence, which stays inside the strip `|D_h(N+i)| < N+i+h+2` forever — but the certificate says every candidate provably escapes within `K` steps. This is the machine-checked form of the wave-21 bounded-orbit reformulation.

declarations.name: tail_diff_mul
declarations.signature: lemma tail_diff_mul (h N m : ℕ) : totientTail (N + m * h) - totientTail N = ∑ i ∈ Finset.range m, (totientTail (N + i * h + h) - totientTail (N + i * h))
declarations.docstring: **Period-ray telescoping.** The `m·h` tail difference is the sum of `m` translated `h` tail differences: integrality flows down every multiple.

declarations.name: tail_diff_mul_mem_int
declarations.signature: lemma tail_diff_mul_mem_int {h N₀ : ℕ} (hint : ∀ N, N₀ ≤ N → totientTail (N + h) - totientTail N ∈ Set.range ((↑) : ℤ → ℝ)) (m N : ℕ) (hN : N₀ ≤ N) : totientTail (N + m * h) - totientTail N ∈ Set.range ((↑) : ℤ → ℝ)
declarations.docstring: Integrality propagates down every period ray: if every `h`-difference from `N₀` on is an integer, so is every `m·h`-difference.

declarations.name: irrational_totient_series_of_multiple_survivor_supply
declarations.signature: theorem irrational_totient_series_of_multiple_survivor_supply (hsupply : ∀ h₀ : ℕ, 0 < h₀ → ∀ N₀ : ℕ, ∃ m, 0 < m ∧ ∃ N, N₀ ≤ N ∧ ∃ K, survivorKill (m * h₀) N K) : Irrational (∑' n : ℕ, (Nat.totient n : ℝ) / 2 ^ n)
declarations.docstring: **The wave-22 reduction: multiple-period survivor supply.** The weakest supply hypothesis in the ladder so far: for every primitive period `h₀` it suffices to certify a survivor kill along ANY multiple `m·h₀`, at arbitrarily large `N`. Rationality forces integrality along the whole period ray, so one dead multiple kills the primitive.

declarations.name: irrational_totient_series_of_multiple_certificate_supply
declarations.signature: theorem irrational_totient_series_of_multiple_certificate_supply (hsupply : ∀ h₀ : ℕ, 0 < h₀ → ∀ N₀ : ℕ, ∃ m, 0 < m ∧ ∃ N, N₀ ≤ N ∧ ∃ L, certifiedKill (m * h₀) N L) : Irrational (∑' n : ℕ, (Nat.totient n : ℝ) / 2 ^ n)
declarations.docstring: The endpoint-certificate variant of the multiple-period reduction: one certified window kill along any multiple of each primitive period suffices.

declarations.name: periodLcm
declarations.signature: def periodLcm : ℕ → ℕ | 0 => 1 | t + 1 => Nat.lcm (periodLcm t) (t + 1) lemma periodLcm_pos (t : ℕ) : 0 < periodLcm t
declarations.docstring: `periodLcm t = lcm(1, …, t)`: the universal period at scale `t`. Every primitive period `h₀ ≤ t` divides it.

declarations.name: dvd_periodLcm
declarations.signature: lemma dvd_periodLcm {h t : ℕ} (h1 : 1 ≤ h) (ht : h ≤ t) : h ∣ periodLcm t

declarations.name: irrational_totient_series_of_lcm_survivor_supply
declarations.signature: theorem irrational_totient_series_of_lcm_survivor_supply (hsupply : ∀ t₀ N₀ : ℕ, ∃ t, t₀ ≤ t ∧ ∃ N, N₀ ≤ N ∧ ∃ K, survivorKill (periodLcm t) N K) : Irrational (∑' n : ℕ, (Nat.totient n : ℝ) / 2 ^ n)
