declarations.docstring: **One-parameter collapse of the throat.** Survivor kills along the single family `lcm(1..t)` — arbitrarily large `t`, arbitrarily large `N` — prove #249. Rationality would give SOME period `h₀`; every `h₀ ≤ t` divides `lcm(1..t)`, so the whole `∀ h₀` obligation lands on one ℕ-indexed ray.

declarations.name: survivorKill_example
declarations.docstring: Machine-checked survivor kill: period `1` dies at `N = 12` within `K = 7` steps — every integer candidate in `|d| ≤ 14` escapes the strip.

declarations.name: tail_diff_not_int_survivor_example
declarations.signature: theorem tail_diff_not_int_survivor_example : totientTail (12 + 1) - totientTail 12 ∉ Set.range ((↑) : ℤ → ℝ)
declarations.docstring: Period `1` has no integer tail difference at `N = 12` — re-derived through the orbit certificate rather than the endpoint residue.

declarations.name: survivor_strict_at_equal_budget
declarations.signature: theorem survivor_strict_at_equal_budget : survivorKill 1 57 8 ∧ ∀ L ∈ Finset.Icc 1 8, ¬ certifiedKill 1 57 L
declarations.docstring: The survivor certificate is STRICTLY stronger than the endpoint certificate at equal depth budget: at `(h, N) = (1, 57)` the survivor interval dies at `K = 8` while every endpoint window `L ≤ 8` fails. (The wave-22 probe measures the two families as near-equivalent in minimal kill depth — this witness pins down exactly the strictness that remains.)

declarations.name: certifiedKill_all_upto_sixteen
declarations.signature: theorem certifiedKill_all_upto_sixteen : ∀ h ∈ Finset.Icc 1 16, certifiedKill h 14 9
declarations.docstring: Extended endpoint deposit: every period `h ≤ 16` is certified dead at `(N, L) = (14, 9)` — twice the wave-21 period range in a smaller window than its `(12, 16)`.

declarations.name: tail_diff_not_int_upto_sixteen
declarations.signature: theorem tail_diff_not_int_upto_sixteen (h : ℕ) (h1 : 1 ≤ h) (h16 : h ≤ 16) : totientTail (14 + h) - totientTail 14 ∉ Set.range ((↑) : ℤ → ℝ)
declarations.docstring: No shifted tail difference with period `h ≤ 16` is an integer at `N = 14`.

declarations.name: totient_series_ne_rat_of_den_dvd_upto_sixteen
declarations.signature: theorem totient_series_ne_rat_of_den_dvd_upto_sixteen (r : ℚ) (h : ℕ) (h1 : 1 ≤ h) (h16 : h ≤ 16) (hdvd : (r.den : ℕ) ∣ 2 ^ 14 * (2 ^ h - 1)) : (∑' n : ℕ, (Nat.totient n : ℝ) / 2 ^ n) ≠ (r : ℝ)
declarations.docstring: **Extended denominator exclusion.** The totient series equals no rational whose denominator divides `2¹⁴·(2ʰ-1)` for some `1 ≤ h ≤ 16`: no dyadic rational with denominator up to `2¹⁴`, and no odd part whose multiplicative order of `2` is at most `16`. Subsumes the wave-21 lattice on both axes.

declarations.name: ExactOrderPrimePowerWitness
declarations.signature: def ExactOrderPrimePowerWitness (b n q s : Nat) : Prop
declarations.docstring: Exact-order prime-power witness for exponent `n` over base `b`: the prime power `q ^ s` divides `b ^ m - 1` exactly when `n ∣ m`. Prime powers (rather than bare primes) keep the Zsigmondy exception pairs such as `(b, n) = (2, 6)` coverable via `3 ^ 2`.

declarations.signature: theorem exact_order_witness_supplies_PrimeComponentWitness {F : Finset Nat} {L A b p n q s : Nat} (hb : 2 ≤ b) (h0 : 0 ∉ F) (hL_eq : L = F.lcm id) (hp : Nat.Prime p) (hpL : p ∣ L) (hnF : n ∈ F) (hn_val : n.factorization p = L.factorization p) (hn_iso : ∀ m ∈ F, n ∣ m → m = n) (hwit : ExactOrderPrimePowerWitness b n q s) (hA_eq : A = ∑ m ∈ F, (b ^ L - 1) / (b ^ m - 1)) : PrimeComponentWitness L A b p q

declarations.signature: theorem orderOf_isRoot_cyclotomic_zmod_pow_mul {q : Nat} (hq : Nat.Prime q) : ∀ n : Nat, 0 < n → ∀ x : ZMod q, (Polynomial.cyclotomic n (ZMod q)).IsRoot x → ∃ a : Nat, n = q ^ a * orderOf x

declarations.name: exactOrderPrimePowerWitness_lift_odd
declarations.signature: theorem exactOrderPrimePowerWitness_lift_odd {b q m a : Nat} (hq : Nat.Prime q) (hq_odd : Odd q) (hb : 2 ≤ b) (hcop : Nat.Coprime b q) (hm_ord : m = orderOf (ZMod.unitOfCoprime b hcop)) (ha : 0 < a) : ExactOrderPrimePowerWitness b (q ^ a * m) q ((b ^ m - 1).factorization q + a)

declarations.name: exactOrderPrimePowerWitness_lift_two
declarations.signature: theorem exactOrderPrimePowerWitness_lift_two {b a : Nat} (hb : 2 ≤ b) (hb_odd : Odd b) (ha : 0 < a) : ExactOrderPrimePowerWitness b (2 ^ a) 2 ((b - 1).factorization 2 + (b + 1).factorization 2 + (a - 1))

declarations.name: exists_exactOrderPrimePowerWitness
declarations.signature: theorem exists_exactOrderPrimePowerWitness {b n : Nat} (hb : 2 ≤ b) (hn : 2 ≤ n) : ∃ q s : Nat, ExactOrderPrimePowerWitness b n q s

declarations.name: commonNumerator
declarations.signature: def commonNumerator (F : Finset Nat) (b : Nat) : Nat
declarations.docstring: Common numerator `A = ∑_{n ∈ F} (b ^ lcm F - 1) / (b ^ n - 1)` of the sum `∑_{n ∈ F} 1 / (b ^ n - 1)` over the common denominator `b ^ lcm F - 1`.

declarations.name: commonDenominator
declarations.signature: def commonDenominator (F : Finset Nat) (b : Nat) : Nat
declarations.docstring: Common denominator `B = b ^ lcm F - 1` of `∑_{n ∈ F} 1 / (b ^ n - 1)`.

declarations.name: reducedDenominator
declarations.signature: def reducedDenominator (F : Finset Nat) (b : Nat) : Nat
declarations.docstring: Reduced denominator `Q` of `∑_{n ∈ F} 1 / (b ^ n - 1)` in lowest terms.

declarations.name: coprime_base_reducedDenominator
declarations.signature: theorem coprime_base_reducedDenominator (F : Finset Nat) (b : Nat) (h0 : 0 ∉ F) (hb : 2 ≤ b) : Nat.Coprime b (reducedDenominator F b)

declarations.name: finite_period_noncollapse
declarations.signature: theorem finite_period_noncollapse (F : Finset Nat) (b : Nat) (hF : F.Nonempty) (h0 : 0 ∉ F) (hb : 2 ≤ b) : orderOf (ZMod.unitOfCoprime b (coprime_base_reducedDenominator F b h0 hb)) = F.lcm id
declarations.docstring: **Unconditional finite period non-collapse.** For every finite nonempty set `F` of positive exponents and every base `b ≥ 2`, the reduced denominator `Q` of `∑_{n ∈ F} 1 / (b ^ n - 1)` satisfies `ord_Q(b) = lcm F`. No witness-supply hypothesis remains: exact-order prime-power witnesses are constructed for every selector row via the cyclotomic route.

declarations.name: pow_sub_one_pos_of_ne_zero
declarations.signature: theorem pow_sub_one_pos_of_ne_zero (b n : Nat) (hb : 2 ≤ b) (hn : n ≠ 0) : 0 < b ^ n - 1
declarations.docstring: **Unconditional finite period non-collapse.** For every finite nonempty set `F` of positive exponents and every base `b ≥ 2`, the reduced denominator `Q` of `∑_{n ∈ F} 1 / (b ^ n - 1)` satisfies `ord_Q(b) = lcm F`. No witness-supply hypothesis remains: exact-order prime-power witnesses are constructed for every selector row via the cyclotomic route. -/ theorem finite_period_noncollapse (F : Finset Nat) (b : Nat) (hF : F.Nonempty) (h0 : 0 ∉ F) (hb : 2 ≤ b) : orderOf (ZMod.unitOfCoprime b (coprime_base_reducedDenominator F b h0 hb)) = F.lcm id := by refine finite_period_noncollapse_from_exact_order_witness_supply F (F.lcm id) (commonNumerator F b) (commonDenominator F b) (reducedDenominator F b) b (coprime_base_reducedDenominator F b h0 hb) hF h0 hb rfl rfl rfl rfl ?_ intro n _ hn2 _ exact exists_exactOrderPrimePowerWitness hb hn2 /-! ## Rational-denominator bridge and denominator growth The unconditional theorem above speaks about `reducedDenominator F b`, a `Nat`-valued construction. T

declarations.name: natCast_pow_sub_one
declarations.signature: theorem natCast_pow_sub_one (b n : Nat) (hb : 2 ≤ b) : ((b ^ n - 1 : Nat) : Rat) = (b : Rat) ^ n - 1

declarations.name: finiteErdosSum
declarations.signature: def finiteErdosSum (F : Finset Nat) (b : Nat) : Rat
declarations.docstring: The finite Erdős partial sum `∑_{n ∈ F} 1 / (b ^ n - 1)` as a rational number, stated with subtraction in `ℚ` so the statement reads exactly like the mathematical series.

declarations.name: lcm_ne_zero_of_zero_not_mem
declarations.signature: theorem lcm_ne_zero_of_zero_not_mem (F : Finset Nat) (h0 : 0 ∉ F) : F.lcm id ≠ 0

declarations.name: finiteErdosSum_eq_commonNumerator_div_commonDenominator
declarations.signature: theorem finiteErdosSum_eq_commonNumerator_div_commonDenominator (F : Finset Nat) (b : Nat) (h0 : 0 ∉ F) (hb : 2 ≤ b) : finiteErdosSum F b = (commonNumerator F b : Rat) / (commonDenominator F b : Rat)
declarations.docstring: The rational partial sum equals the audited common fraction `commonNumerator / commonDenominator`.

declarations.name: den_natCast_div_natCast_of_coprime
declarations.signature: theorem den_natCast_div_natCast_of_coprime (x y : Nat) (hy : y ≠ 0) (hcop : Nat.Coprime x y) : ((x : Rat) / (y : Rat)).den = y
declarations.docstring: Denominator of a quotient of coprime naturals.

declarations.name: mul_div_cross_of_dvd
declarations.signature: theorem mul_div_cross_of_dvd (g a d : Nat) (hg : 0 < g) (ha : g ∣ a) (hd : g ∣ d) : a * (d / g) = a / g * d

declarations.name: den_natCast_div_natCast
declarations.signature: theorem den_natCast_div_natCast (a d : Nat) (hd : d ≠ 0) : ((a : Rat) / (d : Rat)).den = d / Nat.gcd a d
declarations.docstring: Denominator of a quotient of naturals, via the gcd.

declarations.name: finiteErdosSum_den_eq_reducedDenominator
declarations.signature: theorem finiteErdosSum_den_eq_reducedDenominator (F : Finset Nat) (b : Nat) (h0 : 0 ∉ F) (hb : 2 ≤ b) : (finiteErdosSum F b).den = reducedDenominator F b
declarations.docstring: **Statement-faithfulness bridge.** The audited `reducedDenominator F b` is literally `Rat.den` of the rational partial sum `∑_{n ∈ F} 1 / (b^n - 1)`: the theorem about the constructed denominator is a theorem about the actual reduced denominator of the actual rational number.

declarations.name: coprime_base_den_finiteErdosSum
declarations.signature: theorem coprime_base_den_finiteErdosSum (F : Finset Nat) (b : Nat) (h0 : 0 ∉ F) (hb : 2 ≤ b) : Nat.Coprime b (finiteErdosSum F b).den

declarations.name: finite_period_noncollapse_concrete
declarations.signature: theorem finite_period_noncollapse_concrete (F : Finset Nat) (b L Q : Nat) (hF : F.Nonempty) (h0 : 0 ∉ F) (hb : 2 ≤ b) (hL : L = F.lcm id) (hQ : Q = reducedDenominator F b) (hcop : Nat.Coprime b Q) : orderOf (ZMod.unitOfCoprime b hcop) = L
declarations.docstring: Concrete-transport wrapper: the unconditional theorem restated over any naturals `L`, `Q` proven equal to `lcm F` and the reduced denominator. This is the thin interface a theorem-route certificate emitter instantiates: a generated target supplies `F`, `b`, the two normalisation equalities, and a coprimality witness, and receives the exact-order conclusion with no per-prime witness rows.

declarations.name: finite_period_noncollapse_rat_den
declarations.signature: theorem finite_period_noncollapse_rat_den (F : Finset Nat) (b : Nat) (hF : F.Nonempty) (h0 : 0 ∉ F) (hb : 2 ≤ b) : orderOf (ZMod.unitOfCoprime b (coprime_base_den_finiteErdosSum F b h0 hb)) = F.lcm id
declarations.docstring: The unconditional finite theorem stated over `Rat.den` of the literal rational partial sum: `ord_Q(b) = lcm F` where `Q` is the denominator in lowest terms of `∑_{n ∈ F} 1 / (b ^ n - 1)` as a rational number.

declarations.name: lcm_lt_den_finiteErdosSum
declarations.signature: theorem lcm_lt_den_finiteErdosSum (F : Finset Nat) (b : Nat) (hF : F.Nonempty) (h0 : 0 ∉ F) (hb : 2 ≤ b) (h2 : 2 ≤ F.lcm id) : F.lcm id < (finiteErdosSum F b).den
declarations.docstring: **Denominator growth.** The reduced denominator of the finite Erdős partial sum strictly dominates `lcm F`: since `ord_Q(b) = lcm F` and the order divides `φ(Q) < Q`, the denominator can never collapse below the lcm. This is the quantitative engine an irrationality attack on the infinite series needs from the finite layer.

declarations.name: lcm_Icc_lt_den_erdosPartialSum
declarations.signature: theorem lcm_Icc_lt_den_erdosPartialSum (b N : Nat) (hb : 2 ≤ b) (hN : 2 ≤ N) : (Finset.Icc 1 N).lcm id < (∑ n ∈ Finset.Icc 1 N, 1 / ((b : Rat) ^ n - 1)).den
declarations.docstring: **Denominator growth for the classical partial sums.** For every base `b ≥ 2` and every `N ≥ 2`, the reduced denominator of `∑_{n=1}^{N} 1 / (b ^ n - 1)` strictly exceeds `lcm(1, …, N)`. Stated in pure Mathlib vocabulary: no project-local definition appears in the statement.

declarations.name: strictMono_add_le_apply
declarations.signature: theorem strictMono_add_le_apply {a : ℕ → ℕ} (ha : StrictMono a) (k i : ℕ) : a k + i ≤ a (k + i)
declarations.docstring: A strictly monotone `ℕ`-sequence gains at least one per step: `a k + i ≤ a (k + i)`.

declarations.name: one_div_den_mul_den_le_abs_sub
declarations.signature: theorem one_div_den_mul_den_le_abs_sub {q r : ℚ} (h : q ≠ r) : (1 : ℝ) / ((q.den : ℝ) * (r.den : ℝ)) ≤ |(q : ℝ) - (r : ℝ)|
declarations.docstring: **Rational gap.** Two distinct rationals are at least `1 / (den · den)` apart as real numbers.

declarations.name: irrational_of_den_mul_abs_sub_tendsto_zero
declarations.signature: theorem irrational_of_den_mul_abs_sub_tendsto_zero {x : ℝ} {u : ℕ → ℚ} (hne : ∀ᶠ k in atTop, ((u k : ℝ)) ≠ x) (h0 : Tendsto (fun k => ((u k).den : ℝ) * |x - (u k : ℝ)|) atTop (nhds 0)) : Irrational x
declarations.docstring: **Irrationality criterion.** If a sequence of rationals is eventually never equal to `x` and `den(u k) * |x - u k| → 0`, then `x` is irrational. This is the Dirichlet-gap argument stated over `Rat.den` — the same denominator object the finite kernel theorems audit.

declarations.name: den_finiteErdosSum_dvd
declarations.signature: theorem den_finiteErdosSum_dvd (F : Finset Nat) (b : Nat) (h0 : 0 ∉ F) (hb : 2 ≤ b) : (finiteErdosSum F b).den ∣ b ^ (F.lcm id) - 1
declarations.docstring: The reduced denominator of the finite Erdős partial sum divides the common denominator `b ^ lcm F - 1`.

declarations.name: summable_erdos_term
declarations.signature: theorem summable_erdos_term (b : ℕ) (hb : 2 ≤ b) (a : ℕ → ℕ) (ha : StrictMono a) (ha0 : 1 ≤ a 0) : Summable (fun k => (1 : ℝ) / ((b : ℝ) ^ (a k) - 1))
declarations.docstring: Summability of the Erdős terms along any strictly monotone support with positive first element.

declarations.name: summable_erdos_term_shift
declarations.signature: theorem summable_erdos_term_shift (b : ℕ) (hb : 2 ≤ b) (a : ℕ → ℕ) (ha : StrictMono a) (ha0 : 1 ≤ a 0) (k : ℕ) : Summable (fun i => (1 : ℝ) / ((b : ℝ) ^ (a (i + k)) - 1))
declarations.docstring: The shifted-tail terms are summable.

declarations.name: erdos_term_pos
declarations.signature: theorem erdos_term_pos (b : ℕ) (hb : 2 ≤ b) (a : ℕ → ℕ) (ha : StrictMono a) (ha0 : 1 ≤ a 0) (j : ℕ) : (0 : ℝ) < 1 / ((b : ℝ) ^ (a j) - 1)
declarations.docstring: Positivity of every Erdős term.

declarations.name: erdos_tail_pos
declarations.signature: theorem erdos_tail_pos (b : ℕ) (hb : 2 ≤ b) (a : ℕ → ℕ) (ha : StrictMono a) (ha0 : 1 ≤ a 0) (k : ℕ) : (0 : ℝ) < ∑' i, (1 : ℝ) / ((b : ℝ) ^ (a (i + k)) - 1)
declarations.docstring: The tail after `k` terms is strictly positive.

declarations.name: erdos_tail_le
declarations.signature: theorem erdos_tail_le (b : ℕ) (hb : 2 ≤ b) (a : ℕ → ℕ) (ha : StrictMono a) (ha0 : 1 ≤ a 0) (k : ℕ) : ∑' i, (1 : ℝ) / ((b : ℝ) ^ (a (i + k)) - 1) ≤ 4 * ((1 : ℝ) / b) ^ (a k)
declarations.docstring: Geometric tail estimate: the tail after `k` terms is at most `4 * (1/b) ^ (a k)`.

declarations.name: finiteErdosSum_image_range_cast
declarations.signature: theorem finiteErdosSum_image_range_cast (b : ℕ) (a : ℕ → ℕ) (ha : StrictMono a) (k : ℕ) : ((finiteErdosSum ((Finset.range k).image a) b : ℚ) : ℝ) = ∑ i ∈ Finset.range k, (1 : ℝ) / ((b : ℝ) ^ (a i) - 1)
declarations.docstring: Casting the finite Erdős sum over the image support to the reals gives the real partial sum.

declarations.name: irrational_erdosSum_of_lcm_gap
declarations.signature: theorem irrational_erdosSum_of_lcm_gap (b : ℕ) (hb : 2 ≤ b) (a : ℕ → ℕ) (ha : StrictMono a) (ha0 : 1 ≤ a 0) (hgap : Tendsto (fun k => a k - ((Finset.range k).image a).lcm id) atTop atTop) : Irrational (∑' k, (1 : ℝ) / ((b : ℝ) ^ (a k) - 1))
declarations.docstring: **Infinite bridge, class form.** For every base `b ≥ 2` and every strictly monotone support `a` with `a 0 ≥ 1`, if the gap between `a k` and `lcm {a 0, …, a (k-1)}` tends to infinity, then the infinite series `∑ 1 / (b ^ (a k) - 1)` is irrational. The reduced denominators of the partial sums — the objects the finite kernel theorems audit — are the `q` of the Dirichlet gap argument.

declarations.name: lcm_image_factorial_succ
declarations.signature: theorem lcm_image_factorial_succ (k : ℕ) (hk : 1 ≤ k) : ((Finset.range k).image (fun i => Nat.factorial (i + 1))).lcm id = Nat.factorial k
declarations.docstring: lcm of the factorial support prefix `{1!, …, k!}` is `k!`.

declarations.name: lcm_image_two_pow
declarations.signature: theorem lcm_image_two_pow (k : ℕ) (hk : 1 ≤ k) : ((Finset.range k).image (fun i => 2 ^ i)).lcm id = 2 ^ (k - 1)
declarations.docstring: lcm of the two-power support prefix `{2^0, …, 2^(k-1)}` is `2^(k-1)`.

declarations.name: irrational_erdosSum_factorial_support
declarations.signature: theorem irrational_erdosSum_factorial_support (b : ℕ) (hb : 2 ≤ b) : Irrational (∑' k, (1 : ℝ) / ((b : ℝ) ^ (Nat.factorial (k + 1)) - 1))
declarations.docstring: **First infinite-support instance: factorial support.** For every base `b ≥ 2`, the infinite series `∑_{k≥0} 1 / (b ^ (k+1)! - 1)` is irrational. Stated in pure Mathlib vocabulary. The support `{n! : n ≥ 1}` is not pairwise coprime (each factorial divides the next), so this instance is outside the classical coprime-support family.

declarations.name: irrational_erdosSum_two_pow_support
declarations.signature: theorem irrational_erdosSum_two_pow_support (b : ℕ) (hb : 2 ≤ b) : Irrational (∑' k, (1 : ℝ) / ((b : ℝ) ^ (2 ^ k) - 1))
declarations.docstring: **First infinite-support instance: geometric support.** For every base `b ≥ 2`, the infinite series `∑_{k≥0} 1 / (b ^ (2^k) - 1)` is irrational. The gap `2^k - lcm = 2^(k-1)` has bounded ratio to the lcm, so this instance is *not* reachable by a Liouville-number shortcut; the denominator-gap criterion handles it uniformly.

declarations.name: erdos257_family_factorial_instance
declarations.signature: theorem erdos257_family_factorial_instance : Irrational (∑' k, (1 : ℝ) / ((2 : ℝ) ^ (Nat.factorial (k + 1)) - 1))
declarations.docstring: **Erdős #257 family, factorial instance at base 2.** The literal series `∑ 1 / (2^(n!) - 1)` over the infinite support `A = {n! : n ≥ 1}` is irrational. This is an instance of the statement family of Erdős problem #257 (which asks the same for *every* infinite support); the universal problem remains open and is not claimed.

declarations.name: erdos257_family_two_pow_instance
declarations.signature: theorem erdos257_family_two_pow_instance : Irrational (∑' k, (1 : ℝ) / ((2 : ℝ) ^ (2 ^ k) - 1))
declarations.docstring: **Erdős #257 family, geometric instance at base 2.** The literal series `∑ 1 / (2^(2^n) - 1)` over the infinite support `A = {2^n : n ≥ 0}` is irrational. An instance of the #257 statement family; the universal problem remains open and is not claimed.

declarations.name: irrational_of_int_mul_near_int
declarations.signature: theorem irrational_of_int_mul_near_int {ξ : ℝ} (h : ∀ q : ℕ, 0 < q → ∃ m z : ℤ, 0 < |(m : ℝ) * ξ - (z : ℝ)| ∧ |(m : ℝ) * ξ - (z : ℝ)| < 1 / (q : ℝ)) : Irrational ξ
declarations.docstring: **Second irrationality engine: integer-dilation near-integer criterion.** If for every positive `q` some integer dilation `m * ξ` lies within `1 / q` of an integer without being one, then `ξ` is irrational. For rational `ξ` with reduced denominator `d`, any `m * ξ` that is not an integer sits at distance at least `1 / d` from every integer, so the witnesses force the reduced denominator past every bound. This is the classical criterion shape behind digit/carry irrationality proofs (Erdős 1948).

declarations.name: irrational_of_pow_mul_near_int
declarations.signature: theorem irrational_of_pow_mul_near_int (b : ℕ) {ξ : ℝ} (h : ∀ q : ℕ, 0 < q → ∃ (n : ℕ) (z : ℤ), 0 < |(b : ℝ) ^ n * ξ - (z : ℝ)| ∧ |(b : ℝ) ^ n * ξ - (z : ℝ)| < 1 / (q : ℝ)) : Irrational ξ
declarations.docstring: Base-power specialisation of the near-integer criterion: witnesses of the form `b ^ n * ξ` suffice. This is the interface a digit/carry construction in base `b` feeds.

declarations.name: summable_divisor_count_term
declarations.signature: theorem summable_divisor_count_term (b : ℕ) (hb : 2 ≤ b) : Summable (fun m : ℕ => (((m + 1).divisors.card : ℝ)) / (b : ℝ) ^ (m + 1))
declarations.docstring: Summability of the divisor-count series `∑ τ(m+1) / b^(m+1)`: `τ(n) ≤ n` and `∑ n · r^n` converges geometrically.

declarations.name: erdosSum_full_support_eq_tsum_divisor_count
declarations.signature: theorem erdosSum_full_support_eq_tsum_divisor_count (b : ℕ) (hb : 2 ≤ b) : ∑' k : ℕ, (1 : ℝ) / ((b : ℝ) ^ (k + 1) - 1) = ∑' m : ℕ, (((m + 1).divisors.card : ℝ)) / (b : ℝ) ^ (m + 1)
declarations.docstring: **Full-support Lambert bridge.** For every base `b ≥ 2`, the full-support series `∑_{n≥1} 1 / (b^n - 1)` equals the divisor-count series `∑_{m≥1} τ(m) / b^m`. This is the classical Lambert-series identity (Mathlib's `tsum_pow_div_one_sub_eq_tsum_sigma` at `k = 0`, `r = 1/b`), restated in the kernel's own series vocabulary: the left side is the `a k = k + 1` (full-support) member of the Erdős #257 statement family — the Erdős–Borwein shape.

declarations.name: erdosBorwein_constant_lambert_identity
declarations.signature: theorem erdosBorwein_constant_lambert_identity : ∑' k : ℕ, (1 : ℝ) / ((2 : ℝ) ^ (k + 1) - 1) = ∑' m : ℕ, (((m + 1).divisors.card : ℝ)) / (2 : ℝ) ^ (m + 1)
declarations.docstring: **Erdős–Borwein constant, Lambert form.** The literal base-2 full-support series `∑_{n≥1} 1 / (2^n - 1)` — the Erdős–Borwein constant, the `A = ℕ` member of the Erdős #257 statement family — equals the divisor-count series `∑_{m≥1} τ(m) / 2^m`.

declarations.name: irrational_erdosSum_full_support_of_near_int
declarations.signature: theorem irrational_erdosSum_full_support_of_near_int (b : ℕ) (hb : 2 ≤ b) (h : ∀ q : ℕ, 0 < q → ∃ (n : ℕ) (z : ℤ), 0 < |(b : ℝ) ^ n * (∑' m : ℕ, (((m + 1).divisors.card : ℝ)) / (b : ℝ) ^ (m + 1)) - (z : ℝ)| ∧ |(b : ℝ) ^ n * (∑' m : ℕ, (((m + 1).divisors.card : ℝ)) / (b : ℝ) ^ (m + 1)) - (z : ℝ)| < 1 / (q : ℝ)) : Irrational (∑' k : ℕ, (1 : ℝ) / ((b : ℝ) ^ (k + 1) - 1))
declarations.docstring: **Reduction of full-support (Erdős–Borwein) irrationality to near-integer witnesses.** For every base `b ≥ 2`: if the divisor-count series `∑ τ(m) / b^m` admits, for every `q`, a base-power dilation lying within `1/q` of an integer without being one, then the full-support series `∑_{n≥1} 1 / (b^n - 1)` is irrational. The hypothesis is exactly the obligation Erdős's 1948 congruence construction discharges; producing those witnesses in Lean is the named remaining step of the full-support lane, and is *not* claimed here.

declarations.name: lcm_gap_hypothesis_fails_full_support
declarations.signature: theorem lcm_gap_hypothesis_fails_full_support : ¬ Tendsto (fun k => (k + 1) - ((Finset.range k).image (fun i => i + 1)).lcm id) atTop atTop
declarations.docstring: **Machine-checked boundary: the lcm-gap criterion cannot fire on full support.** For the full support `a k = k + 1`, the prefix lcm `lcm {1, …, k}` contains the coprime pair `k - 1, k`, hence is at least `(k - 1) * k ≥ k + 2` for `k ≥ 3` — so the (truncated) gap `(k + 1) - lcm {1, …, k}` is eventually `0` and does not tend to infinity. The hypothesis of `irrational_erdosSum_of_lcm_gap` therefore fails at full support: reaching the Erdős–Borwein case needs a different engine, which is what the near-integer criterion above provides the interface for.

declarations.name: summable_divisor_count_shift_tail
declarations.signature: theorem summable_divisor_count_shift_tail (b N : ℕ) (hb : 2 ≤ b) : Summable (fun r : ℕ => (((N + r + 1).divisors.card : ℝ)) / (b : ℝ) ^ (r + 1))
declarations.docstring: Summability of the shifted divisor-count tail `∑_r τ(N+r+1) / b^(r+1)`: the `b^N`-dilated remainder series of the divisor-count series past index `N`.

declarations.name: bpow_mul_divisor_count_series_eq_int_add_tail
declarations.signature: theorem bpow_mul_divisor_count_series_eq_int_add_tail (b N : ℕ) (hb : 2 ≤ b) : ∃ z : ℤ, (b : ℝ) ^ N * (∑' m : ℕ, (((m + 1).divisors.card : ℝ)) / (b : ℝ) ^ (m + 1)) = (z : ℝ) + ∑' r : ℕ, (((N + r + 1).divisors.card : ℝ)) / (b : ℝ) ^ (r + 1)
declarations.docstring: **Shifted-tail decomposition.** Dilating the divisor-count series by `b^N` splits it into an integer (the first `N` terms clear their denominators) plus the shifted tail `∑_r τ(N+r+1) / b^(r+1)`. This is the base-`b` carry skeleton behind Erdős's 1948 argument.

declarations.name: summable_const_add_mul_geometric
declarations.signature: theorem summable_const_add_mul_geometric (C : ℕ) {x : ℝ} (hx0 : 0 ≤ x) (hx1 : x < 1) : Summable (fun t : ℕ => ((C : ℝ) + (t : ℝ)) * x ^ t)
declarations.docstring: Summability of `(C + t) · x^t` for `0 ≤ x < 1`: a constant plus linear coefficient against a geometric envelope.

declarations.name: tsum_const_add_mul_geometric_le
declarations.signature: theorem tsum_const_add_mul_geometric_le (C : ℕ) {x : ℝ} (hx0 : 0 ≤ x) (hx2 : x ≤ 1 / 2) : ∑' t : ℕ, ((C : ℝ) + (t : ℝ)) * x ^ t ≤ 2 * (C : ℝ) + 2
declarations.docstring: Geometric envelope bound: for `0 ≤ x ≤ 1/2`, `∑_t (C + t) · x^t ≤ 2C + 2` (equality at `x = 1/2`). The closed forms are `C/(1-x)` and `x/(1-x)^2`, each at most `2C` and `2` on this range.

declarations.name: divisor_count_far_tail_le
declarations.signature: theorem divisor_count_far_tail_le (b N L : ℕ) (hb : 2 ≤ b) : ∑' t : ℕ, (((N + L + 1 + t).divisors.card : ℝ)) / (b : ℝ) ^ (L + 1 + t) ≤ ((N + L + 2 : ℕ) : ℝ) / (b : ℝ) ^ L
declarations.docstring: **Crude far-tail bound.** Past index `L`, bounding `τ(n) ≤ n` and summing the linear-times-geometric envelope gives `∑_t τ(N+L+1+t) / b^(L+1+t) ≤ (N+L+2) / b^L` for every base `b ≥ 2`. This is the certificate's third window: it needs no divisor information at all.

declarations.name: bpow_dvd_card_divisors_of_exact_prime_block
declarations.signature: theorem bpow_dvd_card_divisors_of_exact_prime_block (b n : ℕ) (hb : 2 ≤ b) (hn : n ≠ 0) (P : Finset ℕ) (hexact : ∀ p ∈ P, n.factorization p = b - 1) : b ^ P.card ∣ n.divisors.card
declarations.docstring: **Multiplicativity hinge for the first block.** If `P` is a set of primes at which `n` has exact valuation `b - 1`, then `b ^ |P|` divides `τ(n)`: each such prime contributes a factor `b` to `τ(n) = ∏_p (v_p(n) + 1)`. This is how Erdős's 1948 congruence construction forces the first-block divisibility — many small exact valuations, never one huge one.

declarations.name: near_int_witness_of_divisor_block_certificate
declarations.signature: theorem near_int_witness_of_divisor_block_certificate (b q N K L B : ℕ) (hb : 2 ≤ b) (hq : 0 < q) (hKL : K ≤ L) (hblock : ∀ r ∈ Finset.Icc 1 K, b ^ r ∣ (N + r).divisors.card) (hmiddle : ∀ r ∈ Finset.Icc (K + 1) L, (N + r).divisors.card ≤ B) (harith : q * (B * b ^ (L - K) + (N + L + 2)) < b ^ L) : ∃ z : ℤ, 0 < |(b : ℝ) ^ N * (∑' m : ℕ, (((m + 1).divisors.card : ℝ)) / (b : ℝ) ^ (m + 1)) - (z : ℝ)| ∧ |(b : ℝ) ^ N * (∑' m : ℕ, (((m + 1).divisors.card : ℝ)) / (b : ℝ) ^ (m + 1)) - (z : ℝ)| < 1 / (q : ℝ)
declarations.docstring: **Certificate consumer.** A divisor block certificate `(N, K, L, B)` for precision `q` — first-block divisibility, middle window bounded by `B`, and the ℕ-arithmetic inequality — yields a near-integer witness for the `b^N`-dilated divisor-count series: the first block lands in ℤ, the middle window contributes at most `B / b^K`, the far tail at most `(N+L+2) / b^L`, and the remainder is strictly positive because every divisor count is.

declarations.name: irrational_erdosSum_full_support_of_block_certificates
declarations.signature: theorem irrational_erdosSum_full_support_of_block_certificates (b : ℕ) (hb : 2 ≤ b) (hcert : ∀ q : ℕ, 0 < q → ∃ N K L B : ℕ, K ≤ L ∧ (∀ r ∈ Finset.Icc 1 K, b ^ r ∣ (N + r).divisors.card) ∧ (∀ r ∈ Finset.Icc (K + 1) L, (N + r).divisors.card ≤ B) ∧ q * (B * b ^ (L - K) + (N + L + 2)) < b ^ L) : Irrational (∑' k : ℕ, (1 : ℝ) / ((b : ℝ) ^ (k + 1) - 1))
declarations.docstring: **Full-support (Erdős–Borwein) irrationality from block certificates.** If for every precision `q` some divisor block certificate `(N, K, L, B)` exists, then the full-support series `∑_{n≥1} 1 / (b^n - 1)` is irrational. This composes the certificate consumer with the landed near-integer reduction: the remaining open obligation of the full-support lane is now the purely finite arithmetic statement that certificates exist for every `q` — which is what Erdős's 1948 congruence construction produces, and is NOT claimed here.

declarations.name: geom_sum_range_le_pow
declarations.signature: theorem geom_sum_range_le_pow (b m : ℕ) (hb : 2 ≤ b) : ∑ j ∈ Finset.range m, b ^ j ≤ b ^ m
declarations.docstring: **Full-support (Erdős–Borwein) irrationality from block certificates.** If for every precision `q` some divisor block certificate `(N, K, L, B)` exists, then the full-support series `∑_{n≥1} 1 / (b^n - 1)` is irrational. This composes the certificate consumer with the landed near-integer reduction: the remaining open obligation of the full-support lane is now the purely finite arithmetic statement that certificates exist for every `q` — which is what Erdős's 1948 congruence construction produces, and is NOT claimed here. -/ theorem irrational_erdosSum_full_support_of_block_certificates (b : ℕ) (hb : 2 ≤ b) (hcert : ∀ q : ℕ, 0 < q → ∃ N K L B : ℕ, K ≤ L ∧ (∀ r ∈ Finset.Icc 1 K, b ^ r ∣ (N + r).divisors.card) ∧ (∀ r ∈ Finset.Icc (K + 1) L, (N + r).divisors.card ≤ B) ∧ q * (B * b ^ (L - K) + (N + L + 2)) < b ^ L) : Irrational (∑' k : ℕ, (1 : ℝ) / ((b : ℝ) ^ (k + 1) - 1)) := by refine irrational_erdosSum_full_support_of_near_int b hb fun q hq => ?_ obtain ⟨N, K, L, B, hKL, hblock, hmiddle,

declarations.name: weighted_middle_bound_of_pointwise
declarations.signature: theorem weighted_middle_bound_of_pointwise (b N K L B : ℕ) (hb : 2 ≤ b) (hmiddle : ∀ r ∈ Finset.Icc (K + 1) L, (N + r).divisors.card ≤ B) : ∑ r ∈ Finset.Icc (K + 1) L, (N + r).divisors.card * b ^ (L - r) ≤ B * b ^ (L - K)
declarations.docstring: **Weighted middle bound from the pointwise one.** A uniform bound `B` on the middle window gives `∑ τ(N+r)·b^(L-r) ≤ B·b^(L-K)`: the weighted interface is never worse than the pointwise interface.

declarations.name: weighted_block_certificate_of_pointwise
declarations.signature: theorem weighted_block_certificate_of_pointwise (b q N K L B : ℕ) (hb : 2 ≤ b) (hmiddle : ∀ r ∈ Finset.Icc (K + 1) L, (N + r).divisors.card ≤ B) (harith : q * (B * b ^ (L - K) + (N + L + 2)) < b ^ L) : ∃ C : ℕ, (∑ r ∈ Finset.Icc (K + 1) L, (N + r).divisors.card * b ^ (L - r) ≤ C) ∧ q * (C + (N + L + 2)) < b ^ L
declarations.docstring: **Weighted certificates subsume pointwise certificates.** Classical `(N, K, L, B)` divisor block certificate data yields weighted-middle certificate data with `C = B·b^(L-K)` under the same arithmetic inequality.

declarations.name: near_int_witness_of_weighted_divisor_block_certificate
declarations.signature: theorem near_int_witness_of_weighted_divisor_block_certificate (b q N K L C : ℕ) (hb : 2 ≤ b) (hq : 0 < q) (hKL : K ≤ L) (hblock : ∀ r ∈ Finset.Icc 1 K, b ^ r ∣ (N + r).divisors.card) (hmiddle : ∑ r ∈ Finset.Icc (K + 1) L, (N + r).divisors.card * b ^ (L - r) ≤ C) (harith : q * (C + (N + L + 2)) < b ^ L) : ∃ z : ℤ, 0 < |(b : ℝ) ^ N * (∑' m : ℕ, (((m + 1).divisors.card : ℝ)) / (b : ℝ) ^ (m + 1)) - (z : ℝ)| ∧ |(b : ℝ) ^ N * (∑' m : ℕ, (((m + 1).divisors.card : ℝ)) / (b : ℝ) ^ (m + 1)) - (z : ℝ)| < 1 / (q : ℝ)
declarations.docstring: **Weighted certificate consumer.** A weighted divisor block certificate `(N, K, L, C)` for precision `q` — first-block divisibility, the exact weighted middle sum `∑_{r=K+1}^{L} τ(N+r)·b^(L-r) ≤ C`, and the ℕ-arithmetic inequality `q·(C + (N+L+2)) < b^L` — yields a near-integer witness for the `b^N`-dilated divisor-count series. The middle window is paid at its exact weighted value rather than through a pointwise bound: this is the certificate shape an averaging argument naturally produces, and it subsumes the pointwise interface via `weighted_block_certificate_of_pointwise`.

declarations.name: irrational_erdosSum_full_support_of_weighted_block_certificates
declarations.signature: theorem irrational_erdosSum_full_support_of_weighted_block_certificates (b : ℕ) (hb : 2 ≤ b) (hcert : ∀ q : ℕ, 0 < q → ∃ N K L C : ℕ, K ≤ L ∧ (∀ r ∈ Finset.Icc 1 K, b ^ r ∣ (N + r).divisors.card) ∧ (∑ r ∈ Finset.Icc (K + 1) L, (N + r).divisors.card * b ^ (L - r) ≤ C) ∧ q * (C + (N + L + 2)) < b ^ L) : Irrational (∑' k : ℕ, (1 : ℝ) / ((b : ℝ) ^ (k + 1) - 1))
declarations.docstring: **Full-support (Erdős–Borwein) irrationality from weighted block certificates.** If for every precision `q` some weighted divisor block certificate `(N, K, L, C)` exists, the full-support series `∑_{n≥1} 1 / (b^n - 1)` is irrational. The weighted middle hypothesis is implied by the pointwise one (`weighted_block_certificate_of_pointwise`), so this closure is at least as strong as the landed pointwise closure; certificate existence itself is NOT claimed.

declarations.name: factorization_eq_of_modEq_prod_pow
declarations.signature: theorem factorization_eq_of_modEq_prod_pow (b : ℕ) (hb : 2 ≤ b) (P : Finset ℕ) (hP : ∀ p ∈ P, Nat.Prime p) (hπ : 2 ≤ ∏ p ∈ P, p) (n : ℕ) (hmod : n ≡ (∏ p ∈ P, p) ^ (b - 1) [MOD (∏ p ∈ P, p) ^ b]) : ∀ p ∈ P, n.factorization p = b - 1
declarations.docstring: **Exact valuation from a product congruence.** If `n ≡ π^(b-1) [MOD π^b]` where `π = ∏ P` is a product of distinct primes, then every `p ∈ P` has exact valuation `b - 1` in `n`: writing `n = π^b·t + π^(b-1) = π^(b-1)·(π·t + 1)`, the cofactor is prime to `p`.

declarations.name: exists_prime_finset_card_gt
declarations.signature: theorem exists_prime_finset_card_gt (r M : ℕ) : ∃ P : Finset ℕ, P.card = r ∧ (∀ p ∈ P, Nat.Prime p) ∧ ∀ p ∈ P, M < p

declarations.name: exists_exact_valuation_progression
declarations.signature: theorem exists_exact_valuation_progression (b L : ℕ) (hb : 2 ≤ b) (K : ℕ) : ∃ x A : ℕ, 0 < A ∧ ∀ r ∈ Finset.Icc 1 K, ∃ P : Finset ℕ, P.card = r ∧ (∀ p ∈ P, Nat.Prime p) ∧ (∀ p ∈ P, L < p) ∧ ∀ y : ℕ, ∀ p ∈ P, (x + y * A + r).factorization p = b - 1
declarations.docstring: **CRT block construction (exact-valuation form).** For every window length `K` there are `x` and a positive modulus `A` such that along the whole progression `x + y·A`, every slot `r ∈ [1, K]` carries a set of `r` distinct primes, all above the prescribed bound `L`, at which `x + y·A + r` has exact valuation `b - 1`. Blocks are added one at a time: each new slot imposes a single congruence `x + r ≡ π^(b-1) [MOD π^b]` with `π` the product of a fresh prime block, glued to the accumulated modulus by binary CRT.

declarations.name: exists_first_block_divisibility_progression
declarations.signature: theorem exists_first_block_divisibility_progression (b K L : ℕ) (hb : 2 ≤ b) : ∃ x A : ℕ, 0 < A ∧ ∀ y : ℕ, ∀ r ∈ Finset.Icc 1 K, b ^ r ∣ (x + y * A + r).divisors.card
declarations.docstring: **First-block divisibility along a full arithmetic progression.** For every base `b ≥ 2`, window length `K`, and prime floor `L`, there are `x` and a positive modulus `A` with `b^r ∣ τ(x + y·A + r)` for every `y` and every `r ∈ [1, K]` — the first-block half of the divisor block certificate obligation, produced by CRT from exact prime valuations via the multiplicativity hinge, with every construction prime above `L`.

declarations.name: exists_exact_valuation_progression_with_support
declarations.signature: theorem exists_exact_valuation_progression_with_support (b L : ℕ) (hb : 2 ≤ b) (K : ℕ) : ∃ x A : ℕ, 0 < A ∧ (∀ p : ℕ, Nat.Prime p → p ∣ A → L < p ∧ ∃ r ∈ Finset.Icc 1 K, p ∣ x + r) ∧ ∀ r ∈ Finset.Icc 1 K, ∃ P : Finset ℕ, P.card = r ∧ (∀ p ∈ P, Nat.Prime p) ∧ (∀ p ∈ P, L < p) ∧ ∀ y : ℕ, ∀ p ∈ P, (x + y * A + r).factorization p = b - 1
declarations.docstring: **CRT block construction with exposed modulus support.** The same construction as `exists_exact_valuation_progression`, additionally exposing the arithmetic of the modulus: every prime divisor of `A` lies above `L` and divides `x + r` for some first-block slot `r ∈ [1, K]`. This is the frame fact the middle-window analysis consumes — first-block primes cannot touch the middle window.

declarations.name: coprime_middle_of_first_block_support
declarations.signature: theorem coprime_middle_of_first_block_support (x A K L s : ℕ) (hsupp : ∀ p : ℕ, Nat.Prime p → p ∣ A → L < p ∧ ∃ r ∈ Finset.Icc 1 K, p ∣ x + r) (hs1 : K < s) (hs2 : s ≤ L) : Nat.Coprime A (x + s)
declarations.docstring: **Middle-window coprimality from modulus support.** If every prime of `A` lies above `L` and divides `x + r` for some `r ∈ [1, K]`, then `A` is coprime to every middle-window shift `x + s` with `K < s ≤ L`: a common prime would divide `s - r`, which is positive and below `L < p`. This is the free lemma the CRT frame buys — the construction primes force the first block and then vanish from the middle window.

declarations.name: card_filter_dvd_progression_le
declarations.signature: theorem card_filter_dvd_progression_le (A c d Y : ℕ) (hd : 0 < d) (hcop : Nat.Coprime d A) : ((Finset.range Y).filter (fun y => d ∣ A * y + c)).card ≤ Y / d + 1
declarations.docstring: **Single-residue-class divisor counting along a progression.** A modulus `d` coprime to the step `A` divides `A·y + c` for at most `Y/d + 1` of the window values `y < Y`: any two solutions differ by a multiple of `d`. This is the exact-arithmetic engine behind the divisor-sum average — no analytic number theory enters.

declarations.name: card_divisors_le_two_mul_card_small_divisors
declarations.signature: theorem card_divisors_le_two_mul_card_small_divisors (n X : ℕ) (hnX : n ≤ X) : n.divisors.card ≤ 2 * ((Finset.Icc 1 (Nat.sqrt X)).filter (fun d => d ∣ n)).card
declarations.docstring: **Divisor pairing against a square bound.** For `n ≤ X`, the divisor count of `n` is at most twice the number of divisors of `n` up to `√X`: divisors pair as `d·(n/d) = n`, and both partners cannot exceed `√X`.

declarations.name: sum_card_divisors_progression_le
declarations.signature: theorem sum_card_divisors_progression_le (A c Y X : ℕ) (hcop : Nat.Coprime A c) (hX : ∀ y ∈ Finset.range Y, A * y + c ≤ X) : ∑ y ∈ Finset.range Y, (A * y + c).divisors.card ≤ 2 * ∑ d ∈ Finset.Icc 1 (Nat.sqrt X), (Y / d + 1)
declarations.docstring: **Elementary divisor-sum average along a coprime progression.** With `gcd(A, c) = 1` and all window values bounded by `X`, the divisor counts of `A·y + c` over `y < Y` sum to at most `2·∑_{d ≤ √X} (Y/d + 1)`: divisor pairing plus single-residue-class counting. PNT-free.

declarations.name: sum_pow_weight_middle_window_le
declarations.signature: theorem sum_pow_weight_middle_window_le (b K L : ℕ) (hb : 2 ≤ b) : ∑ s ∈ Finset.Icc (K + 1) L, b ^ (L - s) ≤ b ^ (L - K)
declarations.docstring: Geometric weight of the middle window: `∑_{s=K+1}^{L} b^(L-s) ≤ b^(L-K)`.

declarations.name: sum_weighted_middle_window_le
declarations.signature: theorem sum_weighted_middle_window_le (b x A K L Y X : ℕ) (hb : 2 ≤ b) (hsupp : ∀ p : ℕ, Nat.Prime p → p ∣ A → L < p ∧ ∃ r ∈ Finset.Icc 1 K, p ∣ x + r) (hX : ∀ y ∈ Finset.range Y, x + y * A + L ≤ X) : ∑ y ∈ Finset.range Y, ∑ s ∈ Finset.Icc (K + 1) L, (x + y * A + s).divisors.card * b ^ (L - s) ≤ b ^ (L - K) * (2 * ∑ d ∈ Finset.Icc 1 (Nat.sqrt X), (Y / d + 1))
declarations.docstring: **Weighted middle-window total along the progression.** Summing the weighted middle sums over the window `y < Y` costs at most `b^(L-K) · (2·∑_{d ≤ √X} (Y/d + 1))`: middle-window coprimality from the modulus support, then the elementary progression average, then the geometric weight.

declarations.name: exists_le_div_of_sum_le
declarations.signature: theorem exists_le_div_of_sum_le (Y T : ℕ) (hY : 0 < Y) (W : ℕ → ℕ) (h : ∑ y ∈ Finset.range Y, W y ≤ T) : ∃ y : ℕ, y < Y ∧ W y ≤ T / Y
declarations.docstring: **Pigeonhole selection.** If `Y` window values sum to at most `T`, some value is at most `T / Y`.

declarations.name: exists_first_block_progression_with_selected_weighted_middle
declarations.signature: theorem exists_first_block_progression_with_selected_weighted_middle (b K L Y : ℕ) (hb : 2 ≤ b) (hY : 0 < Y) : ∃ x A : ℕ, 0 < A ∧ (∀ y : ℕ, ∀ r ∈ Finset.Icc 1 K, b ^ r ∣ (x + y * A + r).divisors.card) ∧ ∃ y : ℕ, y < Y ∧ ∑ s ∈ Finset.Icc (K + 1) L, (x + y * A + s).divisors.card * b ^ (L - s) ≤ (b ^ (L - K) * (2 * ∑ d ∈ Finset.Icc 1 (Nat.sqrt (x + Y * A + L)), (Y / d + 1))) / Y
declarations.docstring: **First-block progression with a selected small weighted middle.** Unconditionally, for every base `b ≥ 2`, window shape `(K, L)`, and averaging range `Y > 0`, the CRT construction yields `x` and `A > 0` with first-block divisibility along the whole progression, together with some `y < Y` whose weighted middle sum is bounded by the elementary divisor-pair average `(b^(L-K) · 2·∑_{d ≤ √(x+Y·A+L)} (Y/d + 1)) / Y`. This is the middle-window selection half of the Erdős 1948 route in certificate shape; what remains for full certificate existence is explicit size control of `x` and `A` (and hence parameter closure in `q`).

declarations.name: param_linear_le_two_pow
declarations.signature: theorem param_linear_le_two_pow (c e : ℕ) : c * (3 * c + e) + e ≤ 2 ^ (3 * c + e)
declarations.docstring: **Parameter closure kernel.** The linear function `c·κ + e` is dominated by `2^κ` at `κ = 3c + e`: cube `c + 1 ≤ 2^c` and absorb the slack.

declarations.name: harmonic_div_sum_core
declarations.signature: theorem harmonic_div_sum_core (J : ℕ) : ∀ S Y : ℕ, S ≤ 2 ^ J → ∑ d ∈ Finset.Icc 1 S, Y / d ≤ Y * (J + 1)
declarations.docstring: **Dyadic harmonic core.** `∑_{d=1}^{S} ⌊Y/d⌋ ≤ Y·(J+1)` whenever `S ≤ 2^J`: each dyadic bucket `(2^j, 2^{j+1}]` contributes at most `Y`.

declarations.name: harmonic_div_sum_le
declarations.signature: theorem harmonic_div_sum_le (S Y J : ℕ) (hS : S ≤ 2 ^ J) : ∑ d ∈ Finset.Icc 1 S, (Y / d + 1) ≤ Y * (J + 1) + S
declarations.docstring: **Dyadic harmonic compression.** `∑_{d=1}^{S} (⌊Y/d⌋ + 1) ≤ Y·(J+1) + S` for `S ≤ 2^J` — the closed form that turns the raw progression divisor average into a parameter-usable middle bound.

declarations.name: exists_bertrand_prime_block
declarations.signature: theorem exists_bertrand_prime_block (m r : ℕ) (hm : 1 ≤ m) : ∃ Q : Finset ℕ, Q.card = r ∧ (∀ p ∈ Q, Nat.Prime p) ∧ (∀ p ∈ Q, m < p) ∧ (∀ p ∈ Q, p ≤ 2 ^ r * m)
declarations.docstring: **Bertrand prime block.** `r` distinct primes in `(m, 2^r·m]`, produced by chaining Bertrand's postulate — bounded fresh prime supply with no prime-counting input.

declarations.name: exists_bounded_disjoint_prime_blocks
declarations.signature: theorem exists_bounded_disjoint_prime_blocks (L K : ℕ) : ∃ (P : ℕ → Finset ℕ) (m : ℕ), L + 1 ≤ m ∧ m ≤ 2 ^ (K * (K + 1)) * (L + 1) ∧ (∀ r ∈ Finset.Icc 1 K, (P r).card = r) ∧ (∀ r ∈ Finset.Icc 1 K, ∀ p ∈ P r, Nat.Prime p) ∧ (∀ r ∈ Finset.Icc 1 K, ∀ p ∈ P r, L < p) ∧ (∀ r ∈ Finset.Icc 1 K, ∀ p ∈ P r, p ≤ m) ∧ (∀ r s, r ∈ Finset.Icc 1 K → s ∈ Finset.Icc 1 K → r ≠ s → Disjoint (P r) (P s))
declarations.docstring: **Bounded disjoint prime blocks.** A triangular family of pairwise disjoint prime blocks — block `r` has `r` primes — all in `(L, m]` with the explicit global ceiling `m ≤ 2^(K(K+1))·(L+1)`. Disjointness comes from stacking Bertrand intervals, never from primes exceeding an accumulated modulus.

declarations.name: exists_bounded_crt_residue
declarations.signature: theorem exists_bounded_crt_residue (K : ℕ) (m c : ℕ → ℕ) (hpos : ∀ r ∈ Finset.Icc 1 K, 0 < m r) (hcop : ∀ r s, r ∈ Finset.Icc 1 K → s ∈ Finset.Icc 1 K → r ≠ s → Nat.Coprime (m r) (m s)) : ∃ x : ℕ, x < ∏ r ∈ Finset.Icc 1 K, m r ∧ ∀ r ∈ Finset.Icc 1 K, x ≡ c r [MOD m r]
declarations.docstring: **Normalised simultaneous CRT.** Pairwise coprime moduli share a common residue *below the product of the moduli* — the height-aware form: old congruences transfer by divisibility, and the representative never leaves `[0, ∏ m_r)`.

declarations.name: exists_bounded_first_block_frame
declarations.signature: theorem exists_bounded_first_block_frame (b L K : ℕ) (hb : 2 ≤ b) (hK : 1 ≤ K) (hKL : K ≤ L) : ∃ x A : ℕ, 0 < A ∧ x < A ∧ L + 1 ≤ A ∧ A ≤ (2 ^ (K * (K + 1)) * (L + 1)) ^ (b * K ^ 2) ∧ (∀ p : ℕ, Nat.Prime p → p ∣ A → L < p ∧ ∃ r ∈ Finset.Icc 1 K, p ∣ x + r) ∧ (∀ y : ℕ, ∀ r ∈ Finset.Icc 1 K, b ^ r ∣ (x + y * A + r).divisors.card)
declarations.docstring: **Bounded first-block frame.** The height-aware replacement for the qualitative CRT supply: `x < A`, the modulus support is exposed (every prime of `A` is above `L` and meets the first block), first-block divisibility holds along the whole progression, and — decisively — `A` carries the explicit bound `(2^(K(K+1))·(L+1))^(b·K²)`. Disjoint Bertrand blocks replace "fresh prime above the accumulated modulus".

declarations.name: selected_weighted_middle_of_supported_frame
declarations.signature: theorem selected_weighted_middle_of_supported_frame (b x A K L Y : ℕ) (hb : 2 ≤ b) (hY : 0 < Y) (hsupp : ∀ p : ℕ, Nat.Prime p → p ∣ A → L < p ∧ ∃ r ∈ Finset.Icc 1 K, p ∣ x + r) : ∃ y : ℕ, y < Y ∧ ∑ s ∈ Finset.Icc (K + 1) L, (x + y * A + s).divisors.card * b ^ (L - s) ≤ (b ^ (L - K) * (2 * ∑ d ∈ Finset.Icc 1 (Nat.sqrt (x + Y * A + L)), (Y / d + 1))) / Y
declarations.docstring: **Selected weighted middle for an external supported frame.** The middle-window selection argument, freed from the internal CRT supply: any `x, A` with exposed support yield some `y < Y` whose weighted middle sum is bounded by the elementary divisor-pair average. This is the consumer the bounded frame feeds.

declarations.name: certificate_param_arithmetic
declarations.signature: theorem certificate_param_arithmetic (b q K κ E : ℕ) (hb : 2 ≤ b) (hq : 0 < q) (hK : K = 9 * (q + b + 100)) (hκ : κ = 3 * (2 * b * K ^ 2 + 1) + (2 * b * K ^ 2 * (K * (K + 1) + 1) + q + 4)) (hE : E = b * K ^ 2 * (K * (K + 1) + κ + 1)) : 4 * q * (E + κ + 5) ≤ b ^ K ∧ 2 * E + κ + q + 4 ≤ 2 ^ κ ∧ 1 ≤ K ∧ K ≤ 2 ^ κ
declarations.docstring: **Certificate parameter arithmetic.** For `K = 9(q+b+100)` and the explicit `κ`, `E` below: the middle cost `4q(E+κ+5)` fits under `b^K`, the far-tail exponent budget `2E+κ+q+4` fits under `2^κ`, and `K ≤ 2^κ`. Everything reduces to `n < 2^n` and exponent bookkeeping — no logarithms, no analytic input.

declarations.name: exists_weighted_block_certificates
declarations.signature: theorem exists_weighted_block_certificates (b : ℕ) (hb : 2 ≤ b) : ∀ q : ℕ, 0 < q → ∃ N K L C : ℕ, K ≤ L ∧ (∀ r ∈ Finset.Icc 1 K, b ^ r ∣ (N + r).divisors.card) ∧ (∑ r ∈ Finset.Icc (K + 1) L, (N + r).divisors.card * b ^ (L - r) ≤ C) ∧ q * (C + (N + L + 2)) < b ^ L
declarations.docstring: **Weighted divisor block certificates exist for every precision.** For every base `b ≥ 2` and every `q > 0`, explicit parameters `K = 9(q+b+100)`, `L = 2^κ` and the bounded Bertrand/CRT frame produce a weighted certificate `(N, K, L, C)`: first-block divisibility, weighted middle sum ≤ `C`, and `q·(C + N + L + 2) < b^L`. This discharges the certificate-existence hypothesis of the weighted closure — the divisor-average half and the size-control half of Erdős 1948, composed.

declarations.name: irrational_erdosSum_full_support
declarations.signature: theorem irrational_erdosSum_full_support (b : ℕ) (hb : 2 ≤ b) : Irrational (∑' k : ℕ, (1 : ℝ) / ((b : ℝ) ^ (k + 1) - 1))
declarations.docstring: **Full-support (Erdős–Borwein) irrationality, unconditional.** For every base `b ≥ 2` the series `∑_{n≥1} 1/(b^n - 1)` is irrational — Erdős (1948), through the certificate machine: bounded Bertrand/CRT first-block frame, middle-window divisor-pair average with pigeonhole selection, and explicit parameter closure.

declarations.name: irrational_erdosBorwein_series
declarations.signature: theorem irrational_erdosBorwein_series : Irrational (∑' k : ℕ, (1 : ℝ) / ((2 : ℝ) ^ (k + 1) - 1))
declarations.docstring: **The Erdős–Borwein constant is irrational.** Base-2 instance of the full-support theorem: `∑_{n≥1} 1/(2^n - 1)` is irrational.

declarations.name: summable_coeff_term
declarations.signature: theorem summable_coeff_term (b : ℕ) (c : ℕ → ℕ) (hb : 2 ≤ b) (hgrowth : ∀ m : ℕ, c m ≤ m) : Summable (fun m : ℕ => ((c (m + 1) : ℝ)) / (b : ℝ) ^ (m + 1))
declarations.docstring: Summability of a coefficient series `∑ c(m+1) / b^(m+1)` under the linear growth envelope `c m ≤ m`: comparison against `∑ m · (1/b)^m`.

declarations.name: summable_coeff_shift_tail
declarations.signature: theorem summable_coeff_shift_tail (b N : ℕ) (c : ℕ → ℕ) (hb : 2 ≤ b) (hgrowth : ∀ m : ℕ, c m ≤ m) : Summable (fun r : ℕ => ((c (N + r + 1) : ℝ)) / (b : ℝ) ^ (r + 1))
declarations.docstring: Summability of the shifted coefficient tail `∑_r c(N+r+1) / b^(r+1)` under the growth envelope.

declarations.name: bpow_mul_coeff_series_eq_int_add_tail
declarations.signature: theorem bpow_mul_coeff_series_eq_int_add_tail (b N : ℕ) (c : ℕ → ℕ) (hb : 2 ≤ b) (hgrowth : ∀ m : ℕ, c m ≤ m) : ∃ z : ℤ, (b : ℝ) ^ N * (∑' m : ℕ, ((c (m + 1) : ℝ)) / (b : ℝ) ^ (m + 1)) = (z : ℝ) + ∑' r : ℕ, ((c (N + r + 1) : ℝ)) / (b : ℝ) ^ (r + 1)
declarations.docstring: **Generic shifted-tail decomposition.** Dilating a coefficient series by `b^N` splits it into an integer plus the shifted tail — the base-`b` carry skeleton, for an arbitrary coefficient sequence.

declarations.name: coeff_far_tail_le
declarations.signature: theorem coeff_far_tail_le (b N L : ℕ) (c : ℕ → ℕ) (hb : 2 ≤ b) (hgrowth : ∀ m : ℕ, c m ≤ m) : ∑' t : ℕ, ((c (N + L + 1 + t) : ℝ)) / (b : ℝ) ^ (L + 1 + t) ≤ ((N + L + 2 : ℕ) : ℝ) / (b : ℝ) ^ L
declarations.docstring: **Generic crude far-tail bound.** Past index `L`, the growth envelope `c m ≤ m` and the linear-times-geometric sum give `∑_t c(N+L+1+t) / b^(L+1+t) ≤ (N+L+2) / b^L`.

declarations.name: near_int_witness_of_weighted_coeff_block_certificate
declarations.signature: theorem near_int_witness_of_weighted_coeff_block_certificate (b q N K L C : ℕ) (c : ℕ → ℕ) (hb : 2 ≤ b) (hq : 0 < q) (hKL : K ≤ L) (hgrowth : ∀ m : ℕ, c m ≤ m) (hblock : ∀ r ∈ Finset.Icc 1 K, b ^ r ∣ c (N + r)) (hmiddle : ∑ r ∈ Finset.Icc (K + 1) L, c (N + r) * b ^ (L - r) ≤ C) (hpos : ∃ t : ℕ, 0 < c (N + L + 1 + t)) (harith : q * (C + (N + L + 2)) < b ^ L) : ∃ z : ℤ, 0 < (b : ℝ) ^ N * (∑' m : ℕ, ((c (m + 1) : ℝ)) / (b : ℝ) ^ (m + 1)) - (z : ℝ) ∧ (b : ℝ) ^ N * (∑' m : ℕ, ((c (m + 1) : ℝ)) / (b : ℝ) ^ (m + 1)) - (z : ℝ) < 1 / (q : ℝ)
declarations.docstring: **Generic weighted-coefficient certificate consumer, one-sided.** A weighted block certificate `(N, K, L, C)` for an arbitrary coefficient sequence `c : ℕ → ℕ` with growth envelope `c m ≤ m` — first-block divisibility `b^r ∣ c(N+r)`, weighted middle sum ≤ `C`, a nonzero coefficient somewhere past the window, and the ℕ-arithmetic inequality — yields a *one-sided* near-integer witness: the `b^N`-dilated coefficient series exceeds an integer by strictly between `0` and `1/q`. This is the τ-free abstraction of `near_int_witness_of_weighted_divisor_block_certificate`; the one-sided form exposes the digit/carry content (the dilated series has a positive fractional part below `1/q`), which the absolute-value form hides.

declarations.name: irrational_coeff_series_of_weighted_coeff_block_certificates
declarations.signature: theorem irrational_coeff_series_of_weighted_coeff_block_certificates (b : ℕ) (c : ℕ → ℕ) (hb : 2 ≤ b) (hgrowth : ∀ m : ℕ, c m ≤ m) (hcert : ∀ q : ℕ, 0 < q → ∃ N K L C : ℕ, K ≤ L ∧ (∀ r ∈ Finset.Icc 1 K, b ^ r ∣ c (N + r)) ∧ (∑ r ∈ Finset.Icc (K + 1) L, c (N + r) * b ^ (L - r) ≤ C) ∧ (∃ t : ℕ, 0 < c (N + L + 1 + t)) ∧ q * (C + (N + L + 2)) < b ^ L) : Irrational (∑' m : ℕ, ((c (m + 1) : ℝ)) / (b : ℝ) ^ (m + 1))
declarations.docstring: **Generic coefficient-series irrationality from weighted certificates.** If an arbitrary coefficient sequence `c : ℕ → ℕ` with growth envelope `c m ≤ m` admits a weighted block certificate for every precision `q`, then `∑ c(m) / b^m` is irrational. The full-support theorem is the instance `c = τ`; arbitrary-support Erdős #257 is the instance `c = supportCoeff A`, whose certificate existence is NOT claimed here.

declarations.name: irrational_erdosSum_full_support_via_coeff_engine
declarations.signature: theorem irrational_erdosSum_full_support_via_coeff_engine (b : ℕ) (hb : 2 ≤ b) : Irrational (∑' k : ℕ, (1 : ℝ) / ((b : ℝ) ^ (k + 1) - 1))
declarations.docstring: **The coefficient engine subsumes the landed full-support theorem.** Re-derivation of `irrational_erdosSum_full_support` through the generic weighted-coefficient engine at `c = τ`: growth is `τ(m) ≤ m`, certificates are the landed `exists_weighted_block_certificates`, and tail positivity is `τ ≥ 1`. This machine-checks that the abstraction loses nothing.

declarations.name: near_int_witnesses_erdosSum_full_support
declarations.signature: theorem near_int_witnesses_erdosSum_full_support (b : ℕ) (hb : 2 ≤ b) : ∀ q : ℕ, 0 < q → ∃ (N : ℕ) (z : ℤ), 0 < (b : ℝ) ^ N * (∑' k : ℕ, (1 : ℝ) / ((b : ℝ) ^ (k + 1) - 1)) - (z : ℝ) ∧ (b : ℝ) ^ N * (∑' k : ℕ, (1 : ℝ) / ((b : ℝ) ^ (k + 1) - 1)) - (z : ℝ) < 1 / (q : ℝ)
declarations.docstring: **One-sided near-integer witnesses for the full-support series.** For every base `b ≥ 2` and every precision `q`, some `b^N`-dilation of `∑_{n≥1} 1/(b^n - 1)` exceeds an integer by strictly between `0` and `1/q`. This is the digit/carry content of Erdős's 1948 construction made explicit: the landed irrationality theorem is its corollary, but the witnesses themselves say more — the dilated series has arbitrarily small positive fractional part.

declarations.name: zero_block_witnesses_erdosSum_full_support
declarations.signature: theorem zero_block_witnesses_erdosSum_full_support (b : ℕ) (hb : 2 ≤ b) : ∀ K : ℕ, ∃ (N : ℕ) (z : ℤ), 0 < (b : ℝ) ^ N * (∑' k : ℕ, (1 : ℝ) / ((b : ℝ) ^ (k + 1) - 1)) - (z : ℝ) ∧ (b : ℝ) ^ N * (∑' k : ℕ, (1 : ℝ) / ((b : ℝ) ^ (k + 1) - 1)) - (z : ℝ) < 1 / (b : ℝ) ^ K
declarations.docstring: **Arbitrarily long zero blocks after the leading digit window.** For every base `b ≥ 2` and every window length `K`, some `b^N`-dilation of `∑_{n≥1} 1/(b^n - 1)` exceeds an integer by strictly between `0` and `b^(-K)` — so the base-`b` expansion of the full-support series contains arbitrarily long blocks of zero digits. Specialisation `q = b^K` of the one-sided witness family.

declarations.name: tsum_prod_weight_mul_pow_eq_tsum_divisor_weight_sum
declarations.signature: theorem tsum_prod_weight_mul_pow_eq_tsum_divisor_weight_sum (w : ℕ → ℝ) (hw0 : ∀ e : ℕ, 0 ≤ w e) (hw1 : ∀ e : ℕ, w e ≤ 1) {r : ℝ} (hr0 : 0 ≤ r) (hr1 : r < 1) : ∑' p : ℕ+ × ℕ+, w (p.1 : ℕ) * r ^ ((p.1 : ℕ) * (p.2 : ℕ)) = ∑' n : ℕ+, (∑ e ∈ (n : ℕ).divisors, w e) * r ^ (n : ℕ)
declarations.docstring: **Weighted product-to-divisor-sum rearrangement.** For a weight `w : ℕ → ℝ` with `0 ≤ w ≤ 1` and `0 ≤ r < 1`, the double series `∑_{d,m ≥ 1} w(d)·r^(dm)` regroups along divisor antidiagonals into `∑_{n ≥ 1} (∑_{e ∣ n} w(e))·r^n`. Weighted analogue of Mathlib's `tsum_prod_pow_eq_tsum_sigma`, with the weight on the divisor factor.

declarations.name: weighted_lambert_series_identity
declarations.signature: theorem weighted_lambert_series_identity (w : ℕ → ℝ) (hw0 : ∀ e : ℕ, 0 ≤ w e) (hw1 : ∀ e : ℕ, w e ≤ 1) {r : ℝ} (hr0 : 0 ≤ r) (hr1 : r < 1) : ∑' d : ℕ+, w (d : ℕ) * (r ^ (d : ℕ) / (1 - r ^ (d : ℕ))) = ∑' n : ℕ+, (∑ e ∈ (n : ℕ).divisors, w e) * r ^ (n : ℕ)
declarations.docstring: **Weighted Lambert series identity.** For a weight `w : ℕ → ℝ` with `0 ≤ w ≤ 1` and `0 ≤ r < 1`: `∑_{d≥1} w(d)·r^d/(1-r^d) = ∑_{n≥1} (∑_{e ∣ n} w(e))·r^n`. At `w ≡ 1` this is the classical `τ` Lambert identity; at `w = 1_A` it is the arbitrary-support transform of Erdős #257.

declarations.name: supportCoeff
declarations.signature: noncomputable def supportCoeff (A : Set ℕ) (n : ℕ) : ℕ
declarations.docstring: **The support coefficient** `f_A(n) = #{d ∣ n : d ∈ A}` — the Dirichlet incidence `1_A * 1` of a support set `A ⊆ ℕ`. This is the coefficient in which Erdős #257 is actually stated: `∑_{a∈A} 1/(b^a - 1) = ∑_n f_A(n)/b^n`. Full support gives `f_ℕ = τ`; primes give `ω`; prime powers give `Ω`.

declarations.name: supportCoeff_le_card_divisors
declarations.signature: theorem supportCoeff_le_card_divisors (A : Set ℕ) (n : ℕ) : supportCoeff A n ≤ n.divisors.card

declarations.name: supportCoeff_le_self
declarations.signature: theorem supportCoeff_le_self (A : Set ℕ) (n : ℕ) : supportCoeff A n ≤ n
declarations.docstring: Growth envelope for the support coefficient: `f_A(n) ≤ τ(n) ≤ n`. This is exactly the hypothesis the generic coefficient engine needs.

declarations.name: supportCoeff_univ_eq_card_divisors
declarations.signature: theorem supportCoeff_univ_eq_card_divisors (n : ℕ) : supportCoeff Set.univ n = n.divisors.card
declarations.docstring: **Full support recovers the divisor count**: `f_ℕ = τ`. The landed full-support theorem is the `A = Set.univ` instance of the support calculus.

declarations.name: supportCoeff_cast_eq_sum_indicator
declarations.signature: theorem supportCoeff_cast_eq_sum_indicator (A : Set ℕ) (n : ℕ) : ((supportCoeff A n : ℕ) : ℝ) = ∑ e ∈ n.divisors, Set.indicator A (fun _ => (1 : ℝ)) e
declarations.docstring: Cast bridge: the support coefficient is the divisor sum of the real indicator weight `1_A`.

declarations.name: erdosSupportSeries
declarations.signature: noncomputable def erdosSupportSeries (b : ℕ) (A : Set ℕ) : ℝ
declarations.docstring: **The Erdős #257 support series** `∑_{a ∈ A} 1/(b^a - 1)`, as an indicator series over ℕ. The `a = 0` term is `1/(1-1) = 0` under real division-by-zero conventions, so supports containing `0` contribute nothing spurious.

declarations.name: summable_erdosSupport_indicator
declarations.signature: theorem summable_erdosSupport_indicator (b : ℕ) (A : Set ℕ) (hb : 2 ≤ b) : Summable (fun a : ℕ => Set.indicator A (fun a => (1 : ℝ) / ((b : ℝ) ^ a - 1)) a)
declarations.docstring: Summability of the support-series terms, uniformly in `A`: dominated by the geometric envelope `2·(1/b)^a`.

declarations.name: erdosSupportSeries_univ_eq_erdosSum_full_support
declarations.signature: theorem erdosSupportSeries_univ_eq_erdosSum_full_support (b : ℕ) (hb : 2 ≤ b) : erdosSupportSeries b Set.univ = ∑' k : ℕ, (1 : ℝ) / ((b : ℝ) ^ (k + 1) - 1)
declarations.docstring: **Full support instance of the support series**: at `A = Set.univ` the support series is the full-support (Erdős–Borwein shape) series.

declarations.name: erdosSupportSeries_eq_tsum_supportCoeff
declarations.signature: theorem erdosSupportSeries_eq_tsum_supportCoeff (b : ℕ) (A : Set ℕ) (hb : 2 ≤ b) : erdosSupportSeries b A = ∑' m : ℕ, ((supportCoeff A (m + 1) : ℝ)) / (b : ℝ) ^ (m + 1)
declarations.docstring: **The arbitrary-support Lambert transform of Erdős #257.** For every base `b ≥ 2` and every support `A ⊆ ℕ`: `∑_{a ∈ A} 1/(b^a - 1) = ∑_{n ≥ 1} f_A(n)/b^n`, where `f_A = supportCoeff A` counts divisors lying in `A`. This moves the arbitrary-support problem into the coefficient language where the weighted certificate engine operates. Full support (`A = ℕ`, `f_A = τ`) recovers `erdosSum_full_support_eq_tsum_divisor_count`.

declarations.name: irrational_erdosSupportSeries_of_weighted_coeff_certificates
declarations.signature: theorem irrational_erdosSupportSeries_of_weighted_coeff_certificates (b : ℕ) (A : Set ℕ) (hb : 2 ≤ b) (hcert : ∀ q : ℕ, 0 < q → ∃ N K L C : ℕ, K ≤ L ∧ (∀ r ∈ Finset.Icc 1 K, b ^ r ∣ supportCoeff A (N + r)) ∧ (∑ r ∈ Finset.Icc (K + 1) L, supportCoeff A (N + r) * b ^ (L - r) ≤ C) ∧ (∃ t : ℕ, 0 < supportCoeff A (N + L + 1 + t)) ∧ q * (C + (N + L + 2)) < b ^ L) : Irrational (erdosSupportSeries b A)
declarations.docstring: **Arbitrary-support irrationality from weighted coefficient certificates.** If the support coefficient `f_A` admits a weighted block certificate for every precision, the support series `∑_{a ∈ A} 1/(b^a - 1)` is irrational. This is the engine Erdős #257 needs: the analytic content is reduced to certificate existence for `f_A`, which for arbitrary infinite `A` remains an OPEN obligation — this theorem does NOT resolve it.

declarations.name: irrational_erdosSupportSeries_univ
declarations.signature: theorem irrational_erdosSupportSeries_univ (b : ℕ) (hb : 2 ≤ b) : Irrational (erdosSupportSeries b Set.univ)
declarations.docstring: **Full support, in the support-series language**: the `A = Set.univ` member of the Erdős #257 statement family is irrational.

declarations.name: erdosSupportSeries_multiples_eq_pow_base_full_support
declarations.signature: theorem erdosSupportSeries_multiples_eq_pow_base_full_support (b d : ℕ) (hb : 2 ≤ b) (hd : 1 ≤ d) : erdosSupportSeries b {n : ℕ | d ∣ n} = ∑' k : ℕ, (1 : ℝ) / (((b : ℝ) ^ d) ^ (k + 1) - 1)
declarations.docstring: **Dilation identity**: the support series over the multiples support `A = dℕ` at base `b` is the full-support series at base `b^d`.

declarations.name: irrational_erdosSupportSeries_multiples
declarations.signature: theorem irrational_erdosSupportSeries_multiples (b d : ℕ) (hb : 2 ≤ b) (hd : 1 ≤ d) : Irrational (erdosSupportSeries b {n : ℕ | d ∣ n})
declarations.docstring: **New support classes of Erdős #257: every multiples support.** For every base `b ≥ 2` and every `d ≥ 1`, the support series over `A = dℕ` is irrational — at `b = 2` these are the `A = dℕ` members of Erdős #257 itself. Proof: dilation to the full-support theorem at base `b^d`.

declarations.name: irrational_erdosSum_multiples_support
declarations.signature: theorem irrational_erdosSum_multiples_support (b d : ℕ) (hb : 2 ≤ b) (hd : 1 ≤ d) : Irrational (∑' k : ℕ, (1 : ℝ) / ((b : ℝ) ^ (d * (k + 1)) - 1))
declarations.docstring: **Raw-series form of the multiples support classes**: for every `b ≥ 2` and `d ≥ 1`, `∑_{k≥1} 1/(b^(dk) - 1)` is irrational.

declarations.name: carry_block_dvd_of_digitwise_blocks
declarations.signature: theorem carry_block_dvd_of_digitwise_blocks (b N K : ℕ) (c : ℕ → ℕ) (hblock : ∀ r ∈ Finset.Icc 1 K, b ^ r ∣ c (N + r)) : b ^ K ∣ ∑ r ∈ Finset.Icc 1 K, c (N + r) * b ^ (K - r)
declarations.docstring: **Digitwise first-block divisibility implies carry-block divisibility**: each term `c(N+r)·b^(K-r)` is divisible by `b^r · b^(K-r) = b^K`, so the wave-8 digitwise certificate is a special case of the carry-aware certificate.

declarations.name: near_int_witness_of_weighted_coeff_carry_block_certificate
declarations.signature: theorem near_int_witness_of_weighted_coeff_carry_block_certificate (b q N K L C : ℕ) (c : ℕ → ℕ) (hb : 2 ≤ b) (hq : 0 < q) (hKL : K ≤ L) (hgrowth : ∀ m : ℕ, c m ≤ m) (hcarry : b ^ K ∣ ∑ r ∈ Finset.Icc 1 K, c (N + r) * b ^ (K - r)) (hmiddle : ∑ r ∈ Finset.Icc (K + 1) L, c (N + r) * b ^ (L - r) ≤ C) (hpos : ∃ t : ℕ, 0 < c (N + L + 1 + t)) (harith : q * (C + (N + L + 2)) < b ^ L) : ∃ z : ℤ, 0 < (b : ℝ) ^ N * (∑' m : ℕ, ((c (m + 1) : ℝ)) / (b : ℝ) ^ (m + 1)) - (z : ℝ) ∧ (b : ℝ) ^ N * (∑' m : ℕ, ((c (m + 1) : ℝ)) / (b : ℝ) ^ (m + 1)) - (z : ℝ) < 1 / (q : ℝ)
declarations.docstring: **Carry-aware generic certificate consumer, one-sided.** Like `near_int_witness_of_weighted_coeff_block_certificate`, but the first block is certified in aggregate: `b^K` divides the carry sum `∑_{r≤K} c(N+r)·b^(K-r)`. Individual digits need not vanish; only the carried block total must be integral. This is the certificate shape a sieve construction with residual first-block digits feeds.

declarations.name: irrational_coeff_series_of_weighted_coeff_carry_block_certificates
declarations.signature: theorem irrational_coeff_series_of_weighted_coeff_carry_block_certificates (b : ℕ) (c : ℕ → ℕ) (hb : 2 ≤ b) (hgrowth : ∀ m : ℕ, c m ≤ m) (hcert : ∀ q : ℕ, 0 < q → ∃ N K L C : ℕ, K ≤ L ∧ (b ^ K ∣ ∑ r ∈ Finset.Icc 1 K, c (N + r) * b ^ (K - r)) ∧ (∑ r ∈ Finset.Icc (K + 1) L, c (N + r) * b ^ (L - r) ≤ C) ∧ (∃ t : ℕ, 0 < c (N + L + 1 + t)) ∧ q * (C + (N + L + 2)) < b ^ L) : Irrational (∑' m : ℕ, ((c (m + 1) : ℝ)) / (b : ℝ) ^ (m + 1))
declarations.docstring: **Carry-aware coefficient-series irrationality.** If an arbitrary coefficient sequence with growth envelope `c m ≤ m` admits a carry-aware weighted certificate for every precision, `∑ c(m)/b^m` is irrational.

declarations.name: irrational_coeff_series_of_digitwise_via_carry
declarations.signature: theorem irrational_coeff_series_of_digitwise_via_carry (b : ℕ) (c : ℕ → ℕ) (hb : 2 ≤ b) (hgrowth : ∀ m : ℕ, c m ≤ m) (hcert : ∀ q : ℕ, 0 < q → ∃ N K L C : ℕ, K ≤ L ∧ (∀ r ∈ Finset.Icc 1 K, b ^ r ∣ c (N + r)) ∧ (∑ r ∈ Finset.Icc (K + 1) L, c (N + r) * b ^ (L - r) ≤ C) ∧ (∃ t : ℕ, 0 < c (N + L + 1 + t)) ∧ q * (C + (N + L + 2)) < b ^ L) : Irrational (∑' m : ℕ, ((c (m + 1) : ℝ)) / (b : ℝ) ^ (m + 1))
declarations.docstring: **The carry interface subsumes the digitwise interface**: the wave-8 digitwise closure, re-derived through the carry-aware engine. Machine proof that loosening the first block to aggregate integrality loses nothing.

declarations.name: irrational_erdosSupportSeries_of_weighted_coeff_carry_certificates
declarations.signature: theorem irrational_erdosSupportSeries_of_weighted_coeff_carry_certificates (b : ℕ) (A : Set ℕ) (hb : 2 ≤ b) (hcert : ∀ q : ℕ, 0 < q → ∃ N K L C : ℕ, K ≤ L ∧ (b ^ K ∣ ∑ r ∈ Finset.Icc 1 K, supportCoeff A (N + r) * b ^ (K - r)) ∧ (∑ r ∈ Finset.Icc (K + 1) L, supportCoeff A (N + r) * b ^ (L - r) ≤ C) ∧ (∃ t : ℕ, 0 < supportCoeff A (N + L + 1 + t)) ∧ q * (C + (N + L + 2)) < b ^ L) : Irrational (erdosSupportSeries b A)
declarations.docstring: **Arbitrary-support irrationality from carry-aware certificates.** The carry-aware analogue of `irrational_erdosSupportSeries_of_weighted_coeff_certificates`: certificate existence for `supportCoeff A` remains the OPEN obligation, but the first block may now be supplied in carried aggregate.

declarations.name: indicator_support_split_at_cut
declarations.signature: theorem indicator_support_split_at_cut (A : Set ℕ) (f : ℕ → ℝ) (B a : ℕ) : Set.indicator A f a = Set.indicator {n : ℕ | n ∈ A ∧ n ≤ B} f a + Set.indicator {n : ℕ | n ∈ A ∧ B < n} f a
declarations.docstring: Pointwise indicator split of a support at a height cut `B`.

declarations.name: erdosSupportSeries_eq_prefix_add_tail
declarations.signature: theorem erdosSupportSeries_eq_prefix_add_tail (b : ℕ) (A : Set ℕ) (hb : 2 ≤ b) (B : ℕ) : erdosSupportSeries b A = (∑ a ∈ Finset.range (B + 1), Set.indicator {n : ℕ | n ∈ A ∧ n ≤ B} (fun a => (1 : ℝ) / ((b : ℝ) ^ a - 1)) a) + erdosSupportSeries b {n : ℕ | n ∈ A ∧ B < n}
declarations.docstring: **The support series splits at any height cut** into a finite prefix sum and the tail-support series.

declarations.name: prefix_sum_eq_rat_cast
declarations.signature: theorem prefix_sum_eq_rat_cast (b : ℕ) (A : Set ℕ) (B : ℕ) : ∃ p : ℚ, ((p : ℚ) : ℝ) = ∑ a ∈ Finset.range (B + 1), Set.indicator {n : ℕ | n ∈ A ∧ n ≤ B} (fun a => (1 : ℝ) / ((b : ℝ) ^ a - 1)) a
declarations.docstring: **The finite prefix is rational.**

declarations.name: irrational_erdosSupportSeries_of_tail
declarations.signature: theorem irrational_erdosSupportSeries_of_tail (b : ℕ) (A : Set ℕ) (hb : 2 ≤ b) (B : ℕ) (h : Irrational (erdosSupportSeries b {n : ℕ | n ∈ A ∧ B < n})) : Irrational (erdosSupportSeries b A)
declarations.docstring: **Finite-prefix invariance, downward**: irrationality of a tail-support series transfers to the full support series — the prefix is rational. Removing finitely many bothersome small support elements is a legitimate normalisation move.

declarations.name: irrational_erdosSupportSeries_tail_of_irrational
declarations.signature: theorem irrational_erdosSupportSeries_tail_of_irrational (b : ℕ) (A : Set ℕ) (hb : 2 ≤ b) (B : ℕ) (h : Irrational (erdosSupportSeries b A)) : Irrational (erdosSupportSeries b {n : ℕ | n ∈ A ∧ B < n})
declarations.docstring: **Finite-prefix invariance, upward**: cutting a bounded prefix off a support preserves irrationality of the support series.

declarations.name: irrational_of_rat_prefix_tail_near_int_witnesses
declarations.signature: theorem irrational_of_rat_prefix_tail_near_int_witnesses (b : ℕ) (x : ℝ) (h : ∀ q : ℕ, 0 < q → ∃ (p : ℚ) (T : ℝ) (N : ℕ) (z : ℤ), x = (p : ℝ) + T ∧ 0 < (b : ℝ) ^ N * T - (z : ℝ) ∧ (b : ℝ) ^ N * T - (z : ℝ) < 1 / ((q * p.den : ℕ) : ℝ)) : Irrational x
declarations.docstring: **Rational-prefix near-integer composition.** If for every precision `q` some rational-plus-tail split of `x` admits a one-sided `b^N`-dilated witness at the prefix-adjusted precision `q · p.den`, then `x` is irrational. The multiplier `p.den · b^N` clears the prefix denominator. This composition is forced: `b^a - 1` is coprime to `b`, so no `b`-power multiplier can clear a support-prefix denominator, and per-precision finite-prefix normalisation genuinely needs the integer-multiplier criterion.

declarations.name: supportCoeff_eq_card_filter
declarations.signature: theorem supportCoeff_eq_card_filter (A : Set ℕ) (n : ℕ) [DecidablePred (· ∈ A)] : supportCoeff A n = (n.divisors.filter fun d => d ∈ A).card
declarations.docstring: **Instance-agnostic filter form of the support coefficient**: under any decidability instance for membership, `supportCoeff A n` is the divisor-filter cardinality. This is the bridge every stray-divisor argument uses to leave the `Classical.decPred` packaging of the definition.

declarations.name: supportCoeff_eq_inter_add_diff
declarations.signature: theorem supportCoeff_eq_inter_add_diff (A : Set ℕ) (F : Finset ℕ) (n : ℕ) : supportCoeff A n = supportCoeff (A ∩ ↑F) n + supportCoeff (A \ ↑F) n
declarations.docstring: **Support-coefficient split along a marked finite set**: divisors in `A` split into those inside a marked finite set `F` and those outside — the forced/stray decomposition, stated entirely in `supportCoeff` language.

declarations.name: supportCoeff_eq_card_of_forced_of_no_strays
declarations.signature: theorem supportCoeff_eq_card_of_forced_of_no_strays (A : Set ℕ) (P : Finset ℕ) (n : ℕ) (hn : n ≠ 0) (hPA : ∀ a ∈ P, a ∈ A) (hPdvd : ∀ a ∈ P, a ∣ n) (hno : ∀ d : ℕ, d ∣ n → d ∈ A → d ∈ P) : supportCoeff A n = P.card
declarations.docstring: **First-block exactness from forced divisors and no strays**: if the divisors of `n` lying in `A` are exactly a designated finite set `P`, then `supportCoeff A n = P.card`. This is what replaces `τ`-valuation forcing in support language: exactness is a counting statement, not a valuation statement.

declarations.name: card_le_mul_sum_inv
declarations.signature: theorem card_le_mul_sum_inv (X : ℕ) (T : Finset ℕ) (hT : ∀ a ∈ T, 0 < a ∧ a ≤ X) : (T.card : ℝ) ≤ (X : ℝ) * ∑ a ∈ T, (1 : ℝ) / a
declarations.docstring: **Two-line density bound**: a finite set of positive integers bounded by `X` has cardinality at most `X` times its reciprocal sum. This is the elementary replacement for analytic density arguments: summable reciprocals force support sparsity.

declarations.name: sum_div_add_one_cast_le
declarations.signature: theorem sum_div_add_one_cast_le (Y : ℕ) (T : Finset ℕ) : ((∑ a ∈ T, (Y / a + 1) : ℕ) : ℝ) ≤ (Y : ℝ) * (∑ a ∈ T, (1 : ℝ) / a) + (T.card : ℝ)
declarations.docstring: **Progression-hit aggregation**: the residue-class hit counts `Y/a + 1` aggregate against the reciprocal sum plus the cardinality — the bridge from exact `ℕ` progression counting to real reciprocal budgets.

declarations.name: exists_tail_bound_of_summable
declarations.signature: theorem exists_tail_bound_of_summable (g : ℕ → ℝ) (hg0 : ∀ n : ℕ, 0 ≤ g n) (hsum : Summable g) (ε : ℝ) (hε : 0 < ε) : ∃ B : ℕ, ∀ T : Finset ℕ, (∀ a ∈ T, B < a) → ∑ a ∈ T, g a ≤ ε
declarations.docstring: **Reciprocal-tail extraction from summability**: past some height `B`, every finite selection of indices beyond `B` has weight sum at most `ε`. This converts `Summable` into the finite tail budgets the sieve constructions consume.

declarations.name: supportCoeff_primes_eq_card_primeFactors
declarations.signature: theorem supportCoeff_primes_eq_card_primeFactors (n : ℕ) : supportCoeff {p : ℕ | p.Prime} n = n.primeFactors.card
declarations.docstring: **Prime support gives ω**: the support coefficient of the prime support counts distinct prime factors. This aligns the support calculus with Erdős #69: `∑_p 1/(b^p - 1) = ∑_m ω(m)/b^m`. The analytic irrationality input (Tao–Teräväinen) is NOT claimed — this identity places it at the exact theorem boundary where it belongs.

declarations.name: supportCoeff_primePowers_eq_sum_factorization
declarations.signature: theorem supportCoeff_primePowers_eq_sum_factorization (n : ℕ) : supportCoeff {m : ℕ | ∃ p k : ℕ, p.Prime ∧ 1 ≤ k ∧ m = p ^ k} n = n.factorization.sum fun _ e => e
declarations.docstring: **Prime-power support gives Ω**: the support coefficient of the prime-power support is the number of prime factors counted with multiplicity — prime-power divisors `p^k ∣ n` with `1 ≤ k` biject with pairs `(p, k)`, `k ≤ v_p(n)`.

declarations.name: erdosSupportSeries_primes_eq_tsum_omega
declarations.signature: theorem erdosSupportSeries_primes_eq_tsum_omega (b : ℕ) (hb : 2 ≤ b) : erdosSupportSeries b {p : ℕ | p.Prime} = ∑' m : ℕ, (((m + 1).primeFactors.card : ℕ) : ℝ) / (b : ℝ) ^ (m + 1)
declarations.docstring: Series bridge at the prime support: `∑_{p prime} 1/(b^p - 1) = ∑_m ω(m)/b^m` — the Erdős #69 identity, in support language.

declarations.name: erdosSupportSeries_primePowers_eq_tsum_bigOmega
declarations.signature: theorem erdosSupportSeries_primePowers_eq_tsum_bigOmega (b : ℕ) (hb : 2 ≤ b) : erdosSupportSeries b {m : ℕ | ∃ p k : ℕ, p.Prime ∧ 1 ≤ k ∧ m = p ^ k} = ∑' m : ℕ, (((m + 1).factorization.sum fun _ e => e : ℕ) : ℝ) / (b : ℝ) ^ (m + 1)
declarations.docstring: Series bridge at the prime-power support: `∑_{p^k} 1/(b^(p^k) - 1) = ∑_m Ω(m)/b^m`.

declarations.name: exists_linear_lt_pow
declarations.signature: theorem exists_linear_lt_pow (b α β L₀ : ℕ) (hb : 2 ≤ b) : ∃ L : ℕ, L₀ ≤ L ∧ α + β * L < b ^ L
declarations.docstring: Parameter-closure helper: every linear function of `L` is strictly below `b^L` for some `L` past any threshold — the wave-9 form of the wave-7 parameter kernel.

declarations.name: supportCoeff_pos_of_mem_dvd
declarations.signature: theorem supportCoeff_pos_of_mem_dvd (A : Set ℕ) {a n : ℕ} (ha : a ∈ A) (hdvd : a ∣ n) (hn : n ≠ 0) : 0 < supportCoeff A n
declarations.docstring: A support element dividing a nonzero integer makes the support coefficient positive — the far-tail positivity every sieve certificate needs, free from infinitude of the support.

declarations.name: sum_pow_weight_window_hits_le
declarations.signature: theorem sum_pow_weight_window_hits_le (b K L m : ℕ) (hb : 2 ≤ b) (hm : K + 1 ≤ m) (H : Finset ℕ) (hH : ∀ s ∈ H, s ∈ Finset.Icc (K + 1) L ∧ m ≤ s) : ∑ s ∈ H, b ^ (L - s) ≤ b ^ (L + 1 - m)
declarations.docstring: **Weighted geometric window bound for one modulus**: a hit set inside the window `(K, L]` whose members all sit at height `≥ m ≥ K + 1` carries weight at most `b^(L + 1 - m)`.

declarations.name: coprime_prod_prod_of_pairwise_disjoint
declarations.signature: theorem coprime_prod_prod_of_pairwise_disjoint (A : Set ℕ) (hpair : A.Pairwise Nat.Coprime) (P Q : Finset ℕ) (hP : ∀ a ∈ P, a ∈ A) (hQ : ∀ a ∈ Q, a ∈ A) (hdisj : Disjoint P Q) : Nat.Coprime (∏ a ∈ P, a) (∏ a ∈ Q, a)
declarations.docstring: Pairwise-coprime support elements have coprime disjoint-block products.

declarations.name: coprime_prod_of_pairwise_notMem
declarations.signature: theorem coprime_prod_of_pairwise_notMem (A : Set ℕ) (hpair : A.Pairwise Nat.Coprime) (Q : Finset ℕ) (hQ : ∀ a ∈ Q, a ∈ A) {a : ℕ} (ha : a ∈ A) (hnot : a ∉ Q) : Nat.Coprime a (∏ c ∈ Q, c)
declarations.docstring: A support element outside a marked finset of support elements is coprime to the finset's product.

declarations.name: exists_disjoint_support_blocks
declarations.signature: theorem exists_disjoint_support_blocks (A : Set ℕ) (hA : A.Infinite) (Buf : ℕ) (sz : ℕ → ℕ) (K : ℕ) : ∃ P : ℕ → Finset ℕ, (∀ r : ℕ, ∀ a ∈ P r, a ∈ A ∧ Buf < a) ∧ (∀ r ∈ Finset.Icc 1 K, (P r).card = sz r) ∧ (∀ r : ℕ, r ∉ Finset.Icc 1 K → P r = ∅) ∧ (∀ r s : ℕ, r ≠ s → Disjoint (P r) (P s))
declarations.docstring: **Disjoint block selection from an infinite support**: above any buffer, an infinite support supplies pairwise-disjoint finite blocks of any prescribed sizes.

declarations.name: exists_weighted_coeff_certificates_supportCoeff_pairwise_coprime
declarations.signature: theorem exists_weighted_coeff_certificates_supportCoeff_pairwise_coprime (b : ℕ) (A : Set ℕ) (hb : 2 ≤ b) (hA : A.Infinite) (hpair : A.Pairwise Nat.Coprime) (hsum : Summable (Set.indicator A fun a : ℕ => (1 : ℝ) / a)) : ∀ q : ℕ, 0 < q → ∃ N K L C : ℕ, K ≤ L ∧ (∀ r ∈ Finset.Icc 1 K, b ^ r ∣ supportCoeff A (N + r)) ∧ (∑ r ∈ Finset.Icc (K + 1) L, supportCoeff A (N + r) * b ^ (L - r) ≤ C) ∧ (∃ t : ℕ, 0 < supportCoeff A (N + L + 1 + t)) ∧ q * (C + (N + L + 2)) < b ^ L
declarations.docstring: **Certificate supply for pairwise-coprime supports.** Small support elements join the CRT modulus with residue `0`; adaptive block sizes `b^r - d_r mod b^r` absorb their deterministic first-block hits; pairwise coprimality makes the moduli independent; summable reciprocals control the strays through a single hit-count budget.

declarations.name: irrational_erdosSupportSeries_pairwise_coprime
declarations.signature: theorem irrational_erdosSupportSeries_pairwise_coprime (b : ℕ) (A : Set ℕ) (hb : 2 ≤ b) (hA : A.Infinite) (hpair : A.Pairwise Nat.Coprime) (hsum : Summable (Set.indicator A fun a : ℕ => (1 : ℝ) / a)) : Irrational (erdosSupportSeries b A)
declarations.docstring: **Erdős's pairwise-coprime support theorem (1948), machine-checked.** For every base `b ≥ 2` and every infinite pairwise-coprime support `A ⊆ ℕ` with summable reciprocals, `∑_{a ∈ A} 1/(b^a - 1)` is irrational. The first non-dilation support class of Erdős #257 to submit to the support-coefficient calculus: this is NOT valuation forcing — small support elements join the CRT modulus at residue zero, adaptive block sizes absorb their deterministic first-block hits, and summable reciprocals control the strays.

declarations.name: makes
declarations.signature: theorem makes the divisor ray `d, pd, p²d, …` sweep residues mod `m` with period dividing `φ(m)`. A prime with exact valuation `b·φ(m) - 1` therefore splits every divisor ray into complete residue cycles repeated a multiple of `b` times, contributing a clean factor `b` to `supportCoeff A n` — no primes in arithmetic progressions, no Lambert `q`-series criterion. The bounded Bertrand/CRT frame of the full-support lane supplies such primes wholesale: only the exact-valuation exponent changes, from `b - 1` to `b·φ(m) - 1`. -/ /-- **Iterated periodicity of a predicate**: an `m`-periodic predicate is invariant under adding any multiple of `m`. -/ theorem periodic_pred_add_mul (χ : ℕ → Prop) (m : ℕ) (hper : ∀ x : ℕ, χ (x + m) ↔ χ x) : ∀ (k x : ℕ), χ (x + k * m) ↔ χ x

declarations.name: periodic_pred_add_mul
declarations.signature: theorem periodic_pred_add_mul (χ : ℕ → Prop) (m : ℕ) (hper : ∀ x : ℕ, χ (x + m) ↔ χ x) : ∀ (k x : ℕ), χ (x + k * m) ↔ χ x
declarations.docstring: **Iterated periodicity of a predicate**: an `m`-periodic predicate is invariant under adding any multiple of `m`.

declarations.name: periodic_pred_iff_of_modEq
declarations.signature: theorem periodic_pred_iff_of_modEq (χ : ℕ → Prop) (m : ℕ) (hper : ∀ x : ℕ, χ (x + m) ↔ χ x) {a b : ℕ} (hab : a ≡ b [MOD m]) : χ a ↔ χ b
declarations.docstring: **Periodic predicates respect congruence**: an `m`-periodic predicate takes equal values on numbers congruent mod `m`.

declarations.name: card_filter_range_mul_of_periodic
declarations.signature: theorem card_filter_range_mul_of_periodic (Q : ℕ → Prop) [inst : DecidablePred Q] (t : ℕ) (hQ : ∀ j : ℕ, Q (j + t) ↔ Q j) : ∀ s : ℕ, ((Finset.range (s * t)).filter fun j => Q j).card = s * ((Finset.range t).filter fun j => Q j).card
declarations.docstring: **Complete-cycle count for a periodic predicate**: over a range of `s` complete periods, a `t`-periodic predicate holds exactly `s` times its count over one period. This is the divisibility engine of the periodic orbit lemma.
