No irrationality of `S` is claimed; the coordinate change relocates #249 onto the visible lattice, it does not solve it. -/ /-- **Index bridge, `ℕ`-power form**: the #249 statement-shape series with explicit `(1/2)ⁿ` terms, still over `ℕ`. -/ theorem tsum_totient_div_pow_two_eq_nat_half_pow : (∑' n : ℕ, ((Nat.totient n : ℝ)) / (2 : ℝ) ^ n) = ∑' n : ℕ, (Nat.totient n : ℝ) * ((1 : ℝ) / 2) ^ n

declarations.name: tsum_totient_div_pow_two_eq_nat_half_pow
declarations.signature: theorem tsum_totient_div_pow_two_eq_nat_half_pow : (∑' n : ℕ, ((Nat.totient n : ℝ)) / (2 : ℝ) ^ n) = ∑' n : ℕ, (Nat.totient n : ℝ) * ((1 : ℝ) / 2) ^ n
declarations.docstring: **Index bridge, `ℕ`-power form**: the #249 statement-shape series with explicit `(1/2)ⁿ` terms, still over `ℕ`.

declarations.name: tsum_visible_coprime_pairs_eq_totient_series
declarations.signature: theorem tsum_visible_coprime_pairs_eq_totient_series : (∑' p : ℕ × ℕ, if 0 < p.1 ∧ Nat.Coprime p.1 p.2 then ((1 : ℝ) / 2) ^ (p.1 + p.2) else 0) = ∑' n : ℕ, ((Nat.totient n : ℝ)) / (2 : ℝ) ^ n
declarations.docstring: **The #249 constant IS a visible-lattice mass**: summing `2^{-(a+b)}` over the half-open coprime pairs (`a ≥ 1`, `b ≥ 0`, `gcd(a,b) = 1`) gives exactly `∑ φ(n)/2ⁿ` — no boundary correction, because the visible-point count on the half-open antidiagonal equals `φ(n)` for every `n` including `0` and `1`.

declarations.name: totient_series_eq_half_add_visible_coprime_pairs
declarations.signature: theorem totient_series_eq_half_add_visible_coprime_pairs : (∑' n : ℕ, ((Nat.totient n : ℝ)) / (2 : ℝ) ^ n) = 1 / 2 + ∑' p : ℕ × ℕ, (if 0 < p.1 ∧ 0 < p.2 ∧ Nat.Coprime p.1 p.2 then ((1 : ℝ) / 2) ^ (p.1 + p.2) else 0)
declarations.docstring: **Fair-coin geometric coprimality**: `∑ φ(n)/2ⁿ = 1/2 + P(gcd(X,Y) = 1)`, where the probability is spelled as its defining series `∑_{(a,b) coprime, a,b ≥ 1} 2^{-(a+b)}`. The boundary term `1/2` is `P(X = 1) = L(μ)` — the ladder's Möbius rung. Erdős #249 is equivalent to irrationality of this coprimality probability.

declarations.name: tsum_visible_coprime_pairs_ne_int_div_of_den_le_39819823323350687661677887437915526
declarations.docstring: **Record-bound transfer to the coprimality probability.** The probability that two independent fair-coin waiting times are coprime, `P(gcd(X,Y) = 1) = ∑_{(a,b)=1} 2^{-(a+b)}`, has no rational representation with denominator `≤ 39819823323350687661677887437915526` (half the wave-17.5 Farey record): `P = a/d` would force `S = (d+2a)/(2d)` with `2d` under the record bound.

declarations.name: tsum_visible_coprime_gcd_layers_eq_one
declarations.signature: theorem tsum_visible_coprime_gcd_layers_eq_one : ∑' g : ℕ, (∑' p : ℕ × ℕ, if 0 < p.1 ∧ 0 < p.2 ∧ Nat.Coprime p.1 p.2 then (((1 : ℝ) / 2) ^ (g + 1)) ^ (p.1 + p.2) else 0) = 1
declarations.docstring: **The fair-coin gcd law is exactly normalized**: layer `g` of the sum is `P(gcd(X,Y) = g+1)` — the coprime-pair mass at scale `(1/2)^(g+1)`, by the unique factorization `(a,b) = g·(a',b')` — and the layers sum to `1`: the gcd of two independent fair-coin waiting times is finite almost surely. Through the visible-coprime bridge this is the probabilistic content of the wave-18 rational rung `L(φ) = 2`. The open #249 value `S - 1/2` is the `g = 1` atom of this exactly-normalized distribution.

declarations.name: tsum_inv_mersenne_sq_eq_sigma_sub_tau_series
declarations.signature: theorem tsum_inv_mersenne_sq_eq_sigma_sub_tau_series : ∑' d : ℕ+, 1 / ((2 : ℝ) ^ (d : ℕ) - 1) ^ 2 = ∑' n : ℕ+, ((∑ e ∈ (n : ℕ).divisors, (((n : ℕ) / e : ℕ) : ℝ)) - ((n : ℕ).divisors.card : ℝ)) * ((1 : ℝ) / 2) ^ (n : ℕ)
declarations.docstring: **E[τ(gcd)] — the q-zeta anchor of the level-2 ladder**: `∑_{d≥1} 1/(2ᵈ-1)² = ∑_{n≥1} (σ(n) - τ(n))·(1/2)ⁿ`, exactly, with the coefficient spelled as its divisor sums. This is `ζ_q(2) - ζ_q(1)` at `q = 1/2`; its irrationality (q-Padé) is cited, not formalised.

declarations.name: tsum_totient_div_mersenne_sq_eq_gcd_moment_series
declarations.signature: theorem tsum_totient_div_mersenne_sq_eq_gcd_moment_series : ∑' d : ℕ+, (Nat.totient (d : ℕ) : ℝ) / ((2 : ℝ) ^ (d : ℕ) - 1) ^ 2 = ∑' n : ℕ+, ((∑ e ∈ (n : ℕ).divisors, (Nat.totient e : ℝ) * (((n : ℕ) / e : ℕ) : ℝ)) - ((n : ℕ) : ℝ)) * ((1 : ℝ) / 2) ^ (n : ℕ)
declarations.docstring: **E[gcd(X,Y)] — the first moment of the fair-coin gcd law**: `∑_{d≥1} φ(d)/(2ᵈ-1)² = ∑_{n≥1} (P(n) - n)·(1/2)ⁿ`, where `P(n) = ∑_{e∣n} φ(e)·(n/e)` is Pillai's gcd-sum function.

declarations.name: tsum_pos_pair_both_dvd_half_eq_inv_mersenne_sq
declarations.signature: theorem tsum_pos_pair_both_dvd_half_eq_inv_mersenne_sq (d : ℕ) (hd : 0 < d) : (∑' p : ℕ × ℕ, if 0 < p.1 ∧ 0 < p.2 ∧ d ∣ p.1 ∧ d ∣ p.2 then ((1 : ℝ) / 2) ^ (p.1 + p.2) else 0) = 1 / ((2 : ℝ) ^ d - 1) ^ 2
declarations.docstring: **The divisibility factorization** — foundation stone of the squared kernel: the fair-coin mass of `{(a,b) : a,b ≥ 1, d ∣ a, d ∣ b}` is exactly `1/(2ᵈ-1)²`, i.e. `P(d ∣ gcd(X,Y)) = P(d ∣ X)·P(d ∣ Y)`.

declarations.name: tsum_visible_coprime_inv_mersenne_eq_one
declarations.signature: theorem tsum_visible_coprime_inv_mersenne_eq_one : (∑' p : ℕ × ℕ, if 0 < p.1 ∧ 0 < p.2 ∧ Nat.Coprime p.1 p.2 then 1 / ((2 : ℝ) ^ (p.1 + p.2) - 1) else 0) = 1
declarations.docstring: **The reduced-direction law**: the Mersenne masses of the reduced slopes sum to one — `∑_{(a,b) coprime, a,b ≥ 1} 1/(2^{a+b}-1) = 1`. This is the root cylinder of the Stern–Brocot law, proved through the wave-19 gcd-layer normalization.

declarations.name: stern_brocot_cylinder_mass_error_le
declarations.signature: theorem stern_brocot_cylinder_mass_error_le (dp : ℕ) (a b : ℕ+) : GcdMomentCalculus.sternBrocotDepthMass dp a b ≤ 1 / (((2 : ℝ) ^ (a : ℕ) - 1) * ((2 : ℝ) ^ (b : ℕ) - 1)) ∧ 1 / (((2 : ℝ) ^ (a : ℕ) - 1) * ((2 : ℝ) ^ (b : ℕ) - 1)) - GcdMomentCalculus.sternBrocotDepthMass dp a b ≤ (2 / 3 : ℝ) ^ dp * (1 / (((2 : ℝ) ^ (a : ℕ) - 1) * ((2 : ℝ) ^ (b : ℕ) - 1)))
declarations.docstring: **Stern–Brocot cylinder approximation with explicit rate**: the depth-`d` unfolding of the mediant recursion at `(a,b)` under-approximates the closed form `1/((2ᵃ-1)(2ᵇ-1))` with error at most `(2/3)ᵈ` of it.

declarations.name: tendsto_stern_brocot_cylinder_mass
declarations.signature: theorem tendsto_stern_brocot_cylinder_mass (a b : ℕ+) : Filter.Tendsto (fun dp : ℕ => GcdMomentCalculus.sternBrocotDepthMass dp a b) Filter.atTop (nhds (1 / (((2 : ℝ) ^ (a : ℕ) - 1) * ((2 : ℝ) ^ (b : ℕ) - 1))))
declarations.docstring: **The Stern–Brocot cylinder law, limit form**: the mediant recursion's depth-`d` masses converge to `M(a,b) = 1/((2ᵃ-1)(2ᵇ-1)) = P(a∣X)·P(b∣Y)`. At the root, `M(1,1) = 1` — the reduced-direction law.

declarations.name: stern_brocot_pair_of_run_lengths
declarations.signature: theorem stern_brocot_pair_of_run_lengths (newest : SternBrocotRunGeometry.LR) (ns : List ℕ) : SternBrocotRunGeometry.sbPair (SternBrocotRunGeometry.runWord newest ns) = match newest with | .left => SternBrocotRunGeometry.runBoundaryPair ns | .right => ((SternBrocotRunGeometry.runBoundaryPair ns).2, (SternBrocotRunGeometry.runBoundaryPair ns).1)
declarations.docstring: Exact transport from alternating run lengths to the literal Stern–Brocot pair.

declarations.name: stern_brocot_run_height_fib_lower
declarations.signature: theorem stern_brocot_run_height_fib_lower (newest : SternBrocotRunGeometry.LR) (ns : List ℕ) (hpos : ∀ n ∈ ns, 0 < n) : Nat.fib (ns.length + 3) ≤ SternBrocotRunGeometry.sbHeight (SternBrocotRunGeometry.runWord newest ns)
declarations.docstring: Fibonacci minimum height for positive alternating run lengths.

declarations.name: stern_brocot_unit_run_height_eq_fib
declarations.signature: theorem stern_brocot_unit_run_height_eq_fib (r : ℕ) : SternBrocotRunGeometry.runHeight (List.replicate r 1) = Nat.fib (r + 3)
declarations.docstring: The all-unit alternating spine attains the Fibonacci floor exactly.

declarations.name: stern_brocot_two_run_height
declarations.signature: theorem stern_brocot_two_run_height (m n : ℕ) : SternBrocotRunGeometry.runHeight [m, n] = (m + 1) * (n + 1) + 1
declarations.docstring: Exact two-run height, the finite identity behind the second induced Lambert layer.

declarations.name: stern_brocot_zero_run_mass_eq_quarter
declarations.docstring: Exact first two induced contributions: the root has mass `1/4`, and the two one-run cusps together have mass `1/2`.

declarations.name: stern_brocot_natural_run_denominator_exponent_add_two
declarations.docstring: Natural run-resolvent exponent clearing is exactly two below the Fibonacci height scale.

declarations.name: two_pow_mul_totient_series_eq_prefix_add_tail
declarations.signature: theorem two_pow_mul_totient_series_eq_prefix_add_tail (N : ℕ) : (2 : ℝ) ^ N * (∑' n : ℕ, ((Nat.totient n : ℝ)) / (2 : ℝ) ^ n) = (TotientTailPeriodKiller.totientPrefix N : ℝ) + TotientTailPeriodKiller.totientTail N
declarations.docstring: **Shift identity**: `2^N·S = Φ_N + R_N` — the local totient tail is the fractional layer of `2^N·S`.

declarations.name: totient_tail_diff_not_int_of_certified_kill
declarations.signature: theorem totient_tail_diff_not_int_of_certified_kill {h N L : ℕ} (hcert : TotientTailPeriodKiller.certifiedKill h N L) : TotientTailPeriodKiller.totientTail (N + h) - TotientTailPeriodKiller.totientTail N ∉ Set.range ((↑) : ℤ → ℝ)
declarations.docstring: **Certificate soundness**: a residue of the window discrepancy `A_{h,N,L}` modulo `2^L` outside the radius-`(N+h+L+2)` neighbourhood of `0` kills period `h` at position `N` — the tail difference is provably not an integer.

declarations.name: rational_totient_series_forces_eventual_tail_period
declarations.signature: theorem rational_totient_series_forces_eventual_tail_period (hrat : ¬ Irrational (∑' n : ℕ, ((Nat.totient n : ℝ)) / (2 : ℝ) ^ n)) : ∃ h : ℕ, 0 < h ∧ ∃ N₀ : ℕ, ∀ N, N₀ ≤ N → TotientTailPeriodKiller.totientTail (N + h) - TotientTailPeriodKiller.totientTail N ∈ Set.range ((↑) : ℤ → ℝ)
declarations.docstring: **Tail-period law**: if `S` is rational then some period `h ≥ 1` makes every shifted tail difference an integer from the pre-period `v₂(den)` on.

declarations.name: irrational_totient_series_of_period_kill_supply
declarations.signature: theorem irrational_totient_series_of_period_kill_supply (hsupply : ∀ h : ℕ, 0 < h → ∀ N₀ : ℕ, ∃ N, N₀ ≤ N ∧ ∃ L, TotientTailPeriodKiller.certifiedKill h N L) : Irrational (∑' n : ℕ, ((Nat.totient n : ℝ)) / (2 : ℝ) ^ n)
declarations.docstring: **The wave-21 reduction (the throat of #249)**: a certified period-kill supply — every period `h ≥ 1` killed beyond every threshold — proves `S` irrational.

declarations.name: totient_tail_period_killed_upto_eight
declarations.signature: theorem totient_tail_period_killed_upto_eight : ∀ h ∈ Finset.Icc 1 8, TotientTailPeriodKiller.certifiedKill h 12 16
declarations.docstring: **Concrete deposit**: every period `h ≤ 8` is certified dead at `(N, L) = (12, 16)` — machine-checked residue certificates.

declarations.name: totient_series_ne_rat_of_den_dvd_pow_two_mul_mersenne
declarations.signature: theorem totient_series_ne_rat_of_den_dvd_pow_two_mul_mersenne (r : ℚ) (h : ℕ) (h1 : 1 ≤ h) (h8 : h ≤ 8) (hdvd : (r.den : ℕ) ∣ 2 ^ 12 * (2 ^ h - 1)) : (∑' n : ℕ, ((Nat.totient n : ℝ)) / (2 : ℝ) ^ n) ≠ (r : ℝ)
declarations.docstring: **Concrete denominator exclusion**: `S` is not a rational whose denominator divides `2¹²·(2ʰ-1)` for any `1 ≤ h ≤ 8` — no dyadic rational up to `2¹²`, and no odd part whose multiplicative order of `2` is at most `8`.

declarations.name: totient_tail_carry_recurrence
declarations.signature: theorem totient_tail_carry_recurrence (M : ℕ) : TotientTailPeriodKiller.totientTail (M + 1) = 2 * TotientTailPeriodKiller.totientTail M - (Nat.totient (M + 1) : ℝ)
declarations.docstring: **Carry recurrence**: `R_{M+1} = 2·R_M - φ(M+1)` — doubling the tail shifts the window by one and expels the head totient.

declarations.name: totient_tail_diff_not_int_of_survivor_kill
declarations.signature: theorem totient_tail_diff_not_int_of_survivor_kill {h N K : ℕ} (hkill : TotientTailPeriodKiller.survivorKill h N K) : TotientTailPeriodKiller.totientTail (N + h) - TotientTailPeriodKiller.totientTail N ∉ Set.range ((↑) : ℤ → ℝ)
declarations.docstring: **Survivor soundness**: the decidable carry-survivor certificate — every integer candidate in the initial box escapes the strip `|·| < N+i+h+2` within `K` steps — kills period `h` at position `N`.

declarations.name: totient_tail_diff_multiple_telescope
declarations.signature: theorem totient_tail_diff_multiple_telescope (h N m : ℕ) : TotientTailPeriodKiller.totientTail (N + m * h) - TotientTailPeriodKiller.totientTail N = ∑ i ∈ Finset.range m, (TotientTailPeriodKiller.totientTail (N + i * h + h) - TotientTailPeriodKiller.totientTail (N + i * h))
declarations.docstring: **Period-ray telescoping**: the `m·h` tail difference is the sum of `m` translated `h` tail differences — integrality flows down every multiple.

declarations.name: irrational_totient_series_of_multiple_period_kill_supply
declarations.signature: theorem irrational_totient_series_of_multiple_period_kill_supply (hsupply : ∀ h₀ : ℕ, 0 < h₀ → ∀ N₀ : ℕ, ∃ m, 0 < m ∧ ∃ N, N₀ ≤ N ∧ ∃ K, TotientTailPeriodKiller.survivorKill (m * h₀) N K) : Irrational (∑' n : ℕ, ((Nat.totient n : ℝ)) / (2 : ℝ) ^ n)
declarations.docstring: **The wave-22 reduction**: a survivor kill along ANY multiple of each primitive period, at arbitrarily large `N`, proves `S` irrational — the weakest supply hypothesis in the ladder.

declarations.name: irrational_totient_series_of_multiple_window_kill_supply
declarations.signature: theorem irrational_totient_series_of_multiple_window_kill_supply (hsupply : ∀ h₀ : ℕ, 0 < h₀ → ∀ N₀ : ℕ, ∃ m, 0 < m ∧ ∃ N, N₀ ≤ N ∧ ∃ L, TotientTailPeriodKiller.certifiedKill (m * h₀) N L) : Irrational (∑' n : ℕ, ((Nat.totient n : ℝ)) / (2 : ℝ) ^ n)
declarations.docstring: The endpoint-window variant of the multiple-period reduction.

declarations.name: irrational_totient_series_of_lcm_period_kill_supply
declarations.signature: theorem irrational_totient_series_of_lcm_period_kill_supply (hsupply : ∀ t₀ N₀ : ℕ, ∃ t, t₀ ≤ t ∧ ∃ N, N₀ ≤ N ∧ ∃ K, TotientTailPeriodKiller.survivorKill (TotientTailPeriodKiller.periodLcm t) N K) : Irrational (∑' n : ℕ, ((Nat.totient n : ℝ)) / (2 : ℝ) ^ n)
declarations.docstring: **One-parameter collapse**: survivor kills along the single family `lcm(1..t)` at arbitrarily large `t` and `N` prove #249.

declarations.name: totient_tail_survivor_strict_at_equal_budget
declarations.signature: theorem totient_tail_survivor_strict_at_equal_budget : TotientTailPeriodKiller.survivorKill 1 57 8 ∧ ∀ L ∈ Finset.Icc 1 8, ¬ TotientTailPeriodKiller.certifiedKill 1 57 L
declarations.docstring: **Strictness witness**: at `(h, N) = (1, 57)` the survivor certificate kills at depth `8` while every endpoint window `L ≤ 8` fails — the survivor predicate is strictly stronger at equal budget (and measured near-equivalent in minimal depth by the wave-22 probe).

declarations.name: totient_tail_period_killed_upto_sixteen
declarations.signature: theorem totient_tail_period_killed_upto_sixteen : ∀ h ∈ Finset.Icc 1 16, TotientTailPeriodKiller.certifiedKill h 14 9
declarations.docstring: **Concrete deposit**: every period `h ≤ 16` is certified dead at `(N, L) = (14, 9)` — twice the wave-21 period range in a smaller window.

declarations.name: totient_series_ne_rat_of_den_dvd_pow_two_mul_mersenne_upto_sixteen
declarations.signature: theorem totient_series_ne_rat_of_den_dvd_pow_two_mul_mersenne_upto_sixteen (r : ℚ) (h : ℕ) (h1 : 1 ≤ h) (h16 : h ≤ 16) (hdvd : (r.den : ℕ) ∣ 2 ^ 14 * (2 ^ h - 1)) : (∑' n : ℕ, ((Nat.totient n : ℝ)) / (2 : ℝ) ^ n) ≠ (r : ℝ)
declarations.docstring: **Extended denominator exclusion**: `S` is not a rational whose denominator divides `2¹⁴·(2ʰ-1)` for any `1 ≤ h ≤ 16` — subsumes the wave-21 lattice on both axes.

declarations.name: irrational_totient_series_of_lcm_window_kill_supply
declarations.signature: theorem irrational_totient_series_of_lcm_window_kill_supply (hsupply : ∀ t₀ N₀ : ℕ, ∃ t, t₀ ≤ t ∧ ∃ N, N₀ ≤ N ∧ ∃ L, TotientTailPeriodKiller.certifiedKill (TotientTailPeriodKiller.periodLcm t) N L) : Irrational (∑' n : ℕ, ((Nat.totient n : ℝ)) / (2 : ℝ) ^ n)
declarations.docstring: **Endpoint lcm collapse (general position)**: certified window kills along `lcm(1..t)` at arbitrarily large `t` and `N` prove #249 — fills the endpoint gap of the wave-22 survivor-only lcm reduction.

declarations.name: irrational_totient_series_of_lcm_diagonal_window_kill_supply
declarations.signature: theorem irrational_totient_series_of_lcm_diagonal_window_kill_supply (hsupply : ∀ t₀ : ℕ, ∃ t, t₀ ≤ t ∧ ∃ L, TotientTailPeriodKiller.certifiedKill (TotientTailPeriodKiller.periodLcm t) (TotientTailPeriodKiller.periodLcm t) L) : Irrational (∑' n : ℕ, ((Nat.totient n : ℝ)) / (2 : ℝ) ^ n)
declarations.docstring: **The wave-23 diagonal collapse**: #249 follows from ONE ℕ-indexed sequence of decidable statements — the endpoint certificate firing on the diagonal `(h, N) = (lcm(1..t), lcm(1..t))` for infinitely many `t`. No second parameter remains.

declarations.name: irrational_totient_series_of_lcm_diagonal_period_kill_supply
declarations.signature: theorem irrational_totient_series_of_lcm_diagonal_period_kill_supply (hsupply : ∀ t₀ : ℕ, ∃ t, t₀ ≤ t ∧ ∃ K, TotientTailPeriodKiller.survivorKill (TotientTailPeriodKiller.periodLcm t) (TotientTailPeriodKiller.periodLcm t) K) : Irrational (∑' n : ℕ, ((Nat.totient n : ℝ)) / (2 : ℝ) ^ n)
declarations.docstring: Survivor variant of the diagonal collapse.

declarations.name: period_lcm_window_index_prime_pow_of_not_dvd
declarations.signature: theorem period_lcm_window_index_prime_pow_of_not_dvd {t j : ℕ} (hj : 0 < j) (hlt : j < 2 * t) (hnd : ¬ j ∣ TotientTailPeriodKiller.periodLcm t) : ∃ p k : ℕ, Nat.Prime p ∧ j = p ^ k ∧ t < j
declarations.docstring: **Window structure of the lcm ray**: below `2t` every non-divisor of `lcm(1..t)` is a bare prime power exceeding `t` — all other window indices divide the period.

declarations.name: totient_period_lcm_ray_factorisation
declarations.signature: theorem totient_period_lcm_ray_factorisation {t j : ℕ} (q : ℕ) (hdvd : j ∣ TotientTailPeriodKiller.periodLcm t) (hclean : ∀ p : ℕ, Nat.Prime p → p ∣ j → p ∣ (TotientTailPeriodKiller.periodLcm t / j)) : Nat.totient (q * TotientTailPeriodKiller.periodLcm t + j) = Nat.totient j * Nat.totient (q * (TotientTailPeriodKiller.periodLcm t / j) + 1)
declarations.docstring: **Lcm-ray window factorisation**: on a clean divisor `j` of `lcm(1..t)` the window totient splits exactly into the known local part `φ(j)` and a cofactor totient whose argument is forced `≡ 1` mod every non-exhausted prime of `j`.

declarations.name: totient_tail_period_lcm_diagonal_killed_upto_six
declarations.signature: theorem totient_tail_period_lcm_diagonal_killed_upto_six : ∀ t ∈ Finset.Icc 1 6, TotientTailPeriodKiller.certifiedKill (TotientTailPeriodKiller.periodLcm t) (TotientTailPeriodKiller.periodLcm t) (TotientTailPeriodKiller.diagonalKillDepth t)
declarations.docstring: **Diagonal deposits**: the wave-23 predicate `P t` is kernel-checked for every `t ≤ 6` at the probe-tabulated depths.

declarations.name: totient_tail_period_lcm_diagonal_killed_at_seven_and_eight
declarations.docstring: `P 7` and `P 8`: diagonal kills at `lcm(1..7) = 420` (depth `14`) and `lcm(1..8) = 840` (depth `15`).

declarations.name: period_lcm_dvd_period_lcm_of_le
declarations.signature: theorem period_lcm_dvd_period_lcm_of_le {t u : ℕ} (htu : t ≤ u) : TotientTailPeriodKiller.periodLcm t ∣ TotientTailPeriodKiller.periodLcm u
declarations.docstring: **Scale-ladder divisibility**: `lcm(1..t) ∣ lcm(1..u)` for `t ≤ u` — the cones at all scales form one directed system, so cross-scale cone pairs are same-scale pairs on the finer ray.

declarations.name: rational_totient_series_forces_lcm_cone_flatness
declarations.signature: theorem rational_totient_series_forces_lcm_cone_flatness (hrat : ¬ Irrational (∑' n : ℕ, ((Nat.totient n : ℝ)) / (2 : ℝ) ^ n)) : ∃ t₁ : ℕ, ∀ t, t₁ ≤ t → ∀ q m : ℕ, 0 < q → TotientTailPeriodKiller.totientTail (q * TotientTailPeriodKiller.periodLcm t + m * TotientTailPeriodKiller.periodLcm t) - TotientTailPeriodKiller.totientTail (q * TotientTailPeriodKiller.periodLcm t) ∈ Set.range ((↑) : ℤ → ℝ)
declarations.docstring: **The lcm-cone flatness law**: if the totient series is rational, then from some scale on the whole lcm cone is fractionally flat — every difference `R_{q·H_t + m·H_t} - R_{q·H_t}` is an integer.

declarations.name: irrational_totient_series_of_lcm_cone_window_kill_supply
declarations.signature: theorem irrational_totient_series_of_lcm_cone_window_kill_supply (hsupply : ∀ t₀ : ℕ, ∃ t, t₀ ≤ t ∧ ∃ q m L : ℕ, 0 < q ∧ TotientTailPeriodKiller.certifiedKill (m * TotientTailPeriodKiller.periodLcm t) (q * TotientTailPeriodKiller.periodLcm t) L) : Irrational (∑' n : ℕ, ((Nat.totient n : ℝ)) / (2 : ℝ) ^ n)
declarations.docstring: **The wave-24 cone collapse**: certified window kills anywhere on the two-multiplier lcm cone — `(h, N) = (m·H_t, q·H_t)` at arbitrarily large `t` — prove #249. The wave-23 diagonal is the cell `q = m = 1`.

declarations.name: totient_tail_window_kill_exists_iff_tail_diff_not_int
declarations.signature: theorem totient_tail_window_kill_exists_iff_tail_diff_not_int (h N : ℕ) : (∃ L, TotientTailPeriodKiller.certifiedKill h N L) ↔ TotientTailPeriodKiller.totientTail (N + h) - TotientTailPeriodKiller.totientTail N ∉ Set.range ((↑) : ℤ → ℝ)
declarations.docstring: **Certificate completeness**: some depth certifies `(h, N)` iff the tail difference `R_{N+h} - R_N` is a non-integer. Certificates are receipts; non-integrality is the object.

declarations.name: irrational_totient_series_of_lcm_diagonal_nonintegrality_supply
declarations.signature: theorem irrational_totient_series_of_lcm_diagonal_nonintegrality_supply (hsupply : ∀ t₀ : ℕ, ∃ t, t₀ ≤ t ∧ TotientTailPeriodKiller.totientTail (TotientTailPeriodKiller.periodLcm t + TotientTailPeriodKiller.periodLcm t) - TotientTailPeriodKiller.totientTail (TotientTailPeriodKiller.periodLcm t) ∉ Set.range ((↑) : ℤ → ℝ)) : Irrational (∑' n : ℕ, ((Nat.totient n : ℝ)) / (2 : ℝ) ^ n)
declarations.docstring: **The diagonal frontier in pure form**: non-integral diagonal tail differences at arbitrarily large `t` prove #249 — no certificate vocabulary in the hypothesis.

declarations.name: irrational_totient_series_of_lcm_cone_nonintegrality_supply
declarations.signature: theorem irrational_totient_series_of_lcm_cone_nonintegrality_supply (hsupply : ∀ t₀ : ℕ, ∃ t, t₀ ≤ t ∧ ∃ q m : ℕ, 0 < q ∧ TotientTailPeriodKiller.totientTail (q * TotientTailPeriodKiller.periodLcm t + m * TotientTailPeriodKiller.periodLcm t) - TotientTailPeriodKiller.totientTail (q * TotientTailPeriodKiller.periodLcm t) ∉ Set.range ((↑) : ℤ → ℝ)) : Irrational (∑' n : ℕ, ((Nat.totient n : ℝ)) / (2 : ℝ) ^ n)
declarations.docstring: **The cone frontier in pure form**: one non-integral cone difference at arbitrarily large scale proves #249.

declarations.name: totient_tail_second_diff_not_int_of_rank_two_kill
declarations.signature: theorem totient_tail_second_diff_not_int_of_rank_two_kill {h N L : ℕ} (hcert : TotientTailPeriodKiller.certifiedRank2Kill h N L) : (TotientTailPeriodKiller.totientTail (N + 2 * h) - TotientTailPeriodKiller.totientTail (N + h)) - (TotientTailPeriodKiller.totientTail (N + h) - TotientTailPeriodKiller.totientTail N) ∉ Set.range ((↑) : ℤ → ℝ)
declarations.docstring: **Rank-2 soundness**: a certified second-difference residue proves `(R_{N+2h} - R_{N+h}) - (R_{N+h} - R_N)` is not an integer.

declarations.name: irrational_totient_series_of_lcm_qray_rank_two_kill_supply
declarations.signature: theorem irrational_totient_series_of_lcm_qray_rank_two_kill_supply (hsupply : ∀ t₀ : ℕ, ∃ t, t₀ ≤ t ∧ ∃ q L : ℕ, 0 < q ∧ TotientTailPeriodKiller.certifiedRank2Kill (TotientTailPeriodKiller.periodLcm t) (q * TotientTailPeriodKiller.periodLcm t) L) : Irrational (∑' n : ℕ, ((Nat.totient n : ℝ)) / (2 : ℝ) ^ n)
declarations.docstring: **Rank-2 cone supply**: certified second-difference kills along the q-rays of the lcm cone at arbitrarily large `t` prove #249.

declarations.name: totient_tail_lcm_cone_cells_killed
declarations.signature: theorem totient_tail_lcm_cone_cells_killed : TotientTailPeriodKiller.certifiedKill (1 * TotientTailPeriodKiller.periodLcm 2) (3 * TotientTailPeriodKiller.periodLcm 2) 6 ∧ TotientTailPeriodKiller.certifiedKill (2 * TotientTailPeriodKiller.periodLcm 3) (2 * TotientTailPeriodKiller.periodLcm 3) 7 ∧ TotientTailPeriodKiller.certifiedKill (3 * TotientTailPeriodKiller.periodLcm 3) (1 * TotientTailPeriodKiller.periodLcm 3) 8
declarations.docstring: **Cone-lattice deposits off the diagonal**: certified kills at `(t, q, m) = (2, 3, 1)`, `(3, 2, 2)`, `(3, 1, 3)` at the probe-tabulated depths.

declarations.name: totient_tail_rank_two_kill_sound_but_not_shallower_cell
declarations.docstring: **The measured rank-2 verdict, kernel-checked**: at `(h, N) = (1, 8)` the rank-1 certificate fires at depth `8`, no rank-2 certificate exists at any depth `≤ 8`, and rank-2 first fires at depth `9` — sound but not shallower (probe v2, `t ≤ 20`: rank-1 at least as shallow in 30 of 40 cells).

declarations.name: totient_tail_period_lcm_diagonal_not_int_at_seven_and_eight
declarations.signature: theorem totient_tail_period_lcm_diagonal_not_int_at_seven_and_eight : (TotientTailPeriodKiller.totientTail (TotientTailPeriodKiller.periodLcm 7 + TotientTailPeriodKiller.periodLcm 7) - TotientTailPeriodKiller.totientTail (TotientTailPeriodKiller.periodLcm 7) ∉ Set.range ((↑) : ℤ → ℝ)) ∧ (TotientTailPeriodKiller.totientTail (TotientTailPeriodKiller.periodLcm 8 + TotientTailPeriodKiller.periodLcm 8) - TotientTailPeriodKiller.totientTail (TotientTailPeriodKiller.periodLcm 8) ∉ Set.range ((↑) : ℤ → ℝ))
declarations.docstring: The diagonal tail differences at `t = 7` and `t = 8` are non-integers — the wave-23 corollary extended past its decide ceiling.

declarations.name: totient_tail_nonintegral_pair_of_cone_nonflat_cert
declarations.signature: theorem totient_tail_nonintegral_pair_of_cone_nonflat_cert {H L : ℕ} {Q : List ℕ} (hQ : Q ≠ []) (hfloor : ∀ q ∈ Q, (q * H + L + 2 : ℤ) < 2 ^ L) (hcert : TotientTailPeriodKiller.coneNonflatCert H L Q) : ∃ qi ∈ Q, ∃ qj ∈ Q, TotientTailPeriodKiller.totientTail (qj * H) - TotientTailPeriodKiller.totientTail (qi * H) ∉ Set.range ((↑) : ℤ → ℝ)
declarations.docstring: **Menu refutation soundness**: a firing non-flatness certificate (with one-sided floors) forces some pair of menu vertices to have a non-integral tail difference.

declarations.name: irrational_totient_series_of_lcm_cone_nonflat_supply
declarations.signature: theorem irrational_totient_series_of_lcm_cone_nonflat_supply (hsupply : ∀ t₀ : ℕ, ∃ t, t₀ ≤ t ∧ ∃ L : ℕ, ∃ Q : List ℕ, Q ≠ [] ∧ (∀ q ∈ Q, 0 < q) ∧ (∀ q ∈ Q, (q * TotientTailPeriodKiller.periodLcm t + L + 2 : ℤ) < 2 ^ L) ∧ TotientTailPeriodKiller.coneNonflatCert (TotientTailPeriodKiller.periodLcm t) L Q) : Irrational (∑' n : ℕ, ((Nat.totient n : ℝ)) / (2 : ℝ) ^ n)
declarations.docstring: **The cone non-flatness supply theorem**: one firing vertex menu at arbitrarily large scale proves #249.

declarations.name: irrational_totient_series_of_lcm_jump_window_kill_supply
declarations.signature: theorem irrational_totient_series_of_lcm_jump_window_kill_supply (hsupply : ∀ t₀ : ℕ, ∃ t, t₀ ≤ t ∧ ∃ L : ℕ, TotientTailPeriodKiller.certifiedKill (TotientTailPeriodKiller.periodLcm (t + 1) - TotientTailPeriodKiller.periodLcm t) (TotientTailPeriodKiller.periodLcm t) L) : Irrational (∑' n : ℕ, ((Nat.totient n : ℝ)) / (2 : ℝ) ^ n)
declarations.docstring: **The lcm-jump supply theorem**: certified kills at the jumps `(h, N) = (H_{t+1} - H_t, H_t)` at arbitrarily large `t` prove #249 — the cone cells `(q, m) = (1, p-1)`, with no side conditions at all.

declarations.name: totient_tail_cone_nonflat_cells_certified
declarations.docstring: First firing menus at the probe-tabulated smallest cells.

declarations.name: totient_tail_cone_nonflat_strict_win_at_cell
declarations.signature: theorem totient_tail_cone_nonflat_strict_win_at_cell : TotientTailPeriodKiller.coneNonflatCert (TotientTailPeriodKiller.periodLcm 2) 8 [1, 3] ∧ (∀ L ∈ Finset.Icc 1 8, ¬ TotientTailPeriodKiller.certifiedKill (2 * TotientTailPeriodKiller.periodLcm 2) (1 * TotientTailPeriodKiller.periodLcm 2) L) ∧ TotientTailPeriodKiller.certifiedKill (2 * TotientTailPeriodKiller.periodLcm 2) (1 * TotientTailPeriodKiller.periodLcm 2) 9
declarations.docstring: **The measured strict win, kernel-checked**: at `t = 2` the menu `[1, 3]` refutes joint flatness at depth `8` while no pairwise certificate exists at any depth `≤ 8` (the pair first kills at depth `9`).

declarations.name: totient_tail_cone_nonflat_joint_win_at_cell
declarations.signature: theorem totient_tail_cone_nonflat_joint_win_at_cell : TotientTailPeriodKiller.coneNonflatCert (TotientTailPeriodKiller.periodLcm 2) 5 [2, 3, 4] ∧ (¬ TotientTailPeriodKiller.coneNonflatCert (TotientTailPeriodKiller.periodLcm 2) 5 [2, 3]) ∧ (¬ TotientTailPeriodKiller.coneNonflatCert (TotientTailPeriodKiller.periodLcm 2) 5 [2, 4]) ∧ (¬ TotientTailPeriodKiller.coneNonflatCert (TotientTailPeriodKiller.periodLcm 2) 5 [3, 4]) ∧ (¬ TotientTailPeriodKiller.certifiedKill (1 * TotientTailPeriodKiller.periodLcm 2) (2 * TotientTailPeriodKiller.periodLcm 2) 5) ∧ (¬ TotientTailPeriodKiller.certifiedKill (2 * TotientTailPeriodKiller.periodLcm 2)
declarations.docstring: **The measured joint win, kernel-checked**: at `t = 2` the menu `[2, 3, 4]` refutes joint flatness at depth `5` while every 2-vertex submenu refuter and every pairwise certificate is silent at depth `5` — the menu is inconsistent at a depth where every pair alone stays consistent.

declarations.name: totient_tail_cone_nonflat_cert_iff_no_common_point
declarations.signature: theorem totient_tail_cone_nonflat_cert_iff_no_common_point {H L : ℕ} {Q : List ℕ} (hQ : Q ≠ []) : TotientTailPeriodKiller.coneNonflatCert H L Q ↔ ¬ TotientTailPeriodKiller.coneCommonPoint H L Q
declarations.docstring: **Exactness of the menu refuter**: the certificate fires iff the menu arcs share NO common point — the probe's brute-force gate (`nonflat_matches_common_point_bruteforce`), now a theorem at every cell.

declarations.name: totient_tail_cone_common_point_halving_descent
declarations.signature: theorem totient_tail_cone_common_point_halving_descent {H L : ℕ} {Q : List ℕ} (hfloor : ∀ q ∈ Q, (q * H + L + 2 : ℤ) < 2 ^ L) (h : TotientTailPeriodKiller.coneCommonPoint H (L + 1) Q) : TotientTailPeriodKiller.coneCommonPoint H L Q
declarations.docstring: **Halving descent**: a common point of the depth-`L+1` menu arcs yields one at depth `L` (floors held at `L`).

declarations.name: totient_tail_cone_nonflat_cert_depth_monotone
declarations.signature: theorem totient_tail_cone_nonflat_cert_depth_monotone {H L L' : ℕ} {Q : List ℕ} (hQ : Q ≠ []) (hfloor : ∀ q ∈ Q, (q * H + L + 2 : ℤ) < 2 ^ L) (hcert : TotientTailPeriodKiller.coneNonflatCert H L Q) (hle : L ≤ L') : TotientTailPeriodKiller.coneNonflatCert H L' Q
declarations.docstring: **Depth monotonicity of the menu refuter**: a firing certificate keeps firing at every deeper depth (floors held at the base), so `nonflat_L_min` is THE firing threshold.

declarations.name: totient_tail_cone_nonflat_cert_supermenu
declarations.signature: theorem totient_tail_cone_nonflat_cert_supermenu {H L : ℕ} {Q Q' : List ℕ} (hQ : Q ≠ []) (hsub : ∀ q ∈ Q, q ∈ Q') (hcert : TotientTailPeriodKiller.coneNonflatCert H L Q) : TotientTailPeriodKiller.coneNonflatCert H L Q'
declarations.docstring: **Menu monotonicity**: a firing certificate keeps firing for every supermenu at the SAME depth — adding cone vertices cannot refill an empty arc intersection. This is why the wave-25 joint wins exist (firing runs upward in the menu) and why a small seed suffices.

declarations.name: totient_tail_cone_nonflat_cert_seed_lift
declarations.signature: theorem totient_tail_cone_nonflat_cert_seed_lift {H L L' : ℕ} {Q Q' : List ℕ} (hQ : Q ≠ []) (hsub : ∀ q ∈ Q, q ∈ Q') (hfloor : ∀ q ∈ Q, (q * H + L + 2 : ℤ) < 2 ^ L) (hcert : TotientTailPeriodKiller.coneNonflatCert H L Q) (hle : L ≤ L') : TotientTailPeriodKiller.coneNonflatCert H L' Q'
declarations.docstring: **Seed-lift**: a small seed menu firing at its own floor inflates to every supermenu at every deeper depth (depth monotonicity up, menu monotonicity out). For supply it suffices that some prefix seed fires at its floor for arbitrarily large `t`.

declarations.name: compositeDilationDefect
declarations.signature: noncomputable def compositeDilationDefect (A : Set ℕ) (a x : ℕ) : ℕ
declarations.docstring: Support divisors created by multiplication by `a`, excluding the distinguished divisor `a` itself.

declarations.name: supportCoeff_mul_eq_add_defect
declarations.signature: theorem supportCoeff_mul_eq_add_defect (A : Set ℕ) {a x : ℕ} (ha : a ∈ A) (ha0 : 0 < a) (hx0 : 0 < x) : supportCoeff A (a * x) = supportCoeff A x + (if a ∣ x then 0 else 1) + compositeDilationDefect A a x
declarations.docstring: Exact composite replacement for the prime-dilation identity. The new support divisors split into the distinguished member `a` (when it was not already present at `x`) and the composite dilation defect.

declarations.name: compositeDilationDefect_eq_zero_of_prime_support
declarations.signature: theorem compositeDilationDefect_eq_zero_of_prime_support (A : Set ℕ) {p x : ℕ} (hp : p.Prime) (hAprime : ∀ d ∈ A, d.Prime) : compositeDilationDefect A p x = 0
declarations.docstring: On a support consisting only of primes, no composite dilation defect can survive. Thus the composite identity specializes exactly to the familiar prime-support formula.

declarations.name: supportCoeff_mul_prime_support
declarations.signature: theorem supportCoeff_mul_prime_support (A : Set ℕ) {p x : ℕ} (hp : p.Prime) (hpA : p ∈ A) (hx0 : 0 < x) (hAprime : ∀ d ∈ A, d.Prime) : supportCoeff A (p * x) = supportCoeff A x + (if p ∣ x then 0 else 1)
declarations.docstring: Prime-support specialization of `supportCoeff_mul_eq_add_defect`.

declarations.name: mem_compositeDilationDefect_bouquet_imp_exceptional_or_petal_dvd
declarations.signature: theorem mem_compositeDilationDefect_bouquet_imp_exceptional_or_petal_dvd {A : Set ℕ} (hB : OrthogonalPetalBouquet A) (i x d : ℕ) (hdiv : d ∣ hB.ray i * x) (hdA : d ∈ A) (hdne : d ≠ hB.ray i) : d ∈ hB.exceptional ∨ ∃ j : ℕ, j ≠ i ∧ d = hB.ray j ∧ hB.petal j ∣ x
declarations.docstring: Every individual bouquet defect is a local signed cost. It is either an exceptional finite-core channel, or it comes from a unique foreign ray whose petal already divides the undilated argument. This is the pointwise engine behind the bound by `exceptional.card + supportCoeff (Set.range petal) x`; it makes no appeal to lower-rank irrationality.

declarations.name: compositeDilationDefect_le_exceptional_add_petalCoeff
declarations.signature: theorem compositeDilationDefect_le_exceptional_add_petalCoeff {A : Set ℕ} (hB : OrthogonalPetalBouquet A) (i x : ℕ) (hx : 0 < x) : compositeDilationDefect A (hB.ray i) x ≤ hB.exceptional.card + supportCoeff (Set.range hB.petal) x
declarations.docstring: Cardinal form of the local bouquet defect budget. Exceptional channels cost at most the finite frame cardinality. Every remaining defect injects, via its unique ray index, into a petal divisor of `x`.

declarations.name: compositeDilationDefect_two_six_fixture
declarations.docstring: The smallest composite-defect witness: multiplying by `6` creates both the distinguished support member `6` and the lower-rank support divisor `2`.

declarations.name: supportCoeff_mul_eq_add_defect_two_six_fixture
declarations.signature: theorem supportCoeff_mul_eq_add_defect_two_six_fixture : supportCoeff ({2, 6} : Set ℕ) (6 * 1) = supportCoeff ({2, 6} : Set ℕ) 1 + 1 + compositeDilationDefect ({2, 6} : Set ℕ) 6 1
declarations.docstring: The exact identity on the same fixture records the extra defect term.

declarations.name: curvatureTail
declarations.docstring: The affine curvature of the three LCM-cone tail vertices `H, 2H, 4H`.

declarations.name: curvatureWindow
declarations.docstring: The signed depth-`L` integer window for `curvatureTail`.

declarations.name: curvatureShiftedTail
declarations.signature: noncomputable def curvatureShiftedTail (H L : ℕ) : ℝ
declarations.docstring: The shifted tail left after truncating `curvatureTail` at depth `L`.

declarations.name: curvatureDrive
declarations.docstring: The fresh integer letter appended to the curvature word at offset `s`.

declarations.name: abs_curvatureDrive_le
declarations.signature: theorem abs_curvatureDrive_le (H s : ℕ) : |curvatureDrive H s| ≤ (6 * H + 3 * s : ℤ)
declarations.docstring: The curvature drive has a uniform elementary bound. This deliberately uses only `Nat.totient_le`; no factorisation or finite fixture enters.

declarations.name: sharpCurvatureRadius
declarations.docstring: The sharp direct curvature edge radius.

declarations.name: totientTail_le_succ
declarations.signature: lemma totientTail_le_succ (M : ℕ) (hM : 1 ≤ M) : totientTail M ≤ (M : ℝ) + 1
declarations.docstring: For a positive starting index, the elementary totient tail bound improves from `R_M <= M + 2` to `R_M <= M + 1`, using `phi(n) <= n - 1` termwise.

declarations.name: two_pow_mul_curvature_eq_window_add_shifted
declarations.signature: theorem two_pow_mul_curvature_eq_window_add_shifted (H L : ℕ) : (2 : ℝ) ^ L * curvatureTail H = (curvatureWindow H L : ℝ) + curvatureShiftedTail H L
declarations.docstring: Exact curvature prefix/tail split.

declarations.name: abs_curvatureShiftedTail_le
declarations.signature: theorem abs_curvatureShiftedTail_le (H L : ℕ) (hH : 1 ≤ H) : |curvatureShiftedTail H L| ≤ (sharpCurvatureRadius H L : ℝ)
declarations.docstring: The direct shifted curvature tail lies in the sharp symmetric strip.

declarations.name: sharpCurvatureCert
declarations.docstring: The direct residue-gap certificate for curvature.

declarations.name: curvature_notMem_int_of_sharpCurvatureCert
declarations.signature: theorem curvature_notMem_int_of_sharpCurvatureCert {H L : ℕ} (hH : 1 ≤ H) (hcert : sharpCurvatureCert H L) : curvatureTail H ∉ Set.range ((↑) : ℤ → ℝ)
declarations.docstring: A sharp curvature residue gap directly excludes integral curvature.

declarations.name: SharpCurvatureSupply
declarations.docstring: The unbounded direct-curvature certificate supply.

declarations.name: SharpCurvatureAnyDepthSupply
declarations.docstring: The depth need not be tied to the old LCM height. This is the correct consumer for a phase-separation certificate found later in a successor block.

declarations.name: irrational_totientSeries_of_sharpCurvatureSupply
declarations.signature: theorem irrational_totientSeries_of_sharpCurvatureSupply (hsupply : SharpCurvatureSupply) : Irrational (∑' n : ℕ, (Nat.totient n : ℝ) / 2 ^ n)
declarations.docstring: The sharp curvature supply is a complete proof socket for #249.

declarations.name: irrational_totientSeries_of_sharpCurvatureAnyDepthSupply
declarations.signature: theorem irrational_totientSeries_of_sharpCurvatureAnyDepthSupply (hsupply : SharpCurvatureAnyDepthSupply) : Irrational (∑' n : ℕ, (Nat.totient n : ℝ) / 2 ^ n)
declarations.docstring: Arbitrarily large sharp curvature certificates at arbitrary depths still contradict the same LCM-cone flatness conclusion.

declarations.name: curvatureWindow_succ
declarations.signature: theorem curvatureWindow_succ (H L : ℕ) : curvatureWindow H (L + 1) = 2 * curvatureWindow H L + curvatureDrive H (L + 1)
declarations.docstring: The curvature word is an exact input-driven doubling recurrence.

declarations.name: curvatureCarryOrbit
declarations.signature: def curvatureCarryOrbit (H m : ℕ) (e : ℤ) : ℕ → ℤ | 0 => e | j + 1 => 2 * curvatureCarryOrbit H m e j + curvatureDrive H (m + j + 1) /-- An entering phase remains inside the sharp corridor throughout a block. -/ def curvatureBlockLocked (H m r : ℕ) (e : ℤ) : Prop
declarations.docstring: Exact affine curvature orbit launched at depth `m`.

declarations.name: curvatureBlockLocked
declarations.signature: def curvatureBlockLocked (H m r : ℕ) (e : ℤ) : Prop
declarations.docstring: An entering phase remains inside the sharp corridor throughout a block.

declarations.name: curvatureEndpointSurvivor
declarations.signature: def curvatureEndpointSurvivor (H L : ℕ) (e : ℤ) : Prop
declarations.docstring: Signed survivor fibre at one depth. The congruence records the complete prefix history exposed to a suffix checker.

declarations.name: curvatureCarryOrbit_curvatureWindow
declarations.signature: theorem curvatureCarryOrbit_curvatureWindow (H m j : ℕ) : curvatureCarryOrbit H m (curvatureWindow H m) j = curvatureWindow H (m + j)
declarations.docstring: The affine orbit starting from the genuine prefix window is exactly the curvature window at every later depth.

declarations.name: CurvaturePhaseCertificate
declarations.docstring: Proof-valued phase-fibre certificate. A future reflected checker may produce this structure from exact arithmetic receipts; the final `sharp` field is the small kernel-facing conclusion.

declarations.name: CurvaturePhaseCertificateSupply
declarations.docstring: Arbitrarily large proof-producing phase certificates.

declarations.name: irrational_totientSeries_of_phaseCertificateSupply
declarations.signature: theorem irrational_totientSeries_of_phaseCertificateSupply (hsupply : CurvaturePhaseCertificateSupply) : Irrational (∑' n : ℕ, (Nat.totient n : ℝ) / 2 ^ n)
declarations.docstring: Phase-certificate supply feeds the any-depth curvature consumer.

declarations.name: scaledRoughLoss
declarations.docstring: A denominator-free rough loss scaled by an arbitrary common carrier `g`.

declarations.name: curvatureDrive_commonCarrier
declarations.signature: theorem curvatureDrive_commonCarrier (g H s : ℕ) : (g : ℤ) * curvatureDrive H s = 3 * scaledRoughLoss g (2 * H + s) - 2 * scaledRoughLoss g (H + s) - scaledRoughLoss g (4 * H + s)
declarations.docstring: The exact common-carrier identity. No divisibility or factorisation hypothesis is needed at this scaled level; choosing `g` as a smooth carrier can be deferred to downstream arithmetic.

declarations.name: cyclicModulus
declarations.docstring: The defining relation of the cyclic polynomial algebra of order `r`.

declarations.name: subgroupSum
declarations.signature: noncomputable def subgroupSum (r q : ℕ) : ℤ[X]
declarations.docstring: The sum of the order-`q` subgroup in the cyclic algebra of order `r`.

declarations.name: localNumeratorFactor
declarations.signature: noncomputable def localNumeratorFactor (r q : ℕ) : ℤ[X]
declarations.docstring: The prime-local factor occurring in the tensor product for the numerator.

declarations.name: localCofactorFactor
declarations.signature: noncomputable def localCofactorFactor (r q : ℕ) : ℤ[X]
declarations.docstring: The integral cofactor of the prime-local numerator factor.

declarations.name: numeratorFactorProduct
declarations.docstring: Product of all prime-local numerator factors.

declarations.name: cofactorProduct
declarations.docstring: Product of all integral prime-local cofactors.

declarations.name: oddJordanScalar
declarations.docstring: The scalar contributed by the odd prime factors of `r`.

declarations.name: localNumeratorValue
declarations.signature: noncomputable def localNumeratorValue (r q : ℕ) : ℤ
declarations.docstring: Base-two evaluation of a local numerator factor.

declarations.name: localCofactorValue
declarations.signature: noncomputable def localCofactorValue (r q : ℕ) : ℤ
declarations.docstring: Base-two evaluation of a local cofactor factor.

declarations.name: subgroupSum_mul_X_pow_sub_one
declarations.signature: private theorem subgroupSum_mul_X_pow_sub_one {r q : ℕ} (hq : q ∣ r) : subgroupSum r q * ((Polynomial.X : ℤ[X]) ^ (r / q) - 1) = cyclicModulus r

declarations.name: X_pow_sub_one_dvd_subgroupSum_sub_C
declarations.signature: private theorem X_pow_sub_one_dvd_subgroupSum_sub_C (r q : ℕ) : (Polynomial.X : ℤ[X]) ^ (r / q) - 1 ∣ subgroupSum r q - Polynomial.C (q : ℤ)

declarations.name: cyclicModulus_dvd_subgroupSum_sq_sub_smul
declarations.signature: theorem cyclicModulus_dvd_subgroupSum_sq_sub_smul {r q : ℕ} (hq : q ∣ r) : cyclicModulus r ∣ subgroupSum r q ^ 2 - Polynomial.C (q : ℤ) * subgroupSum r q
declarations.docstring: In the cyclic algebra, the subgroup sum satisfies `Σ_q² = q Σ_q`.

declarations.name: cyclicModulus_dvd_localNumerator_mul_localCofactor_sub_scalar
declarations.signature: theorem cyclicModulus_dvd_localNumerator_mul_localCofactor_sub_scalar {r q : ℕ} (hq : q ∣ r) : cyclicModulus r ∣ localNumeratorFactor r q * localCofactorFactor r q - Polynomial.C ((q : ℤ) ^ 2 - 1)
declarations.docstring: The local numerator and cofactor multiply to the scalar `q² - 1` modulo `X^r - 1`.

declarations.name: dvd_finset_prod_sub_prod
declarations.signature: private theorem dvd_finset_prod_sub_prod {ι : Type*} [DecidableEq ι] (m : ℤ[X]) (s : Finset ι) (f g : ι → ℤ[X]) (hfg : ∀ i ∈ s, m ∣ f i - g i) : m ∣ (∏ i ∈ s, f i) - ∏ i ∈ s, g i

declarations.name: polynomial_dvd_sub
declarations.signature: private theorem polynomial_dvd_sub {a b c : ℤ[X]} (hb : a ∣ b) (hc : a ∣ c) : a ∣ b - c

declarations.name: monic_dvd_of_map_rat_dvd
declarations.signature: private theorem monic_dvd_of_map_rat_dvd {m d : ℤ[X]} (hm : m.Monic) (h : m.map (Int.castRingHom ℚ) ∣ d.map (Int.castRingHom ℚ)) : m ∣ d

declarations.name: cyclicModulus_dvd_mobiusNumeratorPolynomial_sub_expand
declarations.signature: theorem cyclicModulus_dvd_mobiusNumeratorPolynomial_sub_expand {r p : ℕ} (hr : Squarefree r) (hp : p.Prime) (hpr : ¬ p ∣ r) : cyclicModulus r ∣ mobiusNumeratorPolynomial r - Polynomial.expand ℤ p (mobiusNumeratorPolynomial r)
declarations.docstring: A fresh-prime expansion acts trivially on the squarefree Möbius numerator in the cyclic algebra of the old order. This is the algebraic transport lemma used by prime-adjunction induction.

declarations.name: expand_cyclicModulus
declarations.signature: private theorem expand_cyclicModulus (r p : ℕ) : Polynomial.expand ℤ p (cyclicModulus r) = cyclicModulus (r * p)

declarations.name: localNumeratorFactor_mul_prime
declarations.signature: private theorem localNumeratorFactor_mul_prime {a p q : ℕ} (hqa : q ∣ a) : localNumeratorFactor (p * a) q = Polynomial.expand ℤ p (localNumeratorFactor a q)

declarations.name: numeratorFactorProduct_mul_prime
declarations.signature: private theorem numeratorFactorProduct_mul_prime {a p : ℕ} (hp : p.Prime) (ha0 : a ≠ 0) (hpa : ¬ p ∣ a) : numeratorFactorProduct (p * a) = localNumeratorFactor (p * a) p * Polynomial.expand ℤ p (numeratorFactorProduct a)

declarations.name: subgroupSum_mul_prime_at_prime
declarations.signature: private theorem subgroupSum_mul_prime_at_prime {a p : ℕ} (hp : p.Prime) : subgroupSum (p * a) p = spacedRepunit a p

declarations.name: subgroupSum_mul_cyclicModulus
declarations.signature: private theorem subgroupSum_mul_cyclicModulus {a p : ℕ} (hp : p.Prime) : subgroupSum (p * a) p * cyclicModulus a = cyclicModulus (p * a)

declarations.name: cyclicModulus_dvd_mobiusNumeratorPolynomial_sub_numeratorFactorProduct
declarations.signature: theorem cyclicModulus_dvd_mobiusNumeratorPolynomial_sub_numeratorFactorProduct {r : ℕ} (hr : Squarefree r) : cyclicModulus r ∣ mobiusNumeratorPolynomial r - numeratorFactorProduct r
declarations.docstring: Prime-factor tensor factorisation of the existing Möbius numerator in the cyclic algebra. The polynomial on the left is the original T1 owner; no parallel numerator definition is introduced.

declarations.name: cyclicModulus_dvd_numeratorFactorProduct_mul_cofactorProduct_sub_jordanTotientTwo
declarations.signature: theorem cyclicModulus_dvd_numeratorFactorProduct_mul_cofactorProduct_sub_jordanTotientTwo {r : ℕ} (hr : Squarefree r) : cyclicModulus r ∣ numeratorFactorProduct r * cofactorProduct r - Polynomial.C (jordanTotientTwo r)
declarations.docstring: Multiplying all local adjugate identities gives the integral scalar adjugate for the tensor product itself.

declarations.name: cyclicModulus_dvd_mobiusNumeratorPolynomial_mul_cofactorProduct_sub_jordanTotientTwo
declarations.signature: theorem cyclicModulus_dvd_mobiusNumeratorPolynomial_mul_cofactorProduct_sub_jordanTotientTwo {r : ℕ} (hr : Squarefree r) : cyclicModulus r ∣ mobiusNumeratorPolynomial r * cofactorProduct r - Polynomial.C (jordanTotientTwo r)
declarations.docstring: The cofactor product is an integral adjugate for the original Möbius numerator polynomial, not merely for the auxiliary tensor product.

declarations.name: mersenne_dvd_mobiusNumerator_sub_localNumeratorProduct
declarations.signature: theorem mersenne_dvd_mobiusNumerator_sub_localNumeratorProduct {r : ℕ} (hr : Squarefree r) : (RadicalMobiusShadow.mersenne r : ℤ) ∣ RadicalMobiusShadow.mobiusNumerator r - ∏ q ∈ r.primeFactors, localNumeratorValue r q
declarations.docstring: Evaluation at `X = 2` gives the prime-factor product congruence modulo the Mersenne denominator.

declarations.name: jordanTotientTwo
declarations.docstring: The second Jordan totient, as the integer-valued Dirichlet convolution `μ * id²`.

declarations.name: cyclotomicEval
declarations.docstring: Integer evaluation `Φ_m(2)`.

declarations.name: cyclotomicValue
declarations.docstring: The unsigned cyclotomic channel modulus `|Φ_m(2)|`.

declarations.name: jordanTotientTwo_eq_divisorSum
declarations.signature: theorem jordanTotientTwo_eq_divisorSum {n : ℕ} (_hn : 0 < n) : jordanTotientTwo n = ∑ d ∈ n.divisors, ArithmeticFunction.moebius d * (((n / d : ℕ) : ℤ) ^ 2)

declarations.name: jordanTotientTwo_prime
declarations.signature: theorem jordanTotientTwo_prime {p : ℕ} (hp : p.Prime) : jordanTotientTwo p = (p : ℤ) ^ 2 - 1

declarations.name: jordanTotientTwo_eq_prod_primeFactors
declarations.signature: theorem jordanTotientTwo_eq_prod_primeFactors {n : ℕ} (hn : Squarefree n) : jordanTotientTwo n = ∏ p ∈ n.primeFactors, ((p : ℤ) ^ 2 - 1)

declarations.name: jordanTotientTwo_natAbs_primeFactors_le
declarations.signature: theorem jordanTotientTwo_natAbs_primeFactors_le {t n : ℕ} (ht : 3 ≤ t) (hnSq : Squarefree n) (hnSupp : ∀ q : ℕ, q.Prime → q ∣ n → q ≤ t) : ∀ ℓ : ℕ, ℓ.Prime → ℓ ∣ (jordanTotientTwo n).natAbs → ℓ ≤ t
declarations.docstring: If a squarefree argument has prime support at most `t`, then the same is true of the absolute second Jordan totient, once `t` contains the exceptional factor `3` coming from `2² - 1`.

declarations.name: spacedRepunit_eq_sum_X_pow
declarations.signature: private theorem spacedRepunit_eq_sum_X_pow (d q : ℕ) : RepunitMobiusNumerator.spacedRepunit d q = ∑ j ∈ Finset.range q, (Polynomial.X : ℤ[X]) ^ (d * j)

declarations.name: spacedRepunit_mul_X_pow_sub_one
declarations.signature: private theorem spacedRepunit_mul_X_pow_sub_one (d q : ℕ) : RepunitMobiusNumerator.spacedRepunit d q * ((Polynomial.X : ℤ[X]) ^ d - 1) = (Polynomial.X : ℤ[X]) ^ (d * q) - 1

declarations.name: spacedRepunit_eq_prod_cyclotomic_sdiff
declarations.signature: private theorem spacedRepunit_eq_prod_cyclotomic_sdiff {r d : ℕ} (hr : 0 < r) (hd : d ∣ r) : RepunitMobiusNumerator.spacedRepunit d (r / d) = ∏ e ∈ r.divisors \ d.divisors, Polynomial.cyclotomic e ℤ

declarations.name: cyclotomic_dvd_spacedRepunit_of_not_dvd
declarations.signature: theorem cyclotomic_dvd_spacedRepunit_of_not_dvd {r d m : ℕ} (hr : 0 < r) (hd : d ∣ r) (hm : m ∣ r) (hmd : ¬m ∣ d) : Polynomial.cyclotomic m ℤ ∣ RepunitMobiusNumerator.spacedRepunit d (r / d)
declarations.docstring: A cyclotomic fibre absent from the block spacing divides the corresponding spaced repunit. This public wrapper is the geometric input used by the T5 new-prime fibre split.

declarations.name: cyclotomic_dvd_spacedRepunit_sub_C_of_dvd
declarations.signature: theorem cyclotomic_dvd_spacedRepunit_sub_C_of_dvd {r d m : ℕ} (hmd : m ∣ d) : Polynomial.cyclotomic m ℤ ∣ RepunitMobiusNumerator.spacedRepunit d (r / d) - Polynomial.C (((r / d : ℕ) : ℤ))
declarations.docstring: On a cyclotomic fibre already present in the block spacing, the spaced repunit is congruent to its number of blocks. This is the old-fibre geometric input for T5.

declarations.name: cyclotomicFibreConstant
declarations.signature: private noncomputable def cyclotomicFibreConstant (r m : ℕ) : ℤ

declarations.name: cyclotomic_dvd_polynomial_sub_fibreConstant
declarations.signature: private theorem cyclotomic_dvd_polynomial_sub_fibreConstant {r m : ℕ} (hr : 0 < r) (hm : m ∣ r) : Polynomial.cyclotomic m ℤ ∣ RepunitMobiusNumerator.mobiusNumeratorPolynomial r - Polynomial.C (cyclotomicFibreConstant r m)

declarations.name: cyclotomicFibreConstant_eq
declarations.signature: private theorem cyclotomicFibreConstant_eq {r m : ℕ} (hr : Squarefree r) (hm : m ∣ r) : cyclotomicFibreConstant r m = ArithmeticFunction.moebius m * jordanTotientTwo (r / m)

declarations.name: cyclotomic_dvd_mobiusNumeratorPolynomial_sub
declarations.signature: theorem cyclotomic_dvd_mobiusNumeratorPolynomial_sub {r m : ℕ} (hr : Squarefree r) (hm : m ∣ r) : Polynomial.cyclotomic m ℤ ∣ RepunitMobiusNumerator.mobiusNumeratorPolynomial r - Polynomial.C (ArithmeticFunction.moebius m * jordanTotientTwo (r / m))
declarations.docstring: T2: the complete integral cyclotomic congruence.

declarations.name: mobiusNumerator_mod_cyclotomicEval
declarations.signature: theorem mobiusNumerator_mod_cyclotomicEval {r m : ℕ} (hr : Squarefree r) (hm : m ∣ r) : cyclotomicEval m ∣ RadicalMobiusShadow.mobiusNumerator r - ArithmeticFunction.moebius m * jordanTotientTwo (r / m)
declarations.docstring: Evaluation form of T2, designed for T3 and T4.

declarations.name: cyclotomicValue_dvd_mersenne
declarations.signature: theorem cyclotomicValue_dvd_mersenne (m : ℕ) : cyclotomicValue m ∣ RadicalMobiusShadow.mersenne m
declarations.docstring: Every cyclotomic channel divides its corresponding Mersenne value.

declarations.name: cyclotomicEval_prime
declarations.signature: theorem cyclotomicEval_prime {p : ℕ} (hp : p.Prime) : cyclotomicEval p = (RadicalMobiusShadow.mersenne p : ℤ)
declarations.docstring: Prime cyclotomic channels are exactly Mersenne channels.

declarations.name: cyclotomicValue_prime
declarations.signature: theorem cyclotomicValue_prime {p : ℕ} (hp : p.Prime) : cyclotomicValue p = RadicalMobiusShadow.mersenne p

declarations.name: mobiusNumerator_gcd_cyclotomicValue
declarations.signature: theorem mobiusNumerator_gcd_cyclotomicValue {r : ℕ} (hr : Squarefree r) : Nat.gcd (RadicalMobiusShadow.mobiusNumerator r).natAbs (cyclotomicValue r) = 1
declarations.docstring: T3: the top cyclotomic fibre cannot cancel from the numerator.

declarations.name: cyclotomicValue_dvd_baseMobiusShadow_den
declarations.signature: theorem cyclotomicValue_dvd_baseMobiusShadow_den {r : ℕ} (hr : Squarefree r) : cyclotomicValue r ∣ (RadicalMobiusShadow.baseMobiusShadow r).den
declarations.docstring: The exact unscaled-denominator consequence of T3.

declarations.name: eval_spacedRepunit_two
declarations.signature: private theorem eval_spacedRepunit_two {r d : ℕ} (hr : 0 < r) (hd : d ∣ r) : (RepunitMobiusNumerator.spacedRepunit d (r / d)).eval 2 = (((RadicalMobiusShadow.mersenne r / RadicalMobiusShadow.mersenne d : ℕ) : ℤ))

declarations.name: mersenneQuotient_mod_cyclotomicEval
declarations.signature: theorem mersenneQuotient_mod_cyclotomicEval {r m : ℕ} (hr : 0 < r) (hm : m ∣ r) : cyclotomicEval m ∣ (((RadicalMobiusShadow.mersenne r / RadicalMobiusShadow.mersenne m : ℕ) : ℤ) - ((r / m : ℕ) : ℤ))
declarations.docstring: The geometric cofactor is congruent to `r / m` in the `m`-th channel. This is the second algebraic bridge consumed by T4.

declarations.name: squarefree_210
declarations.docstring: The geometric cofactor is congruent to `r / m` in the `m`-th channel. This is the second algebraic bridge consumed by T4. -/ theorem mersenneQuotient_mod_cyclotomicEval {r m : ℕ} (hr : 0 < r) (hm : m ∣ r) : cyclotomicEval m ∣ (((RadicalMobiusShadow.mersenne r / RadicalMobiusShadow.mersenne m : ℕ) : ℤ) - ((r / m : ℕ) : ℤ)) := by have hpoly := cyclotomic_dvd_spacedRepunit_sub_C_of_dvd (r := r) (d := m) (m := m) (dvd_refl m) have heval := map_dvd (Polynomial.evalRingHom (R := ℤ) 2) hpoly simpa [cyclotomicEval, eval_spacedRepunit_two hr hm] using heval /-! ## Finite regression anchors These are theorem-level elaboration anchors, not finite substitutes for T2 or T3. The larger generated matrix remains receipt evidence.

declarations.name: t2_210_105_anchor
declarations.signature: theorem t2_210_105_anchor : Polynomial.cyclotomic 105 ℤ ∣ RepunitMobiusNumerator.mobiusNumeratorPolynomial 210 - Polynomial.C (ArithmeticFunction.moebius 105 * jordanTotientTwo (210 / 105))

declarations.name: t2_210_210_anchor
declarations.signature: theorem t2_210_210_anchor : Polynomial.cyclotomic 210 ℤ ∣ RepunitMobiusNumerator.mobiusNumeratorPolynomial 210 - Polynomial.C (ArithmeticFunction.moebius 210 * jordanTotientTwo (210 / 210))

declarations.name: diagonalFreshLossProjectionCert
declarations.docstring: The exact two-vertex operational projection at the LCM height `H_t`. For this menu, the two rows of `coneNonflatCert` are precisely the asymmetric safe-interval test on the residue of `windowNumerator (2 * H_t) L - windowNumerator H_t L`.

declarations.name: diagonalWindowResidue
declarations.docstring: The signed depth-`L` binary window residue on the diagonal pair.

declarations.name: diagonalFreshLossResidueCert
declarations.docstring: The explicit asymmetric safe interval for the diagonal residue. This is the denominator-free form of the finite full-target certificate.

declarations.name: diagonalFreshLossResidueCert_iff_projectionCert
declarations.signature: theorem diagonalFreshLossResidueCert_iff_projectionCert (t L : ℕ) : diagonalFreshLossResidueCert t L ↔ diagonalFreshLossProjectionCert t L
declarations.docstring: On the two-vertex menu, the generic cone certificate is exactly the asymmetric residue-safe interval. This pins the operational shape used by the diagnostic probe to a kernel theorem rather than a comment-level analogy.

declarations.name: foreignDiagonalDefect_misses_fullTarget_of_projectionCert
declarations.signature: theorem foreignDiagonalDefect_misses_fullTarget_of_projectionCert {t L : ℕ} (hfloor : (2 * periodLcm t + L + 2 : ℤ) < 2 ^ L) (hcert : diagonalFreshLossProjectionCert t L) : ¬ForeignDiagonalDefectHitsFullTarget t
declarations.docstring: A firing exact `[1, 2]` projection misses the complete reduced pincer target. Only the largest one-sided floor is needed; the `q = 1` floor follows from it. This is the proof-bearing composition seam between the cone/window owner and the complement-free pincer owner.

declarations.name: foreignDiagonalDefect_misses_fullTarget_of_residueCert
declarations.signature: theorem foreignDiagonalDefect_misses_fullTarget_of_residueCert {t L : ℕ} (hcert : diagonalFreshLossResidueCert t L) : ¬ForeignDiagonalDefectHitsFullTarget t
declarations.docstring: The explicit asymmetric residue certificate alone is a complete finite consumer: its right-hand inequality already implies the largest one-sided floor required by the generic cone soundness theorem.

declarations.name: DiagonalFreshLossProjectionSupply
declarations.docstring: The exact unresolved operational supply exposed by this bridge.

declarations.name: irrational_totientSeries_of_diagonalFreshLossProjectionSupply
declarations.signature: theorem irrational_totientSeries_of_diagonalFreshLossProjectionSupply (hsupply : DiagonalFreshLossProjectionSupply) : Irrational (∑' n : ℕ, (Nat.totient n : ℝ) / 2 ^ n)
declarations.docstring: Unbounded exact `[1, 2]` projection certificates feed the full-target avoidance consumer and therefore prove irrationality of the totient series.

declarations.name: windowNumerator_prefix_suffix
declarations.signature: theorem windowNumerator_prefix_suffix (M J m : ℕ) : windowNumerator M (J + m) = 2 ^ m * windowNumerator M J + windowNumerator (M + J) m
declarations.docstring: Prefix–suffix split of the window numerator: the depth-`(J + m)` window is `2^m` times its depth-`J` prefix plus the depth-`m` window at the translated base `M + J`.

declarations.name: diagonalWindowIncrement
declarations.docstring: The signed diagonal window increment `φ(2·H_t+s) − φ(H_t+s)` at offset `s`.

declarations.name: diagonalSuffixResidue
declarations.signature: def diagonalSuffixResidue (t J m : ℕ) : ℤ
declarations.docstring: The depth-`m` binary residue of the diagonal window suffix that starts after the cut `J`: the last `m` bits of the depth-`(J + m)` diagonal window, computed from the translated windows alone.

declarations.name: diagonalSuffixResidue_eq_increment_sum
declarations.signature: theorem diagonalSuffixResidue_eq_increment_sum (t J m : ℕ) : diagonalSuffixResidue t J m = (∑ r ∈ Finset.range m, diagonalWindowIncrement t (J + 1 + r) * 2 ^ (m - 1 - r)) % 2 ^ m
declarations.docstring: The suffix residue is the dyadically weighted sum of the signed window increments strictly after the cut — the operational shape used by the sliding-suffix probe.

declarations.name: diagonalWindowResidue_mod_suffix
declarations.signature: theorem diagonalWindowResidue_mod_suffix (t J m : ℕ) : diagonalWindowResidue t (J + m) % 2 ^ m = diagonalSuffixResidue t J m
declarations.docstring: Low-bit projection: the depth-`(J + m)` diagonal residue reduces modulo `2^m` to the sliding-suffix residue at cut `J`. The `J`-bit prefix vanishes; no estimate of it is required.

declarations.name: diagonalFreshLossResidueCert_of_suffix_central
declarations.signature: theorem diagonalFreshLossResidueCert_of_suffix_central {t J m : ℕ} (hleft : (periodLcm t + (J + m) + 2 : ℤ) < diagonalSuffixResidue t J m) (hright : diagonalSuffixResidue t J m < 2 ^ m - (2 * periodLcm t + (J + m) + 2)) : diagonalFreshLossResidueCert t (J + m)
declarations.docstring: **Sliding-suffix transference.** A central residue in the last `m` bits of the depth-`(J + m)` diagonal window already implies the full asymmetric safe-interval certificate at depth `J + m`: both excluded edge bands of the full window reduce into the corresponding edge bands of the suffix modulus.

declarations.name: diagonalAdjacentSuffixResidue
declarations.signature: def diagonalAdjacentSuffixResidue (t J m : ℕ) : ℤ
declarations.docstring: The canonical modular displacement from the suffix at cut `J` to the suffix at cut `J + 1`.

declarations.name: diagonalSuffixResidue_succ_cut
declarations.signature: theorem diagonalSuffixResidue_succ_cut (t J m : ℕ) : diagonalSuffixResidue t (J + 1) m = (2 * diagonalSuffixResidue t J m + diagonalWindowIncrement t (J + m + 1)) % 2 ^ m
declarations.docstring: Sliding the cut once doubles the retained suffix and appends the next diagonal increment.

declarations.name: diagonalAdjacentSuffixResidue_eq_terminal_corrected
declarations.signature: theorem diagonalAdjacentSuffixResidue_eq_terminal_corrected (t J m : ℕ) : diagonalAdjacentSuffixResidue t J m = (diagonalSuffixResidue t J m + diagonalWindowIncrement t (J + m + 1)) % 2 ^ m
declarations.docstring: The adjacent displacement is the terminal-corrected suffix residue.

declarations.name: diagonalFreshLossResidueCert_or_of_adjacent_suffix_gap_sharp
declarations.signature: theorem diagonalFreshLossResidueCert_or_of_adjacent_suffix_gap_sharp {t J m : ℕ} (hgap : let B : ℤ
declarations.docstring: Sharp adjacent-gap geometry. The constant is exactly the sum of the right edge width at cut `J` and the left edge width at cut `J + 1` (equally, the other cross-edge sum).

declarations.name: diagonalFreshLossResidueCert_or_of_adjacent_suffix_gap
declarations.signature: theorem diagonalFreshLossResidueCert_or_of_adjacent_suffix_gap {t J m : ℕ} (hgap : let B : ℤ
declarations.docstring: If the modular displacement between adjacent suffixes exceeds the sum of their two edge widths in both directions, then at least one suffix gives a full-depth fresh-loss certificate.

declarations.name: diagonalFreshLossResidueCert_or_of_adjacent_dyadic_gap
declarations.signature: theorem diagonalFreshLossResidueCert_or_of_adjacent_dyadic_gap {t J m : ℕ} (hm : 5 ≤ m) (hwidth : 32 * (3 * periodLcm t + 2 * (J + m + 3) : ℤ) < 2 ^ m) (hlo : 2 ^ (m - 5) ≤ diagonalAdjacentSuffixResidue t J m) (hhi : diagonalAdjacentSuffixResidue t J m ≤ 2 ^ m - 2 ^ (m - 5)) : diagonalFreshLossResidueCert t (J + m) ∨ diagonalFreshLossResidueCert t (J + 1 + m)
declarations.docstring: A fixed `1/32` circular gap dominates the adjacent edge budget.

declarations.name: canonicalAdjacentSuffixDepth
declarations.docstring: The canonical adjacent-suffix depth: ten guard bits beyond the binary scale of the LCM height. At this depth the analytic width budget is automatic; the only remaining arithmetic input is centrality of the adjacent residue.

declarations.name: canonicalAdjacentSuffixDepth_width
declarations.signature: theorem canonicalAdjacentSuffixDepth_width {t : ℕ} (ht : 3 ≤ t) : 5 ≤ canonicalAdjacentSuffixDepth t ∧ 32 * (3 * periodLcm t + 2 * (canonicalAdjacentSuffixDepth t + 3) : ℤ) < 2 ^ canonicalAdjacentSuffixDepth t
declarations.docstring: From `t ≥ 3`, the canonical depth has enough guard bits and its fixed `1/32` central band dominates the whole adjacent-suffix edge budget.

declarations.name: DiagonalAdjacentSuffixGapSupply
declarations.docstring: The unresolved arithmetic input after the adjacent finite reduction. It uses the fixed first pair of cuts, so sliding is no longer an artificial waiting parameter.

declarations.name: CanonicalAdjacentSuffixCentralSupply
declarations.docstring: The canonical producer throat after removing the automatic width budget. Only centrality of one exact modular observable at arbitrarily large LCM heights remains.

declarations.name: CanonicalAdjacentSuffixJumpCentralSupply
declarations.docstring: The prime-power-jump-aligned producer throat. Repeated LCM heights carry no new arithmetic state, so it is enough to establish canonical centrality at arbitrarily large strict jumps.

declarations.name: canonicalAdjacentSuffixCentralSlack
declarations.docstring: Signed distance of the canonical adjacent residue from the nearer edge of the fixed central band. Nonnegative slack is exactly the two-sided centrality condition; this scalar is the proof-oriented cocycle to study at strict LCM jumps.

declarations.name: canonicalAdjacentSuffixCentral_iff_slack_nonneg
declarations.signature: theorem canonicalAdjacentSuffixCentral_iff_slack_nonneg (t : ℕ) : (2 ^ (canonicalAdjacentSuffixDepth t - 5) ≤ diagonalAdjacentSuffixResidue t 0 (canonicalAdjacentSuffixDepth t) ∧ diagonalAdjacentSuffixResidue t 0 (canonicalAdjacentSuffixDepth t) ≤ 2 ^ canonicalAdjacentSuffixDepth t - 2 ^ (canonicalAdjacentSuffixDepth t - 5)) ↔ 0 ≤ canonicalAdjacentSuffixCentralSlack t
declarations.docstring: The two central-band inequalities collapse to one exact integer sign.

declarations.name: CanonicalAdjacentSuffixJumpSlackSupply
declarations.docstring: Scalar strict-jump producer. This is equivalent to the original two-sided producer but exposes a single integer observable for recurrence, congruence, and exact-computation arguments.

declarations.name: canonicalAdjacentSuffixCentralSupply_of_jump
declarations.docstring: Restricting the producer to strict LCM jumps still supplies the canonical centrality interface.

declarations.name: diagonalAdjacentSuffixGapSupply_of_canonical
declarations.docstring: Canonical centrality supplies the existing adjacent-gap interface; the guard-bit and width obligations are discharged uniformly.

declarations.name: irrational_totientSeries_of_diagonalAdjacentSuffixGapSupply
declarations.signature: theorem irrational_totientSeries_of_diagonalAdjacentSuffixGapSupply (hsupply : DiagonalAdjacentSuffixGapSupply) : Irrational (∑' n : ℕ, (Nat.totient n : ℝ) / 2 ^ n)
declarations.docstring: The adjacent integer-gap interface is already sufficient for the global irrationality consumer.

declarations.name: irrational_totientSeries_of_canonicalAdjacentSuffixCentralSupply
declarations.signature: theorem irrational_totientSeries_of_canonicalAdjacentSuffixCentralSupply (hsupply : CanonicalAdjacentSuffixCentralSupply) : Irrational (∑' n : ℕ, (Nat.totient n : ℝ) / 2 ^ n)
declarations.docstring: Canonical adjacent-suffix centrality is sufficient for Erdős #249's irrationality target through the already-landed adjacent-gap consumer.

declarations.name: irrational_totientSeries_of_canonicalAdjacentSuffixJumpCentralSupply
declarations.signature: theorem irrational_totientSeries_of_canonicalAdjacentSuffixJumpCentralSupply (hsupply : CanonicalAdjacentSuffixJumpCentralSupply) : Irrational (∑' n : ℕ, (Nat.totient n : ℝ) / 2 ^ n)
declarations.docstring: Centrality at arbitrarily large strict LCM jumps already closes the irrationality consumer.

declarations.name: irrational_totientSeries_of_canonicalAdjacentSuffixJumpSlackSupply
declarations.signature: theorem irrational_totientSeries_of_canonicalAdjacentSuffixJumpSlackSupply (hsupply : CanonicalAdjacentSuffixJumpSlackSupply) : Irrational (∑' n : ℕ, (Nat.totient n : ℝ) / 2 ^ n)
declarations.docstring: Nonnegative canonical slack at arbitrarily large strict LCM jumps closes the same irrationality consumer.

declarations.name: diagonalFreshLossProjectionCell_seven
declarations.docstring: At `t = 7`, depth `13` gives the exact diagonal projection and its floor. This is a bridge fixture, not evidence for an unbounded supply.

declarations.name: foreignDiagonalDefect_misses_fullTarget_seven_via_projection
declarations.docstring: The existing `t = 7` full-target miss can be recovered through the new operational composition seam.

declarations.name: diagonalSuffixCell_seven
declarations.signature: theorem diagonalSuffixCell_seven : (periodLcm 7 + (1 + 12) + 2 : ℤ) < diagonalSuffixResidue 7 1 12 ∧ diagonalSuffixResidue 7 1 12 < 2 ^ 12 - (2 * periodLcm 7 + (1 + 12) + 2)
declarations.docstring: At `t = 7`, the cut `J = 1` with suffix length `m = 12` is centrally placed: the last twelve bits alone recover the landed `(7, 13)` projection. A bridge fixture, not evidence for an unbounded supply.

declarations.name: foreignDiagonalDefect_misses_fullTarget_seven_via_suffix
declarations.docstring: The `t = 7` full-target miss re-derived from suffix bits only, through the sliding-suffix transference theorem.

declarations.name: binaryPow
declarations.signature: def binaryPow {α : Type*} [Monoid α] (a : α) : (n : ℕ) → α | 0 => 1 | n + 1 => let x
declarations.docstring: Square-and-multiply exponentiation, used to keep Lucas certificates logarithmic.

declarations.name: binaryPow_eq_pow
declarations.signature: theorem binaryPow_eq_pow {α : Type*} [Monoid α] (a : α) : ∀ n : ℕ, binaryPow a n = a ^ n

declarations.name: binaryPowMod
declarations.signature: def binaryPowMod (a m : ℕ) : (n : ℕ) → ℕ | 0 => 1 % m | n + 1 => let x
declarations.docstring: The same square-and-multiply chain with explicit natural remainders.

declarations.name: binaryPow_natCast
declarations.signature: theorem binaryPow_natCast (a m n : ℕ) : binaryPow (a : ZMod m) n = (binaryPowMod a m n : ZMod m)

declarations.name: binaryPow_zmod
declarations.signature: theorem binaryPow_zmod {m : ℕ} [NeZero m] (a : ZMod m) (n : ℕ) : binaryPow a n = (binaryPowMod a.val m n : ZMod m)

declarations.name: totient_factorBlocks
declarations.signature: theorem totient_factorBlocks (bs : List FactorBlock) (hprime : ∀ b ∈ bs, Nat.Prime b.1) (hpos : ∀ b ∈ bs, 0 < b.2) (hpair : bs.Pairwise (Function.onFun Nat.Coprime factorBlockValue)) : Nat.totient ((bs.map factorBlockValue).prod) = (bs.map factorBlockTotient).prod
declarations.docstring: A checked prime-power factor list computes Euler's totient exactly.

declarations.name: prime_dvd_factorBlocks
declarations.signature: theorem prime_dvd_factorBlocks {q : ℕ} (bs : List FactorBlock) (hq : Nat.Prime q) (hprime : ∀ b ∈ bs, Nat.Prime b.1) (hdvd : q ∣ (bs.map factorBlockValue).prod) : ∃ b ∈ bs, q = b.1
declarations.docstring: A prime divisor of a product of checked prime powers is one of their bases.

declarations.name: prime_2
declarations.docstring: A prime divisor of a product of checked prime powers is one of their bases. -/ theorem prime_dvd_factorBlocks {q : ℕ} (bs : List FactorBlock) (hq : Nat.Prime q) (hprime : ∀ b ∈ bs, Nat.Prime b.1) (hdvd : q ∣ (bs.map factorBlockValue).prod) : ∃ b ∈ bs, q = b.1 := by induction bs with | nil => simp only [List.map_nil, List.prod_nil] at hdvd have hqle : q ≤ 1 := Nat.le_of_dvd (by decide) hdvd exact (not_le_of_gt hq.one_lt hqle).elim | cons b bs ih => rw [List.map_cons, List.prod_cons] at hdvd rcases (hq.dvd_mul.mp hdvd) with hhead | htail · have hbase : q ∣ b.1 := hq.dvd_of_dvd_pow hhead exact ⟨b, by simp, (Nat.prime_dvd_prime_iff_eq hq (hprime b (by simp))).mp hbase⟩ · obtain ⟨c, hc, heq⟩ := ih (fun c hc => hprime c (by simp [hc])) htail exact ⟨c, by simp [hc], heq⟩ /-! ## Kernel-checked primality leaves

declarations.name: certifiedKill_diagonal_all_imported
declarations.signature: theorem certifiedKill_diagonal_all_imported : ∀ t ∈ diagonalPincerCertificateScales, certifiedKill (periodLcm t) (periodLcm t) (diagonalPincerKillDepth t)
declarations.docstring: All 12 distinct LCM scales in the landed `t ≤ 17` prefix fire.

declarations.name: certifiedKill_diagonal_all_imported_through_t19
declarations.signature: theorem certifiedKill_diagonal_all_imported_through_t19 : (∀ t ∈ diagonalPincerCertificateScales, certifiedKill (periodLcm t) (periodLcm t) (diagonalPincerKillDepth t)) ∧ certifiedKill (periodLcm 19) (periodLcm 19) 32
declarations.docstring: The twelve-scale base prefix plus the t=19 extension all fire.

declarations.name: certifiedKill_diagonal_all_imported_through_t23
declarations.signature: theorem certifiedKill_diagonal_all_imported_through_t23 : ((∀ t ∈ diagonalPincerCertificateScales, certifiedKill (periodLcm t) (periodLcm t) (diagonalPincerKillDepth t)) ∧ certifiedKill (periodLcm 19) (periodLcm 19) 32) ∧ certifiedKill (periodLcm 23) (periodLcm 23) 35
declarations.docstring: The thirteen-scale prefix through t=19 plus the t=23 extension all fire.

declarations.name: certifiedKill_diagonal_all_imported_through_t25
