declarations.name: and
declarations.signature: theorem and the T4 channel family. -/ theorem periodLcm_eq_lcmHeight (t : ℕ) : periodLcm t = lcmHeight t

declarations.name: periodLcm_eq_lcmHeight
declarations.signature: theorem periodLcm_eq_lcmHeight (t : ℕ) : periodLcm t = lcmHeight t
declarations.docstring: The recursive universal-period LCM and T4's finite-LCM definition are the same integer. This is the bridge between the existing diagonal completeness theorem and the T4 channel family.

declarations.name: diagonalTailDifference
declarations.docstring: The actual lcm-diagonal tail difference consumed by the established non-integrality completeness theorem.

declarations.name: explicitLcmShadowRat
declarations.docstring: The canonical exact rational explicit shadow `Q_t = H_t * numericMobiusShadow H_t`.

declarations.name: lcmShadowNumerator
declarations.docstring: The reduced numerator `a_t` of the exact explicit shadow.

declarations.name: lcmShadowDenominator
declarations.docstring: The reduced denominator `d_t` of the exact explicit shadow.

declarations.name: explicitLcmShadow
declarations.docstring: The real explicit shadow is exactly the cast of its canonical rational representative. The public real-valued API is retained for existing users.

declarations.name: lcmShadowNumerator_coprime_denominator
declarations.docstring: `a_t / d_t` is in lowest terms by construction.

declarations.name: explicitLcmShadow_eq_num_div_den
declarations.signature: theorem explicitLcmShadow_eq_num_div_den (t : ℕ) : explicitLcmShadow t = (lcmShadowNumerator t : ℝ) / (lcmShadowDenominator t : ℝ)
declarations.docstring: The real shadow displayed through its reduced numerator and denominator.

declarations.name: foreignDiagonalDefect
declarations.docstring: Everything in the actual diagonal not contained in the explicit finite Möbius shadow. This is the foreign defect, kept exact rather than assigned a sign or asymptotic model.

declarations.name: diagonalTailDifference_eq_explicit_add_foreign
declarations.signature: theorem diagonalTailDifference_eq_explicit_add_foreign (t : ℕ) : diagonalTailDifference t = explicitLcmShadow t + foreignDiagonalDefect t

declarations.name: ForeignDiagonalDefectHitsFullTarget
declarations.docstring: Predicate that the foreign defect alone hits the exact reduced target `-a_t / d_t` modulo the integer lattice.

declarations.name: lcmChannelNumerator
declarations.signature: noncomputable def lcmChannelNumerator (t p : ℕ) : ℤ
declarations.docstring: The canonical integer representative of T4's upper-half unit target.

declarations.name: lcmChannelTarget_isUnit
declarations.signature: theorem lcmChannelTarget_isUnit {t p : ℕ} (ht : 5 ≤ t) (hpMem : p ∈ upperHalfPrimes t) : IsUnit (upperHalfUnitTarget (lcmScale t) (lcmRadical t / p) p (jordanTotientTwo (lcmRadical t / p)))
declarations.docstring: The selected concrete T4 target is genuinely a unit for every upper-half prime. This prevents the pincer target from degenerating to a zero channel.

declarations.name: channelModulus
declarations.docstring: The Mersenne modulus of the `p`-channel.

declarations.name: channelComplement
declarations.signature: noncomputable def channelComplement (t p : ℕ) : ℝ
declarations.docstring: The part of the explicit shadow left after splitting off the selected `u/(2^p-1)` channel representative. No denominator claim about this complement is hidden in the definition.

declarations.name: renormalizedDefect
declarations.signature: noncomputable def renormalizedDefect (t p : ℕ) : ℝ
declarations.docstring: The foreign defect plus the non-selected explicit fibres, rescaled by the selected Mersenne modulus.

declarations.name: freshTarget
declarations.signature: noncomputable def freshTarget (t p : ℕ) : ℤ
declarations.docstring: The additive target the residual must hit modulo the selected channel.

declarations.name: RenormalizedDefectHitsFreshTarget
declarations.docstring: Exact real-lattice formulation of a modular target hit.

declarations.name: IsIntegralValue
declarations.docstring: Predicate for a real quantity to be an integer.

declarations.name: diagonal_int_iff_hit_generic
declarations.signature: private theorem diagonal_int_iff_hit_generic {D E G C : ℝ} {m : ℕ} {u : ℤ} (hm : 0 < m) (hD : D = E + G) (hE : E = (u : ℝ) / (m : ℝ) + C) : IsIntegralValue D ↔ ∃ k : ℤ, (m : ℝ) * (G + C) = (-u : ℝ) + (m : ℝ) * (k : ℝ)

declarations.name: diagonal_int_iff_foreignDiagonalDefect_hits_fullTarget
declarations.signature: theorem diagonal_int_iff_foreignDiagonalDefect_hits_fullTarget (t : ℕ) : IsIntegralValue (diagonalTailDifference t) ↔ ForeignDiagonalDefectHitsFullTarget t
declarations.docstring: The primary complement-free pincer. Integrality of the actual diagonal is exactly a hit by `foreignDiagonalDefect` alone on the full reduced target `-a_t / d_t` modulo `ℤ`.

declarations.name: diagonal_int_iff_exists_fullTarget_lattice
declarations.signature: theorem diagonal_int_iff_exists_fullTarget_lattice (t : ℕ) : IsIntegralValue (diagonalTailDifference t) ↔ ∃ k : ℤ, (lcmShadowDenominator t : ℝ) * foreignDiagonalDefect t = (-lcmShadowNumerator t : ℝ) + (lcmShadowDenominator t : ℝ) * (k : ℝ)
declarations.docstring: Expanded form of the complement-free pincer, exposing the lattice witness without an intermediate predicate.

declarations.name: diagonal_int_iff_renormalizedDefect_hits_freshTarget
declarations.signature: theorem diagonal_int_iff_renormalizedDefect_hits_freshTarget {t p : ℕ} (hp : p.Prime) : IsIntegralValue (diagonalTailDifference t) ↔ RenormalizedDefectHitsFreshTarget t p
declarations.docstring: The load-bearing channel-hit equivalence requested by the master development. For every prime channel, integrality of the actual diagonal is exactly a hit of the rescaled foreign-plus-complement residual on the negative T4 target.

declarations.name: diagonal_not_int_iff_renormalizedDefect_misses_freshTarget
declarations.signature: theorem diagonal_not_int_iff_renormalizedDefect_misses_freshTarget {t p : ℕ} (hp : p.Prime) : ¬IsIntegralValue (diagonalTailDifference t) ↔ ¬RenormalizedDefectHitsFreshTarget t p

declarations.name: foreignDiagonalDefect_misses_fullTarget_seven
declarations.docstring: The existing kernel-checked diagonal certificate at `t = 7` misses the new full reduced target. This confirms that the complement-free pincer is connected to the established finite certificate lane.

declarations.name: FullTargetAvoidanceSupply
declarations.docstring: The complement-free analytic supply: at arbitrarily large LCM heights, the foreign defect misses the complete reduced shadow target.

declarations.name: irrational_totientSeries_of_full_target_avoidance_supply
declarations.signature: theorem irrational_totientSeries_of_full_target_avoidance_supply (hsupply : FullTargetAvoidanceSupply) : Irrational (∑' n : ℕ, (Nat.totient n : ℝ) / 2 ^ n)
declarations.docstring: Full-target avoidance at arbitrarily large LCM heights closes the existing diagonal non-integrality socket and hence conditionally proves #249.

declarations.name: UpperHalfChannelAvoidanceSupply
declarations.docstring: The development's selectable upper-half-channel avoidance supply.

declarations.name: FreshPrimeChannelAvoidanceSupply
declarations.docstring: The minimal new-prime-scale avoidance supply, using `t=p`.

declarations.name: irrational_totientSeries_of_upper_half_channel_avoidance_supply
declarations.signature: theorem irrational_totientSeries_of_upper_half_channel_avoidance_supply (hsupply : UpperHalfChannelAvoidanceSupply) : Irrational (∑' n : ℕ, (Nat.totient n : ℝ) / 2 ^ n)
declarations.docstring: A selectable upper-half miss at arbitrarily large LCM heights closes the existing diagonal non-integrality socket and hence conditionally proves the totient series irrational. The hypothesis is the unresolved analytic supply.

declarations.name: irrational_totientSeries_of_fresh_prime_channel_avoidance_supply
declarations.signature: theorem irrational_totientSeries_of_fresh_prime_channel_avoidance_supply (hsupply : FreshPrimeChannelAvoidanceSupply) : Irrational (∑' n : ℕ, (Nat.totient n : ℝ) / 2 ^ n)
declarations.docstring: The one-new-prime-scale version of the same conditional closure.

declarations.name: periodLcm_eq_lcmHeight_five
declarations.docstring: The one-new-prime-scale version of the same conditional closure. -/ theorem irrational_totientSeries_of_fresh_prime_channel_avoidance_supply (hsupply : FreshPrimeChannelAvoidanceSupply) : Irrational (∑' n : ℕ, (Nat.totient n : ℝ) / 2 ^ n) := by apply irrational_totient_series_of_lcm_diagonal_nonintegrality_supply intro t₀ obtain ⟨p, hp0, _hp5, hp, hmiss⟩ := hsupply t₀ refine ⟨p, hp0, ?_⟩ change ¬IsIntegralValue (diagonalTailDifference p) exact (diagonal_not_int_iff_renormalizedDefect_misses_freshTarget hp).2 hmiss /-! Kernel boundary fixtures.

declarations.name: dyadicResidualIntNumerator
declarations.signature: def dyadicResidualIntNumerator (p r : ℤ) (c D : ℕ) : ℤ
declarations.docstring: Integer residual numerator. This is the algebraically natural core: unlike the interface natural specialization below, it needs no ordering hypothesis on the subtraction.

declarations.name: dyadicResidualIntRat
declarations.signature: def dyadicResidualIntRat (p r : ℤ) (c D : ℕ) : ℚ
declarations.docstring: Integer displayed residual normalized by `Rat.divInt`.

declarations.name: dyadicResidualIntNumerator_coprime_oddDenominator
declarations.signature: theorem dyadicResidualIntNumerator_coprime_oddDenominator (p r : ℤ) (c D : ℕ) (hDodd : Odd D) (hrD : r.natAbs.Coprime D) : (dyadicResidualIntNumerator p r c D).natAbs.Coprime D
declarations.docstring: The decisive congruence argument in `ℤ`: adding the multiple `pD` to `-2^c r` preserves coprimality with the odd modulus `D`.

declarations.name: dyadicResidualInt_denominator_sandwich
declarations.signature: theorem dyadicResidualInt_denominator_sandwich (p r : ℤ) (c D : ℕ) (hDpos : 0 < D) (hND : (dyadicResidualIntNumerator p r c D).natAbs.Coprime D) : D ∣ (dyadicResidualIntRat p r c D).den ∧ (dyadicResidualIntRat p r c D).den ∣ 2 ^ c * D
declarations.docstring: Integer form of the exact denominator sandwich. It works for positive, zero, or negative displayed residual numerators once coprimality with `D` is known.

declarations.name: dyadicResidualNumerator
declarations.signature: def dyadicResidualNumerator (p r c D : ℕ) : ℕ
declarations.docstring: Numerator left after subtracting a reduced finite prefix `r / D` from a dyadic rational `p / 2^c`. The transport theorem below assumes the subtraction is nonnegative, so natural subtraction is exact.

declarations.name: dyadicResidualRat
declarations.signature: def dyadicResidualRat (p r c D : ℕ) : ℚ
declarations.docstring: The displayed residual rational before its remaining power-of-two cancellation is normalized by `Rat`.

declarations.name: dyadicResidualNumerator_coprime_oddDenominator
declarations.signature: theorem dyadicResidualNumerator_coprime_oddDenominator (p r c D : ℕ) (hDodd : Odd D) (hrD : r.Coprime D) (hle : 2 ^ c * r ≤ p * D) : (dyadicResidualNumerator p r c D).Coprime D
declarations.docstring: Subtracting a reduced odd-denominator fraction from a dyadic fraction cannot cancel any factor of the odd denominator.

declarations.name: dyadicResidual_denominator_sandwich
declarations.signature: theorem dyadicResidual_denominator_sandwich (p r c D : ℕ) (hDpos : 0 < D) (hDodd : Odd D) (hrD : r.Coprime D) (hle : 2 ^ c * r ≤ p * D) : D ∣ (dyadicResidualRat p r c D).den ∧ (dyadicResidualRat p r c D).den ∣ 2 ^ c * D
declarations.docstring: Exact denominator sandwich. If `Q` is the normalized denominator of the residual, then `D ∣ Q ∣ 2^c D`. This is the division-free statement that the odd part inherited from the finite prefix is exactly `D`.

declarations.name: finiteErdosSum_den_odd
declarations.signature: theorem finiteErdosSum_den_odd (F : Finset ℕ) (h0 : 0 ∉ F) : Odd (finiteErdosSum F 2).den
declarations.docstring: At base two, the actual reduced denominator of every positive finite Erdős fragment is odd.

declarations.name: oddDoublingOrder_finiteErdosSum_den_eq_lcm
declarations.signature: theorem oddDoublingOrder_finiteErdosSum_den_eq_lcm (F : Finset ℕ) (hF : F.Nonempty) (h0 : 0 ∉ F) : oddDoublingOrder (finiteErdosSum F 2).den (finiteErdosSum_den_odd F h0) = F.lcm id
declarations.docstring: The existing finite noncollapse theorem rewritten in the T9 order API: the order of two modulo the actual reduced denominator is `lcm F`.

declarations.name: erdosSupportSeries_finset_eq_cast_finiteErdosSum
declarations.signature: theorem erdosSupportSeries_finset_eq_cast_finiteErdosSum (F : Finset ℕ) : erdosSupportSeries 2 (↑F : Set ℕ) = ((finiteErdosSum F 2 : ℚ) : ℝ)
declarations.docstring: A finite support series is the real cast of the audited rational `finiteErdosSum`.

declarations.name: erdosSupportSeries_diff_finset
declarations.signature: theorem erdosSupportSeries_diff_finset (A : Set ℕ) (F : Finset ℕ) (hFA : (↑F : Set ℕ) ⊆ A) : erdosSupportSeries 2 (A \ (↑F : Set ℕ)) = erdosSupportSeries 2 A - ((finiteErdosSum F 2 : ℚ) : ℝ)
declarations.docstring: Removing a finite fragment from a support subtracts exactly its audited finite rational sum. No ordering or prefix hypothesis is used.

declarations.name: finiteFragment_residual_value
declarations.signature: theorem finiteFragment_residual_value (A : Set ℕ) (F : Finset ℕ) (p r c D : ℕ) (hFA : (↑F : Set ℕ) ⊆ A) (hwhole : erdosSupportSeries 2 A = (p : ℝ) / ((2 ^ c : ℕ) : ℝ)) (hprefix : finiteErdosSum F 2 = (r : ℚ) / (D : ℚ)) (hDpos : 0 < D) (hle : 2 ^ c * r ≤ p * D) : erdosSupportSeries 2 (A \ (↑F : Set ℕ)) = (dyadicResidualNumerator p r c D : ℝ) / ((2 ^ c * D : ℕ) : ℝ)
declarations.docstring: Algebraic composition of the support-difference bridge with displayed dyadic and reduced-prefix fractions.

declarations.name: DyadicPrefixCompressionCertificate
declarations.signature: structure DyadicPrefixCompressionCertificate (A : Set ℕ) (F : Finset ℕ) (p r c D : ℕ) : Prop
declarations.docstring: The proof-bearing T12 product. It records the no-odd-cancellation transport, exact residual value, exact finite period, and the T9 analytic conclusion in one reusable certificate.

declarations.name: finiteFragment_dyadicPrefixCompression
declarations.signature: theorem finiteFragment_dyadicPrefixCompression (A : Set ℕ) (F : Finset ℕ) (p r c D : ℕ) (hF : F.Nonempty) (h0 : 0 ∉ F) (hFA : (↑F : Set ℕ) ⊆ A) (hwhole : erdosSupportSeries 2 A = (p : ℝ) / ((2 ^ c : ℕ) : ℝ)) (hprefix : finiteErdosSum F 2 = (r : ℚ) / (D : ℚ)) (hDactual : D = (finiteErdosSum F 2).den) (hrD : r.Coprime D) (hDgtOne : 1 < D) (hle : 2 ^ c * r ≤ p * D) (hNpos : 0 < dyadicResidualNumerator p r c D) (hResidual : ∃ a : ℕ, 0 < a ∧ a ∈ A \ (↑F : Set ℕ)) : DyadicPrefixCompressionCertificate A F p r c D
declarations.docstring: Generic finite-fragment T12 transport. The prefix certificate names the actual reduced denominator and numerator; the existing noncollapse theorem supplies the exact order, while T9 supplies the analytic disjunction.

declarations.name: two_three_dyadicPrefix_fixture
declarations.signature: theorem two_three_dyadicPrefix_fixture : finiteErdosSum ({2, 3} : Finset ℕ) 2 = (10 : ℚ) / 21 ∧ (finiteErdosSum ({2, 3} : Finset ℕ) 2).den = 21 ∧ oddDoublingOrder 21 (by decide : Odd 21) = 6 ∧ doublingWrapCount 1 21 6 = 2 ∧ dyadicResidualRat 1 10 1 21 = (1 : ℚ) / 42
declarations.docstring: Exact arithmetic, reduced odd denominator, order and wrap count for the `{2,3}` finite fragment.

declarations.name: five_term_dyadicPrefix_fixture
declarations.signature: theorem five_term_dyadicPrefix_fixture : finiteErdosSum ({2, 3, 6, 7, 14} : Finset ℕ) 2 = (172021 : ℚ) / 344043 ∧ (finiteErdosSum ({2, 3, 6, 7, 14} : Finset ℕ) 2).den = 344043 ∧ oddDoublingOrder 344043 (by decide : Odd 344043) = 42 ∧ doublingWrapCount 1 344043 42 = 13 ∧ dyadicResidualRat 1 172021 1 344043 = (1 : ℚ) / 688086
declarations.docstring: Exact arithmetic, reduced odd denominator, order and wrap count for the five-term finite fragment.

declarations.name: two_three_residual_reciprocalMassDivergesOrAtLeast
declarations.signature: theorem two_three_residual_reciprocalMassDivergesOrAtLeast (A : Set ℕ) (hFA : (↑({2, 3} : Finset ℕ) : Set ℕ) ⊆ A) (hwhole : erdosSupportSeries 2 A = (1 : ℝ) / 2) (hResidual : ∃ a : ℕ, 0 < a ∧ a ∈ A \ (↑({2, 3} : Finset ℕ) : Set ℕ)) : ReciprocalMassDivergesOrAtLeast (A \ (↑({2, 3} : Finset ℕ) : Set ℕ)) (1 / 3 : ℝ)
declarations.docstring: Conditional wrapper for `F={2,3}`. Any support of dyadic value `1/2` containing this fragment and a positive residual witness inherits the exact lower bound `1/3` (or divergent reciprocal mass).

declarations.name: five_term_residual_reciprocalMassDivergesOrAtLeast
declarations.signature: theorem five_term_residual_reciprocalMassDivergesOrAtLeast (A : Set ℕ) (hFA : (↑({2, 3, 6, 7, 14} : Finset ℕ) : Set ℕ) ⊆ A) (hwhole : erdosSupportSeries 2 A = (1 : ℝ) / 2) (hResidual : ∃ a : ℕ, 0 < a ∧ a ∈ A \ (↑({2, 3, 6, 7, 14} : Finset ℕ) : Set ℕ)) : ReciprocalMassDivergesOrAtLeast (A \ (↑({2, 3, 6, 7, 14} : Finset ℕ) : Set ℕ)) (13 / 42 : ℝ)
declarations.docstring: Conditional wrapper for `F={2,3,6,7,14}` with the exact `13/42` residual wrap ratio.

declarations.name: squarefree_dvd_mul_iff_of_dvd
declarations.signature: theorem squarefree_dvd_mul_iff_of_dvd {p H d : ℕ} (hpH : p ∣ H) (hsq : Squarefree d) : d ∣ p * H ↔ d ∣ H
declarations.docstring: If `p` is already a divisor of `H`, multiplication by `p` adds no squarefree divisor channels. This is stronger than the prime-power-jump specialization: the exponent and primality of `p` are not needed.

declarations.name: squarefree_dvd_prime_mul_iff_of_prime_dvd
declarations.signature: theorem squarefree_dvd_prime_mul_iff_of_prime_dvd {p H d : ℕ} (_hp : p.Prime) (hpH : p ∣ H) (hsq : Squarefree d) : d ∣ p * H ↔ d ∣ H
declarations.docstring: Prime-form API used by the exponent-only LCM jump.

declarations.name: mobius_ne_zero_dvd_mul_iff_of_dvd
declarations.signature: theorem mobius_ne_zero_dvd_mul_iff_of_dvd {p H d : ℕ} (hpH : p ∣ H) (hmu : ArithmeticFunction.moebius d ≠ 0) : d ∣ p * H ↔ d ∣ H
declarations.docstring: The nonzero Möbius support is unchanged across `H -> pH` once `p ∣ H`.

declarations.name: transportResidueOffset
declarations.docstring: Least positive shift sending `N` to a multiple of `d`.

declarations.name: transportResidueKernel
declarations.signature: noncomputable def transportResidueKernel (d N : ℕ) : ℝ
declarations.docstring: Exact Möbius residue kernel, stated locally so this disjoint transport owner can be validated independently of adjacent projection files.

declarations.name: transportResidueIncrement
declarations.signature: noncomputable def transportResidueIncrement (d H : ℕ) : ℝ
declarations.docstring: Diagonal increment of the exact transport kernel.

declarations.name: residueTransportDefect
declarations.signature: noncomputable def residueTransportDefect (d H p : ℕ) : ℝ
declarations.docstring: Channelwise first-order transport of the diagonal residue increment.

declarations.name: transportResidueIncrement_of_dvd
declarations.signature: theorem transportResidueIncrement_of_dvd {d H : ℕ} (hd : 0 < d) (hdvd : d ∣ H) : transportResidueIncrement d H = ((ArithmeticFunction.moebius d : ℤ) : ℝ) * (H : ℝ) / ((d : ℝ) * ((2 : ℝ) ^ d - 1))
declarations.docstring: A divisor channel has the standard linear form.

declarations.name: residueTransportDefect_eq_zero_of_dvd
declarations.signature: theorem residueTransportDefect_eq_zero_of_dvd {d H p : ℕ} (hd : 0 < d) (hdH : d ∣ H) : residueTransportDefect d H p = 0
declarations.docstring: Every old divisor channel is annihilated exactly by first-order transport.

declarations.name: residueSlope
declarations.signature: noncomputable def residueSlope (d N : ℕ) : ℝ
declarations.docstring: Periodic coefficient of the linear-in-`N` part of the residue kernel.

declarations.name: residueIntercept
declarations.signature: noncomputable def residueIntercept (d N : ℕ) : ℝ
declarations.docstring: Periodic intercept of the residue kernel.

declarations.name: transportResidueKernel_eq_affineState
declarations.signature: theorem transportResidueKernel_eq_affineState {d N : ℕ} (hd : 0 < d) : transportResidueKernel d N = (N : ℝ) * residueSlope d N + residueIntercept d N
declarations.docstring: The exact kernel is affine in the scale coordinate and periodic in the residue coordinate. This is the state omitted by a residue-permutation-only transport matrix.

declarations.name: residueSlope_add_period
declarations.signature: theorem residueSlope_add_period {d N : ℕ} (_hd : 0 < d) : residueSlope d (N + d) = residueSlope d N
declarations.docstring: The slope state is periodic modulo the channel index.

declarations.name: residueIntercept_add_period
declarations.signature: theorem residueIntercept_add_period {d N : ℕ} (_hd : 0 < d) : residueIntercept d (N + d) = residueIntercept d N
declarations.docstring: The intercept state is periodic modulo the channel index.

declarations.name: threeTransportSlopePart
declarations.signature: noncomputable def threeTransportSlopePart (d H : ℕ) : ℝ
declarations.docstring: Linear block of the four-vertex `H -> 3H` transport.

declarations.name: threeTransportInterceptPart
declarations.signature: noncomputable def threeTransportInterceptPart (d H : ℕ) : ℝ
declarations.docstring: Constant block of the four-vertex `H -> 3H` transport.

declarations.name: residueTransportDefect_three_eq_affineState
declarations.signature: theorem residueTransportDefect_three_eq_affineState {d H : ℕ} (hd : 0 < d) : residueTransportDefect d H 3 = threeTransportSlopePart d H + threeTransportInterceptPart d H
declarations.docstring: Exact first-order affine transfer. Both blocks are present; invertibility of a constant residue-permutation block alone cannot prove this expression is nonzero or prevent cancellation between channel indices.

declarations.name: threeTransportTailCommutator
declarations.docstring: The actual four-vertex transport on the totient tail.

declarations.name: ExponentOnlyThreeTransportSupply
declarations.docstring: Exact unresolved supply on the fixed `3^a` LCM tower.

declarations.name: irrational_totient_series_of_exponentOnlyThreeTransportSupply
declarations.signature: theorem irrational_totient_series_of_exponentOnlyThreeTransportSupply (hsupply : ExponentOnlyThreeTransportSupply) : Irrational (∑' n : ℕ, (Nat.totient n : ℝ) / 2 ^ n)
declarations.docstring: The fixed-three transport supply contradicts rational LCM-cone flatness and therefore proves irrationality of the totient series.

declarations.name: windowFirstCos
declarations.signature: noncomputable def windowFirstCos (h N L : ℕ) : ℝ
declarations.docstring: Real part of the first additive character of the endpoint discrepancy modulo `2^L`.

declarations.name: windowFirstCos_gt_of_not_certifiedKill
declarations.signature: private lemma windowFirstCos_gt_of_not_certifiedKill {h X L N : ℕ} (hN : N < 2 * X) (hroom : 16 * (2 * X + h + L + 2) ≤ 2 ^ L) (hnot : ¬ certifiedKill h N L) : (9 / 10 : ℝ) < windowFirstCos h N L

declarations.name: exists_certifiedKill_of_first_harmonic_gap_subset
declarations.signature: theorem exists_certifiedKill_of_first_harmonic_gap_subset {h X L : ℕ} (T : Finset ℕ) (hTlt : ∀ N ∈ T, N < 2 * X) (hTne : T.Nonempty) (hroom : 16 * (2 * X + h + L + 2) ≤ 2 ^ L) (hgap : (∑ N ∈ T, windowFirstCos h N L) ≤ (9 / 10 : ℝ) * T.card) : ∃ N ∈ T, certifiedKill h N L
declarations.docstring: A first-harmonic saving on any nonempty finite subset of a dyadic block forces a certificate in that subset. This is the subset consumer needed by supplier-fibre arguments; no density or partition hypothesis is used.

declarations.name: exists_certifiedKill_of_first_harmonic_gap
declarations.signature: theorem exists_certifiedKill_of_first_harmonic_gap {h X L : ℕ} (hX : 0 < X) (hroom : 16 * (2 * X + h + L + 2) ≤ 2 ^ L) (hgap : (∑ N ∈ Finset.Ico X (2 * X), windowFirstCos h N L) ≤ (9 / 10 : ℝ) * X) : ∃ N ∈ Finset.Ico X (2 * X), certifiedKill h N L
declarations.docstring: A constant-saving first-harmonic gap on one dyadic block forces a finite certificate somewhere in that block. Establishing such gaps cofinally is the analytic input still required for #249.

declarations.name: windowFirstAngle
declarations.signature: noncomputable def windowFirstAngle (h N L : ℕ) : ℝ
declarations.docstring: The residue angle used by the first additive character.

declarations.name: windowFirstExp
declarations.signature: noncomputable def windowFirstExp (h N L : ℕ) : ℂ
declarations.docstring: The complex first additive character of the endpoint discrepancy.

declarations.name: windowFirstExp_re
declarations.signature: theorem windowFirstExp_re (h N L : ℕ) : (windowFirstExp h N L).re = windowFirstCos h N L

declarations.name: norm_windowFirstExp
declarations.signature: theorem norm_windowFirstExp (h N L : ℕ) : ‖windowFirstExp h N L‖ = 1

declarations.name: exists_certifiedKill_of_first_harmonic_norm_gap
declarations.signature: theorem exists_certifiedKill_of_first_harmonic_norm_gap {h X L : ℕ} (hX : 0 < X) (hroom : 16 * (2 * X + h + L + 2) ≤ 2 ^ L) (hgap : ‖∑ N ∈ Finset.Ico X (2 * X), windowFirstExp h N L‖ ≤ (21 / 25 : ℝ) * X) : ∃ N ∈ Finset.Ico X (2 * X), certifiedKill h N L
declarations.docstring: A complex first-harmonic saving is stronger than the real-part saving used by the certificate theorem.

declarations.name: DTWFirstHarmonicNormGap
declarations.docstring: Cofinal complex first-harmonic norm saving. This is a checked direct analytic interface, stronger than the already-landed real-part interface.

declarations.name: irrational_totient_series_of_first_harmonic_norm_gap
declarations.signature: theorem irrational_totient_series_of_first_harmonic_norm_gap (hgap : DTWFirstHarmonicNormGap) : Irrational (∑' n : ℕ, (Nat.totient n : ℝ) / 2 ^ n)
declarations.docstring: The complex norm gap composes all the way to irrationality.

declarations.name: pivotOffset
declarations.docstring: Offset of the pivot argument `N + (L-s+1)`.

declarations.name: overlap_weight_identity
declarations.signature: theorem overlap_weight_identity {h L t : ℕ} (hht : h ≤ t) (htL : t < L) : (2 : ℤ) ^ (L - 1 - (t - h)) - (2 : ℤ) ^ (L - 1 - t) = ((2 : ℤ) ^ h - 1) * (2 : ℤ) ^ (L - 1 - t)
declarations.docstring: The two occurrences of an overlap totient value have the advertised net integer weight. Dividing this identity by `2^L` gives the coefficient `(2^h-1)/2^(t+1)` from the paper calculation.

declarations.name: pivotPrimeAngle
declarations.signature: noncomputable def pivotPrimeAngle (h L s m p : ℕ) : ℝ
declarations.docstring: Real angle of the proposed one-dimensional pivot phase.

declarations.name: pivotPrimePhase
declarations.signature: noncomputable def pivotPrimePhase (h L s m p : ℕ) : ℂ
declarations.docstring: The proposed one-dimensional phase after the identity `φ(mp)=φ(m)(p-1)` has been applied at the overlap pivot.

declarations.name: norm_pivotPrimePhase
declarations.signature: theorem norm_pivotPrimePhase (h L s m p : ℕ) : ‖pivotPrimePhase h L s m p‖ = 1

declarations.name: pivotResidualWeight
declarations.signature: noncomputable def pivotResidualWeight (h N L s m p : ℕ) : ℂ
declarations.docstring: Removing the pivot by division. Division rather than an informal "independence" claim makes the residual definition exact.

declarations.name: windowFirstExp_eq_residual_mul_pivot
declarations.signature: theorem windowFirstExp_eq_residual_mul_pivot (h N L s m p : ℕ) : windowFirstExp h N L = pivotResidualWeight h N L s m p * pivotPrimePhase h L s m p
declarations.docstring: The pivot and its residual reconstruct the original phase exactly.

declarations.name: totient_mul_prime_of_not_dvd
declarations.signature: theorem totient_mul_prime_of_not_dvd {m p : ℕ} (hp : p.Prime) (hpm : ¬p ∣ m) : Nat.totient (m * p) = Nat.totient m * (p - 1)
declarations.docstring: The elementary arithmetic input used at a clean prime pivot.

declarations.name: not_dvd_of_pos_of_lt
declarations.signature: theorem not_dvd_of_pos_of_lt {m p : ℕ} (hm : 0 < m) (hmp : m < p) : ¬p ∣ m
declarations.docstring: A factor larger than a positive cofactor cannot divide that cofactor.

declarations.name: sum_mul_eq_centered_add_mean
declarations.signature: theorem sum_mul_eq_centered_add_mean {α : Type*} [DecidableEq α] (P : Finset α) (W z : α → ℂ) (zbar : ℂ) : (∑ p ∈ P, W p * z p) = (∑ p ∈ P, W p * (z p - zbar)) + zbar * ∑ p ∈ P, W p
declarations.docstring: Exact centring identity on one finite supplier fibre.

declarations.name: pivotArgument
declarations.signature: def pivotArgument (N L s : ℕ) : ℕ
declarations.docstring: Actual integer at the selected overlap position.

declarations.name: pivotPrime
declarations.signature: def pivotPrime (N L s : ℕ) : ℕ
declarations.docstring: Canonical large-prime choice: maximum-fold the finite prime-factor set. The fallback value `1` is rejected by `pivotSupplier`.

declarations.name: pivotCofactor
declarations.signature: def pivotCofactor (N L s : ℕ) : ℕ

declarations.name: pivotPrime_eq_pivotArgument_of_prime
declarations.signature: theorem pivotPrime_eq_pivotArgument_of_prime {N L s : ℕ} (hp : (pivotArgument N L s).Prime) : pivotPrime N L s = pivotArgument N L s
declarations.docstring: On a prime pivot argument the canonical maximum-prime-factor selector is the argument itself.

declarations.name: pivotCofactor_eq_one_of_pivotArgument_prime
declarations.signature: theorem pivotCofactor_eq_one_of_pivotArgument_prime {N L s : ℕ} (hp : (pivotArgument N L s).Prime) : pivotCofactor N L s = 1
declarations.docstring: A prime pivot argument has canonical cofactor one.

declarations.name: pivotSupplier
declarations.signature: def pivotSupplier (X L s N : ℕ) : Prop
declarations.docstring: Decidable clean-supplier predicate. The explicit multiplication receipt avoids relying on division simplification outside this definition.

declarations.name: pivotSupplierBases
declarations.signature: def pivotSupplierBases (X L s : ℕ) : Finset ℕ

declarations.name: pivotGoodCofactor
declarations.signature: def pivotGoodCofactor (L s N : ℕ) (η : ℝ) : Prop

declarations.name: pivotGoodBases
declarations.signature: def pivotGoodBases (X L s : ℕ) (η : ℝ) : Finset ℕ

declarations.name: pivotBadBases
declarations.signature: def pivotBadBases (X L s : ℕ) (η : ℝ) : Finset ℕ

declarations.name: pivotNonSupplierBases
declarations.signature: def pivotNonSupplierBases (X L s : ℕ) : Finset ℕ

declarations.name: pivotPhaseAt
declarations.signature: noncomputable def pivotPhaseAt (h N L s : ℕ) : ℂ

declarations.name: pivotResidualAt
declarations.signature: noncomputable def pivotResidualAt (h N L s : ℕ) : ℂ

declarations.name: windowFirstExp_eq_pivotResidualAt_mul_phase
declarations.signature: theorem windowFirstExp_eq_pivotResidualAt_mul_phase (h N L s : ℕ) : windowFirstExp h N L = pivotResidualAt h N L s * pivotPhaseAt h N L s

declarations.name: totient_pivotArgument_eq_mul
declarations.signature: theorem totient_pivotArgument_eq_mul {X L s N : ℕ} (hN : N ∈ pivotSupplierBases X L s) : Nat.totient (pivotArgument N L s) = Nat.totient (pivotCofactor N L s) * (pivotPrime N L s - 1)
declarations.docstring: The canonical clean supplier really gives the advertised totient factorisation at the pivot argument.

declarations.name: pivotFiber
declarations.signature: def pivotFiber (X L s m : ℕ) : Finset ℕ

declarations.name: mem_pivotFiber_one_overlap_iff
declarations.signature: theorem mem_pivotFiber_one_overlap_iff {h X L N : ℕ} (hX : 4 ≤ X) (hL : h ≤ L) : N ∈ pivotFiber X L (L - h) 1 ↔ X ≤ N ∧ N < 2 * X ∧ (N + h + 1).Prime
declarations.docstring: The canonical natural fibre `m=1, s=L-h` is literally the shifted dyadic interval of primes. This is a membership equality for the actual largest-prime supplier set, not a separately sampled prime surrogate.

declarations.name: pivotFiberMean
declarations.signature: noncomputable def pivotFiberMean (h X L s m : ℕ) : ℂ

declarations.name: pivotCenteredCorrelation
declarations.signature: noncomputable def pivotCenteredCorrelation (h X L s : ℕ) (η : ℝ) : ℂ

declarations.name: pivotFiberMeanContribution
declarations.signature: noncomputable def pivotFiberMeanContribution (h X L s : ℕ) (η : ℝ) : ℂ

declarations.name: pivotBadContribution
declarations.signature: noncomputable def pivotBadContribution (h X L s : ℕ) (η : ℝ) : ℂ

declarations.name: pivotNonSupplierContribution
declarations.signature: noncomputable def pivotNonSupplierContribution (h X L s : ℕ) : ℂ

declarations.name: pivot_good_split
declarations.signature: private theorem pivot_good_split (h X L s : ℕ) (η : ℝ) : (∑ N ∈ pivotGoodBases X L s η, windowFirstExp h N L) = pivotCenteredCorrelation h X L s η + pivotFiberMeanContribution h X L s η

declarations.name: windowFirstExp_sum_eq_pivot_decomposition
declarations.signature: theorem windowFirstExp_sum_eq_pivot_decomposition (h X L s : ℕ) (η : ℝ) : (∑ N ∈ Finset.Ico X (2 * X), windowFirstExp h N L) = pivotCenteredCorrelation h X L s η + pivotFiberMeanContribution h X L s η + pivotBadContribution h X L s η + pivotNonSupplierContribution h X L s
declarations.docstring: Exact decomposition into the four actual, evaluator-visible terms.

declarations.name: PivotBudgetAt
declarations.signature: def PivotBudgetAt (h X L s : ℕ) (η : ℝ) : Prop
declarations.docstring: Unlike the earlier `X/2` complex-norm proposal, the consumer only needs a one-sided real bound. With the same other budgets, `14/25` is sufficient.

declarations.name: first_harmonic_gap_of_pivotBudgetAt
declarations.signature: theorem first_harmonic_gap_of_pivotBudgetAt {h X L s : ℕ} {η : ℝ} (hbudget : PivotBudgetAt h X L s η) : (∑ N ∈ Finset.Ico X (2 * X), windowFirstCos h N L) ≤ (9 / 10 : ℝ) * X

declarations.name: DTWPivotResidualDecorrelation
declarations.docstring: The genuinely narrower cofinal socket: all four terms are fixed finite sums over canonical largest-prime supplier fibres. The remaining estimate is only `Re centred ≤ 14X/25`, not `‖centred‖ ≤ X/2`.

declarations.name: irrational_totient_series_of_pivotResidualDecorrelation
declarations.signature: theorem irrational_totient_series_of_pivotResidualDecorrelation (hmix : DTWPivotResidualDecorrelation) : Irrational (∑' n : ℕ, (Nat.totient n : ℝ) / 2 ^ n)

declarations.name: controlling
declarations.signature: theorem controlling the discarded complement; explicit-shadow fibres alone cannot supply it. -/ namespace Erdos249257.FullTargetPrimeAdjunctionNoGo open scoped BigOperators open TotientTailPeriodKiller open RadicalMobiusShadow open DiagonalPincerDecomposition /-- The #249 constant, named locally for the generic-scale transport. -/ noncomputable def totientSeries : ℝ

declarations.name: totientSeries
declarations.docstring: The #249 constant, named locally for the generic-scale transport.

declarations.name: diagonalCoefficient
declarations.docstring: The coefficient of the #249 constant in `R_(2H) - R_H`.

declarations.name: scaleDiagonalTailDifference
declarations.docstring: The actual diagonal tail difference at an arbitrary scale.

declarations.name: diagonalPrefixCorrection
declarations.docstring: The integral prefix correction in the generic diagonal formula.

declarations.name: scaleDiagonalTailDifference_eq
declarations.signature: theorem scaleDiagonalTailDifference_eq (H : ℕ) : scaleDiagonalTailDifference H = (diagonalCoefficient H : ℝ) * totientSeries + (diagonalPrefixCorrection H : ℝ)
declarations.docstring: Exact generic-scale diagonal formula `D_H = C_H S + Z_H`.

declarations.name: diagonalScaleQuotient
declarations.docstring: The exact natural multiplier carrying the coefficient at `H` to `kH`.

declarations.name: diagonalCoefficient_pos
declarations.signature: theorem diagonalCoefficient_pos {H : ℕ} (hH : 0 < H) : 0 < diagonalCoefficient H

declarations.name: diagonalCoefficient_mul
declarations.signature: theorem diagonalCoefficient_mul (H k : ℕ) (hk : 0 < k) : diagonalCoefficient (k * H) = diagonalScaleQuotient H k * diagonalCoefficient H
declarations.docstring: `C_(kH) = Q_k(H) C_H`, with the quotient displayed as a geometric sum.

declarations.name: diagonalTransportCorrection
declarations.docstring: The integer correction in affine diagonal transport.

declarations.name: scaleDiagonalTailDifference_mul
declarations.signature: theorem scaleDiagonalTailDifference_mul (H k : ℕ) (hk : 0 < k) : scaleDiagonalTailDifference (k * H) = (diagonalScaleQuotient H k : ℝ) * scaleDiagonalTailDifference H + (diagonalTransportCorrection H k : ℝ)
declarations.docstring: Exact affine transport of the actual diagonal along every positive multiplicative scale ray.

declarations.name: scaleExplicitShadowRat
declarations.docstring: The exact rational explicit shadow at an arbitrary scale.

declarations.name: scaleForeignDefect
declarations.docstring: The actual part of the generic diagonal left after removing the explicit finite Möbius shadow.

declarations.name: ScaleFullTargetHit
declarations.docstring: The full reduced-rational pincer target at a generic scale.

declarations.name: scaleFullTargetHit_iff_integral
declarations.signature: theorem scaleFullTargetHit_iff_integral (H : ℕ) : ScaleFullTargetHit H ↔ IsIntegralValue (scaleDiagonalTailDifference H)
declarations.docstring: The generic full-target pincer is exactly integrality of the actual diagonal; its lattice witness is not an independent state variable.

declarations.name: scaleFullTarget_miss_of_enclosure
declarations.signature: theorem scaleFullTarget_miss_of_enclosure {H : ℕ} {q ε : ℝ} (hbound : |scaleDiagonalTailDifference H - q| ≤ ε) (hsep : ∀ z : ℤ, ε < |q - (z : ℝ)|) : ¬ScaleFullTargetHit H
declarations.docstring: A producer-independent enclosure consumer for the actual diagonal. Any centre separated from every integer by more than a proved error radius forces a miss of the full reduced target.

declarations.name: scaleFullTargetHit_mul
declarations.signature: theorem scaleFullTargetHit_mul {H k : ℕ} (hk : 0 < k) (hHit : ScaleFullTargetHit H) : ScaleFullTargetHit (k * H)
declarations.docstring: Full-target hits propagate upward along divisibility rays.

declarations.name: fullTarget_primeAdjunction_diamond_iff_root
declarations.signature: theorem fullTarget_primeAdjunction_diamond_iff_root (H p q : ℕ) (hp : 0 < p) (hq : 0 < q) : (ScaleFullTargetHit H ∧ ScaleFullTargetHit (p * H) ∧ ScaleFullTargetHit (q * H) ∧ ScaleFullTargetHit (p * q * H)) ↔ ScaleFullTargetHit H
declarations.docstring: The four-hit prime-adjunction condition contains exactly the information of the bottom hit. No primality or freshness assumption is needed.

declarations.name: foreignTransportCorrection
declarations.signature: noncomputable def foreignTransportCorrection (H k : ℕ) : ℝ
declarations.docstring: The inhomogeneous foreign correction is an endpoint coboundary.

declarations.name: scaleForeignDefect_mul
declarations.signature: theorem scaleForeignDefect_mul (H k : ℕ) (hk : 0 < k) : scaleForeignDefect (k * H) = (diagonalScaleQuotient H k : ℝ) * scaleForeignDefect H + foreignTransportCorrection H k + (diagonalTransportCorrection H k : ℝ)
declarations.docstring: First theorem that transports the actual foreign defect rather than only the explicit polynomial shadow.

declarations.name: diagonalScaleQuotient_comp
declarations.signature: theorem diagonalScaleQuotient_comp (H k l : ℕ) (hH : 0 < H) (hk : 0 < k) (hl : 0 < l) : diagonalScaleQuotient (k * H) l * diagonalScaleQuotient H k = diagonalScaleQuotient H (l * k)
declarations.docstring: Scale quotients compose, which is the algebra behind zero curvature.

declarations.name: foreignTransportCorrection_primeAdjunction_flat
declarations.signature: theorem foreignTransportCorrection_primeAdjunction_flat (H p q : ℕ) (hH : 0 < H) (hp : 0 < p) (hq : 0 < q) : (diagonalScaleQuotient (p * H) q : ℝ) * foreignTransportCorrection H p + foreignTransportCorrection (p * H) q = (diagonalScaleQuotient (q * H) p : ℝ) * foreignTransportCorrection H q + foreignTransportCorrection (q * H) p
declarations.docstring: The complete foreign correction has exactly zero `(p,q)` diamond curvature.

declarations.name: IsFirstGapFailure
declarations.signature: def IsFirstGapFailure (V K H qstar : ℕ) : Prop
declarations.docstring: `qstar` is the exact first displayed denominator at which a gap certificate fails. This is the small checker-facing contract emitted by the untrusted Stern--Brocot producer: all smaller positive denominators pass, while `qstar` itself fails.

declarations.name: farey_gap
declarations.signature: theorem farey_gap {a b c d r s : ℤ} (hb : 0 < b) (hd : 0 < d) (hdet : b * c - a * d = 1) (hleft : a * s < r * b) (hright : r * d < c * s) : b + d ≤ s
declarations.docstring: **Mediant / Farey gap lemma.** If `a/b < r/s < c/d` (with `b, d > 0`) and the neighbours are unimodular (`b*c - a*d = 1`), then `b + d ≤ s`. Elementary: `s = b·(c·s - r·d) + d·(r·b - a·s)` and both bracketed factors are `≥ 1`.

declarations.name: det_one
declarations.docstring: Unimodularity of the two Farey neighbours: `b*c - a*d = 1`.

declarations.name: left_in
declarations.docstring: Left neighbour is strictly below the left endpoint `(V'-123)/2^120`: `a/b < (V'-123)/2^120`, i.e. `a * 2^120 < (V'-123) * b`.

declarations.name: right_in
declarations.docstring: Right endpoint `V'/2^120` is `≤` the right neighbour `c/d`: `V' * d ≤ c * 2^120`.

declarations.name: gap_check_window_1_120_le_248672326362367909
declarations.docstring: **The bound.** Every denominator `q` with `0 < q ≤ 248672326362367909` satisfies the wave-17 gap certificate for the `(N=1, K=120)` window.

declarations.name: gap_check_window_1_120_first_failure
declarations.docstring: The Farey lower bound at `K = 120` is sharp: the mediant denominator `248672326362367910` is the exact first displayed denominator for which the gap certificate fails.

declarations.name: det_one_240
declarations.docstring: Unimodularity of the `K = 240` Farey neighbours: `b*c - a*d = 1`.

declarations.name: left_in_240
declarations.docstring: Left neighbour is strictly below the left endpoint `(V'-243)/2^240`.

declarations.name: right_in_240
declarations.docstring: Right endpoint `V'/2^240` is `≤` the right neighbour `c/d`.

declarations.name: gap_check_window_1_240_le_79639646646701375323355774875831053
declarations.docstring: **The `K = 240` bound.** Every denominator `q` with `0 < q ≤ 79639646646701375323355774875831053` satisfies the gap certificate for the `(N=1, K=240)` window.

declarations.name: gap_check_window_1_240_first_failure
declarations.docstring: The Farey lower bound at `K = 240` is sharp: the mediant denominator `79639646646701375323355774875831054` is the exact first displayed denominator for which the gap certificate fails.

declarations.name: tsum_pnat_pred_mul_geometric
declarations.signature: private lemma tsum_pnat_pred_mul_geometric {x : ℝ} (hx0 : 0 ≤ x) (hx1 : x < 1) : ∑' m : ℕ+, (((m : ℕ) : ℝ) - 1) * x ^ (m : ℕ) = (x / (1 - x)) ^ 2
declarations.docstring: `∑_{m≥1} (m-1)·xᵐ = (x/(1-x))²` — the pure square of the geometric tail, with the `m = 1` term vanishing. This is the second-weight profile that turns the wave-18 pair engine into the squared-kernel transfer.

declarations.name: tsum_lambert_linear_weight_sq_pure
declarations.signature: theorem tsum_lambert_linear_weight_sq_pure (w : ℕ → ℝ) (hw : ∀ d : ℕ, 0 < d → |w d| ≤ (d : ℝ)) {r : ℝ} (hr0 : 0 ≤ r) (hr1 : r < 1) : ∑' d : ℕ+, w (d : ℕ) * (r ^ (d : ℕ) / (1 - r ^ (d : ℕ))) ^ 2 = ∑' n : ℕ+, (∑ e ∈ (n : ℕ).divisors, w e * ((((n : ℕ) / e : ℕ) : ℝ) - 1)) * r ^ (n : ℕ)
declarations.docstring: **Signed linear-growth PURE-squared-Lambert identity**: `∑_d w(d)·(rᵈ/(1-rᵈ))² = ∑_n (∑_{e∣n} w(e)·(n/e - 1))·rⁿ` for `|w(d)| ≤ d`. The divisor coefficient is `(w * Id)(n) - (w * ζ)(n)`: one theorem carries the whole level-2 ladder. Rides wave 18's `tsum_lambert_pair_regroup` with second weight `v(m) = m - 1`.

declarations.name: tsum_one_lambert_sq_pure
declarations.signature: theorem tsum_one_lambert_sq_pure {r : ℝ} (hr0 : 0 ≤ r) (hr1 : r < 1) : ∑' d : ℕ+, (r ^ (d : ℕ) / (1 - r ^ (d : ℕ))) ^ 2 = ∑' n : ℕ+, ((∑ e ∈ (n : ℕ).divisors, (((n : ℕ) / e : ℕ) : ℝ)) - ((n : ℕ).divisors.card : ℝ)) * r ^ (n : ℕ)
declarations.docstring: **`L₂(1)`-rung, Lambert form**: `∑_d (rᵈ/(1-rᵈ))² = ∑_n (σ(n) - τ(n))·rⁿ`. The coefficient is spelled as its divisor sums: `σ(n) = ∑_{e∣n} n/e` and `τ(n) = #divisors(n)`.

declarations.name: tsum_totient_lambert_sq_pure
declarations.signature: theorem tsum_totient_lambert_sq_pure {r : ℝ} (hr0 : 0 ≤ r) (hr1 : r < 1) : ∑' d : ℕ+, (Nat.totient (d : ℕ) : ℝ) * (r ^ (d : ℕ) / (1 - r ^ (d : ℕ))) ^ 2 = ∑' n : ℕ+, ((∑ e ∈ (n : ℕ).divisors, (Nat.totient e : ℝ) * (((n : ℕ) / e : ℕ) : ℝ)) - ((n : ℕ) : ℝ)) * r ^ (n : ℕ)
declarations.docstring: **`L₂(φ)`-rung, Lambert form**: `∑_d φ(d)·(rᵈ/(1-rᵈ))² = ∑_n (P(n) - n)·rⁿ`, where `P(n) = ∑_{e∣n} φ(e)·(n/e)` is Pillai's gcd-sum function (`φ * Id`); the subtracted `n` is `φ * ζ = Id`.

declarations.name: half_pow_sq_term
declarations.signature: private lemma half_pow_sq_term' (d : ℕ) (hd : 0 < d) : (((1 : ℝ) / 2) ^ d / (1 - ((1 : ℝ) / 2) ^ d)) ^ 2 = 1 / ((2 : ℝ) ^ d - 1) ^ 2

declarations.name: tsum_one_div_mersenne_sq_eq_sigma_sub_tau_series
declarations.signature: theorem tsum_one_div_mersenne_sq_eq_sigma_sub_tau_series : ∑' d : ℕ+, 1 / ((2 : ℝ) ^ (d : ℕ) - 1) ^ 2 = ∑' n : ℕ+, ((∑ e ∈ (n : ℕ).divisors, (((n : ℕ) / e : ℕ) : ℝ)) - ((n : ℕ).divisors.card : ℝ)) * ((1 : ℝ) / 2) ^ (n : ℕ)
declarations.docstring: **The q-zeta anchor of the level-2 ladder**: `∑_{d≥1} 1/(2ᵈ-1)² = ∑_{n≥1} (σ(n) - τ(n))·(1/2)ⁿ`, exactly. Probability reading: `E[τ(gcd(X,Y))] = ∑_d P(d ∣ gcd)`. In q-zeta terms this value is `ζ_q(2) - ζ_q(1)` at `q = 1/2` (Jouhet–Mosaki convention), irrational by the Postelmans–Van Assche q-Padé theorem — cited, NOT formalised; what is machine-checked here is the identity.

declarations.name: tsum_totient_div_mersenne_sq_eq_gcd_moment_series
declarations.signature: theorem tsum_totient_div_mersenne_sq_eq_gcd_moment_series : ∑' d : ℕ+, (Nat.totient (d : ℕ) : ℝ) / ((2 : ℝ) ^ (d : ℕ) - 1) ^ 2 = ∑' n : ℕ+, ((∑ e ∈ (n : ℕ).divisors, (Nat.totient e : ℝ) * (((n : ℕ) / e : ℕ) : ℝ)) - ((n : ℕ) : ℝ)) * ((1 : ℝ) / 2) ^ (n : ℕ)
declarations.docstring: **The first gcd moment**: `∑_{d≥1} φ(d)/(2ᵈ-1)² = ∑_{n≥1} (P(n) - n)·(1/2)ⁿ` with `P` Pillai's gcd-sum function. Probability reading: `E[gcd(X,Y)] = ∑_d φ(d)·P(d ∣ gcd)` — the expected gcd of two independent fair-coin waiting times, as a totient-weighted squared-Mersenne series.

declarations.name: tsum_pow_succ_geometric
declarations.signature: private lemma tsum_pow_succ_geometric' {x : ℝ} (hx0 : 0 ≤ x) (hx1 : x < 1) : ∑' g : ℕ, x ^ (g + 1) = x / (1 - x)
declarations.docstring: **The first gcd moment**: `∑_{d≥1} φ(d)/(2ᵈ-1)² = ∑_{n≥1} (P(n) - n)·(1/2)ⁿ` with `P` Pillai's gcd-sum function. Probability reading: `E[gcd(X,Y)] = ∑_d φ(d)·P(d ∣ gcd)` — the expected gcd of two independent fair-coin waiting times, as a totient-weighted squared-Mersenne series. -/ theorem tsum_totient_div_mersenne_sq_eq_gcd_moment_series : ∑' d : ℕ+, (Nat.totient (d : ℕ) : ℝ) / ((2 : ℝ) ^ (d : ℕ) - 1) ^ 2 = ∑' n : ℕ+, ((∑ e ∈ (n : ℕ).divisors, (Nat.totient e : ℝ) * (((n : ℕ) / e : ℕ) : ℝ)) - ((n : ℕ) : ℝ)) * ((1 : ℝ) / 2) ^ (n : ℕ) := by have h := tsum_totient_lambert_sq_pure (r := 1 / 2) (by norm_num) (by norm_num) calc ∑' d : ℕ+, (Nat.totient (d : ℕ) : ℝ) / ((2 : ℝ) ^ (d : ℕ) - 1) ^ 2 = ∑' d : ℕ+, (Nat.totient (d : ℕ) : ℝ) * (((1 : ℝ) / 2) ^ (d : ℕ) / (1 - ((1 : ℝ) / 2) ^ (d : ℕ))) ^ 2 := by refine tsum_congr fun d => ?_ rw [half_pow_sq_term' (d : ℕ) d.pos, mul_one_div] _ = ∑' n : ℕ+, ((∑ e ∈ (n : ℕ).divisors, (Nat.totient e : ℝ) * (((n : ℕ) / e : ℕ) : ℝ)) - ((n : ℕ) : ℝ)) * ((1 : ℝ

declarations.name: tsum_pos_pair_both_dvd_half_eq_inv_mersenne_sq
declarations.signature: theorem tsum_pos_pair_both_dvd_half_eq_inv_mersenne_sq (d : ℕ) (hd : 0 < d) : (∑' p : ℕ × ℕ, if 0 < p.1 ∧ 0 < p.2 ∧ d ∣ p.1 ∧ d ∣ p.2 then ((1 : ℝ) / 2) ^ (p.1 + p.2) else 0) = 1 / ((2 : ℝ) ^ d - 1) ^ 2
declarations.docstring: **Divisibility of the pair factorizes**: the fair-coin mass of `{(a,b) : a,b ≥ 1, d ∣ a, d ∣ b}` is exactly `1/(2ᵈ-1)²` — i.e. `P(d ∣ gcd(X,Y)) = P(d ∣ X)·P(d ∣ Y)`. This is the foundation stone of the squared kernel: `L₂(f) = ∑ f(d)·P(d ∣ gcd)`, and it is also why the Stern–Brocot cylinder closed form `1/((2ᵃ-1)(2ᵇ-1))` is a product of one-variable divisor probabilities.

declarations.name: tsum_pos_coprime_inv_mersenne_eq_one
declarations.signature: theorem tsum_pos_coprime_inv_mersenne_eq_one : (∑' p : ℕ × ℕ, if 0 < p.1 ∧ 0 < p.2 ∧ Nat.Coprime p.1 p.2 then 1 / ((2 : ℝ) ^ (p.1 + p.2) - 1) else 0) = 1
declarations.docstring: **The reduced-direction law**: `∑_{(a,b) coprime, a,b ≥ 1} 1/(2^{a+b}-1) = 1`. Every positive pair `(X,Y)` has a unique reduced slope, and `P(slope = a/b) = ∑_g 2^{-g(a+b)} = 1/(2^{a+b}-1)` for coprime `(a,b)`; the masses sum to one. Proved by expanding each Mersenne reciprocal as its geometric layer series and swapping with the wave-19 gcd-layer normalization. This is the root cylinder of the Stern–Brocot law: `M(1,1) = 1`.

declarations.name: cylinderMass
declarations.signature: noncomputable def cylinderMass (a b : ℕ+) : ℝ
declarations.docstring: The closed-form cylinder mass at the coprime node `(a,b)`: `M(a,b) = 1/((2ᵃ-1)(2ᵇ-1)) = P(a ∣ X)·P(b ∣ Y)`.

declarations.name: sternBrocotDepthMass
declarations.signature: noncomputable def sternBrocotDepthMass : ℕ → ℕ+ → ℕ+ → ℝ | 0, _, _ => 0 | (dp + 1), a, b => 1 / ((2 : ℝ) ^ ((a : ℕ) + (b : ℕ)) - 1) + sternBrocotDepthMass dp (a + b) b + sternBrocotDepthMass dp a (a + b) private lemma two_le_two_pow (a : ℕ+) : (2 : ℝ) ≤ (2 : ℝ) ^ (a : ℕ)
declarations.docstring: The depth-`d` finite unfolding of the mediant recursion: sum the stop mass `1/(2^{a+b}-1)` at every node of the first `d` generations of the subtree rooted at `(a,b)`, under the children `(a+b, b)` and `(a, a+b)`.

declarations.name: two_le_two_pow
declarations.signature: private lemma two_le_two_pow (a : ℕ+) : (2 : ℝ) ≤ (2 : ℝ) ^ (a : ℕ)

declarations.name: cylinderMass_pos
declarations.signature: theorem cylinderMass_pos (a b : ℕ+) : 0 < cylinderMass a b

declarations.name: cylinderMass_split
declarations.signature: theorem cylinderMass_split (a b : ℕ+) : cylinderMass a b = 1 / ((2 : ℝ) ^ ((a : ℕ) + (b : ℕ)) - 1) + cylinderMass (a + b) b + cylinderMass a (a + b)
declarations.docstring: **The exact telescoping recursion of the cylinder mass**: `M(a,b) = 1/(2^{a+b}-1) + M(a+b, b) + M(a, a+b)`. With `A = 2ᵃ`, `B = 2ᵇ` this is `1/((A-1)(B-1)) = 1/(AB-1) + 1/((AB-1)(B-1)) + 1/((A-1)(AB-1))` — the algebra that makes the reduced-direction distribution an exact rational Markov measure on the Stern–Brocot tree.

declarations.name: stop_ge_third_cylinderMass
declarations.signature: private lemma stop_ge_third_cylinderMass (a b : ℕ+) : (1 / 3) * cylinderMass a b ≤ 1 / ((2 : ℝ) ^ ((a : ℕ) + (b : ℕ)) - 1)
declarations.docstring: The stop mass at a node is at least a third of its cylinder mass: `(1/3)·M(a,b) ≤ 1/(2^{a+b}-1)`, i.e. `2^{a+b} - 1 ≤ 3(2ᵃ-1)(2ᵇ-1)` — equality exactly at the root `(1,1)`.

declarations.name: cylinderMass_children_le
declarations.signature: theorem cylinderMass_children_le (a b : ℕ+) : cylinderMass (a + b) b + cylinderMass a (a + b) ≤ (2 / 3) * cylinderMass a b
declarations.docstring: The two children carry at most `2/3` of the parent cylinder mass.

declarations.name: sternBrocotDepthMass_error
declarations.signature: theorem sternBrocotDepthMass_error (dp : ℕ) : ∀ a b : ℕ+, 0 ≤ cylinderMass a b - sternBrocotDepthMass dp a b ∧ cylinderMass a b - sternBrocotDepthMass dp a b ≤ (2 / 3 : ℝ) ^ dp * cylinderMass a b
declarations.docstring: **Finite-depth cylinder approximation with explicit geometric rate**: `0 ≤ M(a,b) - M_d(a,b) ≤ (2/3)ᵈ·M(a,b)`. The depth-`d` unfolding of the mediant recursion exhausts the closed form at rate `(2/3)ᵈ`, uniformly over all nodes — the error at depth `d` is exactly the frontier mass, and each generation retains at most `2/3` of its parent mass.

declarations.name: tendsto_sternBrocotDepthMass
declarations.signature: theorem tendsto_sternBrocotDepthMass (a b : ℕ+) : Filter.Tendsto (fun dp : ℕ => sternBrocotDepthMass dp a b) Filter.atTop (nhds (cylinderMass a b))
declarations.docstring: **The Stern–Brocot cylinder law, limit form**: the depth-`d` unfolding of the mediant recursion at `(a,b)` converges to the closed form `M(a,b) = 1/((2ᵃ-1)(2ᵇ-1))`, with explicit rate `(2/3)ᵈ` from `sternBrocotDepthMass_error`. At the root: `M(1,1) = 1`, matching the reduced-direction law `tsum_pos_coprime_inv_mersenne_eq_one`.

declarations.name: says
declarations.signature: theorem says that `X_c` is rational exactly when there is a positive integer `v` and an integer orbit `u(N+1) = 2*u(N) - v*c(N+1)` whose growth is tempered

declarations.name: binaryCoeffSeries
declarations.signature: noncomputable def binaryCoeffSeries (c : ℕ → ℕ) : ℝ
declarations.docstring: The binary coefficient series `X_c = ∑_{n≥1} c(n)/2^n`.

declarations.name: binaryCoeffTail
declarations.signature: noncomputable def binaryCoeffTail (c : ℕ → ℕ) (N : ℕ) : ℝ
declarations.docstring: The scaled tail `T_c(N) = ∑_{j≥1} c(N+j)/2^j`.

declarations.name: HasRationalValue
declarations.docstring: A real number represented by an integer numerator and a positive natural denominator. This is the explicit positive-denominator form of membership in `ℚ`; it keeps the carry multiplier visible in theorem statements.

declarations.name: hasRationalValue_iff_not_irrational
declarations.signature: theorem hasRationalValue_iff_not_irrational (x : ℝ) : HasRationalValue x ↔ ¬ Irrational x
declarations.docstring: The explicit positive-denominator predicate is exactly standard rationality (`¬ Irrational x`).

declarations.name: IsTemperedBinaryOrbit
declarations.signature: def IsTemperedBinaryOrbit (c : ℕ → ℕ) (v : ℕ) (u : ℕ → ℤ) : Prop
declarations.docstring: The exact integer recurrence together with the subexponential boundary `u(N) = o(2^N)`, expressed as convergence of the quotient.

declarations.name: binaryCoeffTail_zero
declarations.signature: @[simp] theorem binaryCoeffTail_zero (c : ℕ → ℕ) : binaryCoeffTail c 0 = binaryCoeffSeries c
declarations.docstring: The scaled tail at `N = 0` is the original coefficient series.

declarations.name: binaryCoeffTail_nonneg
declarations.signature: theorem binaryCoeffTail_nonneg (c : ℕ → ℕ) (N : ℕ) : 0 ≤ binaryCoeffTail c N
declarations.docstring: Nonnegative coefficients give a nonnegative scaled tail.

declarations.name: binaryCoeffTail_le
declarations.signature: theorem binaryCoeffTail_le (c : ℕ → ℕ) (hgrowth : ∀ n : ℕ, c n ≤ n) (N : ℕ) : binaryCoeffTail c N ≤ (N : ℝ) + 2
declarations.docstring: A linear-growth coefficient sequence has the uniform tail bound `T_c(N) ≤ N+2`.

declarations.name: binaryCoeffTail_div_pow_tendsto_zero
declarations.signature: theorem binaryCoeffTail_div_pow_tendsto_zero (c : ℕ → ℕ) (hgrowth : ∀ n : ℕ, c n ≤ n) : Tendsto (fun N : ℕ ↦ binaryCoeffTail c N / (2 : ℝ) ^ N) atTop (nhds 0)
declarations.docstring: The scaled coefficient tail itself is subexponential.

declarations.name: binaryCoeffTail_succ
declarations.signature: theorem binaryCoeffTail_succ (c : ℕ → ℕ) (hgrowth : ∀ n : ℕ, c n ≤ n) (N : ℕ) : binaryCoeffTail c (N + 1) = 2 * binaryCoeffTail c N - (c (N + 1) : ℝ)
declarations.docstring: Doubling the tail shifts the window by one and expels its head coefficient.

declarations.name: not
declarations.signature: structure not present in the generic tail recurrence. -/ /-- The radius of the balanced-pulse family at location `m`. -/ def balancedPulseRadius (m : ℕ) : ℕ

declarations.name: balancedPulseRadius
declarations.docstring: The radius of the balanced-pulse family at location `m`.

declarations.name: balancedPulseCoeff
declarations.signature: def balancedPulseCoeff (m r : ℕ) : ℕ → ℕ
declarations.docstring: A two-site pulse whose mass can be moved from position `m` to `m+1` without changing its binary-series value.

declarations.name: balancedPulseCoeff_at_left
declarations.signature: @[simp] theorem balancedPulseCoeff_at_left (m r : ℕ) : balancedPulseCoeff m r m = balancedPulseRadius m - r

declarations.name: balancedPulseCoeff_at_right
declarations.signature: @[simp] theorem balancedPulseCoeff_at_right (m r : ℕ) : balancedPulseCoeff m r (m + 1) = 2 * r

declarations.name: balancedPulseCoeff_eq_zero_of_ne
declarations.signature: theorem balancedPulseCoeff_eq_zero_of_ne {m r n : ℕ} (hnm : n ≠ m) (hnm1 : n ≠ m + 1) : balancedPulseCoeff m r n = 0

declarations.name: balancedPulseCoeff_le_self
declarations.signature: theorem balancedPulseCoeff_le_self {m r : ℕ} (hm : 2 ≤ m) (hr : r ≤ balancedPulseRadius m) : ∀ n : ℕ, balancedPulseCoeff m r n ≤ n
declarations.docstring: Every admissible balanced pulse lies in the generic linear-growth class.

declarations.name: balancedPulse_weighted_pair
declarations.signature: theorem balancedPulse_weighted_pair {m r : ℕ} (hr : r ≤ balancedPulseRadius m) : 2 * balancedPulseCoeff m r m + balancedPulseCoeff m r (m + 1) = 2 * balancedPulseRadius m
declarations.docstring: Exact cancellation: all pulse parameters have the same two-site binary numerator. This is the finite algebra behind equality of the rational series and of every tail strictly before `m`.

declarations.name: balancedPulse_endpoint_fanout
declarations.signature: theorem balancedPulse_endpoint_fanout (m r : ℕ) : balancedPulseCoeff m r (m + 1) / 2 = r
declarations.docstring: The first tail after the left pulse site decodes the fresh parameter exactly.

declarations.name: balancedPulse_label_injective
declarations.signature: theorem balancedPulse_label_injective {m : ℕ} {Λ : Type*} (label : Fin (balancedPulseRadius m + 1) → Λ) (decode : Λ → ℕ) (hdecode : ∀ r, decode (label r) = r) : Function.Injective label
declarations.docstring: Any exact label/decoder for the balanced-pulse successors is injective. Consequently its label range has at least `balancedPulseRadius m + 1` elements, which is unbounded with `m`.

declarations.name: balancedPulse_label_card_lower_bound
declarations.signature: theorem balancedPulse_label_card_lower_bound {m : ℕ} {Λ : Type*} [Fintype Λ] (label : Fin (balancedPulseRadius m + 1) → Λ) (decode : Λ → ℕ) (hdecode : ∀ r, decode (label r) = r) : balancedPulseRadius m + 1 ≤ Fintype.card Λ
declarations.docstring: Finite labels capable of exact balanced-pulse decoding need at least the full pulse fan-out.

declarations.name: balancedPulse_fanout_unbounded
declarations.signature: theorem balancedPulse_fanout_unbounded (k : ℕ) : k + 1 ≤ balancedPulseRadius (2 * k) + 1
declarations.docstring: The exact successor fan-out is unbounded along the even pulse locations.

declarations.name: balancedPulse_no_autonomous_decoder
declarations.signature: theorem balancedPulse_no_autonomous_decoder {State : Type*} (m : ℕ) (hm : 2 ≤ m) (state : Fin (balancedPulseRadius m + 1) → State) (hstate : ∀ r, state r = state ⟨0,
declarations.docstring: **Finite-state no-go.** If a proposed predecessor state identifies all members of one balanced-pulse family (as every state determined by the common pre-`m` history must), no autonomous decoder can recover every exact successor. The contradiction already uses the parameters `0` and `1`; `balancedPulse_label_injective` records the full unbounded fan-out.

declarations.name: affineBinaryOrbit
declarations.signature: def affineBinaryOrbit (a : ℕ → ℤ) (u0 : ℤ) : ℕ → ℤ | 0 => u0 | n + 1 => 2 * affineBinaryOrbit a u0 n - a (n + 1) @[simp] theorem affineBinaryOrbit_zero (a : ℕ → ℤ) (u0 : ℤ) : affineBinaryOrbit a u0 0 = u0
declarations.docstring: The exact binary affine orbit driven by the fresh coefficient word `a`.

declarations.name: affineBinaryOrbit_zero
declarations.signature: @[simp] theorem affineBinaryOrbit_zero (a : ℕ → ℤ) (u0 : ℤ) : affineBinaryOrbit a u0 0 = u0

declarations.name: affineBinaryOrbit_succ
declarations.signature: @[simp] theorem affineBinaryOrbit_succ (a : ℕ → ℤ) (u0 : ℤ) (n : ℕ) : affineBinaryOrbit a u0 (n + 1) = 2 * affineBinaryOrbit a u0 n - a (n + 1)

declarations.name: affineBinaryOrbit_sub
declarations.signature: theorem affineBinaryOrbit_sub (a : ℕ → ℤ) (u0 v0 : ℤ) : ∀ L : ℕ, affineBinaryOrbit a u0 L - affineBinaryOrbit a v0 L = (2 : ℤ) ^ L * (u0 - v0)
declarations.docstring: Common forcing amplifies only the initial difference, by the exact homogeneous factor `2^L`.

declarations.name: affineBinaryOrbit_mod_twoPow_eq
declarations.signature: theorem affineBinaryOrbit_mod_twoPow_eq (a : ℕ → ℤ) (u0 v0 : ℤ) (L : ℕ) : affineBinaryOrbit a u0 L ≡ affineBinaryOrbit a v0 L [ZMOD (2 : ℤ) ^ L]
declarations.docstring: **Fixed-depth reset.** After `L` common affine carry steps, the endpoint residue modulo `2^L` is independent of the predecessor carry.

declarations.name: doublingOrbit_eq_zero_of_tempered
declarations.signature: theorem doublingOrbit_eq_zero_of_tempered (d : ℕ → ℝ) (hrec : ∀ N : ℕ, d (N + 1) = 2 * d N) (htemp : Tendsto (fun N : ℕ ↦ d N / (2 : ℝ) ^ N) atTop (nhds 0)) : ∀ N : ℕ, d N = 0
declarations.docstring: A real orbit satisfying `d(N+1)=2d(N)` and `d(N)=o(2^N)` is identically zero. This is the abstract rigidity engine.

declarations.name: temperedBinaryOrbit_eq_scaledTail
declarations.signature: theorem temperedBinaryOrbit_eq_scaledTail (c : ℕ → ℕ) (hgrowth : ∀ n : ℕ, c n ≤ n) {v : ℕ} {u : ℕ → ℤ} (horbit : IsTemperedBinaryOrbit c v u) : ∀ N : ℕ, (u N : ℝ) = (v : ℝ) * binaryCoeffTail c N
declarations.docstring: **Tail-orbit rigidity.** Every tempered integer orbit is the canonical scaled tail `v*T_c`.

declarations.name: exists_temperedBinaryOrbit_of_rational
declarations.signature: theorem exists_temperedBinaryOrbit_of_rational (c : ℕ → ℕ) (hgrowth : ∀ n : ℕ, c n ≤ n) (hrat : HasRationalValue (binaryCoeffSeries c)) : ∃ v : ℕ, 0 < v ∧ ∃ u : ℕ → ℤ, IsTemperedBinaryOrbit c v u
declarations.docstring: Rationality supplies the canonical positive-multiplier tempered integer orbit.

declarations.name: rational_of_exists_temperedBinaryOrbit
declarations.signature: theorem rational_of_exists_temperedBinaryOrbit (c : ℕ → ℕ) (hgrowth : ∀ n : ℕ, c n ≤ n) (horbit : ∃ v : ℕ, 0 < v ∧ ∃ u : ℕ → ℤ, IsTemperedBinaryOrbit c v u) : HasRationalValue (binaryCoeffSeries c)
declarations.docstring: A tempered integer orbit forces rationality of the coefficient series.

declarations.name: binaryCoeffSeries_rational_iff_exists_temperedBinaryOrbit
declarations.signature: theorem binaryCoeffSeries_rational_iff_exists_temperedBinaryOrbit (c : ℕ → ℕ) (hgrowth : ∀ n : ℕ, c n ≤ n) : HasRationalValue (binaryCoeffSeries c) ↔ ∃ v : ℕ, 0 < v ∧ ∃ u : ℕ → ℤ, IsTemperedBinaryOrbit c v u
declarations.docstring: **Subexponential tail-orbit rigidity, equivalence form (T7).**

declarations.name: not_irrational_binaryCoeffSeries_iff_exists_temperedBinaryOrbit
declarations.signature: theorem not_irrational_binaryCoeffSeries_iff_exists_temperedBinaryOrbit (c : ℕ → ℕ) (hgrowth : ∀ n : ℕ, c n ≤ n) : ¬ Irrational (binaryCoeffSeries c) ↔ ∃ v : ℕ, 0 < v ∧ ∃ u : ℕ → ℤ, IsTemperedBinaryOrbit c v u
declarations.docstring: T7 in Mathlib's standard irrationality vocabulary.

declarations.name: card_antidiagonal_filter_pos_coprime
declarations.signature: theorem card_antidiagonal_filter_pos_coprime (n : ℕ) : ((Finset.antidiagonal n).filter fun p : ℕ × ℕ => 0 < p.1 ∧ Nat.Coprime p.1 p.2).card = Nat.totient n
declarations.docstring: **Visible-point count = totient, uniformly in `n`.** The half-open filter (`0 < a`, `b` free) makes the identity hypothesis-free: `n = 0` gives the empty filter (`φ(0) = 0`), `n = 1` gives exactly `(1,0)` (`φ(1) = 1`), and for `n ≥ 2` the pair `(a, n-a)` is coprime iff `a` is a totient witness of `n`.

declarations.name: summable_pow_add
declarations.signature: theorem summable_pow_add {r : ℝ} (hr0 : 0 ≤ r) (hr1 : r < 1) : Summable (fun p : ℕ × ℕ => r ^ (p.1 + p.2))
declarations.docstring: **Visible-point count = totient, uniformly in `n`.** The half-open filter (`0 < a`, `b` free) makes the identity hypothesis-free: `n = 0` gives the empty filter (`φ(0) = 0`), `n = 1` gives exactly `(1,0)` (`φ(1) = 1`), and for `n ≥ 2` the pair `(a, n-a)` is coprime iff `a` is a totient witness of `n`. -/ theorem card_antidiagonal_filter_pos_coprime (n : ℕ) : ((Finset.antidiagonal n).filter fun p : ℕ × ℕ => 0 < p.1 ∧ Nat.Coprime p.1 p.2).card = Nat.totient n := by rw [Nat.totient_eq_card_coprime] refine Finset.card_bij' (fun p _ => p.2) (fun a _ => (n - a, a)) ?_ ?_ ?_ ?_ · intro p hp dsimp only rw [Finset.mem_filter, Finset.mem_antidiagonal] at hp obtain ⟨hsum, hpos, hcop⟩ := hp rw [Finset.mem_filter, Finset.mem_range] refine ⟨by omega, ?_⟩ rw [← hsum] exact Nat.coprime_add_self_left.mpr hcop · intro a ha dsimp only rw [Finset.mem_filter, Finset.mem_range] at ha obtain ⟨hlt, hcop⟩ := ha rw [Finset.mem_filter, Finset.mem_antidiagonal] refine ⟨by omega, by omega, ?_⟩ exact (Nat.coprime_s

declarations.name: summable_coprime_pair_pow
declarations.signature: theorem summable_coprime_pair_pow {r : ℝ} (hr0 : 0 ≤ r) (hr1 : r < 1) : Summable (fun p : ℕ × ℕ => if 0 < p.1 ∧ Nat.Coprime p.1 p.2 then r ^ (p.1 + p.2) else 0)

declarations.name: summable_pos_coprime_pair_pow
declarations.signature: theorem summable_pos_coprime_pair_pow {r : ℝ} (hr0 : 0 ≤ r) (hr1 : r < 1) : Summable (fun p : ℕ × ℕ => if 0 < p.1 ∧ 0 < p.2 ∧ Nat.Coprime p.1 p.2 then r ^ (p.1 + p.2) else 0)

declarations.name: tsum_coprime_pair_pow_eq_tsum_totient_mul_pow
declarations.signature: theorem tsum_coprime_pair_pow_eq_tsum_totient_mul_pow {r : ℝ} (hr0 : 0 ≤ r) (hr1 : r < 1) : (∑' p : ℕ × ℕ, if 0 < p.1 ∧ Nat.Coprime p.1 p.2 then r ^ (p.1 + p.2) else 0) = ∑' n : ℕ, (Nat.totient n : ℝ) * r ^ n
declarations.docstring: **Half-open visible-coprime bridge.** Regrouping the coprime-pair power sum along addition antidiagonals turns the visible-point count into the totient coefficient: the #249 power series IS a lattice-point mass.

declarations.name: halfopen_split
declarations.signature: private lemma halfopen_split (r : ℝ) (p : ℕ × ℕ) : (if 0 < p.1 ∧ Nat.Coprime p.1 p.2 then r ^ (p.1 + p.2) else 0) = (if 0 < p.1 ∧ 0 < p.2 ∧ Nat.Coprime p.1 p.2 then r ^ (p.1 + p.2) else 0) + (if p = ((1 : ℕ), (0 : ℕ)) then r else 0)
declarations.docstring: **Half-open visible-coprime bridge.** Regrouping the coprime-pair power sum along addition antidiagonals turns the visible-point count into the totient coefficient: the #249 power series IS a lattice-point mass. -/ theorem tsum_coprime_pair_pow_eq_tsum_totient_mul_pow {r : ℝ} (hr0 : 0 ≤ r) (hr1 : r < 1) : (∑' p : ℕ × ℕ, if 0 < p.1 ∧ Nat.Coprime p.1 p.2 then r ^ (p.1 + p.2) else 0) = ∑' n : ℕ, (Nat.totient n : ℝ) * r ^ n := by have hFsum := summable_coprime_pair_pow hr0 hr1 have hsig : Summable (fun x : (Σ n : ℕ, {y // y ∈ Finset.antidiagonal n}) => if 0 < (x.2 : ℕ × ℕ).1 ∧ Nat.Coprime (x.2 : ℕ × ℕ).1 (x.2 : ℕ × ℕ).2 then r ^ ((x.2 : ℕ × ℕ).1 + (x.2 : ℕ × ℕ).2) else 0) := by have h := (Equiv.summable_iff Finset.sigmaAntidiagonalEquivProd).mpr hFsum exact h.congr fun x => rfl calc (∑' p : ℕ × ℕ, if 0 < p.1 ∧ Nat.Coprime p.1 p.2 then r ^ (p.1 + p.2) else 0) = ∑' x : (Σ n : ℕ, {y // y ∈ Finset.antidiagonal n}), (if 0 < (x.2 : ℕ × ℕ).1 ∧ Nat.Coprime (x.2 : ℕ × ℕ).1 (x.2 : ℕ × ℕ).2 then r ^

declarations.name: tsum_pos_coprime_pair_pow
declarations.signature: theorem tsum_pos_coprime_pair_pow {r : ℝ} (hr0 : 0 ≤ r) (hr1 : r < 1) : (∑' p : ℕ × ℕ, if 0 < p.1 ∧ 0 < p.2 ∧ Nat.Coprime p.1 p.2 then r ^ (p.1 + p.2) else 0) = (∑' n : ℕ, (Nat.totient n : ℝ) * r ^ n) - r
declarations.docstring: **Positive visible-coprime bridge**: over strictly positive coprime pairs, `∑ r^(a+b) = ∑ φ(n)·rⁿ - r`. At `r = 1/2` the left side is the probability that two independent fair-coin waiting times are coprime, and the boundary term `r` that separates it from the #249 constant is `P(X = 1) = L(μ)` — the ladder's Möbius rung.

declarations.name: summable_totient_mul_pow
declarations.signature: theorem summable_totient_mul_pow {x : ℝ} (hx0 : 0 ≤ x) (hx1 : x < 1) : Summable (fun n : ℕ => (Nat.totient n : ℝ) * x ^ n)
declarations.docstring: **Positive visible-coprime bridge**: over strictly positive coprime pairs, `∑ r^(a+b) = ∑ φ(n)·rⁿ - r`. At `r = 1/2` the left side is the probability that two independent fair-coin waiting times are coprime, and the boundary term `r` that separates it from the #249 constant is `P(X = 1) = L(μ)` — the ladder's Möbius rung. -/ theorem tsum_pos_coprime_pair_pow {r : ℝ} (hr0 : 0 ≤ r) (hr1 : r < 1) : (∑' p : ℕ × ℕ, if 0 < p.1 ∧ 0 < p.2 ∧ Nat.Coprime p.1 p.2 then r ^ (p.1 + p.2) else 0) = (∑' n : ℕ, (Nat.totient n : ℝ) * r ^ n) - r := by have hpos := summable_pos_coprime_pair_pow hr0 hr1 have hedge : Summable (fun p : ℕ × ℕ => if p = ((1 : ℕ), (0 : ℕ)) then r else 0) := by refine Summable.of_nonneg_of_le (fun p => ?_) (fun p => ?_) (summable_pow_add hr0 hr1) · by_cases h : p = ((1 : ℕ), (0 : ℕ)) · rw [if_pos h]; exact hr0 · rw [if_neg h] · by_cases h : p = ((1 : ℕ), (0 : ℕ)) · subst h simp · rw [if_neg h] positivity have hhalf := tsum_coprime_pair_pow_eq_tsum_totient_mul_pow hr0 hr1 have hsp

declarations.name: tsum_totient_mul_pow_sub_self
declarations.signature: private lemma tsum_totient_mul_pow_sub_self {x : ℝ} (hx0 : 0 ≤ x) (hx1 : x < 1) : (∑' n : ℕ, (Nat.totient n : ℝ) * x ^ n) - x = ∑' n : ℕ, (Nat.totient (n + 2) : ℝ) * x ^ (n + 2)

declarations.name: tsum_pow_succ_geometric
declarations.signature: private lemma tsum_pow_succ_geometric {x : ℝ} (hx0 : 0 ≤ x) (hx1 : x < 1) : ∑' g : ℕ, x ^ (g + 1) = x / (1 - x)

declarations.name: tsum_pnat_mul_geometric
declarations.signature: theorem tsum_pnat_mul_geometric {r : ℝ} (hr0 : 0 ≤ r) (hr1 : r < 1) : ∑' n : ℕ+, ((n : ℕ) : ℝ) * r ^ (n : ℕ) = r / (1 - r) ^ 2
declarations.docstring: `∑_{n≥1} n·rⁿ = r/(1-r)²` — the generic-`r` value of the totient Lambert series (wave-18 `tsum_totient_lambert` lands here through `φ * ζ = Id`).

declarations.name: summable_totient_lambert_shift
declarations.signature: private lemma summable_totient_lambert_shift {r : ℝ} (hr0 : 0 ≤ r) (hr1 : r < 1) : Summable (fun j : ℕ => (Nat.totient (j + 1) : ℝ) * (r ^ (j + 1) / (1 - r ^ (j + 1))))

declarations.name: tsum_totient_lambert_tail
declarations.signature: theorem tsum_totient_lambert_tail {r : ℝ} (hr0 : 0 ≤ r) (hr1 : r < 1) : ∑' n : ℕ, (Nat.totient (n + 2) : ℝ) * (r ^ (n + 2) / (1 - r ^ (n + 2))) = r / (1 - r) ^ 2 - r / (1 - r)
declarations.docstring: **Tail Lambert evaluation**: `∑_{n≥2} φ(n)·rⁿ/(1-rⁿ) = r/(1-r)² - r/(1-r)`. This is the wave-18 rung `L(φ)` with its `d = 1` term peeled off — the analytic identity behind the gcd-layer normalization.

declarations.name: tsum_gcd_layer_pos_coprime_pow
declarations.signature: theorem tsum_gcd_layer_pos_coprime_pow {r : ℝ} (hr0 : 0 ≤ r) (hr1 : r < 1) : ∑' g : ℕ, (∑' p : ℕ × ℕ, if 0 < p.1 ∧ 0 < p.2 ∧ Nat.Coprime p.1 p.2 then (r ^ (g + 1)) ^ (p.1 + p.2) else 0) = (r / (1 - r)) ^ 2
declarations.docstring: **The gcd-layer decomposition, generic `0 ≤ r < 1`.** Every positive pair `(a,b)` factors uniquely as `g·(a',b')` with `gcd(a',b') = 1`, so summing the positive coprime-pair mass at every scale `r, r², r³, …` recovers the full positive-quadrant mass `(∑_{a≥1} rᵃ)² = (r/(1-r))²`. Layer `g` is the mass of `{gcd = g}`; the `g = 1` layer at `r = 1/2` is the open #249 value `S - 1/2`.

declarations.name: tsum_gcd_layer_pos_coprime_half_eq_one
declarations.signature: theorem tsum_gcd_layer_pos_coprime_half_eq_one : ∑' g : ℕ, (∑' p : ℕ × ℕ, if 0 < p.1 ∧ 0 < p.2 ∧ Nat.Coprime p.1 p.2 then (((1 : ℝ) / 2) ^ (g + 1)) ^ (p.1 + p.2) else 0) = 1
declarations.docstring: **The fair-coin gcd law is exactly normalized.** At `r = 1/2` the layer masses are genuine probabilities — layer `g` is `P(gcd(X,Y) = g+1)` for independent fair-coin waiting times — and they sum to `1`: the gcd is finite almost surely. Through the visible-coprime bridge this is precisely the probabilistic content of the wave-18 rational rung `L(φ) = 2`.

declarations.name: mersenneWeightRat
declarations.docstring: The exact rational Mersenne weight `1 / (2^n - 1)`. Its meaningful support indices are positive; at index zero Lean's division convention gives zero.

declarations.name: mersenneWeight
declarations.docstring: The real Mersenne weight `1 / (2^n - 1)`.

declarations.name: cast_mersenneWeightRat
declarations.signature: @[simp] theorem cast_mersenneWeightRat (n : ℕ) : ((mersenneWeightRat n : ℚ) : ℝ) = mersenneWeight n

declarations.name: mersenneWeight_pos
declarations.signature: theorem mersenneWeight_pos {n : ℕ} (hn : 0 < n) : 0 < mersenneWeight n

declarations.name: mersenneWeightRat_pos
declarations.signature: theorem mersenneWeightRat_pos {n : ℕ} (hn : 0 < n) : 0 < mersenneWeightRat n

declarations.name: summable_mersenneWeight
declarations.signature: theorem summable_mersenneWeight : Summable (fun k : ℕ => mersenneWeight (k + 1))
declarations.docstring: The positive-index Mersenne weights form a summable real series.

declarations.name: mersenneTail
declarations.docstring: The remaining mass after processing exponents `1, ..., n`.

declarations.name: summable_mersenneTail
declarations.signature: theorem summable_mersenneTail (n : ℕ) : Summable (fun k : ℕ => mersenneWeight (n + k + 1))

declarations.name: mersenneTail_eq_weight_add
declarations.signature: theorem mersenneTail_eq_weight_add (n : ℕ) : mersenneTail n = mersenneWeight (n + 1) + mersenneTail (n + 1)
declarations.docstring: Splitting off the next positive exponent gives the exact tail recurrence.

declarations.name: two_mul_mersenneWeight_succ_lt
declarations.signature: theorem two_mul_mersenneWeight_succ_lt {n : ℕ} (hn : 0 < n) : 2 * mersenneWeight (n + 1) < mersenneWeight n
declarations.docstring: Adjacent weights shrink by strictly more than a factor of two.

declarations.name: mersenneWeight_add_le_geometric
declarations.signature: theorem mersenneWeight_add_le_geometric (n j : ℕ) (hn : 0 < n) : mersenneWeight (n + j) ≤ mersenneWeight n * ((1 : ℝ) / 2) ^ j
declarations.docstring: Iterating the adjacent ratio gives a geometric envelope.

declarations.name: mersenneTail_le_two_mul_weight
declarations.signature: theorem mersenneTail_le_two_mul_weight (n : ℕ) : mersenneTail n ≤ 2 * mersenneWeight (n + 1)
declarations.docstring: A simple exact-rational upper enclosure for the remaining infinite tail.

declarations.name: mersenneTail_lt_weight
declarations.signature: theorem mersenneTail_lt_weight {n : ℕ} (hn : 0 < n) : mersenneTail n < mersenneWeight n
declarations.docstring: **Strict superincreasingness.** Every positive-index weight is larger than the entire tail following it.

declarations.name: mersenneTail_lt_two_mul_weight
declarations.signature: theorem mersenneTail_lt_two_mul_weight (n : ℕ) : mersenneTail n < 2 * mersenneWeight (n + 1)
declarations.docstring: The one-step enclosure is in fact strict.

declarations.name: tendsto_mersenneTail_zero
declarations.docstring: The remaining Mersenne mass tends to zero.

declarations.name: mersenneWeightRemainder
declarations.docstring: The contribution of geometric channels of order at least three in one Mersenne weight.

declarations.name: mersenneWeight_eq_two_channels_add_remainder
declarations.signature: theorem mersenneWeight_eq_two_channels_add_remainder {n : ℕ} (hn : 0 < n) : mersenneWeight n = ((1 : ℝ) / 2) ^ n + ((1 : ℝ) / 4) ^ n + mersenneWeightRemainder n
declarations.docstring: Exact separation of the first two geometric channels.

declarations.name: mersenneWeightRemainder_nonneg
declarations.signature: theorem mersenneWeightRemainder_nonneg {n : ℕ} (hn : 0 < n) : 0 ≤ mersenneWeightRemainder n

declarations.name: mersenneWeightRemainder_pos
declarations.signature: theorem mersenneWeightRemainder_pos {n : ℕ} (hn : 0 < n) : 0 < mersenneWeightRemainder n

declarations.name: mersenneWeightRemainder_le
declarations.signature: theorem mersenneWeightRemainder_le {n : ℕ} (hn : 0 < n) : mersenneWeightRemainder n ≤ 2 * ((1 : ℝ) / 8) ^ n
declarations.docstring: Uniform third-channel bound for one weight.

declarations.name: tsum_geometric_nat_add_succ
declarations.signature: theorem tsum_geometric_nat_add_succ (r : ℝ) (hr0 : 0 ≤ r) (hr1 : r < 1) (n : ℕ) : ∑' k : ℕ, r ^ (n + k + 1) = r ^ (n + 1) * (1 - r)⁻¹
declarations.docstring: A shifted geometric series in the form used by all three channels.

declarations.name: tsum_half_nat_add_succ
declarations.signature: theorem tsum_half_nat_add_succ (n : ℕ) : ∑' k : ℕ, ((1 : ℝ) / 2) ^ (n + k + 1) = ((1 : ℝ) / 2) ^ n

declarations.name: tsum_quarter_nat_add_succ
declarations.signature: theorem tsum_quarter_nat_add_succ (n : ℕ) : ∑' k : ℕ, ((1 : ℝ) / 4) ^ (n + k + 1) = (1 / 3 : ℝ) * ((1 : ℝ) / 4) ^ n

declarations.name: tsum_eighth_nat_add_succ
declarations.signature: theorem tsum_eighth_nat_add_succ (n : ℕ) : ∑' k : ℕ, ((1 : ℝ) / 8) ^ (n + k + 1) = (1 / 7 : ℝ) * ((1 : ℝ) / 8) ^ n

declarations.name: mersenneWeightRemainderTail
declarations.docstring: The accumulated contribution of channels of order at least three after exponent `n`.

declarations.name: summable_mersenneWeightRemainderTail
declarations.signature: theorem summable_mersenneWeightRemainderTail (n : ℕ) : Summable (fun k : ℕ => mersenneWeightRemainder (n + k + 1))

declarations.name: mersenneWeightRemainderTail_le
declarations.signature: theorem mersenneWeightRemainderTail_le (n : ℕ) : mersenneWeightRemainderTail n ≤ (2 / 7 : ℝ) * ((1 : ℝ) / 8) ^ n
declarations.docstring: The accumulated higher-channel tail is at most `(2/7)·8⁻ⁿ`.

declarations.name: one_seventh_eighth_pow_lt_mersenneWeightRemainderTail
declarations.signature: theorem one_seventh_eighth_pow_lt_mersenneWeightRemainderTail (n : ℕ) : (1 / 7 : ℝ) * ((1 : ℝ) / 8) ^ n < mersenneWeightRemainderTail n
declarations.docstring: The third geometric channel is a strict lower enclosure of the complete higher-channel tail.

declarations.name: mersenneTail_eq_two_channels_add_remainderTail
declarations.signature: theorem mersenneTail_eq_two_channels_add_remainderTail (n : ℕ) : mersenneTail n = ((1 : ℝ) / 2) ^ n + (1 / 3 : ℝ) * ((1 : ℝ) / 4) ^ n + mersenneWeightRemainderTail n
declarations.docstring: Exact tail expansion through the second geometric channel.

declarations.name: mersenneGap_sub_main_eq_remainder_sub_tail
declarations.signature: theorem mersenneGap_sub_main_eq_remainder_sub_tail {n : ℕ} (hn : 0 < n) : (mersenneWeight n - mersenneTail n) - (2 / 3 : ℝ) * ((1 : ℝ) / 4) ^ n = mersenneWeightRemainder n - mersenneWeightRemainderTail n
declarations.docstring: The Mersenne gap has leading term `(2/3)·4⁻ⁿ`; its exact error is the difference of the one-weight and tail higher-channel remainders.

declarations.name: mersenneGap_asymptotic_bound
declarations.signature: theorem mersenneGap_asymptotic_bound {n : ℕ} (hn : 0 < n) : ‖(mersenneWeight n - mersenneTail n) - (2 / 3 : ℝ) * ((1 : ℝ) / 4) ^ n‖ ≤ 3 * ‖((1 : ℝ) / 8) ^ n‖
declarations.docstring: Explicit norm certificate for the formal development.s gap asymptotic.

declarations.name: mersenneGap_isBigOWith
declarations.signature: theorem mersenneGap_isBigOWith : Asymptotics.IsBigOWith 3 atTop (fun n : ℕ => (mersenneWeight n - mersenneTail n) - (2 / 3 : ℝ) * ((1 : ℝ) / 4) ^ n) (fun n : ℕ => ((1 : ℝ) / 8) ^ n)
declarations.docstring: **gap asymptotic.** In positive indexing, `w_n - T_n = (2/3)·4⁻ⁿ + O(8⁻ⁿ)`, with an explicit valid `O` constant `3`.

declarations.name: positiveMersenneSupportValue
declarations.signature: noncomputable def positiveMersenneSupportValue (A : Set ℕ) : ℝ
declarations.docstring: The value coded by a set of positive exponents. The sequence index is zero-based while the exponent supplied to the weight is `k+1`.

declarations.name: summable_positiveMersenneSupportIndicator
declarations.signature: theorem summable_positiveMersenneSupportIndicator (A : Set ℕ) : Summable (fun k : ℕ => Set.indicator A mersenneWeight (k + 1))

declarations.name: positiveMersenneSupportSuffix
declarations.signature: noncomputable def positiveMersenneSupportSuffix (A : Set ℕ) (n : ℕ) : ℝ
declarations.docstring: The portion of a support code strictly after exponent `n`.

declarations.name: summable_positiveMersenneSupportSuffix
