declarations.signature: theorem summable_positiveMersenneSupportSuffix (A : Set ℕ) (n : ℕ) : Summable (fun k : ℕ => Set.indicator A mersenneWeight (n + k + 1))

declarations.name: positiveMersenneSupportSuffix_nonneg
declarations.signature: theorem positiveMersenneSupportSuffix_nonneg (A : Set ℕ) (n : ℕ) : 0 ≤ positiveMersenneSupportSuffix A n

declarations.name: positiveMersenneSupportSuffix_le_tail
declarations.signature: theorem positiveMersenneSupportSuffix_le_tail (A : Set ℕ) (n : ℕ) : positiveMersenneSupportSuffix A n ≤ mersenneTail n
declarations.docstring: A coded suffix is bounded by the full remaining mass.

declarations.name: positiveMersenneSupportSuffix_eq_indicator_add
declarations.signature: theorem positiveMersenneSupportSuffix_eq_indicator_add (A : Set ℕ) (n : ℕ) : positiveMersenneSupportSuffix A n = Set.indicator A mersenneWeight (n + 1) + positiveMersenneSupportSuffix A (n + 1)
declarations.docstring: Exact recurrence for a coded support suffix.

declarations.name: positiveMersenneSupportValue_eq_prefix_add_suffix
declarations.signature: theorem positiveMersenneSupportValue_eq_prefix_add_suffix (A : Set ℕ) (n : ℕ) : positiveMersenneSupportValue A = (∑ k ∈ Finset.range n, Set.indicator A mersenneWeight (k + 1)) + positiveMersenneSupportSuffix A n
declarations.docstring: Finite-prefix decomposition of a normalized support code.

declarations.name: positiveMersenneSupportValue_eq_erdosSupportSeries
declarations.signature: theorem positiveMersenneSupportValue_eq_erdosSupportSeries (A : Set ℕ) : positiveMersenneSupportValue A = erdosSupportSeries 2 A
declarations.docstring: The reindexed support value is exactly the existing #257 support series.

declarations.name: mersenneAchievementSet
declarations.docstring: The Mersenne achievement set, with the analytically invisible zero bit normalized away.

declarations.name: mersenneDigitTerm
declarations.signature: noncomputable def mersenneDigitTerm (k : ℕ) (b : ℕ → Fin 2) : ℝ
declarations.docstring: One term of the binary coding of the achievement set.

declarations.name: positiveMersenneDigitValue
declarations.signature: noncomputable def positiveMersenneDigitValue (b : ℕ → Fin 2) : ℝ
declarations.docstring: The support value coded by a binary sequence.

declarations.name: mersenneDigitSupport
declarations.signature: def mersenneDigitSupport (b : ℕ → Fin 2) : Set ℕ
declarations.docstring: The normalized positive support represented by a binary sequence.

declarations.name: zero_not_mem_mersenneDigitSupport
declarations.signature: @[simp] theorem zero_not_mem_mersenneDigitSupport (b : ℕ → Fin 2) : 0 ∉ mersenneDigitSupport b

declarations.name: succ_mem_mersenneDigitSupport_iff
declarations.signature: @[simp] theorem succ_mem_mersenneDigitSupport_iff (b : ℕ → Fin 2) (k : ℕ) : k + 1 ∈ mersenneDigitSupport b ↔ b k = 1

declarations.name: positiveMersenneDigitValue_eq_supportValue
declarations.signature: theorem positiveMersenneDigitValue_eq_supportValue (b : ℕ → Fin 2) : positiveMersenneDigitValue b = positiveMersenneSupportValue (mersenneDigitSupport b)

declarations.name: norm_mersenneDigitTerm_le
declarations.signature: theorem norm_mersenneDigitTerm_le (k : ℕ) (b : ℕ → Fin 2) : ‖mersenneDigitTerm k b‖ ≤ mersenneWeight (k + 1)

declarations.name: continuous_positiveMersenneDigitValue
declarations.docstring: The binary support coding is continuous in the product topology.

declarations.name: range_positiveMersenneDigitValue_eq
declarations.docstring: The binary coding has exactly the normalized Mersenne achievement set as its range.

declarations.name: isCompact_mersenneAchievementSet
declarations.docstring: **compactness.** The achievement set is the continuous image of compact binary sequence space.

declarations.name: mersenneCylinderStageFrom
declarations.signature: noncomputable def mersenneCylinderStageFrom (n : ℕ) : ℕ → Set ℝ | 0 => Set.Icc 0 (mersenneTail n) | depth + 1 => mersenneCylinderStageFrom (n + 1) depth ∪ (fun x : ℝ => mersenneWeight (n + 1) + x) '' mersenneCylinderStageFrom (n + 1) depth /-- The depth-`n` cylinder enclosure of the full achievement set. -/ noncomputable def mersenneCylinderStage (depth : ℕ) : Set ℝ
declarations.docstring: Starting after exponent `n`, retain `depth` binary choices and replace the unresolved suffix by its full interval enclosure.

declarations.name: mersenneCylinderStage
declarations.signature: noncomputable def mersenneCylinderStage (depth : ℕ) : Set ℝ
declarations.docstring: The depth-`n` cylinder enclosure of the full achievement set.

declarations.name: mersenneCylinderStageFrom_subset_Icc
declarations.signature: theorem mersenneCylinderStageFrom_subset_Icc (n depth : ℕ) : mersenneCylinderStageFrom n depth ⊆ Set.Icc 0 (mersenneTail n)
declarations.docstring: Every tail-cylinder stage stays inside its unresolved convex hull.

declarations.name: isCompact_mersenneCylinderStageFrom
declarations.signature: theorem isCompact_mersenneCylinderStageFrom (n depth : ℕ) : IsCompact (mersenneCylinderStageFrom n depth)

declarations.name: isClosed_mersenneCylinderStageFrom
declarations.signature: theorem isClosed_mersenneCylinderStageFrom (n depth : ℕ) : IsClosed (mersenneCylinderStageFrom n depth)

declarations.name: measurableSet_mersenneCylinderStageFrom
declarations.signature: theorem measurableSet_mersenneCylinderStageFrom (n depth : ℕ) : MeasurableSet (mersenneCylinderStageFrom n depth)

declarations.name: disjoint_mersenneCylinderStageFrom_split
declarations.signature: theorem disjoint_mersenneCylinderStageFrom_split (n depth : ℕ) : Disjoint (mersenneCylinderStageFrom (n + 1) depth) ((fun x : ℝ => mersenneWeight (n + 1) + x) '' mersenneCylinderStageFrom (n + 1) depth)
declarations.docstring: Strict superincreasingness separates the two children at every cylinder split.

declarations.name: mersenneCylinderStageFrom_succ_subset
declarations.signature: theorem mersenneCylinderStageFrom_succ_subset (n depth : ℕ) : mersenneCylinderStageFrom n (depth + 1) ⊆ mersenneCylinderStageFrom n depth
declarations.docstring: Increasing the retained depth shrinks the cylinder enclosure.

declarations.name: positiveMersenneSupportSuffix_zero_index
declarations.signature: @[simp] theorem positiveMersenneSupportSuffix_zero_index (A : Set ℕ) : positiveMersenneSupportSuffix A 0 = positiveMersenneSupportValue A

declarations.name: positiveMersenneSupportSuffix_diff_current
declarations.signature: theorem positiveMersenneSupportSuffix_diff_current (A : Set ℕ) (n : ℕ) : positiveMersenneSupportSuffix (A \ {n + 1}) n = positiveMersenneSupportSuffix A (n + 1)
declarations.docstring: Removing the current exponent shifts a coded suffix by one place.

declarations.name: positiveMersenneSupportSuffix_insert_current
declarations.signature: theorem positiveMersenneSupportSuffix_insert_current (A : Set ℕ) (n : ℕ) : positiveMersenneSupportSuffix (insert (n + 1) A) n = mersenneWeight (n + 1) + positiveMersenneSupportSuffix A (n + 1)
declarations.docstring: Inserting the current exponent splits off exactly its weight.

declarations.name: positiveMersenneSupportSuffix_mem_cylinderStageFrom
declarations.signature: theorem positiveMersenneSupportSuffix_mem_cylinderStageFrom (A : Set ℕ) (n depth : ℕ) : positiveMersenneSupportSuffix A n ∈ mersenneCylinderStageFrom n depth
declarations.docstring: Every coded suffix belongs to every corresponding finite-cylinder enclosure.

declarations.name: exists_normalized_support_near_of_mem_cylinderStageFrom
declarations.signature: theorem exists_normalized_support_near_of_mem_cylinderStageFrom {x : ℝ} {n depth : ℕ} (hx : x ∈ mersenneCylinderStageFrom n depth) : ∃ A : Set ℕ, 0 ∉ A ∧ dist x (positiveMersenneSupportSuffix A n) ≤ mersenneTail (n + depth)
declarations.docstring: Every point of a finite-cylinder stage is within the unresolved tail of an actual normalized support value.

declarations.name: mersenneAchievementSet_eq_iInter_cylinderStage
declarations.signature: theorem mersenneAchievementSet_eq_iInter_cylinderStage : mersenneAchievementSet = ⋂ depth : ℕ, mersenneCylinderStage depth
declarations.docstring: The achievement set is exactly the decreasing intersection of its finite cylinder enclosures.

declarations.name: volume_mersenneCylinderStageFrom
declarations.signature: theorem volume_mersenneCylinderStageFrom (n depth : ℕ) : volume (mersenneCylinderStageFrom n depth) = (2 : ℝ≥0∞) ^ depth * ENNReal.ofReal (mersenneTail (n + depth))
declarations.docstring: A depth-`d` tail stage is a disjoint union of `2^d` intervals, each of length `mersenneTail (n+d)`.

declarations.name: volume_mersenneCylinderStage
declarations.signature: theorem volume_mersenneCylinderStage (depth : ℕ) : volume (mersenneCylinderStage depth) = (2 : ℝ≥0∞) ^ depth * ENNReal.ofReal (mersenneTail depth)

declarations.name: tendsto_two_pow_mul_mersenneWeightRemainderTail_zero
declarations.signature: theorem tendsto_two_pow_mul_mersenneWeightRemainderTail_zero : Tendsto (fun n : ℕ => (2 : ℝ) ^ n * mersenneWeightRemainderTail n) atTop (nhds 0)
declarations.docstring: The higher-channel part of the scaled unresolved tail vanishes.

declarations.name: tendsto_two_pow_mul_mersenneTail_one
declarations.signature: theorem tendsto_two_pow_mul_mersenneTail_one : Tendsto (fun n : ℕ => (2 : ℝ) ^ n * mersenneTail n) atTop (nhds 1)
declarations.docstring: The total length of the level-`n` cylinder cover tends to one.

declarations.name: tendsto_volume_mersenneCylinderStage_one
declarations.signature: theorem tendsto_volume_mersenneCylinderStage_one : Tendsto (fun depth : ℕ => volume (mersenneCylinderStage depth)) atTop (nhds 1)

declarations.name: volume_mersenneAchievementSet
declarations.docstring: **measure one.** Continuity from above transfers the exact cylinder measures to their decreasing intersection.

declarations.name: greedyMersenneRemainder
declarations.signature: noncomputable def greedyMersenneRemainder (x : ℝ) : ℕ → ℝ | 0 => x | n + 1 => if mersenneWeight (n + 1) ≤ greedyMersenneRemainder x n then greedyMersenneRemainder x n - mersenneWeight (n + 1) else greedyMersenneRemainder x n /-- Exact rational version of the greedy residual. -/ def greedyMersenneRemainderRat (x : ℚ) : ℕ → ℚ | 0 => x | n + 1 => if mersenneWeightRat (n + 1) ≤ greedyMersenneRemainderRat x n then greedyMersenneRemainderRat x n - mersenneWeightRat (n + 1)
declarations.docstring: Real greedy residual after processing exponents `1, ..., n`.

declarations.name: greedyMersenneRemainderRat
declarations.signature: def greedyMersenneRemainderRat (x : ℚ) : ℕ → ℚ | 0 => x | n + 1 => if mersenneWeightRat (n + 1) ≤ greedyMersenneRemainderRat x n then greedyMersenneRemainderRat x n - mersenneWeightRat (n + 1) else greedyMersenneRemainderRat x n @[simp] theorem greedyMersenneRemainder_zero (x : ℝ) : greedyMersenneRemainder x 0 = x
declarations.docstring: Exact rational version of the greedy residual.

declarations.name: greedyMersenneRemainder_zero
declarations.signature: @[simp] theorem greedyMersenneRemainder_zero (x : ℝ) : greedyMersenneRemainder x 0 = x

declarations.name: greedyMersenneRemainderRat_zero
declarations.signature: @[simp] theorem greedyMersenneRemainderRat_zero (x : ℚ) : greedyMersenneRemainderRat x 0 = x

declarations.name: greedyMersenneRemainder_succ
declarations.signature: theorem greedyMersenneRemainder_succ (x : ℝ) (n : ℕ) : greedyMersenneRemainder x (n + 1) = if mersenneWeight (n + 1) ≤ greedyMersenneRemainder x n then greedyMersenneRemainder x n - mersenneWeight (n + 1) else greedyMersenneRemainder x n

declarations.name: greedyMersenneRemainderRat_succ
declarations.signature: theorem greedyMersenneRemainderRat_succ (x : ℚ) (n : ℕ) : greedyMersenneRemainderRat x (n + 1) = if mersenneWeightRat (n + 1) ≤ greedyMersenneRemainderRat x n then greedyMersenneRemainderRat x n - mersenneWeightRat (n + 1) else greedyMersenneRemainderRat x n

declarations.name: mersenneSliverCoordinateRat
declarations.docstring: Undoubled excess above the dyadic point at scale `n`. This is the coordinate whose finite-level selected-step law has constant term `-1`. Keeping it separate from the doubled coordinate prevents the two limiting fixed points from being compared under different normalizations.

declarations.name: sharpMersenneSliverCoordinateRat
declarations.docstring: Doubled excess above the dyadic point at scale `n`. For an exact rational remainder `r`, this is `2 * (2^n * r - 1) * (2^n - 1)`.

declarations.name: sharpMersenneSliverCoordinateRat_eq_two_mul
declarations.signature: theorem sharpMersenneSliverCoordinateRat_eq_two_mul (n : ℕ) (r : ℚ) : sharpMersenneSliverCoordinateRat n r = 2 * mersenneSliverCoordinateRat n r

declarations.name: mersenneSliverCoordinateRat_selected_step
declarations.signature: theorem mersenneSliverCoordinateRat_selected_step {n : ℕ} (hn : 0 < n) (r : ℚ) : mersenneSliverCoordinateRat (n + 1) (r - mersenneWeightRat (n + 1)) = (4 + 2 / ((2 : ℚ) ^ n - 1)) * mersenneSliverCoordinateRat n r - 1
declarations.docstring: Exact selected-step law for the undoubled coordinate. Its limiting map is `ρ ↦ 4 * ρ - 1`, with limiting fixed point `1 / 3`; the `-2` and `2 / 3` constants belong to `sharpMersenneSliverCoordinateRat`.

declarations.name: sharpMersenneSliverCoordinateRat_selected_step
declarations.signature: theorem sharpMersenneSliverCoordinateRat_selected_step {n : ℕ} (hn : 0 < n) (r : ℚ) : sharpMersenneSliverCoordinateRat (n + 1) (r - mersenneWeightRat (n + 1)) = 4 * sharpMersenneSliverCoordinateRat n r - 2 + 2 * sharpMersenneSliverCoordinateRat n r / ((2 : ℚ) ^ n - 1)
declarations.docstring: Exact finite-level selected-step law for the doubled sliver coordinate. The final term is the complete finite-level correction to the limiting map `ρ ↦ 4 * ρ - 2`; no asymptotic estimate is used here.

declarations.name: greedyMersenneRemainder_nonneg
declarations.signature: theorem greedyMersenneRemainder_nonneg {x : ℝ} (hx : 0 ≤ x) (n : ℕ) : 0 ≤ greedyMersenneRemainder x n
declarations.docstring: Greedy subtraction preserves nonnegativity.

declarations.name: cast_greedyMersenneRemainderRat
declarations.signature: @[simp] theorem cast_greedyMersenneRemainderRat (x : ℚ) (n : ℕ) : ((greedyMersenneRemainderRat x n : ℚ) : ℝ) = greedyMersenneRemainder (x : ℝ) n
declarations.docstring: Rational and real greedy execution agree exactly under casting.

declarations.name: halfDyadicCap
declarations.docstring: The first geometric channel of the Mersenne tail.

declarations.name: halfTwoChannelCap
declarations.docstring: The first two geometric channels of the Mersenne tail. This cap is strictly weaker than the dyadic cap while still lying below the full tail.

declarations.name: greedyMersenneRemainder_le_cap_of_skipBarrier
declarations.signature: theorem greedyMersenneRemainder_le_cap_of_skipBarrier (c : ℕ → ℝ) (hbase : greedyMersenneRemainder (1 / 2 : ℝ) 0 ≤ c 0) (hselected : ∀ n : ℕ, c n - mersenneWeight (n + 1) ≤ c (n + 1)) (hskipped : ∀ n : ℕ, ¬ mersenneWeight (n + 1) ≤ greedyMersenneRemainder (1 / 2 : ℝ) n → greedyMersenneRemainder (1 / 2 : ℝ) n ≤ c (n + 1)) : ∀ n : ℕ, greedyMersenneRemainder (1 / 2 : ℝ) n ≤ c n
declarations.docstring: If selected greedy branches transport a cap and skipped branches satisfy the next cap directly, then the cap holds at every level. This isolates the only branch on which a channel-cap proof has a genuine arithmetic burden.

declarations.name: halfDyadicCap_le_halfTwoChannelCap
declarations.signature: theorem halfDyadicCap_le_halfTwoChannelCap (n : ℕ) : halfDyadicCap n ≤ halfTwoChannelCap n
declarations.docstring: The one-channel cap is contained in the two-channel cap.

declarations.name: halfTwoChannelCap_le_mersenneTail
declarations.signature: theorem halfTwoChannelCap_le_mersenneTail (n : ℕ) : halfTwoChannelCap n ≤ mersenneTail n
declarations.docstring: The two-channel cap is a lower enclosure of the exact Mersenne tail.

declarations.name: halfTwoChannelCap_lt_mersenneTail
declarations.signature: theorem halfTwoChannelCap_lt_mersenneTail (n : ℕ) : halfTwoChannelCap n < mersenneTail n
declarations.docstring: The two-channel truncation is strictly below the true tail. The omitted higher channels are the exact positive gap, rather than a factor-two margin against a `2 / 3` sliver threshold.

declarations.name: halfDyadicCap_selected_strict
declarations.signature: theorem halfDyadicCap_selected_strict (n : ℕ) : halfDyadicCap n - mersenneWeight (n + 1) < halfDyadicCap (n + 1)
declarations.docstring: A selected greedy branch transports the dyadic cap strictly.

declarations.name: halfTwoChannelCap_selected_strict
declarations.signature: theorem halfTwoChannelCap_selected_strict (n : ℕ) : halfTwoChannelCap n - mersenneWeight (n + 1) < halfTwoChannelCap (n + 1)
declarations.docstring: A selected greedy branch transports the two-channel cap strictly.

declarations.name: halfDyadicCap_selected
declarations.signature: theorem halfDyadicCap_selected (n : ℕ) : halfDyadicCap n - mersenneWeight (n + 1) ≤ halfDyadicCap (n + 1)
declarations.docstring: Non-strict transport form consumed by the generic skip barrier.

declarations.name: halfTwoChannelCap_selected
declarations.signature: theorem halfTwoChannelCap_selected (n : ℕ) : halfTwoChannelCap n - mersenneWeight (n + 1) ≤ halfTwoChannelCap (n + 1)
declarations.docstring: Non-strict transport form consumed by the generic skip barrier.

declarations.name: greedyMersenneSupport
declarations.signature: noncomputable def greedyMersenneSupport (x : ℝ) : Set ℕ
declarations.docstring: The set of positive exponents selected by the real greedy recursion.

declarations.name: greedyMersenneSkippedSupport
declarations.signature: noncomputable def greedyMersenneSkippedSupport (x : ℝ) : Set ℕ
declarations.docstring: The positive exponents omitted by the real greedy recursion.

declarations.name: succ_mem_greedyMersenneSupport_iff
declarations.signature: @[simp] theorem succ_mem_greedyMersenneSupport_iff (x : ℝ) (n : ℕ) : n + 1 ∈ greedyMersenneSupport x ↔ mersenneWeight (n + 1) ≤ greedyMersenneRemainder x n

declarations.name: succ_mem_greedyMersenneSkippedSupport_iff
declarations.signature: @[simp] theorem succ_mem_greedyMersenneSkippedSupport_iff (x : ℝ) (n : ℕ) : n + 1 ∈ greedyMersenneSkippedSupport x ↔ ¬ mersenneWeight (n + 1) ≤ greedyMersenneRemainder x n

declarations.name: greedyMersenne_prefix_add_remainder
declarations.signature: theorem greedyMersenne_prefix_add_remainder (x : ℝ) (n : ℕ) : x = (∑ k ∈ Finset.range n, Set.indicator (greedyMersenneSupport x) mersenneWeight (k + 1)) + greedyMersenneRemainder x n
declarations.docstring: Exact finite-prefix identity for the real greedy recursion.

declarations.name: greedyMersenneRemainder_supportValue
declarations.signature: theorem greedyMersenneRemainder_supportValue (A : Set ℕ) (n : ℕ) : greedyMersenneRemainder (positiveMersenneSupportValue A) n = positiveMersenneSupportSuffix A n
declarations.docstring: On a genuine support value, the greedy residual is exactly the unprocessed support suffix.

declarations.name: succ_mem_greedySupport_supportValue_iff
declarations.signature: theorem succ_mem_greedySupport_supportValue_iff (A : Set ℕ) (n : ℕ) : n + 1 ∈ greedyMersenneSupport (positiveMersenneSupportValue A) ↔ n + 1 ∈ A
declarations.docstring: Greedy selection recovers every positive bit of a representing support.

declarations.name: greedySupport_supportValue_eq
declarations.signature: theorem greedySupport_supportValue_eq (A : Set ℕ) (hA0 : 0 ∉ A) : greedyMersenneSupport (positiveMersenneSupportValue A) = A
declarations.docstring: A normalized support is recovered exactly, including the forced-zero bit at exponent zero.

declarations.name: positiveMersenneSupportValue_nonneg
declarations.signature: theorem positiveMersenneSupportValue_nonneg (A : Set ℕ) : 0 ≤ positiveMersenneSupportValue A
declarations.docstring: Every support value is nonnegative.

declarations.name: greedy_survives_of_mem_mersenneAchievementSet
declarations.signature: theorem greedy_survives_of_mem_mersenneAchievementSet {x : ℝ} (hx : x ∈ mersenneAchievementSet) : 0 ≤ x ∧ ∀ n : ℕ, greedyMersenneRemainder x n ≤ mersenneTail n
declarations.docstring: Membership implies the exact all-level upper-tail survival inequalities.

declarations.name: tendsto_greedyMersenneRemainder_zero
declarations.signature: theorem tendsto_greedyMersenneRemainder_zero {x : ℝ} (hx : 0 ≤ x) (hsurvive : ∀ n : ℕ, greedyMersenneRemainder x n ≤ mersenneTail n) : Tendsto (greedyMersenneRemainder x) atTop (nhds 0)
declarations.docstring: If all greedy residuals stay below the remaining mass, they converge to zero. The nonnegativity guard excludes the false negative-target variant.

declarations.name: mem_mersenneAchievementSet_of_greedy_survival
declarations.signature: theorem mem_mersenneAchievementSet_of_greedy_survival {x : ℝ} (hx : 0 ≤ x) (hsurvive : ∀ n : ℕ, greedyMersenneRemainder x n ≤ mersenneTail n) : x ∈ mersenneAchievementSet
declarations.docstring: All-level survival constructs a genuine normalized support code.

declarations.name: half_mem_mersenneAchievementSet_of_skipped_twoChannelCap
declarations.signature: theorem half_mem_mersenneAchievementSet_of_skipped_twoChannelCap (hskip : ∀ n : ℕ, ¬ mersenneWeight (n + 1) ≤ greedyMersenneRemainder (1 / 2 : ℝ) n → greedyMersenneRemainder (1 / 2 : ℝ) n ≤ halfTwoChannelCap (n + 1)) : (1 / 2 : ℝ) ∈ mersenneAchievementSet
declarations.docstring: It is enough to establish the two-channel cap only when the actual greedy orbit skips the next exponent. Selected branches and the final membership composition are discharged here.

declarations.name: half_mem_mersenneAchievementSet_of_skipped_dyadicCap
declarations.signature: theorem half_mem_mersenneAchievementSet_of_skipped_dyadicCap (hskip : ∀ n : ℕ, ¬ mersenneWeight (n + 1) ≤ greedyMersenneRemainder (1 / 2 : ℝ) n → greedyMersenneRemainder (1 / 2 : ℝ) n ≤ halfDyadicCap (n + 1)) : (1 / 2 : ℝ) ∈ mersenneAchievementSet
declarations.docstring: The stronger one-channel skipped-branch obligation is therefore also a sufficient membership criterion.

declarations.name: mem_mersenneAchievementSet_iff_greedy_survival
declarations.signature: theorem mem_mersenneAchievementSet_iff_greedy_survival (x : ℝ) : x ∈ mersenneAchievementSet ↔ 0 ≤ x ∧ ∀ n : ℕ, greedyMersenneRemainder x n ≤ mersenneTail n
declarations.docstring: **Greedy membership criterion.** A real target belongs to the Mersenne achievement set exactly when it is nonnegative and every residual is at most the full remaining tail.

declarations.name: GreedyMersenneFatalAt
declarations.docstring: A greedy state is fatal when its residual is already larger than all remaining Mersenne mass.

declarations.name: greedyMersenneFatalAt_iff_not_survives
declarations.signature: theorem greedyMersenneFatalAt_iff_not_survives (x : ℝ) (n : ℕ) : GreedyMersenneFatalAt x n ↔ ¬ greedyMersenneRemainder x n ≤ mersenneTail n

declarations.name: greedyMersenneFatalAt_succ
declarations.signature: theorem greedyMersenneFatalAt_succ {x : ℝ} {n : ℕ} (hfatal : GreedyMersenneFatalAt x n) : mersenneWeight (n + 1) ≤ greedyMersenneRemainder x n ∧ GreedyMersenneFatalAt x (n + 1)
declarations.docstring: Fatality is absorbing: the next exponent is selected and the next state is fatal again.

declarations.name: greedyMersenneFatalAt_add
declarations.signature: theorem greedyMersenneFatalAt_add {x : ℝ} {n : ℕ} (hfatal : GreedyMersenneFatalAt x n) (k : ℕ) : GreedyMersenneFatalAt x (n + k)
declarations.docstring: Once a fatal state is reached, every later state is fatal.

declarations.name: mem_greedyMersenneSupport_of_fatalAt_add_succ
declarations.signature: theorem mem_greedyMersenneSupport_of_fatalAt_add_succ {x : ℝ} {n : ℕ} (hfatal : GreedyMersenneFatalAt x n) (k : ℕ) : n + k + 1 ∈ greedyMersenneSupport x
declarations.docstring: Every exponent after a fatal state is selected by the greedy recursion.

declarations.name: finite_greedyMersenneSkippedSupport_of_fatalAt
declarations.signature: theorem finite_greedyMersenneSkippedSupport_of_fatalAt {x : ℝ} {n : ℕ} (hfatal : GreedyMersenneFatalAt x n) : (greedyMersenneSkippedSupport x).Finite
declarations.docstring: A fatal state leaves only finitely many omitted exponents.

declarations.name: mem_mersenneAchievementSet_of_greedySkippedSupport_infinite
declarations.signature: theorem mem_mersenneAchievementSet_of_greedySkippedSupport_infinite {x : ℝ} (hx : 0 ≤ x) (hskips : (greedyMersenneSkippedSupport x).Infinite) : x ∈ mersenneAchievementSet
declarations.docstring: Infinitely many omitted exponents rule out every fatal state and hence force exact achievement-set membership.

declarations.name: positiveMersenneSupportValue_injective_normalized
declarations.signature: theorem positiveMersenneSupportValue_injective_normalized {A B : Set ℕ} (hA0 : 0 ∉ A) (hB0 : 0 ∉ B) (hvalue : positiveMersenneSupportValue A = positiveMersenneSupportValue B) : A = B
declarations.docstring: Strict superincreasingness makes normalized support coding injective.

declarations.name: erdosSupportSeries_two_injective_normalized
declarations.signature: theorem erdosSupportSeries_two_injective_normalized {A B : Set ℕ} (hA0 : 0 ∉ A) (hB0 : 0 ∉ B) (hvalue : erdosSupportSeries 2 A = erdosSupportSeries 2 B) : A = B
declarations.docstring: The support coding through the existing #257 series is likewise unique after the zero-bit normalization.

declarations.name: positiveMersenneDigitValue_injective
declarations.docstring: Strict superincreasingness makes the binary coding injective.

declarations.name: mersenneBitFlip
declarations.docstring: Flip one binary coordinate.

declarations.name: mersenneBitFlip_ne
declarations.signature: theorem mersenneBitFlip_ne (a : Fin 2) : mersenneBitFlip a ≠ a

declarations.name: preperfect_univ_mersenneDigits
declarations.docstring: Cantor binary space has no isolated points. This is proved directly from the finite-coordinate basis of the product topology.

declarations.name: perfect_mersenneAchievementSet
declarations.docstring: **perfection.** The compact achievement set has no isolated points.

declarations.name: isEmbedding_positiveMersenneDigitValue
declarations.docstring: **perfection.** The compact achievement set has no isolated points. -/ theorem perfect_mersenneAchievementSet : Perfect mersenneAchievementSet := by refine ⟨isClosed_mersenneAchievementSet, ?_⟩ rw [← range_positiveMersenneDigitValue_eq] exact preperfect_range_positiveMersenneDigitValue /-! ## Total disconnectedness and nowhere density

declarations.name: isNowhereDense_mersenneAchievementSet
declarations.docstring: **nowhere density.** If the closed achievement set contained an open ball, total disconnectedness would put a complementary point between two points of that ball.

declarations.name: mersenneTail_eq_prefix_add_tail
declarations.signature: theorem mersenneTail_eq_prefix_add_tail (n lookahead : ℕ) : mersenneTail n = (∑ k ∈ Finset.range lookahead, mersenneWeight (n + k + 1)) + mersenneTail (n + lookahead)
declarations.docstring: Split a tail after any finite lookahead.

declarations.name: mersenneTailUpperRat
declarations.docstring: A finite rational upper enclosure for `mersenneTail n`. The lookahead parameter exposes a family of exact rational bounds without comparing an infinite real tail numerically.

declarations.name: mersenneTail_lt_cast_tailUpperRat
declarations.signature: theorem mersenneTail_lt_cast_tailUpperRat (n lookahead : ℕ) : mersenneTail n < ((mersenneTailUpperRat n lookahead : ℚ) : ℝ)
declarations.docstring: Every finite rational enclosure lies strictly above the actual tail.

declarations.name: GreedyMersenneDeath
declarations.docstring: Semantic greedy death: the current nonnegative residual is already larger than all remaining available mass.

declarations.name: greedyMersenneDeath_not_mem
declarations.signature: theorem greedyMersenneDeath_not_mem {x : ℝ} {level : ℕ} (hdeath : GreedyMersenneDeath x level) : x ∉ mersenneAchievementSet
declarations.docstring: Any semantic death level proves nonmembership.

declarations.name: CertifiedGreedyMersenneDeath
declarations.signature: def CertifiedGreedyMersenneDeath (x : ℚ) (level lookahead : ℕ) : Prop
declarations.docstring: A decidable finite certificate: an exact rational upper enclosure is no larger than the exact rational greedy residual.

declarations.name: certifiedGreedyMersenneDeath_not_mem
declarations.signature: theorem certifiedGreedyMersenneDeath_not_mem {x : ℚ} {level lookahead : ℕ} (hcert : CertifiedGreedyMersenneDeath x level lookahead) : ((x : ℚ) : ℝ) ∉ mersenneAchievementSet
declarations.docstring: Every finite exact-rational death certificate is sound.

declarations.name: three_fourths_certifiedGreedyMersenneDeath
declarations.docstring: **Exact level-one certificate.** Greedy skips weight `w₁=1` at `3/4`, leaving residual `3/4`; the zero-lookahead enclosure of the remaining tail is `2*w₂ = 2/3`.

declarations.name: three_fourths_not_mem_mersenneAchievementSet
declarations.docstring: Consequently `3/4` is not in the Mersenne achievement set.

declarations.name: greedyMersennePrefixRat
declarations.signature: def greedyMersennePrefixRat (x : ℚ) (n : ℕ) : Finset ℕ
declarations.docstring: Positive exponents selected through a finite exact-rational greedy run.

declarations.name: zero_not_mem_greedyMersennePrefixRat
declarations.signature: @[simp] theorem zero_not_mem_greedyMersennePrefixRat (x : ℚ) (n : ℕ) : 0 ∉ greedyMersennePrefixRat x n

declarations.name: greedyMersenneRat_prefix_add_remainder
declarations.signature: theorem greedyMersenneRat_prefix_add_remainder (x : ℚ) (n : ℕ) : x = (∑ k ∈ Finset.range n, if mersenneWeightRat (k + 1) ≤ greedyMersenneRemainderRat x k then mersenneWeightRat (k + 1) else 0) + greedyMersenneRemainderRat x n
declarations.docstring: Exact telescoping identity for a finite rational greedy run.

declarations.name: of
declarations.signature: instance of the existing audited finite Erdős sum. -/ theorem finiteErdosSum_greedyMersennePrefixRat (x : ℚ) (n : ℕ) : finiteErdosSum (greedyMersennePrefixRat x n) 2 = ∑ k ∈ Finset.range n, if mersenneWeightRat (k + 1) ≤ greedyMersenneRemainderRat x k then mersenneWeightRat (k + 1) else 0

declarations.name: finiteErdosSum_greedyMersennePrefixRat
declarations.signature: theorem finiteErdosSum_greedyMersennePrefixRat (x : ℚ) (n : ℕ) : finiteErdosSum (greedyMersennePrefixRat x n) 2 = ∑ k ∈ Finset.range n, if mersenneWeightRat (k + 1) ≤ greedyMersenneRemainderRat x k then mersenneWeightRat (k + 1) else 0
declarations.docstring: The finite prefix selected by the rational greedy run is exactly an instance of the existing audited finite Erdős sum.

declarations.name: greedyMersenneRemainderRat_eq_sub_finiteErdosSum
declarations.signature: theorem greedyMersenneRemainderRat_eq_sub_finiteErdosSum (x : ℚ) (n : ℕ) : greedyMersenneRemainderRat x n = x - finiteErdosSum (greedyMersennePrefixRat x n) 2
declarations.docstring: Exact interface to the finite denominator/noncollapse kernel: the rational greedy residual is the target minus an audited `finiteErdosSum`.

declarations.name: rational_greedy_take_iff_real
declarations.signature: theorem rational_greedy_take_iff_real (x : ℚ) (n : ℕ) : mersenneWeightRat (n + 1) ≤ greedyMersenneRemainderRat x n ↔ mersenneWeight (n + 1) ≤ greedyMersenneRemainder (x : ℝ) n
declarations.docstring: A rational comparison decides exactly the same greedy bit as the real comparison after casting.

declarations.name: rational_member_support_bit_iff
declarations.signature: theorem rational_member_support_bit_iff (x : ℚ) (A : Set ℕ) (hA0 : 0 ∉ A) (hvalue : ((x : ℚ) : ℝ) = positiveMersenneSupportValue A) (n : ℕ) : n + 1 ∈ A ↔ mersenneWeightRat (n + 1) ≤ greedyMersenneRemainderRat x n
declarations.docstring: A rational value already known to have normalized support has computable support bits, recovered by the exact rational greedy recurrence. This does not assert decidability of achievement-set membership.

declarations.name: half_sub_five_term_prefix_eq
declarations.signature: theorem half_sub_five_term_prefix_eq : (1 / 2 : ℚ) - finiteErdosSum ({2, 3, 6, 7, 14} : Finset ℕ) 2 = 1 / 688086
declarations.docstring: The formal development.s exact worked prefix identity. Selecting exponents `{2,3,6,7,14}` from the target `1/2` leaves the rational residual `1/688086`.

declarations.name: mersenneCorrectionTail
declarations.docstring: The unresolved correction after exponent `n`: the full Mersenne tail minus its binary skeleton.

declarations.name: mersenneCorrectionTail_eq
declarations.signature: theorem mersenneCorrectionTail_eq (n : ℕ) : mersenneCorrectionTail n = (1 / 3 : ℝ) * ((1 : ℝ) / 4) ^ n + mersenneWeightRemainderTail n
declarations.docstring: The checked two-channel expansion is exactly the correction-tail decomposition used by prefix forcing.

declarations.name: mersenneCorrectionTail_le
declarations.signature: theorem mersenneCorrectionTail_le (n : ℕ) : mersenneCorrectionTail n ≤ (1 / 3 : ℝ) * ((1 : ℝ) / 4) ^ n + (2 / 7 : ℝ) * ((1 : ℝ) / 8) ^ n
declarations.docstring: Explicit enclosure for the unresolved correction.

declarations.name: mersenneCorrectionTail_add_self_succ_lt_remainderTail
declarations.signature: theorem mersenneCorrectionTail_add_self_succ_lt_remainderTail {m : ℕ} (hm : 0 < m) : mersenneCorrectionTail (m + (m + 1)) < mersenneWeightRemainderTail m
declarations.docstring: A simple explicit block length already makes the unresolved correction smaller than the higher-channel gap at the starting level. The sharper `ceil(m/2)+1` length is not needed for the cap consumer; `m+1` keeps the formal inequality transparent while remaining linear and uniform.

declarations.name: mersenneCorrectionTail_ceilingHalf_add_one_lt_remainderTail
declarations.docstring: Sharp explicit block length from the corrected paper analysis: `ceil(m/2)+1`, written over naturals as `(m+1)/2+1`.

declarations.name: mersenneForcedReturnThreshold
declarations.signature: noncomputable def mersenneForcedReturnThreshold (m J : ℕ) : ℝ
declarations.docstring: `C(m,J)`: the exact initial remainder which lands on the dyadic boundary after subtracting the next `J` consecutive Mersenne weights.

declarations.name: mersenneForcedReturnThreshold_zero
declarations.signature: @[simp] theorem mersenneForcedReturnThreshold_zero (m : ℕ) : mersenneForcedReturnThreshold m 0 = ((1 : ℝ) / 2) ^ m

declarations.name: mersenneForcedReturnThreshold_eq_tail_sub_correction
declarations.signature: theorem mersenneForcedReturnThreshold_eq_tail_sub_correction (m J : ℕ) : mersenneForcedReturnThreshold m J = mersenneTail m - mersenneCorrectionTail (m + J)
declarations.docstring: The threshold is exactly the full tail minus the unresolved correction at the post-block level.

declarations.name: forcedBlock_le_dyadic_iff_le_returnThreshold
declarations.signature: theorem forcedBlock_le_dyadic_iff_le_returnThreshold (m J : ℕ) (r : ℝ) : r - (∑ k ∈ Finset.range J, mersenneWeight (m + k + 1)) ≤ ((1 : ℝ) / 2) ^ (m + J) ↔ r ≤ mersenneForcedReturnThreshold m J
declarations.docstring: Finite-block return is a single exact scalar comparison.

declarations.name: mersenneForcedReturnThreshold_succ
declarations.signature: theorem mersenneForcedReturnThreshold_succ (m J : ℕ) : mersenneForcedReturnThreshold m (J + 1) = mersenneForcedReturnThreshold m J + mersenneWeight (m + J + 1) - ((1 : ℝ) / 2) ^ (m + J + 1)

declarations.name: mersenneForcedReturnThreshold_strictMono
declarations.signature: theorem mersenneForcedReturnThreshold_strictMono (m J : ℕ) : mersenneForcedReturnThreshold m J < mersenneForcedReturnThreshold m (J + 1)
declarations.docstring: Every extra forced take strictly enlarges the return region.

declarations.name: tendsto_mersenneForcedReturnThreshold
declarations.signature: theorem tendsto_mersenneForcedReturnThreshold (m : ℕ) : Tendsto (fun J : ℕ => mersenneForcedReturnThreshold m J) atTop (nhds (mersenneTail m))
declarations.docstring: The finite return thresholds increase to the true separatrix `T_m`.

declarations.name: exists_forcedBlock_return_of_lt_tail
declarations.signature: theorem exists_forcedBlock_return_of_lt_tail {m : ℕ} {r : ℝ} (hr : r < mersenneTail m) : ∃ J : ℕ, r - (∑ k ∈ Finset.range J, mersenneWeight (m + k + 1)) ≤ ((1 : ℝ) / 2) ^ (m + J)
declarations.docstring: Any relaxed state strictly below the full remaining tail returns to the dyadic-safe side after finitely many consecutive takes.

declarations.name: tail_sub_forcedBlock_eq_tail
declarations.signature: theorem tail_sub_forcedBlock_eq_tail (m J : ℕ) : mersenneTail m - (∑ k ∈ Finset.range J, mersenneWeight (m + k + 1)) = mersenneTail (m + J)
declarations.docstring: On the separatrix, subtracting a forced block leaves exactly the later tail.

declarations.name: dyadic_lt_forcedBlock_of_tail_lt
declarations.signature: theorem dyadic_lt_forcedBlock_of_tail_lt {m J : ℕ} {r : ℝ} (hr : mersenneTail m < r) : ((1 : ℝ) / 2) ^ (m + J) < r - (∑ k ∈ Finset.range J, mersenneWeight (m + k + 1))
declarations.docstring: Above the separatrix, the positive excess over the tail survives every forced block; in particular the state never reaches the dyadic-safe side.

declarations.name: halfTwoChannelCap_lt_forcedReturnThreshold_add_one
declarations.signature: theorem halfTwoChannelCap_lt_forcedReturnThreshold_add_one {m : ℕ} (hm : 0 < m) : halfTwoChannelCap m < mersenneForcedReturnThreshold m (m + 1)
declarations.docstring: Explicit linear return bound for the two-channel cap. At every positive starting level `m`, `m+1` consecutive forced takes suffice.

declarations.name: forcedBlock_return_of_le_twoChannelCap_add_one
declarations.signature: theorem forcedBlock_return_of_le_twoChannelCap_add_one {m : ℕ} (hm : 0 < m) {r : ℝ} (hr : r ≤ halfTwoChannelCap m) : r - (∑ k ∈ Finset.range (m + 1), mersenneWeight (m + k + 1)) ≤ ((1 : ℝ) / 2) ^ (m + (m + 1))

declarations.name: halfTwoChannelCap_lt_forcedReturnThreshold_ceilingHalf
declarations.docstring: Sharp paper-grade return bound for the landed two-channel cap.

declarations.name: forcedBlock_return_of_le_twoChannelCap_ceilingHalf
declarations.signature: theorem forcedBlock_return_of_le_twoChannelCap_ceilingHalf {m : ℕ} (hm : 0 < m) {r : ℝ} (hr : r ≤ halfTwoChannelCap m) : let J

declarations.name: exists_forcedBlock_return_of_le_twoChannelCap
declarations.signature: theorem exists_forcedBlock_return_of_le_twoChannelCap {m : ℕ} {r : ℝ} (hr : r ≤ halfTwoChannelCap m) : ∃ J : ℕ, r - (∑ k ∈ Finset.range J, mersenneWeight (m + k + 1)) ≤ ((1 : ℝ) / 2) ^ (m + J)
declarations.docstring: The landed two-channel cap is strictly inside the finite-return region for some block length. This is generic return geometry only: proving that the actual half orbit satisfies the cap at every skip remains the arithmetic producer.

declarations.name: undoubled_twoThirds_relaxed_escape_fixture
declarations.docstring: An undoubled coordinate below `2 / 3` does not force return for a generic relaxed sliver state.

declarations.name: doubled_twoThirds_below_but_above_tail_fixture
declarations.docstring: Even the doubled coordinate can lie below `2 / 3` while a generic state is already above the true tail. This prevents the limiting frozen-map fixed point from being reused as an exact finite separatrix.

declarations.name: halfGreedyMersennePrefix
declarations.docstring: The Mersenne part of the greedy prefix for the target `1/2`.

declarations.name: halfGreedyDyadicPrefix
declarations.docstring: The binary-skeleton part selected by the same finite greedy prefix.

declarations.name: halfGreedyCorrectionPrefix
declarations.docstring: The selected correction prefix `E_N`. Exponent one is harmless because the greedy run for `1/2` omits it.

declarations.name: halfGreedyComplementPrefix
declarations.docstring: The complementary dyadic prefix `C_N`. For positive `n`, the omitted exponent-one digit and the finite geometric sum reduce this to `1/2 - 2^{-n}` minus the selected dyadic prefix.

declarations.name: halfGreedyCorrectionPrefix_sub_complementPrefix
declarations.signature: theorem halfGreedyCorrectionPrefix_sub_complementPrefix (n : ℕ) : halfGreedyCorrectionPrefix n - halfGreedyComplementPrefix n = ((1 : ℝ) / 2) ^ n - greedyMersenneRemainder (1 / 2 : ℝ) n
declarations.docstring: The finite correction/complement difference is exactly the dyadic boundary minus the greedy residual.

declarations.name: halfGreedyCorrectionInterval_subset_complementInterval_iff
declarations.signature: theorem halfGreedyCorrectionInterval_subset_complementInterval_iff (n : ℕ) : Set.Icc (halfGreedyCorrectionPrefix n) (halfGreedyCorrectionPrefix n + mersenneCorrectionTail n) ⊆ Set.Icc (halfGreedyComplementPrefix n) (halfGreedyComplementPrefix n + ((1 : ℝ) / 2) ^ n) ↔ mersenneCorrectionTail n ≤ greedyMersenneRemainder (1 / 2 : ℝ) n ∧ greedyMersenneRemainder (1 / 2 : ℝ) n ≤ ((1 : ℝ) / 2) ^ n
declarations.docstring: The complete correction enclosure is trapped in the current complementary dyadic cylinder exactly on the residual self-trapping strip.

declarations.name: greedyMersenneComplementRemainder
declarations.signature: noncomputable def greedyMersenneComplementRemainder (x : ℝ) (n : ℕ) : ℝ
declarations.docstring: The complementary part of the surviving tail mass.

declarations.name: greedyMersenneSignedBalance
declarations.signature: noncomputable def greedyMersenneSignedBalance (x : ℝ) (n : ℕ) : ℝ
declarations.docstring: Signed difference between the residual and its complementary tail bin.

declarations.name: mersenneGap
declarations.docstring: The positive gap between one Mersenne weight and the tail after it.

declarations.name: greedyMersenneSignedBalance_eq
declarations.signature: theorem greedyMersenneSignedBalance_eq (x : ℝ) (n : ℕ) : greedyMersenneSignedBalance x n = 2 * greedyMersenneRemainder x n - mersenneTail n

declarations.name: mersenneGap_pos
declarations.signature: theorem mersenneGap_pos {n : ℕ} (hn : 0 < n) : 0 < mersenneGap n

declarations.name: halfGreedy_selfTrapping_iff_abs_signedBalance_le
declarations.signature: theorem halfGreedy_selfTrapping_iff_abs_signedBalance_le (n : ℕ) : (mersenneCorrectionTail n ≤ greedyMersenneRemainder (1 / 2 : ℝ) n ∧ greedyMersenneRemainder (1 / 2 : ℝ) n ≤ ((1 : ℝ) / 2) ^ n) ↔ |greedyMersenneSignedBalance (1 / 2 : ℝ) n| ≤ ((1 : ℝ) / 2) ^ n - mersenneCorrectionTail n
declarations.docstring: In the signed-balance coordinate, the self-trapping strip is the centered interval of radius `2^{-N} - η_N`.

declarations.name: greedyMersenneSignedBalance_succ
declarations.signature: theorem greedyMersenneSignedBalance_succ (x : ℝ) (n : ℕ) : greedyMersenneSignedBalance x (n + 1) = if mersenneWeight (n + 1) ≤ greedyMersenneRemainder x n then greedyMersenneSignedBalance x n - mersenneWeight (n + 1) else greedyMersenneSignedBalance x n + mersenneWeight (n + 1)
declarations.docstring: Exact one-step affine recurrence before folding: a selected weight moves the balance left by that weight, while an omitted weight moves it right.

declarations.name: greedyMersenne_next_survives_iff_gap_le_abs_signedBalance
declarations.signature: theorem greedyMersenne_next_survives_iff_gap_le_abs_signedBalance {x : ℝ} {n : ℕ} (_hremNonneg : 0 ≤ greedyMersenneRemainder x n) (hremTail : greedyMersenneRemainder x n ≤ mersenneTail n) : greedyMersenneRemainder x (n + 1) ≤ mersenneTail (n + 1) ↔ mersenneGap (n + 1) ≤ |greedyMersenneSignedBalance x n|
declarations.docstring: For a currently surviving residual, survival of the next greedy step is equivalent to the signed balance avoiding the open gap around zero.

declarations.name: abs_greedyMersenneSignedBalance_succ
declarations.signature: theorem abs_greedyMersenneSignedBalance_succ {x : ℝ} {n : ℕ} (_hremTail : greedyMersenneRemainder x n ≤ mersenneTail n) (hnext : greedyMersenneRemainder x (n + 1) ≤ mersenneTail (n + 1)) : |greedyMersenneSignedBalance x (n + 1)| = abs (|greedyMersenneSignedBalance x n| - mersenneWeight (n + 1))
declarations.docstring: On a surviving next step, the signed balance follows the exact folded subtraction recurrence.

declarations.name: erdosBorweinMersenneConstant
declarations.docstring: The Erdős–Borwein constant, expressed in the positive Mersenne-tail coordinate already used throughout this file.

declarations.name: irrational_erdosBorweinMersenneConstant
declarations.docstring: The tail-coordinate version of the Erdős–Borwein constant inherits the unconditional irrationality theorem already proved in the certificate kernel.

declarations.name: erdosBorweinMersenneConstant_eq_greedy_add_skipped
declarations.signature: theorem erdosBorweinMersenneConstant_eq_greedy_add_skipped (x : ℝ) : erdosBorweinMersenneConstant = positiveMersenneSupportValue (greedyMersenneSupport x) + positiveMersenneSupportValue (greedyMersenneSkippedSupport x)
declarations.docstring: Selected and omitted positive exponents partition the full Erdős–Borwein series.

declarations.name: positiveMersenneSupportValue_eq_cast_finiteErdosSum
declarations.signature: theorem positiveMersenneSupportValue_eq_cast_finiteErdosSum (F : Finset ℕ) : positiveMersenneSupportValue (↑F : Set ℕ) = ((finiteErdosSum F 2 : ℚ) : ℝ)
declarations.docstring: A finite normalized support value is the real cast of its exact rational finite Erdős sum.

declarations.name: infinite_greedyMersenneSkippedSupport_of_half_mem
declarations.docstring: If `1/2` is represented, its canonical greedy expansion must omit infinitely many positive exponents. Otherwise the irrational full Erdős–Borwein constant would be a rational number plus a finite rational Mersenne sum.

declarations.name: half_mem_mersenneAchievementSet_iff_greedySkippedSupport_infinite
declarations.docstring: **Greedy skip dichotomy for the half target.** Exact half-membership is equivalent to the canonical greedy orbit omitting infinitely many exponents. The forward direction uses Erdős–Borwein irrationality; the reverse direction uses absorbing fatal states.

declarations.name: mersennePrefixMass
declarations.docstring: The total mass of the first `n` positive Mersenne weights.

declarations.name: mersenneSupportPrefix
declarations.signature: noncomputable def mersenneSupportPrefix (A : Set ℕ) (n : ℕ) : ℝ
declarations.docstring: The mass selected by an arbitrary support through exponent `n`.

declarations.name: mersenneSupportPrefix_succ
declarations.signature: theorem mersenneSupportPrefix_succ (A : Set ℕ) (n : ℕ) : mersenneSupportPrefix A (n + 1) = mersenneSupportPrefix A n + Set.indicator A mersenneWeight (n + 1)

declarations.name: mersenneShadowOfPrefix
declarations.signature: noncomputable def mersenneShadowOfPrefix (A : Set ℕ) (n : ℕ) : ℝ
declarations.docstring: The signed Mersenne shadow associated with an arbitrary support prefix.

declarations.name: halfGreedyMersenneShadow
declarations.docstring: The shadow of the actual greedy prefix for the target `1/2`.

declarations.name: erdosBorweinMersenneConstant_eq_prefix_add_tail
declarations.signature: theorem erdosBorweinMersenneConstant_eq_prefix_add_tail (n : ℕ) : erdosBorweinMersenneConstant = mersennePrefixMass n + mersenneTail n

declarations.name: halfGreedyMersenneShadow_sub_constant_eq_signedBalance
declarations.signature: theorem halfGreedyMersenneShadow_sub_constant_eq_signedBalance (n : ℕ) : halfGreedyMersenneShadow n - erdosBorweinMersenneConstant = greedyMersenneSignedBalance (1 / 2 : ℝ) n
declarations.docstring: Exact shadow/signed-balance duality. This turns a death-hole hit into an Archimedean near-hit of the Erdős–Borwein constant without introducing a new dynamical model.

declarations.name: halfGreedyShadow_closer_of_prefix_separation
declarations.signature: theorem halfGreedyShadow_closer_of_prefix_separation {n : ℕ} (hsurvive : greedyMersenneRemainder (1 / 2 : ℝ) n ≤ mersenneTail n) {A : Set ℕ} (hseparate : mersenneTail n < |mersenneSupportPrefix A n - halfGreedyMersennePrefix n|) : |erdosBorweinMersenneConstant - halfGreedyMersenneShadow n| < |erdosBorweinMersenneConstant - mersenneShadowOfPrefix A n|
declarations.docstring: Once strict superincreasingness supplies a tail-sized separation from a competing prefix, the surviving greedy prefix is strictly closer to the Erdős–Borwein constant. This is the reusable analytic core of the proposed global nearest-shadow argument.

declarations.name: halfGreedy_next_survives_iff_shadow_separated
declarations.signature: theorem halfGreedy_next_survives_iff_shadow_separated {n : ℕ} (hsurvive : greedyMersenneRemainder (1 / 2 : ℝ) n ≤ mersenneTail n) : greedyMersenneRemainder (1 / 2 : ℝ) (n + 1) ≤ mersenneTail (n + 1) ↔ mersenneGap (n + 1) ≤ |halfGreedyMersenneShadow n - erdosBorweinMersenneConstant|
declarations.docstring: Under current survival, next-step survival is exactly separation of the greedy shadow from the open Mersenne-gap neighbourhood of the constant.

declarations.name: greedyMersenneSecondChannelPhase
declarations.docstring: The normalized phase at the first scale where the second geometric channel of the Mersenne tail is visible.

declarations.name: greedyMersenneSecondChannelPhase_le_one_six_iff_twoChannelCap
declarations.signature: theorem greedyMersenneSecondChannelPhase_le_one_six_iff_twoChannelCap (n : ℕ) : greedyMersenneSecondChannelPhase n ≤ 1 / 6 ↔ greedyMersenneRemainder (1 / 2 : ℝ) n ≤ halfTwoChannelCap (n + 1)
declarations.docstring: The existing skipped-branch two-channel cap is exactly the one-sided second-channel phase inequality `φ_n ≤ 1 / 6`. No skip hypothesis is needed for this algebraic equivalence; the arithmetic producer is proving the inequality at every actual skipped rank.

declarations.name: greedyMersenneSecondChannelPhaseRat
declarations.docstring: Exact rational model of the second-channel phase. The unresolved separation producer is arithmetic over `ℚ`; real analysis is needed only by its already-landed consumer.

declarations.name: cast_greedyMersenneSecondChannelPhaseRat
declarations.signature: @[simp] theorem cast_greedyMersenneSecondChannelPhaseRat (n : ℕ) : ((greedyMersenneSecondChannelPhaseRat n : ℚ) : ℝ) = greedyMersenneSecondChannelPhase n

declarations.name: HalfSecondChannelSeparatedRat
declarations.docstring: Decidable rational form of the shrinking-hole avoidance condition.

declarations.name: secondChannelSeparation_of_rat
declarations.signature: theorem secondChannelSeparation_of_rat {n : ℕ} (h : HalfSecondChannelSeparatedRat n) : (1 / 6 : ℝ) + (37 / 56 : ℝ) * ((1 : ℝ) / 2) ^ n ≤ |greedyMersenneSecondChannelPhase n - 1 / 3|
declarations.docstring: The rational certificate casts directly to the real separation estimate consumed by the greedy-survival theorem.

declarations.name: halfSecondChannelSeparatedRat_of_pos_le_six
declarations.signature: theorem halfSecondChannelSeparatedRat_of_pos_le_six {n : ℕ} (hn : 0 < n) (h6 : n ≤ 6) : HalfSecondChannelSeparatedRat n
declarations.docstring: Exact finite certificate for the only small levels visible in the second-channel numerics. The infinite producer can therefore start at `n = 7`, where the observed margin is already large.

declarations.name: four_pow_mul_signedBalance_eq_secondChannelPhase_sub
declarations.signature: theorem four_pow_mul_signedBalance_eq_secondChannelPhase_sub (n : ℕ) : (4 : ℝ) ^ n * greedyMersenneSignedBalance (1 / 2 : ℝ) n = greedyMersenneSecondChannelPhase n - 1 / 3 - (4 : ℝ) ^ n * mersenneWeightRemainderTail n
declarations.docstring: Exact decomposition of the scaled signed balance into its second-channel phase, the universal `1/3` centre, and the higher-channel tail.

declarations.name: four_pow_mul_mersenneWeightRemainderTail_le
declarations.signature: theorem four_pow_mul_mersenneWeightRemainderTail_le (n : ℕ) : (4 : ℝ) ^ n * mersenneWeightRemainderTail n ≤ (2 / 7 : ℝ) * ((1 : ℝ) / 2) ^ n
declarations.docstring: Scaling the higher-channel tail by `4^n` leaves an explicit exponentially small error.

declarations.name: four_pow_mul_next_mersenneGap_le
declarations.signature: theorem four_pow_mul_next_mersenneGap_le (n : ℕ) : (4 : ℝ) ^ n * mersenneGap (n + 1) ≤ (1 / 6 : ℝ) + (3 / 8 : ℝ) * ((1 : ℝ) / 2) ^ n
declarations.docstring: The next Mersenne gap at second-channel scale, including the exact higher-channel error budget used by the separation consumer.

declarations.name: halfGreedy_next_survives_of_secondChannelSeparation
declarations.signature: theorem halfGreedy_next_survives_of_secondChannelSeparation {n : ℕ} (hsurvive : greedyMersenneRemainder (1 / 2 : ℝ) n ≤ mersenneTail n) (hseparate : (1 / 6 : ℝ) + (37 / 56 : ℝ) * ((1 : ℝ) / 2) ^ n ≤ |greedyMersenneSecondChannelPhase n - 1 / 3|) : greedyMersenneRemainder (1 / 2 : ℝ) (n + 1) ≤ mersenneTail (n + 1)
declarations.docstring: A clean second-channel separation estimate is sufficient for one more greedy survival step. The constant `37/56 = 2/7 + 3/8` is exactly the sum of the scaled higher-tail and gap-error budgets.

declarations.name: half_mem_mersenneAchievementSet_of_secondChannelSeparation
declarations.signature: theorem half_mem_mersenneAchievementSet_of_secondChannelSeparation (hseparate : ∀ n : ℕ, 0 < n → (1 / 6 : ℝ) + (37 / 56 : ℝ) * ((1 : ℝ) / 2) ^ n ≤ |greedyMersenneSecondChannelPhase n - 1 / 3|) : (1 / 2 : ℝ) ∈ mersenneAchievementSet
declarations.docstring: An all-level second-channel separation theorem would decide the concrete membership question `1/2 ∈ K`. All analytic error accounting and the finite base cases are discharged here; the hypothesis is precisely the remaining shrinking-hole avoidance obligation.

declarations.name: half_mem_mersenneAchievementSet_of_secondChannelSeparationRat_from_seven
declarations.signature: theorem half_mem_mersenneAchievementSet_of_secondChannelSeparationRat_from_seven (hseparate : ∀ n : ℕ, 7 ≤ n → HalfSecondChannelSeparatedRat n) : (1 / 2 : ℝ) ∈ mersenneAchievementSet
declarations.docstring: Proof-search form of the half-membership consumer: all levels through six are checked exactly, and the remaining hypothesis is a decidable statement about the rational greedy orbit only.

declarations.name: positiveDyadicSupportValue
declarations.signature: noncomputable def positiveDyadicSupportValue (A : Set ℕ) : ℝ
declarations.docstring: The selected part of the binary skeleton, indexed exactly like `positiveMersenneSupportValue`.

declarations.name: summable_positiveDyadicSupportIndicator
declarations.signature: theorem summable_positiveDyadicSupportIndicator (A : Set ℕ) : Summable (fun k : ℕ => Set.indicator A (fun n : ℕ => ((1 : ℝ) / 2) ^ n) (k + 1))

declarations.name: halfCorrectionValue
declarations.signature: noncomputable def halfCorrectionValue (A : Set ℕ) : ℝ
declarations.docstring: The correction side of the half fixed-point equation.

declarations.name: halfComplementValue
declarations.signature: noncomputable def halfComplementValue (A : Set ℕ) : ℝ
declarations.docstring: The complementary binary side of the half fixed-point equation. For a support omitting exponent one this is the sum of the complementary digits from exponent two onward.

declarations.name: halfComplementValue_eq_tsum_complement
declarations.signature: theorem halfComplementValue_eq_tsum_complement (A : Set ℕ) (hA1 : 1 ∉ A) : halfComplementValue A = ∑' k : ℕ, Set.indicator Aᶜ (fun n : ℕ => ((1 : ℝ) / 2) ^ n) (k + 2)
declarations.docstring: The complementary value is genuinely the binary number whose digits from exponent two onward are the complements of the support digits.

declarations.name: positiveMersenneSupportValue_eq_half_iff_correction_eq_complement
declarations.signature: theorem positiveMersenneSupportValue_eq_half_iff_correction_eq_complement (A : Set ℕ) : positiveMersenneSupportValue A = (1 / 2 : ℝ) ↔ halfCorrectionValue A = halfComplementValue A
declarations.docstring: Exact correction/complement reframe of a value-`1/2` support.

declarations.name: HalfPrefixForcingChain
declarations.docstring: An all-level prefix-forcing witness. The interval `[correctionLower k, correctionUpper k]` is the complete correction enclosure at macrostep `k`; `interval_trapped` puts that whole enclosure in one dyadic cylinder. The complementary binary value lies in the same cylinder, and the cylinder depths tend to infinity. The finite-word construction that produces these data is deliberately kept outside the structure: different evaluators may supply the same analytic certificate without creating a second achievement-set model.

declarations.name: HalfPrefixForcingChain.correction_eq_complement
declarations.signature: theorem HalfPrefixForcingChain.correction_eq_complement {A : Set ℕ} (chain : HalfPrefixForcingChain A) : halfCorrectionValue A = halfComplementValue A
declarations.docstring: Two values trapped in the same unbounded sequence of dyadic cylinders are equal. This is the analytic coinduction step: every finite macrostep contributes a genuine error bound, and only unboundedness is asymptotic.

declarations.name: half_mem_mersenneAchievementSet_of_prefixForcingChain
declarations.signature: theorem half_mem_mersenneAchievementSet_of_prefixForcingChain (A : Set ℕ) (hA0 : 0 ∉ A) (_hA1 : 1 ∉ A) (chain : HalfPrefixForcingChain A) : (1 / 2 : ℝ) ∈ mersenneAchievementSet
declarations.docstring: Unbounded prefix forcing constructs an exact value-`1/2` support. The normalization hypotheses make the support genuinely positive-indexed and give the intended complementary-digit interpretation.

declarations.name: integer_crossing_unique
declarations.signature: theorem integer_crossing_unique {x τ : ℝ} (hτ : τ ≤ 1) {m₁ m₂ : ℤ} (hm₁L : x < (m₁ : ℝ)) (hm₁R : (m₁ : ℝ) < x + τ) (hm₂L : x < (m₂ : ℝ)) (hm₂R : (m₂ : ℝ) < x + τ) : m₁ = m₂
declarations.docstring: An interval of width at most one crosses at most one integer. Applied after scaling a correction interval by `4^N`, this isolates the unique boundary whose two-adic divisibility controls the forcing defect.

declarations.name: dyadicBoundary_cancel_powTwo
declarations.signature: theorem dyadicBoundary_cancel_powTwo (N v q : ℕ) (hv : v ≤ 2 * N) : (((2 ^ v * q : ℕ) : ℝ) / (4 : ℝ) ^ N) = (q : ℝ) / (2 : ℝ) ^ (2 * N - v)
declarations.docstring: Cancelling an explicit power of two from a boundary on the `4^N` grid. When `q` is odd and `v` is the exact two-adic valuation of the numerator, the right-hand denominator has the reduced dyadic depth `2*N-v`.

declarations.name: crossedBoundary_forceDepth_gt_iff
declarations.signature: theorem crossedBoundary_forceDepth_gt_iff {N v : ℕ} (hN : 1 < N) : N < 2 * N - v - 1 ↔ v < N - 1
declarations.docstring: The exact growth criterion once a crossed boundary has forcing depth `2*N-v-1`: strict extension beyond `N` is equivalent to excluding `v ≥ N-1`.

declarations.name: parent
declarations.docstring: Canonical inverse parent for a target carry under common coefficient `c`. The extra `1` is the usual natural-number expression for the ceiling of `(k + c) / 2`.

declarations.name: rewind
declarations.signature: def rewind : List ℕ → ℕ → ℕ | [], k => k | c :: cs, k => rewind cs (parent c k) /-- Numerator offset accumulated
declarations.docstring: Apply the coefficient rows in list order, earliest row first.

declarations.name: offset
declarations.signature: def offset : List ℕ → ℕ | [] => 0 | c :: cs => c + 1 + 2 * offset cs /-- Common denominator in the closed form. -/ def denom (cs : List ℕ) : ℕ
declarations.docstring: Numerator offset accumulated by a rewind. A later parent map is one binary place more significant than every earlier map.

declarations.name: denom
declarations.docstring: Common denominator in the closed form.

declarations.name: phase
declarations.docstring: The dyadic phase of the left endpoint `1`.

declarations.name: denom_pos
declarations.signature: @[simp] theorem denom_pos (cs : List ℕ) : 0 < denom cs

declarations.name: rewind_eq_closedForm
declarations.signature: theorem rewind_eq_closedForm (cs : List ℕ) (k : ℕ) : rewind cs k = (k + offset cs) / denom cs
declarations.docstring: Exact iterate formula. Floor-of-floor composition is absorbed into a single division because every later translation is scaled by the appropriate power of two in `offset`.

declarations.name: rewind_mono
declarations.signature: theorem rewind_mono (cs : List ℕ) : Monotone (rewind cs)
declarations.docstring: Rewind is monotone in the terminal carry.

declarations.name: add_div_eq_left_div_iff_phase_fit
declarations.signature: theorem add_div_eq_left_div_iff_phase_fit {x y D : ℕ} (hD : 0 < D) (hy : y < D) : (x + y) / D = x / D ↔ x % D + y < D
declarations.docstring: Adding a numerator shorter than the denominator preserves the current quotient exactly iff it does not overflow the current residue.

declarations.name: rewind_endpoint_le_succ
declarations.signature: theorem rewind_endpoint_le_succ (cs : List ℕ) {B : ℕ} (hB : 1 ≤ B) (hwidth : B ≤ denom cs) : rewind cs B ≤ rewind cs 1 + 1
declarations.docstring: If the input interval `[1,B]` has width at most the rewind denominator, its two endpoint ancestors differ by at most one. This is the corrected contraction statement: a singleton conclusion requires phase information.

declarations.name: rewind_endpoint_eq_iff_phase_fit
declarations.signature: theorem rewind_endpoint_eq_iff_phase_fit (cs : List ℕ) {B : ℕ} (hB : 1 ≤ B) (hwidth : B ≤ denom cs) : rewind cs B = rewind cs 1 ↔ phase cs + (B - 1) < denom cs
declarations.docstring: Exact singleton criterion for the endpoints.

declarations.name: IsSeamPair
declarations.signature: def IsSeamPair (cs : List ℕ) (B : ℕ) : Prop
declarations.docstring: The residual alternative after maximal fixed-coefficient contraction: the right endpoint has landed at the successor of the left endpoint. Every interior target then lands on one of these same two ancestors (proved below), so this is the exact scalar socket for a canonical one-hole seam argument.

declarations.name: isSeamPair_iff_phase_overflow
declarations.signature: theorem isSeamPair_iff_phase_overflow (cs : List ℕ) {B : ℕ} (hB : 1 ≤ B) (hwidth : B ≤ denom cs) : IsSeamPair cs B ↔ denom cs ≤ phase cs + (B - 1)
declarations.docstring: The seam-pair alternative is exactly dyadic phase overflow.

declarations.name: rewind_eq_left_or_right_of_isSeamPair
declarations.signature: theorem rewind_eq_left_or_right_of_isSeamPair (cs : List ℕ) {B k : ℕ} (hk : 1 ≤ k) (hkB : k ≤ B) (hseam : IsSeamPair cs B) : rewind cs k = rewind cs 1 ∨ rewind cs k = rewind cs 1 + 1
declarations.docstring: Under the seam-pair alternative, every rewound target lies on one of the two adjacent ancestors.

declarations.name: rewind_interval_singleton_iff_endpoint_eq
declarations.signature: theorem rewind_interval_singleton_iff_endpoint_eq (cs : List ℕ) {B : ℕ} (hB : 1 ≤ B) : (∀ k : ℕ, 1 ≤ k → k ≤ B → rewind cs k = rewind cs 1) ↔ rewind cs B = rewind cs 1
declarations.docstring: The entire interval is a singleton exactly when its endpoints agree.

declarations.name: rewind_interval_singleton_iff_phase_fit
declarations.signature: theorem rewind_interval_singleton_iff_phase_fit (cs : List ℕ) {B : ℕ} (hB : 1 ≤ B) (hwidth : B ≤ denom cs) : (∀ k : ℕ, 1 ≤ k → k ≤ B → rewind cs k = rewind cs 1) ↔ phase cs + (B - 1) < denom cs
declarations.docstring: Full interval phase criterion.

declarations.name: rewind_singleton_or_isSeamPair
declarations.signature: theorem rewind_singleton_or_isSeamPair (cs : List ℕ) {B : ℕ} (hB : 1 ≤ B) (hwidth : B ≤ denom cs) : (∀ k : ℕ, 1 ≤ k → k ≤ B → rewind cs k = rewind cs 1) ∨ IsSeamPair cs B
declarations.docstring: Correct fixed-tail contraction dichotomy. Width alone proves exactly a singleton-or-seam-pair result, never an unconditional singleton.

declarations.name: HalfCarryCofinalSelectedWindowOrProtectedSeam
declarations.docstring: Cofinal mixed producer: a full selected window or a localized realized seam may witness each requested scale.

declarations.name: cofinalAdmissibility_of_cofinalSelectedWindowOrProtectedSeam
declarations.docstring: Every mixed cofinal receipt contains the one admissible word required by the landed inverse-system compactness theorem.

declarations.name: exists_infinite_support_half_of_cofinalSelectedWindowOrProtectedSeam
declarations.signature: theorem exists_infinite_support_half_of_cofinalSelectedWindowOrProtectedSeam (hsupply : HalfCarryCofinalSelectedWindowOrProtectedSeam) : ∃ A : Set ℕ, A.Infinite ∧ erdosSupportSeries 2 A = (1 : ℝ) / 2
declarations.docstring: The mixed cofinal producer is already sufficient for an infinite support whose Mersenne achievement sum is exactly `1 / 2`.

declarations.name: ProtectedEvenSeamRealizedAt
declarations.signature: def ProtectedEvenSeamRealizedAt (N : ℕ) (δ c : ℤ) : Prop
declarations.docstring: At one depth, every scalar carry in the protected window that survives the exact seam is realized by an admissible finite word. Only the forward realization direction is needed; neither full-strip equivalence nor seam escape is assumed.

declarations.name: HalfCarryCofinalProtectedEvenSeamRealization
declarations.docstring: Cofinal supply of localized, realized one-hole seams.

declarations.name: cofinalAdmissibility_of_cofinalProtectedEvenSeamRealization
declarations.docstring: A one-hole seam cannot delete both protected carries `3` and `4`. Choosing the other one yields the sole finite word required by cofinal admissibility.

declarations.name: exists_infinite_support_half_of_cofinalProtectedEvenSeamRealization
declarations.signature: theorem exists_infinite_support_half_of_cofinalProtectedEvenSeamRealization (hsupply : HalfCarryCofinalProtectedEvenSeamRealization) : ∃ A : Set ℕ, A.Infinite ∧ erdosSupportSeries 2 A = (1 : ℝ) / 2
declarations.docstring: Localized seam realization is already a complete arithmetic producer for an infinite Mersenne support summing to `1/2`.

declarations.name: halfStripBound
declarations.docstring: The discrete square-root strip used by the half-carry search.

declarations.name: integerHalfCarry
declarations.signature: noncomputable def integerHalfCarry (A : Set ℕ) : ℕ → ℤ
declarations.docstring: The integer carry whose state at time `N` is the packet's `K_{N+1}`.

declarations.name: integerHalfCarry_zero
declarations.signature: @[simp] theorem integerHalfCarry_zero (A : Set ℕ) : integerHalfCarry A 0 = 1

declarations.name: integerHalfCarry_succ
declarations.signature: @[simp] theorem integerHalfCarry_succ (A : Set ℕ) (N : ℕ) : integerHalfCarry A (N + 1) = 2 * integerHalfCarry A N - (supportCoeff A (N + 2) : ℤ)

declarations.name: integerHalfCarry_inter_Iic_eq_of_succ_le
declarations.signature: theorem integerHalfCarry_inter_Iic_eq_of_succ_le (A : Set ℕ) (M : ℕ) : ∀ N : ℕ, N + 1 ≤ M → integerHalfCarry (A ∩ Set.Iic M) N = integerHalfCarry A N
declarations.docstring: Restricting a support above `M` does not change its integer half carry through level `M`.

declarations.name: integerHalfCarry_insert_future_eq
declarations.signature: theorem integerHalfCarry_insert_future_eq (A : Set ℕ) {m n : ℕ} (hmn : m + 2 ≤ n) : integerHalfCarry (insert n A) m = integerHalfCarry A m
declarations.docstring: Inserting a support bit at row `n` does not change any carry whose next coefficient row is still strictly before `n`. This is the exact bridge used to regard the two choices of the row-`n` bit as children of one parent.

declarations.name: sqrt_le_pred_sqrt_add_one
declarations.signature: theorem sqrt_le_pred_sqrt_add_one {n : ℕ} (hn : 1 ≤ n) : Nat.sqrt n ≤ Nat.sqrt (n - 1) + 1
declarations.docstring: The elementary square-root change across one row.

declarations.name: halfStripBound_add_coeff_le_two_mul_pred
declarations.signature: theorem halfStripBound_add_coeff_le_two_mul_pred {n c : ℕ} (hn : 2 ≤ n) (hc : c ≤ 2 * Nat.sqrt n) : halfStripBound n + c ≤ 2 * halfStripBound (n - 1)
declarations.docstring: The sharp divisor envelope leaves enough room for the inverse parent at the preceding row.

declarations.name: HalfWord
declarations.docstring: A Boolean support word through exponent `N`. Index zero is retained so restriction is literal; admissibility forces exponents zero and one off.

declarations.name: restrictWord
declarations.signature: def restrictWord {M N : ℕ} (hMN : M ≤ N) (a : HalfWord N) : HalfWord M
declarations.docstring: Restrict a word at level `N` to an earlier level `M`.

declarations.name: restrictWord_refl
declarations.signature: @[simp] theorem restrictWord_refl {N : ℕ} (a : HalfWord N) : restrictWord (le_refl N) a = a

declarations.name: restrictWord_trans
declarations.signature: theorem restrictWord_trans {L M N : ℕ} (hLM : L ≤ M) (hMN : M ≤ N) (a : HalfWord N) : restrictWord hLM (restrictWord hMN a) = restrictWord (hLM.trans hMN) a

declarations.name: wordSupport
declarations.signature: def wordSupport {N : ℕ} (a : HalfWord N) : Set ℕ
declarations.docstring: The set represented by a finite Boolean word.

declarations.name: wordSupport_restrictWord
declarations.signature: theorem wordSupport_restrictWord {M N : ℕ} (hMN : M ≤ N) (a : HalfWord N) : wordSupport (restrictWord hMN a) = wordSupport a ∩ Set.Iic M

declarations.name: HalfStripAdmissible
declarations.signature: def HalfStripAdmissible (N : ℕ) (a : HalfWord N) : Prop
declarations.docstring: All-prefix strip admissibility for one finite Boolean word.

declarations.name: HalfStripAdmissible.restrict
declarations.signature: theorem HalfStripAdmissible.restrict {M N : ℕ} (hMN : M ≤ N) {a : HalfWord N} (ha : HalfStripAdmissible N a) : HalfStripAdmissible M (restrictWord hMN a)
declarations.docstring: Admissibility is stable under restriction, the key functorial fact needed by the inverse-system compactness argument.

declarations.name: HalfTerminalReachable
declarations.docstring: A terminal carry is reachable when some admissible Boolean word has that carry.

declarations.name: HalfCarryFullStripReachabilityAt
declarations.docstring: Full terminal-strip reachability at level `N`.

declarations.name: FixedCoeffChild
declarations.signature: def FixedCoeffChild (c h k : ℤ) : Prop
declarations.docstring: The two children of a parent carry under a fixed proper-divisor coefficient.

declarations.name: fixedCoeffChild_existsUnique
declarations.signature: theorem fixedCoeffChild_existsUnique (c k : ℤ) : ∃! h : ℤ, FixedCoeffChild c h k
declarations.docstring: Every integer has a unique fixed-coefficient parent and Boolean child bit. This is the parity inverse behind interval tiling.

declarations.name: fixedCoeffChild_existsUnique_parent_in_pred_strip
declarations.signature: theorem fixedCoeffChild_existsUnique_parent_in_pred_strip {n c k : ℕ} (hn : 2 ≤ n) (hk : 1 ≤ k) (hkB : k ≤ halfStripBound n) (hc : c ≤ 2 * Nat.sqrt n) : ∃! h : ℤ, (1 : ℤ) ≤ h ∧ h ≤ halfStripBound (n - 1) ∧ FixedCoeffChild c h k
declarations.docstring: The parity inverse for a target inside the new strip has its unique parent inside the preceding strip. This is the proof-bearing fixed-coefficient tiling step, with the square-root headroom discharged explicitly.

declarations.name: EvenSeamReachable
declarations.signature: def EvenSeamReachable (δ c k : ℤ) : Prop
declarations.docstring: Child geometry when the half-divisor bit changes at parent `δ`: parents at or below `δ` have coefficient `c+1`; parents above it have coefficient `c`.

declarations.name: evenSeamReachable_iff
declarations.signature: theorem evenSeamReachable_iff (δ c k : ℤ) : EvenSeamReachable δ c k ↔ k ≠ 2 * δ - c
declarations.docstring: **Exact one-hole theorem.** The unresolved half-divisor transition reaches every integer except `2δ-c`; there are no other holes.

declarations.name: EvenSeamNumeratorReachable
declarations.signature: def EvenSeamNumeratorReachable (δ c T k q : ℤ) : Prop
declarations.docstring: Endpoint-bearing version of `EvenSeamReachable`. If a parent with numerator `T-h` receives child bit `b`, its child numerator is `2*(T-h)+b`. The two sides of the seam use fixed coefficients `c+1` and `c`, respectively.

declarations.name: evenSeamNumeratorReachable_iff_twoSheets
declarations.signature: theorem evenSeamNumeratorReachable_iff_twoSheets (δ c T k q : ℤ) : EvenSeamNumeratorReachable δ c T k q ↔ (k < 2 * δ - c ∧ q = (2 * T - c - 1) - k) ∨ (2 * δ - c < k ∧ q = (2 * T - c) - k)
declarations.docstring: **Exact two-sheet normal form.** Away from the missing carry `2*δ-c`, the seam has one affine numerator sheet on each side. Below the hole its endpoint is `2*T-c-1`; above the hole its endpoint is `2*T-c`.

declarations.name: evenSeam_recylindrifies_of_hole_below_strip
declarations.signature: theorem evenSeam_recylindrifies_of_hole_below_strip {δ c T : ℤ} {k : ℕ} (hk : 1 ≤ k) (hhole : 2 * δ - c < 1) : EvenSeamNumeratorReachable δ c T k ((2 * T - c) - k)
declarations.docstring: If the seam hole lies below the target strip, every target numerator is on the upper sheet, so the strip is a single cylinder with endpoint `2*T-c`.

declarations.name: evenSeam_recylindrifies_of_hole_above_strip
declarations.signature: theorem evenSeam_recylindrifies_of_hole_above_strip {δ c T : ℤ} {B k : ℕ} (hkB : k ≤ B) (hhole : (B : ℤ) < 2 * δ - c) : EvenSeamNumeratorReachable δ c T k ((2 * T - c - 1) - k)
declarations.docstring: If the seam hole lies above the target strip, every target numerator is on the lower sheet, so the strip is a single cylinder with endpoint `2*T-c-1`.

declarations.name: depth14_evenSeam_three_not_reachable
declarations.docstring: Negative regression fixture: the depth-14 scalar data `δ=2`, `c=1` misses terminal carry `3` exactly.

declarations.name: evenSeamReachable_in_strip_iff
declarations.signature: theorem evenSeamReachable_in_strip_iff (B : ℕ) (δ c : ℤ) {k : ℕ} (hk : 1 ≤ k) (hkB : k ≤ B) : EvenSeamReachable δ c k ↔ k ∈ Finset.Icc 1 B ∧ (k : ℤ) ≠ 2 * δ - c
declarations.docstring: Strip-restricted form of the exact one-hole theorem.

declarations.name: IsCanonicalEvenSeam
declarations.signature: def IsCanonicalEvenSeam (d : ℕ) (δ c : ℤ) : Prop
declarations.docstring: The coordinate bridge asserting that one actual even transition is exactly the scalar seam model. The cylinder induction is expected to produce this interface whenever exponent `d` is unresolved at depth `2d-1`.

declarations.name: HalfCarryEvenSeamEscape
declarations.docstring: The open arithmetic obligation: every post-base canonical half-divisor seam must miss the target strip.

declarations.name: HalfCarryCanonicalEvenSeamSupply
declarations.docstring: Producer interface asserting that every post-base even depth admits the canonical one-seam coordinate. This is deliberately separate from seam escape: a solver may construct the coordinate and prove its scalar escape by different arguments.

declarations.name: fullStripAt_even_of_canonicalSeam_escape
declarations.signature: theorem fullStripAt_even_of_canonicalSeam_escape {d : ℕ} {δ c : ℤ} (hcanonical : IsCanonicalEvenSeam d δ c) (hescape : 2 * δ - c < 1 ∨ (halfStripBound (2 * d) : ℤ) < 2 * δ - c) : HalfCarryFullStripReachabilityAt (2 * d)
declarations.docstring: Once a canonical seam is outside the strip, the exact one-hole theorem immediately restores full terminal reachability at that even level.

declarations.name: fullStripAt_even_of_evenSeamSupply_escape
declarations.signature: theorem fullStripAt_even_of_evenSeamSupply_escape (hcanonical : HalfCarryCanonicalEvenSeamSupply) (hescape : HalfCarryEvenSeamEscape) {d : ℕ} (hd : 10 ≤ d) : HalfCarryFullStripReachabilityAt (2 * d)
declarations.docstring: The two independent even-level producers compose to full terminal-strip reachability at every post-base even depth.

declarations.name: exists_bool_sequence_of_prefix_closed_finite_words
declarations.signature: theorem exists_bool_sequence_of_prefix_closed_finite_words (Good : ∀ N : ℕ, HalfWord N → Prop) (hrestrict : ∀ {M N : ℕ} (hMN : M ≤ N) {a : HalfWord N}, Good N a → Good M (restrictWord hMN a)) (hnonempty : ∀ N : ℕ, ∃ a : HalfWord N, Good N a) : ∃ a : ℕ → Bool, ∀ N : ℕ, Good N (fun i ↦ a i)
declarations.docstring: A coherent sequence of nonempty finite Boolean-prefix families has an infinite Boolean diagonal whose every finite prefix belongs to the family.

declarations.name: exists_infinite_halfStripBranch
declarations.signature: theorem exists_infinite_halfStripBranch (hfinite : ∀ N : ℕ, ∃ a : HalfWord N, HalfStripAdmissible N a) : ∃ a : ℕ → Bool, ∀ N : ℕ, HalfStripAdmissible N (fun i ↦ a i)
declarations.docstring: Nonempty admissible words at every height yield one infinite Boolean word whose every finite prefix is strip-admissible.

declarations.name: exists_infinite_halfStripBranch_of_fullStrip
declarations.signature: theorem exists_infinite_halfStripBranch_of_fullStrip (hfull : ∀ N : ℕ, 1 ≤ N → HalfCarryFullStripReachabilityAt N) : ∃ a : ℕ → Bool, ∀ N : ℕ, HalfStripAdmissible N (fun i ↦ a i)
declarations.docstring: Full terminal-strip reachability at every positive height supplies the nonempty levels required by compactness.

declarations.name: exists_infinite_halfStripBranch_of_eventualFullStrip
declarations.signature: theorem exists_infinite_halfStripBranch_of_eventualFullStrip (N₀ : ℕ) (hbase : ∃ a : HalfWord N₀, HalfStripAdmissible N₀ a) (hfull : ∀ N : ℕ, N₀ ≤ N → HalfCarryFullStripReachabilityAt N) : ∃ a : ℕ → Bool, ∀ N : ℕ, HalfStripAdmissible N (fun i ↦ a i)
declarations.docstring: One admissible base level together with full terminal strips from that level onward supplies the nonempty finite levels required by compactness. Earlier levels are obtained by restricting the base word.

declarations.name: boolSupport
declarations.signature: def boolSupport (a : ℕ → Bool) : Set ℕ
declarations.docstring: The support selected by an infinite Boolean word.

declarations.name: wordSupport_sequence_eq_inter_Iic
declarations.signature: theorem wordSupport_sequence_eq_inter_Iic (a : ℕ → Bool) (N : ℕ) : wordSupport (fun i : Fin (N + 1) ↦ a i) = boolSupport a ∩ Set.Iic N
declarations.docstring: A finite word cut from an infinite Boolean word represents exactly the intersection of its support with the corresponding initial interval.

declarations.name: halfStripBranch_integerHalfCarry_bounds
declarations.signature: theorem halfStripBranch_integerHalfCarry_bounds (a : ℕ → Bool) (hbranch : ∀ N : ℕ, HalfStripAdmissible N (fun i ↦ a i)) (n : ℕ) (hn : 1 ≤ n) : (1 : ℤ) ≤ integerHalfCarry (boolSupport a) (n - 1) ∧ integerHalfCarry (boolSupport a) (n - 1) ≤ halfStripBound n
declarations.docstring: Every coherent finite-prefix strip bound transfers to the global support carry at the same level.

declarations.name: one_not_mem_boolSupport_of_halfStripBranch
declarations.signature: theorem one_not_mem_boolSupport_of_halfStripBranch (a : ℕ → Bool) (hbranch : ∀ N : ℕ, HalfStripAdmissible N (fun i ↦ a i)) : 1 ∉ boolSupport a
declarations.docstring: The exponent-one guard carried by every admissible branch.

declarations.name: zero_not_mem_boolSupport_of_halfStripBranch
declarations.signature: theorem zero_not_mem_boolSupport_of_halfStripBranch (a : ℕ → Bool) (hbranch : ∀ N : ℕ, HalfStripAdmissible N (fun i ↦ a i)) : 0 ∉ boolSupport a
declarations.docstring: The index-zero guard carried by every admissible branch.

declarations.name: support_half_of_infinite_halfStripBranch
declarations.signature: theorem support_half_of_infinite_halfStripBranch (a : ℕ → Bool) (hbranch : ∀ N : ℕ, HalfStripAdmissible N (fun i ↦ a i)) : erdosSupportSeries 2 (boolSupport a) = (1 : ℝ) / 2
declarations.docstring: A coherent positive square-root-strip branch has exact support-series value `1/2`. This is the compactness consumer: it reuses the existing denominator-two carry factorisation and temperedness theorem.

declarations.name: finite_boolSupport_ne_half
declarations.signature: theorem finite_boolSupport_ne_half (A : Set ℕ) (hfinite : A.Finite) (hzero : 0 ∉ A) : erdosSupportSeries 2 A ≠ (1 : ℝ) / 2
declarations.docstring: A finite positive-index support cannot have support-series value `1/2`, because its audited reduced denominator is odd.

declarations.name: signedMobiusBaseline
declarations.docstring: The signed Möbius baseline behind the exact representation of `1/2`. The exceptional value at one removes the terminating binary digit.

declarations.name: sum_divisors_signedMobiusBaseline
declarations.signature: theorem sum_divisors_signedMobiusBaseline (n : ℕ) (hn : 0 < n) : (∑ d ∈ n.divisors, signedMobiusBaseline d) = if n = 1 then 0 else 1
