declarations.docstring: The signed baseline has the target binary coefficient word: zero in row one and one in every positive row after it.

declarations.name: mobiusCenteredHalfCarry
declarations.signature: noncomputable def mobiusCenteredHalfCarry (A : Set ℕ) (N : ℕ) : ℤ
declarations.docstring: The canonical integer half carry measured relative to the signed Möbius solution. Index `N` corresponds to the packet's state `e_{N+1}`.

declarations.name: mobiusCenteredHalfCarry_zero
declarations.signature: @[simp] theorem mobiusCenteredHalfCarry_zero (A : Set ℕ) : mobiusCenteredHalfCarry A 0 = 0

declarations.name: mobiusCenteredHalfCarry_succ
declarations.signature: theorem mobiusCenteredHalfCarry_succ (A : Set ℕ) (N : ℕ) : mobiusCenteredHalfCarry A (N + 1) = 2 * mobiusCenteredHalfCarry A N + 1 - (supportCoeff A (N + 2) : ℤ)
declarations.docstring: Exact centred recurrence. Its forcing term is the Boolean divisor coefficient minus the signed baseline coefficient one.

declarations.name: mobiusCenteredHalfCarry_coboundary
declarations.signature: theorem mobiusCenteredHalfCarry_coboundary (A : Set ℕ) (N : ℕ) : (supportCoeff A (N + 2) : ℤ) - 1 = 2 * mobiusCenteredHalfCarry A N - mobiusCenteredHalfCarry A (N + 1)
declarations.docstring: Coboundary normal form for Booleanisation relative to the signed Möbius baseline.

declarations.name: boolean_sub_signedMobiusBaseline_eq_centeredCoboundary
declarations.signature: theorem boolean_sub_signedMobiusBaseline_eq_centeredCoboundary (A : Set ℕ) (N : ℕ) : (supportCoeff A (N + 2) : ℤ) - (∑ d ∈ (N + 2).divisors, signedMobiusBaseline d) = 2 * mobiusCenteredHalfCarry A N - mobiusCenteredHalfCarry A (N + 1)
declarations.docstring: The same coboundary with the signed divisor baseline left visible. This is the literal `D = 2e - e⁺` intake equation from the returned packet.

declarations.name: support_half_of_mobiusCenteredHalfCarry_tempered
declarations.signature: theorem support_half_of_mobiusCenteredHalfCarry_tempered (A : Set ℕ) (hone : 1 ∉ A) (htempered : Tendsto (fun N : ℕ ↦ (mobiusCenteredHalfCarry A N : ℝ) / (2 : ℝ) ^ N) atTop (nhds 0)) : erdosSupportSeries 2 A = (1 : ℝ) / 2
declarations.docstring: Any subexponential Möbius-centred carry already closes exact half-membership through the existing canonical denominator-two orbit.

declarations.name: infinite_support_half_of_mobiusCenteredHalfCarry_tempered
declarations.signature: theorem infinite_support_half_of_mobiusCenteredHalfCarry_tempered (A : Set ℕ) (hzero : 0 ∉ A) (hone : 1 ∉ A) (htempered : Tendsto (fun N : ℕ ↦ (mobiusCenteredHalfCarry A N : ℝ) / (2 : ℝ) ^ N) atTop (nhds 0)) : A.Infinite ∧ erdosSupportSeries 2 A = (1 : ℝ) / 2
declarations.docstring: The exact endpoint wanted by the construction lane: a normalized support with tempered centred carry is automatically infinite and sums to `1/2`.

declarations.name: greedy_half_infinite_of_mobiusCenteredHalfCarry_tempered
declarations.signature: theorem greedy_half_infinite_of_mobiusCenteredHalfCarry_tempered (htempered : Tendsto (fun N : ℕ ↦ (mobiusCenteredHalfCarry (greedyMersenneSupport (1 / 2 : ℝ)) N : ℝ) / (2 : ℝ) ^ N) atTop (nhds 0)) : (greedyMersenneSupport (1 / 2 : ℝ)).Infinite ∧ erdosSupportSeries 2 (greedyMersenneSupport (1 / 2 : ℝ)) = (1 : ℝ) / 2
declarations.docstring: Greedy-specialized endpoint. A continuation now needs to prove only the displayed subexponential centred-carry limit for the canonical greedy support; zero/one normalization, exact half-membership, and infinitude are discharged here.

declarations.name: directly
declarations.signature: theorem directly at the exact support-series consumer. -/ theorem infinite_support_half_of_mobiusCenteredHalfCarry_sqrtBound (A : Set ℕ) (hzero : 0 ∉ A) (hone : 1 ∉ A) (hnonneg : ∀ N : ℕ, 0 ≤ mobiusCenteredHalfCarry A N) (hbound : ∀ N : ℕ, (mobiusCenteredHalfCarry A N : ℝ) ≤ 2 * Real.sqrt (N : ℝ) + 4) : A.Infinite ∧ erdosSupportSeries 2 A = (1 : ℝ) / 2

declarations.name: infinite_support_half_of_mobiusCenteredHalfCarry_sqrtBound
declarations.signature: theorem infinite_support_half_of_mobiusCenteredHalfCarry_sqrtBound (A : Set ℕ) (hzero : 0 ∉ A) (hone : 1 ∉ A) (hnonneg : ∀ N : ℕ, 0 ≤ mobiusCenteredHalfCarry A N) (hbound : ∀ N : ℕ, (mobiusCenteredHalfCarry A N : ℝ) ≤ 2 * Real.sqrt (N : ℝ) + 4) : A.Infinite ∧ erdosSupportSeries 2 A = (1 : ℝ) / 2
declarations.docstring: Pointwise square-root control is a concrete sufficient producer for the Möbius-centred endpoint. This exposes the existing generic carry-growth theorem directly at the exact support-series consumer.

declarations.name: greedy_half_infinite_of_mobiusCenteredHalfCarry_sqrtBound
declarations.signature: theorem greedy_half_infinite_of_mobiusCenteredHalfCarry_sqrtBound (hnonneg : ∀ N : ℕ, 0 ≤ mobiusCenteredHalfCarry (greedyMersenneSupport (1 / 2 : ℝ)) N) (hbound : ∀ N : ℕ, (mobiusCenteredHalfCarry (greedyMersenneSupport (1 / 2 : ℝ)) N : ℝ) ≤ 2 * Real.sqrt (N : ℝ) + 4) : (greedyMersenneSupport (1 / 2 : ℝ)).Infinite ∧ erdosSupportSeries 2 (greedyMersenneSupport (1 / 2 : ℝ)) = (1 : ℝ) / 2
declarations.docstring: Greedy-facing square-root endpoint. The open construction problem is now expressible as the two pointwise carry inequalities witnessed by all current finite runs, rather than as an abstract filter limit.

declarations.name: integerHalfCarry_eq_scaled_residual_add_tail
declarations.signature: theorem integerHalfCarry_eq_scaled_residual_add_tail (A : Set ℕ) (hone : 1 ∉ A) (N : ℕ) : (integerHalfCarry A N : ℝ) = (2 : ℝ) ^ (N + 1) * ((1 : ℝ) / 2 - erdosSupportSeries 2 A) + binaryCoeffTail (supportCoeff A) (N + 1)
declarations.docstring: Exact bridge: the integer half carry equals the scaled half-residual plus the analytic support-coefficient tail.

declarations.name: erdosSupportSeries_greedyMersenneSupport_le
declarations.signature: theorem erdosSupportSeries_greedyMersenneSupport_le {x : ℝ} (hx : 0 ≤ x) : erdosSupportSeries 2 (greedyMersenneSupport x) ≤ x
declarations.docstring: Greedy prefix sums never overshoot, so the greedy support value is at most its target.

declarations.name: two_mem_greedyMersenneSupport_half
declarations.docstring: The greedy half orbit takes exponent two immediately.

declarations.name: greedy_mobiusCenteredHalfCarry_nonneg
declarations.signature: theorem greedy_mobiusCenteredHalfCarry_nonneg (N : ℕ) : 0 ≤ mobiusCenteredHalfCarry (greedyMersenneSupport (1 / 2 : ℝ)) N
declarations.docstring: **Unconditional nonnegativity of the greedy centred carry.** The first pointwise producer obligation for exact greedy half-membership holds with no hypotheses: greedy prefix sums cannot overshoot `1/2`, the pending future-multiple tail of the taken exponents is strictly positive, and the carry is an integer, so it is at least one.

declarations.name: greedy_half_infinite_of_mobiusCenteredHalfCarry_upperBound
declarations.signature: theorem greedy_half_infinite_of_mobiusCenteredHalfCarry_upperBound (hbound : ∀ N : ℕ, (mobiusCenteredHalfCarry (greedyMersenneSupport (1 / 2 : ℝ)) N : ℝ) ≤ 2 * Real.sqrt (N : ℝ) + 4) : (greedyMersenneSupport (1 / 2 : ℝ)).Infinite ∧ erdosSupportSeries 2 (greedyMersenneSupport (1 / 2 : ℝ)) = (1 : ℝ) / 2
declarations.docstring: **Producer reduction.** With nonnegativity discharged unconditionally, the sole remaining producer obligation for exact greedy half-membership is the pointwise square-root upper bound on the centred carry.

declarations.name: boolSupport_infinite_of_halfStripBranch
declarations.signature: theorem boolSupport_infinite_of_halfStripBranch (a : ℕ → Bool) (hbranch : ∀ N : ℕ, HalfStripAdmissible N (fun i ↦ a i)) : (boolSupport a).Infinite
declarations.docstring: The support of a coherent half-strip branch is necessarily infinite.

declarations.name: exists_infinite_support_half_of_fullStrip
declarations.signature: theorem exists_infinite_support_half_of_fullStrip (hfull : ∀ N : ℕ, 1 ≤ N → HalfCarryFullStripReachabilityAt N) : ∃ A : Set ℕ, A.Infinite ∧ erdosSupportSeries 2 A = (1 : ℝ) / 2
declarations.docstring: **Conditional decision theorem.** Full finite reachability at every positive level yields an infinite support with exact value `1/2`. Thus the remaining mathematical work is entirely on the finite reachability side, whose only scalar defect in the canonical cylinder coordinate is the even seam isolated above.

declarations.name: exists_infinite_support_half_of_eventualFullStrip
declarations.signature: theorem exists_infinite_support_half_of_eventualFullStrip (N₀ : ℕ) (hbase : ∃ a : HalfWord N₀, HalfStripAdmissible N₀ a) (hfull : ∀ N : ℕ, N₀ ≤ N → HalfCarryFullStripReachabilityAt N) : ∃ A : Set ℕ, A.Infinite ∧ erdosSupportSeries 2 A = (1 : ℝ) / 2
declarations.docstring: **Eventual full-strip consumer.** It is enough to have one admissible base level and full terminal-strip reachability from that level onward. In particular, finite holes below the base level do not obstruct the inverse- system construction.

declarations.name: HalfCarryCofinalAdmissibility
declarations.docstring: Minimal compactness-facing producer: admissible finite words exist at cofinally many levels. Restriction then supplies a nonempty admissible family at every earlier level.

declarations.name: exists_infinite_support_half_of_cofinalAdmissibility
declarations.signature: theorem exists_infinite_support_half_of_cofinalAdmissibility (hcofinal : HalfCarryCofinalAdmissibility) : ∃ A : Set ℕ, A.Infinite ∧ erdosSupportSeries 2 A = (1 : ℝ) / 2
declarations.docstring: Cofinal finite admissibility is already enough for König compactness and an infinite support summing exactly to `1/2`.

declarations.name: cofinalAdmissibility_of_canonicalEvenSeamSupply
declarations.docstring: An exact one-hole even seam always leaves at least one of carries `1` and `2`, so canonical seam supply gives admissible words at cofinally many levels.

declarations.name: exists_infinite_support_half_of_canonicalEvenSeamSupply
declarations.signature: theorem exists_infinite_support_half_of_canonicalEvenSeamSupply (hcanonical : HalfCarryCanonicalEvenSeamSupply) : ∃ A : Set ℕ, A.Infinite ∧ erdosSupportSeries 2 A = (1 : ℝ) / 2
declarations.docstring: **One-hole seam consumer.** Canonical even-seam supply alone gives cofinally many nonempty admissible levels. The cofinal compactness consumer then produces an infinite support. Neither seam escape nor a separate odd-level full-strip theorem is needed for this existence conclusion.

declarations.name: exists_infinite_support_half_of_evenSeamSupply_escape
declarations.signature: theorem exists_infinite_support_half_of_evenSeamSupply_escape (hcanonical : HalfCarryCanonicalEvenSeamSupply) (hescape : HalfCarryEvenSeamEscape) (hodd : ∀ d : ℕ, 10 ≤ d → HalfCarryFullStripReachabilityAt (2 * d + 1)) (hbase : ∃ a : HalfWord 20, HalfStripAdmissible 20 a) : ∃ A : Set ℕ, A.Infinite ∧ erdosSupportSeries 2 A = (1 : ℝ) / 2
declarations.docstring: Solver-facing fan-in for the seam route. A canonical coordinate and scalar escape settle every even level from `20` onward; an independent odd level producer and one admissible depth-`20` base then feed the existing inverse-system consumer.

declarations.name: suffixNumeral
declarations.signature: def suffixNumeral : List Bool → ℕ | [] => 0 | b :: bs => (if b then 1 else 0) + 2 * suffixNumeral bs /-- A Boolean suffix numeral fits in exactly the dyadic denominator determined
declarations.docstring: Binary numeral in the same reverse-chronological order as `rewind`: the newest row is the low bit and every older row is one place higher.

declarations.name: suffixNumeral_lt_two_pow_length
declarations.signature: theorem suffixNumeral_lt_two_pow_length (bits : List Bool) : suffixNumeral bits < 2 ^ bits.length
declarations.docstring: A Boolean suffix numeral fits in exactly the dyadic denominator determined by its length.

declarations.name: phase_eq_denom_sub_one_sub_suffixNumeral
declarations.signature: theorem phase_eq_denom_sub_one_sub_suffixNumeral (cs : List ℕ) (bits : List Bool) (ancestor : ℕ) (hlen : bits.length = cs.length) (hrealized : offset cs + suffixNumeral bits + 2 = denom cs * (ancestor + 1)) : phase cs = denom cs - 1 - suffixNumeral bits
declarations.docstring: Exact phase identity for a realized carry-one history. The equation `offset + suffix + 2 = denom * (ancestor + 1)` is the natural-number form obtained by unrolling `1 = denom * ancestor - coefficientNumeral - suffixNumeral` and using `offset = coefficientNumeral + denom - 1`.

declarations.name: rewind_interval_singleton_iff_suffixNumeral_covers
declarations.signature: theorem rewind_interval_singleton_iff_suffixNumeral_covers (cs : List ℕ) (bits : List Bool) (ancestor B : ℕ) (hlen : bits.length = cs.length) (hrealized : offset cs + suffixNumeral bits + 2 = denom cs * (ancestor + 1)) (hB : 1 ≤ B) (hwidth : B ≤ denom cs) : (∀ k : ℕ, 1 ≤ k → k ≤ B → rewind cs k = rewind cs 1) ↔ B - 1 ≤ suffixNumeral bits
declarations.docstring: The exact fixed-coefficient singleton criterion in support-history coordinates: the terminal interval `[1,B]` rewinds to one ancestor exactly when the carry-one suffix numeral covers the other `B-1` targets.

declarations.name: supportSuffixBits
declarations.signature: def supportSuffixBits (A : Set ℕ) [DecidablePred (· ∈ A)] (M : ℕ) : ℕ → List Bool | 0 => [] | L + 1 => decide (M + L + 1 ∈ A) :: supportSuffixBits A M L @[simp] theorem supportSuffixBits_length (A : Set ℕ) [DecidablePred (· ∈ A)] (M L : ℕ) : (supportSuffixBits A M L).length = L
declarations.docstring: The actual suffix bits in reverse chronological order.

declarations.name: supportSuffixBits_length
declarations.signature: @[simp] theorem supportSuffixBits_length (A : Set ℕ) [DecidablePred (· ∈ A)] (M L : ℕ) : (supportSuffixBits A M L).length = L

declarations.name: supportSuffixNumeral
declarations.signature: def supportSuffixNumeral (A : Set ℕ) [DecidablePred (· ∈ A)] (M : ℕ) : ℕ → ℕ | 0 => 0 | L + 1 => 2 * supportSuffixNumeral A M L + if M + L + 1 ∈ A then 1 else 0 theorem suffixNumeral_supportSuffixBits (A : Set ℕ) [DecidablePred (· ∈ A)] (M L : ℕ) : suffixNumeral (supportSuffixBits A M L) = supportSuffixNumeral A M L
declarations.docstring: Recursive binary value of the support suffix.

declarations.name: suffixNumeral_supportSuffixBits
declarations.signature: theorem suffixNumeral_supportSuffixBits (A : Set ℕ) [DecidablePred (· ∈ A)] (M L : ℕ) : suffixNumeral (supportSuffixBits A M L) = supportSuffixNumeral A M L

declarations.name: supportCoeff_eq_prefix_add_currentBit
declarations.signature: theorem supportCoeff_eq_prefix_add_currentBit (A : Set ℕ) [DecidablePred (· ∈ A)] {M n : ℕ} (hMn : M < n) (hnfeedback : n ≤ 2 * M + 1) : supportCoeff A n = supportCoeff (A ∩ Set.Iic M) n + if n ∈ A then 1 else 0
declarations.docstring: Before the first possible feedback row, truncating at `M` removes from row `n` only the current bit `n` itself.

declarations.name: integerHalfCarry_prefix_eq_add_supportSuffixNumeral
declarations.signature: theorem integerHalfCarry_prefix_eq_add_supportSuffixNumeral (A : Set ℕ) [DecidablePred (· ∈ A)] (M L : ℕ) (hM : 1 ≤ M) (hhorizon : M + L ≤ 2 * M + 1) : integerHalfCarry (A ∩ Set.Iic M) (M + L - 1) = integerHalfCarry A (M + L - 1) + (supportSuffixNumeral A M L : ℤ)
declarations.docstring: On a horizon `M+L ≤ 2M+1`, the prefix-zero-extension carry exceeds the actual carry by exactly the binary numeral of the removed suffix.

declarations.name: integerHalfCarry_prefix_eq_one_add_supportSuffixNumeral
declarations.signature: theorem integerHalfCarry_prefix_eq_one_add_supportSuffixNumeral (A : Set ℕ) [DecidablePred (· ∈ A)] (M L : ℕ) (hM : 1 ≤ M) (hhorizon : M + L ≤ 2 * M + 1) (hterminal : integerHalfCarry A (M + L - 1) = 1) : integerHalfCarry (A ∩ Set.Iic M) (M + L - 1) = 1 + (supportSuffixNumeral A M L : ℤ)
declarations.docstring: Terminal carry one turns the support bridge into the exact frozen-prefix endpoint `Z = 1 + X` used by the phase criterion.

declarations.name: supportCoeffRewindHistory
declarations.signature: noncomputable def supportCoeffRewindHistory (P : Set ℕ) (M : ℕ) : ℕ → List ℕ | 0 => [] | L + 1 => supportCoeff P (M + L + 1) :: supportCoeffRewindHistory P M L @[simp] theorem supportCoeffRewindHistory_length (P : Set ℕ) (M L : ℕ) : (supportCoeffRewindHistory P M L).length = L
declarations.docstring: Proper-coefficient history for rewinding from row `M+L` to row `M`, newest coefficient first, exactly as `FixedCoeffRewind.rewind` consumes it.

declarations.name: supportCoeffRewindHistory_length
declarations.signature: @[simp] theorem supportCoeffRewindHistory_length (P : Set ℕ) (M L : ℕ) : (supportCoeffRewindHistory P M L).length = L

declarations.name: integerHalfCarry_add_offset_eq_denom_mul_initial
declarations.signature: theorem integerHalfCarry_add_offset_eq_denom_mul_initial (P : Set ℕ) (M L : ℕ) (hM : 1 ≤ M) : integerHalfCarry P (M + L - 1) + (offset (supportCoeffRewindHistory P M L) : ℤ) + 1 = (denom (supportCoeffRewindHistory P M L) : ℤ) * (integerHalfCarry P (M - 1) + 1)
declarations.docstring: Exact affine realization of an actual coefficient history. This is the integer identity behind the natural-number phase equation; it uses the live `integerHalfCarry` recurrence rather than an independently described orbit.

declarations.name: supportHistory_realization
declarations.signature: theorem supportHistory_realization (A : Set ℕ) [DecidablePred (· ∈ A)] (M L ancestor : ℕ) (hM : 1 ≤ M) (hhorizon : M + L ≤ 2 * M + 1) (hterminal : integerHalfCarry A (M + L - 1) = 1) (hancestor : integerHalfCarry A (M - 1) = (ancestor : ℤ)) : offset (supportCoeffRewindHistory (A ∩ Set.Iic M) M L) + supportSuffixNumeral A M L + 2 = denom (supportCoeffRewindHistory (A ∩ Set.Iic M) M L) * (ancestor + 1)
declarations.docstring: The terminal-carry-one support history satisfies the exact realization equation required by `phase_eq_denom_sub_one_sub_suffixNumeral`.

declarations.name: phase_supportCoeffRewindHistory_eq
declarations.signature: theorem phase_supportCoeffRewindHistory_eq (A : Set ℕ) [DecidablePred (· ∈ A)] (M L ancestor : ℕ) (hM : 1 ≤ M) (hhorizon : M + L ≤ 2 * M + 1) (hterminal : integerHalfCarry A (M + L - 1) = 1) (hancestor : integerHalfCarry A (M - 1) = (ancestor : ℤ)) : phase (supportCoeffRewindHistory (A ∩ Set.Iic M) M L) = denom (supportCoeffRewindHistory (A ∩ Set.Iic M) M L) - 1 - supportSuffixNumeral A M L
declarations.docstring: Exact live-support phase identity before feedback can return from the removed suffix.

declarations.name: rewind_supportCoeffHistory_singleton_iff
declarations.signature: theorem rewind_supportCoeffHistory_singleton_iff (A : Set ℕ) [DecidablePred (· ∈ A)] (M L ancestor B : ℕ) (hM : 1 ≤ M) (hhorizon : M + L ≤ 2 * M + 1) (hterminal : integerHalfCarry A (M + L - 1) = 1) (hancestor : integerHalfCarry A (M - 1) = (ancestor : ℤ)) (hB : 1 ≤ B) (hwidth : B ≤ denom (supportCoeffRewindHistory (A ∩ Set.Iic M) M L)) : (∀ k : ℕ, 1 ≤ k → k ≤ B → rewind (supportCoeffRewindHistory (A ∩ Set.Iic M) M L) k = rewind (supportCoeffRewindHistory (A ∩ Set.Iic M) M L) 1) ↔ B - 1 ≤ supportSuffixNumeral A M L
declarations.docstring: Exact support-history singleton criterion. It reduces the whole rewind interval question to the single frozen-prefix inequality `B - 1 ≤ X`.

declarations.name: rewind_supportCoeffHistory_singleton_iff_prefixCarry_covers
declarations.signature: theorem rewind_supportCoeffHistory_singleton_iff_prefixCarry_covers (A : Set ℕ) [DecidablePred (· ∈ A)] (M L ancestor B : ℕ) (hM : 1 ≤ M) (hhorizon : M + L ≤ 2 * M + 1) (hterminal : integerHalfCarry A (M + L - 1) = 1) (hancestor : integerHalfCarry A (M - 1) = (ancestor : ℤ)) (hB : 1 ≤ B) (hwidth : B ≤ denom (supportCoeffRewindHistory (A ∩ Set.Iic M) M L)) : (∀ k : ℕ, 1 ≤ k → k ≤ B → rewind (supportCoeffRewindHistory (A ∩ Set.Iic M) M L) k = rewind (supportCoeffRewindHistory (A ∩ Set.Iic M) M L) 1) ↔ (B : ℤ) ≤ integerHalfCarry (A ∩ Set.Iic M) (M + L - 1)
declarations.docstring: Equivalent frozen-prefix form of the exact criterion. Under terminal carry one the prefix-zero-extension endpoint is `Z = 1 + X`, so singleton rewind is exactly `B ≤ Z`; no phase or suffix encoding remains downstream.

declarations.name: ProtectedChild
declarations.signature: def ProtectedChild (p : ℕ → ℤ) (k q : ℕ) : Prop
declarations.docstring: A row forcing term and the two Boolean children of parent carry `k`.

declarations.name: protectedChild_cover_Icc_of_constant
declarations.signature: theorem protectedChild_cover_Icc_of_constant (p : ℕ → ℤ) {R S C : ℕ} (hp_const : ∀ k : ℕ, 1 ≤ k → k ≤ R → p k = C) (hbuffer : S + C ≤ 2 * R) : ∀ q : ℕ, 1 ≤ q → q ≤ S → ∃ k : ℕ, 1 ≤ k ∧ k ≤ R ∧ ProtectedChild p k q
declarations.docstring: Scalar protected-window coverage for the stronger invariant seen in the exact replay: every selected parent has one common proper-divisor coefficient `C`. The children of consecutive parents are the adjacent pairs `{2k-C-1, 2k-C}`, so `S+C ≤ 2R` tiles the whole positive target window.

declarations.name: extendHalfWord
declarations.signature: def extendHalfWord {N : ℕ} (a : HalfWord N) (β : Bool) : HalfWord (N + 1)
declarations.docstring: Append one Boolean bit to a finite half word.

declarations.name: extendHalfWord_castSucc
declarations.signature: @[simp] theorem extendHalfWord_castSucc {N : ℕ} (a : HalfWord N) (β : Bool) (i : Fin (N + 1)) : extendHalfWord a β i.castSucc = a i

declarations.name: extendHalfWord_last
declarations.signature: @[simp] theorem extendHalfWord_last {N : ℕ} (a : HalfWord N) (β : Bool) : extendHalfWord a β (Fin.last (N + 1)) = β

declarations.name: mem_wordSupport_extendHalfWord_iff
declarations.signature: theorem mem_wordSupport_extendHalfWord_iff {N n : ℕ} (a : HalfWord N) (β : Bool) : n ∈ wordSupport (extendHalfWord a β) ↔ n ∈ wordSupport a ∨ (n = N + 1 ∧ β = true)
declarations.docstring: Membership in the support of an extended word is old membership or the new terminal bit.

declarations.name: wordSupport_extendHalfWord
declarations.signature: theorem wordSupport_extendHalfWord {N : ℕ} (a : HalfWord N) (β : Bool) : wordSupport (extendHalfWord a β) = if β then insert (N + 1) (wordSupport a) else wordSupport a
declarations.docstring: Set-level support normal form for a one-bit extension.

declarations.name: restrictWord_extendHalfWord
declarations.signature: theorem restrictWord_extendHalfWord {N : ℕ} (a : HalfWord N) (β : Bool) : restrictWord (Nat.le_succ N) (extendHalfWord a β) = a
declarations.docstring: Restricting an extension recovers its parent word.

declarations.name: HalfStripAdmissible.extend
declarations.signature: theorem HalfStripAdmissible.extend {N : ℕ} (hN : 1 ≤ N) {a : HalfWord N} (ha : HalfStripAdmissible N a) (β : Bool) (hterminal : (1 : ℤ) ≤ integerHalfCarry (wordSupport (extendHalfWord a β)) N ∧ integerHalfCarry (wordSupport (extendHalfWord a β)) N ≤ halfStripBound (N + 1)) : HalfStripAdmissible (N + 1) (extendHalfWord a β)
declarations.docstring: If the new terminal carry lies in the next strip, extending an admissible word preserves all earlier strip inequalities and establishes the new one.

declarations.name: SelectedHalfWindow
declarations.docstring: A selected family contains one explicit admissible representative for every carry in the protected initial window. No global uniqueness claim is built into this interface.

declarations.name: selectedRowCoeff
declarations.signature: noncomputable def selectedRowCoeff {N R : ℕ} (W : SelectedHalfWindow N R) (k : ℕ) : ℤ
declarations.docstring: The actual next-row proper-divisor coefficient of the selected representative. Values outside the protected window are set to zero only so the scalar lemma can consume a total function.

declarations.name: SelectedHalfWindow.NextRowDivisorAgreement
declarations.signature: def SelectedHalfWindow.NextRowDivisorAgreement {N R : ℕ} (W : SelectedHalfWindow N R) : Prop
declarations.docstring: The chosen terminal representatives agree on every divisor inspected by the next proper coefficient. This is the exact cylinder-prefix consequence needed by the common-coefficient one-step atom; it avoids claiming that the whole words are equal.

declarations.name: supportCoeff_eq_of_divisorAgreement
declarations.signature: theorem supportCoeff_eq_of_divisorAgreement (A B : Set ℕ) (n : ℕ) (hagree : ∀ d : ℕ, d ∈ n.divisors → (d ∈ A ↔ d ∈ B)) : supportCoeff A n = supportCoeff B n
declarations.docstring: Divisor-membership agreement is precisely enough to identify two support coefficients.

declarations.name: SelectedHalfWindow.selectedRowCoeff_eq
declarations.signature: theorem SelectedHalfWindow.selectedRowCoeff_eq {N R : ℕ} (W : SelectedHalfWindow N R) (hagree : W.NextRowDivisorAgreement) {k ℓ : ℕ} (hk : 1 ≤ k) (hkR : k ≤ R) (hℓ : 1 ≤ ℓ) (hℓR : ℓ ≤ R) : selectedRowCoeff W k = selectedRowCoeff W ℓ
declarations.docstring: A coherent next-row divisor cylinder makes `selectedRowCoeff` genuinely constant on the protected window.

declarations.name: SelectedHalfWindow.HistoryAlternative
declarations.signature: def SelectedHalfWindow.HistoryAlternative {N R : ℕ} (W : SelectedHalfWindow N R) : Prop
declarations.docstring: Exact unresolved global history alternative. Repeated common inverse maps contract a carry interval to either one ancestor or two adjacent ancestors. The singleton case must establish `NextRowDivisorAgreement`; the adjacent-pair case must identify the actual transition with the already landed canonical one-hole seam. No false phase-free singleton claim is encoded here.

declarations.name: SelectedHalfWindow.stepOpaque
declarations.signature: noncomputable def SelectedHalfWindow.stepOpaque {N R S C : ℕ} (hN : 1 ≤ N) (W : SelectedHalfWindow N R) (_hR : 1 ≤ R) (_hS : 1 ≤ S) (hSstrip : S ≤ halfStripBound (N + 1)) (hp_common : ∀ k : ℕ, 1 ≤ k → k ≤ R → selectedRowCoeff W k = C) (hbuffer : S + C ≤ 2 * R) : SelectedHalfWindow (N + 1) S
declarations.docstring: Legacy existence-by-choice implementation of the object-level one-step protected-window theorem. The public `step` below uses the canonical fixed-coefficient parent and exposes exact restriction provenance.

declarations.name: explicitParent_pos
declarations.signature: theorem explicitParent_pos {q C : ℕ} (hq : 1 ≤ q) : 1 ≤ parent C q
declarations.docstring: Every positive target has a positive canonical fixed-coefficient parent.

declarations.name: explicitParent_le
declarations.signature: theorem explicitParent_le {q R S C : ℕ} (hqS : q ≤ S) (hbuffer : S + C ≤ 2 * R) : parent C q ≤ R
declarations.docstring: The protected-window buffer inequality puts the canonical parent in the preceding protected window.

declarations.name: explicitParentBitZ
declarations.docstring: Exact parity bit associated to the canonical parent.

declarations.name: explicitParentBitZ_zero_or_one
declarations.signature: theorem explicitParentBitZ_zero_or_one (C q : ℕ) : explicitParentBitZ C q = 0 ∨ explicitParentBitZ C q = 1
declarations.docstring: The canonical parent leaves exactly one Boolean remainder bit.

declarations.name: explicitParent_recurrence
declarations.signature: theorem explicitParent_recurrence (C q : ℕ) : (q : ℤ) = 2 * (parent C q : ℤ) - C - explicitParentBitZ C q
declarations.docstring: The canonical parent and parity bit solve the scalar child recurrence.

declarations.name: SelectedHalfWindow.FixedCoeffSuccessor
declarations.signature: structure SelectedHalfWindow.FixedCoeffSuccessor {N R S C : ℕ} (W : SelectedHalfWindow N R) (hbuffer : S + C ≤ 2 * R)
declarations.docstring: A selected window together with exact one-row provenance. Keeping the restriction equation in the constructor makes later rewind composition independent of the implementation details of the data-producing proof.

declarations.name: SelectedHalfWindow.stepExplicitWithHistory
declarations.signature: noncomputable def SelectedHalfWindow.stepExplicitWithHistory {N R S C : ℕ} (hN : 1 ≤ N) (W : SelectedHalfWindow N R) (hSstrip : S ≤ halfStripBound (N + 1)) (hp_common : ∀ k : ℕ, 1 ≤ k → k ≤ R → selectedRowCoeff W k = C) (hbuffer : S + C ≤ 2 * R) : W.FixedCoeffSuccessor hbuffer
declarations.docstring: Explicit-parent selected-window step with its restriction provenance in the same bundle.

declarations.name: SelectedHalfWindow.stepExplicit
declarations.signature: noncomputable def SelectedHalfWindow.stepExplicit {N R S C : ℕ} (hN : 1 ≤ N) (W : SelectedHalfWindow N R) (hSstrip : S ≤ halfStripBound (N + 1)) (hp_common : ∀ k : ℕ, 1 ≤ k → k ≤ R → selectedRowCoeff W k = C) (hbuffer : S + C ≤ 2 * R) : SelectedHalfWindow (N + 1) S
declarations.docstring: Data-only projection of the explicit-parent successor.

declarations.name: SelectedHalfWindow.restrictWord_stepExplicit
declarations.signature: theorem SelectedHalfWindow.restrictWord_stepExplicit {N R S C : ℕ} (hN : 1 ≤ N) (W : SelectedHalfWindow N R) (hSstrip : S ≤ halfStripBound (N + 1)) (hp_common : ∀ k : ℕ, 1 ≤ k → k ≤ R → selectedRowCoeff W k = C) (hbuffer : S + C ≤ 2 * R) (q : ℕ) (hq : 1 ≤ q) (hqS : q ≤ S) : restrictWord (Nat.le_succ N) ((W.stepExplicit hN hSstrip hp_common hbuffer).word q hq hqS) = W.word (parent C q) (explicitParent_pos hq) (explicitParent_le hqS hbuffer)
declarations.docstring: One-step restriction provenance for the public explicit constructor.

declarations.name: SelectedHalfWindow.CommonRestrictionAt
declarations.signature: def SelectedHalfWindow.CommonRestrictionAt {M N R : ℕ} (W : SelectedHalfWindow N R) (hMN : M ≤ N) : Prop
declarations.docstring: All selected representatives have the same prefix at cutoff `M`.

declarations.name: SelectedHalfWindow.nextRowDivisorAgreement_of_commonRestriction
declarations.signature: theorem SelectedHalfWindow.nextRowDivisorAgreement_of_commonRestriction {M N R : ℕ} (W : SelectedHalfWindow N R) (hMN : M ≤ N) (hhalf : (N + 1) / 2 ≤ M) (hcommon : W.CommonRestrictionAt hMN) : W.NextRowDivisorAgreement
declarations.docstring: A common restriction beyond the half-depth cutoff forces the exact next-row divisor-membership agreement required by `selectedRowCoeff_eq`. Every proper divisor of `N + 1` is at most `(N + 1) / 2`; the remaining divisor `N + 1` is absent from every depth-`N` word.

declarations.name: SelectedHalfWindow.HasRewindRestrictionAt
declarations.signature: def SelectedHalfWindow.HasRewindRestrictionAt {M N R₀ R : ℕ} (W : SelectedHalfWindow N R) (W₀ : SelectedHalfWindow M R₀) (hMN : M ≤ N) (cs : List ℕ) : Prop
declarations.docstring: Composed one-step provenance, stated without exposing dependent bound proofs. Every selected word restricts to the base representative indexed by its exact closed-form rewind ancestor.

declarations.name: SelectedHalfWindow.word_eq_of_index_eq
declarations.signature: theorem SelectedHalfWindow.word_eq_of_index_eq {N R k ell : ℕ} (W : SelectedHalfWindow N R) (h : k = ell) (hk : 1 ≤ k) (hkR : k ≤ R) (hell : 1 ≤ ell) (hellR : ell ≤ R) : W.word k hk hkR = W.word ell hell hellR
declarations.docstring: Dependent proof arguments to `word` are irrelevant once the carry index is identified.

declarations.name: SelectedHalfWindow.hasRewindRestrictionAt_nil
declarations.signature: theorem SelectedHalfWindow.hasRewindRestrictionAt_nil {N R : ℕ} (W : SelectedHalfWindow N R) : W.HasRewindRestrictionAt W (le_refl N) []
declarations.docstring: Reflexive rewind provenance at an empty coefficient history.

declarations.name: SelectedHalfWindow.FixedCoeffSuccessor.hasRewindRestrictionAt_cons
declarations.signature: theorem SelectedHalfWindow.FixedCoeffSuccessor.hasRewindRestrictionAt_cons {M N R₀ R S C : ℕ} (W₀ : SelectedHalfWindow M R₀) (W : SelectedHalfWindow N R) (hMN : M ≤ N) (cs : List ℕ) (hbuffer : S + C ≤ 2 * R) (next : W.FixedCoeffSuccessor hbuffer) (hprov : W.HasRewindRestrictionAt W₀ hMN cs) : next.window.HasRewindRestrictionAt W₀ (hMN.trans (Nat.le_succ N)) (C :: cs)
declarations.docstring: One history-bearing explicit successor conses its coefficient onto exact rewind provenance.

declarations.name: SelectedHalfWindow.hasRewindRestrictionAt_stepExplicit
declarations.signature: theorem SelectedHalfWindow.hasRewindRestrictionAt_stepExplicit {M N R₀ R S C : ℕ} (W₀ : SelectedHalfWindow M R₀) (W : SelectedHalfWindow N R) (hMN : M ≤ N) (cs : List ℕ) (hN : 1 ≤ N) (hSstrip : S ≤ halfStripBound (N + 1)) (hp_common : ∀ k : ℕ, 1 ≤ k → k ≤ R → selectedRowCoeff W k = C) (hbuffer : S + C ≤ 2 * R) (hprov : W.HasRewindRestrictionAt W₀ hMN cs) : (W.stepExplicit hN hSstrip hp_common hbuffer).HasRewindRestrictionAt W₀ (hMN.trans (Nat.le_succ N)) (C :: cs)
declarations.docstring: The public explicit-parent step preserves rewind provenance with the new coefficient at the head of the history list.

declarations.name: SelectedHalfWindow.commonRestrictionAt_of_rewind_singleton
declarations.signature: theorem SelectedHalfWindow.commonRestrictionAt_of_rewind_singleton {M N R₀ R : ℕ} (W : SelectedHalfWindow N R) (W₀ : SelectedHalfWindow M R₀) (hMN : M ≤ N) (cs : List ℕ) (hR : 1 ≤ R) (hprov : W.HasRewindRestrictionAt W₀ hMN cs) (hsingle : ∀ k : ℕ, 1 ≤ k → k ≤ R → rewind cs k = rewind cs 1) : W.CommonRestrictionAt hMN
declarations.docstring: If the rewind map is constant on the protected interval, composed provenance supplies one literal common prefix.

declarations.name: SelectedHalfWindow.nextRowDivisorAgreement_of_rewind_singleton
declarations.signature: theorem SelectedHalfWindow.nextRowDivisorAgreement_of_rewind_singleton {M N R₀ R : ℕ} (W : SelectedHalfWindow N R) (W₀ : SelectedHalfWindow M R₀) (hMN : M ≤ N) (cs : List ℕ) (hhalf : (N + 1) / 2 ≤ M) (hR : 1 ≤ R) (hprov : W.HasRewindRestrictionAt W₀ hMN cs) (hsingle : ∀ k : ℕ, 1 ≤ k → k ≤ R → rewind cs k = rewind cs 1) : W.NextRowDivisorAgreement
declarations.docstring: Complete singleton fan-in: exact rewind provenance and the arithmetic singleton branch imply common next-row proper-divisor coefficients through the existing live interface.

declarations.name: SelectedHalfWindow.nextRowDivisorAgreement_of_rewind_phaseFit
declarations.signature: theorem SelectedHalfWindow.nextRowDivisorAgreement_of_rewind_phaseFit {M N R₀ R : ℕ} (W : SelectedHalfWindow N R) (W₀ : SelectedHalfWindow M R₀) (hMN : M ≤ N) (cs : List ℕ) (hhalf : (N + 1) / 2 ≤ M) (hR : 1 ≤ R) (hwidth : R ≤ denom cs) (hprov : W.HasRewindRestrictionAt W₀ hMN cs) (hphase : phase cs + (R - 1) < denom cs) : W.NextRowDivisorAgreement
declarations.docstring: Exact phase-fit fan-in from a composed history to the live common coefficient interface.

declarations.name: SelectedHalfWindow.ScalarHistoryAlternative
declarations.signature: def SelectedHalfWindow.ScalarHistoryAlternative {N R : ℕ} (W : SelectedHalfWindow N R) (cs : List ℕ) : Prop
declarations.docstring: The exact arithmetic history alternative before semantic seam identification.

declarations.name: SelectedHalfWindow.scalarHistoryAlternative_of_rewindHistory
declarations.signature: theorem SelectedHalfWindow.scalarHistoryAlternative_of_rewindHistory {M N R₀ R : ℕ} (W : SelectedHalfWindow N R) (W₀ : SelectedHalfWindow M R₀) (hMN : M ≤ N) (cs : List ℕ) (hhalf : (N + 1) / 2 ≤ M) (hR : 1 ≤ R) (hwidth : R ≤ denom cs) (hprov : W.HasRewindRestrictionAt W₀ hMN cs) : W.ScalarHistoryAlternative cs
declarations.docstring: A sufficiently long exact rewind either lands in the live common-coefficient interface or leaves precisely two adjacent scalar ancestors.

declarations.name: SelectedHalfWindow.historyAlternative_of_rewindHistory
declarations.signature: theorem SelectedHalfWindow.historyAlternative_of_rewindHistory {M N R₀ R : ℕ} (W : SelectedHalfWindow N R) (W₀ : SelectedHalfWindow M R₀) (hMN : M ≤ N) (cs : List ℕ) (hhalf : (N + 1) / 2 ≤ M) (hR : 1 ≤ R) (hwidth : R ≤ denom cs) (hprov : W.HasRewindRestrictionAt W₀ hMN cs) (hseamBridge : IsSeamPair cs R → ∃ d : ℕ, ∃ δ c : ℤ, N + 1 = 2 * d ∧ IsCanonicalEvenSeam d δ c) : W.HistoryAlternative
declarations.docstring: Once the adjacent scalar ancestors are identified with the actual canonical one-hole seam at row `N + 1`, the existing object-level history alternative follows with no further arithmetic work.

declarations.name: SelectedHalfWindow.step
declarations.signature: noncomputable def SelectedHalfWindow.step {N R S C : ℕ} (hN : 1 ≤ N) (W : SelectedHalfWindow N R) (_hR : 1 ≤ R) (_hS : 1 ≤ S) (hSstrip : S ≤ halfStripBound (N + 1)) (hp_common : ∀ k : ℕ, 1 ≤ k → k ≤ R → selectedRowCoeff W k = C) (hbuffer : S + C ≤ 2 * R) : SelectedHalfWindow (N + 1) S
declarations.docstring: Public object-level one-step theorem. Unlike `stepOpaque`, this route uses the canonical parent and therefore has the provenance theorem above.

declarations.name: SelectedHalfWindow.stepOfAgreement
declarations.signature: noncomputable def SelectedHalfWindow.stepOfAgreement {N : ℕ} (hN : 1 ≤ N) (W : SelectedHalfWindow N (halfStripBound N)) (hagree : W.NextRowDivisorAgreement) : SelectedHalfWindow (N + 1) (halfStripBound (N + 1))
declarations.docstring: A coherent next-row divisor cylinder advances the entire square-root strip. The carry-`1` representative supplies the common coefficient; the sharp divisor envelope and strip-headroom theorem discharge the scalar buffer required by `SelectedHalfWindow.step`.

declarations.name: selectedHalfWindowIterate
declarations.signature: noncomputable def selectedHalfWindowIterate (N₀ : ℕ) (R : ℕ → ℕ) (hbase : SelectedHalfWindow N₀ (R N₀)) (hstep : ∀ N : ℕ, N₀ ≤ N → SelectedHalfWindow N (R N) → SelectedHalfWindow (N + 1) (R (N + 1))) : (d : ℕ) → SelectedHalfWindow (N₀ + d) (R (N₀ + d)) | 0 =>
declarations.docstring: Iterate a data-producing selected-window step by an explicit number of rows. Using a data definition here (rather than Prop induction) preserves the actual chosen words needed by the next coefficient row.

declarations.name: cofinalAdmissibility_of_selectedHalfWindows
declarations.signature: theorem cofinalAdmissibility_of_selectedHalfWindows (N₀ : ℕ) (R : ℕ → ℕ) (hR : ∀ N : ℕ, N₀ ≤ N → 1 ≤ R N) (hwindow : ∀ N : ℕ, N₀ ≤ N → SelectedHalfWindow N (R N)) : HalfCarryCofinalAdmissibility
declarations.docstring: Protected selected windows at all sufficiently large levels imply the landed minimal producer interface. Only one representative (carry `1`) is needed at each chosen level.

declarations.name: cofinalAdmissibility_of_selectedHalfWindow_induction
declarations.signature: theorem cofinalAdmissibility_of_selectedHalfWindow_induction (N₀ : ℕ) (R : ℕ → ℕ) (hR : ∀ N : ℕ, N₀ ≤ N → 1 ≤ R N) (hbase : SelectedHalfWindow N₀ (R N₀)) (hstep : ∀ N : ℕ, N₀ ≤ N → SelectedHalfWindow N (R N) → SelectedHalfWindow (N + 1) (R (N + 1))) : HalfCarryCofinalAdmissibility
declarations.docstring: Packaged forward-induction route to the exact half-sum theorem's minimal cofinal producer.

declarations.name: exists_infinite_support_half_of_selectedHalfWindows
declarations.signature: theorem exists_infinite_support_half_of_selectedHalfWindows (N₀ : ℕ) (R : ℕ → ℕ) (hR : ∀ N : ℕ, N₀ ≤ N → 1 ≤ R N) (hwindow : ∀ N : ℕ, N₀ ≤ N → SelectedHalfWindow N (R N)) : ∃ A : Set ℕ, A.Infinite ∧ erdosSupportSeries 2 A = (1 : ℝ) / 2
declarations.docstring: A supply of selected protected windows already yields an infinite support whose Mersenne achievement sum is exactly `1/2`.

declarations.name: exists_infinite_support_half_of_selectedHalfWindow_induction
declarations.signature: theorem exists_infinite_support_half_of_selectedHalfWindow_induction (N₀ : ℕ) (R : ℕ → ℕ) (hR : ∀ N : ℕ, N₀ ≤ N → 1 ≤ R N) (hbase : SelectedHalfWindow N₀ (R N₀)) (hstep : ∀ N : ℕ, N₀ ≤ N → SelectedHalfWindow N (R N) → SelectedHalfWindow (N + 1) (R (N + 1))) : ∃ A : Set ℕ, A.Infinite ∧ erdosSupportSeries 2 A = (1 : ℝ) / 2
declarations.docstring: A base selected window plus a forward selected-window induction closes the exact infinite half-sum endpoint directly.

declarations.name: mersenneCorrectionTail_lt_doubleDepth
declarations.signature: theorem mersenneCorrectionTail_lt_doubleDepth {m : ℕ} (hm : 0 < m) : mersenneCorrectionTail m < ((1 : ℝ) / 2) ^ (2 * m + 1)
declarations.docstring: **Packet §3 upper constant.** The unresolved correction after `m` is strictly below one half of the binary cell `4^{-m}`: the sharp uniform window depth is `2m+1`.

declarations.name: doubleDepth_succ_lt_mersenneCorrectionTail
declarations.signature: theorem doubleDepth_succ_lt_mersenneCorrectionTail (m : ℕ) : ((1 : ℝ) / 2) ^ (2 * m + 2) < mersenneCorrectionTail m
declarations.docstring: **Packet §3 lower constant.** The correction tail strictly exceeds the quarter cell, so no uniform tail-width argument can promise depth `2m+2`.

declarations.name: mersenneGap_eq_weightCorrection_sub_correctionTail
declarations.signature: theorem mersenneGap_eq_weightCorrection_sub_correctionTail (n : ℕ) : mersenneGap n = (mersenneWeight n - ((1 : ℝ) / 2) ^ n) - mersenneCorrectionTail n
declarations.docstring: **Packet §2 jump identity.** The Mersenne gap is exactly the one-weight correction minus the correction tail: `g_m = c_m - η_m`.

declarations.name: correctionWindow_dyadic_crossing_unique
declarations.signature: theorem correctionWindow_dyadic_crossing_unique {m : ℕ} (hm : 0 < m) {x : ℝ} {k₁ k₂ : ℤ} (h₁L : x < (k₁ : ℝ) / 2 ^ (2 * m + 1)) (h₁R : (k₁ : ℝ) / 2 ^ (2 * m + 1) < x + mersenneCorrectionTail m) (h₂L : x < (k₂ : ℝ) / 2 ^ (2 * m + 1)) (h₂R : (k₂ : ℝ) / 2 ^ (2 * m + 1) < x + mersenneCorrectionTail m) : k₁ = k₂
declarations.docstring: **Packet §5 boundary uniqueness.** A correction window of width `η_m` meets at most one dyadic boundary of depth `2m+1`: the two candidate numerators coincide. This is the quadratic cut-localisation step.

declarations.name: positiveMersenneSupportValue_coe_finset
declarations.signature: theorem positiveMersenneSupportValue_coe_finset (F : Finset ℕ) : positiveMersenneSupportValue (↑F : Set ℕ) = ∑ n ∈ F, mersenneWeight n
declarations.docstring: The value coded by a finite positive support is its literal finite Mersenne sum.

declarations.name: positiveMersenneSupportValue_insert
declarations.signature: theorem positiveMersenneSupportValue_insert {F : Finset ℕ} {a : ℕ} (ha : a ∉ F) : positiveMersenneSupportValue (↑(insert a F) : Set ℕ) = mersenneWeight a + positiveMersenneSupportValue (↑F : Set ℕ)
declarations.docstring: Selecting one new exponent adds exactly its weight.

declarations.name: IsStraddlePrefix
declarations.signature: structure IsStraddlePrefix (t : ℝ) (u : Finset ℕ) (d : ℕ) : Prop
declarations.docstring: **Packet §4.** A finite support word certified to straddle the target at depth `d`: the coded value is at most `t` and the value plus the complete unresolved tail mass still reaches `t`. This is deliberately *weaker* than the `HalfPrefixForcingChain.interval_trapped` containment condition: overlap of the correction image with the cylinder, not containment inside it.

declarations.name: IsStraddlePrefix.zero_notMem
declarations.signature: theorem IsStraddlePrefix.zero_notMem {t : ℝ} {u : Finset ℕ} {d : ℕ} (hu : IsStraddlePrefix t u d) : 0 ∉ u
declarations.docstring: Zero is never an exponent of a straddle word.

declarations.name: mem_mersenneAchievementSet_of_straddle_all_depths
declarations.signature: theorem mem_mersenneAchievementSet_of_straddle_all_depths {t : ℝ} (h : ∀ d : ℕ, ∃ u : Finset ℕ, IsStraddlePrefix t u d) : t ∈ mersenneAchievementSet
declarations.docstring: **Closed-set straddle criterion.** If some finite word straddles the target at every depth, the target is achieved. Compactness of the achievement set replaces the packet's nested-cylinder limit: the straddle words need not be nested, consistent, or produced by any common process.

declarations.name: isStraddlePrefix_step_trichotomy
declarations.signature: theorem isStraddlePrefix_step_trichotomy {t : ℝ} {u : Finset ℕ} {d : ℕ} (hu : IsStraddlePrefix t u d) : IsStraddlePrefix t u (d + 1) ∨ IsStraddlePrefix t (insert (d + 1) u) (d + 1) ∨ (positiveMersenneSupportValue (↑u : Set ℕ) + mersenneTail (d + 1) < t ∧ t < positiveMersenneSupportValue (↑u : Set ℕ) + mersenneWeight (d + 1))
declarations.docstring: **Packet (23), rank-granular form.** From a straddle at depth `d`, one exact comparison of the residual against `(τ_{d+1}, w_{d+1})` yields: the word still straddles after skipping `d+1`, the extended word straddles after selecting `d+1`, or the target sits in the fatal Mersenne gap.

declarations.name: positiveMersenneSupportValue_coe_finset_ne_half
declarations.signature: theorem positiveMersenneSupportValue_coe_finset_ne_half {u : Finset ℕ} (h0 : 0 ∉ u) : positiveMersenneSupportValue (↑u : Set ℕ) ≠ (1 / 2 : ℝ)
declarations.docstring: **Greedy endpoint kill.** No finite positive support word has value exactly `1/2`: the audited reduced denominator of the finite Erdős sum is odd.

declarations.name: half_ne_coe_finset_add_mersenneTail
declarations.signature: theorem half_ne_coe_finset_add_mersenneTail (u : Finset ℕ) (d : ℕ) : positiveMersenneSupportValue (↑u : Set ℕ) + mersenneTail d ≠ (1 / 2 : ℝ)
declarations.docstring: **Lazy endpoint kill.** No finite positive support word plus a complete depth-`d` tail equals `1/2`: otherwise the Erdős–Borwein constant would be rational.

declarations.name: IsStraddlePrefix.half_strict
declarations.signature: theorem IsStraddlePrefix.half_strict {u : Finset ℕ} {d : ℕ} (hu : IsStraddlePrefix (1 / 2 : ℝ) u d) : positiveMersenneSupportValue (↑u : Set ℕ) < 1 / 2 ∧ (1 / 2 : ℝ) < positiveMersenneSupportValue (↑u : Set ℕ) + mersenneTail d
declarations.docstring: **Packet §7.** For the half target every straddle is strictly interior: both dyadic-expansion endpoint cases of the crossed-boundary classification are impossible.

declarations.name: IsStraddlePrefix.half_step_forced
declarations.signature: theorem IsStraddlePrefix.half_step_forced {u : Finset ℕ} {d : ℕ} (hu : IsStraddlePrefix (1 / 2 : ℝ) u d) : (IsStraddlePrefix (1 / 2 : ℝ) u (d + 1) ∧ ¬ IsStraddlePrefix (1 / 2 : ℝ) (insert (d + 1) u) (d + 1)) ∨ (IsStraddlePrefix (1 / 2 : ℝ) (insert (d + 1) u) (d + 1) ∧ ¬ IsStraddlePrefix (1 / 2 : ℝ) u (d + 1)) ∨ (positiveMersenneSupportValue (↑u : Set ℕ) + mersenneTail (d + 1) < 1 / 2 ∧ (1 / 2 : ℝ) < positiveMersenneSupportValue (↑u : Set ℕ) + mersenneWeight (d + 1))
declarations.docstring: **Packet (25).** The half-specialized macrostep is deterministic: from a straddle at depth `d`, exactly one of skip-refinement, take-refinement, or a fatal gap occurs, and the two refinement branches exclude each other.

declarations.name: IsStraddlePrefix.erase_top
declarations.signature: theorem IsStraddlePrefix.erase_top {t : ℝ} {u : Finset ℕ} {d : ℕ} (hu : IsStraddlePrefix t u (d + 1)) : IsStraddlePrefix t (u.erase (d + 1)) d
declarations.docstring: Removing the top bit from a depth-`d+1` straddling word recovers a depth-`d` straddling word. This is the backwards edge needed to compare the cut-locator tree with the canonical greedy recursion.

declarations.name: positiveMersenneSupportValue_ne_of_rank_gap
declarations.signature: theorem positiveMersenneSupportValue_ne_of_rank_gap {t : ℝ} {A : Set ℕ} {d : ℕ} (hlo : mersenneSupportPrefix A d + mersenneTail (d + 1) < t) (hhi : t < mersenneSupportPrefix A d + mersenneWeight (d + 1)) : positiveMersenneSupportValue A ≠ t
declarations.docstring: **Packet (24), general form.** If the target sits strictly inside the rank-`(d+1)` Mersenne gap over the prefix mass of `A` through `d`, then the support `A` does not achieve the target — whichever way it continues.

declarations.name: mersenneSupportPrefix_eq_coe_finset
declarations.signature: theorem mersenneSupportPrefix_eq_coe_finset {A : Set ℕ} {u : Finset ℕ} {d : ℕ} (hu : ∀ n ∈ u, 0 < n ∧ n ≤ d) (hagree : ∀ n : ℕ, 0 < n → n ≤ d → (n ∈ A ↔ n ∈ u)) : mersenneSupportPrefix A d = positiveMersenneSupportValue (↑u : Set ℕ)
declarations.docstring: Prefix agreement transports a finite word's value onto the support prefix mass.

declarations.name: IsStraddlePrefix.half_agrees_greedy
declarations.signature: theorem IsStraddlePrefix.half_agrees_greedy {u : Finset ℕ} {d : ℕ} (hu : IsStraddlePrefix (1 / 2 : ℝ) u d) : ∀ n : ℕ, 0 < n → n ≤ d → (n ∈ u ↔ n ∈ greedyMersenneSupport (1 / 2 : ℝ))
declarations.docstring: Every half-straddling word is the actual greedy word through the same depth. Strict superincreasingness enters only in the skipped top-bit case; the selected case follows from the prefix being below the target.

declarations.name: half_ne_of_prefix_gap
declarations.signature: theorem half_ne_of_prefix_gap {u : Finset ℕ} {d : ℕ} (hu : ∀ n ∈ u, 0 < n ∧ n ≤ d) (hlo : positiveMersenneSupportValue (↑u : Set ℕ) + mersenneTail (d + 1) < 1 / 2) (hhi : (1 / 2 : ℝ) < positiveMersenneSupportValue (↑u : Set ℕ) + mersenneWeight (d + 1)) {A : Set ℕ} (hagree : ∀ n : ℕ, 0 < n → n ≤ d → (n ∈ A ↔ n ∈ u)) : positiveMersenneSupportValue A ≠ (1 / 2 : ℝ)
declarations.docstring: **Packet (24), word form.** A fatal gap certified over a finite word excludes the half target for every support agreeing with that word below the gap rank.

declarations.name: ExistsFatalHalfGap
declarations.docstring: A finite half-gap witness in the cut-locator coordinates.

declarations.name: half_not_mem_mersenneAchievementSet_of_exists_fatal_gap
declarations.docstring: A fatal half-gap witness is global, not merely branch-local. Its two strict inequalities already make its finite word a depth-`d` straddle; the canonicality theorem therefore identifies that word with the actual greedy prefix, after which the word-level exclusion applies to every hypothetical half representation.

declarations.name: initial_half_straddle_zero
declarations.docstring: The empty word straddles the half target at depth zero.

declarations.name: initial_half_straddle
declarations.docstring: **Packet §8/§9 base case.** The empty word still straddles at depth one: the depth-one tail exceeds `19/35 > 1/2`.

declarations.name: half_mem_mersenneAchievementSet_of_no_fatal_gap
declarations.signature: theorem half_mem_mersenneAchievementSet_of_no_fatal_gap (hnogap : ∀ (u : Finset ℕ) (d : ℕ), (∀ n ∈ u, 0 < n ∧ n ≤ d) → ¬ (positiveMersenneSupportValue (↑u : Set ℕ) + mersenneTail (d + 1) < 1 / 2 ∧ (1 / 2 : ℝ) < positiveMersenneSupportValue (↑u : Set ℕ) + mersenneWeight (d + 1))) : (1 / 2 : ℝ) ∈ mersenneAchievementSet
declarations.docstring: **No fatal gap forces membership.** If no finite word ever certifies a fatal Mersenne gap, the rank-step trichotomy produces straddle words at every depth and the closed-set criterion achieves `1/2`.

declarations.name: half_mem_mersenneAchievementSet_or_exists_fatal_gap
declarations.signature: theorem half_mem_mersenneAchievementSet_or_exists_fatal_gap : (1 / 2 : ℝ) ∈ mersenneAchievementSet ∨ ∃ (u : Finset ℕ) (d : ℕ), (∀ n ∈ u, 0 < n ∧ n ≤ d) ∧ positiveMersenneSupportValue (↑u : Set ℕ) + mersenneTail (d + 1) < 1 / 2 ∧ (1 / 2 : ℝ) < positiveMersenneSupportValue (↑u : Set ℕ) + mersenneWeight (d + 1)
declarations.docstring: **Packet (29): the complete mechanism dichotomy.** Either some finite word certifies a fatal Mersenne gap — a finite, exact, checkable nonmembership certificate against all its extensions — or `1/2` is achieved. Deciding that the fatal branch never fires is exactly the open half case of Erdős #257; no branch decision is claimed here.

declarations.name: existsFatalHalfGap_iff_half_not_mem_mersenneAchievementSet
declarations.docstring: The cut-locator fatal branch is exactly the complement of half membership. The reverse direction uses the landed global dichotomy; the forward direction is the new canonical-prefix exclusion above.

declarations.name: half_mem_mersenneAchievementSet_iff_no_existsFatalHalfGap
declarations.docstring: Positive form of the complete cut-locator classification.

declarations.name: half_sub_four_term_prefix_eq
declarations.signature: theorem half_sub_four_term_prefix_eq : (1 / 2 : ℚ) - finiteErdosSum ({2, 3, 6, 7} : Finset ℕ) 2 = 1 / 16002
declarations.docstring: **Packet §9 first crossed boundary.** Selecting `{2,3,6,7}` from the half target leaves the exact rational residual `1/16002`: the `m = 3` boundary at `51/128` resolves to a right refinement.

declarations.name: mersenneCorrectionTail_one_le
declarations.docstring: **Packet §9 first window.** The depth-one correction tail is at most `5/42`.

declarations.name: three_eighths_lt_half_sub_mersenneCorrectionTail_one
declarations.docstring: **Packet §9 boundary-free first macrostep.** The depth-one correction window sits strictly above `3/8`, so the first refinement crosses no dyadic boundary.

declarations.name: toNatWord
declarations.signature: def toNatWord {s : ℕ} (b : SeamRowWord s) : ℕ → Bool

declarations.name: toList
declarations.signature: def toList {s : ℕ} (b : SeamRowWord s) : List Bool

declarations.name: ofList
declarations.signature: def ofList {s : ℕ} (bits : List Bool) (hlen : bits.length = s - 2) : SeamRowWord s

declarations.name: toList_ofList
declarations.signature: @[simp] theorem toList_ofList {s : ℕ} (bits : List Bool) (hlen : bits.length = s - 2) : toList (ofList bits hlen) = bits

declarations.name: toList_injective
declarations.signature: theorem toList_injective {s : ℕ} : Function.Injective (toList : SeamRowWord s → List Bool)

declarations.name: seamWeightsFrom_length_eq
declarations.signature: theorem seamWeightsFrom_length_eq (s d : ℕ) : (seamWeightsFrom s d).length = s - d

declarations.name: seamWeightsFrom_eq_ofFn
declarations.signature: theorem seamWeightsFrom_eq_ofFn {s d : ℕ} (hds : d ≤ s) : seamWeightsFrom s d = List.ofFn (fun i : Fin (s - d) => truncatedMersenneWeight s (d + i))

declarations.name: seamWeights_eq_ofFn
declarations.signature: theorem seamWeights_eq_ofFn {s : ℕ} (hs : 2 ≤ s) : seamWeights s = List.ofFn (fun i : Fin (s - 2) => truncatedMersenneWeight s (i + 2))

declarations.name: weightedBoolSum_ofFn
declarations.signature: theorem weightedBoolSum_ofFn {n : ℕ} (w : Fin n → ℕ) (b : Fin n → Bool) : weightedBoolSum (List.ofFn w) (List.ofFn b) = ∑ i : Fin n, if b i then w i else 0

declarations.name: weightedBoolSum_toList_eq_wordWeightSum
declarations.signature: theorem weightedBoolSum_toList_eq_wordWeightSum {s : ℕ} (hs : 2 ≤ s) (b : SeamRowWord s) : weightedBoolSum (seamWeights s) b.toList = wordWeightSum s b.toNatWord

declarations.name: seamPerturbedFamily
declarations.signature: def seamPerturbedFamily (s : ℕ) (hs : 3 ≤ s) : PerturbedFamily (SeamRowWord s)

declarations.name: seamPerturbedFamily_newSum_eq
declarations.signature: theorem seamPerturbedFamily_newSum_eq {s : ℕ} (hs : 3 ≤ s) (b : SeamRowWord s) : (seamPerturbedFamily s hs).newSum b = wordWeightSum (s + 1) (extendWord s false b.toNatWord)

declarations.name: seamPulseCap_lt_gap
declarations.signature: theorem seamPulseCap_lt_gap {s : ℕ} (hs : 3 ≤ s) : (seamPerturbedFamily s hs).pulseCap < (seamPerturbedFamily s hs).gap

declarations.name: seamGreedyWord_toList
declarations.signature: @[simp] theorem seamGreedyWord_toList (s : ℕ) : (seamGreedyWord s).toList = integerGreedyBits (seamWeights s) (seamSubsetTarget s)

declarations.name: wordWeightSum_seamHeadThreeWord
declarations.signature: theorem wordWeightSum_seamHeadThreeWord {s : ℕ} (hs : 5 ≤ s) : wordWeightSum s (seamHeadThreeWord s).toNatWord = truncatedMersenneWeight s 2 + truncatedMersenneWeight s 3 + truncatedMersenneWeight s 4

declarations.name: seamHeadThreeWord_above_target
declarations.signature: theorem seamHeadThreeWord_above_target {s : ℕ} (hs : 5 ≤ s) : seamSubsetTarget s < wordWeightSum s (seamHeadThreeWord s).toNatWord

declarations.name: exists_seamWord_minimal_above
declarations.signature: theorem exists_seamWord_minimal_above {s : ℕ} (hs : 5 ≤ s) : ∃ a : SeamRowWord s, seamSubsetTarget s < (seamPerturbedFamily s (by omega)).oldSum a ∧ ∀ x : SeamRowWord s, seamSubsetTarget s < (seamPerturbedFamily s (by omega)).oldSum x → (seamPerturbedFamily s (by omega)).oldSum a ≤ (seamPerturbedFamily s (by omega)).oldSum x

declarations.name: seamAboveWord
declarations.signature: noncomputable def seamAboveWord (s : ℕ) (hs : 5 ≤ s) : SeamRowWord s

declarations.name: seamAboveWord_strict
declarations.signature: theorem seamAboveWord_strict {s : ℕ} (hs : 5 ≤ s) : seamSubsetTarget s < (seamPerturbedFamily s (by omega)).oldSum (seamAboveWord s hs)

declarations.name: seamAboveWord_minimal
declarations.signature: theorem seamAboveWord_minimal {s : ℕ} (hs : 5 ≤ s) (x : SeamRowWord s) (hx : seamSubsetTarget s < (seamPerturbedFamily s (by omega)).oldSum x) : (seamPerturbedFamily s (by omega)).oldSum (seamAboveWord s hs) ≤ (seamPerturbedFamily s (by omega)).oldSum x

declarations.name: seamAdjacentCut
declarations.signature: noncomputable def seamAdjacentCut (s : ℕ) (hs : 5 ≤ s) : (seamPerturbedFamily s (by omega)).AdjacentCut (seamSubsetTarget s)

declarations.name: seamAdjacentCut_remainder
declarations.signature: @[simp] theorem seamAdjacentCut_remainder {s : ℕ} (hs : 5 ≤ s) : (seamAdjacentCut s hs).remainder = seamIntegerGreedyRemainder s

declarations.name: adjacentCutSuccessorCarriesDecidable
declarations.signature: noncomputable local instance adjacentCutSuccessorCarriesDecidable {α : Type*} {F : PerturbedFamily α} {C : ℕ} (K : F.AdjacentCut C) : Decidable K.successorCarries

declarations.name: extend
declarations.signature: def extend {s : ℕ} (b : SeamRowWord s) (beta : Bool) : SeamRowWord (s + 1)

declarations.name: dropTerminal
declarations.signature: def dropTerminal {s : ℕ} (hs : 3 ≤ s) (b : SeamRowWord (s + 1)) : SeamRowWord s

declarations.name: terminal
declarations.signature: def terminal {s : ℕ} (hs : 3 ≤ s) (b : SeamRowWord (s + 1)) : Bool

declarations.name: dropTerminal_extend
declarations.signature: @[simp] theorem dropTerminal_extend {s : ℕ} (hs : 3 ≤ s) (b : SeamRowWord s) (beta : Bool) : dropTerminal hs (extend b beta) = b

declarations.name: terminal_extend
declarations.signature: @[simp] theorem terminal_extend {s : ℕ} (hs : 3 ≤ s) (b : SeamRowWord s) (beta : Bool) : terminal hs (extend b beta) = beta

declarations.name: extend_dropTerminal_terminal
declarations.signature: theorem extend_dropTerminal_terminal {s : ℕ} (hs : 3 ≤ s) (b : SeamRowWord (s + 1)) : extend (dropTerminal hs b) (terminal hs b) = b

declarations.name: toNatWord_extend
declarations.signature: theorem toNatWord_extend {s : ℕ} (hs : 3 ≤ s) (b : SeamRowWord s) (beta : Bool) : (extend b beta).toNatWord = extendWord s beta b.toNatWord

declarations.name: seamAdjacentCut_newCapacity
declarations.signature: theorem seamAdjacentCut_newCapacity {s : ℕ} (hs : 5 ≤ s) : (seamAdjacentCut s hs).newCapacity = seamSubsetTarget (s + 1)

declarations.name: seamAdjacentCut_terminalWeight
declarations.signature: @[simp] theorem seamAdjacentCut_terminalWeight {s : ℕ} (hs : 5 ≤ s) : (seamAdjacentCut s hs).terminalWeight = 2 ^ (s + 2) + 4

declarations.name: wordWeightSum_rowWord_extend
declarations.signature: theorem wordWeightSum_rowWord_extend {s : ℕ} (hs : 3 ≤ s) (b : SeamRowWord s) (beta : Bool) : wordWeightSum (s + 1) (b.extend beta).toNatWord = (seamPerturbedFamily s hs).newSum b + if beta then 2 ^ (s + 2) + 4 else 0

declarations.name: seamPrefixChoice_admissible
declarations.signature: theorem seamPrefixChoice_admissible {s : ℕ} (hs : 5 ≤ s) : let K

declarations.name: seamCutNextWord
declarations.signature: noncomputable def seamCutNextWord (s : ℕ) (hs : 5 ≤ s) : SeamRowWord (s + 1)

declarations.name: seamCutNextWord_sum
declarations.signature: theorem seamCutNextWord_sum {s : ℕ} (hs : 5 ≤ s) : wordWeightSum (s + 1) (seamCutNextWord s hs).toNatWord = (seamPerturbedFamily s (by omega)).newSum (seamAdjacentCut s hs).prefixChoice + if (seamAdjacentCut s hs).terminalWeight ≤ (seamAdjacentCut s hs).prefixRemainder then (seamAdjacentCut s hs).terminalWeight else 0

declarations.name: seamCutNextWord_admissible
declarations.signature: theorem seamCutNextWord_admissible {s : ℕ} (hs : 5 ≤ s) : wordWeightSum (s + 1) (seamCutNextWord s hs).toNatWord ≤ seamSubsetTarget (s + 1)

declarations.name: seamCut_nextRemainder_eq_target_sub_sum
declarations.signature: theorem seamCut_nextRemainder_eq_target_sub_sum {s : ℕ} (hs : 5 ≤ s) : (seamAdjacentCut s hs).nextRemainder = seamSubsetTarget (s + 1) - wordWeightSum (s + 1) (seamCutNextWord s hs).toNatWord

declarations.name: terminalWeight_le_newSum_gap
declarations.signature: theorem terminalWeight_le_newSum_gap {s : ℕ} (hs : 5 ≤ s) {x y : SeamRowWord s} (hxy : (seamPerturbedFamily s (by omega)).oldSum x < (seamPerturbedFamily s (by omega)).oldSum y) : (seamPerturbedFamily s (by omega)).newSum x + (seamAdjacentCut s hs).terminalWeight ≤ (seamPerturbedFamily s (by omega)).newSum y

declarations.name: seamCutNextWord_maximal
declarations.signature: theorem seamCutNextWord_maximal {s : ℕ} (hs : 5 ≤ s) {x : SeamRowWord (s + 1)} (hx : wordWeightSum (s + 1) x.toNatWord ≤ seamSubsetTarget (s + 1)) : wordWeightSum (s + 1) x.toNatWord ≤ wordWeightSum (s + 1) (seamCutNextWord s hs).toNatWord

declarations.name: seamCutNextWord_eq_greedyWord_succ
declarations.signature: theorem seamCutNextWord_eq_greedyWord_succ {s : ℕ} (hs : 5 ≤ s) : seamCutNextWord s hs = seamGreedyWord (s + 1)

declarations.name: seamAdjacentCut_nextRemainder
declarations.signature: @[simp] theorem seamAdjacentCut_nextRemainder {s : ℕ} (hs : 5 ≤ s) : (seamAdjacentCut s hs).nextRemainder = seamIntegerGreedyRemainder (s + 1)

declarations.name: wordSupport_subset_Iic
declarations.signature: theorem wordSupport_subset_Iic {N : ℕ} (a : HalfWord N) : wordSupport a ⊆ Set.Iic N
declarations.docstring: A finite word has no support beyond its declared depth.

declarations.name: wordSupport_inter_Iic
declarations.signature: @[simp] theorem wordSupport_inter_Iic {N : ℕ} (a : HalfWord N) : wordSupport a ∩ Set.Iic N = wordSupport a
declarations.docstring: Intersecting the support of a depth-`N` word with `Iic N` changes nothing.

declarations.name: one_not_mem_wordSupport_of_admissible
declarations.signature: theorem one_not_mem_wordSupport_of_admissible {N : ℕ} (a : HalfWord N) (ha : HalfStripAdmissible N a) : 1 ∉ wordSupport a
declarations.docstring: Admissibility at a positive depth excludes exponent one from the word support.

declarations.name: halfStripAdmissible_residual_eq
declarations.signature: theorem halfStripAdmissible_residual_eq {N : ℕ} (hN : 1 ≤ N) (a : HalfWord N) (ha : HalfStripAdmissible N a) : (1 : ℝ) / 2 - erdosSupportSeries 2 (wordSupport a) = ((integerHalfCarry (wordSupport a) (N - 1) : ℤ) - binaryCoeffTail (supportCoeff (wordSupport a)) N) / (2 : ℝ) ^ N
declarations.docstring: **Exact finite-shadow identity.** For an admissible word at positive depth `N`, the discrepancy of its finite Mersenne support sum from `1/2` is the terminal integer half carry minus the future-multiple coefficient tail, scaled by `2^N`.

declarations.name: finiteCoeffWindow
declarations.signature: noncomputable def finiteCoeffWindow (A : Set ℕ) (n J : ℕ) : ℝ
declarations.docstring: The first `J` coefficient rows strictly inside the complete analytic tail after row `n`.

declarations.name: one_sub_pow_mul_halfResidual_eq_tail_sub_centered
declarations.signature: theorem one_sub_pow_mul_halfResidual_eq_tail_sub_centered (A : Set ℕ) (hone : 1 ∉ A) (n : ℕ) (hn : 1 ≤ n) : 1 - (2 : ℝ) ^ n * ((1 : ℝ) / 2 - erdosSupportSeries 2 A) = binaryCoeffTail (supportCoeff A) n - (mobiusCenteredHalfCarry A (n - 1) : ℝ)
declarations.docstring: Exact carry-coordinate form of normalized raw half slack. The index `n-1` is essential: its coefficient horizon ends at row `n`, while the analytic tail begins at row `n+1`.

declarations.name: finiteCoeffWindow_le_binaryCoeffTail
declarations.signature: theorem finiteCoeffWindow_le_binaryCoeffTail (A : Set ℕ) (n J : ℕ) : finiteCoeffWindow A n J ≤ binaryCoeffTail (supportCoeff A) n
declarations.docstring: Every finite coefficient window is termwise below the complete analytic tail. This strict truncation is what makes the next theorem a sufficient producer rather than an equivalent restatement of raw safety.

declarations.name: scaledHalfResidual_le_one_of_finiteCoeffWindow
declarations.signature: theorem scaledHalfResidual_le_one_of_finiteCoeffWindow (A : Set ℕ) (hone : 1 ∉ A) (n J : ℕ) (hn : 1 ≤ n) (hwindow : (mobiusCenteredHalfCarry A (n - 1) : ℝ) ≤ finiteCoeffWindow A n J) : (2 : ℝ) ^ n * ((1 : ℝ) / 2 - erdosSupportSeries 2 A) ≤ 1
declarations.docstring: A finite future-divisor incidence inequality forces the normalized residual below one. The remaining unbounded problem is precisely to produce `hwindow` on every actual greedy prefix.

declarations.name: rawHalfSlack_nonneg_of_finiteCoeffWindow
declarations.signature: theorem rawHalfSlack_nonneg_of_finiteCoeffWindow (A : Set ℕ) (hone : 1 ∉ A) (n J : ℕ) (hn : 1 ≤ n) (hwindow : (mobiusCenteredHalfCarry A (n - 1) : ℝ) ≤ finiteCoeffWindow A n J) : 0 ≤ 1 - (2 : ℝ) ^ n * ((1 : ℝ) / 2 - erdosSupportSeries 2 A)
declarations.docstring: The same finite-incidence implication in explicitly nonnegative-slack form.

declarations.name: finiteCoeffWindowNumerator
declarations.signature: noncomputable def finiteCoeffWindowNumerator (A : Set ℕ) (n : ℕ) : ℕ → ℕ | 0 => 0 | J + 1 => 2 * finiteCoeffWindowNumerator A n J + supportCoeff A (n + J + 1) /-- Binary capacity carried
declarations.docstring: Integer numerator of the frozen coefficient window. The recurrence uses the binary weights `2^(J-i)` without division.

declarations.name: futureSkipCapacity
declarations.signature: noncomputable def futureSkipCapacity (A : Set ℕ) (n : ℕ) : ℕ → ℕ | 0 => 0 | J + 1 => 2 * futureSkipCapacity A n J + (by classical exact if n + J + 1 ∈ A then 0 else 1) /-- The recursive numerator is exactly `2^J` times the existing real-valued finite coefficient window. -/ theorem finiteCoeffWindow_eq_numerator_div_pow (A : Set ℕ) (n J : ℕ) : finiteCoeffWindow A n J =
declarations.docstring: Binary capacity carried by skipped support bits in the next `J` rows. The earliest skip receives the largest binary weight.

declarations.name: finiteCoeffWindow_eq_numerator_div_pow
declarations.signature: theorem finiteCoeffWindow_eq_numerator_div_pow (A : Set ℕ) (n J : ℕ) : finiteCoeffWindow A n J = (finiteCoeffWindowNumerator A n J : ℝ) / (2 : ℝ) ^ J
declarations.docstring: The recursive numerator is exactly `2^J` times the existing real-valued finite coefficient window.

declarations.name: binaryCoeffTail_eq_finiteCoeffWindow_add_shiftedTail
declarations.signature: theorem binaryCoeffTail_eq_finiteCoeffWindow_add_shiftedTail (A : Set ℕ) (n J : ℕ) : binaryCoeffTail (supportCoeff A) n = finiteCoeffWindow A n J + binaryCoeffTail (supportCoeff A) (n + J) / (2 : ℝ) ^ J
declarations.docstring: Exact decomposition of the complete coefficient tail into its first `J` rows and the shifted complete tail that remains.

declarations.name: futureSkipCapacity_factorization
declarations.signature: theorem futureSkipCapacity_factorization (A : Set ℕ) (n J : ℕ) (hn : 1 ≤ n) (hJ : J ≤ n) : (finiteCoeffWindowNumerator (primitivePrefix A n) n J : ℤ) - (2 : ℤ) ^ J * mobiusCenteredHalfCarry A (n - 1) = (futureSkipCapacity A n J : ℤ) - mobiusCenteredHalfCarry A (n - 1 + J)
declarations.docstring: **Exact future-skip factorization.** Freeze the support at depth `n` and inspect any window `J <= n`. The weighted frozen incidence surplus is exactly the binary capacity of actual future skips minus the actual centered carry at the end of the window.

declarations.name: finiteCoeffWindowNumerator_covers_iff_terminal_le_skipCapacity
declarations.signature: theorem finiteCoeffWindowNumerator_covers_iff_terminal_le_skipCapacity (A : Set ℕ) (n J : ℕ) (hn : 1 ≤ n) (hJ : J ≤ n) : (2 : ℤ) ^ J * mobiusCenteredHalfCarry A (n - 1) ≤ (finiteCoeffWindowNumerator (primitivePrefix A n) n J : ℤ) ↔ mobiusCenteredHalfCarry A (n - 1 + J) ≤ (futureSkipCapacity A n J : ℤ)
declarations.docstring: Integer form of the exact producer: the frozen coefficient numerator covers the initial centered carry iff the future skip word covers the actual terminal centered carry.

declarations.name: finiteCoeffWindow_covers_iff_terminal_le_skipCapacity
declarations.signature: theorem finiteCoeffWindow_covers_iff_terminal_le_skipCapacity (A : Set ℕ) (n J : ℕ) (hn : 1 ≤ n) (hJ : J ≤ n) : (mobiusCenteredHalfCarry A (n - 1) : ℝ) ≤ finiteCoeffWindow (primitivePrefix A n) n J ↔ mobiusCenteredHalfCarry A (n - 1 + J) ≤ (futureSkipCapacity A n J : ℤ)
declarations.docstring: Real-valued form consumed by `scaledHalfResidual_le_one_of_finiteCoeffWindow`. The finite-incidence hypothesis is not merely sufficient on the first shell: it is exactly terminal-carry coverage by the actual future skip word.

declarations.name: ReducedTailGapCoordinates
declarations.signature: def ReducedTailGapCoordinates (P : Set ℕ) (n : ℕ) (E : ℤ) (D : ℕ) : Prop
declarations.docstring: A support prefix has reduced tail-gap coordinates `(E,D)` at row `n` when its centered carry minus its complete coefficient tail is exactly `E/D`.

declarations.name: halfGreedyPrefix_reducedTailGapCoordinates
declarations.signature: theorem halfGreedyPrefix_reducedTailGapCoordinates (k : ℕ) : ReducedTailGapCoordinates (↑(halfGreedyPrefixSupport k) : Set ℕ) (k + 1) (halfGreedyNextDyadicExcessNumerator k) (halfGreedyPrefixDenominator k)
declarations.docstring: The landed half-greedy excess and denominator are literally reduced tail-gap coordinates for the finite greedy prefix.

declarations.name: futureSkipCapacityMargin_eq_reducedTailTax
declarations.signature: theorem futureSkipCapacityMargin_eq_reducedTailTax (A P : Set ℕ) (n J : ℕ) (E : ℤ) (D : ℕ) (hn : 1 ≤ n) (hJ : J ≤ n) (hprefix : primitivePrefix A n = P) (hred : ReducedTailGapCoordinates P n E D) : (futureSkipCapacity A n J : ℝ) - (mobiusCenteredHalfCarry A (n - 1 + J) : ℝ) = -(2 : ℝ) ^ J * ((E : ℝ) / (D : ℝ)) - binaryCoeffTail (supportCoeff P) (n + J)
declarations.docstring: **Reduced-state skip-capacity identity.** If the prefix of an actual support `A` through `n` has reduced coordinates `(E,D)`, then the future-skip margin is the negative reduced excess, amplified by `2^J`, minus the exact coefficient tail omitted beyond the finite window. This is the proof socket `S_J - e_J = -2^J E/D - R_(n,J)`.

declarations.name: futureSkipCoverage_iff_reducedTailTax
declarations.signature: theorem futureSkipCoverage_iff_reducedTailTax (A P : Set ℕ) (n J : ℕ) (E : ℤ) (D : ℕ) (hn : 1 ≤ n) (hJ : J ≤ n) (hprefix : primitivePrefix A n = P) (hred : ReducedTailGapCoordinates P n E D) : mobiusCenteredHalfCarry A (n - 1 + J) ≤ (futureSkipCapacity A n J : ℤ) ↔ binaryCoeffTail (supportCoeff P) (n + J) ≤ -(2 : ℝ) ^ J * ((E : ℝ) / (D : ℝ))
declarations.docstring: Coverage by the future skip word is exactly the reduced tail-tax inequality.

declarations.name: futureSkipCoverage_succ
declarations.signature: theorem futureSkipCoverage_succ (A : Set ℕ) (n t : ℕ) (hn : 1 ≤ n) (ht : t + 1 ≤ n) (hcover : mobiusCenteredHalfCarry A (n - 1 + t) ≤ (futureSkipCapacity A n t : ℤ)) : mobiusCenteredHalfCarry A (n - 1 + (t + 1)) ≤ (futureSkipCapacity A n (t + 1) : ℤ)
declarations.docstring: One-step permanence: once future skip capacity covers the actual centered carry, the next first-shell row remains covered.

declarations.name: futureSkipCoverage_persistent
declarations.signature: theorem futureSkipCoverage_persistent (A : Set ℕ) (n t u : ℕ) (hn : 1 ≤ n) (htu : t + u ≤ n) (hcover : mobiusCenteredHalfCarry A (n - 1 + t) ≤ (futureSkipCapacity A n t : ℤ)) : mobiusCenteredHalfCarry A (n - 1 + (t + u)) ≤ (futureSkipCapacity A n (t + u) : ℤ)
declarations.docstring: **First-shell persistence.** Coverage at any intermediate time remains true at every later time that stays inside the first dyadic shell.

declarations.name: futureSkipWeight_le_capacity_of_not_mem
declarations.signature: theorem futureSkipWeight_le_capacity_of_not_mem (A : Set ℕ) (n J i : ℕ) (hi : i < J) (hskip : n + i + 1 ∉ A) : 2 ^ (J - (i + 1)) ≤ futureSkipCapacity A n J
declarations.docstring: Every omitted row contributes its own binary weight to the total skip capacity. In particular, the earliest skip is the strongest single-row certificate.

declarations.name: finiteCoeffWindow_covers_of_one_future_skip
declarations.signature: theorem finiteCoeffWindow_covers_of_one_future_skip (A : Set ℕ) (n J i : ℕ) (hn : 1 ≤ n) (hJ : J ≤ n) (hi : i < J) (hskip : n + i + 1 ∉ A) (hterminal : mobiusCenteredHalfCarry A (n - 1 + J) ≤ (2 ^ (J - (i + 1)) : ℕ)) : (mobiusCenteredHalfCarry A (n - 1) : ℝ) ≤ finiteCoeffWindow (primitivePrefix A n) n J
declarations.docstring: One sufficiently early actual skip is therefore enough to establish the finite incidence producer. The governed receipt tests precisely this stronger one-skip condition with `i` chosen as the first future skip.

declarations.name: scaledPrefixHalfResidual_le_one_of_terminal_le_skipCapacity
declarations.signature: theorem scaledPrefixHalfResidual_le_one_of_terminal_le_skipCapacity (A : Set ℕ) (hone : 1 ∉ A) (n J : ℕ) (hn : 1 ≤ n) (hJ : J ≤ n) (hterminal : mobiusCenteredHalfCarry A (n - 1 + J) ≤ (futureSkipCapacity A n J : ℤ)) : (2 : ℝ) ^ n * ((1 : ℝ) / 2 - erdosSupportSeries 2 (primitivePrefix A n)) ≤ 1
declarations.docstring: The exact skip-capacity condition feeds the already-landed residual consumer for the frozen prefix.

declarations.name: halfStripAdmissible_abs_residual_le
declarations.signature: theorem halfStripAdmissible_abs_residual_le {N : ℕ} (hN : 1 ≤ N) (a : HalfWord N) (ha : HalfStripAdmissible N a) : |(1 : ℝ) / 2 - erdosSupportSeries 2 (wordSupport a)| ≤ (4 * Real.sqrt (N : ℝ) + 8) / (2 : ℝ) ^ N
declarations.docstring: **Finite-shadow upper bound.** An admissible finite word is within `(4√N+8)/2^N` of `1/2`. This is an unconditional finite theorem; it does not assert that such words exist at every depth.

declarations.name: preFeedbackGaugeSymmetry
declarations.signature: theorem preFeedbackGaugeSymmetry (left right : Set ℕ) (s n : ℕ) (hs : 2 ≤ s) (hsn : s ≤ n) (hbase : integerHalfCarry right (s - 2) = integerHalfCarry left (s - 2)) (hseam : supportCoeff right s + 1 = supportCoeff left s) (htail : ∀ m : ℕ, s < m → m ≤ n → supportCoeff right m = supportCoeff left m + 1) : integerHalfCarry right (n - 1) = integerHalfCarry left (n - 1) + 1
declarations.docstring: **Pre-feedback gauge symmetry.** Suppose two support-coefficient words agree up to the carry immediately before row `s`, the left word has one additional coefficient at row `s`, and the right word has one additional coefficient at every later row through `n`. Their integer half carries then differ by exactly one at every row from `s` through `n`. This isolates the named pre-feedback no-go: the recurrence and Boolean-sized coefficient difference pattern are jointly consistent on the whole window, so a contradiction must use information not represented by those local equations.

declarations.name: supportCoeff_insert_divisor
declarations.signature: theorem supportCoeff_insert_divisor (A : Set ℕ) {d n : ℕ} (hd : d ∈ n.divisors) (hdA : d ∉ A) : supportCoeff (insert d A) n = supportCoeff A n + 1
declarations.docstring: Inserting one previously absent divisor adds exactly one to the Boolean divisor coefficient at that row.

declarations.name: supportCoeff_insert_self
declarations.signature: theorem supportCoeff_insert_self (A : Set ℕ) {n : ℕ} (hn : 0 < n) (hnA : n ∉ A) : supportCoeff (insert n A) n = supportCoeff A n + 1
declarations.docstring: Adding the current row itself to a support increases its divisor-count coefficient by exactly one.

declarations.name: firstFeedbackChildCarries
declarations.signature: theorem firstFeedbackChildCarries (left right : Set ℕ) (s c : ℕ) (δ : ℤ) (hs : 1 ≤ s) (hleftParent : integerHalfCarry left (2 * s - 2) = δ) (hrightParent : integerHalfCarry right (2 * s - 2) = δ + 1) (hleftCoeff : supportCoeff left (2 * s) = c + 1) (hrightCoeff : supportCoeff right (2 * s) = c) (hleftFresh : 2 * s ∉ left) (hrightFresh : 2 * s ∉ right) : integerHalfCarry left (2 * s - 1) = 2 * δ - c - 1 ∧ integerHalfCarry (insert (2 * s) left) (2 * s - 1) = 2 * δ - c - 2 ∧ integerHalfCarry right (2 * s - 1) = 2 * δ - c + 2 ∧ integerHalfCarry (insert (2 * s) right) (2 * s - 1) = 2 * δ - c + 1
declarations.docstring: **Exact row-`2s` feedback theorem.** Assume the two boundary parents have carries `δ` and `δ+1`, while their first feedback coefficients are `c+1` and `c`. The zero/one children then have carries `H-1,H-2,H+2,H+1`, respectively, where `H=2δ-c`. The hypotheses isolate the set-level divisor-realisation question from the now-closed feedback algebra.

declarations.name: firstFeedbackChildCarries_of_preFeedbackGauge
declarations.signature: theorem firstFeedbackChildCarries_of_preFeedbackGauge (left right : Set ℕ) (s c : ℕ) (δ : ℤ) (hs : 2 ≤ s) (hbase : integerHalfCarry right (s - 2) = integerHalfCarry left (s - 2)) (hseam : supportCoeff right s + 1 = supportCoeff left s) (htail : ∀ m : ℕ, s < m → m ≤ 2 * s - 1 → supportCoeff right m = supportCoeff left m + 1) (hdelta : integerHalfCarry left (2 * s - 2) = δ) (hleftCoeff : supportCoeff left (2 * s) = c + 1) (hrightCoeff : supportCoeff right (2 * s) = c) (hleftFresh : 2 * s ∉ left) (hrightFresh : 2 * s ∉ right) : integerHalfCarry left (2 * s - 1) = 2 * δ - c - 1 ∧ integerHalfCarry (insert (2 * s) left) (2 * s - 1) = 2 * δ - c - 2 ∧
declarations.docstring: The complete pre-feedback-to-first-feedback composition. The gauge hypotheses determine the right parent carry from the left one; only the row-`2s` coefficient reversal and the absence of an already-selected current bit remain as explicit Boolean-divisor inputs.

declarations.name: FixedCoeffNumeratorReachable
declarations.signature: def FixedCoeffNumeratorReachable (c T k q : ℤ) : Prop
declarations.docstring: Endpoint-bearing fixed-coefficient transition.

declarations.name: fixedCoeffCylinder_endpoint
declarations.signature: theorem fixedCoeffCylinder_endpoint {T h c k q b : ℤ} (hk : k = 2 * h - c - b) (hq : q = 2 * (T - h) + b) : q = (2 * T - c) - k
declarations.docstring: Under one common proper-divisor coefficient, the child numerator and carry add to the common endpoint `2T-c`.

declarations.name: fixedCoeffNumeratorReachable_iff_endpoint
declarations.signature: theorem fixedCoeffNumeratorReachable_iff_endpoint (c T k q : ℤ) : FixedCoeffNumeratorReachable c T k q ↔ q = (2 * T - c) - k
declarations.docstring: Complete scalar recurrence: every fixed-coefficient target has exactly the affine numerator with next endpoint `2T-c`.

declarations.name: firstWrap_rawEndpoint_decomposition
declarations.signature: theorem firstWrap_rawEndpoint_decomposition {P Q T δ c : ℤ} (hT : T = P * (2 * Q) + Q + δ) : 2 * T - c = (2 * P + 1) * (2 * Q) + (2 * δ - c)
declarations.docstring: At a first half-divisor wrap, write the old endpoint as a fixed prefix, the half-bit boundary, and boundary carry `δ`. The next raw endpoint is the extended prefix boundary plus the seam hole `2δ-c`.

declarations.name: actualFirstWrap_hole_eq_leftZeroCarry_add_one
declarations.signature: theorem actualFirstWrap_hole_eq_leftZeroCarry_add_one (P : Set ℕ) {s : ℕ} (hs : 1 ≤ s) (hsP : s ∉ P) (δ : ℤ) (hδ : integerHalfCarry (insert s P) (2 * s - 2) = δ) : 2 * δ - (supportCoeff P (2 * s) : ℤ) = integerHalfCarry (insert s P) (2 * s - 1) + 1
declarations.docstring: For an actual Boolean stem `P`, the scalar `c` is literally its divisor coefficient at row `2s`, and the seam hole is one more than the row-`2s` carry of the left boundary word `P ∪ {s}` with the new row left off.

declarations.name: hole_in_strip_iff_shiftedCarry_in_strip
declarations.signature: theorem hole_in_strip_iff_shiftedCarry_in_strip {B H K : ℤ} (hHK : H = K + 1) : (1 ≤ H ∧ H ≤ B) ↔ (0 ≤ K ∧ K < B)
declarations.docstring: Consequently an in-strip seam hole is exactly a left zero-child carry in the shifted strip `[0,B-1]`.

declarations.name: hole_avoids_integerStrip_iff
declarations.signature: theorem hole_avoids_integerStrip_iff {B H : ℤ} : (∀ k : ℤ, 1 ≤ k → k ≤ B → k ≠ H) ↔ H < 1 ∨ B < H
declarations.docstring: An integer hole misses an integer strip exactly when it is below the strip or above it.

declarations.name: evenSeam_fullIntegerStrip_iff_escape
declarations.signature: theorem evenSeam_fullIntegerStrip_iff_escape {B δ c : ℤ} : (∀ k : ℤ, 1 ≤ k → k ≤ B → EvenSeamReachable δ c k) ↔ 2 * δ - c < 1 ∨ B < 2 * δ - c
declarations.docstring: The landed one-hole theorem makes seam escape exactly equivalent to full scalar reachability of the integer strip.

declarations.name: greedyHalf_rankThree_prefix_fixture
