declarations.signature: theorem greedyHalf_rankThree_prefix_fixture : greedyMersennePrefixRat (1 / 2 : ℚ) 3 = ({2, 3} : Finset ℕ) ∧ (1 / 2 : ℚ) - finiteErdosSum ({2, 3} : Finset ℕ) 2 = 1 / 42
declarations.docstring: The exact first greedy post-take state is the `{2,3}` fragment with residual `1/42`.

declarations.name: rankThree_rawSafe_but_seamNotEscaped
declarations.signature: theorem rankThree_rawSafe_but_seamNotEscaped : BlockDyadicSafeAt 1 21 5 ∧ halfStripBound 6 = 8 ∧ ¬ ((2 : ℤ) * 2 - 1 < 1 ∨ (8 : ℤ) < (2 : ℤ) * 2 - 1) ∧ ¬ EvenSeamReachable 2 1 3
declarations.docstring: Exact non-equivalence witness. The greedy state after taking rank `3` is dyadically safe at the last predecessor before rank `6`, but its scalar half-cylinder seam has the in-strip hole `H=3`.

declarations.name: primitivePrefix_greedyMersenneSupport_eq_prefixRat
declarations.signature: theorem primitivePrefix_greedyMersenneSupport_eq_prefixRat (x : ℚ) (n : ℕ) : primitivePrefix (greedyMersenneSupport (x : ℝ)) n = (↑(greedyMersennePrefixRat x n) : Set ℕ)
declarations.docstring: The exact-rational finite greedy prefix is the cutoff of the real greedy support. This bridge is independent of any skip assumption.

declarations.name: primitivePrefix_greedyMersenneSupport_succ_eq_of_skip
declarations.signature: theorem primitivePrefix_greedyMersenneSupport_succ_eq_of_skip (x : ℝ) (k : ℕ) (hskip : ¬ mersenneWeight (k + 1) ≤ greedyMersenneRemainder x k) : primitivePrefix (greedyMersenneSupport x) (k + 1) = primitivePrefix (greedyMersenneSupport x) k
declarations.docstring: If the next real greedy decision skips rank `k + 1`, extending the cutoff from `k` to `k + 1` does not change the primitive prefix.

declarations.name: primitivePrefix_greedyHalf_eq_halfGreedyPrefixSupport_of_skip
declarations.signature: theorem primitivePrefix_greedyHalf_eq_halfGreedyPrefixSupport_of_skip (k : ℕ) (hskip : ¬ mersenneWeight (k + 1) ≤ greedyMersenneRemainder (1 / 2 : ℝ) k) : primitivePrefix (greedyMersenneSupport (1 / 2 : ℝ)) (k + 1) = (↑(halfGreedyPrefixSupport k) : Set ℕ)
declarations.docstring: At an actual skipped half-greedy rank, the full real support through `k + 1` is exactly the landed finite rational support through `k`.

declarations.name: greedyHalf_futureSkipCapacityMargin_eq_reducedTailTax
declarations.signature: theorem greedyHalf_futureSkipCapacityMargin_eq_reducedTailTax (k J : ℕ) (hskip : ¬ mersenneWeight (k + 1) ≤ greedyMersenneRemainder (1 / 2 : ℝ) k) (hJ : J ≤ k + 1) : (futureSkipCapacity (greedyMersenneSupport (1 / 2 : ℝ)) (k + 1) J : ℝ) - (mobiusCenteredHalfCarry (greedyMersenneSupport (1 / 2 : ℝ)) (k + J) : ℝ) = -(2 : ℝ) ^ J * ((halfGreedyNextDyadicExcessNumerator k : ℝ) / (halfGreedyPrefixDenominator k : ℝ)) - binaryCoeffTail (supportCoeff (↑(halfGreedyPrefixSupport k) : Set ℕ))
declarations.docstring: The generic reduced skip-capacity margin, instantiated at an actual skipped half-greedy rank.

declarations.name: greedyHalf_futureSkipCoverage_iff_reducedTailTax
declarations.signature: theorem greedyHalf_futureSkipCoverage_iff_reducedTailTax (k J : ℕ) (hskip : ¬ mersenneWeight (k + 1) ≤ greedyMersenneRemainder (1 / 2 : ℝ) k) (hJ : J ≤ k + 1) : mobiusCenteredHalfCarry (greedyMersenneSupport (1 / 2 : ℝ)) (k + J) ≤ (futureSkipCapacity (greedyMersenneSupport (1 / 2 : ℝ)) (k + 1) J : ℤ) ↔ binaryCoeffTail (supportCoeff (↑(halfGreedyPrefixSupport k) : Set ℕ)) (k + 1 + J) ≤ -(2 : ℝ) ^ J * ((halfGreedyNextDyadicExcessNumerator k : ℝ) /
declarations.docstring: Actual future-skip coverage is exactly the specialized reduced tail-tax inequality at every governed horizon `J ≤ k + 1`.

declarations.name: greedyHalfFrozenMargin
declarations.signature: noncomputable def greedyHalfFrozenMargin (k J : ℕ) : ℤ
declarations.docstring: Signed frozen-prefix margin. Its nonnegativity says that the first `J` future divisor-incidence rows cover the centered carry at depth `k`.

declarations.name: greedyHalfFrozenMargin_zero
declarations.signature: @[simp] theorem greedyHalfFrozenMargin_zero (k : ℕ) : greedyHalfFrozenMargin k 0 = -mobiusCenteredHalfCarry (↑(halfGreedyPrefixSupport k) : Set ℕ) k

declarations.name: greedyHalfFrozenMargin_succ
declarations.signature: theorem greedyHalfFrozenMargin_succ (k J : ℕ) : greedyHalfFrozenMargin k (J + 1) = 2 * greedyHalfFrozenMargin k J + (supportCoeff (↑(halfGreedyPrefixSupport k) : Set ℕ) (k + J + 2) : ℤ)
declarations.docstring: Exact integer recurrence. The forcing term is a divisor count in the frozen finite prefix, with no real tail or future greedy decision.

declarations.name: greedyHalfFrozenMargin_cast_eq_pow_mul_window_sub_carry
declarations.signature: theorem greedyHalfFrozenMargin_cast_eq_pow_mul_window_sub_carry (k J : ℕ) : (greedyHalfFrozenMargin k J : ℝ) = (2 : ℝ) ^ J * (finiteCoeffWindow (↑(halfGreedyPrefixSupport k) : Set ℕ) (k + 1) J - (mobiusCenteredHalfCarry (↑(halfGreedyPrefixSupport k) : Set ℕ) k : ℝ))
declarations.docstring: Normalized margin is exactly the finite coefficient window minus the initial centered carry.

declarations.name: greedyHalfFrozenMargin_nonneg_iff_window_covers
declarations.signature: theorem greedyHalfFrozenMargin_nonneg_iff_window_covers (k J : ℕ) : 0 ≤ greedyHalfFrozenMargin k J ↔ (mobiusCenteredHalfCarry (↑(halfGreedyPrefixSupport k) : Set ℕ) k : ℝ) ≤ finiteCoeffWindow (↑(halfGreedyPrefixSupport k) : Set ℕ) (k + 1) J
declarations.docstring: The signed recurrence crosses zero exactly when the finite frozen window covers the initial centered carry.

declarations.name: greedyHalfFrozenMargin_nonneg_succ
declarations.signature: theorem greedyHalfFrozenMargin_nonneg_succ (k J : ℕ) (h : 0 ≤ greedyHalfFrozenMargin k J) : 0 ≤ greedyHalfFrozenMargin k (J + 1)
declarations.docstring: Once the signed recurrence crosses zero, every later recurrence state stays nonnegative.

declarations.name: greedyHalfFrozenMargin_eq_actual_skip_margin
declarations.signature: theorem greedyHalfFrozenMargin_eq_actual_skip_margin (k J : ℕ) (hskip : ¬ mersenneWeight (k + 1) ≤ greedyMersenneRemainder (1 / 2 : ℝ) k) (hJ : J ≤ k + 1) : greedyHalfFrozenMargin k J = (futureSkipCapacity (greedyMersenneSupport (1 / 2 : ℝ)) (k + 1) J : ℤ) - mobiusCenteredHalfCarry (greedyMersenneSupport (1 / 2 : ℝ)) (k + J)
declarations.docstring: On an actual skipped rank and inside the first shell, the frozen margin is literally actual future-skip capacity minus the actual terminal carry.

declarations.name: greedyHalfFrozenMargin_nonneg_iff_reducedTailTax
declarations.signature: theorem greedyHalfFrozenMargin_nonneg_iff_reducedTailTax (k J : ℕ) (hskip : ¬ mersenneWeight (k + 1) ≤ greedyMersenneRemainder (1 / 2 : ℝ) k) (hJ : J ≤ k + 1) : 0 ≤ greedyHalfFrozenMargin k J ↔ binaryCoeffTail (supportCoeff (↑(halfGreedyPrefixSupport k) : Set ℕ)) (k + 1 + J) ≤ -(2 : ℝ) ^ J * ((halfGreedyNextDyadicExcessNumerator k : ℝ) / (halfGreedyPrefixDenominator k : ℝ))
declarations.docstring: The landed reduced tail-tax inequality is exactly nonnegativity of the finite integer recurrence.

declarations.name: tendsto_finiteCoeffWindow_atTop
declarations.signature: theorem tendsto_finiteCoeffWindow_atTop (P : Set ℕ) (n : ℕ) : Tendsto (fun J : ℕ ↦ finiteCoeffWindow P n J) atTop (nhds (binaryCoeffTail (supportCoeff P) n))
declarations.docstring: Finite coefficient windows converge to the complete normalized coefficient tail.

declarations.name: exists_greedyHalfFrozenMargin_nonneg_of_excess_neg
declarations.signature: theorem exists_greedyHalfFrozenMargin_nonneg_of_excess_neg (k : ℕ) (hE : halfGreedyNextDyadicExcessNumerator k < 0) : ∃ J : ℕ, 0 ≤ greedyHalfFrozenMargin k J
declarations.docstring: Negative dyadic excess forces an eventual zero crossing of the frozen integer recurrence. This horizon is not yet bounded by the first shell.

declarations.name: exists_greedyHalfFrozenMargin_nonneg_iff_excess_neg
declarations.signature: theorem exists_greedyHalfFrozenMargin_nonneg_iff_excess_neg (k : ℕ) (hk : 0 < k) : (∃ J : ℕ, 0 ≤ greedyHalfFrozenMargin k J) ↔ halfGreedyNextDyadicExcessNumerator k < 0
declarations.docstring: At positive depth, eventual first passage is equivalent to strict dyadic safety. Oddness excludes the zero-excess boundary.

declarations.name: HalfGreedyGovernedFrozenMarginProducer
declarations.docstring: Exact remaining governed producer after all reductions: the first nonnegative state of the frozen recurrence must occur by `k + 1`.

declarations.name: half_mem_mersenneAchievementSet_of_governedFrozenMarginProducer
declarations.docstring: The governed first-passage producer closes exact half-membership.

declarations.name: sqrt_shift_le_sqrt_add_one_add_div
declarations.signature: theorem sqrt_shift_le_sqrt_add_one_add_div (N j : ℕ) : Nat.sqrt (N + j + 1) ≤ Nat.sqrt N + 1 + j / (Nat.sqrt N + 1)
declarations.docstring: A shifted natural square root grows much more slowly than the shift. This form is tuned to the binary tail sum.

declarations.name: binaryCoeffTail_supportCoeff_lt_two_natSqrt_add_three
declarations.signature: theorem binaryCoeffTail_supportCoeff_lt_two_natSqrt_add_three (A : Set ℕ) (N : ℕ) (hN : 4 ≤ N) : binaryCoeffTail (supportCoeff A) N < 2 * (Nat.sqrt N : ℝ) + 3
declarations.docstring: A one-unit sharpening of the generic support-coefficient tail strip. The gain comes from summing the slow square-root growth in blocks of length `sqrt N + 1`, rather than bounding that growth by the row shift itself.

declarations.name: finiteCoeffWindowNumerator_eq_weighted_sum
declarations.signature: theorem finiteCoeffWindowNumerator_eq_weighted_sum (A : Set ℕ) (n J : ℕ) : finiteCoeffWindowNumerator A n J = ∑ j ∈ Finset.range J, 2 ^ (J - 1 - j) * supportCoeff A (n + j + 1)
declarations.docstring: Closed weighted-sum form of the recursive finite-window numerator.

declarations.name: frozenMargin_fullShell_eq_stemTruncatedSum_sub_target
declarations.signature: theorem frozenMargin_fullShell_eq_stemTruncatedSum_sub_target (P : Finset ℕ) (n : ℕ) (hn : 2 ≤ n) (hbelow : ∀ d ∈ P, 2 ≤ d ∧ d < n) : (finiteCoeffWindowNumerator (↑P : Set ℕ) n n : ℤ) - (2 : ℤ) ^ n * mobiusCenteredHalfCarry (↑P : Set ℕ) (n - 1) = (stemTruncatedSum n P : ℤ) - (seamSubsetTarget n : ℤ)
declarations.docstring: At the full first shell `J=n`, the frozen margin is exactly the signed integer-seam subset-sum defect.

declarations.name: halfGreedyPrefixSupport_pred_below
declarations.signature: theorem halfGreedyPrefixSupport_pred_below (n : ℕ) (hn : 2 ≤ n) : ∀ d ∈ halfGreedyPrefixSupport (n - 1), 2 ≤ d ∧ d < n
declarations.docstring: The rational greedy prefix before rank `n` is supported exactly on the proper exponent range needed by the seam machinery.

declarations.name: greedyHalfFrozenMargin_fullShell_eq_stemTruncatedSum_sub_target
declarations.signature: theorem greedyHalfFrozenMargin_fullShell_eq_stemTruncatedSum_sub_target (n : ℕ) (hn : 2 ≤ n) : greedyHalfFrozenMargin (n - 1) n = (stemTruncatedSum n (halfGreedyPrefixSupport (n - 1)) : ℤ) - (seamSubsetTarget n : ℤ)
declarations.docstring: Concrete greedy specialization of the full-shell integer identity.

declarations.name: greedyHalfFrozenMargin_fullShell_cast_eq_slack_sub_tail
declarations.signature: theorem greedyHalfFrozenMargin_fullShell_cast_eq_slack_sub_tail (k : ℕ) : (greedyHalfFrozenMargin k (k + 1) : ℝ) = (2 : ℝ) ^ (k + 1) * (1 - (2 : ℝ) ^ (k + 1) * greedyMersenneRemainder (1 / 2 : ℝ) k) - binaryCoeffTail (supportCoeff (↑(halfGreedyPrefixSupport k) : Set ℕ)) ((k + 1) + (k + 1))
declarations.docstring: Analytic form of the same full-shell margin: amplified dyadic safety slack minus the coefficient tail still pending after row `2(k+1)`.

declarations.name: greedyHalf_skip_amplified_dyadic_overshoot_lt_two
declarations.signature: theorem greedyHalf_skip_amplified_dyadic_overshoot_lt_two (n : ℕ) (hn : 2 ≤ n) (hskip : ¬ mersenneWeight n ≤ greedyMersenneRemainder (1 / 2 : ℝ) (n - 1)) : (2 : ℝ) ^ n * ((2 : ℝ) ^ n * greedyMersenneRemainder (1 / 2 : ℝ) (n - 1) - 1) < 2
declarations.docstring: A skipped rank can exceed its dyadic boundary by less than two after the full-shell amplification. This is the only use of the skip inequality in the seam adapter.

declarations.name: neg_greedyHalfFrozenMargin_fullShell_le_halfStripBound
declarations.signature: theorem neg_greedyHalfFrozenMargin_fullShell_le_halfStripBound (n : ℕ) (hn : 3 ≤ n) (hskip : ¬ mersenneWeight n ≤ greedyMersenneRemainder (1 / 2 : ℝ) (n - 1)) (hneg : greedyHalfFrozenMargin (n - 1) n < 0) : -greedyHalfFrozenMargin (n - 1) n ≤ (halfStripBound (2 * n) : ℤ)
declarations.docstring: If the full-shell margin is still negative at an actual skip, its integer seam defect lies inside the existing square-root strip.

declarations.name: neg_greedyHalfFrozenMargin_fullShell_implies_seamRemainder_small
declarations.signature: theorem neg_greedyHalfFrozenMargin_fullShell_implies_seamRemainder_small (n : ℕ) (hn : 3 ≤ n) (hskip : ¬ mersenneWeight n ≤ greedyMersenneRemainder (1 / 2 : ℝ) (n - 1)) (hneg : greedyHalfFrozenMargin (n - 1) n < 0) : 1 ≤ seamIntegerGreedyRemainder n ∧ seamIntegerGreedyRemainder n ≤ halfStripBound (2 * n)
declarations.docstring: Exact adapter requested by the seam lane: a negative full-shell frozen margin at an actual skipped rank produces the right-hand small integer-greedy remainder statement of `seam_smallPositiveDefect_iff_integerGreedyRemainder`.

declarations.name: smallPositiveDefect_bits_eq_integerGreedyBits
declarations.signature: theorem smallPositiveDefect_bits_eq_integerGreedyBits {gap B C : ℕ} (hgap : 0 < gap) (hB : B < gap) {weights : List ℕ} (hdom : GapDominates gap weights) {bits : List Bool} (hlen : bits.length = weights.length) (hlt : weightedBoolSum weights bits < C) (hdef : C - weightedBoolSum weights bits ≤ B) : bits = integerGreedyBits weights C
declarations.docstring: Any Boolean word giving a positive defect shorter than the separation gap is the descending greedy word itself.

declarations.name: greedyHalfFrozenMargin_fullShell_neg_of_take
declarations.signature: theorem greedyHalfFrozenMargin_fullShell_neg_of_take (n : ℕ) (hn : 1 ≤ n) (htake : mersenneWeight n ≤ greedyMersenneRemainder (1 / 2 : ℝ) (n - 1)) : greedyHalfFrozenMargin (n - 1) n < 0
declarations.docstring: Taking rank `n` forces the actual full-shell frozen margin to be strictly negative. This is the easy half of the observed sign dichotomy.

declarations.name: skip_of_fullShell_margin_nonneg
declarations.signature: theorem skip_of_fullShell_margin_nonneg (n : ℕ) (hn : 1 ≤ n) (hmargin : 0 ≤ greedyHalfFrozenMargin (n - 1) n) : ¬ mersenneWeight n ≤ greedyMersenneRemainder (1 / 2 : ℝ) (n - 1)
declarations.docstring: Therefore nonnegativity of the full-shell margin forces an actual skip. The converse is the exact remaining arithmetic direction.

declarations.name: stemBits_eq_integerGreedyBits_of_skipped_fullShell_neg
declarations.signature: theorem stemBits_eq_integerGreedyBits_of_skipped_fullShell_neg (n : ℕ) (hn : 3 ≤ n) (hskip : ¬ mersenneWeight n ≤ greedyMersenneRemainder (1 / 2 : ℝ) (n - 1)) (hneg : greedyHalfFrozenMargin (n - 1) n < 0) : stemBits n (halfGreedyPrefixSupport (n - 1)) = integerGreedyBits (seamWeights n) (seamSubsetTarget n)
declarations.docstring: A hypothetical negative full-shell margin at an actual skip aligns the actual finite Mersenne prefix with the integer-seam greedy word exactly.

declarations.name: greedyHalfFrozenMargin_fullShell_eq_neg_seamRemainder_of_alignment
declarations.signature: theorem greedyHalfFrozenMargin_fullShell_eq_neg_seamRemainder_of_alignment (n : ℕ) (hn : 3 ≤ n) (halign : stemBits n (halfGreedyPrefixSupport (n - 1)) = integerGreedyBits (seamWeights n) (seamSubsetTarget n)) : greedyHalfFrozenMargin (n - 1) n = -(seamIntegerGreedyRemainder n : ℤ)
declarations.docstring: Once the actual stem is the descending seam-greedy word, the full-shell margin is exactly the negative integer-greedy seam remainder.

declarations.name: skipped_fullShell_neg_iff_alignment_and_seamRemainder_pos
declarations.signature: theorem skipped_fullShell_neg_iff_alignment_and_seamRemainder_pos (n : ℕ) (hn : 3 ≤ n) (hskip : ¬ mersenneWeight n ≤ greedyMersenneRemainder (1 / 2 : ℝ) (n - 1)) : greedyHalfFrozenMargin (n - 1) n < 0 ↔ stemBits n (halfGreedyPrefixSupport (n - 1)) = integerGreedyBits (seamWeights n) (seamSubsetTarget n) ∧ 1 ≤ seamIntegerGreedyRemainder n
declarations.docstring: At a genuine skip, negativity of the full-shell margin is equivalent to positive seam remainder together with exact actual/seam word alignment.

declarations.name: HalfGreedySkippedSeamAlignmentZero
declarations.docstring: Exact word-level replacement for the skipped full-shell sign invariant: if a skipped actual word equals the seam-greedy word, its seam remainder must vanish.

declarations.name: HalfGreedySkippedFullShellNonnegative
declarations.docstring: Direct full-shell sign socket. It asks only that the actual frozen margin has already crossed by the full-shell horizon at every genuine skipped rank; no seam or abstract adjacent-cut coordinate remains in the hypothesis.

declarations.name: governedFrozenMarginProducer_of_skippedFullShellNonnegative
declarations.docstring: The full-shell sign socket is itself a governed frozen-margin producer, with the two initial ranks discharged directly.

declarations.name: half_mem_mersenneAchievementSet_of_skippedFullShellNonnegative
declarations.docstring: Final direct consumer for the actual full-shell sign invariant.

declarations.name: skippedFullShellNonnegative_of_seamAlignmentZero
declarations.docstring: The exact word-level condition implies the skipped full-shell sign invariant.

declarations.name: seamAlignmentZero_of_skippedFullShellNonnegative
declarations.docstring: Conversely, nonnegative skipped margins force zero seam remainder at every aligned skipped word.

declarations.name: skippedSeamAlignmentZero_iff_skippedFullShellNonnegative
declarations.docstring: The word-alignment-zero condition and the direct sign socket are exactly equivalent.

declarations.name: half_mem_mersenneAchievementSet_of_skippedSeamAlignmentZero
declarations.docstring: Final exact half-membership consumer in word-level seam coordinates.

declarations.name: HalfGreedySkippedSeamEscape
declarations.docstring: Smallest skip-conditioned seam escape socket needed by the actual half orbit. Exceptional seam rows that are actual takes are deliberately absent.

declarations.name: governedFrozenMarginProducer_of_skippedSeamEscape
declarations.docstring: The skip-conditioned seam escape socket forces a governed crossing at the full-shell horizon `J=k+1`.

declarations.name: half_mem_mersenneAchievementSet_of_skippedSeamEscape
declarations.docstring: Final conditional consumer: the skip-conditioned seam escape statement is sufficient for exact half-membership.

declarations.name: truncatedMersenneWeight
declarations.docstring: The exact integer weight contributed at seam rank `s` by selecting a proper divisor rank `d < s`.

declarations.name: truncatedMersenneWeight_eq_geometricCore
declarations.signature: theorem truncatedMersenneWeight_eq_geometricCore {s d : ℕ} (hd : 2 ≤ d) : truncatedMersenneWeight s d = 2 ^ ((2 * s) % d) * ∑ i ∈ Finset.range ((2 * s) / d), (2 ^ d) ^ i
declarations.docstring: Quotient form, with the Euclidean remainder made explicit.

declarations.name: truncatedMersenneWeight_eq_descendingMultiples
declarations.signature: theorem truncatedMersenneWeight_eq_descendingMultiples {s d : ℕ} (hd : 2 ≤ d) : truncatedMersenneWeight s d = ∑ i ∈ Finset.range ((2 * s) / d), 2 ^ (2 * s - d * (i + 1))
declarations.docstring: Descending-multiple form used by the carry-incidence swap.

declarations.name: descendingMultiples_eq_divisorIndicatorSum
declarations.signature: theorem descendingMultiples_eq_divisorIndicatorSum {s d : ℕ} (hd : 2 ≤ d) : (∑ i ∈ Finset.range ((2 * s) / d), 2 ^ (2 * s - d * (i + 1))) = ∑ j ∈ Finset.range (2 * s - 1), if d ∈ (j + 2).divisors then 2 ^ (2 * s - 2 - j) else 0
declarations.docstring: Reindex descending multiples as precisely the rows divisible by `d`.

declarations.name: truncatedMersenneWeight_eq_divisorIndicatorSum
declarations.signature: theorem truncatedMersenneWeight_eq_divisorIndicatorSum {s d : ℕ} (hd : 2 ≤ d) : truncatedMersenneWeight s d = ∑ j ∈ Finset.range (2 * s - 1), if d ∈ (j + 2).divisors then 2 ^ (2 * s - 2 - j) else 0

declarations.name: seamSubsetTarget
declarations.docstring: The binary-boundary target before any selected divisor weights are removed.

declarations.name: seamWeightsFrom
declarations.signature: def seamWeightsFrom (s : ℕ) : ℕ → List ℕ | d => if h : d < s then truncatedMersenneWeight s d :: seamWeightsFrom s (d + 1) else [] termination_by d => s - d decreasing_by omega /-- The actual proper-divisor weight word, indexed
declarations.docstring: The weights with indices `d,d+1,…,s-1`, in descending size order.

declarations.name: seamWeights
declarations.docstring: The actual proper-divisor weight word, indexed by `2,…,s-1`.

declarations.name: seamWeightsFrom_eq_nil
declarations.signature: theorem seamWeightsFrom_eq_nil {s d : ℕ} (h : s ≤ d) : seamWeightsFrom s d = []

declarations.name: seamWeightsFrom_eq_cons
declarations.signature: theorem seamWeightsFrom_eq_cons {s d : ℕ} (h : d < s) : seamWeightsFrom s d = truncatedMersenneWeight s d :: seamWeightsFrom s (d + 1)

declarations.name: truncatedMersenneWeight_cast_le_scaled
declarations.signature: theorem truncatedMersenneWeight_cast_le_scaled {s d : ℕ} (hd : 1 ≤ d) : ((truncatedMersenneWeight s d : ℕ) : ℝ) ≤ (4 : ℝ) ^ s * mersenneWeight d
declarations.docstring: The actual proper-divisor weight word, indexed by `2,…,s-1`. -/ def seamWeights (s : ℕ) : List ℕ := seamWeightsFrom s 2 theorem seamWeightsFrom_eq_nil {s d : ℕ} (h : s ≤ d) : seamWeightsFrom s d = [] := by rw [seamWeightsFrom] simp [Nat.not_lt.mpr h] theorem seamWeightsFrom_eq_cons {s d : ℕ} (h : d < s) : seamWeightsFrom s d = truncatedMersenneWeight s d :: seamWeightsFrom s (d + 1) := by rw [seamWeightsFrom] simp [h] /-! ## Floor enclosures and the concrete dominance gap

declarations.name: scaled_lt_truncatedMersenneWeight_cast_add_one
declarations.signature: theorem scaled_lt_truncatedMersenneWeight_cast_add_one {s d : ℕ} (hd : 1 ≤ d) : (4 : ℝ) ^ s * mersenneWeight d < (((truncatedMersenneWeight s d + 1 : ℕ) : ℕ) : ℝ)

declarations.name: four_pow_mul_half_pow_pred
declarations.signature: theorem four_pow_mul_half_pow_pred {s : ℕ} (hs : 1 ≤ s) : (4 : ℝ) ^ s * ((1 : ℝ) / 2) ^ (s - 1) = (2 : ℝ) ^ (s + 1)

declarations.name: seamWeightsFrom_cast_sum_add_tail_le
declarations.signature: theorem seamWeightsFrom_cast_sum_add_tail_le (s d : ℕ) (hd : 1 ≤ d) (hds : d ≤ s) : (((seamWeightsFrom s d).sum : ℕ) : ℝ) + (4 : ℝ) ^ s * mersenneTail (s - 1) ≤ (4 : ℝ) ^ s * mersenneTail (d - 1)
declarations.docstring: The recursively listed floor weights are bounded by the matching scaled finite Mersenne tail. The infinite tail at `s-1` is retained explicitly; this is what later supplies the quantitative `2^(s+1)` gap.

declarations.name: truncatedMersenneWeight_dominanceGap
declarations.signature: theorem truncatedMersenneWeight_dominanceGap {s d : ℕ} (hs : 2 ≤ s) (hd : 1 ≤ d) (hds : d < s) : 2 ^ (s + 1) + (seamWeightsFrom s (d + 1)).sum ≤ truncatedMersenneWeight s d
declarations.docstring: Quantitative strict superincreasingness of the actual truncated weights. The entire later integer tail plus `2^(s+1)` still fits under the current weight.

declarations.name: GapDominates
declarations.signature: def GapDominates (gap : ℕ) : List ℕ → Prop | [] => True | w :: ws => gap + ws.sum ≤ w ∧ GapDominates gap ws theorem seamWeightsFrom_gapDominates {s d : ℕ} (hs : 2 ≤ s) (hd : 1 ≤ d) : GapDominates (2 ^ (s + 1)) (seamWeightsFrom s d)
declarations.docstring: Every head exceeds the sum of its complete tail by at least `gap`.

declarations.name: seamWeightsFrom_gapDominates
declarations.signature: theorem seamWeightsFrom_gapDominates {s d : ℕ} (hs : 2 ≤ s) (hd : 1 ≤ d) : GapDominates (2 ^ (s + 1)) (seamWeightsFrom s d)

declarations.name: seamWeights_gapDominates
declarations.signature: theorem seamWeights_gapDominates {s : ℕ} (hs : 2 ≤ s) : GapDominates (2 ^ (s + 1)) (seamWeights s)

declarations.name: weightedBoolSum
declarations.signature: def weightedBoolSum : List ℕ → List Bool → ℕ | w :: ws, true :: bs => w + weightedBoolSum ws bs | _ :: ws, false :: bs => weightedBoolSum ws bs | _, _ => 0 theorem weightedBoolSum_le_sum (weights : List ℕ) (bits : List Bool) : weightedBoolSum weights bits ≤ weights.sum
declarations.docstring: Weighted sum of a Boolean word. The equal-length hypotheses below make the two fallback equations irrelevant.

declarations.name: weightedBoolSum_le_sum
declarations.signature: theorem weightedBoolSum_le_sum (weights : List ℕ) (bits : List Bool) : weightedBoolSum weights bits ≤ weights.sum

declarations.name: weightedBoolSum_separated
declarations.signature: theorem weightedBoolSum_separated {gap : ℕ} {weights : List ℕ} (hgap : 0 < gap) (hdom : GapDominates gap weights) {a b : List Bool} (ha : a.length = weights.length) (hb : b.length = weights.length) (hne : a ≠ b) : weightedBoolSum weights a + gap ≤ weightedBoolSum weights b ∨ weightedBoolSum weights b + gap ≤ weightedBoolSum weights a
declarations.docstring: Distinct Boolean subset sums of a gap-dominating word differ by at least the gap.

declarations.name: integerGreedyBits
declarations.signature: def integerGreedyBits : List ℕ → ℕ → List Bool | [], _ => [] | w :: ws, C => if w ≤ C then true :: integerGreedyBits ws (C - w) else false :: integerGreedyBits ws C def integerGreedyRemainder (weights : List ℕ) (C : ℕ) : ℕ
declarations.docstring: Descending greedy subset for an integer capacity.

declarations.name: integerGreedyRemainder
declarations.signature: def integerGreedyRemainder (weights : List ℕ) (C : ℕ) : ℕ

declarations.name: integerGreedyBits_length
declarations.signature: theorem integerGreedyBits_length (weights : List ℕ) (C : ℕ) : (integerGreedyBits weights C).length = weights.length

declarations.name: integerGreedyBits_admissible
declarations.signature: theorem integerGreedyBits_admissible (weights : List ℕ) (C : ℕ) : weightedBoolSum weights (integerGreedyBits weights C) ≤ C

declarations.name: integerGreedyBits_maximal
declarations.signature: theorem integerGreedyBits_maximal {gap : ℕ} (hgap : 0 < gap) {weights : List ℕ} (hdom : GapDominates gap weights) {C : ℕ} {bits : List Bool} (hlen : bits.length = weights.length) (hadm : weightedBoolSum weights bits ≤ C) : weightedBoolSum weights bits ≤ weightedBoolSum weights (integerGreedyBits weights C)
declarations.docstring: For a strictly gap-dominating word, descending greedy is the maximal subset sum not exceeding `C`.

declarations.name: exists_smallPositiveDefect_iff_integerGreedyRemainder
declarations.signature: theorem exists_smallPositiveDefect_iff_integerGreedyRemainder {gap B C : ℕ} (hgap : 0 < gap) (hB : B < gap) {weights : List ℕ} (hdom : GapDominates gap weights) : (∃ bits : List Bool, bits.length = weights.length ∧ weightedBoolSum weights bits < C ∧ C - weightedBoolSum weights bits ≤ B) ↔ 1 ≤ integerGreedyRemainder weights C ∧ integerGreedyRemainder weights C ≤ B
declarations.docstring: Exact abstract reduction. If the subset sums are separated by a gap strictly larger than the target window, then a positive defect in that window exists iff the descending greedy remainder is itself positive and in the window.

declarations.name: halfStripBound_lt_truncatedWeightGap
declarations.signature: theorem halfStripBound_lt_truncatedWeightGap {s : ℕ} (hs : 3 ≤ s) : halfStripBound (2 * s) < 2 ^ (s + 1)

declarations.name: seam_smallPositiveDefect_iff_integerGreedyRemainder
declarations.signature: theorem seam_smallPositiveDefect_iff_integerGreedyRemainder {s : ℕ} (hs : 3 ≤ s) : (∃ bits : List Bool, bits.length = (seamWeights s).length ∧ weightedBoolSum (seamWeights s) bits < seamSubsetTarget s ∧ seamSubsetTarget s - weightedBoolSum (seamWeights s) bits ≤ halfStripBound (2 * s)) ↔ 1 ≤ seamIntegerGreedyRemainder s ∧ seamIntegerGreedyRemainder s ≤ halfStripBound (2 * s)
declarations.docstring: Universal first-wrap reduction for every `s ≥ 3`: arbitrary Boolean divisor incidence has a seam defect in the carry strip exactly when the one descending integer-greedy residual does.

declarations.name: integerHalfCarry_eq_pow_sub_sum
declarations.signature: theorem integerHalfCarry_eq_pow_sub_sum (A : Set ℕ) (N : ℕ) : integerHalfCarry A N = (2 : ℤ) ^ N - ∑ j ∈ Finset.range N, (2 : ℤ) ^ (N - 1 - j) * (supportCoeff A (j + 2) : ℤ)
declarations.docstring: Closed form of the affine half-carry.

declarations.name: stemBitsFrom
declarations.signature: def stemBitsFrom (s : ℕ) (P : Finset ℕ) : ℕ → List Bool | d => if h : d < s then decide (d ∈ P) :: stemBitsFrom s P (d + 1) else [] termination_by d => s - d decreasing_by omega def stemBits (s : ℕ) (P : Finset ℕ) : List Bool
declarations.docstring: Boolean incidence word aligned with `seamWeightsFrom`.

declarations.name: stemBits
declarations.signature: def stemBits (s : ℕ) (P : Finset ℕ) : List Bool

declarations.name: stemBitsFrom_eq_nil
declarations.signature: theorem stemBitsFrom_eq_nil {s d : ℕ} {P : Finset ℕ} (h : s ≤ d) : stemBitsFrom s P d = []

declarations.name: stemBitsFrom_eq_cons
declarations.signature: theorem stemBitsFrom_eq_cons {s d : ℕ} {P : Finset ℕ} (h : d < s) : stemBitsFrom s P d = decide (d ∈ P) :: stemBitsFrom s P (d + 1)

declarations.name: stemBitsFrom_length_eq_seamWeightsFrom_length
declarations.signature: theorem stemBitsFrom_length_eq_seamWeightsFrom_length (s d : ℕ) (P : Finset ℕ) : (stemBitsFrom s P d).length = (seamWeightsFrom s d).length

declarations.name: stemBits_length_eq_seamWeights_length
declarations.signature: theorem stemBits_length_eq_seamWeights_length (s : ℕ) (P : Finset ℕ) : (stemBits s P).length = (seamWeights s).length

declarations.name: stemTruncatedSumFrom
declarations.signature: def stemTruncatedSumFrom (s : ℕ) (P : Finset ℕ) : ℕ → ℕ | d => if h : d < s then (if d ∈ P then truncatedMersenneWeight s d else 0) + stemTruncatedSumFrom s P (d + 1) else 0 termination_by d => s - d decreasing_by omega def stemTruncatedSum (s : ℕ) (P : Finset ℕ) : ℕ
declarations.docstring: Recursive form of the selected truncated-weight sum.

declarations.name: stemTruncatedSum
declarations.signature: def stemTruncatedSum (s : ℕ) (P : Finset ℕ) : ℕ

declarations.name: stemTruncatedSumFrom_eq_Ico
declarations.signature: theorem stemTruncatedSumFrom_eq_Ico (s d : ℕ) (P : Finset ℕ) : stemTruncatedSumFrom s P d = ∑ e ∈ Finset.Ico d s, if e ∈ P then truncatedMersenneWeight s e else 0

declarations.name: stemTruncatedSum_eq_sum_support
declarations.signature: theorem stemTruncatedSum_eq_sum_support {s : ℕ} {P : Finset ℕ} (hbelow : ∀ d ∈ P, 2 ≤ d ∧ d < s) : stemTruncatedSum s P = ∑ d ∈ P, truncatedMersenneWeight s d

declarations.name: weightedBoolSum_stemBitsFrom
declarations.signature: theorem weightedBoolSum_stemBitsFrom (s d : ℕ) (P : Finset ℕ) : weightedBoolSum (seamWeightsFrom s d) (stemBitsFrom s P d) = stemTruncatedSumFrom s P d

declarations.name: weightedBoolSum_stemBits
declarations.signature: theorem weightedBoolSum_stemBits (s : ℕ) (P : Finset ℕ) : weightedBoolSum (seamWeights s) (stemBits s P) = stemTruncatedSum s P

declarations.name: StemIncidenceSwap
declarations.signature: def StemIncidenceSwap (s : ℕ) (P : Finset ℕ) : Prop
declarations.docstring: The finite divisor/multiple reindexing needed to connect the affine carry expansion to the truncated-weight word. Keeping it as a named socket makes the remaining semantic dependency exact: it contains neither the inserted seam rank nor the terminal row twice.

declarations.name: supportCoeff_finset_eq_indicatorSum
declarations.signature: theorem supportCoeff_finset_eq_indicatorSum (P : Finset ℕ) (n : ℕ) : supportCoeff (↑P : Set ℕ) n = ∑ d ∈ P, if d ∈ n.divisors then 1 else 0

declarations.name: supportIncidenceTotal_eq_sum_weights
declarations.signature: theorem supportIncidenceTotal_eq_sum_weights {s : ℕ} {P : Finset ℕ} (hbelow : ∀ d ∈ P, 2 ≤ d ∧ d < s) : (∑ j ∈ Finset.range (2 * s - 1), 2 ^ (2 * s - 2 - j) * supportCoeff (↑P : Set ℕ) (j + 2)) = ∑ d ∈ P, truncatedMersenneWeight s d

declarations.name: stemIncidenceSwap
declarations.signature: theorem stemIncidenceSwap {s : ℕ} {P : Finset ℕ} (hbelow : ∀ d ∈ P, 2 ≤ d ∧ d < s) : StemIncidenceSwap s P
declarations.docstring: The finite divisor/multiple swap is unconditional for a Boolean stem supported on the proper ranks `2,…,s-1`.

declarations.name: supportCoeff_insert_eq_add_indicator
declarations.signature: theorem supportCoeff_insert_eq_add_indicator (A : Set ℕ) {d n : ℕ} (hdA : d ∉ A) : supportCoeff (insert d A) n = supportCoeff A n + if d ∈ n.divisors then 1 else 0
declarations.docstring: Inserting an absent support rank changes a Boolean divisor coefficient by exactly the indicator that the inserted rank divides the row.

declarations.name: actualSeamHole_eq_target_sub_stemTruncatedSum
declarations.signature: theorem actualSeamHole_eq_target_sub_stemTruncatedSum (s : ℕ) (P : Finset ℕ) (hs : 2 ≤ s) (hsP : s ∉ P) (hbelow : ∀ d ∈ P, 2 ≤ d ∧ d < s) (hswap : StemIncidenceSwap s P) : 2 * integerHalfCarry (insert s (↑P : Set ℕ)) (2 * s - 2) - (supportCoeff (↑P : Set ℕ) (2 * s) : ℤ) = (seamSubsetTarget s : ℤ) - (stemTruncatedSum s P : ℤ)
declarations.docstring: Once the finite incidence swap is supplied, the actual first-wrap seam hole is exactly the abstract subset-sum defect classified above.

declarations.name: actualSeamHole_eq_target_sub_stemTruncatedSum_unconditional
declarations.signature: theorem actualSeamHole_eq_target_sub_stemTruncatedSum_unconditional (s : ℕ) (P : Finset ℕ) (hs : 2 ≤ s) (hsP : s ∉ P) (hbelow : ∀ d ∈ P, 2 ≤ d ∧ d < s) : 2 * integerHalfCarry (insert s (↑P : Set ℕ)) (2 * s - 2) - (supportCoeff (↑P : Set ℕ) (2 * s) : ℤ) = (seamSubsetTarget s : ℤ) - (stemTruncatedSum s P : ℤ)
declarations.docstring: Fully discharged semantic socket: every Boolean proper-rank stem has its actual first-wrap seam hole equal to the abstract integer subset-sum defect.

declarations.name: rowPulse
declarations.docstring: The exact quotient digit created when the row is multiplied by four.

declarations.name: truncatedMersenneWeight_succ
declarations.signature: theorem truncatedMersenneWeight_succ {s d : ℕ} (hs : 2 ≤ s) (hd : 2 ≤ d) : truncatedMersenneWeight (s + 1) d = 4 * truncatedMersenneWeight s d + rowPulse s d
declarations.docstring: Exact row recurrence for every old truncated Mersenne weight.

declarations.name: seamSubsetTarget_succ
declarations.signature: theorem seamSubsetTarget_succ {s : ℕ} (hs : 2 ≤ s) : seamSubsetTarget (s + 1) = 4 * seamSubsetTarget s + 2 ^ (s + 1)
declarations.docstring: The seam target itself has an exact affine row recurrence.

declarations.name: truncatedMersenneWeight_newRank
declarations.signature: theorem truncatedMersenneWeight_newRank {s : ℕ} (hs : 3 ≤ s) : truncatedMersenneWeight (s + 1) s = 2 ^ (s + 2) + 4
declarations.docstring: The new terminal weight has a closed form once `s ≥ 3`.

declarations.name: extendWord
declarations.signature: def extendWord (s : ℕ) (beta : Bool) (b : ℕ → Bool) : ℕ → Bool
declarations.docstring: A Boolean word with its new rank overwritten by `beta`.

declarations.name: wordWeightSum
declarations.signature: def wordWeightSum (s : ℕ) (b : ℕ → Bool) : ℕ
declarations.docstring: The selected weight sum of a Boolean word on ranks `2,...,s-1`.

declarations.name: wordPulse
declarations.signature: def wordPulse (s : ℕ) (b : ℕ → Bool) : ℕ
declarations.docstring: The accumulated quotient-digit pulse of a Boolean word.

declarations.name: wordDefect
declarations.signature: def wordDefect (s : ℕ) (b : ℕ → Bool) : ℤ
declarations.docstring: Signed subset-sum defect of an arbitrary Boolean row word.

declarations.name: wordWeightSum_succ
declarations.signature: theorem wordWeightSum_succ {s : ℕ} (hs : 3 ≤ s) (b : ℕ → Bool) (beta : Bool) : wordWeightSum (s + 1) (extendWord s beta b) = 4 * wordWeightSum s b + wordPulse s b + if beta then 2 ^ (s + 2) + 4 else 0

declarations.name: wordDefect_succ
declarations.signature: theorem wordDefect_succ {s : ℕ} (hs : 3 ≤ s) (b : ℕ → Bool) (beta : Bool) : wordDefect (s + 1) (extendWord s beta b) = 4 * wordDefect s b + 2 ^ (s + 1) - wordPulse s b - (if beta then 2 ^ (s + 2) + 4 else 0)
declarations.docstring: Carry-safe affine extension formula for every Boolean word.

declarations.name: rowPulse_le_two
declarations.signature: theorem rowPulse_le_two (s d : ℕ) (hd : 2 ≤ d) : rowPulse s d ≤ 2
declarations.docstring: Carry-safe affine extension formula for every Boolean word. -/ theorem wordDefect_succ {s : ℕ} (hs : 3 ≤ s) (b : ℕ → Bool) (beta : Bool) : wordDefect (s + 1) (extendWord s beta b) = 4 * wordDefect s b + 2 ^ (s + 1) - wordPulse s b - (if beta then 2 ^ (s + 2) + 4 else 0) := by unfold wordDefect rw [wordWeightSum_succ hs b beta, seamSubsetTarget_succ (by omega)] push_cast ring /-! ## Carry-safe one-successor and quarter-band reduction

declarations.name: wordPulse_le
declarations.signature: theorem wordPulse_le (s : ℕ) (b : ℕ → Bool) : wordPulse s b ≤ 2 * (s - 2)

declarations.name: oldRank_order_preserved
declarations.signature: theorem oldRank_order_preserved {s : ℕ} (hs : 3 ≤ s) {b c : ℕ → Bool} (hsep : wordWeightSum s b + 2 ^ (s + 1) ≤ wordWeightSum s c) : wordWeightSum (s + 1) (extendWord s false b) < wordWeightSum (s + 1) (extendWord s false c)
declarations.docstring: Old-word order survives the row perturbation whenever the landed superincreasing gap separates the two old sums.

declarations.name: PerturbedFamily
declarations.docstring: Old-word order survives the row perturbation whenever the landed superincreasing gap separates the two old sums. -/ theorem oldRank_order_preserved {s : ℕ} (hs : 3 ≤ s) {b c : ℕ → Bool} (hsep : wordWeightSum s b + 2 ^ (s + 1) ≤ wordWeightSum s c) : wordWeightSum (s + 1) (extendWord s false b) < wordWeightSum (s + 1) (extendWord s false c) := by rw [wordWeightSum_succ hs b false, wordWeightSum_succ hs c false] simp only [Bool.false_eq_true, ↓reduceIte, Nat.add_zero] have hb := wordPulse_le s b have hc : 0 ≤ wordPulse s c := Nat.zero_le _ have hsmall : 2 * (s - 2) < 4 * 2 ^ (s + 1) := by have hlin : 2 * s + 4 < 2 ^ (s + 1) := two_mul_add_four_lt_two_pow_succ hs omega omega /-! ### Abstract adjacent-cut packet

declarations.name: newSum_strictMono
declarations.signature: theorem newSum_strictMono {x y : α} (hxy : F.oldSum x < F.oldSum y) : F.newSum x < F.newSum y

declarations.name: newSum_mono
declarations.signature: theorem newSum_mono {x y : α} (hxy : F.oldSum x ≤ F.oldSum y) : F.newSum x ≤ F.newSum y

declarations.name: old_below_add_remainder
declarations.signature: theorem old_below_add_remainder : F.oldSum K.below + K.remainder = C

declarations.name: capacity_add_overshoot
declarations.signature: theorem capacity_add_overshoot : C + K.overshoot = F.oldSum K.above

declarations.name: below_newSum_le_capacity
declarations.signature: theorem below_newSum_le_capacity (hcap : F.pulseCap < F.gap) : F.newSum K.below ≤ K.newCapacity

declarations.name: above_newSum_le_capacity_iff
declarations.signature: theorem above_newSum_le_capacity_iff : F.newSum K.above ≤ K.newCapacity ↔ K.successorCarries

declarations.name: above_residual_eq
declarations.signature: theorem above_residual_eq : K.newCapacity - F.newSum K.above = F.gap - (4 * K.overshoot + K.abovePulse)

declarations.name: below_residual_eq
declarations.signature: theorem below_residual_eq : K.newCapacity - F.newSum K.below = 4 * K.remainder + F.gap - K.belowPulse

declarations.name: prefixChoice_maximal
declarations.signature: theorem prefixChoice_maximal (hcap : F.pulseCap < F.gap) [Decidable K.successorCarries] {x : α} (hx : F.newSum x ≤ K.newCapacity) : F.newSum x ≤ F.newSum K.prefixChoice
declarations.docstring: The updated old-rank prefix is the old greedy word or its immediate successor. No later word can remain admissible.

declarations.name: prefixRemainder_eq_capacity_sub_choice
declarations.signature: theorem prefixRemainder_eq_capacity_sub_choice [Decidable K.successorCarries] : K.prefixRemainder = K.newCapacity - F.newSum K.prefixChoice

declarations.name: carry_prefix_lt_terminal
declarations.signature: theorem carry_prefix_lt_terminal [Decidable K.successorCarries] (hcarry : K.successorCarries) : K.prefixRemainder < K.terminalWeight

declarations.name: nextRemainder_trichotomy
declarations.signature: theorem nextRemainder_trichotomy [Decidable K.successorCarries] : K.nextRemainder = if K.successorCarries then F.gap - (4 * K.overshoot + K.abovePulse) else if 4 * K.remainder + F.gap - K.belowPulse < K.terminalWeight then 4 * K.remainder + F.gap - K.belowPulse else 4 * K.remainder - F.gap - K.belowPulse - 4
declarations.docstring: Exact carry-safe three-branch recurrence.

declarations.name: QuarterBandEscapeAt
declarations.docstring: Exact carry-safe three-branch recurrence. -/ theorem nextRemainder_trichotomy [Decidable K.successorCarries] : K.nextRemainder = if K.successorCarries then F.gap - (4 * K.overshoot + K.abovePulse) else if 4 * K.remainder + F.gap - K.belowPulse < K.terminalWeight then 4 * K.remainder + F.gap - K.belowPulse else 4 * K.remainder - F.gap - K.belowPulse - 4 := by by_cases hcarry : K.successorCarries · have hlt := K.carry_prefix_lt_terminal hcarry have hlt' : F.gap - (4 * K.overshoot + K.abovePulse) < K.terminalWeight := by simpa [prefixRemainder, hcarry] using hlt have hnle : ¬ K.terminalWeight ≤ F.gap - (4 * K.overshoot + K.abovePulse) := Nat.not_le_of_lt hlt' simp [nextRemainder, prefixRemainder, hcarry, hnle] · by_cases hterminal : 4 * K.remainder + F.gap - K.belowPulse < K.terminalWeight · have hnle : ¬ K.terminalWeight ≤ 4 * K.remainder + F.gap - K.belowPulse := Nat.not_le_of_lt hterminal simp [nextRemainder, prefixRemainder, hcarry, hterminal, hnle] · have hle : K.terminalWeight ≤ 4 *

declarations.name: bound_lt_nextRemainder_of_quarterBandEscape
declarations.signature: theorem bound_lt_nextRemainder_of_quarterBandEscape [Decidable K.successorCarries] {B : ℕ} (hcap : F.pulseCap < F.gap) (hmiddle : B < F.gap - F.pulseCap) (hquarter : K.QuarterBandEscapeAt B) : B < K.nextRemainder
declarations.docstring: Once the two pulse-correlated quarter bands are excluded, every branch of the exact recurrence lies above the target strip.

declarations.name: quarterBandEscape_iff_bound_lt_nextRemainder
declarations.signature: theorem quarterBandEscape_iff_bound_lt_nextRemainder [Decidable K.successorCarries] {B : ℕ} (hcap : F.pulseCap < F.gap) (hmiddle : B < F.gap - F.pulseCap) : K.QuarterBandEscapeAt B ↔ B < K.nextRemainder
declarations.docstring: Under the already-landed middle-branch separation, the two quarter-band clauses are not merely sufficient: they are exactly the assertion that the updated residual escapes the target strip.

declarations.name: stemBitsFrom_eq_ofFn
declarations.signature: theorem stemBitsFrom_eq_ofFn {s d : ℕ} (P : Finset ℕ) (hds : d ≤ s) : stemBitsFrom s P d = List.ofFn (fun i : Fin (s - d) => decide (d + i ∈ P))

declarations.name: stemBits_eq_ofFn
declarations.signature: theorem stemBits_eq_ofFn {s : ℕ} (P : Finset ℕ) (hs : 2 ≤ s) : stemBits s P = List.ofFn (fun i : Fin (s - 2) => decide ((i : ℕ) + 2 ∈ P))

declarations.name: halfActualSeamWord
declarations.docstring: The actual Boolean row word supplied by the rational greedy prefix.

declarations.name: halfActualSeamWord_toList
declarations.signature: @[simp] theorem halfActualSeamWord_toList (s : ℕ) (hs : 2 ≤ s) : (halfActualSeamWord s).toList = stemBits s (halfGreedyPrefixSupport (s - 1))

declarations.name: mem_halfGreedyPrefixSupport_succ_iff_of_lt
declarations.signature: theorem mem_halfGreedyPrefixSupport_succ_iff_of_lt {s d : ℕ} (hd : 0 < d) (hds : d < s) : d ∈ halfGreedyPrefixSupport s ↔ d ∈ halfGreedyPrefixSupport (s - 1)

declarations.name: self_mem_halfGreedyPrefixSupport_iff_take
declarations.signature: theorem self_mem_halfGreedyPrefixSupport_iff_take (s : ℕ) (hs : 1 ≤ s) : s ∈ halfGreedyPrefixSupport s ↔ mersenneWeight s ≤ greedyMersenneRemainder (1 / 2 : ℝ) (s - 1)

declarations.name: halfActualSeamWord_succ
declarations.signature: theorem halfActualSeamWord_succ (s : ℕ) (hs : 3 ≤ s) : halfActualSeamWord (s + 1) = (halfActualSeamWord s).extend (decide (mersenneWeight s ≤ greedyMersenneRemainder (1 / 2 : ℝ) (s - 1)))
declarations.docstring: The actual word evolves by appending precisely the real greedy decision at the terminal coordinate.

declarations.name: seamPerturbedFamily_oldSum_halfActualSeamWord
declarations.signature: theorem seamPerturbedFamily_oldSum_halfActualSeamWord (s : ℕ) (hs : 3 ≤ s) : (seamPerturbedFamily s hs).oldSum (halfActualSeamWord s) = stemTruncatedSum s (halfGreedyPrefixSupport (s - 1))
declarations.docstring: The actual word evolves by appending precisely the real greedy decision at the terminal coordinate. -/ theorem halfActualSeamWord_succ (s : ℕ) (hs : 3 ≤ s) : halfActualSeamWord (s + 1) = (halfActualSeamWord s).extend (decide (mersenneWeight s ≤ greedyMersenneRemainder (1 / 2 : ℝ) (s - 1))) := by funext i by_cases hi : (i : ℕ) < s - 2 · have hdpos : 0 < (i : ℕ) + 2 := by omega have hdlt : (i : ℕ) + 2 < s := by omega have hmem := mem_halfGreedyPrefixSupport_succ_iff_of_lt hdpos hdlt simp only [halfActualSeamWord, SeamRowWord.extend, dif_pos hi] rw [show s + 1 - 1 = s by omega] exact Bool.decide_congr hmem · have hilast : (i : ℕ) = s - 2 := by omega have hself := self_mem_halfGreedyPrefixSupport_iff_take s (by omega) have hindex : (i : ℕ) + 2 = s := by omega simp only [halfActualSeamWord, SeamRowWord.extend, dif_neg hi] rw [hindex, show s + 1 - 1 = s by omega] exact Bool.decide_congr hself /-! ## Full-shell margin in actual-word coordinates

declarations.name: greedyHalfFrozenMargin_fullShell_eq_actualOldSum_sub_target
declarations.signature: theorem greedyHalfFrozenMargin_fullShell_eq_actualOldSum_sub_target (s : ℕ) (hs : 3 ≤ s) : greedyHalfFrozenMargin (s - 1) s = ((seamPerturbedFamily s hs).oldSum (halfActualSeamWord s) : ℤ) - (seamSubsetTarget s : ℤ)

declarations.name: greedyHalfFrozenMargin_fullShell_lt_gap
declarations.signature: theorem greedyHalfFrozenMargin_fullShell_lt_gap (s : ℕ) (hs : 1 ≤ s) : greedyHalfFrozenMargin (s - 1) s < ((2 ^ (s + 1) : ℕ) : ℤ)
declarations.docstring: The full-shell margin is always below one adjacent-word separation gap.

declarations.name: halfActualSeamWord_eq_greedy_of_margin_eq_zero
declarations.signature: theorem halfActualSeamWord_eq_greedy_of_margin_eq_zero (s : ℕ) (hs : 5 ≤ s) (hzero : greedyHalfFrozenMargin (s - 1) s = 0) : halfActualSeamWord s = seamGreedyWord s ∧ seamIntegerGreedyRemainder s = 0
declarations.docstring: The full-shell margin is always below one adjacent-word separation gap. -/ theorem greedyHalfFrozenMargin_fullShell_lt_gap (s : ℕ) (hs : 1 ≤ s) : greedyHalfFrozenMargin (s - 1) s < ((2 ^ (s + 1) : ℕ) : ℤ) := by let r : ℝ := greedyMersenneRemainder (1 / 2 : ℝ) (s - 1) let T : ℝ := binaryCoeffTail (supportCoeff (↑(halfGreedyPrefixSupport (s - 1)) : Set ℕ)) (2 * s) have hid := greedyHalfFrozenMargin_fullShell_cast_eq_slack_sub_tail (s - 1) have hid' : (greedyHalfFrozenMargin (s - 1) s : ℝ) = (2 : ℝ) ^ s * (1 - (2 : ℝ) ^ s * r) - T := by simpa [r, T, Nat.sub_add_cancel hs, two_mul] using hid have hr : 0 ≤ r := by exact greedyMersenneRemainder_nonneg (by norm_num) (s - 1) have hT : 0 ≤ T := binaryCoeffTail_nonneg _ _ have hle : (greedyHalfFrozenMargin (s - 1) s : ℝ) ≤ (2 : ℝ) ^ s := by rw [hid'] have hsquareRem : 0 ≤ ((2 : ℝ) ^ s) ^ 2 * r := mul_nonneg (sq_nonneg _) hr nlinarith have hlt : (2 : ℝ) ^ s < (2 : ℝ) ^ (s + 1) := by rw [pow_succ] nlinarith [show 0 < (2 : ℝ) ^ s by positivity] hav

declarations.name: halfActualSeamWord_eq_above_of_margin_pos
declarations.signature: theorem halfActualSeamWord_eq_above_of_margin_pos (s : ℕ) (hs : 5 ≤ s) (hpos : 0 < greedyHalfFrozenMargin (s - 1) s) : halfActualSeamWord s = seamAboveWord s hs ∧ greedyHalfFrozenMargin (s - 1) s = ((seamAdjacentCut s hs).overshoot : ℤ)

declarations.name: halfGreedy_skipped_endpoint_trichotomy
declarations.signature: theorem halfGreedy_skipped_endpoint_trichotomy (s : ℕ) (hs : 5 ≤ s) (hskip : ¬ mersenneWeight s ≤ greedyMersenneRemainder (1 / 2 : ℝ) (s - 1)) : (greedyHalfFrozenMargin (s - 1) s < 0 ∧ halfActualSeamWord s = seamGreedyWord s ∧ 1 ≤ seamIntegerGreedyRemainder s ∧ greedyHalfFrozenMargin (s - 1) s = -(seamIntegerGreedyRemainder s : ℤ)) ∨ (greedyHalfFrozenMargin (s - 1) s = 0 ∧ halfActualSeamWord s = seamGreedyWord s ∧ seamIntegerGreedyRemainder s = 0) ∨ (0 < greedyHalfFrozenMargin (s - 1) s ∧ halfActualSeamWord s = seamAboveWord s hs ∧
declarations.docstring: A skipped endpoint has exactly three signed cells. The zero seam hit is an allowed cell; only the negative-below cell remains to be excluded.

declarations.name: seamGreedyWord_succ_eq_upperBranch
declarations.signature: theorem seamGreedyWord_succ_eq_upperBranch (s : ℕ) (hs : 5 ≤ s) (hcarry : (seamAdjacentCut s hs).successorCarries) : seamGreedyWord (s + 1) = (seamAdjacentCut s hs).above.extend false

declarations.name: seamGreedyWord_succ_eq_middleBranch
declarations.signature: theorem seamGreedyWord_succ_eq_middleBranch (s : ℕ) (hs : 5 ≤ s) (hcarry : ¬ (seamAdjacentCut s hs).successorCarries) (hmiddle : 4 * (seamAdjacentCut s hs).remainder + (seamPerturbedFamily s (by omega)).gap - (seamAdjacentCut s hs).belowPulse < (seamAdjacentCut s hs).terminalWeight) : seamGreedyWord (s + 1) = (seamAdjacentCut s hs).below.extend false

declarations.name: seamGreedyWord_succ_eq_rightBranch
declarations.signature: theorem seamGreedyWord_succ_eq_rightBranch (s : ℕ) (hs : 5 ≤ s) (hcarry : ¬ (seamAdjacentCut s hs).successorCarries) (hright : (seamAdjacentCut s hs).terminalWeight ≤ 4 * (seamAdjacentCut s hs).remainder + (seamPerturbedFamily s (by omega)).gap - (seamAdjacentCut s hs).belowPulse) : seamGreedyWord (s + 1) = (seamAdjacentCut s hs).below.extend true

declarations.name: alignedSkip_next_alignment_iff_middle
declarations.signature: theorem alignedSkip_next_alignment_iff_middle (s : ℕ) (hs : 5 ≤ s) (hskip : ¬ mersenneWeight s ≤ greedyMersenneRemainder (1 / 2 : ℝ) (s - 1)) (halign : halfActualSeamWord s = seamGreedyWord s) : halfActualSeamWord (s + 1) = seamGreedyWord (s + 1) ↔ ¬ (seamAdjacentCut s hs).successorCarries ∧ 4 * (seamAdjacentCut s hs).remainder + (seamPerturbedFamily s (by omega)).gap - (seamAdjacentCut s hs).belowPulse < (seamAdjacentCut s hs).terminalWeight
declarations.docstring: An aligned skip remains aligned at the next row exactly in the middle seam branch.

declarations.name: alignedSkip_nextMargin_eq_neg_middleQuantity
declarations.signature: theorem alignedSkip_nextMargin_eq_neg_middleQuantity (s : ℕ) (hs : 5 ≤ s) (hskip : ¬ mersenneWeight s ≤ greedyMersenneRemainder (1 / 2 : ℝ) (s - 1)) (halign : halfActualSeamWord s = seamGreedyWord s) : greedyHalfFrozenMargin s (s + 1) = -((4 * (seamAdjacentCut s hs).remainder + (seamPerturbedFamily s (by omega)).gap - (seamAdjacentCut s hs).belowPulse : ℕ) : ℤ)
declarations.docstring: The next signed full-shell margin of any aligned skip is the negative middle-branch quantity, independently of which seam branch the greedy word chooses.

declarations.name: alignedSkip_nextMargin_neg
declarations.signature: theorem alignedSkip_nextMargin_neg (s : ℕ) (hs : 5 ≤ s) (hskip : ¬ mersenneWeight s ≤ greedyMersenneRemainder (1 / 2 : ℝ) (s - 1)) (halign : halfActualSeamWord s = seamGreedyWord s) : greedyHalfFrozenMargin s (s + 1) < 0

declarations.name: relationInvariantLinearChannels_det_eq_zero
declarations.signature: theorem relationInvariantLinearChannels_det_eq_zero {V ι : Type*} [AddCommGroup V] [Module ℚ V] [Fintype ι] [DecidableEq ι] [Nontrivial ι] (ev : V →ₗ[ℚ] ℚ) (channel : ι → V →ₗ[ℚ] ℚ) (hker : ∀ j : ι, LinearMap.ker ev ≤ LinearMap.ker (channel j)) (he : ∃ e : V, ev e = 1) (row : ι → V) : Matrix.det (fun i j : ι ↦ channel j (row i)) = 0
declarations.docstring: If every channel kills every relation killed by scalar evaluation, then the evaluation matrix has rank at most one. Hence every square minor indexed by a nontrivial finite type has determinant zero. This is the all-rank determinant consequence of `AdelicHeightObstruction.linearDescender_eq_smul_eval`.

declarations.name: ReverseCarryWord
declarations.docstring: A finite or infinite coefficient word together with a binary-normalized reverse carry. The intended equation is `bit m + 2 * carry m = coeff m + carry (m + 1)`. No Boolean hypothesis is built into the structure: the overlap theorem needs only the explicit seam and overlap bits that it consumes.

declarations.name: carryDifference
declarations.signature: def carryDifference (left right : ReverseCarryWord) (m : ℕ) : ℤ
declarations.docstring: Carry difference between two reverse-carry words.

declarations.name: carryDifference_succ_eq_two_mul
declarations.signature: theorem carryDifference_succ_eq_two_mul (left right : ReverseCarryWord) (m : ℕ) (hcoeff : left.coeff m = right.coeff m) (hbit : left.bit m = right.bit m) : carryDifference left right (m + 1) = 2 * carryDifference left right m
declarations.docstring: When the coefficient and output bits agree at one position, the carry difference doubles across that position.

declarations.name: carryDifference_succ_eq_two_mul_add_one
declarations.signature: theorem carryDifference_succ_eq_two_mul_add_one (left right : ReverseCarryWord) (m : ℕ) (hcoeff : left.coeff m = right.coeff m) (hleft : left.bit m = 1) (hright : right.bit m = 0) : carryDifference left right (m + 1) = 2 * carryDifference left right m + 1
declarations.docstring: A `1/0` output-bit seam with a common coefficient makes the next carry difference `2D + 1`, hence odd.

declarations.name: iterate_doubling
declarations.signature: private theorem iterate_doubling (D : ℕ → ℤ) (start length : ℕ) (hdouble : ∀ j < length, D (start + j + 1) = 2 * D (start + j)) : D (start + length) = (2 : ℤ) ^ length * D start
declarations.docstring: Iterating a pure doubling recurrence over an interval.

declarations.name: overlappingReverseCarryWords_carryDifference_eq_twoPow_mul_odd
declarations.signature: theorem overlappingReverseCarryWords_carryDifference_eq_twoPow_mul_odd (left right : ReverseCarryWord) (seam length : ℕ) (hcoeffSeam : left.coeff seam = right.coeff seam) (hleftSeam : left.bit seam = 1) (hrightSeam : right.bit seam = 0) (hcoeffOverlap : ∀ j < length, left.coeff (seam + 1 + j) = right.coeff (seam + 1 + j)) (hbitOverlap : ∀ j < length, left.bit (seam + 1 + j) = right.bit (seam + 1 + j)) : ∃ z : ℤ, Odd z ∧ carryDifference left right (seam + 1 + length) = (2 : ℤ) ^ length * z
declarations.docstring: **Nonlinear carry-transfer core.** A common coefficient at the seam, a `1/0` seam bit, and a common coefficient/output word for `length` further positions force the terminal carry difference to be `2^length` times an odd integer.

declarations.name: twoPow_le_realBound_of_carryDifference_eq_twoPow_mul_odd
declarations.signature: theorem twoPow_le_realBound_of_carryDifference_eq_twoPow_mul_odd (left right : ReverseCarryWord) (top length : ℕ) (bound : ℝ) (hfactor : ∃ z : ℤ, Odd z ∧ carryDifference left right top = (2 : ℤ) ^ length * z) (hleftNonneg : 0 ≤ left.carry top) (hrightNonneg : 0 ≤ right.carry top) (hleftBound : (left.carry top : ℝ) ≤ bound) (hrightBound : (right.carry top : ℝ) ≤ bound) : (2 : ℝ) ^ length ≤ bound
declarations.docstring: If both terminal carries lie between zero and a common real bound, the exact odd factorisation forces the same-scale Archimedean inequality `2^length ≤ bound`.

declarations.name: overlappingReverseCarryWords_twoPow_le_realBound
declarations.signature: theorem overlappingReverseCarryWords_twoPow_le_realBound (left right : ReverseCarryWord) (seam length : ℕ) (bound : ℝ) (hcoeffSeam : left.coeff seam = right.coeff seam) (hleftSeam : left.bit seam = 1) (hrightSeam : right.bit seam = 0) (hcoeffOverlap : ∀ j < length, left.coeff (seam + 1 + j) = right.coeff (seam + 1 + j)) (hbitOverlap : ∀ j < length, left.bit (seam + 1 + j) = right.bit (seam + 1 + j)) (hleftNonneg : 0 ≤ left.carry (seam + 1 + length)) (hrightNonneg : 0 ≤ right.carry (seam + 1 + length)) (hleftBound : (left.carry (seam + 1 + length) : ℝ) ≤ bound) (hrightBound : (right.carry (seam + 1 + length) : ℝ) ≤ bound) : (2 : ℝ) ^ length ≤ bound
declarations.docstring: Combined overlap-and-smallness theorem. This is the precise abstract same-scale obstruction used by the trapping-return route.

declarations.name: overlappingMidpointReturns_carryDifference_eq_twoPow_mul_odd
declarations.signature: theorem overlappingMidpointReturns_carryDifference_eq_twoPow_mul_odd (left right : ReverseCarryWord) (N M : ℕ) (hN : 1 ≤ N) (hNM : M ≤ 2 * N - 2) (hcoeffSeam : left.coeff (M + 1) = right.coeff (M + 1)) (hleftSeam : left.bit (M + 1) = 1) (hrightSeam : right.bit (M + 1) = 0) (hcoeffOverlap : ∀ j < 2 * N - M - 1, left.coeff (M + 2 + j) = right.coeff (M + 2 + j)) (hbitOverlap : ∀ j < 2 * N - M - 1, left.bit (M + 2 + j) = right.bit (M + 2 + j)) : ∃ z : ℤ, Odd z ∧ carryDifference left right (2 * N + 1) = (2 : ℤ) ^ (2 * N - M - 1) * z
declarations.docstring: Midpoint-indexed form of the exact overlap factorisation. Under `M ≤ 2N-2`, the seam is `M+1`, the overlap length is `2N-M-1`, and its top carry index is exactly `2N+1`.

declarations.name: overlappingMidpointReturns_twoPow_le_realBound
declarations.signature: theorem overlappingMidpointReturns_twoPow_le_realBound (left right : ReverseCarryWord) (N M : ℕ) (bound : ℝ) (hN : 1 ≤ N) (hNM : M ≤ 2 * N - 2) (hcoeffSeam : left.coeff (M + 1) = right.coeff (M + 1)) (hleftSeam : left.bit (M + 1) = 1) (hrightSeam : right.bit (M + 1) = 0) (hcoeffOverlap : ∀ j < 2 * N - M - 1, left.coeff (M + 2 + j) = right.coeff (M + 2 + j)) (hbitOverlap : ∀ j < 2 * N - M - 1, left.bit (M + 2 + j) = right.bit (M + 2 + j)) (hleftNonneg : 0 ≤ left.carry (2 * N + 1)) (hrightNonneg : 0 ≤ right.carry (2 * N + 1)) (hleftBound : (left.carry (2 * N + 1) : ℝ) ≤ bound) (hrightBound : (right.carry (2 * N + 1) : ℝ) ≤ bound) :
declarations.docstring: Midpoint-indexed same-scale spacing inequality. Once the two endpoint carries have a common nonnegative real bound, their overlap length cannot exceed the dyadic size permitted by that bound.

declarations.name: mobiusCompanionCoeff
declarations.docstring: Coefficient of `q^n` in the Möbius companion `M_mu(q^r)`.

declarations.name: incidenceMobiusMatrix
declarations.signature: def incidenceMobiusMatrix (N : ℕ) : Matrix (Fin N) (Fin N) ℤ
declarations.docstring: The finite Möbius-incidence matrix on the positive jet coordinates `q, q^2, ..., q^N`. Columns are companion jets.

declarations.name: incidenceMobiusMatrix_apply
declarations.signature: @[simp] theorem incidenceMobiusMatrix_apply (N : ℕ) (i j : Fin N) : incidenceMobiusMatrix N i j = if j.val + 1 ∣ i.val + 1 then moebius ((i.val + 1) / (j.val + 1)) else 0

declarations.name: incidenceMobiusMatrix_diagonal
declarations.signature: @[simp] theorem incidenceMobiusMatrix_diagonal (N : ℕ) (i : Fin N) : incidenceMobiusMatrix N i i = 1

declarations.name: incidenceMobiusMatrix_lowerTriangular
declarations.docstring: The companion coefficient matrix has no entries strictly above the diagonal.

declarations.name: incidenceMobius_det_eq_one
declarations.docstring: **Finite incidence unimodularity.** Möbius incidence is an integral change of basis on every positive jet space.

declarations.name: incidenceMobius_isUnit
declarations.docstring: The finite incidence matrix is a unit over `ℤ`; in particular its inverse also has integer entries.

declarations.name: mobiusCompanionJet_linearIndependent
declarations.signature: theorem mobiusCompanionJet_linearIndependent (N : ℕ) : LinearIndependent ℤ (incidenceMobiusMatrix N).col
declarations.docstring: Companion jets are linearly independent over `ℤ`.

declarations.name: mobiusCompanionJetMap_injective
declarations.docstring: The evaluated finite companion family has zero kernel. This is the exact finite no-compression statement needed to reject a non-trivial incidence quotient at jet horizon `N`.

declarations.name: mobiusCompanionJetMap_eq_zero_iff
declarations.signature: theorem mobiusCompanionJetMap_eq_zero_iff (N : ℕ) (c : Fin N → ℤ) : (incidenceMobiusMatrix N).mulVec c = 0 ↔ c = 0

declarations.name: IsFiniteIncidenceQuotientRelation
declarations.signature: def IsFiniteIncidenceQuotientRelation (N : ℕ) (c : Fin N → ℤ) : Prop
declarations.docstring: A finite incidence-quotient relation is a coefficient vector whose actual companion jet evaluates to zero. The definition builds evaluation compatibility into the named class.

declarations.name: finiteIncidenceQuotientRelation_iff
declarations.signature: theorem finiteIncidenceQuotientRelation_iff (N : ℕ) (c : Fin N → ℤ) : IsFiniteIncidenceQuotientRelation N c ↔ c = 0
declarations.docstring: **Finite incidence-quotient non-compression.** The named class has only the zero relation at every horizon.

declarations.name: oldChannel_affine_moment_annihilation
declarations.signature: theorem oldChannel_affine_moment_annihilation {ι : Type*} [Fintype ι] (m : ι → ℕ) (c : ι → ℝ) (hzero : ∑ i, c i = 0) (hfirst : ∑ i, c i * (m i : ℝ) = 0) {d H : ℕ} (hd : 0 < d) (hdH : d ∣ H) : ∑ i, c i * transportResidueKernel d (m i * H) = 0
declarations.docstring: Any finite real combination with zero constant and first moments kills an old affine residue channel. This is independent of the particular `(3,5)` operator.

declarations.name: joint35_oldChannel_zero
declarations.signature: theorem joint35_oldChannel_zero {d H : ℕ} (hd : 0 < d) (hdH : d ∣ H) : transportResidueKernel d (15 * H) - 3 * transportResidueKernel d (3 * H) - 2 * transportResidueKernel d (5 * H) + 4 * transportResidueKernel d H = 0
declarations.docstring: The primitive joint `(3,5)` observable kills every old divisor channel.

declarations.name: joint35ConeTail
declarations.docstring: The curvature-preserving four-vertex cone observable.

declarations.name: joint35ConeWindow
declarations.docstring: The integer window obtained from the three cone differences based at `H`.

declarations.name: joint35ConeShiftedTail
declarations.signature: noncomputable def joint35ConeShiftedTail (H L : ℕ) : ℝ
declarations.docstring: The shifted tail left after truncating the joint observable at depth `L`.

declarations.name: sharpJoint35ConeRadius
declarations.docstring: Sharp symmetric error radius for the joint four-vertex window.

declarations.name: two_pow_mul_joint35Cone_eq_window_add_shifted
declarations.signature: theorem two_pow_mul_joint35Cone_eq_window_add_shifted (H L : ℕ) : (2 : ℝ) ^ L * joint35ConeTail H = (joint35ConeWindow H L : ℝ) + joint35ConeShiftedTail H L
declarations.docstring: Exact joint prefix/tail split.

declarations.name: abs_joint35ConeShiftedTail_le
declarations.signature: theorem abs_joint35ConeShiftedTail_le (H L : ℕ) (hH : 1 ≤ H) : |joint35ConeShiftedTail H L| ≤ (sharpJoint35ConeRadius H L : ℝ)
declarations.docstring: The shifted joint tail lies in the sharp symmetric strip.

declarations.name: sharpJoint35ConeCert
declarations.docstring: A dyadic residue separated from both endpoint error bands.

declarations.name: joint35Cone_notMem_int_of_cert
declarations.signature: theorem joint35Cone_notMem_int_of_cert {H L : ℕ} (hH : 1 ≤ H) (hcert : sharpJoint35ConeCert H L) : joint35ConeTail H ∉ Set.range ((↑) : ℤ → ℝ)
declarations.docstring: A sharp joint residue gap excludes integral four-vertex curvature.

declarations.name: AnchoredJoint35ConeSupply
declarations.docstring: The only open arithmetic throat for this anchored joint observable.

declarations.name: irrational_totient_series_of_anchoredJoint35ConeSupply
declarations.signature: theorem irrational_totient_series_of_anchoredJoint35ConeSupply (hsupply : AnchoredJoint35ConeSupply) : Irrational (∑' n : ℕ, (Nat.totient n : ℝ) / 2 ^ n)
declarations.docstring: An unbounded joint certificate supply contradicts rational LCM-cone flatness and proves irrationality of the totient series.

declarations.name: joint35ConeDrive
declarations.docstring: The new integer letter appended to the joint window at offset `s`.

declarations.name: joint35ConeWindow_succ
declarations.signature: theorem joint35ConeWindow_succ (H L : ℕ) : joint35ConeWindow H (L + 1) = 2 * joint35ConeWindow H L + joint35ConeDrive H (L + 1)
declarations.docstring: The joint window is an exact input-driven doubling recurrence.

declarations.name: joint35TransportTail
declarations.docstring: Eight-vertex compatibility object for the existing diagonal increment owner. It is not a second supply hypothesis.

declarations.name: joint35TransportTail_expand
declarations.signature: theorem joint35TransportTail_expand (H : ℕ) : joint35TransportTail H = (totientTail (30 * H) - totientTail (15 * H)) - 3 * (totientTail (6 * H) - totientTail (3 * H)) - 2 * (totientTail (10 * H) - totientTail (5 * H)) + 4 * (totientTail (2 * H) - totientTail H)

declarations.name: ForeignResidueTailLimit
declarations.docstring: The exact analytic obligation connecting the finite residue windows to the omitted part of the actual foreign defect.

declarations.name: controlledForeignProjection_of_tail_limit
declarations.signature: theorem controlledForeignProjection_of_tail_limit {H D : ℕ} (hcutoff : 2 * H ≤ D) (htail : ForeignResidueTailLimit H D) : ControlledForeignProjection H D
declarations.docstring: The uniform finite-window estimate survives the tail limit. This is the missing topology step between the finite geometric calculation and the existing full-target enclosure consumer.

declarations.name: scaleFullTarget_miss_of_tail_limit
declarations.signature: theorem scaleFullTarget_miss_of_tail_limit {H D : ℕ} (hcutoff : 2 * H ≤ D) (htail : ForeignResidueTailLimit H D) (hseparation : ProjectedFullTargetSeparation H D) : ¬ScaleFullTargetHit H
declarations.docstring: A tail-limit identity plus exact projected separation rules out a full-target hit at the scale `H`.

declarations.name: scaleFullTarget_miss_of_tail_limit_of_forall_int
declarations.signature: theorem scaleFullTarget_miss_of_tail_limit_of_forall_int {H D : ℕ} (hcutoff : 2 * H ≤ D) (htail : ForeignResidueTailLimit H D) (hseparation : ∀ z : ℤ, foreignComplementBound H D < |scaleExplicitShadow H + projectedForeignDefect H D - (z : ℝ)|) : ¬ScaleFullTargetHit H
declarations.docstring: Expanded separation form for generated exact-rational certificates.

declarations.name: periodLcm_dvd_periodLcm
declarations.signature: lemma periodLcm_dvd_periodLcm {t u : ℕ} (htu : t ≤ u) : periodLcm t ∣ periodLcm u
declarations.docstring: `lcm(1..t) ∣ lcm(1..u)` for `t ≤ u`: the cones at all scales fit into one directed system, so every cross-scale pair `(q·H_t, r·H_u)` is a same-scale pair on the `t`-ray.

declarations.name: rational_totient_series_forces_lcm_cone_flatness
declarations.signature: theorem rational_totient_series_forces_lcm_cone_flatness (hrat : ¬ Irrational (∑' n : ℕ, (Nat.totient n : ℝ) / 2 ^ n)) : ∃ t₁ : ℕ, ∀ t, t₁ ≤ t → ∀ q m : ℕ, 0 < q → totientTail (q * periodLcm t + m * periodLcm t) - totientTail (q * periodLcm t) ∈ Set.range ((↑) : ℤ → ℝ)
declarations.docstring: **The lcm-cone flatness law.** If the totient series is rational, then from some scale on, the ENTIRE lcm cone is fractionally flat: every difference `R_{q·H_t + m·H_t} - R_{q·H_t}` is an integer, for every `t` past the threshold, every base multiplier `q ≥ 1`, and every step multiplier `m`. One constant fractional part governs every cone point — the maximal spend of the rationality hypothesis on the lcm lattice.

declarations.name: irrational_totient_series_of_lcm_cone_certificate_supply
declarations.signature: theorem irrational_totient_series_of_lcm_cone_certificate_supply (hsupply : ∀ t₀ : ℕ, ∃ t, t₀ ≤ t ∧ ∃ q m L : ℕ, 0 < q ∧ certifiedKill (m * periodLcm t) (q * periodLcm t) L) : Irrational (∑' n : ℕ, (Nat.totient n : ℝ) / 2 ^ n)
declarations.docstring: **The wave-24 cone collapse.** #249 follows from certified kills anywhere on the two-multiplier lcm cone: `(h, N) = (m·H_t, q·H_t)` at arbitrarily large `t`. Subsumes the wave-23 diagonal (`q = m = 1`), every q-ray step (`m = 1`), every q-gap (free `m`), and — via `periodLcm_dvd_periodLcm` — every cross-scale pair, since `H_u` is itself a multiple of `H_t` for `u ≥ t`. Rationality flattens the cone; one certified annihilator at arbitrarily large scale is a contradiction.

declarations.name: summable_linear_half
declarations.signature: private lemma summable_linear_half' : Summable (fun j : ℕ => (j : ℝ) * (1 / 2 : ℝ) ^ j)
declarations.docstring: **The wave-24 cone collapse.** #249 follows from certified kills anywhere on the two-multiplier lcm cone: `(h, N) = (m·H_t, q·H_t)` at arbitrarily large `t`. Subsumes the wave-23 diagonal (`q = m = 1`), every q-ray step (`m = 1`), every q-gap (free `m`), and — via `periodLcm_dvd_periodLcm` — every cross-scale pair, since `H_u` is itself a multiple of `H_t` for `u ≥ t`. Rationality flattens the cone; one certified annihilator at arbitrarily large scale is a contradiction. -/ theorem irrational_totient_series_of_lcm_cone_certificate_supply (hsupply : ∀ t₀ : ℕ, ∃ t, t₀ ≤ t ∧ ∃ q m L : ℕ, 0 < q ∧ certifiedKill (m * periodLcm t) (q * periodLcm t) L) : Irrational (∑' n : ℕ, (Nat.totient n : ℝ) / 2 ^ n) := by by_contra hrat obtain ⟨t₁, hflat⟩ := rational_totient_series_forces_lcm_cone_flatness hrat obtain ⟨t, ht, q, m, L, hq, hkill⟩ := hsupply t₁ exact tail_diff_notMem_int_of_certifiedKill hkill (hflat t ht q m hq) /-! ## Certificate completeness: kills exist exactly at non-integers

declarations.name: summable_const_add_mul_half
declarations.signature: private lemma summable_const_add_mul_half' (C : ℝ) : Summable (fun j : ℕ => (C + (j : ℝ)) * (1 / 2 : ℝ) ^ j)

declarations.name: exists_depth_lt
declarations.signature: private lemma exists_depth_lt (C δ : ℝ) (hδ : 0 < δ) : ∃ L : ℕ, (C + (L : ℝ)) * (1 / 2 : ℝ) ^ L < δ

declarations.name: exists_certifiedKill_of_tail_diff_notMem_int
declarations.signature: theorem exists_certifiedKill_of_tail_diff_notMem_int {h N : ℕ} (hD : totientTail (N + h) - totientTail N ∉ Set.range ((↑) : ℤ → ℝ)) : ∃ L, certifiedKill h N L
declarations.docstring: **Certificate completeness (the converse of wave 21).** If the tail difference `R_{N+h} - R_N` is a non-integer, some finite depth certifies it: its distance `δ` to the nearest integer is positive, and any window depth `L` with `2·(N+h+L+2) < δ·2^L` places the residue outside the forbidden band.

declarations.name: exists_certifiedKill_iff_tail_diff_notMem_int
declarations.signature: theorem exists_certifiedKill_iff_tail_diff_notMem_int (h N : ℕ) : (∃ L, certifiedKill h N L) ↔ totientTail (N + h) - totientTail N ∉ Set.range ((↑) : ℤ → ℝ)
declarations.docstring: **Certificate completeness, iff form.** Some depth certifies `(h, N)` exactly when the tail difference is a non-integer. Finite certificates are receipts; non-integrality is the object.

declarations.name: tail_diff_mem_int_iff_scaled_series_mem_int
declarations.signature: theorem tail_diff_mem_int_iff_scaled_series_mem_int (h N : ℕ) : (totientTail (N + h) - totientTail N ∈ Set.range ((↑) : ℤ → ℝ)) ↔ ((2 : ℝ) ^ N * ((2 : ℝ) ^ h - 1) * (∑' n : ℕ, (Nat.totient n : ℝ) / 2 ^ n) ∈ Set.range ((↑) : ℤ → ℝ))
declarations.docstring: Integrality of one shifted tail difference is exactly integrality of the corresponding integer multiple `2^N (2^h - 1) S` of the #249 constant. This is the algebraic converse left implicit by the period-killer reduction.

declarations.name: not_irrational_of_tail_diff_mem_int
declarations.signature: theorem not_irrational_of_tail_diff_mem_int {h N : ℕ} (hh : 0 < h) (hint : totientTail (N + h) - totientTail N ∈ Set.range ((↑) : ℤ → ℝ)) : ¬ Irrational (∑' n : ℕ, (Nat.totient n : ℝ) / 2 ^ n)
declarations.docstring: One integral tail difference at a positive shift already makes the #249 constant rational. Thus a perpetually trapped single pair is not merely a failure of the certificate route: it resolves #249 in the opposite direction.

declarations.name: irrational_totient_series_iff_all_tail_diffs_nonintegral
declarations.signature: theorem irrational_totient_series_iff_all_tail_diffs_nonintegral : Irrational (∑' n : ℕ, (Nat.totient n : ℝ) / 2 ^ n) ↔ ∀ h : ℕ, 0 < h → ∀ N : ℕ, totientTail (N + h) - totientTail N ∉ Set.range ((↑) : ℤ → ℝ)
declarations.docstring: #249 is equivalent to pointwise non-integrality of every positive shifted tail difference.

declarations.name: irrational_totient_series_iff_pointwise_certificates
declarations.signature: theorem irrational_totient_series_iff_pointwise_certificates : Irrational (∑' n : ℕ, (Nat.totient n : ℝ) / 2 ^ n) ↔ ∀ h : ℕ, 0 < h → ∀ N : ℕ, ∃ L, certifiedKill h N L
declarations.docstring: Certificate completeness turns the pointwise non-integrality normal form into a pointwise finite-receipt normal form.

declarations.name: irrational_totient_series_iff_certificate_supply
declarations.signature: theorem irrational_totient_series_iff_certificate_supply : Irrational (∑' n : ℕ, (Nat.totient n : ℝ) / 2 ^ n) ↔ ∀ h : ℕ, 0 < h → ∀ N₀ : ℕ, ∃ N, N₀ ≤ N ∧ ∃ L, certifiedKill h N L
declarations.docstring: The wave-21 cofinal certificate supply is not a weaker large-measure surrogate: together with completeness it is exactly equivalent to #249.

declarations.name: periodLcm_diagonal_kill_iff_tail_diff_notMem_int
declarations.signature: theorem periodLcm_diagonal_kill_iff_tail_diff_notMem_int (t : ℕ) : (∃ L, certifiedKill (periodLcm t) (periodLcm t) L) ↔ totientTail (periodLcm t + periodLcm t) - totientTail (periodLcm t) ∉ Set.range ((↑) : ℤ → ℝ)
declarations.docstring: The wave-23 predicate `P t` in pure form: a diagonal certificate depth exists iff the diagonal tail difference is a non-integer.

declarations.name: irrational_totient_series_of_lcm_diagonal_nonintegrality_supply
declarations.signature: theorem irrational_totient_series_of_lcm_diagonal_nonintegrality_supply (hsupply : ∀ t₀ : ℕ, ∃ t, t₀ ≤ t ∧ totientTail (periodLcm t + periodLcm t) - totientTail (periodLcm t) ∉ Set.range ((↑) : ℤ → ℝ)) : Irrational (∑' n : ℕ, (Nat.totient n : ℝ) / 2 ^ n)
declarations.docstring: **The diagonal frontier without certificate vocabulary.** If the diagonal tail difference `R_{2H_t} - R_{H_t}` is a non-integer for arbitrarily large `t`, the totient series is irrational. (Completeness converts each non-integrality into a finite decidable certificate.)

declarations.name: irrational_totient_series_of_lcm_cone_nonintegrality_supply
declarations.signature: theorem irrational_totient_series_of_lcm_cone_nonintegrality_supply (hsupply : ∀ t₀ : ℕ, ∃ t, t₀ ≤ t ∧ ∃ q m : ℕ, 0 < q ∧ totientTail (q * periodLcm t + m * periodLcm t) - totientTail (q * periodLcm t) ∉ Set.range ((↑) : ℤ → ℝ)) : Irrational (∑' n : ℕ, (Nat.totient n : ℝ) / 2 ^ n)
declarations.docstring: **The cone frontier in pure form**: one non-integral cone difference at arbitrarily large scale proves #249 — no certificate vocabulary at all.

declarations.name: windowDiscrepancy2
declarations.signature: def windowDiscrepancy2 (h N L : ℕ) : ℤ
declarations.docstring: Second-difference window discrepancy `A₂ = A(h, N+h, L) - A(h, N, L)`: the depth-`L` truncation of `2^L·((R_{N+2h} - R_{N+h}) - (R_{N+h} - R_N))`.

declarations.name: certifiedRank2Kill
declarations.signature: def certifiedRank2Kill (h N L : ℕ) : Prop
declarations.docstring: The decidable rank-2 certificate: the residue of `A₂` modulo `2^L` avoids the radius-`2(N+2h+L+2)` neighbourhood of `0`. The doubled radius pays for two window truncations; in exchange the second difference cancels the whole `H·C` clean shadow on the lcm cone.

declarations.name: second_diff_notMem_int_of_certifiedRank2Kill
declarations.signature: theorem second_diff_notMem_int_of_certifiedRank2Kill {h N L : ℕ} (hcert : certifiedRank2Kill h N L) : (totientTail (N + 2 * h) - totientTail (N + h)) - (totientTail (N + h) - totientTail N) ∉ Set.range ((↑) : ℤ → ℝ)
declarations.docstring: **Rank-2 soundness.** A certified rank-2 residue proves the second difference `(R_{N+2h} - R_{N+h}) - (R_{N+h} - R_N)` is not an integer.

declarations.name: irrational_totient_series_of_lcm_qray_rank2_supply
declarations.signature: theorem irrational_totient_series_of_lcm_qray_rank2_supply (hsupply : ∀ t₀ : ℕ, ∃ t, t₀ ≤ t ∧ ∃ q L : ℕ, 0 < q ∧ certifiedRank2Kill (periodLcm t) (q * periodLcm t) L) : Irrational (∑' n : ℕ, (Nat.totient n : ℝ) / 2 ^ n)
declarations.docstring: **Rank-2 cone supply.** Certified second-difference kills along the q-rays of the lcm cone — `(h, N) = (H_t, q·H_t)` — at arbitrarily large `t` prove #249: rationality flattens the cone, so BOTH first differences are forced integral and their difference is an integer.

declarations.name: certifiedKill_lcm_cone_cells
declarations.signature: theorem certifiedKill_lcm_cone_cells : certifiedKill (1 * periodLcm 2) (3 * periodLcm 2) 6 ∧ certifiedKill (2 * periodLcm 3) (2 * periodLcm 3) 7 ∧ certifiedKill (3 * periodLcm 3) (1 * periodLcm 3) 8
declarations.docstring: Cone-lattice deposits beyond the diagonal: kills at `(h, N) = (m·H_t, q·H_t)` for `(t, q, m) = (2, 3, 1)`, `(3, 2, 2)`, `(3, 1, 3)` — the first machine-checked annihilators off the diagonal, witnessing the cone supply shape at its probe-tabulated depths.

declarations.name: certifiedRank2Kill_example
declarations.docstring: A machine-checked rank-2 kill: the second difference at `(h, N) = (1, 8)` is certified non-integral at depth `9` (totient arguments ≤ 19).

declarations.name: rank2_kill_sound_but_not_shallower_at_cell
declarations.signature: theorem rank2_kill_sound_but_not_shallower_at_cell : certifiedKill 1 8 8 ∧ (∀ L ∈ Finset.Icc 1 8, ¬ certifiedRank2Kill 1 8 L) ∧ certifiedRank2Kill 1 8 9
declarations.docstring: **The measured wave-24 verdict, kernel-checked at the fixture cell**: rank-2 is SOUND but NOT shallower. At `(h, N) = (1, 8)` the rank-1 certificate fires at depth `8` while no rank-2 certificate exists at any depth `≤ 8`. (Probe, `t ≤ 20`: rank-1 at least as shallow in 30 of 40 cells, median depth delta `+1` against rank-2 — the advisory-1 promotion gate is measured and denied.)

declarations.name: second_diff_not_int_example
declarations.signature: theorem second_diff_not_int_example : (totientTail (8 + 2 * 1) - totientTail (8 + 1)) - (totientTail (8 + 1) - totientTail 8) ∉ Set.range ((↑) : ℤ → ℝ)
declarations.docstring: Concrete rank-2 non-integrality: `(R₁₀ - R₉) - (R₉ - R₈)` is not an integer — the first machine-checked second-difference obstruction.

declarations.name: tail_diff_not_int_periodLcm_diagonal_seven_eight
declarations.signature: theorem tail_diff_not_int_periodLcm_diagonal_seven_eight : (totientTail (periodLcm 7 + periodLcm 7) - totientTail (periodLcm 7) ∉ Set.range ((↑) : ℤ → ℝ)) ∧ (totientTail (periodLcm 8 + periodLcm 8) - totientTail (periodLcm 8) ∉ Set.range ((↑) : ℤ → ℝ))
declarations.docstring: The diagonal tail differences at `t = 7` and `t = 8` are non-integers — extending the wave-23 corollary past its `t ≤ 6` decide ceiling via the `_at_seven` / `_at_eight` deposits.

declarations.name: plus
declarations.signature: structure plus a non-negative fresh-prime (`> t`) defect. It is NOT a supply step: it does not fire any certificate, it fixes the arithmetic of the window argument. -/ theorem small_prime_dvd_cone_window_iff_dvd_offset {t q s p : ℕ} (hp : p.Prime) (hpt : p ≤ t) : p ∣ q * periodLcm t + s ↔ p ∣ s

declarations.name: small_prime_dvd_cone_window_iff_dvd_offset
declarations.signature: theorem small_prime_dvd_cone_window_iff_dvd_offset {t q s p : ℕ} (hp : p.Prime) (hpt : p ≤ t) : p ∣ q * periodLcm t + s ↔ p ∣ s

declarations.name: windowNumerator
declarations.docstring: The depth-`L` window numerator `P_L(M) = Σ_{j<L} φ(M+1+j)·2^{L-1-j}`: the integer layer of `2^L·R_M`, exact up to the one-sided deep tail `0 ≤ 2^L·R_M - P_L(M) ≤ M+L+2`.

declarations.name: windowNumerator_div_eq
declarations.signature: lemma windowNumerator_div_eq (M L : ℕ) : ((windowNumerator M L : ℕ) : ℝ) / 2 ^ L = ∑ j ∈ Finset.range L, (Nat.totient (M + 1 + j) : ℝ) / 2 ^ (j + 1)
declarations.docstring: The window numerator over `2^L` is the depth-`L` partial tail.

declarations.name: coneNonflatCert
