declarations.signature: def coneNonflatCert (H L : ℕ) (Q : List ℕ) : Prop
declarations.docstring: **The cone non-flatness certificate.** For the vertex menu `Q` at scale `H` and depth `L`: every left endpoint `P_L(qᵢ·H)` escapes some arc `[P_L(qⱼ·H), P_L(qⱼ·H) + (qⱼ·H+L+2)]` modulo `2^L`. A `|Q|²` one-sided window check; decidable.

declarations.name: exists_nonintegral_pair_of_coneNonflatCert
declarations.signature: theorem exists_nonintegral_pair_of_coneNonflatCert {H L : ℕ} {Q : List ℕ} (hQ : Q ≠ []) (hfloor : ∀ q ∈ Q, (q * H + L + 2 : ℤ) < 2 ^ L) (hcert : coneNonflatCert H L Q) : ∃ qi ∈ Q, ∃ qj ∈ Q, totientTail (qj * H) - totientTail (qi * H) ∉ Set.range ((↑) : ℤ → ℝ)
declarations.docstring: **Menu refutation soundness.** If the certificate fires and the one-sided floor `2^L > q·H+L+2` holds at every vertex, then some pair of menu vertices has a NON-integral tail difference. Proof shape: were every pair integral, the vertex `q⋆` of minimal deep tail would give, for every menu vertex `q`, the integer `C = P(q⋆·H) - P(q·H) + k·2^L` equal to the real number `(T_q - T_{q⋆})·2^L ∈ [0, q·H+L+2]` — so the left endpoint `P(q⋆·H)` sits inside EVERY arc, and the certificate row at `q⋆` is exactly the denial of that.

declarations.name: irrational_totient_series_of_lcm_cone_nonflat_supply
declarations.signature: theorem irrational_totient_series_of_lcm_cone_nonflat_supply (hsupply : ∀ t₀ : ℕ, ∃ t, t₀ ≤ t ∧ ∃ L : ℕ, ∃ Q : List ℕ, Q ≠ [] ∧ (∀ q ∈ Q, 0 < q) ∧ (∀ q ∈ Q, (q * periodLcm t + L + 2 : ℤ) < 2 ^ L) ∧ coneNonflatCert (periodLcm t) L Q) : Irrational (∑' n : ℕ, (Nat.totient n : ℝ) / 2 ^ n)
declarations.docstring: **The cone non-flatness supply theorem.** If for arbitrarily large `t` some finite positive vertex menu on the `lcm(1..t)` cone fires the non-flatness certificate (with its one-sided floors), the totient series is irrational. Rationality flattens the cone — every pair of menu vertices would have integral difference — and the refuter denies all of them at once.

declarations.name: irrational_totient_series_of_lcm_jump_kill_supply
declarations.signature: theorem irrational_totient_series_of_lcm_jump_kill_supply (hsupply : ∀ t₀ : ℕ, ∃ t, t₀ ≤ t ∧ ∃ L : ℕ, certifiedKill (periodLcm (t + 1) - periodLcm t) (periodLcm t) L) : Irrational (∑' n : ℕ, (Nat.totient n : ℝ) / 2 ^ n)
declarations.docstring: **Jump supply.** Certified kills at the lcm jumps — `(h, N) = (H_{t+1} - H_t, H_t)` at arbitrarily large `t` — prove #249. Each jump is the cone cell `(q, m) = (1, H_{t+1}/H_t - 1)`; no side condition is needed because `H_t ∣ H_{t+1}` makes the multiplier exact (and a `h = 0` window can never be certified).

declarations.name: coneCommonPoint
declarations.signature: def coneCommonPoint (H L : ℕ) (Q : List ℕ) : Prop
declarations.docstring: A **common point** of the depth-`L` menu arcs: an integer residue whose one-sided distance from every left endpoint `P_L(q·H)` stays inside that vertex's arc radius `q·H+L+2` modulo `2^L`. Joint flatness of the menu would pin `2^L·μ` at exactly such a point.

declarations.name: windowNumerator_succ
declarations.signature: lemma windowNumerator_succ (M L : ℕ) : windowNumerator M (L + 1) = 2 * windowNumerator M L + Nat.totient (M + 1 + L)
declarations.docstring: The window recursion: one more depth bit appends one totient value, `P_{L+1}(M) = 2·P_L(M) + φ(M+L+1)`.

declarations.name: not_coneCommonPoint_of_coneNonflatCert
declarations.signature: theorem not_coneCommonPoint_of_coneNonflatCert {H L : ℕ} {Q : List ℕ} (hQ : Q ≠ []) (hcert : coneNonflatCert H L Q) : ¬ coneCommonPoint H L Q
declarations.docstring: **Refuter soundness against common points**: a firing certificate denies every common point — the combinatorial argmin. If `y` were common, the vertex `q⋆` of minimal one-sided distance to `y` would have its left endpoint inside EVERY arc, and `q⋆`'s certificate row denies exactly that.

declarations.name: coneNonflatCert_of_not_coneCommonPoint
declarations.signature: theorem coneNonflatCert_of_not_coneCommonPoint {H L : ℕ} {Q : List ℕ} (h : ¬ coneCommonPoint H L Q) : coneNonflatCert H L Q
declarations.docstring: **Refuter completeness against common points**: if no common point exists, the certificate fires — a silent row at `qi` would make the left endpoint `P_L(qi·H)` itself a common point.

declarations.name: coneNonflatCert_iff_no_common_point
declarations.signature: theorem coneNonflatCert_iff_no_common_point {H L : ℕ} {Q : List ℕ} (hQ : Q ≠ []) : coneNonflatCert H L Q ↔ ¬ coneCommonPoint H L Q
declarations.docstring: **Exactness of the menu refuter, kernel-checked**: the certificate fires iff the menu arcs share NO common point. This is the law the probe gate `nonflat_matches_common_point_bruteforce` measured at every tested cell; it is now a theorem at every cell.

declarations.name: coneCommonPoint_of_succ
declarations.signature: theorem coneCommonPoint_of_succ {H L : ℕ} {Q : List ℕ} (hfloor : ∀ q ∈ Q, (q * H + L + 2 : ℤ) < 2 ^ L) (h : coneCommonPoint H (L + 1) Q) : coneCommonPoint H L Q
declarations.docstring: **Halving descent**: a common point at depth `L+1` yields one at depth `L` (floors held at `L`). The reduced witness halves; the window recursion turns the arc bookkeeping into `⌊(φ + b)/2⌋ ≤ q·H+L+2`.

declarations.name: coneNonflat_floor_succ
declarations.signature: lemma coneNonflat_floor_succ {H q L : ℕ} (h : (q * H + L + 2 : ℤ) < 2 ^ L) : (q * H + (L + 1) + 2 : ℤ) < 2 ^ (L + 1)
declarations.docstring: The one-sided floor propagates up one depth bit.

declarations.name: coneNonflat_floor_mono
declarations.signature: lemma coneNonflat_floor_mono {H q L L' : ℕ} (hle : L ≤ L') (h : (q * H + L + 2 : ℤ) < 2 ^ L) : (q * H + L' + 2 : ℤ) < 2 ^ L'
declarations.docstring: The one-sided floor propagates to every larger depth.

declarations.name: coneNonflatCert_mono_depth
declarations.signature: theorem coneNonflatCert_mono_depth {H L : ℕ} {Q : List ℕ} (hQ : Q ≠ []) (hfloor : ∀ q ∈ Q, (q * H + L + 2 : ℤ) < 2 ^ L) (hcert : coneNonflatCert H L Q) : coneNonflatCert H (L + 1) Q
declarations.docstring: **Depth monotonicity, one bit**: a firing certificate keeps firing one depth deeper (floors held). Chain: cert(L) ⟹ no common point at `L` (argmin) ⟹ no common point at `L+1` (halving) ⟹ cert(L+1) (completeness).

declarations.name: coneNonflatCert_mono_of_le
declarations.signature: theorem coneNonflatCert_mono_of_le {H L L' : ℕ} {Q : List ℕ} (hQ : Q ≠ []) (hfloor : ∀ q ∈ Q, (q * H + L + 2 : ℤ) < 2 ^ L) (hcert : coneNonflatCert H L Q) (hle : L ≤ L') : coneNonflatCert H L' Q
declarations.docstring: **Depth monotonicity**: a firing certificate fires at EVERY deeper depth (floors held at the base). `nonflat_L_min` is therefore THE firing threshold: below it silence, at and above it certification. In any supply statement `∃L` may be replaced by any canonical large-enough depth.

declarations.name: coneCommonPoint_subset
declarations.signature: theorem coneCommonPoint_subset {H L : ℕ} {Q Q' : List ℕ} (hsub : ∀ q ∈ Q, q ∈ Q') (h : coneCommonPoint H L Q') : coneCommonPoint H L Q
declarations.docstring: A common point of a menu restricts to a common point of any sub-menu: the same witness satisfies fewer arc constraints.

declarations.name: coneNonflatCert_supermenu
declarations.signature: theorem coneNonflatCert_supermenu {H L : ℕ} {Q Q' : List ℕ} (hQ : Q ≠ []) (hsub : ∀ q ∈ Q, q ∈ Q') (hcert : coneNonflatCert H L Q) : coneNonflatCert H L Q'
declarations.docstring: **Menu monotonicity, same depth, no floor**: a firing certificate keeps firing for every supermenu at the SAME depth. If the sub-menu arcs already have empty intersection, adding arcs cannot refill it.

declarations.name: coneNonflatCert_seed_lift
declarations.signature: theorem coneNonflatCert_seed_lift {H L L' : ℕ} {Q Q' : List ℕ} (hQ : Q ≠ []) (hsub : ∀ q ∈ Q, q ∈ Q') (hfloor : ∀ q ∈ Q, (q * H + L + 2 : ℤ) < 2 ^ L) (hcert : coneNonflatCert H L Q) (hle : L ≤ L') : coneNonflatCert H L' Q'
declarations.docstring: **The seed-lift theorem**: a small seed menu `Q` firing at its own floor `L` inflates to every supermenu `Q'` at every deeper depth `L'`. Depth monotonicity carries the seed up to `L'`; menu monotonicity carries it out to `Q'`. Consequence for supply: it suffices that SOME prefix seed fires at its floor for arbitrarily large `t` — the full menu at the full depth follows for free, at no extra arithmetic cost.

declarations.name: coneNonflatCert_cells
declarations.docstring: First firing menus: the non-flatness certificate fires at `(t, Q, L) = (1, [1,2,3], 5)`, `(2, [1,2,3,4], 5)`, `(3, [1,2,3], 6)` — the probe-tabulated cells with the smallest totient arguments (all one-sided floors hold).

declarations.name: cone_nonflat_strict_win_at_cell
declarations.signature: theorem cone_nonflat_strict_win_at_cell : coneNonflatCert (periodLcm 2) 8 [1, 3] ∧ (∀ L ∈ Finset.Icc 1 8, ¬ certifiedKill (2 * periodLcm 2) (1 * periodLcm 2) L) ∧ certifiedKill (2 * periodLcm 2) (1 * periodLcm 2) 9
declarations.docstring: **The measured strict win, kernel-checked**: at `t = 2` the menu `[1, 3]` refutes joint flatness at depth `8`, while NO pairwise certificate exists at any depth `≤ 8` — the pair first kills at depth `9`. The one-sided menu band is strictly sharper than the two-sided pairwise band (probe v3: 30 strict wins among the named menus, 169 in the exhaustive search).

declarations.name: cone_nonflat_joint_win_at_cell
declarations.signature: theorem cone_nonflat_joint_win_at_cell : coneNonflatCert (periodLcm 2) 5 [2, 3, 4] ∧ (¬ coneNonflatCert (periodLcm 2) 5 [2, 3]) ∧ (¬ coneNonflatCert (periodLcm 2) 5 [2, 4]) ∧ (¬ coneNonflatCert (periodLcm 2) 5 [3, 4]) ∧ (¬ certifiedKill (1 * periodLcm 2) (2 * periodLcm 2) 5) ∧ (¬ certifiedKill (2 * periodLcm 2) (2 * periodLcm 2) 5) ∧ (¬ certifiedKill (1 * periodLcm 2) (3 * periodLcm 2) 5)
declarations.docstring: **The measured joint win, kernel-checked**: at `t = 2` the menu `[2, 3, 4]` refutes joint flatness at depth `5` while EVERY 2-vertex submenu refuter is silent at depth `5` and every pairwise certificate fails there too. The refutation is genuinely joint: the menu is inconsistent at a depth where every pair alone stays consistent. (Probe v3 tabulates 52 such joint cells over `t ≤ 7`, menus `⊆ {1..8}`; the named-menu census cell at `t = 8`, menu `[1,2,3,4]`, fires at depth 12 — its one-sided floor, slack ZERO — with the same joint silence.)

declarations.name: periodLcm_dvd_succ
declarations.signature: theorem periodLcm_dvd_succ (t : ℕ) : periodLcm t ∣ periodLcm (t + 1)
declarations.docstring: Consecutive LCM heights are nested by divisibility.

declarations.name: periodLcm_strict_jump_sub_ge
declarations.signature: theorem periodLcm_strict_jump_sub_ge {t : ℕ} (hjump : periodLcm t < periodLcm (t + 1)) : periodLcm t ≤ periodLcm (t + 1) - periodLcm t
declarations.docstring: A strict LCM jump is at least as long as its starting height.

declarations.name: le_periodLcm
declarations.signature: lemma le_periodLcm (t : ℕ) : t ≤ periodLcm t

declarations.name: irrational_totient_series_of_lcm_certificate_supply
declarations.signature: theorem irrational_totient_series_of_lcm_certificate_supply (hsupply : ∀ t₀ N₀ : ℕ, ∃ t, t₀ ≤ t ∧ ∃ N, N₀ ≤ N ∧ ∃ L, certifiedKill (periodLcm t) N L) : Irrational (∑' n : ℕ, (Nat.totient n : ℝ) / 2 ^ n)
declarations.docstring: Endpoint-certificate variant of the wave-22 one-parameter collapse: certified window kills along `lcm(1..t)` — arbitrarily large `t`, arbitrarily large `N` — prove #249. (Wave 22 landed only the survivor form.)

declarations.name: irrational_totient_series_of_lcm_diagonal_certificate_supply
declarations.signature: theorem irrational_totient_series_of_lcm_diagonal_certificate_supply (hsupply : ∀ t₀ : ℕ, ∃ t, t₀ ≤ t ∧ ∃ L, certifiedKill (periodLcm t) (periodLcm t) L) : Irrational (∑' n : ℕ, (Nat.totient n : ℝ) / 2 ^ n)
declarations.docstring: **The wave-23 diagonal collapse.** #249 follows from a SINGLE ℕ-indexed sequence of decidable statements: the endpoint certificate firing on the diagonal `(h, N) = (periodLcm t, periodLcm t)` for infinitely many `t`. Rationality would supply a primitive period `h₀` and pre-period `N₀`; any `t ≥ max(h₀, N₀)` gives `h₀ ∣ periodLcm t` and `periodLcm t ≥ t ≥ N₀` simultaneously, so a diagonal kill at such `t` is a contradiction. No second parameter remains.

declarations.name: irrational_totient_series_of_lcm_diagonal_survivor_supply
declarations.signature: theorem irrational_totient_series_of_lcm_diagonal_survivor_supply (hsupply : ∀ t₀ : ℕ, ∃ t, t₀ ≤ t ∧ ∃ K, survivorKill (periodLcm t) (periodLcm t) K) : Irrational (∑' n : ℕ, (Nat.totient n : ℝ) / 2 ^ n)
declarations.docstring: Survivor variant of the diagonal collapse.

declarations.name: eq_prime_pow_of_not_dvd_periodLcm
declarations.signature: theorem eq_prime_pow_of_not_dvd_periodLcm {t j : ℕ} (hj : 0 < j) (hlt : j < 2 * t) (hnd : ¬ j ∣ periodLcm t) : ∃ p k : ℕ, Nat.Prime p ∧ j = p ^ k ∧ t < j
declarations.docstring: **Window-structure theorem for the lcm ray.** Below `2t`, the only non-divisors of `lcm(1..t)` are bare prime powers exceeding `t`: every other window index divides the period. (Indices `j ≤ t` divide outright by `dvd_periodLcm`; this pins down the sparse exceptional set above `t`.)

declarations.name: coprime_ray_cofactor
declarations.signature: lemma coprime_ray_cofactor {j a : ℕ} (q : ℕ) (hcof : ∀ p : ℕ, Nat.Prime p → p ∣ j → p ∣ a) : Nat.Coprime j (q * a + 1)
declarations.docstring: If every prime of `j` divides `a`, then `j` and `q·a + 1` are coprime: the cofactor is forced `≡ 1` mod every prime of `j`.

declarations.name: totient_ray_split
declarations.signature: theorem totient_ray_split {j a : ℕ} (q : ℕ) (hcof : ∀ p : ℕ, Nat.Prime p → p ∣ j → p ∣ a) : Nat.totient (j * (q * a + 1)) = Nat.totient j * Nat.totient (q * a + 1)
declarations.docstring: Totient splits across the ray product `j·(q·a + 1)` whenever every prime of `j` divides `a`.

declarations.name: totient_periodLcm_ray_split
declarations.signature: theorem totient_periodLcm_ray_split {t j : ℕ} (q : ℕ) (hdvd : j ∣ periodLcm t) (hclean : ∀ p : ℕ, Nat.Prime p → p ∣ j → p ∣ (periodLcm t / j)) : Nat.totient (q * periodLcm t + j) = Nat.totient j * Nat.totient (q * (periodLcm t / j) + 1)
declarations.docstring: **Lcm-ray window factorisation.** For a clean divisor `j` of `H = periodLcm t` — every prime of `j` still divides `H/j` — the window totient at `q·H + j` splits into the KNOWN local part `φ(j)` and the cofactor `φ(q·(H/j) + 1)`, whose argument avoids every prime of `j`'s saturation. This is the exact identity behind the wave-23 shadow decomposition: on the lcm ray the window is structured arithmetic, not totient noise.

declarations.name: diagonalKillDepth
declarations.signature: def diagonalKillDepth : ℕ → ℕ | 1 => 6 | 2 => 5 | 3 => 7 | 4 => 7 | 5 => 9 | 6 => 9 | 7 => 14 | 8 => 15 | _ => 0 /-- **Diagonal deposit.** The wave-23 predicate `P t` holds for every `1 ≤ t ≤ 6`: the endpoint certificate fires at `(H_t, H_t)` with the tabulated depth. Totient arguments stay ≤ 130 — a light kernel `decide`. -/
declarations.docstring: Certified diagonal kill depths for `1 ≤ t ≤ 8`, matching the wave-23 probe (`lcm_diagonal_shadow_probe.json`).

declarations.name: certifiedKill_periodLcm_diagonal_upto_six
declarations.signature: theorem certifiedKill_periodLcm_diagonal_upto_six : ∀ t ∈ Finset.Icc 1 6, certifiedKill (periodLcm t) (periodLcm t) (diagonalKillDepth t)
declarations.docstring: **Diagonal deposit.** The wave-23 predicate `P t` holds for every `1 ≤ t ≤ 6`: the endpoint certificate fires at `(H_t, H_t)` with the tabulated depth. Totient arguments stay ≤ 130 — a light kernel `decide`.

declarations.name: certifiedKill_periodLcm_diagonal_at_seven
declarations.docstring: `P 7`: the diagonal certificate at `lcm(1..7) = 420`, depth `14`.

declarations.name: certifiedKill_periodLcm_diagonal_at_eight
declarations.docstring: `P 8`: the diagonal certificate at `lcm(1..8) = 840`, depth `15`.

declarations.name: tail_diff_not_int_periodLcm_diagonal
declarations.signature: theorem tail_diff_not_int_periodLcm_diagonal (t : ℕ) (h1 : 1 ≤ t) (h6 : t ≤ 6) : totientTail (periodLcm t + periodLcm t) - totientTail (periodLcm t) ∉ Set.range ((↑) : ℤ → ℝ)
declarations.docstring: No lcm-diagonal tail difference is an integer at the first six scales: `D_{H_t}(H_t) ∉ ℤ` for `1 ≤ t ≤ 6`.

declarations.name: sparsePulseState
declarations.signature: def sparsePulseState (A : ℤ) (S : Finset ℕ) (k : ℕ) : ℤ
declarations.docstring: A state which is `-A` on a finite anchor set and zero elsewhere.

declarations.name: sparsePulseLetter
declarations.signature: def sparsePulseLetter (A : ℤ) (S : Finset ℕ) (i : ℕ) : ℤ
declarations.docstring: The zero-based forcing letter determined by `c_{i+1} = 2c_i - a_i`.

declarations.name: sparsePulseInput
declarations.signature: def sparsePulseInput (A : ℤ) (S : Finset ℕ) : ℕ → ℤ | 0 => 0 | i + 1 => sparsePulseLetter A S i /-- Sparse pulses satisfy the forced affine recurrence
declarations.docstring: The one-based input convention used by `affineBinaryOrbit`.

declarations.name: sparsePulse_recurrence
declarations.signature: theorem sparsePulse_recurrence (A : ℤ) (S : Finset ℕ) (i : ℕ) : sparsePulseState A S (i + 1) = 2 * sparsePulseState A S i - sparsePulseLetter A S i
declarations.docstring: Sparse pulses satisfy the forced affine recurrence by construction.

declarations.name: affineBinaryOrbit_sparsePulseInput
declarations.signature: theorem affineBinaryOrbit_sparsePulseInput (A : ℤ) (S : Finset ℕ) : ∀ k : ℕ, affineBinaryOrbit (sparsePulseInput A S) (sparsePulseState A S 0) k = sparsePulseState A S k
declarations.docstring: The synthetic state is exactly an instance of the existing generic `affineBinaryOrbit`; this module does not introduce a second orbit theory.

declarations.name: sparsePulse_dvd_letter
declarations.signature: theorem sparsePulse_dvd_letter (A : ℤ) (S : Finset ℕ) (i : ℕ) : A ∣ sparsePulseLetter A S i
declarations.docstring: Every sparse-pulse letter lies in the principal ideal generated by `A`.

declarations.name: abs_sparsePulseState_le
declarations.signature: theorem abs_sparsePulseState_le (A : ℤ) (S : Finset ℕ) (k : ℕ) : |sparsePulseState A S k| ≤ |A|
declarations.docstring: The state has magnitude at most the pulse amplitude.

declarations.name: abs_sparsePulseLetter_le
declarations.signature: theorem abs_sparsePulseLetter_le (A : ℤ) (S : Finset ℕ) (i : ℕ) : |sparsePulseLetter A S i| ≤ 2 * |A|
declarations.docstring: The forcing letter has magnitude at most twice the pulse amplitude.

declarations.name: dyadicCoboundary
declarations.signature: def dyadicCoboundary (c : ℕ → ℤ) (n : ℕ) : ℤ
declarations.docstring: The zero-based dyadic coboundary `(2 - E)c`.

declarations.name: dyadicClearedPrefix
declarations.signature: def dyadicClearedPrefix (a : ℕ → ℤ) (n : ℕ) : ℕ → ℤ | 0 => 0 | L + 1 => 2 * dyadicClearedPrefix a n L + a (n + L) /-- A dyadic coboundary has exact prefix/terminal-state cancellation at every start and every finite horizon. -/ theorem dyadicClearedPrefix_dyadicCoboundary (c : ℕ → ℤ) (n L : ℕ) : dyadicClearedPrefix (dyadicCoboundary c) n L = (2 : ℤ) ^ L * c n - c (n + L)
declarations.docstring: The depth-`L` cleared binary prefix, accumulated from left to right. Equivalently this is `∑ j < L, a (n+j) * 2^(L-1-j)`.

declarations.name: dyadicClearedPrefix_dyadicCoboundary
declarations.signature: theorem dyadicClearedPrefix_dyadicCoboundary (c : ℕ → ℤ) (n L : ℕ) : dyadicClearedPrefix (dyadicCoboundary c) n L = (2 : ℤ) ^ L * c n - c (n + L)
declarations.docstring: A dyadic coboundary has exact prefix/terminal-state cancellation at every start and every finite horizon.

declarations.name: forwardDifference
declarations.signature: def forwardDifference (h : ℕ) (f : ℕ → ℤ) (n : ℕ) : ℤ
declarations.docstring: Additive forward difference with step `h`.

declarations.name: iteratedForwardDifference
declarations.signature: def iteratedForwardDifference : List ℕ → (ℕ → ℤ) → (ℕ → ℤ) | [], f => f | h :: hs, f => forwardDifference h (iteratedForwardDifference hs f) /-- Additive forward differences commute. -/ theorem forwardDifference_comm (h k : ℕ) (f : ℕ → ℤ) : forwardDifference h (forwardDifference k f) = forwardDifference k (forwardDifference h f)
declarations.docstring: An arbitrary finite-rank additive cube, represented as a list of shift steps. Repeated steps and noncommensurate steps are both allowed.

declarations.name: forwardDifference_comm
declarations.signature: theorem forwardDifference_comm (h k : ℕ) (f : ℕ → ℤ) : forwardDifference h (forwardDifference k f) = forwardDifference k (forwardDifference h f)
declarations.docstring: Additive forward differences commute.

declarations.name: forwardDifference_iteratedForwardDifference
declarations.signature: theorem forwardDifference_iteratedForwardDifference (h : ℕ) (shifts : List ℕ) (f : ℕ → ℤ) : forwardDifference h (iteratedForwardDifference shifts f) = iteratedForwardDifference shifts (forwardDifference h f)
declarations.docstring: One forward difference commutes with an arbitrary finite list of the others.

declarations.name: iteratedForwardDifference_append
declarations.signature: theorem iteratedForwardDifference_append (left right : List ℕ) (f : ℕ → ℤ) : iteratedForwardDifference (left ++ right) f = iteratedForwardDifference left (iteratedForwardDifference right f)
declarations.docstring: Splitting a shift list splits the corresponding operator composition.

declarations.name: iteratedForwardDifference_zero
declarations.signature: theorem iteratedForwardDifference_zero (shifts : List ℕ) : iteratedForwardDifference shifts (fun _ => 0) = fun _ => 0
declarations.docstring: Every finite shift list preserves the zero sequence.

declarations.name: forwardDifference_dyadicCoboundary
declarations.signature: theorem forwardDifference_dyadicCoboundary (h : ℕ) (c : ℕ → ℤ) : forwardDifference h (dyadicCoboundary c) = dyadicCoboundary (forwardDifference h c)
declarations.docstring: One shift difference commutes with the dyadic coboundary.

declarations.name: iteratedForwardDifference_dyadicCoboundary
declarations.signature: theorem iteratedForwardDifference_dyadicCoboundary (shifts : List ℕ) (c : ℕ → ℤ) : iteratedForwardDifference shifts (dyadicCoboundary c) = dyadicCoboundary (iteratedForwardDifference shifts c)
declarations.docstring: Every finite-rank additive cube preserves the coboundary class.

declarations.name: iteratedForwardDifference_dyadicClearedPrefix
declarations.signature: theorem iteratedForwardDifference_dyadicClearedPrefix (shifts : List ℕ) (c : ℕ → ℤ) (n L : ℕ) : dyadicClearedPrefix (iteratedForwardDifference shifts (dyadicCoboundary c)) n L = (2 : ℤ) ^ L * iteratedForwardDifference shifts c n - iteratedForwardDifference shifts c (n + L)
declarations.docstring: Exact all-rank, all-horizon cancellation. The cleared residue of any finite additive cube is precisely the negative terminal transformed state modulo `2^L`.

declarations.name: dvd_iteratedForwardDifference
declarations.signature: theorem dvd_iteratedForwardDifference (d : ℤ) (f : ℕ → ℤ) (hdiv : ∀ n, d ∣ f n) (shifts : List ℕ) (n : ℕ) : d ∣ iteratedForwardDifference shifts f n
declarations.docstring: Principal ideals are preserved by arbitrary finite additive rank.

declarations.name: abs_iteratedForwardDifference_le
declarations.signature: theorem abs_iteratedForwardDifference_le (f : ℕ → ℤ) (B : ℤ) (hB : ∀ n, |f n| ≤ B) (shifts : List ℕ) (n : ℕ) : |iteratedForwardDifference shifts f n| ≤ (2 : ℤ) ^ shifts.length * B
declarations.docstring: A uniform bound grows by at most `2^rank` under a finite additive cube.

declarations.name: shiftLinearCombination
declarations.signature: def shiftLinearCombination : List (ℕ × ℤ) → (ℕ → ℤ) → (ℕ → ℤ) | [], _ => fun _ => 0 | (h, q) :: terms, f => fun n => q * f (n + h) + shiftLinearCombination terms f n /-- The `ℓ1` weight of a finite shift polynomial. -/ def shiftLinearWeight : List (ℕ × ℤ) → ℤ | [] => 0 | (_, q) :: terms => |q| + shiftLinearWeight terms /-- The dyadic coboundary commutes with every finite integer shift polynomial, not only products of forward differences. -/ theorem shiftLinearCombination_dyadicCoboundary (terms : List (ℕ × ℤ)) (c : ℕ → ℤ) :
declarations.docstring: Evaluation of a finite integer shift polynomial on a sequence. A term `(h, q)` contributes `q * f(n+h)`.

declarations.name: shiftLinearWeight
declarations.signature: def shiftLinearWeight : List (ℕ × ℤ) → ℤ | [] => 0 | (_, q) :: terms => |q| + shiftLinearWeight terms /-- The dyadic coboundary commutes with every finite integer shift polynomial, not only products of forward differences. -/ theorem shiftLinearCombination_dyadicCoboundary (terms : List (ℕ × ℤ)) (c : ℕ → ℤ) : shiftLinearCombination terms (dyadicCoboundary c) = dyadicCoboundary (shiftLinearCombination terms c)
declarations.docstring: The `ℓ1` weight of a finite shift polynomial.

declarations.name: shiftLinearCombination_dyadicCoboundary
declarations.signature: theorem shiftLinearCombination_dyadicCoboundary (terms : List (ℕ × ℤ)) (c : ℕ → ℤ) : shiftLinearCombination terms (dyadicCoboundary c) = dyadicCoboundary (shiftLinearCombination terms c)
declarations.docstring: The dyadic coboundary commutes with every finite integer shift polynomial, not only products of forward differences.

declarations.name: shiftLinearCombination_dyadicClearedPrefix
declarations.signature: theorem shiftLinearCombination_dyadicClearedPrefix (terms : List (ℕ × ℤ)) (c : ℕ → ℤ) (n L : ℕ) : dyadicClearedPrefix (shiftLinearCombination terms (dyadicCoboundary c)) n L = (2 : ℤ) ^ L * shiftLinearCombination terms c n - shiftLinearCombination terms c (n + L)
declarations.docstring: Exact prefix/terminal cancellation for an arbitrary finite shift polynomial.

declarations.name: dvd_shiftLinearCombination
declarations.signature: theorem dvd_shiftLinearCombination (d : ℤ) (f : ℕ → ℤ) (hdiv : ∀ n, d ∣ f n) (terms : List (ℕ × ℤ)) (n : ℕ) : d ∣ shiftLinearCombination terms f n
declarations.docstring: Principal ideals are preserved by arbitrary finite integer shift polynomials.

declarations.name: abs_shiftLinearCombination_le
declarations.signature: theorem abs_shiftLinearCombination_le (f : ℕ → ℤ) (B : ℤ) (hB : ∀ n, |f n| ≤ B) (terms : List (ℕ × ℤ)) (n : ℕ) : |shiftLinearCombination terms f n| ≤ shiftLinearWeight terms * B
declarations.docstring: A finite shift polynomial enlarges a uniform bound by at most the `ℓ1`-weight of its coefficients.

declarations.name: mobiusTotientChannel
declarations.docstring: The `d`-th Möbius channel in `φ(n) = ∑_{d∣n} μ(d) (n/d)`, totalized by zero off the divisor progression.

declarations.name: totient_eq_sum_mobiusTotientChannel
declarations.signature: theorem totient_eq_sum_mobiusTotientChannel (n : ℕ) (hn : 0 < n) : (Nat.totient n : ℤ) = ∑ d ∈ n.divisors, mobiusTotientChannel d n
declarations.docstring: The channel definition reconstructs the actual totient on its finite divisor support.

declarations.name: forwardDifference_mobiusTotientChannel_of_dvd
declarations.signature: theorem forwardDifference_mobiusTotientChannel_of_dvd {d h : ℕ} (hd : 0 < d) (hdh : d ∣ h) : forwardDifference h (mobiusTotientChannel d) = fun n => if d ∣ n then ArithmeticFunction.moebius d * ((h / d : ℕ) : ℤ) else 0
declarations.docstring: Along a `d`-divisible shift, the affine Möbius channel becomes a constant multiple of the divisor indicator.

declarations.name: rankTwo_mobiusTotientChannel_eq_zero_of_dvd
declarations.signature: theorem rankTwo_mobiusTotientChannel_eq_zero_of_dvd {d h k : ℕ} (hd : 0 < d) (hdh : d ∣ h) (hdk : d ∣ k) : forwardDifference k (forwardDifference h (mobiusTotientChannel d)) = fun _ => 0
declarations.docstring: **Exact old-channel cancellation.** A Möbius divisor channel vanishes after two shift differences whose axes are both divisible by `d`. Thus only channels not covered by such a divisible pair can contribute to the rank-cube sum; no converse nonvanishing claim is made.

declarations.name: iteratedForwardDifference_mobiusTotientChannel_eq_zero_of_two_dvd
declarations.signature: theorem iteratedForwardDifference_mobiusTotientChannel_eq_zero_of_two_dvd (before between after : List ℕ) {d h k : ℕ} (hd : 0 < d) (hdh : d ∣ h) (hdk : d ∣ k) : iteratedForwardDifference (before ++ (h :: between ++ (k :: after))) (mobiusTotientChannel d) = fun _ => 0
declarations.docstring: **All-rank old-channel cancellation.** Arbitrary shifts may occur before, between, and after two axes divisible by `d`; the whole finite-rank Möbius channel still vanishes. The displayed list split is an exact witness that the two divisible axes occur in the shift cube.

declarations.name: lcmAnchorStates
declarations.docstring: State anchors corresponding to the exact whole-ray letters at `q * periodLcm t`, for `2 ≤ q < t`.

declarations.name: lcmAnchorPulseState
declarations.docstring: The LCM pulse state with amplitude `φ(periodLcm t)`.

declarations.name: lcmAnchorPulseLetter
declarations.docstring: The LCM pulse forcing word.

declarations.name: lcmAnchorPulseLetter_eq_dyadicCoboundary
declarations.signature: theorem lcmAnchorPulseLetter_eq_dyadicCoboundary (t : ℕ) : lcmAnchorPulseLetter t = dyadicCoboundary (lcmAnchorPulseState t)
declarations.docstring: The pulse letter is literally the dyadic coboundary of the pulse state.

declarations.name: lcmAnchorFiniteRankState
declarations.signature: def lcmAnchorFiniteRankState (t : ℕ) (shifts : List ℕ) : ℕ → ℤ
declarations.docstring: The transformed state attached to an arbitrary finite shift cube.

declarations.name: lcmAnchorFiniteRankLetter
declarations.signature: def lcmAnchorFiniteRankLetter (t : ℕ) (shifts : List ℕ) : ℕ → ℤ
declarations.docstring: The transformed pulse letters attached to the same finite shift cube.

declarations.name: lcmAnchorFiniteRankLetter_eq_dyadicCoboundary
declarations.signature: theorem lcmAnchorFiniteRankLetter_eq_dyadicCoboundary (t : ℕ) (shifts : List ℕ) : lcmAnchorFiniteRankLetter t shifts = dyadicCoboundary (lcmAnchorFiniteRankState t shifts)
declarations.docstring: Every finite-rank transformed pulse remains an exact dyadic coboundary.

declarations.name: lcmAnchorFiniteRank_clearedPrefix
declarations.signature: theorem lcmAnchorFiniteRank_clearedPrefix (t : ℕ) (shifts : List ℕ) (n L : ℕ) : dyadicClearedPrefix (lcmAnchorFiniteRankLetter t shifts) n L = (2 : ℤ) ^ L * lcmAnchorFiniteRankState t shifts n - lcmAnchorFiniteRankState t shifts (n + L)
declarations.docstring: Exact all-horizon terminal-state compensation for every finite-rank LCM shift cube (indeed, for arbitrary additive shifts).

declarations.name: lcmAnchorShiftPolynomialState
declarations.signature: def lcmAnchorShiftPolynomialState (t : ℕ) (terms : List (ℕ × ℤ)) : ℕ → ℤ
declarations.docstring: Pulse state transformed by an arbitrary finite integer shift polynomial.

declarations.name: lcmAnchorShiftPolynomialLetter
declarations.signature: def lcmAnchorShiftPolynomialLetter (t : ℕ) (terms : List (ℕ × ℤ)) : ℕ → ℤ
declarations.docstring: Pulse letters transformed by the same shift polynomial.

declarations.name: lcmAnchorShiftPolynomialLetter_eq_dyadicCoboundary
declarations.signature: theorem lcmAnchorShiftPolynomialLetter_eq_dyadicCoboundary (t : ℕ) (terms : List (ℕ × ℤ)) : lcmAnchorShiftPolynomialLetter t terms = dyadicCoboundary (lcmAnchorShiftPolynomialState t terms)
declarations.docstring: Every finite integer shift polynomial preserves the exact coboundary relation.

declarations.name: lcmAnchorShiftPolynomial_clearedPrefix
declarations.signature: theorem lcmAnchorShiftPolynomial_clearedPrefix (t : ℕ) (terms : List (ℕ × ℤ)) (n L : ℕ) : dyadicClearedPrefix (lcmAnchorShiftPolynomialLetter t terms) n L = (2 : ℤ) ^ L * lcmAnchorShiftPolynomialState t terms n - lcmAnchorShiftPolynomialState t terms (n + L)
declarations.docstring: Exact all-horizon terminal-state compensation for every finite shift polynomial, including every commensurate finite-rank LCM cube normal form.

declarations.name: two_le_periodLcm
declarations.signature: lemma two_le_periodLcm {t : ℕ} (ht : 3 ≤ t) : 2 ≤ periodLcm t

declarations.name: lcmAnchorStates_mem
declarations.signature: lemma lcmAnchorStates_mem {t q : ℕ} (hq2 : 2 ≤ q) (hqt : q < t) : (q - 1) * periodLcm t ∈ lcmAnchorStates t

declarations.name: lcmAnchorStates_pairwise_gap
declarations.signature: theorem lcmAnchorStates_pairwise_gap {t x y : ℕ} (ht : 3 ≤ t) (hx : x ∈ lcmAnchorStates t) (hy : y ∈ lcmAnchorStates t) (hxy : x ≠ y) : x + 2 ≤ y ∨ y + 2 ≤ x
declarations.docstring: Distinct LCM state anchors are separated by at least two positions.

declarations.name: lcmAnchorStates_succ_not_mem
declarations.signature: lemma lcmAnchorStates_succ_not_mem {t x : ℕ} (ht : 3 ≤ t) (hx : x ∈ lcmAnchorStates t) : x + 1 ∉ lcmAnchorStates t

declarations.name: lcmAnchorStates_pred_not_mem
declarations.signature: lemma lcmAnchorStates_pred_not_mem {t x : ℕ} (ht : 3 ≤ t) (hx : x ∈ lcmAnchorStates t) (hxpos : 0 < x) : x - 1 ∉ lcmAnchorStates t

declarations.name: lcmAnchorPulseLetter_eq
declarations.signature: theorem lcmAnchorPulseLetter_eq (t i : ℕ) (ht : 3 ≤ t) : lcmAnchorPulseLetter t i = if i + 1 ∈ lcmAnchorStates t then (Nat.totient (periodLcm t) : ℤ) else if i ∈ lcmAnchorStates t then -2 * (Nat.totient (periodLcm t) : ℤ) else 0
declarations.docstring: Explicit support formula: an anchor-entry letter is `A`, an anchor-exit letter is `-2A`, and every other letter is zero.

declarations.name: lcmAnchorPulse_letter_at_anchor
declarations.signature: theorem lcmAnchorPulse_letter_at_anchor {t q : ℕ} (ht : 3 ≤ t) (hq2 : 2 ≤ q) (hqt : q < t) : lcmAnchorPulseLetter t ((q - 1) * periodLcm t - 1) = (Nat.totient (periodLcm t) : ℤ)
declarations.docstring: The pulse letter entering every prescribed state anchor is exactly `φ(H)`.

declarations.name: lcmAnchorPulse_letter_after_anchor
declarations.signature: theorem lcmAnchorPulse_letter_after_anchor {t q : ℕ} (ht : 3 ≤ t) (hq2 : 2 ≤ q) (hqt : q < t) : lcmAnchorPulseLetter t ((q - 1) * periodLcm t) = -2 * (Nat.totient (periodLcm t) : ℤ)
declarations.docstring: The compensation letter immediately after every anchor is `-2φ(H)`.

declarations.name: totient_mul_eq_left_mul_totient_of_dvd
declarations.signature: theorem totient_mul_eq_left_mul_totient_of_dvd {q H : ℕ} (hqH : q ∣ H) : Nat.totient (q * H) = q * Nat.totient H
declarations.docstring: If `q ∣ H`, multiplying `H` by `q` adds only already-present prime factors, so the totient scales by exactly `q`.

declarations.name: deltaTotient_periodLcm_anchor
declarations.signature: theorem deltaTotient_periodLcm_anchor {t q : ℕ} (hq2 : 2 ≤ q) (hqt : q < t) : deltaTotient (periodLcm t) (q * periodLcm t) = (Nat.totient (periodLcm t) : ℤ)
declarations.docstring: Every prescribed whole-ray forcing letter is an exact arithmetic anchor.

declarations.name: lcmAnchorPulse_letter_eq_deltaTotient
declarations.signature: theorem lcmAnchorPulse_letter_eq_deltaTotient {t q : ℕ} (ht : 3 ≤ t) (hq2 : 2 ≤ q) (hqt : q < t) : lcmAnchorPulseLetter t ((q - 1) * periodLcm t - 1) = deltaTotient (periodLcm t) (q * periodLcm t)
declarations.docstring: The synthetic word agrees exactly with every sparse whole-ray anchor.

declarations.name: lcmAnchorPulse_recurrence
declarations.signature: theorem lcmAnchorPulse_recurrence (t i : ℕ) : lcmAnchorPulseState t (i + 1) = 2 * lcmAnchorPulseState t i - lcmAnchorPulseLetter t i
declarations.docstring: The synthetic word agrees exactly with every sparse whole-ray anchor. -/ theorem lcmAnchorPulse_letter_eq_deltaTotient {t q : ℕ} (ht : 3 ≤ t) (hq2 : 2 ≤ q) (hqt : q < t) : lcmAnchorPulseLetter t ((q - 1) * periodLcm t - 1) = deltaTotient (periodLcm t) (q * periodLcm t) := by rw [lcmAnchorPulse_letter_at_anchor ht hq2 hqt, deltaTotient_periodLcm_anchor hq2 hqt] /-! ## Bounds, factor ideals, and the formal no-go

declarations.name: lcmAnchorPulse_dvd_totient_periodLcm
declarations.signature: theorem lcmAnchorPulse_dvd_totient_periodLcm (t i : ℕ) : (Nat.totient (periodLcm t) : ℤ) ∣ lcmAnchorPulseLetter t i

declarations.name: lcmAnchorPulse_dvd_totient_of_dvd
declarations.signature: theorem lcmAnchorPulse_dvd_totient_of_dvd {t j : ℕ} (hj : j ∣ periodLcm t) (i : ℕ) : (Nat.totient j : ℤ) ∣ lcmAnchorPulseLetter t i
declarations.docstring: Every lower factor ideal coming from a divisor `j ∣ H` is absorbed by the pulse word.

declarations.name: lcmAnchorFiniteRank_dvd_totient_periodLcm
declarations.signature: theorem lcmAnchorFiniteRank_dvd_totient_periodLcm (t : ℕ) (shifts : List ℕ) (i : ℕ) : (Nat.totient (periodLcm t) : ℤ) ∣ lcmAnchorFiniteRankLetter t shifts i
declarations.docstring: Finite shift rank preserves divisibility by the scale totient.

declarations.name: lcmAnchorFiniteRank_dvd_totient_of_dvd
declarations.signature: theorem lcmAnchorFiniteRank_dvd_totient_of_dvd {t j : ℕ} (hj : j ∣ periodLcm t) (shifts : List ℕ) (i : ℕ) : (Nat.totient j : ℤ) ∣ lcmAnchorFiniteRankLetter t shifts i
declarations.docstring: Every lower factor ideal remains absorbed after arbitrary finite shift rank.

declarations.name: lcmAnchorFiniteRank_state_bound
declarations.signature: theorem lcmAnchorFiniteRank_state_bound (t : ℕ) (shifts : List ℕ) (i : ℕ) : |lcmAnchorFiniteRankState t shifts i| ≤ (2 : ℤ) ^ shifts.length * |(Nat.totient (periodLcm t) : ℤ)|
declarations.docstring: Generic rank bound for the transformed pulse state. Sharper commensurate bounds are evaluator-level refinements; this symbolic bound is uniform over arbitrary shifts.

declarations.name: lcmAnchorFiniteRank_letter_bound
declarations.signature: theorem lcmAnchorFiniteRank_letter_bound (t : ℕ) (shifts : List ℕ) (i : ℕ) : |lcmAnchorFiniteRankLetter t shifts i| ≤ (2 : ℤ) ^ shifts.length * (2 * |(Nat.totient (periodLcm t) : ℤ)|)
declarations.docstring: Generic rank bound for the transformed pulse letters.

declarations.name: lcmAnchorShiftPolynomial_dvd_totient_periodLcm
declarations.signature: theorem lcmAnchorShiftPolynomial_dvd_totient_periodLcm (t : ℕ) (terms : List (ℕ × ℤ)) (i : ℕ) : (Nat.totient (periodLcm t) : ℤ) ∣ lcmAnchorShiftPolynomialLetter t terms i
declarations.docstring: Finite shift polynomials preserve divisibility by the scale totient.

declarations.name: lcmAnchorShiftPolynomial_dvd_totient_of_dvd
declarations.signature: theorem lcmAnchorShiftPolynomial_dvd_totient_of_dvd {t j : ℕ} (hj : j ∣ periodLcm t) (terms : List (ℕ × ℤ)) (i : ℕ) : (Nat.totient j : ℤ) ∣ lcmAnchorShiftPolynomialLetter t terms i
declarations.docstring: Every lower factor ideal remains absorbed under a finite shift polynomial.

declarations.name: lcmAnchorShiftPolynomial_state_bound
declarations.signature: theorem lcmAnchorShiftPolynomial_state_bound (t : ℕ) (terms : List (ℕ × ℤ)) (i : ℕ) : |lcmAnchorShiftPolynomialState t terms i| ≤ shiftLinearWeight terms * |(Nat.totient (periodLcm t) : ℤ)|
declarations.docstring: Uniform state bound for a transformed shift polynomial.

declarations.name: lcmAnchorShiftPolynomial_letter_bound
declarations.signature: theorem lcmAnchorShiftPolynomial_letter_bound (t : ℕ) (terms : List (ℕ × ℤ)) (i : ℕ) : |lcmAnchorShiftPolynomialLetter t terms i| ≤ shiftLinearWeight terms * (2 * |(Nat.totient (periodLcm t) : ℤ)|)
declarations.docstring: Uniform letter bound for a transformed shift polynomial.

declarations.name: lcmAnchorPulse_letter_bound
declarations.signature: theorem lcmAnchorPulse_letter_bound (t i : ℕ) : |lcmAnchorPulseLetter t i| ≤ (2 * periodLcm t + i + 1 : ℕ)

declarations.name: lcmAnchorPulse_strict_survivor
declarations.signature: theorem lcmAnchorPulse_strict_survivor (t i : ℕ) : |lcmAnchorPulseState t i| < (2 * periodLcm t + i + 2 : ℕ)
declarations.docstring: The synthetic state remains strictly inside every diagonal carry strip.

declarations.name: lcm_factorIdeal_finiteRank_shiftAlgebra_not_sufficient
declarations.signature: theorem lcm_factorIdeal_finiteRank_shiftAlgebra_not_sufficient (t : ℕ) (ht : 3 ≤ t) : ∃ c a : ℕ → ℤ, (∃ k, c k ≠ 0) ∧ (∀ i, a i = dyadicCoboundary c i) ∧ ∀ terms : List (ℕ × ℤ), ∃ d b : ℕ → ℤ, (∀ i, d i = shiftLinearCombination terms c i) ∧ (∀ i, b i = shiftLinearCombination terms a i) ∧ (∀ i, b i = dyadicCoboundary d i) ∧ (∀ n L, dyadicClearedPrefix b n L = (2 : ℤ) ^ L * d n - d (n + L)) ∧ (∀ i, (Nat.totient (periodLcm t) : ℤ) ∣ b i) ∧ (∀ j, j ∣ periodLcm t → ∀ i, (Nat.totient j : ℤ) ∣ b i) ∧
declarations.docstring: **All-rank shift-algebra no-go.** For every `t ≥ 3`, one nonzero synthetic pulse simultaneously compensates every finite integer shift polynomial. Each transformed word remains a dyadic coboundary, every cleared prefix is cancelled exactly by its terminal transformed state, every lower factor ideal is inherited, and uniform coefficient-weight bounds remain available. The shifts need not be commensurate. Hence the theorem covers every finite family of rank-two/rank-three LCM cubes, arbitrary finite rank, and each entry of a matrix built from finitely many such linear observations. It makes no claim about nonlinear combinations such as determinants, and it does not assert that the base or transformed letters are actual totient differences.

declarations.name: LcmFactorIdealAnchorAdmissible
declarations.signature: def LcmFactorIdealAnchorAdmissible (t : ℕ) (c a : ℕ → ℤ) : Prop
declarations.docstring: The exact hypothesis class defeated by the sparse pulse.

declarations.name: lcm_factorIdeal_sparseAnchor_not_sufficient
declarations.signature: theorem lcm_factorIdeal_sparseAnchor_not_sufficient (t : ℕ) (ht : 3 ≤ t) : ∃ c a : ℕ → ℤ, LcmFactorIdealAnchorAdmissible t c a ∧ (∃ k, c k ≠ 0) ∧ ∀ K i, i ≤ K → |c i| < (2 * periodLcm t + i + 2 : ℕ)
declarations.docstring: **LCM factor-ideal sparse-anchor no-go.** For every `t ≥ 3` there is a nonzero synthetic carry orbit satisfying the exact affine recurrence, all homogeneous lower factor ideals, the natural letter and strip bounds, and every prescribed whole-ray anchor. It survives every finite horizon. This does not assert arithmetic realisability of the compensation letters; it proves that the named factor-only hypotheses are not sufficient.

declarations.name: lcmAnchorPulse_t3_letters
declarations.docstring: **LCM factor-ideal sparse-anchor no-go.** For every `t ≥ 3` there is a nonzero synthetic carry orbit satisfying the exact affine recurrence, all homogeneous lower factor ideals, the natural letter and strip bounds, and every prescribed whole-ray anchor. It survives every finite horizon. This does not assert arithmetic realisability of the compensation letters; it proves that the named factor-only hypotheses are not sufficient. -/ theorem lcm_factorIdeal_sparseAnchor_not_sufficient (t : ℕ) (ht : 3 ≤ t) : ∃ c a : ℕ → ℤ, LcmFactorIdealAnchorAdmissible t c a ∧ (∃ k, c k ≠ 0) ∧ ∀ K i, i ≤ K → |c i| < (2 * periodLcm t + i + 2 : ℕ) := by refine ⟨lcmAnchorPulseState t, lcmAnchorPulseLetter t, ?_, ?_, ?_⟩ · exact ⟨lcmAnchorPulse_recurrence t, lcmAnchorPulse_dvd_totient_periodLcm t, fun j hj i => lcmAnchorPulse_dvd_totient_of_dvd hj i, lcmAnchorPulse_letter_bound t, lcmAnchorPulse_strict_survivor t, fun q hq2 hqt => lcmAnchorPulse_letter_eq_deltaTotient ht hq2 hqt⟩ · let x := periodLcm t have hx

declarations.name: lcmAnchorPulse_t3_strict_survivor
declarations.signature: theorem lcmAnchorPulse_t3_strict_survivor (i : ℕ) : |lcmAnchorPulseState 3 i| < (12 + i + 2 : ℕ)

declarations.name: primePowerLayer
declarations.signature: def primePowerLayer (p e : ℕ) (g : ℕ → ℤ) (n : ℕ) : ℤ
declarations.docstring: The signed coefficient layer between exact `p`-adic levels `e-1` and `e`.

declarations.name: mixedPrimePowerLayerTwo
declarations.signature: def mixedPrimePowerLayerTwo (p e q f : ℕ) (g : ℕ → ℤ) (n : ℕ) : ℤ
declarations.docstring: The two-signature mixed layer. Further list-level iteration can use this as its checked algebraic step without committing to a factorization API.

declarations.name: primePowerLayer_comm
declarations.signature: theorem primePowerLayer_comm (p e q f : ℕ) (g : ℕ → ℤ) (n : ℕ) : primePowerLayer q f (primePowerLayer p e g) n = primePowerLayer p e (primePowerLayer q f g) n
declarations.docstring: Prime-power layer operators commute as integer finite differences.

declarations.name: mixedPrimePowerLayerTwo_supportCoeffInt
declarations.signature: theorem mixedPrimePowerLayerTwo_supportCoeffInt (A : Set ℕ) {p e q f n : ℕ} (hp : p.Prime) (he : 0 < e) (hq : q.Prime) (hf : 0 < f) (hpq : p ≠ q) (hn : n.Coprime (p * q)) : mixedPrimePowerLayerTwo p e q f (supportCoeffInt A) n = supportCoeffInt (exactPrimePowerPullback q f (exactPrimePowerPullback p e A)) n
declarations.docstring: Two distinct exact prime-power layers extract the iterated exact-valuation pullback. This is the mixed repeated-prime algebra missing from the distinct-prime dilation owner.

declarations.name: mixedPrimePowerLayerTwo_twelve_fixture
declarations.docstring: Regression for the mixed `2^2`/`3` signature: the singleton support `{12}` contributes exactly one at residual argument `1`.

declarations.name: summable_lambert_pair
declarations.signature: theorem summable_lambert_pair (w v : ℕ → ℝ) (hw : ∀ d : ℕ, 0 < d → |w d| ≤ (d : ℝ)) (hv : ∀ m : ℕ, 0 < m → |v m| ≤ (m : ℝ)) {r : ℝ} (hr0 : 0 ≤ r) (hr1 : r < 1) : Summable (fun p : ℕ+ × ℕ+ => w (p.1 : ℕ) * v (p.2 : ℕ) * r ^ ((p.1 : ℕ) * (p.2 : ℕ)))
declarations.docstring: Summability of the product-weighted geometric family over `ℕ+ × ℕ+` for weights of at most linear growth. The majorant is AM–GM: `|w(d)·v(m)| ≤ d·m ≤ d² + m²`, and each square rides Mathlib's `summable_prod_mul_pow` (once directly, once through `Prod.swap`).

declarations.name: tsum_lambert_pair_regroup
declarations.signature: theorem tsum_lambert_pair_regroup (w v : ℕ → ℝ) (hw : ∀ d : ℕ, 0 < d → |w d| ≤ (d : ℝ)) (hv : ∀ m : ℕ, 0 < m → |v m| ≤ (m : ℝ)) {r : ℝ} (hr0 : 0 ≤ r) (hr1 : r < 1) : ∑' p : ℕ+ × ℕ+, w (p.1 : ℕ) * v (p.2 : ℕ) * r ^ ((p.1 : ℕ) * (p.2 : ℕ)) = ∑' n : ℕ+, (∑ e ∈ (n : ℕ).divisors, w e * v ((n : ℕ) / e)) * r ^ (n : ℕ)
declarations.docstring: **Signed weighted product-to-divisor-sum rearrangement** with product weight `w(d)·v(m)`, both of at most linear growth on positive arguments. The double series over `ℕ+ × ℕ+` regroups along divisor antidiagonals into `∑_n (∑_{e∣n} w(e)·v(n/e))·rⁿ` — the Dirichlet convolution appears as the coefficient. Signed analogue of the kernel's `[0,1]`-weight identity.

declarations.name: tsum_lambert_linear_weight
declarations.signature: theorem tsum_lambert_linear_weight (w : ℕ → ℝ) (hw : ∀ d : ℕ, 0 < d → |w d| ≤ (d : ℝ)) {r : ℝ} (hr0 : 0 ≤ r) (hr1 : r < 1) : ∑' d : ℕ+, w (d : ℕ) * (r ^ (d : ℕ) / (1 - r ^ (d : ℕ))) = ∑' n : ℕ+, (∑ e ∈ (n : ℕ).divisors, w e) * r ^ (n : ℕ)
declarations.docstring: **Signed linear-growth Lambert series identity**: `∑_d w(d)·r^d/(1-r^d) = ∑_n (∑_{e∣n} w(e))·rⁿ` for `|w(d)| ≤ d`.

declarations.name: tsum_lambert_linear_weight_sq
declarations.signature: theorem tsum_lambert_linear_weight_sq (w : ℕ → ℝ) (hw : ∀ d : ℕ, 0 < d → |w d| ≤ (d : ℝ)) {r : ℝ} (hr0 : 0 ≤ r) (hr1 : r < 1) : ∑' d : ℕ+, w (d : ℕ) * (r ^ (d : ℕ) / (1 - r ^ (d : ℕ)) ^ 2) = ∑' n : ℕ+, (∑ e ∈ (n : ℕ).divisors, w e * (((n : ℕ) / e : ℕ) : ℝ)) * r ^ (n : ℕ)
declarations.docstring: **Signed linear-growth squared-Lambert identity**: `∑_d w(d)·r^d/(1-r^d)² = ∑_n (∑_{e∣n} w(e)·(n/e))·rⁿ` for `|w(d)| ≤ d`. The inner geometric weight `m` is the derivative structure that makes the divisor coefficient a genuine Dirichlet convolution against `Id`.

declarations.name: totientZ
declarations.docstring: The Euler totient as an integer-valued arithmetic function.

declarations.name: totientZ_apply
declarations.signature: @[simp] theorem totientZ_apply (n : ℕ) : totientZ n = (Nat.totient n : ℤ)

declarations.name: primWeight
declarations.docstring: **The primitive-conductor weight** `A = φ * μ` (Dirichlet convolution). `A(n)` counts the primitive Dirichlet characters of conductor `n` (OEIS A007431); it is multiplicative, nonnegative, vanishes exactly on `n ≡ 2 (mod 4)`, and satisfies `A(p) = p - 2`, `A(p^e) = (p-1)²·p^(e-2)`.

declarations.name: primWeight_mul_zeta
declarations.signature: theorem primWeight_mul_zeta : primWeight * (↑(zeta : ArithmeticFunction ℕ) : ArithmeticFunction ℤ) = totientZ
declarations.docstring: `A * ζ = φ`: summing the primitive-conductor weight over divisors recovers the totient — the inversion that makes `L(A) = S`.

declarations.name: sum_divisors_primWeight
declarations.signature: theorem sum_divisors_primWeight (n : ℕ) : ∑ e ∈ n.divisors, primWeight e = (Nat.totient n : ℤ)

declarations.name: primWeight_apply_prime_pow
declarations.signature: theorem primWeight_apply_prime_pow {p : ℕ} (hp : p.Prime) {k : ℕ} (hk : k ≠ 0) : primWeight (p ^ k) = (Nat.totient (p ^ k) : ℤ) - (Nat.totient (p ^ (k - 1)) : ℤ)
declarations.docstring: Prime-power values: `A(p^k) = φ(p^k) - φ(p^(k-1))`. Only the divisor pairs `(p^k, 1)` and `(p^(k-1), p)` survive the Möbius factor.

declarations.name: primWeight_apply_prime
declarations.signature: theorem primWeight_apply_prime {p : ℕ} (hp : p.Prime) : primWeight p = (p : ℤ) - 2

declarations.name: primWeight_two
declarations.docstring: The weight vanishes at `2` — primitive characters mod 2 do not exist. This is the single local zero: `A(n) = 0` iff `n ≡ 2 (mod 4)`.

declarations.name: primWeight_nonneg
declarations.docstring: **Nonnegativity of the primitive-conductor weight.** Each prime-power factor is `φ(p^k) - φ(p^(k-1)) ≥ 0` since `φ` respects divisibility.

declarations.name: primWeight_le_totient
declarations.signature: theorem primWeight_le_totient (n : ℕ) : primWeight n ≤ (Nat.totient n : ℤ)
declarations.docstring: Upper growth: `A(n) ≤ φ(n)` (immediate from `A ≥ 0` and `A * ζ = φ`).

declarations.name: abs_moebius_le_one
declarations.docstring: Upper growth: `A(n) ≤ φ(n)` (immediate from `A ≥ 0` and `A * ζ = φ`). -/ theorem primWeight_le_totient (n : ℕ) : primWeight n ≤ (Nat.totient n : ℤ) := by rcases Nat.eq_zero_or_pos n with rfl | hn · simp · calc primWeight n ≤ ∑ e ∈ n.divisors, primWeight e := Finset.single_le_sum (fun e _ => primWeight_nonneg e) (Nat.mem_divisors_self n hn.ne') _ = (Nat.totient n : ℤ) := sum_divisors_primWeight n /-! ## Möbius divisor sums

declarations.name: sum_divisors_moebius
declarations.signature: theorem sum_divisors_moebius (n : ℕ) : ∑ e ∈ n.divisors, moebius e = if n = 1 then 1 else 0

declarations.name: sum_divisors_moebius_mul_div
declarations.signature: theorem sum_divisors_moebius_mul_div (n : ℕ) (hn : 0 < n) : ∑ e ∈ n.divisors, moebius e * ((n / e : ℕ) : ℤ) = (Nat.totient n : ℤ)
declarations.docstring: Möbius inversion of `∑_{d∣n} φ(d) = n`: the totient as the `μ * Id` convolution, in exactly the divisor-sum shape the squared Lambert engine emits.

declarations.name: tsum_totient_lambert
declarations.signature: theorem tsum_totient_lambert {r : ℝ} (hr0 : 0 ≤ r) (hr1 : r < 1) : ∑' d : ℕ+, (Nat.totient (d : ℕ) : ℝ) * (r ^ (d : ℕ) / (1 - r ^ (d : ℕ))) = ∑' n : ℕ+, ((n : ℕ) : ℝ) * r ^ (n : ℕ)
declarations.docstring: **`L(φ)`-rung, Lambert form**: `∑ φ(d)·r^d/(1-r^d) = ∑ n·rⁿ`. The totient Lambert series collapses to the rational function `r/(1-r)²`.

declarations.name: tsum_moebius_lambert
declarations.signature: theorem tsum_moebius_lambert {r : ℝ} (hr0 : 0 ≤ r) (hr1 : r < 1) : ∑' d : ℕ+, ((moebius (d : ℕ) : ℤ) : ℝ) * (r ^ (d : ℕ) / (1 - r ^ (d : ℕ))) = r
declarations.docstring: **`L(μ)`-rung, Lambert form**: `∑ μ(d)·r^d/(1-r^d) = r`. Only the `n = 1` divisor sum survives (`μ * ζ = ε`).

declarations.name: tsum_primWeight_lambert
declarations.signature: theorem tsum_primWeight_lambert {r : ℝ} (hr0 : 0 ≤ r) (hr1 : r < 1) : ∑' d : ℕ+, ((primWeight (d : ℕ) : ℤ) : ℝ) * (r ^ (d : ℕ) / (1 - r ^ (d : ℕ))) = ∑' n : ℕ+, (Nat.totient (n : ℕ) : ℝ) * r ^ (n : ℕ)
declarations.docstring: **The positive lift, Lambert form**: `∑ A(d)·r^d/(1-r^d) = ∑ φ(n)·rⁿ`. The `#249` power series IS the Mersenne–Lambert transform of the nonnegative primitive-conductor weight (`A * ζ = φ`).

declarations.name: tsum_moebius_lambert_sq
declarations.signature: theorem tsum_moebius_lambert_sq {r : ℝ} (hr0 : 0 ≤ r) (hr1 : r < 1) : ∑' d : ℕ+, ((moebius (d : ℕ) : ℤ) : ℝ) * (r ^ (d : ℕ) / (1 - r ^ (d : ℕ)) ^ 2) = ∑' n : ℕ+, (Nat.totient (n : ℕ) : ℝ) * r ^ (n : ℕ)
declarations.docstring: **Möbius-square lens, Lambert form**: `∑ μ(d)·r^d/(1-r^d)² = ∑ φ(n)·rⁿ`. The `#249` power series as a signed squared-Mersenne Lambert series (`μ * Id = φ`).

declarations.name: two_pow_sub_one_pos
declarations.signature: private lemma two_pow_sub_one_pos (d : ℕ) (hd : 0 < d) : (0 : ℝ) < (2 : ℝ) ^ d - 1
declarations.docstring: **Möbius-square lens, Lambert form**: `∑ μ(d)·r^d/(1-r^d)² = ∑ φ(n)·rⁿ`. The `#249` power series as a signed squared-Mersenne Lambert series (`μ * Id = φ`). -/ theorem tsum_moebius_lambert_sq {r : ℝ} (hr0 : 0 ≤ r) (hr1 : r < 1) : ∑' d : ℕ+, ((moebius (d : ℕ) : ℤ) : ℝ) * (r ^ (d : ℕ) / (1 - r ^ (d : ℕ)) ^ 2) = ∑' n : ℕ+, (Nat.totient (n : ℕ) : ℝ) * r ^ (n : ℕ) := by rw [tsum_lambert_linear_weight_sq (fun e => ((moebius e : ℤ) : ℝ)) (fun d hd => by show |((moebius d : ℤ) : ℝ)| ≤ (d : ℝ) have h1 : |((moebius d : ℤ) : ℝ)| ≤ 1 := by rw [← Int.cast_abs] exact_mod_cast abs_moebius_le_one d exact h1.trans (by exact_mod_cast hd)) hr0 hr1] refine tsum_congr fun n => ?_ congr 1 have h := congrArg (fun z : ℤ => (z : ℝ)) (sum_divisors_moebius_mul_div (n : ℕ) n.pos) push_cast at h simpa using h /-! ## Evaluations at `r = 1/2`: the Mersenne forms

declarations.name: half_pow_term
declarations.signature: private lemma half_pow_term (d : ℕ) (hd : 0 < d) : ((1 : ℝ) / 2) ^ d / (1 - ((1 : ℝ) / 2) ^ d) = 1 / ((2 : ℝ) ^ d - 1)

declarations.name: half_pow_sq_term
declarations.signature: private lemma half_pow_sq_term (d : ℕ) (hd : 0 < d) : ((1 : ℝ) / 2) ^ d / (1 - ((1 : ℝ) / 2) ^ d) ^ 2 = (2 : ℝ) ^ d / ((2 : ℝ) ^ d - 1) ^ 2

declarations.name: mersenne_split
declarations.signature: private lemma mersenne_split (d : ℕ) (hd : 0 < d) : (2 : ℝ) ^ d / ((2 : ℝ) ^ d - 1) ^ 2 = 1 / ((2 : ℝ) ^ d - 1) + 1 / ((2 : ℝ) ^ d - 1) ^ 2

declarations.name: tsum_pnat_mul_half_pow
declarations.signature: private lemma tsum_pnat_mul_half_pow : ∑' n : ℕ+, ((n : ℕ) : ℝ) * ((1 : ℝ) / 2) ^ (n : ℕ) = 2
declarations.docstring: `∑_{n≥1} n/2ⁿ = 2` — the rational value the totient Lambert series hits.

declarations.name: tsum_totient_div_two_pow_sub_one_eq_two
declarations.signature: theorem tsum_totient_div_two_pow_sub_one_eq_two : ∑' d : ℕ+, (Nat.totient (d : ℕ) : ℝ) / ((2 : ℝ) ^ (d : ℕ) - 1) = 2
declarations.docstring: **Rational rung `L(φ) = 2`**: `∑_{d≥1} φ(d)/(2^d - 1) = 2`, exactly. The Lambert transform of the `#249` coefficients at the `#249` point is rational — the entire difficulty of #249 lives on the power-series side.

declarations.name: tsum_moebius_div_two_pow_sub_one_eq_half
declarations.signature: theorem tsum_moebius_div_two_pow_sub_one_eq_half : ∑' d : ℕ+, ((moebius (d : ℕ) : ℤ) : ℝ) / ((2 : ℝ) ^ (d : ℕ) - 1) = 1 / 2
declarations.docstring: **Rational rung `L(μ) = 1/2`**: `∑_{d≥1} μ(d)/(2^d - 1) = 1/2`, exactly.

declarations.name: tsum_primWeight_div_two_pow_sub_one
declarations.signature: theorem tsum_primWeight_div_two_pow_sub_one : ∑' d : ℕ+, ((primWeight (d : ℕ) : ℤ) : ℝ) / ((2 : ℝ) ^ (d : ℕ) - 1) = ∑' n : ℕ+, (Nat.totient (n : ℕ) : ℝ) * ((1 : ℝ) / 2) ^ (n : ℕ)
declarations.docstring: **The positive lift at `1/2`**: `∑_{d≥1} A(d)/(2^d - 1) = ∑_{n≥1} φ(n)·(1/2)ⁿ`. The `#249` constant IS a positive Erdős–Borwein-type constant: same shape as `E = ∑ 1/(2^d - 1)` (irrational, machine-checked in the kernel), with the weight `1` replaced by the primitive-conductor count `A ≥ 0`.

declarations.name: summable_moebius_div_mersenne
declarations.signature: private lemma summable_moebius_div_mersenne : Summable (fun d : ℕ+ => ((moebius (d : ℕ) : ℤ) : ℝ) / ((2 : ℝ) ^ (d : ℕ) - 1))

declarations.name: summable_moebius_div_mersenne_sq
declarations.signature: theorem summable_moebius_div_mersenne_sq : Summable (fun d : ℕ+ => ((moebius (d : ℕ) : ℤ) : ℝ) / ((2 : ℝ) ^ (d : ℕ) - 1) ^ 2)
declarations.docstring: Absolute convergence of the squared-Mersenne Möbius series. This is public because finite-enclosure owners need to split the series at a cutoff without duplicating the Lambert-ladder convergence proof.

declarations.name: tsum_totient_half_pow_eq_half_add_moebius_sq
declarations.signature: theorem tsum_totient_half_pow_eq_half_add_moebius_sq : ∑' n : ℕ+, (Nat.totient (n : ℕ) : ℝ) * ((1 : ℝ) / 2) ^ (n : ℕ) = 1 / 2 + ∑' d : ℕ+, ((moebius (d : ℕ) : ℤ) : ℝ) / ((2 : ℝ) ^ (d : ℕ) - 1) ^ 2
declarations.docstring: **The Möbius-square lens at `1/2`**: `∑ φ(n)·(1/2)ⁿ = 1/2 + ∑_{d≥1} μ(d)/(2^d - 1)²`.

declarations.name: baseTwoRepunit
declarations.docstring: The natural geometric sum appearing after evaluating a cyclic subgroup sum at `X = 2`.

declarations.name: baseTwoRepunit_mul
declarations.signature: private theorem baseTwoRepunit_mul (r q : ℕ) : baseTwoRepunit r q * (2 ^ (r / q) - 1) = (2 ^ (r / q)) ^ q - 1

declarations.name: localNumeratorValue_eq
declarations.signature: theorem localNumeratorValue_eq (r q : ℕ) : localNumeratorValue r q = (q : ℤ) * (baseTwoRepunit r q : ℕ) - 1
declarations.docstring: Concrete evaluation of the local numerator factor.

declarations.name: localCofactorValue_eq
declarations.signature: theorem localCofactorValue_eq (r q : ℕ) : localCofactorValue r q = (q : ℤ) * (baseTwoRepunit r q : ℕ) - ((q : ℤ) ^ 2 - 1)
declarations.docstring: Concrete evaluation of the integral local cofactor.

declarations.name: baseTwoRepunit_coprime_prime_sq_sub_one
declarations.signature: theorem baseTwoRepunit_coprime_prime_sq_sub_one {r q : ℕ} (hq : q.Prime) (hqodd : q ≠ 2) : Nat.Coprime (baseTwoRepunit r q) (q ^ 2 - 1)
declarations.docstring: For odd prime `q`, the evaluated subgroup sum is coprime to `q² - 1`. The proof uses the order of `2^(r/q)` modulo a hypothetical common prime.

declarations.name: localCofactorValue_coprime_mersenne
declarations.signature: theorem localCofactorValue_coprime_mersenne {r q : ℕ} (hq : q.Prime) (hqodd : q ≠ 2) (hqr : q ∣ r) : Nat.Coprime (localCofactorValue r q).natAbs (RadicalMobiusShadow.mersenne r)
declarations.docstring: Every odd-prime evaluated local cofactor is coprime to the full Mersenne modulus.

declarations.name: localCofactorValue_isUnit_mod_mersenne
declarations.signature: theorem localCofactorValue_isUnit_mod_mersenne {r q : ℕ} (hq : q.Prime) (hqodd : q ≠ 2) (hqr : q ∣ r) : IsUnit (localCofactorValue r q : ZMod (RadicalMobiusShadow.mersenne r))
declarations.docstring: Unit form of `localCofactorValue_coprime_mersenne`.

declarations.name: localNumeratorValue_two_coprime_mersenne
declarations.signature: theorem localNumeratorValue_two_coprime_mersenne {r : ℕ} (hr : Squarefree r) (h2r : 2 ∣ r) : Nat.Coprime (localNumeratorValue r 2).natAbs (RadicalMobiusShadow.mersenne r)
declarations.docstring: At the binary prime, squarefreeness makes `r / 2` odd, and the numerator factor `2 * 2^(r/2) + 1` is coprime to the full Mersenne modulus.

declarations.name: localNumeratorValue_two_isUnit_mod_mersenne
declarations.signature: theorem localNumeratorValue_two_isUnit_mod_mersenne {r : ℕ} (hr : Squarefree r) (h2r : 2 ∣ r) : IsUnit (localNumeratorValue r 2 : ZMod (RadicalMobiusShadow.mersenne r))
declarations.docstring: Unit form of the squarefree binary numerator theorem.

declarations.name: localNumeratorValue_four_gcd
declarations.docstring: Squarefreeness is essential: at `r = 4`, the binary numerator factor has gcd `3` with `2^4 - 1`.

declarations.name: localNumerator_mul_localCofactor_mod_mersenne
declarations.signature: theorem localNumerator_mul_localCofactor_mod_mersenne {r q : ℕ} (hqr : q ∣ r) : (localNumeratorValue r q : ZMod (RadicalMobiusShadow.mersenne r)) * localCofactorValue r q = ((q : ℤ) ^ 2 - 1 : ℤ)
declarations.docstring: Evaluation of the integral local adjugate identity in the Mersenne quotient.

declarations.name: localNumeratorProduct_associated_oddJordanScalar
declarations.signature: theorem localNumeratorProduct_associated_oddJordanScalar {r : ℕ} (hr : Squarefree r) : Associated ((∏ q ∈ r.primeFactors, localNumeratorValue r q : ℤ) : ZMod (RadicalMobiusShadow.mersenne r)) (oddJordanScalar r : ZMod (RadicalMobiusShadow.mersenne r))
declarations.docstring: Prime-by-prime, the numerator factor product is associated to the odd Jordan scalar in the Mersenne quotient. The binary factor contributes only a unit.

declarations.name: mobiusNumerator_associated_oddJordanScalar
declarations.signature: theorem mobiusNumerator_associated_oddJordanScalar {r : ℕ} (hr : Squarefree r) : Associated (RadicalMobiusShadow.mobiusNumerator r : ZMod (RadicalMobiusShadow.mersenne r)) (oddJordanScalar r : ZMod (RadicalMobiusShadow.mersenne r))
declarations.docstring: Base-two unit normal form: the full Möbius numerator is associated to the product of the odd-prime scalars modulo the Mersenne denominator.

declarations.name: associated_modulus_dvd_natAbs
declarations.signature: private theorem associated_modulus_dvd_natAbs {a b : ℤ} {M d : ℕ} (hdM : d ∣ M) (hassoc : Associated (a : ZMod M) (b : ZMod M)) (hda : d ∣ a.natAbs) : d ∣ b.natAbs

declarations.name: associated_modulus_dvd_natAbs_iff
declarations.signature: private theorem associated_modulus_dvd_natAbs_iff {a b : ℤ} {M d : ℕ} (hdM : d ∣ M) (hassoc : Associated (a : ZMod M) (b : ZMod M)) : d ∣ a.natAbs ↔ d ∣ b.natAbs

declarations.name: gcd_natAbs_eq_of_associated_mod
declarations.signature: theorem gcd_natAbs_eq_of_associated_mod {a b : ℤ} {M : ℕ} (hassoc : Associated (a : ZMod M) (b : ZMod M)) : Nat.gcd a.natAbs M = Nat.gcd b.natAbs M
declarations.docstring: Associated residue classes have exactly the same gcd with the modulus.

declarations.name: scaledMobiusNumerator_gcd_eq_oddJordanScalar
declarations.signature: theorem scaledMobiusNumerator_gcd_eq_oddJordanScalar {r : ℕ} (hr : Squarefree r) (h : ℕ) : Nat.gcd (((h : ℤ) * RadicalMobiusShadow.mobiusNumerator r).natAbs) (RadicalMobiusShadow.mersenne r) = Nat.gcd (h * (oddJordanScalar r).natAbs) (RadicalMobiusShadow.mersenne r)
declarations.docstring: Exact cancellation after an arbitrary natural scale: the giant Möbius numerator may be replaced by the small odd Jordan scalar inside the gcd.

declarations.name: mobiusNumerator_gcd_eq_oddJordanScalar
declarations.signature: theorem mobiusNumerator_gcd_eq_oddJordanScalar {r : ℕ} (hr : Squarefree r) : Nat.gcd (RadicalMobiusShadow.mobiusNumerator r).natAbs (RadicalMobiusShadow.mersenne r) = Nat.gcd (oddJordanScalar r).natAbs (RadicalMobiusShadow.mersenne r)
declarations.docstring: The unscaled exact cancellation formula.

declarations.name: scaledMobiusNumerator_den_exact
declarations.signature: theorem scaledMobiusNumerator_den_exact {r : ℕ} (hr : Squarefree r) (h : ℕ) : (Rat.divInt ((h : ℤ) * RadicalMobiusShadow.mobiusNumerator r) (RadicalMobiusShadow.mersenne r : ℤ)).den = RadicalMobiusShadow.mersenne r / Nat.gcd (RadicalMobiusShadow.mersenne r) (h * (oddJordanScalar r).natAbs)
declarations.docstring: Exact reduced denominator of every naturally scaled base-two Möbius shadow.

declarations.name: prime_dvd_mersenne_gt_scale
declarations.signature: theorem prime_dvd_mersenne_gt_scale {t p ℓ : ℕ} (hp : p.Prime) (hp3 : 3 ≤ p) (htp : t < 2 * p) (hℓ : ℓ.Prime) (hℓM : ℓ ∣ RadicalMobiusShadow.mersenne p) : t < ℓ
declarations.docstring: A prime divisor of a prime-index Mersenne number is above every scale strictly below `2p`. The extra factor two comes from oddness of the divisor.

declarations.name: prime_dvd_mersenne_lower_bound
declarations.signature: theorem prime_dvd_mersenne_lower_bound {p ℓ : ℕ} (hp : p.Prime) (hp3 : 3 ≤ p) (hℓ : ℓ.Prime) (hℓM : ℓ ∣ RadicalMobiusShadow.mersenne p) : 2 * p + 1 ≤ ℓ
declarations.docstring: Strong form of `prime_dvd_mersenne_gt_scale`: a prime factor of a prime-index Mersenne number is at least `2p + 1` for odd prime `p`.

declarations.name: coprime_mersenne_of_primeFactors_le
declarations.signature: theorem coprime_mersenne_of_primeFactors_le {t p a : ℕ} (hp : p.Prime) (hp3 : 3 ≤ p) (htp : t < 2 * p) (ha : ∀ ℓ : ℕ, ℓ.Prime → ℓ ∣ a → ℓ ≤ t) : Nat.Coprime a (RadicalMobiusShadow.mersenne p)
declarations.docstring: Any natural number supported on primes at most `t` is coprime to the upper-half Mersenne channel.

declarations.name: isUnit_mod_mersenne_of_primeFactors_le
declarations.signature: theorem isUnit_mod_mersenne_of_primeFactors_le {t p a : ℕ} (hp : p.Prime) (hp3 : 3 ≤ p) (htp : t < 2 * p) (ha : ∀ ℓ : ℕ, ℓ.Prime → ℓ ∣ a → ℓ ≤ t) : IsUnit (a : ZMod (RadicalMobiusShadow.mersenne p))
declarations.docstring: The supported natural factor is a unit in the upper-half Mersenne channel.

declarations.name: upperHalfUnitTarget
declarations.signature: noncomputable def upperHalfUnitTarget (h n p : ℕ) (j : ℤ) : ZMod (RadicalMobiusShadow.mersenne p)
declarations.docstring: The abstract residue target supplied by T2 after inverting the geometric cofactor.

declarations.name: upperHalfUnitTarget_isUnit_of_primeFactors_le
declarations.signature: theorem upperHalfUnitTarget_isUnit_of_primeFactors_le {t p h n : ℕ} {j : ℤ} (hp : p.Prime) (hp3 : 3 ≤ p) (htp : t < 2 * p) (hh : ∀ ℓ : ℕ, ℓ.Prime → ℓ ∣ h → ℓ ≤ t) (hn : ∀ ℓ : ℕ, ℓ.Prime → ℓ ∣ n → ℓ ≤ t) (hj : ∀ ℓ : ℕ, ℓ.Prime → ℓ ∣ j.natAbs → ℓ ≤ t) : IsUnit (upperHalfUnitTarget h n p j)
declarations.docstring: Once the three arithmetic factors have no prime support above `t`, the T2 target is a unit in every prime channel with `t < 2p`. This packages the unit argument without pretending the LCM-height factor bounds are already wired.

declarations.name: divisor_dvd_divInt_den
declarations.signature: theorem divisor_dvd_divInt_den {a : ℤ} {D m : ℕ} (hD : 0 < D) (hmD : m ∣ D) (hcop : Nat.Coprime m a.natAbs) : m ∣ (Rat.divInt a (D : ℤ)).den
declarations.docstring: A divisor of a displayed denominator survives rational reduction when it is coprime to the displayed numerator.

declarations.name: survivingDivisor_dvd_scaled_divInt_den
declarations.signature: theorem survivingDivisor_dvd_scaled_divInt_den {a : ℤ} {D C h : ℕ} (hD : 0 < D) (hCD : C ∣ D) (hcop : Nat.Coprime C a.natAbs) : C / Nat.gcd C h ∣ (Rat.divInt ((h : ℤ) * a) (D : ℤ)).den
declarations.docstring: Exact scale-aware survivor: scaling can cancel the part `gcd C h`, but cannot cancel any further part of `C` when `C` was coprime to the unscaled numerator.

declarations.name: jordanTotientTwo_natAbs_eq_prod
declarations.signature: theorem jordanTotientTwo_natAbs_eq_prod {n : ℕ} (hn : Squarefree n) : (jordanTotientTwo n).natAbs = ∏ q ∈ n.primeFactors, (q ^ 2 - 1)
declarations.docstring: Exact scale-aware survivor: scaling can cancel the part `gcd C h`, but cannot cancel any further part of `C` when `C` was coprime to the unscaled numerator. -/ theorem survivingDivisor_dvd_scaled_divInt_den {a : ℤ} {D C h : ℕ} (hD : 0 < D) (hCD : C ∣ D) (hcop : Nat.Coprime C a.natAbs) : C / Nat.gcd C h ∣ (Rat.divInt ((h : ℤ) * a) (D : ℤ)).den := RationalDenominatorSurvival.survivingDivisor_dvd_scaled_divInt_den hD hCD hcop /-! ## T2 prime channels and their aggregate product

declarations.name: coprime_mersenne_prime_sq_sub_one
declarations.signature: theorem coprime_mersenne_prime_sq_sub_one {t p q : ℕ} (hp : p.Prime) (hp3 : 3 ≤ p) (htp : t < 2 * p) (hq : q.Prime) (hqt : q ≤ t) : Nat.Coprime (RadicalMobiusShadow.mersenne p) (q ^ 2 - 1)

declarations.name: coprime_mersenne_jordanTotientTwo
declarations.signature: theorem coprime_mersenne_jordanTotientTwo {t r p : ℕ} (hr : Squarefree r) (hp : p.Prime) (hp3 : 3 ≤ p) (hpr : p ∣ r) (htp : t < 2 * p) (hcut : ∀ q : ℕ, q.Prime → q ∣ r → q ≤ t) : Nat.Coprime (RadicalMobiusShadow.mersenne p) (jordanTotientTwo (r / p)).natAbs

declarations.name: mobiusNumerator_mod_mersenne_prime
declarations.signature: theorem mobiusNumerator_mod_mersenne_prime {r p : ℕ} (hr : Squarefree r) (hp : p.Prime) (hpr : p ∣ r) : (RadicalMobiusShadow.mersenne p : ℤ) ∣ RadicalMobiusShadow.mobiusNumerator r + jordanTotientTwo (r / p)

declarations.name: coprime_natAbs_of_sum_dvd
declarations.signature: theorem coprime_natAbs_of_sum_dvd {m : ℕ} {a b : ℤ} (hdiv : (m : ℤ) ∣ a + b) (hcop : Nat.Coprime m b.natAbs) : Nat.Coprime m a.natAbs

declarations.name: coprime_mersenne_mobiusNumerator
declarations.signature: theorem coprime_mersenne_mobiusNumerator {t r p : ℕ} (hr : Squarefree r) (hp : p.Prime) (hp3 : 3 ≤ p) (hpr : p ∣ r) (htp : t < 2 * p) (hcut : ∀ q : ℕ, q.Prime → q ∣ r → q ≤ t) : Nat.Coprime (RadicalMobiusShadow.mersenne p) (RadicalMobiusShadow.mobiusNumerator r).natAbs

declarations.name: coprime_mersenne_of_distinct_primes
declarations.signature: theorem coprime_mersenne_of_distinct_primes {p q : ℕ} (hp : p.Prime) (hq : q.Prime) (hpq : p ≠ q) : Nat.Coprime (RadicalMobiusShadow.mersenne p) (RadicalMobiusShadow.mersenne q)

declarations.name: channelProduct_dvd_mersenne
declarations.signature: theorem channelProduct_dvd_mersenne {P : Finset ℕ} {r : ℕ} (hprime : ∀ p ∈ P, p.Prime) (hpr : ∀ p ∈ P, p ∣ r) : (∏ p ∈ P, RadicalMobiusShadow.mersenne p) ∣ RadicalMobiusShadow.mersenne r

declarations.name: channelProduct_coprime_mobiusNumerator
declarations.signature: theorem channelProduct_coprime_mobiusNumerator {P : Finset ℕ} {t r : ℕ} (ht : 5 ≤ t) (hr : Squarefree r) (hprime : ∀ p ∈ P, p.Prime) (hpr : ∀ p ∈ P, p ∣ r) (hupper : ∀ p ∈ P, t < 2 * p) (hcut : ∀ q : ℕ, q.Prime → q ∣ r → q ≤ t) : Nat.Coprime (∏ p ∈ P, RadicalMobiusShadow.mersenne p) (RadicalMobiusShadow.mobiusNumerator r).natAbs

declarations.name: upperHalfChannel_survivorProduct_dvd_den
declarations.signature: theorem upperHalfChannel_survivorProduct_dvd_den (P : Finset ℕ) {t r h : ℕ} (ht : 5 ≤ t) (hr : Squarefree r) (hprime : ∀ p ∈ P, p.Prime) (hpr : ∀ p ∈ P, p ∣ r) (hupper : ∀ p ∈ P, t < 2 * p) (hcut : ∀ q : ℕ, q.Prime → q ∣ r → q ≤ t) : let C
declarations.docstring: Canonical unconditional T4: the pairwise-coprime channel product survives exactly up to cancellation by the displayed scale `h`.

declarations.name: upperHalfChannel_survivor_dvd_den
declarations.signature: theorem upperHalfChannel_survivor_dvd_den {t r h p : ℕ} (ht : 5 ≤ t) (hr : Squarefree r) (hp : p.Prime) (hpr : p ∣ r) (hupper : t < 2 * p) (hcut : ∀ q : ℕ, q.Prime → q ∣ r → q ≤ t) : RadicalMobiusShadow.mersenne p / Nat.gcd (RadicalMobiusShadow.mersenne p) h ∣ (Rat.divInt ((h : ℤ) * RadicalMobiusShadow.mobiusNumerator r) (RadicalMobiusShadow.mersenne r : ℤ)).den
declarations.docstring: A single upper-half prime contributes its Mersenne modulus after removing exactly the part that the scale can cancel.

declarations.name: upperHalfChannel_product_dvd_den_of_coprime_scale
declarations.signature: theorem upperHalfChannel_product_dvd_den_of_coprime_scale (P : Finset ℕ) {t r h : ℕ} (ht : 5 ≤ t) (hr : Squarefree r) (hprime : ∀ p ∈ P, p.Prime) (hpr : ∀ p ∈ P, p ∣ r) (hupper : ∀ p ∈ P, t < 2 * p) (hcut : ∀ q : ℕ, q.Prime → q ∣ r → q ≤ t) (hscale : Nat.Coprime (∏ p ∈ P, RadicalMobiusShadow.mersenne p) h) : (∏ p ∈ P, RadicalMobiusShadow.mersenne p) ∣ (Rat.divInt ((h : ℤ) * RadicalMobiusShadow.mobiusNumerator r) (RadicalMobiusShadow.mersenne r : ℤ)).den

declarations.name: upperHalfChannel_product_dvd_den_of_scale_primeFactors_le
declarations.signature: theorem upperHalfChannel_product_dvd_den_of_scale_primeFactors_le (P : Finset ℕ) {t r h : ℕ} (ht : 5 ≤ t) (hr : Squarefree r) (hprime : ∀ p ∈ P, p.Prime) (hpr : ∀ p ∈ P, p ∣ r) (hupper : ∀ p ∈ P, t < 2 * p) (hcut : ∀ q : ℕ, q.Prime → q ∣ r → q ≤ t) (hhcut : ∀ q : ℕ, q.Prime → q ∣ h → q ≤ t) : (∏ p ∈ P, RadicalMobiusShadow.mersenne p) ∣ (Rat.divInt ((h : ℤ) * RadicalMobiusShadow.mobiusNumerator r) (RadicalMobiusShadow.mersenne r : ℤ)).den

declarations.name: lcmHeight
declarations.docstring: `Hₜ = lcm(1, ..., t)`. The interval avoids inserting zero into the finite LCM.

declarations.name: lcmHeight_five_fixture
declarations.docstring: Executable pressure test for the through-`t` convention and upper endpoint.

declarations.name: lcmRadical
declarations.docstring: `rₜ = rad(Hₜ)` using the canonical T6 squarefree kernel.

declarations.name: lcmScale
declarations.docstring: `hₜ = Hₜ / rₜ`, the scale multiplying the radical shadow.

declarations.name: prime_dvd_lcmHeight_iff
declarations.signature: theorem prime_dvd_lcmHeight_iff {t ℓ : ℕ} (hℓ : ℓ.Prime) : ℓ ∣ lcmHeight t ↔ ℓ ≤ t
declarations.docstring: The prime support of `Hₜ` is exactly the set of primes at most `t`.

declarations.name: lcmScale_dvd_lcmHeight
declarations.signature: theorem lcmScale_dvd_lcmHeight (t : ℕ) : lcmScale t ∣ lcmHeight t

declarations.name: lcmScale_primeFactors_le
declarations.signature: theorem lcmScale_primeFactors_le (t : ℕ) : ∀ ℓ : ℕ, ℓ.Prime → ℓ ∣ lcmScale t → ℓ ≤ t

declarations.name: prime_dvd_lcmRadical_iff
declarations.signature: theorem prime_dvd_lcmRadical_iff {t p : ℕ} (hp : p.Prime) : p ∣ lcmRadical t ↔ p ≤ t

declarations.name: lcmRadical_div_dvd_lcmHeight
declarations.signature: theorem lcmRadical_div_dvd_lcmHeight {t p : ℕ} (hp : p ∣ lcmRadical t) : lcmRadical t / p ∣ lcmHeight t

declarations.name: lcmRadical_div_primeFactors_le
declarations.signature: theorem lcmRadical_div_primeFactors_le {t p : ℕ} (hp : p ∣ lcmRadical t) : ∀ ℓ : ℕ, ℓ.Prime → ℓ ∣ lcmRadical t / p → ℓ ≤ t

declarations.name: upperHalfPrimes
declarations.docstring: Prime indices in the development's upper half `(t/2, t]`.

declarations.name: upperHalfPrimes_five_fixture
declarations.docstring: Executable pressure test for the strict lower and inclusive upper boundaries of the upper-half prime family.

declarations.name: scaledMobiusShadow_upperHalf_survivorProduct_dvd_den
declarations.signature: theorem scaledMobiusShadow_upperHalf_survivorProduct_dvd_den (P : Finset ℕ) {t H : ℕ} (hH : 0 < H) (ht : 5 ≤ t) (hprime : ∀ p ∈ P, p.Prime) (hpr : ∀ p ∈ P, p ∣ RadicalMobiusShadow.squarefreeKernel H) (hupper : ∀ p ∈ P, t < 2 * p) (hcut : ∀ q : ℕ, q.Prime → q ∣ RadicalMobiusShadow.squarefreeKernel H → q ≤ t) : let h
declarations.docstring: Executable pressure test for the strict lower and inclusive upper boundaries of the upper-half prime family. -/ theorem upperHalfPrimes_five_fixture : upperHalfPrimes 5 = {3, 5} := by decide /-! ## Instantiation at the repository's scaled Möbius shadow

declarations.name: scaledMobiusShadow_upperHalf_product_dvd_den_of_scale_primeFactors_le
declarations.signature: theorem scaledMobiusShadow_upperHalf_product_dvd_den_of_scale_primeFactors_le (P : Finset ℕ) {t H : ℕ} (hH : 0 < H) (ht : 5 ≤ t) (hprime : ∀ p ∈ P, p.Prime) (hpr : ∀ p ∈ P, p ∣ RadicalMobiusShadow.squarefreeKernel H) (hupper : ∀ p ∈ P, t < 2 * p) (hcut : ∀ q : ℕ, q.Prime → q ∣ RadicalMobiusShadow.squarefreeKernel H → q ≤ t) (hhcut : ∀ q : ℕ, q.Prime → q ∣ H / RadicalMobiusShadow.squarefreeKernel H → q ≤ t) : (∏ p ∈ P, RadicalMobiusShadow.mersenne p) ∣ ((H : ℚ) * RadicalMobiusShadow.numericMobiusShadow H).den

declarations.name: lcmHeight_upperHalf_product_dvd_den
declarations.signature: theorem lcmHeight_upperHalf_product_dvd_den {t : ℕ} (ht : 5 ≤ t) : (∏ p ∈ upperHalfPrimes t, RadicalMobiusShadow.mersenne p) ∣ ((lcmHeight t : ℚ) * RadicalMobiusShadow.numericMobiusShadow (lcmHeight t)).den
declarations.docstring: Concrete paper-level T4: at `Hₜ = lcm(1, ..., t)`, the product of all prime-index Mersenne channels with `t/2 < p ≤ t` survives in the reduced denominator of the scaled numeric Möbius shadow.

declarations.name: two_pow_pred_le_mersenne
declarations.signature: private theorem two_pow_pred_le_mersenne {p : ℕ} (hp : 0 < p) : 2 ^ (p - 1) ≤ RadicalMobiusShadow.mersenne p

declarations.name: upperHalfMersenneProduct_factorwise_lower_bound
declarations.signature: theorem upperHalfMersenneProduct_factorwise_lower_bound (t : ℕ) : (∏ p ∈ upperHalfPrimes t, 2 ^ (p - 1)) ≤ ∏ p ∈ upperHalfPrimes t, RadicalMobiusShadow.mersenne p
declarations.docstring: Every selected channel dominates its corresponding predecessor power of two, independently of any prime-distribution estimate.

declarations.name: upperHalfMersenneProduct_sum_lower_bound
declarations.signature: theorem upperHalfMersenneProduct_sum_lower_bound (t : ℕ) : 2 ^ (∑ p ∈ upperHalfPrimes t, (p - 1)) ≤ ∏ p ∈ upperHalfPrimes t, RadicalMobiusShadow.mersenne p
declarations.docstring: Normalized form of the factorwise bound, retaining the full sum of the selected prime exponents.

declarations.name: upperHalfPrimes_nonempty
declarations.docstring: Bertrand's postulate supplies an upper-half prime at every scale at least two. The proof retains the inequality `2 * (t / 2) ≤ t`, which is the correct odd-`t` endpoint.

declarations.name: upperHalfMersenneProduct_lower_bound
declarations.signature: theorem upperHalfMersenneProduct_lower_bound {t : ℕ} (ht : 5 ≤ t) : 2 ^ (t / 2) ≤ ∏ p ∈ upperHalfPrimes t, RadicalMobiusShadow.mersenne p
declarations.docstring: Elementary numerical growth of the full surviving channel product.

declarations.name: lcmHeight_scaledMobiusShadow_den_lower_bound
declarations.signature: theorem lcmHeight_scaledMobiusShadow_den_lower_bound {t : ℕ} (ht : 5 ≤ t) : 2 ^ (t / 2) ≤ ((lcmHeight t : ℚ) * RadicalMobiusShadow.numericMobiusShadow (lcmHeight t)).den
declarations.docstring: T4 plus the elementary product bound gives an explicit lower bound for the reduced denominator of the scaled LCM-height Möbius shadow.

declarations.name: exists_upperHalf_mersenne_channel_dvd_den_with_bounds
declarations.signature: theorem exists_upperHalf_mersenne_channel_dvd_den_with_bounds {t : ℕ} (ht : 5 ≤ t) : ∃ p ∈ upperHalfPrimes t, 2 ^ (t / 2) ≤ RadicalMobiusShadow.mersenne p ∧ RadicalMobiusShadow.mersenne p < 2 ^ t ∧ RadicalMobiusShadow.mersenne p ∣ ((lcmHeight t : ℚ) * RadicalMobiusShadow.numericMobiusShadow (lcmHeight t)).den
declarations.docstring: A concrete upper-half channel survives in the reduced denominator, with both the lower size bound used for growth and the upper bit-scale bound needed by a later discrete channel-separation argument.

declarations.name: primeFactors_thirty
declarations.docstring: A concrete upper-half channel survives in the reduced denominator, with both the lower size bound used for growth and the upper bit-scale bound needed by a later discrete channel-separation argument. -/ theorem exists_upperHalf_mersenne_channel_dvd_den_with_bounds {t : ℕ} (ht : 5 ≤ t) : ∃ p ∈ upperHalfPrimes t, 2 ^ (t / 2) ≤ RadicalMobiusShadow.mersenne p ∧ RadicalMobiusShadow.mersenne p < 2 ^ t ∧ RadicalMobiusShadow.mersenne p ∣ ((lcmHeight t : ℚ) * RadicalMobiusShadow.numericMobiusShadow (lcmHeight t)).den := by obtain ⟨p, hp⟩ := upperHalfPrimes_nonempty (by omega : 2 ≤ t) have hpPrime : p.Prime := (Finset.mem_filter.mp hp).2 have hpBounds := Finset.mem_Ioc.mp (Finset.mem_filter.mp hp).1 have hpowLower : 2 ^ (t / 2) ≤ 2 ^ (p - 1) := Nat.pow_le_pow_right (by norm_num) (by omega) have hpowUpper : 2 ^ p ≤ 2 ^ t := Nat.pow_le_pow_right (by norm_num) hpBounds.2 have hmersenneUpper : RadicalMobiusShadow.mersenne p < 2 ^ p := by simp only [RadicalMobiusShadow.mersenne] have hpos : 0 < 2 ^ p

declarations.name: lcmHeight_scaledMobiusShadow_den_exact
declarations.signature: theorem lcmHeight_scaledMobiusShadow_den_exact (t : ℕ) : ((lcmHeight t : ℚ) * RadicalMobiusShadow.numericMobiusShadow (lcmHeight t)).den = RadicalMobiusShadow.mersenne (lcmRadical t) / Nat.gcd (RadicalMobiusShadow.mersenne (lcmRadical t)) (lcmScale t * (CyclicTensorMobiusShadow.oddJordanScalar (lcmRadical t)).natAbs)
declarations.docstring: The exact reduced denominator at every LCM height. The numerator is replaced completely by the scale times the odd Jordan scalar.

declarations.name: lcmRadical_five_fixture
declarations.docstring: Concrete scale and radical at the first nontrivial LCM height.

declarations.name: lcmHeight_five_scaledMobiusShadow_den_exact
declarations.docstring: At `t = 5`, exact cancellation is `3`, so the denominator is `M₃₀/3`.

declarations.name: upperHalfMersenneProduct_five_fixture
declarations.signature: theorem upperHalfMersenneProduct_five_fixture : (∏ p ∈ upperHalfPrimes 5, RadicalMobiusShadow.mersenne p) = 217
declarations.docstring: Boundary regression: at `t = 5` the surviving product is `(2^3 - 1)(2^5 - 1) = 217`.

declarations.name: mersenneTailAtom
declarations.signature: noncomputable def mersenneTailAtom (d : ℕ+) (N : ℕ) : ℚ
declarations.docstring: The periodic tail atom of positive conductor `d` at index `N`.

declarations.name: mersenneTailAtom_step
