declarations.signature: theorem mersenneTailAtom_step (d : ℕ+) (N : ℕ) : 2 * mersenneTailAtom d N - mersenneTailAtom d (N + 1) = if (d : ℕ) ∣ N + 1 then 1 else 0
declarations.docstring: A periodic atom advances by doubling except at its unique wrap point, where the Mersenne numerator contributes exactly one carry.

declarations.name: mersenneAtomNumeratorMatrix
declarations.signature: def mersenneAtomNumeratorMatrix {m : ℕ} (d : Fin m → ℕ+) (idx : Fin m → Fin m → ℕ) : Matrix (Fin m) (Fin m) ℤ
declarations.docstring: Integer numerator matrix for a finite family of pointed atoms.

declarations.name: mersenneAtomMatrix
declarations.signature: noncomputable def mersenneAtomMatrix {m : ℕ} (d : Fin m → ℕ+) (idx : Fin m → Fin m → ℕ) : Matrix (Fin m) (Fin m) ℚ
declarations.docstring: Rational matrix formed from a finite family of pointed atoms, with one conductor (and therefore one Mersenne denominator) assigned to each column.

declarations.name: det_mersenneAtomMatrix
declarations.signature: theorem det_mersenneAtomMatrix {m : ℕ} (d : Fin m → ℕ+) (idx : Fin m → Fin m → ℕ) : Matrix.det (mersenneAtomMatrix d idx) = ((Matrix.det (mersenneAtomNumeratorMatrix d idx) : ℤ) : ℚ) / ∏ j, ((2 : ℚ) ^ (d j : ℕ) - 1)
declarations.docstring: Exact finite atom determinant factorisation. The numerator is literally the cast of an integer determinant; all apparent smallness is carried by the same column denominators that must be cleared to recover integrality.

declarations.name: mersenneAtom_denominatorProduct_mul_det
declarations.signature: theorem mersenneAtom_denominatorProduct_mul_det {m : ℕ} (d : Fin m → ℕ+) (idx : Fin m → Fin m → ℕ) : (∏ j, ((2 : ℚ) ^ (d j : ℕ) - 1)) * Matrix.det (mersenneAtomMatrix d idx) = ((Matrix.det (mersenneAtomNumeratorMatrix d idx) : ℤ) : ℚ)
declarations.docstring: Clearing the finite Mersenne denominator product recovers the integer numerator determinant exactly.

declarations.name: one_le_abs_mersenneAtom_cleared_det
declarations.signature: theorem one_le_abs_mersenneAtom_cleared_det {m : ℕ} (d : Fin m → ℕ+) (idx : Fin m → Fin m → ℕ) (hdet : Matrix.det (mersenneAtomNumeratorMatrix d idx) ≠ 0) : (1 : ℚ) ≤ |(∏ j, ((2 : ℚ) ^ (d j : ℕ) - 1)) * Matrix.det (mersenneAtomMatrix d idx)|
declarations.docstring: Height-tight finite-atom boundary: a nonzero numerator determinant gives a cleared rational minor whose absolute value is at least one.

declarations.name: weightedMersenneAtomMatrix
declarations.signature: noncomputable def weightedMersenneAtomMatrix {m : ℕ} (d : Fin m → ℕ+) (idx : Fin m → Fin m → ℕ) (w : Fin m → ℤ) : Matrix (Fin m) (Fin m) ℚ
declarations.docstring: Column-weighted atom matrix. In the totient application the weights are `v * A(d_j)`, hence integers supplied by the rationality multiplier and the primitive-conductor arithmetic.

declarations.name: weightedMersenneAtom_denominatorProduct_mul_det
declarations.signature: theorem weightedMersenneAtom_denominatorProduct_mul_det {m : ℕ} (d : Fin m → ℕ+) (idx : Fin m → Fin m → ℕ) (w : Fin m → ℤ) : (∏ j, ((2 : ℚ) ^ (d j : ℕ) - 1)) * Matrix.det (weightedMersenneAtomMatrix d idx w) = ((Matrix.det (mersenneAtomNumeratorMatrix d idx) * ∏ j, w j : ℤ) : ℚ)
declarations.docstring: Integer weights do not create an extra height surplus: after the same Mersenne clearing, they multiply the integer numerator determinant.

declarations.name: one_le_abs_weightedMersenneAtom_cleared_det
declarations.signature: theorem one_le_abs_weightedMersenneAtom_cleared_det {m : ℕ} (d : Fin m → ℕ+) (idx : Fin m → Fin m → ℕ) (w : Fin m → ℤ) (hdet : Matrix.det (mersenneAtomNumeratorMatrix d idx) * ∏ j, w j ≠ 0) : (1 : ℚ) ≤ |(∏ j, ((2 : ℚ) ^ (d j : ℕ) - 1)) * Matrix.det (weightedMersenneAtomMatrix d idx w)|
declarations.docstring: Weighted height-tight boundary in absolute-value form.

declarations.name: positiveMobiusTailTerm
declarations.docstring: The positive Möbius tail, with the exceptional `d = 1` term removed.

declarations.name: negativeMobiusTerm
declarations.docstring: The Boolean support term selected by the negative Möbius sign.

declarations.name: mobiusOneTerm
declarations.docstring: The single `d = 1` term in the signed Möbius Lambert series.

declarations.name: summable_mersenneWeight_pnat
declarations.signature: private theorem summable_mersenneWeight_pnat : Summable (fun d : ℕ+ => mersenneWeight (d : ℕ))

declarations.name: mobius_mersenne_term_eq
declarations.signature: theorem mobius_mersenne_term_eq (d : ℕ+) : ((moebius (d : ℕ) : ℤ) : ℝ) * mersenneWeight (d : ℕ) = mobiusOneTerm d + positiveMobiusTailTerm d - negativeMobiusTerm d
declarations.docstring: Pointwise sign separation of the signed Möbius term.

declarations.name: tsum_mobiusOneTerm
declarations.signature: theorem tsum_mobiusOneTerm : ∑' d : ℕ+, mobiusOneTerm d = 1

declarations.name: tsum_negativeMobius_eq_half_add_positiveMobiusTail
declarations.signature: theorem tsum_negativeMobius_eq_half_add_positiveMobiusTail : (∑' d : ℕ+, negativeMobiusTerm d) = 1 / 2 + ∑' d : ℕ+, positiveMobiusTailTerm d
declarations.docstring: Exact sign-separation identity. The negative-Möbius Boolean support overshoots `1/2` by the complete positive-Möbius tail.

declarations.name: half_add_one_div_sixty_three_le_tsum_negativeMobius
declarations.signature: theorem half_add_one_div_sixty_three_le_tsum_negativeMobius : (1 : ℝ) / 2 + 1 / 63 ≤ ∑' d : ℕ+, negativeMobiusTerm d
declarations.docstring: Quantitative no-go: sign truncation overshoots by at least `1/63`.

declarations.name: half_lt_tsum_negativeMobius
declarations.signature: theorem half_lt_tsum_negativeMobius : (1 : ℝ) / 2 < ∑' d : ℕ+, negativeMobiusTerm d

declarations.name: tailOrbitFirstExp
declarations.signature: noncomputable def tailOrbitFirstExp (h N : ℕ) : ℂ
declarations.docstring: First additive character of the infinite tail difference.

declarations.name: scaledTotientSeriesFirstExp
declarations.signature: noncomputable def scaledTotientSeriesFirstExp (h N : ℕ) : ℂ
declarations.docstring: First additive character of the scaled #249 series orbit.

declarations.name: tail_diff_eq_scaled_totient_series_sub_prefix
declarations.signature: theorem tail_diff_eq_scaled_totient_series_sub_prefix (h N : ℕ) : totientTail (N + h) - totientTail N = (2 : ℝ) ^ N * ((2 : ℝ) ^ h - 1) * (∑' n : ℕ, (Nat.totient n : ℝ) / 2 ^ n) - ((totientPrefix (N + h) : ℝ) - (totientPrefix N : ℝ))
declarations.docstring: Exact real normal form behind the period-lock orbit: the tail difference is the scaled #249 series minus an explicit integer prefix difference.

declarations.name: tailOrbitFirstExp_eq_scaledTotientSeriesFirstExp
declarations.signature: theorem tailOrbitFirstExp_eq_scaledTotientSeriesFirstExp (h N : ℕ) : tailOrbitFirstExp h N = scaledTotientSeriesFirstExp h N
declarations.docstring: Exact tail-orbit conjugacy. Integer prefixes disappear under the first additive character, so rationality locks precisely the scaled `W₀` orbit.

declarations.name: windowFirstExp_eq_unreduced
declarations.signature: theorem windowFirstExp_eq_unreduced (h N L : ℕ) : windowFirstExp h N L = Complex.exp ((((2 * Real.pi * (((windowDiscrepancy h N L : ℤ) : ℝ) / 2 ^ L)) : ℝ) : ℂ) * Complex.I)
declarations.docstring: The reduced finite-window character equals the character of the unreduced integer discrepancy.

declarations.name: windowFirstExp_eq_of_discrepancy_emod_eq
declarations.signature: theorem windowFirstExp_eq_of_discrepancy_emod_eq {h N N' L : ℕ} (hres : windowDiscrepancy h N L % (2 ^ L : ℤ) = windowDiscrepancy h N' L % (2 ^ L : ℤ)) : windowFirstExp h N L = windowFirstExp h N' L
declarations.docstring: Equal dyadic discrepancy residues give exactly equal first-harmonic phases. This is the finite lattice interface for an arithmetic collision argument: no choice of integer representatives or analytic approximation is left at the call site.

declarations.name: windowFirstCos_eq_of_discrepancy_emod_eq
declarations.signature: theorem windowFirstCos_eq_of_discrepancy_emod_eq {h N N' L : ℕ} (hres : windowDiscrepancy h N L % (2 ^ L : ℤ) = windowDiscrepancy h N' L % (2 ^ L : ℤ)) : windowFirstCos h N L = windowFirstCos h N' L
declarations.docstring: Real-part version of the exact dyadic residue collision interface.

declarations.name: tailOrbitFirstExp_eq_window_mul_remainder
declarations.signature: theorem tailOrbitFirstExp_eq_window_mul_remainder (h N L : ℕ) : tailOrbitFirstExp h N = windowFirstExp h N L * Complex.exp (Complex.I * (((2 * Real.pi * ((totientTail (N + L + h) - totientTail (N + L)) / 2 ^ L)) : ℝ) : ℂ))
declarations.docstring: Exact finite-window/infinite-orbit phase factorisation. The second factor is the translated tail omitted at depth `L`.

declarations.name: norm_windowFirstExp_sub_tailOrbitFirstExp_le
declarations.signature: theorem norm_windowFirstExp_sub_tailOrbitFirstExp_le (h N L : ℕ) : ‖windowFirstExp h N L - tailOrbitFirstExp h N‖ ≤ 2 * Real.pi * |totientTail (N + L + h) - totientTail (N + L)| / 2 ^ L
declarations.docstring: The phase error is bounded by the exact omitted tail difference.

declarations.name: norm_windowFirstExp_sub_tailOrbitFirstExp_lt
declarations.signature: theorem norm_windowFirstExp_sub_tailOrbitFirstExp_lt (h N L : ℕ) : ‖windowFirstExp h N L - tailOrbitFirstExp h N‖ < 2 * Real.pi * ((N : ℝ) + L + h + 2) / 2 ^ L
declarations.docstring: Closed phase-error bound obtained from the universal totient-tail strip.

declarations.name: windowFirstCos_le_add_of_tailOrbitGap
declarations.signature: theorem windowFirstCos_le_add_of_tailOrbitGap {h N L : ℕ} {a ε : ℝ} (htail : (tailOrbitFirstExp h N).re ≤ a) (herror : 2 * Real.pi * ((N : ℝ) + L + h + 2) / 2 ^ L ≤ ε) : windowFirstCos h N L ≤ a + ε
declarations.docstring: Any real-part gap in the infinite orbit survives finite truncation with exactly the supplied additive error budget. This is the packet-facing seam: arithmetic input may come from a lattice, variance, or anti-concentration argument without being normalized in advance to `4/5`.

declarations.name: windowFirstCos_le_nine_tenths_of_tailOrbitGap
declarations.signature: theorem windowFirstCos_le_nine_tenths_of_tailOrbitGap {h N L : ℕ} (htail : (tailOrbitFirstExp h N).re ≤ (4 / 5 : ℝ)) (herror : 2 * Real.pi * ((N : ℝ) + L + h + 2) / 2 ^ L ≤ (1 / 10 : ℝ)) : windowFirstCos h N L ≤ (9 / 10 : ℝ)
declarations.docstring: A `4/5` real-part gap in the infinite orbit survives as the `9/10` finite-window gap once the explicit truncation budget is at most `1/10`.

declarations.name: tendsto_linear_mul_half_pow
declarations.signature: private theorem tendsto_linear_mul_half_pow (c : ℕ) : Filter.Tendsto (fun L : ℕ => ((c : ℝ) + L) * (1 / 2 : ℝ) ^ L) Filter.atTop (nhds 0)

declarations.name: exists_natural_window_depth_with_error
declarations.signature: theorem exists_natural_window_depth_with_error (h N L₀ : ℕ) (ε : ℝ) (hε : 0 < ε) : ∃ L : ℕ, L₀ ≤ L ∧ 16 * (2 * N + h + L + 2) ≤ 2 ^ L ∧ 2 * Real.pi * ((N : ℝ) + L + h + 2) / 2 ^ L ≤ ε
declarations.docstring: Exponential depth simultaneously pays the certificate room inequality and any positive phase-error budget past any prescribed depth.

declarations.name: IsDyadicReal
declarations.docstring: A real number is dyadic when some power-of-two multiple is an integer. This multiplicative form avoids quotient normalization in orbit arguments.

declarations.name: cofinally_adjacent_chord_ge_one_of_unit_squaring
declarations.signature: theorem cofinally_adjacent_chord_ge_one_of_unit_squaring (z : ℕ → ℂ) (hnorm : ∀ N, ‖z N‖ = 1) (hsq : ∀ N, z (N + 1) = (z N) ^ 2) (hne : ∀ N, z N ≠ 1) : ∀ N₀, ∃ N ≥ N₀, 1 ≤ ‖z (N + 1) - z N‖
declarations.docstring: A unit-modulus squaring orbit which never reaches `1` has cofinally many adjacent chords of length at least `1`. This is the elementary expansivity input behind the two-point characterization below; no equidistribution is used.

declarations.name: scaledTotientSeriesFirstExp_succ
declarations.signature: theorem scaledTotientSeriesFirstExp_succ (h N : ℕ) : scaledTotientSeriesFirstExp h (N + 1) = (scaledTotientSeriesFirstExp h N) ^ 2
declarations.docstring: The exact #249 scaled phase orbit is a squaring orbit.

declarations.name: norm_scaledTotientSeriesFirstExp
declarations.signature: theorem norm_scaledTotientSeriesFirstExp (h N : ℕ) : ‖scaledTotientSeriesFirstExp h N‖ = 1
declarations.docstring: Every point of the exact #249 scaled phase orbit has unit norm.

declarations.name: cofinally_scaledTotientSeriesFirstExp_adjacent_chord_ge_one_of_not_dyadic
declarations.signature: theorem cofinally_scaledTotientSeriesFirstExp_adjacent_chord_ge_one_of_not_dyadic (h : ℕ) (hnd : ¬ IsDyadicReal (((2 : ℝ) ^ h - 1) * (∑' n : ℕ, (Nat.totient n : ℝ) / 2 ^ n))) : ∀ N₀, ∃ N ≥ N₀, 1 ≤ ‖scaledTotientSeriesFirstExp h (N + 1) - scaledTotientSeriesFirstExp h N‖
declarations.docstring: Non-dyadicity of the fixed scaled series is exactly the hypothesis needed to prevent the corresponding doubling orbit from landing at `1`.

declarations.name: cofinally_scaledTotientSeriesFirstExp_adjacent_chord_ge_one
declarations.signature: theorem cofinally_scaledTotientSeriesFirstExp_adjacent_chord_ge_one (hS : Irrational (∑' n : ℕ, (Nat.totient n : ℝ) / 2 ^ n)) (h : ℕ) (hh : 0 < h) : ∀ N₀, ∃ N ≥ N₀, 1 ≤ ‖scaledTotientSeriesFirstExp h (N + 1) - scaledTotientSeriesFirstExp h N‖
declarations.docstring: Irrationality of the #249 series prevents the scaled phase orbit from ever landing at `1`; doubling expansivity then gives cofinally many adjacent chords of length at least `1`.

declarations.name: firstHarmonicAnchorDefect
declarations.signature: noncomputable def firstHarmonicAnchorDefect (h L : ℕ) (T : Finset ℕ) : ℝ
declarations.docstring: Squared affine distance from the distinguished first-harmonic phase `1`, written in real coordinates so the certificate kernel needs no complex API.

declarations.name: firstHarmonicAnchorDefect_eq
declarations.signature: theorem firstHarmonicAnchorDefect_eq (h L : ℕ) (T : Finset ℕ) : firstHarmonicAnchorDefect h L T = 2 * (T.card : ℝ) - 2 * ∑ N ∈ T, windowFirstCos h N L
declarations.docstring: The anchor defect is exactly the cardinality-minus-real-part expression.

declarations.name: firstHarmonicAnchorDefect_eq_sum_norm_sq
declarations.signature: theorem firstHarmonicAnchorDefect_eq_sum_norm_sq (h L : ℕ) (T : Finset ℕ) : firstHarmonicAnchorDefect h L T = ∑ N ∈ T, ‖windowFirstExp h N L - 1‖ ^ 2
declarations.docstring: The real-coordinate defect is the squared complex distance from the distinguished phase `1`.

declarations.name: firstHarmonicAnchorDefect_ge_iff_first_harmonic_gap
declarations.signature: theorem firstHarmonicAnchorDefect_ge_iff_first_harmonic_gap (h L : ℕ) (T : Finset ℕ) : (T.card : ℝ) / 5 ≤ firstHarmonicAnchorDefect h L T ↔ (∑ N ∈ T, windowFirstCos h N L) ≤ (9 / 10 : ℝ) * T.card
declarations.docstring: The one-fifth anchor-defect threshold is exactly, in both directions, the nine-tenths real first-harmonic threshold on the same finite set.

declarations.name: sum_norm_sq_eq_centered_add_mean
declarations.signature: theorem sum_norm_sq_eq_centered_add_mean {α : Type*} [DecidableEq α] (T : Finset α) (z : α → ℂ) (hT : T.Nonempty) : (∑ i ∈ T, ‖z i‖ ^ 2) = (∑ i ∈ T, ‖z i - ((T.card : ℂ)⁻¹ * ∑ j ∈ T, z j)‖ ^ 2) + (T.card : ℝ) * ‖(T.card : ℂ)⁻¹ * ∑ j ∈ T, z j‖ ^ 2
declarations.docstring: Exact finite complex bias--variance identity. This is kept independent of the pivot definitions so an arithmetic producer may first establish variance for whatever explicit complex error vector its prime toggles expose.

declarations.name: sum_pairwise_norm_sq_eq_two_card_mul_centered
declarations.signature: theorem sum_pairwise_norm_sq_eq_two_card_mul_centered {α : Type*} [DecidableEq α] (T : Finset α) (z : α → ℂ) (hT : T.Nonempty) : (∑ i ∈ T, ∑ j ∈ T, ‖z i - z j‖ ^ 2) = 2 * (T.card : ℝ) * ∑ i ∈ T, ‖z i - ((T.card : ℂ)⁻¹ * ∑ j ∈ T, z j)‖ ^ 2
declarations.docstring: Exact pairwise-energy form of finite complex variance. This is the prime-toggle-friendly companion to `sum_norm_sq_eq_centered_add_mean`: an arithmetic producer may lower-bound separated pairs directly, without first guessing or estimating the fibre mean.

declarations.name: card_mul_sq_le_sum_pairwise_norm_sq_of_separatedPairs
declarations.signature: theorem card_mul_sq_le_sum_pairwise_norm_sq_of_separatedPairs {α : Type*} [DecidableEq α] (T : Finset α) (z : α → ℂ) (P : Finset (α × α)) (δ : ℝ) (hP : P ⊆ T.product T) (hδ : 0 ≤ δ) (hsep : ∀ p ∈ P, δ ≤ ‖z p.1 - z p.2‖) : (P.card : ℝ) * δ ^ 2 ≤ ∑ i ∈ T, ∑ j ∈ T, ‖z i - z j‖ ^ 2
declarations.docstring: A counted set of separated ordered pairs lower-bounds the full pairwise energy. Arithmetic arguments may therefore work on any explicitly selected subset of the fibre square; they do not need to estimate every pair or the unknown fibre mean.

declarations.name: card_div_five_le_sum_anchor_norm_sq_of_pairwise
declarations.signature: theorem card_div_five_le_sum_anchor_norm_sq_of_pairwise {α : Type*} [DecidableEq α] (T : Finset α) (z : α → ℂ) (a : ℂ) (hT : T.Nonempty) (hpair : 2 * (T.card : ℝ) ^ 2 / 5 ≤ ∑ i ∈ T, ∑ j ∈ T, ‖z i - z j‖ ^ 2) : (T.card : ℝ) / 5 ≤ ∑ i ∈ T, ‖z i - a‖ ^ 2
declarations.docstring: Pairwise energy of the original vectors already controls their squared distance from any fixed anchor. This is the mean-free form needed by the window-phase route: the unknown empirical mean contributes only a nonnegative bias, so it never has to be estimated by the arithmetic producer.

declarations.name: pivotReconstructionError
declarations.signature: noncomputable def pivotReconstructionError (h N L s : ℕ) : ℂ
declarations.docstring: Actual reconstruction error on a canonical supplier fibre.

declarations.name: pivotReconstructionError_eq_conj_phase_mul_window_sub_one
declarations.signature: theorem pivotReconstructionError_eq_conj_phase_mul_window_sub_one (h N L s : ℕ) : pivotReconstructionError h N L s = (starRingEnd ℂ) (pivotPhaseAt h N L s) * (windowFirstExp h N L - 1)
declarations.docstring: The reconstruction error is the actual window anchor error, rotated by the conjugate pivot phase. In particular, this identity retains the full residual coupling; no independence or phase-only surrogate is introduced.

declarations.name: pivotFiberWindowMode
declarations.signature: noncomputable def pivotFiberWindowMode (h X L s m : ℕ) : ℂ
declarations.docstring: Unweighted first mode of the actual window phase on one supplier fibre.

declarations.name: pivotFiberResidualMode
declarations.signature: noncomputable def pivotFiberResidualMode (h X L s m : ℕ) : ℂ
declarations.docstring: Residual-weighted first mode on one supplier fibre. This is the anti-lock coefficient: the conjugate pivot phase multiplies the complete window phase, rather than a truncated or independently averaged proxy.

declarations.name: pivotFiberConjugatePhaseMode
declarations.signature: noncomputable def pivotFiberConjugatePhaseMode (h X L s m : ℕ) : ℂ
declarations.docstring: Bare conjugate-pivot mode on one supplier fibre.

declarations.name: pivotFiberReconstructionErrorSum_eq_twoMode
declarations.signature: theorem pivotFiberReconstructionErrorSum_eq_twoMode (h X L s m : ℕ) : (∑ N ∈ pivotFiber X L s m, pivotReconstructionError h N L s) = pivotFiberResidualMode h X L s m - pivotFiberConjugatePhaseMode h X L s m
declarations.docstring: The total reconstruction error is exactly the residual-weighted mode minus the bare conjugate-pivot mode.

declarations.name: pivotFiberReconstructionErrorMean
declarations.signature: noncomputable def pivotFiberReconstructionErrorMean (h X L s m : ℕ) : ℂ
declarations.docstring: Mean reconstruction error on one canonical supplier fibre.

declarations.name: pivotFiberReconstructionVariance
declarations.signature: noncomputable def pivotFiberReconstructionVariance (h X L s m : ℕ) : ℝ
declarations.docstring: Centred reconstruction-error energy on one canonical supplier fibre. This is the literal variance socket for a prime-toggle producer.

declarations.name: pivotFiberReconstructionVariance_eq_pairwise
declarations.signature: theorem pivotFiberReconstructionVariance_eq_pairwise (h X L s m : ℕ) (hne : (pivotFiber X L s m).Nonempty) : 2 * ((pivotFiber X L s m).card : ℝ) * pivotFiberReconstructionVariance h X L s m = ∑ N ∈ pivotFiber X L s m, ∑ N' ∈ pivotFiber X L s m, ‖pivotReconstructionError h N L s - pivotReconstructionError h N' L s‖ ^ 2
declarations.docstring: The actual pivot-fibre variance is exactly normalized pairwise energy. This removes the unknown fibre mean from the remaining arithmetic producer.

declarations.name: pivotFiberReconstructionVariance_ge_one_fifth_of_pairwise
declarations.signature: theorem pivotFiberReconstructionVariance_ge_one_fifth_of_pairwise {h X L s m : ℕ} (hne : (pivotFiber X L s m).Nonempty) (hpair : 2 * ((pivotFiber X L s m).card : ℝ) ^ 2 / 5 ≤ ∑ N ∈ pivotFiber X L s m, ∑ N' ∈ pivotFiber X L s m, ‖pivotReconstructionError h N L s - pivotReconstructionError h N' L s‖ ^ 2) : ((pivotFiber X L s m).card : ℝ) / 5 ≤ pivotFiberReconstructionVariance h X L s m
declarations.docstring: Pairwise-energy intake at the exact one-fifth threshold used by the #249 consumer. A prime-toggle or separated-pairs argument can target this double sum directly; division by the unknown fibre cardinality is handled here once and for all.

declarations.name: exists_certifiedKill_of_firstHarmonicAnchorDefect
declarations.signature: theorem exists_certifiedKill_of_firstHarmonicAnchorDefect {h X L : ℕ} (T : Finset ℕ) (hTlt : ∀ N ∈ T, N < 2 * X) (hTne : T.Nonempty) (hroom : 16 * (2 * X + h + L + 2) ≤ 2 ^ L) (hdefect : (T.card : ℝ) / 5 ≤ firstHarmonicAnchorDefect h L T) : ∃ N ∈ T, certifiedKill h N L
declarations.docstring: A defect of at least one fifth per phase is exactly enough to invoke the arbitrary-subset first-harmonic consumer.

declarations.name: pivotFiberReconstructionDefect
declarations.signature: noncomputable def pivotFiberReconstructionDefect (h X L s m : ℕ) : ℝ
declarations.docstring: Anchor defect restricted to one canonical large-prime supplier fibre.

declarations.name: pivotFiberReconstructionDefect_eq_twoModeAnchor
declarations.signature: theorem pivotFiberReconstructionDefect_eq_twoModeAnchor (h X L s m : ℕ) : pivotFiberReconstructionDefect h X L s m = 2 * ((pivotFiber X L s m).card : ℝ) - 2 * (pivotFiberWindowMode h X L s m).re
declarations.docstring: The uncentred part of reconstruction energy is the first of the two actual fibre modes: cardinality minus the real window mode.

declarations.name: norm_pivotResidualAt
declarations.signature: theorem norm_pivotResidualAt (h N L s : ℕ) : ‖pivotResidualAt h N L s‖ = 1

declarations.name: pivotFiberReconstructionDefect_eq_sum_residual_norm_sq
declarations.signature: theorem pivotFiberReconstructionDefect_eq_sum_residual_norm_sq (h X L s m : ℕ) : pivotFiberReconstructionDefect h X L s m = ∑ N ∈ pivotFiber X L s m, ‖pivotResidualAt h N L s - (starRingEnd ℂ) (pivotPhaseAt h N L s)‖ ^ 2
declarations.docstring: On one pivot fibre, anchor defect is exactly squared distance of the actual residual vector from the locked reconstruction `conj phase`.

declarations.name: pivotFiberReconstructionDefect_eq_variance_add_bias
declarations.signature: theorem pivotFiberReconstructionDefect_eq_variance_add_bias (h X L s m : ℕ) (hne : (pivotFiber X L s m).Nonempty) : pivotFiberReconstructionDefect h X L s m = pivotFiberReconstructionVariance h X L s m + ((pivotFiber X L s m).card : ℝ) * ‖pivotFiberReconstructionErrorMean h X L s m‖ ^ 2
declarations.docstring: The existing reconstruction defect is variance plus the squared bias of the same error vector. In particular, positive conditional variance feeds the #249 consumer without estimating the four-term pivot budget.

declarations.name: card_mul_norm_inv_natCast_mul_sq_eq_div
declarations.signature: theorem card_mul_norm_inv_natCast_mul_sq_eq_div {α : Type*} [DecidableEq α] (T : Finset α) (hT : T.Nonempty) (z : ℂ) : (T.card : ℝ) * ‖(T.card : ℂ)⁻¹ * z‖ ^ 2 = ‖z‖ ^ 2 / (T.card : ℝ)
declarations.docstring: Normalizing a complex sum by a nonempty finite cardinality converts its squared mean contribution to the usual `‖sum‖² / card` form.

declarations.name: pivotFiberReconstructionVariance_eq_twoMode
declarations.signature: theorem pivotFiberReconstructionVariance_eq_twoMode (h X L s m : ℕ) (hne : (pivotFiber X L s m).Nonempty) : pivotFiberReconstructionVariance h X L s m = 2 * ((pivotFiber X L s m).card : ℝ) - 2 * (pivotFiberWindowMode h X L s m).re - ((pivotFiber X L s m).card : ℝ) * ‖((pivotFiber X L s m).card : ℂ)⁻¹ * (pivotFiberResidualMode h X L s m - pivotFiberConjugatePhaseMode h X L s m)‖ ^ 2
declarations.docstring: Exact two-mode formula for centred reconstruction variance. The first mode is the unweighted window phase. The second is the complete residual-weighted mode, centred against the bare conjugate-pivot mode.

declarations.name: pivotFiberReconstructionVariance_eq_twoMode_div_card
declarations.signature: theorem pivotFiberReconstructionVariance_eq_twoMode_div_card (h X L s m : ℕ) (hne : (pivotFiber X L s m).Nonempty) : pivotFiberReconstructionVariance h X L s m = 2 * ((pivotFiber X L s m).card : ℝ) - 2 * (pivotFiberWindowMode h X L s m).re - ‖pivotFiberResidualMode h X L s m - pivotFiberConjugatePhaseMode h X L s m‖ ^ 2 / ((pivotFiber X L s m).card : ℝ)
declarations.docstring: Quotient form of the exact two-mode identity: `V = 2|F| - 2 Re W₀ - ‖W₁-C₁‖²/|F|`.

declarations.name: pivotFiberReconstructionVariance_ge_six_div_twentyfive_of_twoMode
declarations.signature: theorem pivotFiberReconstructionVariance_ge_six_div_twentyfive_of_twoMode {h X L s m : ℕ} (hne : (pivotFiber X L s m).Nonempty) (hwindow : (pivotFiberWindowMode h X L s m).re ≤ (4 / 5 : ℝ) * (pivotFiber X L s m).card) (hantilock : ‖((pivotFiber X L s m).card : ℂ)⁻¹ * (pivotFiberResidualMode h X L s m - pivotFiberConjugatePhaseMode h X L s m)‖ ≤ (2 / 5 : ℝ)) : (6 / 25 : ℝ) * (pivotFiber X L s m).card ≤ pivotFiberReconstructionVariance h X L s m
declarations.docstring: Lean-friendly sufficient two-mode bounds with strict slack over the one-fifth consumer threshold. The second hypothesis is the normalized anti-lock coefficient `‖(W₁-C₁)/|F|‖ ≤ 2/5`.

declarations.name: pivotFiberNormalizedAntiLock_le_two_fifths
declarations.signature: theorem pivotFiberNormalizedAntiLock_le_two_fifths {h X L s m : ℕ} (hne : (pivotFiber X L s m).Nonempty) (hresidual : ‖pivotFiberResidualMode h X L s m‖ ≤ ((pivotFiber X L s m).card : ℝ) / 3) (hphase : ‖pivotFiberConjugatePhaseMode h X L s m‖ ≤ ((pivotFiber X L s m).card : ℝ) / 15) : ‖((pivotFiber X L s m).card : ℂ)⁻¹ * (pivotFiberResidualMode h X L s m - pivotFiberConjugatePhaseMode h X L s m)‖ ≤ (2 / 5 : ℝ)
declarations.docstring: The packet's separate `‖W₁‖ ≤ |F|/3` and `‖C₁‖ ≤ |F|/15` bounds imply the normalized anti-lock bound used above.

declarations.name: pivotFiberReconstructionVariance_ge_six_div_twentyfive_of_modeBounds
declarations.signature: theorem pivotFiberReconstructionVariance_ge_six_div_twentyfive_of_modeBounds {h X L s m : ℕ} (hne : (pivotFiber X L s m).Nonempty) (hwindow : (pivotFiberWindowMode h X L s m).re ≤ (4 / 5 : ℝ) * (pivotFiber X L s m).card) (hresidual : ‖pivotFiberResidualMode h X L s m‖ ≤ ((pivotFiber X L s m).card : ℝ) / 3) (hphase : ‖pivotFiberConjugatePhaseMode h X L s m‖ ≤ ((pivotFiber X L s m).card : ℝ) / 15) : (6 / 25 : ℝ) * (pivotFiber X L s m).card ≤ pivotFiberReconstructionVariance h X L s m
declarations.docstring: The three concrete mode estimates from the natural-prime contour give centred variance at least `6|F|/25`, hence strictly more than the consumer's required `|F|/5`.

declarations.name: exists_certifiedKill_of_pivotFiberReconstructionDefect
declarations.signature: theorem exists_certifiedKill_of_pivotFiberReconstructionDefect {h X L s m : ℕ} (hne : (pivotFiber X L s m).Nonempty) (hroom : 16 * (2 * X + h + L + 2) ≤ 2 ^ L) (hdefect : ((pivotFiber X L s m).card : ℝ) / 5 ≤ pivotFiberReconstructionDefect h X L s m) : ∃ N ∈ pivotFiber X L s m, certifiedKill h N L
declarations.docstring: A one-fibre anti-reconstruction bound produces a certificate in that same fibre.

declarations.name: exists_certifiedKill_of_pivotFiberReconstructionVariance
declarations.signature: theorem exists_certifiedKill_of_pivotFiberReconstructionVariance {h X L s m : ℕ} (hne : (pivotFiber X L s m).Nonempty) (hroom : 16 * (2 * X + h + L + 2) ≤ 2 ^ L) (hvariance : ((pivotFiber X L s m).card : ℝ) / 5 ≤ pivotFiberReconstructionVariance h X L s m) : ∃ N ∈ pivotFiber X L s m, certifiedKill h N L
declarations.docstring: A one-fifth lower bound on centred reconstruction variance is already enough for the established one-fibre certificate consumer.

declarations.name: exists_certifiedKill_of_pivotFiberPairwiseEnergy
declarations.signature: theorem exists_certifiedKill_of_pivotFiberPairwiseEnergy {h X L s m : ℕ} (hne : (pivotFiber X L s m).Nonempty) (hroom : 16 * (2 * X + h + L + 2) ≤ 2 ^ L) (hpair : 2 * ((pivotFiber X L s m).card : ℝ) ^ 2 / 5 ≤ ∑ N ∈ pivotFiber X L s m, ∑ N' ∈ pivotFiber X L s m, ‖pivotReconstructionError h N L s - pivotReconstructionError h N' L s‖ ^ 2) : ∃ N ∈ pivotFiber X L s m, certifiedKill h N L
declarations.docstring: Pairwise-energy version of the one-fibre certificate intake. This is the direct target for prime toggles or separated-pair counts: no fibre mean or intermediate variance estimate remains at the call site.

declarations.name: exists_certifiedKill_of_pivotFiberSeparatedPairs
declarations.signature: theorem exists_certifiedKill_of_pivotFiberSeparatedPairs {h X L s m : ℕ} (hne : (pivotFiber X L s m).Nonempty) (hroom : 16 * (2 * X + h + L + 2) ≤ 2 ^ L) (P : Finset (ℕ × ℕ)) (δ : ℝ) (hP : P ⊆ (pivotFiber X L s m).product (pivotFiber X L s m)) (hδ : 0 ≤ δ) (hsep : ∀ p ∈ P, δ ≤ ‖pivotReconstructionError h p.1 L s - pivotReconstructionError h p.2 L s‖) (hmass : 2 * ((pivotFiber X L s m).card : ℝ) ^ 2 / 5 ≤ (P.card : ℝ) * δ ^ 2) : ∃ N ∈ pivotFiber X L s m, certifiedKill h N L
declarations.docstring: Counted-separated-pairs version of the one-fibre certificate intake. This is a direct finite combinatorial target: supply a subset `P` of the ordered fibre square, a uniform separation `δ`, and enough total mass `|P| δ²`; the analytic variance layer and certificate extraction are then automatic.

declarations.name: pivotFiberReconstructionDefect_ge_one_fifth_of_windowPairwise
declarations.signature: theorem pivotFiberReconstructionDefect_ge_one_fifth_of_windowPairwise {h X L s m : ℕ} (hne : (pivotFiber X L s m).Nonempty) (hpair : 2 * ((pivotFiber X L s m).card : ℝ) ^ 2 / 5 ≤ ∑ N ∈ pivotFiber X L s m, ∑ N' ∈ pivotFiber X L s m, ‖windowFirstExp h N L - windowFirstExp h N' L‖ ^ 2) : ((pivotFiber X L s m).card : ℝ) / 5 ≤ pivotFiberReconstructionDefect h X L s m
declarations.docstring: Pairwise energy of the actual window phases controls the established anchor defect directly. Unlike reconstruction-error energy, this input has no pivot phase or residual weighting: it is exactly a two-point anti-concentration statement for the dyadic window character.

declarations.name: exists_certifiedKill_of_pivotFiberWindowPairwiseEnergy
declarations.signature: theorem exists_certifiedKill_of_pivotFiberWindowPairwiseEnergy {h X L s m : ℕ} (hne : (pivotFiber X L s m).Nonempty) (hroom : 16 * (2 * X + h + L + 2) ≤ 2 ^ L) (hpair : 2 * ((pivotFiber X L s m).card : ℝ) ^ 2 / 5 ≤ ∑ N ∈ pivotFiber X L s m, ∑ N' ∈ pivotFiber X L s m, ‖windowFirstExp h N L - windowFirstExp h N' L‖ ^ 2) : ∃ N ∈ pivotFiber X L s m, certifiedKill h N L
declarations.docstring: Direct finite certificate intake for window-phase pairwise energy.

declarations.name: exists_certifiedKill_of_pivotFiberWindowSeparatedPairs
declarations.signature: theorem exists_certifiedKill_of_pivotFiberWindowSeparatedPairs {h X L s m : ℕ} (hne : (pivotFiber X L s m).Nonempty) (hroom : 16 * (2 * X + h + L + 2) ≤ 2 ^ L) (P : Finset (ℕ × ℕ)) (δ : ℝ) (hP : P ⊆ (pivotFiber X L s m).product (pivotFiber X L s m)) (hδ : 0 ≤ δ) (hsep : ∀ p ∈ P, δ ≤ ‖windowFirstExp h p.1 L - windowFirstExp h p.2 L‖) (hmass : 2 * ((pivotFiber X L s m).card : ℝ) ^ 2 / 5 ≤ (P.card : ℝ) * δ ^ 2) : ∃ N ∈ pivotFiber X L s m, certifiedKill h N L
declarations.docstring: Counted separated pairs of raw window phases feed the direct pairwise energy intake. This is the arithmetic-facing finite lattice socket: a producer may count phase-separated dyadic residues without reconstructing or estimating the pivot residual.

declarations.name: DTWPivotAntiReconstruction
declarations.docstring: General uncentred producer: cofinally, one nonempty actual supplier fibre stays a fixed squared distance from locked reconstruction.

declarations.name: DTWPivotReconstructionVariance
declarations.docstring: Stronger variance-only form of the cofinal producer. Compared with `DTWPivotAntiReconstruction`, this discards the nonnegative mean-error bias and asks a prime-toggle argument only for centred fibre energy.

declarations.name: DTWNaturalPivotWindowGap
declarations.docstring: Cofinal first-harmonic escape on the canonical natural prime fibre. This is the weakest fibre-average input consumed by the uncentred anchor defect: no residual-weighted or centred-variance estimate is present.

declarations.name: DTWNaturalPivotPointEscape
declarations.docstring: Strictly weaker pointwise socket: cofinally, one member of the canonical natural prime fibre escapes the locked first-harmonic arc. The singleton subset consumer turns this directly into a certificate, so neither a fibre average nor centred variance is logically required.

declarations.name: DTWNaturalPrimeTailOrbitGap
declarations.docstring: Prime-indexed gap in the exact infinite tail orbit. This removes all finite-window, dyadic-room, and supplier bookkeeping from the remaining arithmetic theorem: primality selects the lacunary exponent, while the phase is exactly `scaledTotientSeriesFirstExp`.

declarations.name: naturalPivotPointEscape_of_naturalPrimeTailOrbitGap
declarations.docstring: The prime-indexed infinite-orbit gap supplies the finite-window singleton escape. Exponential depth pays the room and truncation budgets; the natural-fibre membership is the exact shifted-prime equivalence.

declarations.name: DTWNaturalPivotTwoModeCancellation
declarations.docstring: Stronger optional spectral producer on the canonical natural prime fibre. Its first conjunct implies `DTWNaturalPivotWindowGap`; the residual and conjugate-pivot conjuncts are needed only for the centred-variance route.

declarations.name: DTWPivotPairwiseReconstructionEnergy
declarations.docstring: Mean-free, cofinal pairwise-energy producer. This is equivalent to a sufficient reconstruction-variance supply through the exact finite-fibre identity, while exposing only pair differences to arithmetic arguments.

declarations.name: DTWPivotSeparatedPairs
declarations.docstring: Cofinal counted-separated-pairs producer. This is a finite lattice and combinatorics socket beneath the pairwise-energy formulation: a proof may select only the arithmetically controlled ordered pairs and certify their common separation.

declarations.name: DTWPivotWindowPairwiseEnergy
declarations.docstring: Cofinal pairwise energy of the raw finite-window phases. This is more direct and arithmetic-facing than reconstruction-error energy because it contains neither the pivot phase nor the residual quotient.

declarations.name: DTWPivotWindowSeparatedPairs
declarations.docstring: Counted-separated-pairs form of raw window-phase anti-concentration. The remaining producer may select any controlled subset of the ordered fibre square and prove one common chord separation.

declarations.name: DTWWindowSeparatedPairsAt
declarations.docstring: Fixed-shift fibre-free counted window-phase anti-concentration. The sample `T` may be any nonempty subset of a cofinal dyadic block.

declarations.name: DTWWindowSeparatedPairs
declarations.docstring: Fibre-free counted window-phase anti-concentration at every positive shift; neither primality nor a pivot factorization is part of the statement.

declarations.name: windowSeparatedPairs_of_pivotWindowSeparatedPairs
declarations.docstring: The fibre-free predicate is genuinely weaker than its pivot-fibre specialization: forget the factorization data and retain the same sample and separated pairs inside the ambient dyadic block.

declarations.name: pivotWindowPairwiseEnergy_of_windowSeparatedPairs
declarations.docstring: Counted raw-window pairs supply the complete window pairwise energy.

declarations.name: pivotAntiReconstruction_of_windowPairwiseEnergy
declarations.docstring: Raw-window pairwise anti-concentration feeds the existing uncentred anchor defect without passing through reconstruction variance.

declarations.name: pivotPairwiseReconstructionEnergy_of_separatedPairs
declarations.docstring: Counted separated pairs supply the complete pairwise energy demanded by the existing #249 variance route.

declarations.name: pivotReconstructionVariance_of_pairwiseEnergy
declarations.docstring: Pairwise-energy supply feeds the established centred-variance producer.

declarations.name: naturalPivotWindowGap_of_naturalPivotTwoModeCancellation
declarations.docstring: The old two-mode socket implies the canonical window-gap socket using only its unweighted first-mode conjunct.

declarations.name: pivotAntiReconstruction_of_naturalPivotWindowGap
declarations.docstring: Natural-prime window escape directly supplies uncentred pivot anti-reconstruction. The proof uses exactly `D_F = 2|F| - 2 Re W₀`; the residual-weighted modes never enter.

declarations.name: pivotReconstructionVariance_of_naturalPivotTwoModeCancellation
declarations.docstring: The concrete natural-prime two-mode cancellation theorem supplies the existing cofinal reconstruction-variance socket, with `6/25` arithmetic slack before the `1/5` consumer threshold.

declarations.name: pivotAntiReconstruction_of_reconstructionVariance
declarations.docstring: Cofinal reconstruction variance is a sufficient arithmetic producer for the established anti-reconstruction predicate.

declarations.name: irrational_totient_series_of_pivotAntiReconstruction
declarations.signature: theorem irrational_totient_series_of_pivotAntiReconstruction (hmix : DTWPivotAntiReconstruction) : Irrational (∑' n : ℕ, (Nat.totient n : ℝ) / 2 ^ n)
declarations.docstring: Cofinal pivot anti-reconstruction composes through the established certificate-supply theorem to the #249 irrationality target.

declarations.name: irrational_totient_series_of_naturalPivotWindowGap
declarations.signature: theorem irrational_totient_series_of_naturalPivotWindowGap (hgap : DTWNaturalPivotWindowGap) : Irrational (∑' n : ℕ, (Nat.totient n : ℝ) / 2 ^ n)
declarations.docstring: Exact natural-prime first-mode endpoint. A cofinal `9/10` window gap on the genuine cofactor-one fibre is sufficient for #249.

declarations.name: irrational_totient_series_of_naturalPivotPointEscape
declarations.signature: theorem irrational_totient_series_of_naturalPivotPointEscape (hpoint : DTWNaturalPivotPointEscape) : Irrational (∑' n : ℕ, (Nat.totient n : ℝ) / 2 ^ n)
declarations.docstring: Exact singleton endpoint. A cofinal point escape inside the canonical natural fibre feeds the arbitrary-subset consumer with the singleton `{N}`.

declarations.name: irrational_totient_series_of_naturalPrimeTailOrbitGap
declarations.signature: theorem irrational_totient_series_of_naturalPrimeTailOrbitGap (hgap : DTWNaturalPrimeTailOrbitGap) : Irrational (∑' n : ℕ, (Nat.totient n : ℝ) / 2 ^ n)
declarations.docstring: Exact prime-orbit endpoint. The only assumed producer is a cofinal real-part gap for the prime-indexed infinite tail orbit; all finite certificate infrastructure is checked by the preceding bridge.

declarations.name: irrational_totient_series_of_pivotReconstructionVariance
declarations.signature: theorem irrational_totient_series_of_pivotReconstructionVariance (hvariance : DTWPivotReconstructionVariance) : Irrational (∑' n : ℕ, (Nat.totient n : ℝ) / 2 ^ n)
declarations.docstring: Exact #249 variance endpoint: proving the cofinal centred-error producer alone implies irrationality through the checked anti-reconstruction bridge.

declarations.name: irrational_totient_series_of_pivotPairwiseReconstructionEnergy
declarations.signature: theorem irrational_totient_series_of_pivotPairwiseReconstructionEnergy (hpair : DTWPivotPairwiseReconstructionEnergy) : Irrational (∑' n : ℕ, (Nat.totient n : ℝ) / 2 ^ n)
declarations.docstring: Exact #249 pairwise-energy endpoint.

declarations.name: irrational_totient_series_of_pivotSeparatedPairs
declarations.signature: theorem irrational_totient_series_of_pivotSeparatedPairs (hsep : DTWPivotSeparatedPairs) : Irrational (∑' n : ℕ, (Nat.totient n : ℝ) / 2 ^ n)
declarations.docstring: Exact #249 counted-separated-pairs endpoint.

declarations.name: irrational_totient_series_of_pivotWindowPairwiseEnergy
declarations.signature: theorem irrational_totient_series_of_pivotWindowPairwiseEnergy (hpair : DTWPivotWindowPairwiseEnergy) : Irrational (∑' n : ℕ, (Nat.totient n : ℝ) / 2 ^ n)
declarations.docstring: Exact #249 raw-window pairwise-energy endpoint.

declarations.name: irrational_totient_series_of_pivotWindowSeparatedPairs
declarations.signature: theorem irrational_totient_series_of_pivotWindowSeparatedPairs (hsep : DTWPivotWindowSeparatedPairs) : Irrational (∑' n : ℕ, (Nat.totient n : ℝ) / 2 ^ n)
declarations.docstring: Exact #249 raw-window counted-separated-pairs endpoint. This is the pivot-fibre two-point socket: the arithmetic producer controls only dyadic window phases and their counted chord separation.

declarations.name: irrational_totient_series_of_windowSeparatedPairs
declarations.signature: theorem irrational_totient_series_of_windowSeparatedPairs (hsep : DTWWindowSeparatedPairs) : Irrational (∑' n : ℕ, (Nat.totient n : ℝ) / 2 ^ n)
declarations.docstring: Exact fibre-free #249 counted-separated-pairs endpoint. Enough two-point anti-concentration on any cofinal finite block forces an actual finite certificate; all mean, anchor, and certificate extraction steps are discharged here.

declarations.name: windowSeparatedPairsAt_of_cofinally_scaled_adjacent_chord
declarations.signature: theorem windowSeparatedPairsAt_of_cofinally_scaled_adjacent_chord (h : ℕ) (hcofinal : ∀ N₀, ∃ N ≥ N₀, 1 ≤ ‖scaledTotientSeriesFirstExp h (N + 1) - scaledTotientSeriesFirstExp h N‖) : DTWWindowSeparatedPairsAt h
declarations.docstring: Any cofinal adjacent-chord escape for the exact scaled orbit supplies the fixed-shift fibre-free endpoint. Depth is chosen so both finite phases are within `1/20`; the sample `{N, N + 1}` with both ordered pairs has separation `9/10` and the required counted energy.

declarations.name: windowSeparatedPairsAt_of_not_dyadic_scaled_totient_series
declarations.signature: theorem windowSeparatedPairsAt_of_not_dyadic_scaled_totient_series (h : ℕ) (hnd : ¬ IsDyadicReal (((2 : ℝ) ^ h - 1) * (∑' n : ℕ, (Nat.totient n : ℝ) / 2 ^ n))) : DTWWindowSeparatedPairsAt h
declarations.docstring: A non-dyadic fixed scaled series supplies the fixed-shift window-pair endpoint by doubling expansivity.

declarations.name: windowSeparatedPairs_of_irrational_totient_series
declarations.signature: theorem windowSeparatedPairs_of_irrational_totient_series (hS : Irrational (∑' n : ℕ, (Nat.totient n : ℝ) / 2 ^ n)) : DTWWindowSeparatedPairs
declarations.docstring: Irrationality itself supplies the fibre-free endpoint at every positive shift.

declarations.name: dtwWindowSeparatedPairs_iff_irrational_totient_series
declarations.signature: theorem dtwWindowSeparatedPairs_iff_irrational_totient_series : DTWWindowSeparatedPairs ↔ Irrational (∑' n : ℕ, (Nat.totient n : ℝ) / 2 ^ n)
declarations.docstring: The fibre-free window-pair endpoint is exactly equivalent to the irrationality of the #249 totient series. It is a useful geometric interface, but not a logically weaker unresolved arithmetic obligation.

declarations.name: irrational_totient_series_of_naturalPivotTwoModeCancellation
declarations.signature: theorem irrational_totient_series_of_naturalPivotTwoModeCancellation (htwo : DTWNaturalPivotTwoModeCancellation) : Irrational (∑' n : ℕ, (Nat.totient n : ℝ) / 2 ^ n)
declarations.docstring: Compatibility endpoint for the stronger two-mode socket. Its proof now flows through the weaker window-gap route, making explicit that the `W₁` and `C₁` conjuncts are not on the certificate consumer's critical path.

declarations.name: residueHeckeDefect
declarations.signature: noncomputable def residueHeckeDefect (d H p : ℕ) : ℝ
declarations.docstring: Contribution of residue channel `d` to `D(pH) - p D(H)`.

declarations.name: postJumpResidueChannel
declarations.signature: noncomputable def postJumpResidueChannel (d H p : ℕ) : ℝ
declarations.docstring: The divisor value seen after the jump. For a newly absorbed channel this is the explicit post-jump contribution.

declarations.name: preJumpMigrationChannel
declarations.signature: noncomputable def preJumpMigrationChannel (d H p : ℕ) : ℝ
declarations.docstring: The pre-jump value of the same channel, transported with coefficient `-p`. This is the migration term omitted by a post-jump-only split.

declarations.name: residueHeckeDefect_eq_postJump_add_migration
declarations.signature: theorem residueHeckeDefect_eq_postJump_add_migration (d H p : ℕ) : residueHeckeDefect d H p = postJumpResidueChannel d H p + preJumpMigrationChannel d H p
declarations.docstring: The actual transition channel is post-jump contribution plus same-channel migration.

declarations.name: residueHeckeDefect_eq_zero_of_dvd
declarations.signature: theorem residueHeckeDefect_eq_zero_of_dvd {d H p : ℕ} (hd : 0 < d) (hdvd : d ∣ H) : residueHeckeDefect d H p = 0
declarations.docstring: Every carrier already dividing `H` cancels exactly in the prime-jump commutator. No analytic estimate is used.

declarations.name: residueHeckeDefect_five_twelve
declarations.docstring: At the first new-prime jump, the `d = 5` transition channel is positive: the pre-jump migration reverses the sign of its negative post-jump divisor contribution.

declarations.name: postJumpResidueChannel_five_twelve
declarations.docstring: The post-jump part of the same fixture is negative.

declarations.name: diagonalTailDifferenceAt
declarations.docstring: The diagonal tail difference `D(H) = R_(2H) - R_H`.

declarations.name: primeJumpTailCommutator
declarations.signature: noncomputable def primeJumpTailCommutator (H p : ℕ) : ℝ
declarations.docstring: The prime-jump commutator `J(H,p) = D(pH) - p D(H)`.

declarations.name: primeJumpWindowCommutator
declarations.signature: def primeJumpWindowCommutator (H p L : ℕ) : ℤ
declarations.docstring: Integer depth-`L` numerator of the four-vertex commutator, with vertices ordered as `H, 2H, pH, 2pH`.

declarations.name: primeJumpSharpRadius
declarations.signature: def primeJumpSharpRadius (H p L : ℕ) : ℤ
declarations.docstring: Exact direct tail radius for the four-vertex commutator.

declarations.name: primeJumpSharpKill
declarations.signature: def primeJumpSharpKill (H p L : ℕ) : Prop
declarations.docstring: Decidable direct consumer: the four-vertex window stays outside the sharp tail band around the integer lattice.

declarations.name: notMem_int_of_window_remainder_bound
declarations.signature: theorem notMem_int_of_window_remainder_bound {x e : ℝ} {A R : ℤ} {L : ℕ} (hsplit : x = (A : ℝ) / 2 ^ L + e) (hbound : |e * 2 ^ L| ≤ (R : ℝ)) (hleft : R < A % 2 ^ L) (hright : A % 2 ^ L < 2 ^ L - R) : x ∉ Set.range ((↑) : ℤ → ℝ)
declarations.docstring: Generic modular soundness lemma for an integer window with a real remainder bounded after multiplication by `2^L`.

declarations.name: primeJumpTailCommutator_eq_window_add_remainder
declarations.signature: theorem primeJumpTailCommutator_eq_window_add_remainder (H p L : ℕ) : ∃ e : ℝ, primeJumpTailCommutator H p = (primeJumpWindowCommutator H p L : ℝ) / 2 ^ L + e ∧ |e * 2 ^ L| ≤ (primeJumpSharpRadius H p L : ℝ)
declarations.docstring: Exact partial-window decomposition of the prime-jump commutator.

declarations.name: primeJumpTailCommutator_notMem_int_of_sharpKill
declarations.signature: theorem primeJumpTailCommutator_notMem_int_of_sharpKill {H p L : ℕ} (hkill : primeJumpSharpKill H p L) : primeJumpTailCommutator H p ∉ Set.range ((↑) : ℤ → ℝ)
declarations.docstring: **Sharp direct soundness.** A central four-vertex residue proves that the actual prime-jump commutator is nonintegral. The radius is `3pH`, not the larger two-cell disjunction radius `4pH`.

declarations.name: primeJumpSharpKill_twelve_five
declarations.docstring: A concrete new-prime-jump deposit.

declarations.name: irrational_totient_series_of_primeJumpSharpKill_supply
declarations.signature: theorem irrational_totient_series_of_primeJumpSharpKill_supply (hsupply : ∀ t₀ : ℕ, ∃ t, t₀ ≤ t ∧ ∃ p L : ℕ, 0 < p ∧ primeJumpSharpKill (periodLcm t) p L) : Irrational (∑' n : ℕ, (Nat.totient n : ℝ) / 2 ^ n)
declarations.docstring: An unbounded supply of sharp prime-jump window kills on the LCM cone would prove Erdős #249. This is the exact remaining consumer socket; the supply is not asserted here.

declarations.name: divisors_mul_prime_split
declarations.signature: private theorem divisors_mul_prime_split {r p : ℕ} (hp : p.Prime) (_hpr : ¬ p ∣ r) : (r * p).divisors = r.divisors ∪ r.divisors.image (fun d => d * p)

declarations.name: divisors_disjoint_prime_multiples
declarations.signature: private theorem divisors_disjoint_prime_multiples {r p : ℕ} (_hp : p.Prime) (hpr : ¬ p ∣ r) : Disjoint r.divisors (r.divisors.image fun d => d * p)

declarations.name: expand_spacedRepunit
declarations.signature: theorem expand_spacedRepunit (d q p : ℕ) : Polynomial.expand ℤ p (spacedRepunit d q) = spacedRepunit (d * p) q

declarations.name: spacedRepunit_eq_sum_X_pow
declarations.signature: private theorem spacedRepunit_eq_sum_X_pow (d q : ℕ) : spacedRepunit d q = ∑ j ∈ Finset.range q, (Polynomial.X : ℤ[X]) ^ (d * j)

declarations.name: spacedRepunit_mul_X_pow_sub_one
declarations.signature: private theorem spacedRepunit_mul_X_pow_sub_one (d q : ℕ) : spacedRepunit d q * ((Polynomial.X : ℤ[X]) ^ d - 1) = (Polynomial.X : ℤ[X]) ^ (d * q) - 1

declarations.name: X_pow_sub_one_ne_zero
declarations.signature: private theorem X_pow_sub_one_ne_zero {d : ℕ} (hd : 0 < d) : (Polynomial.X : ℤ[X]) ^ d - 1 ≠ 0

declarations.name: spacedRepunit_blocks
declarations.signature: private theorem spacedRepunit_blocks {d q p : ℕ} (hd : 0 < d) : spacedRepunit (d * q) p * spacedRepunit d q = spacedRepunit d (q * p)

declarations.name: moebius_mul_prime
declarations.signature: private theorem moebius_mul_prime {d p : ℕ} (hp : p.Prime) (hpd : ¬ p ∣ d) : ArithmeticFunction.moebius (d * p) = -ArithmeticFunction.moebius d

declarations.name: old_divisor_summand
declarations.signature: private theorem old_divisor_summand {r p d : ℕ} (hr : 0 < r) (hd : d ∣ r) : Polynomial.C (ArithmeticFunction.moebius d * ((((r * p) / d : ℕ) : ℤ))) * spacedRepunit d ((r * p) / d) = Polynomial.C (p : ℤ) * spacedRepunit r p * (Polynomial.C (ArithmeticFunction.moebius d * (((r / d : ℕ) : ℤ))) * spacedRepunit d (r / d))

declarations.name: new_divisor_summand
declarations.signature: private theorem new_divisor_summand {r p d : ℕ} (hp : p.Prime) (hd : d ∣ r) (hpr : ¬ p ∣ r) : Polynomial.C (ArithmeticFunction.moebius (d * p) * ((((r * p) / (d * p) : ℕ) : ℤ))) * spacedRepunit (d * p) ((r * p) / (d * p)) = -Polynomial.expand ℤ p (Polynomial.C (ArithmeticFunction.moebius d * (((r / d : ℕ) : ℤ))) * spacedRepunit d (r / d))

declarations.name: mobiusNumeratorPolynomial_mul_prime
declarations.signature: theorem mobiusNumeratorPolynomial_mul_prime {r p : ℕ} (hp : p.Prime) (hpr : ¬ p ∣ r) : mobiusNumeratorPolynomial (r * p) = Polynomial.C (p : ℤ) * spacedRepunit r p * mobiusNumeratorPolynomial r - Polynomial.expand ℤ p (mobiusNumeratorPolynomial r)
declarations.docstring: T5 in its strongest natural algebraic form: adjoining a prime not already dividing `r` produces an old-divisor block minus the `p`-fold expansion. No squarefree hypothesis is needed for this divisor-sum identity.

declarations.name: mobiusNumeratorPolynomial_prime_mul
declarations.signature: theorem mobiusNumeratorPolynomial_prime_mul {p r : ℕ} (hp : p.Prime) (hpr : ¬ p ∣ r) : mobiusNumeratorPolynomial (p * r) = Polynomial.C (p : ℤ) * spacedRepunit r p * mobiusNumeratorPolynomial r - Polynomial.expand ℤ p (mobiusNumeratorPolynomial r)
declarations.docstring: Development-facing commuted orientation of the T5 recurrence.

declarations.name: mobiusNumeratorPolynomial_mul_prime_comp
declarations.signature: theorem mobiusNumeratorPolynomial_mul_prime_comp {r p : ℕ} (hp : p.Prime) (hpr : ¬ p ∣ r) : mobiusNumeratorPolynomial (r * p) = Polynomial.C (p : ℤ) * spacedRepunit r p * mobiusNumeratorPolynomial r - (mobiusNumeratorPolynomial r).comp (Polynomial.X ^ p)
declarations.docstring: Compatibility wrapper exposing substitution as polynomial composition.

declarations.name: mobiusNumeratorPolynomial_mul_prime_eval
declarations.signature: theorem mobiusNumeratorPolynomial_mul_prime_eval {r p : ℕ} (hp : p.Prime) (hpr : ¬ p ∣ r) (x : ℤ) : (mobiusNumeratorPolynomial (r * p)).eval x = (p : ℤ) * (spacedRepunit r p).eval x * (mobiusNumeratorPolynomial r).eval x - (mobiusNumeratorPolynomial r).eval (x ^ p)
declarations.docstring: Evaluation form of T5: expansion at `x` is evaluation at `x ^ p`.

declarations.name: prime_not_dvd_divisor
declarations.signature: private theorem prime_not_dvd_divisor {r p m : ℕ} (hpr : ¬ p ∣ r) (hm : m ∣ r) : ¬ p ∣ m
declarations.docstring: Evaluation form of T5: expansion at `x` is evaluation at `x ^ p`. -/ theorem mobiusNumeratorPolynomial_mul_prime_eval {r p : ℕ} (hp : p.Prime) (hpr : ¬ p ∣ r) (x : ℤ) : (mobiusNumeratorPolynomial (r * p)).eval x = (p : ℤ) * (spacedRepunit r p).eval x * (mobiusNumeratorPolynomial r).eval x - (mobiusNumeratorPolynomial r).eval (x ^ p) := by rw [mobiusNumeratorPolynomial_mul_prime hp hpr] simp /-! ## Old and new cyclotomic fibres The recurrence preserves more than the ambient polynomial identity. A new `m * p` fibre kills the geometric old-divisor block, while an old `m` fibre sees multiplication by `p ^ 2 - 1`. These are finite congruence interfaces; they do not assert that any foreign defect misses the displayed target.

declarations.name: polynomial_dvd_sub
declarations.signature: private theorem polynomial_dvd_sub {a b c : ℤ[X]} (hb : a ∣ b) (hc : a ∣ c) : a ∣ b - c

declarations.name: cyclotomic_dvd_expand_sub_constant
declarations.signature: private theorem cyclotomic_dvd_expand_sub_constant {r p m : ℕ} (hr : Squarefree r) (hp : p.Prime) (hpr : ¬ p ∣ r) (hm : m ∣ r) : Polynomial.cyclotomic m ℤ ∣ Polynomial.expand ℤ p (mobiusNumeratorPolynomial r) - Polynomial.C (ArithmeticFunction.moebius m * jordanTotientTwo (r / m))

declarations.name: cyclotomic_dvd_primeJump_new_fibre
declarations.signature: theorem cyclotomic_dvd_primeJump_new_fibre {r p m : ℕ} (hp : p.Prime) (hpr : ¬ p ∣ r) (hm : m ∣ r) : Polynomial.cyclotomic (m * p) ℤ ∣ mobiusNumeratorPolynomial (r * p) + Polynomial.expand ℤ p (mobiusNumeratorPolynomial r)
declarations.docstring: In a genuinely new `m * p` fibre, the geometric old-divisor block vanishes, so the jump numerator is the negative `p`-fold expansion.

declarations.name: cyclotomic_dvd_primeJump_old_fibre
declarations.signature: theorem cyclotomic_dvd_primeJump_old_fibre {r p m : ℕ} (hr : Squarefree r) (hp : p.Prime) (hpr : ¬ p ∣ r) (hm : m ∣ r) : Polynomial.cyclotomic m ℤ ∣ mobiusNumeratorPolynomial (r * p) - Polynomial.C ((p : ℤ) ^ 2 - 1) * mobiusNumeratorPolynomial r
declarations.docstring: On an old `m` fibre, adjoining the new prime scales the old numerator by `p ^ 2 - 1`. Squarefreeness is needed only to invoke the T2 constant-fibre theorem, not for the underlying T5 recurrence.

declarations.name: cyclotomic_dvd_primeJump_new_fibre_constant
declarations.signature: theorem cyclotomic_dvd_primeJump_new_fibre_constant {r p m : ℕ} (hr : Squarefree r) (hp : p.Prime) (hpr : ¬ p ∣ r) (hm : m ∣ r) : Polynomial.cyclotomic (m * p) ℤ ∣ mobiusNumeratorPolynomial (r * p) + Polynomial.C (ArithmeticFunction.moebius m * jordanTotientTwo (r / m))
declarations.docstring: The new fibre carries the negated old T2 constant explicitly.

declarations.name: mobiusNumeratorPolynomial_two_fixture
declarations.docstring: Smallest explicit sign fixture.

declarations.name: mobiusNumeratorPolynomial_six_fixture
declarations.signature: theorem mobiusNumeratorPolynomial_six_fixture : mobiusNumeratorPolynomial 6 = 2 + 6 * Polynomial.X + 3 * Polynomial.X ^ 2 + 4 * Polynomial.X ^ 3 + 3 * Polynomial.X ^ 4 + 6 * Polynomial.X ^ 5
declarations.docstring: First nontrivial squarefree new-prime jump, expanded coefficientwise.

declarations.name: primeJump_new_fibre_six_factorization
declarations.signature: theorem primeJump_new_fibre_six_factorization : mobiusNumeratorPolynomial 6 + Polynomial.expand ℤ 3 (mobiusNumeratorPolynomial 2) = Polynomial.cyclotomic 6 ℤ * (6 * Polynomial.X ^ 3 + 9 * Polynomial.X ^ 2 + 9 * Polynomial.X + 3)
declarations.docstring: Explicit `Φ₆` quotient for the first new fibre.

declarations.name: primeJump_old_fibre_two_factorization
declarations.signature: theorem primeJump_old_fibre_two_factorization : mobiusNumeratorPolynomial 6 - Polynomial.C (((3 : ℤ) ^ 2) - 1) * mobiusNumeratorPolynomial 2 = Polynomial.cyclotomic 2 ℤ * (6 * Polynomial.X ^ 4 - 3 * Polynomial.X ^ 3 + 7 * Polynomial.X ^ 2 - 4 * Polynomial.X - 6)
declarations.docstring: Explicit `Φ₂` quotient for the corresponding old fibre.

declarations.name: primEulerCoord
declarations.docstring: The rational primitive Euler/Witt coordinate `A(n) / n`.

declarations.name: IntegralPrimitiveEulerJet
declarations.docstring: A common positive index `D` clears every primitive Euler coordinate up to the jet horizon `N`.

declarations.name: integral_primEulerCoord_iff_dvd
declarations.signature: theorem integral_primEulerCoord_iff_dvd {n D : ℕ} (hn : n ≠ 0) : (∃ z : ℤ, (D : ℚ) * primEulerCoord n = (z : ℚ)) ↔ (n : ℤ) ∣ (D : ℤ) * primWeight n
declarations.docstring: Clearing `A(n) / n` is equivalent to the elementary divisibility condition `n ∣ D A(n)`.

declarations.name: primWeight_apply_prime_sq
declarations.signature: theorem primWeight_apply_prime_sq {p : ℕ} (hp : p.Prime) : primWeight (p ^ 2) = ((p - 1 : ℕ) : ℤ) ^ 2
declarations.docstring: Prime-square value `A(p²) = (p-1)²`.

declarations.name: prime_not_dvd_primWeight_prime_sq
declarations.signature: theorem prime_not_dvd_primWeight_prime_sq {p : ℕ} (hp : p.Prime) : ¬ (p : ℤ) ∣ primWeight (p ^ 2)
declarations.docstring: The prime `p` does not divide its prime-square primitive numerator.

declarations.name: oddPrime_dvd_index_of_integral_jet
declarations.signature: theorem oddPrime_dvd_index_of_integral_jet {N D p : ℕ} (hjet : IntegralPrimitiveEulerJet N D) (hp : p.Prime) (hpodd : p ≠ 2) (hpN : p ≤ N) : p ∣ D
declarations.docstring: Every odd prime at or below the jet horizon divides the common index.

declarations.name: primeSq_dvd_index_of_integral_jet
declarations.signature: theorem primeSq_dvd_index_of_integral_jet {N D p : ℕ} (hjet : IntegralPrimitiveEulerJet N D) (hp : p.Prime) (hpSqN : p ^ 2 ≤ N) : p ^ 2 ∣ D
declarations.docstring: A prime whose square lies below the jet horizon contributes two copies to the common index.

declarations.name: four_dvd_index_of_integral_jet
declarations.signature: theorem four_dvd_index_of_integral_jet {N D : ℕ} (hjet : IntegralPrimitiveEulerJet N D) (hN : 4 ≤ N) : 4 ∣ D
declarations.docstring: The exceptional prime `2` first contributes at the coordinate `n = 4`.

declarations.name: localPrimitiveIndex
declarations.docstring: The local index required at one prime through horizon `N`. The prime `2` contributes only its square, while an odd prime contributes once at `p` and a second time at `p²`.

declarations.name: twoTierPrimorial
declarations.docstring: Lcm form of the two-tier primorial index through horizon `N`.

declarations.name: twoTierPrimorial_dvd_of_integral_jet
declarations.signature: theorem twoTierPrimorial_dvd_of_integral_jet {N D : ℕ} (hN : 4 ≤ N) (hjet : IntegralPrimitiveEulerJet N D) : twoTierPrimorial N ∣ D
declarations.docstring: The complete finite-jet obstruction: the two-tier primorial divides every common index clearing all primitive Euler coordinates through `N`.

declarations.name: no_fixed_integral_primitive_euler_index
declarations.signature: theorem no_fixed_integral_primitive_euler_index (D : ℕ) (hD : 0 < D) : ¬ ∀ n : ℕ, 0 < n → ∃ z : ℤ, (D : ℚ) * primEulerCoord n = (z : ℚ)
declarations.docstring: No fixed positive integer clears all normalized primitive coordinates.

declarations.name: positive_rational_difference_lower_bound
declarations.signature: theorem positive_rational_difference_lower_bound {whole pfx : ℚ} (hpositive : pfx < whole) : (1 : ℝ) / (((whole.den * pfx.den : ℕ) : ℝ)) ≤ (whole : ℝ) - (pfx : ℝ)
declarations.docstring: A positive rational difference is at least the reciprocal of the product of the two reduced denominators.

declarations.name: prefixDenominator_shell_power_bound_of_rational_difference
declarations.signature: theorem prefixDenominator_shell_power_bound_of_rational_difference (whole pfx : ℚ) (K X : ℕ) (hpositive : pfx < whole) (hX : 1 ≤ X) (hupper : (whole : ℝ) - (pfx : ℝ) ≤ (K : ℝ) / (2 : ℝ) ^ X + 2 / (2 : ℝ) ^ (2 * X)) : (2 : ℝ) ^ X ≤ ((whole.den * pfx.den : ℕ) : ℝ) * (K + 1)
declarations.docstring: Direct supply adapter for the prefix-denominator shell consumer. Once the analytic shell/far-tail upper bound is known, rationality alone supplies the matching lower bound with the actual reduced denominators.

declarations.name: nextSupport_power_bound_of_rational_difference
declarations.signature: theorem nextSupport_power_bound_of_rational_difference (whole pfx : ℚ) (m : ℕ) (hpositive : pfx < whole) (hupper : (whole : ℝ) - (pfx : ℝ) ≤ 4 / (2 : ℝ) ^ m) : (2 : ℝ) ^ m ≤ 4 * ((whole.den * pfx.den : ℕ) : ℝ)
declarations.docstring: Direct supply adapter for the next-support power consumer. Any analytic bound of the form `whole-prefix ≤ 4/2^m` forces the next exponent below the product-denominator power barrier.

declarations.name: primitivePrefix
declarations.signature: def primitivePrefix (A : Set ℕ) (X : ℕ) : Set ℕ
declarations.docstring: Support already present at or below the shell cutoff.

declarations.name: primitiveResidual
declarations.signature: def primitiveResidual (A : Set ℕ) (X : ℕ) : Set ℕ
declarations.docstring: Primitive innovations not present in the prefix through `X`.

declarations.name: primitiveBit
declarations.signature: noncomputable def primitiveBit (A : Set ℕ) (n : ℕ) : ℕ
declarations.docstring: Natural-valued primitive support bit, with classical membership hidden behind a stable definition.

declarations.name: divisor_eq_self_of_cutoff_lt_of_le_two_mul
declarations.signature: theorem divisor_eq_self_of_cutoff_lt_of_le_two_mul {d n X : ℕ} (hd : d ∣ n) (hn0 : n ≠ 0) (hX : X < d) (hn : n ≤ 2 * X) : d = n
declarations.docstring: A divisor of `n` lying in `(X,2X]` is necessarily `n` itself.

declarations.name: supportCoeff_primitiveResidual_first_shell
declarations.signature: theorem supportCoeff_primitiveResidual_first_shell (A : Set ℕ) (X n : ℕ) (hXn : X < n) (hn2X : n ≤ 2 * X) : supportCoeff (primitiveResidual A X) n = primitiveBit A n
declarations.docstring: On the first shell after removing the prefix, the divisor shadow is the primitive support bit.

declarations.name: supportCoeff_eq_primitivePrefix_add_residual
declarations.signature: theorem supportCoeff_eq_primitivePrefix_add_residual (A : Set ℕ) (X n : ℕ) : supportCoeff A n = supportCoeff (primitivePrefix A X) n + supportCoeff (primitiveResidual A X) n
declarations.docstring: The support coefficient splits exactly into prefix shadow and residual shadow.

declarations.name: binaryCoeffTail_supportCoeff_eq_prefix_add_residual
declarations.signature: theorem binaryCoeffTail_supportCoeff_eq_prefix_add_residual (A : Set ℕ) (X N : ℕ) : binaryCoeffTail (supportCoeff A) N = binaryCoeffTail (supportCoeff (primitivePrefix A X)) N + binaryCoeffTail (supportCoeff (primitiveResidual A X)) N
declarations.docstring: Binary tails respect the exact prefix/residual support split.

declarations.name: scaledSupportCarry
declarations.signature: noncomputable def scaledSupportCarry (q : ℕ) (A : Set ℕ) (N : ℕ) : ℝ
declarations.docstring: Full support carry at a displayed multiplier `q`.

declarations.name: scaledPrefixCarry
declarations.signature: noncomputable def scaledPrefixCarry (D : ℕ) (A : Set ℕ) (X N : ℕ) : ℝ
declarations.docstring: Finite-prefix background carry at its reduced denominator `D`.

declarations.name: primitiveCrossCarry
declarations.signature: noncomputable def primitiveCrossCarry (q D : ℕ) (A : Set ℕ) (X N : ℕ) : ℝ
declarations.docstring: Cross-carry obtained by subtracting the deterministic prefix background from the full carry at common scale `qD`.

declarations.name: primitiveCrossCarry_eq_residualTail
declarations.signature: theorem primitiveCrossCarry_eq_residualTail (q D : ℕ) (A : Set ℕ) (X N : ℕ) : primitiveCrossCarry q D A X N = (q * D : ℕ) * binaryCoeffTail (supportCoeff (primitiveResidual A X)) N
declarations.docstring: Exact cross-carry identity: the subtraction leaves the scaled residual tail and no prefix shadow.

declarations.name: primitiveCrossCarry_first_shell_recurrence
declarations.signature: theorem primitiveCrossCarry_first_shell_recurrence (q D : ℕ) (A : Set ℕ) (X N : ℕ) (hXN : X < N + 1) (hN2X : N + 1 ≤ 2 * X) : primitiveCrossCarry q D A X (N + 1) = 2 * primitiveCrossCarry q D A X N - (q * D : ℕ) * primitiveBit A (N + 1)
declarations.docstring: On the first residual shell, the cross-carry is forced by the primitive bit rather than by the full divisor coefficient.

declarations.name: primitiveCrossCarry_pos
declarations.signature: theorem primitiveCrossCarry_pos (q D : ℕ) (hq : 0 < q) (hD : 0 < D) (A : Set ℕ) (X N : ℕ) (hres : ∃ a : ℕ, 0 < a ∧ a ∈ primitiveResidual A X) : 0 < primitiveCrossCarry q D A X N
declarations.docstring: A nonempty residual support makes the cross-carry strictly positive.

declarations.name: prefixDenominator_shell_power_bound
declarations.signature: theorem prefixDenominator_shell_power_bound (q D K X : ℕ) (hq : 0 < q) (hD : 0 < D) (hX : 1 ≤ X) (beta : ℝ) (hlower : (1 : ℝ) / ((q * D : ℕ) : ℝ) ≤ beta) (hupper : beta ≤ (K : ℝ) / (2 : ℝ) ^ X + 2 / (2 : ℝ) ^ (2 * X)) : ((2 : ℝ) ^ X) ≤ ((q * D : ℕ) : ℝ) * (K + 1)
declarations.docstring: If a positive residual is at least `1/(qD)` but its first dyadic shell and geometric far tail contribute at most `K/2^X + 2/2^(2X)`, then `2^X ≤ qD(K+1)`. This is the division-free denominator/shell pressure inequality; the analytic estimates are explicit hypotheses.

declarations.name: nextSupport_power_bound
declarations.signature: theorem nextSupport_power_bound (q D m : ℕ) (hq : 0 < q) (hD : 0 < D) (beta : ℝ) (hlower : (1 : ℝ) / ((q * D : ℕ) : ℝ) ≤ beta) (hupper : beta ≤ 4 / (2 : ℝ) ^ m) : (2 : ℝ) ^ m ≤ 4 * ((q * D : ℕ) : ℝ)
declarations.docstring: The same elementary pressure excludes a next support exponent whose power of two is larger than `4qD`.

declarations.name: primitiveCarry
declarations.docstring: The dyadic carry residue used by the landed totient gap consumer.

declarations.name: ReducedDenominatorUnitGapCert
declarations.signature: def ReducedDenominatorUnitGapCert (u N K : ℕ) : Prop
declarations.docstring: A reduced-denominator unit-gap certificate. Writing `u * primitiveCarry N K = J * 2^K + W`, the positive tail can only cross the integers `J+1, ..., J+L`, where `L = (W + u*(N+K+2))/2^K`. The certificate says that every such candidate is a nonunit modulo the odd reduced denominator `u`.

declarations.name: reducedDenominatorCandidateCount
declarations.signature: def reducedDenominatorCandidateCount (u N K : ℕ) : ℕ
declarations.docstring: The exact candidate count associated to a unit-gap certificate.

declarations.name: PrimitiveReducedDenominatorUnitGapSupply
declarations.docstring: The exact residual supply exposed by the reduced-denominator consumer. It is deliberately a proposition, not an axiom or a claimed theorem.

declarations.name: reducedDenominatorUnitGapCert_prime_pow_iff
declarations.signature: theorem reducedDenominatorUnitGapCert_prime_pow_iff {p e N K : ℕ} (hp : Nat.Prime p) (he : 0 < e) : ReducedDenominatorUnitGapCert (p ^ e) N K ↔ let M
declarations.docstring: At a prime-power reduced denominator, nonunits are exactly multiples of the underlying prime. Since two consecutive integers are coprime, the unit-gap extension can admit at most one candidate lattice point.

declarations.name: reducedDenominatorCandidateCount_prime_pow_le_one
declarations.signature: theorem reducedDenominatorCandidateCount_prime_pow_le_one {p e N K : ℕ} (hp : Nat.Prime p) (he : 0 < e) (hcert : ReducedDenominatorUnitGapCert (p ^ e) N K) : reducedDenominatorCandidateCount (p ^ e) N K ≤ 1
declarations.docstring: Numerical form of the prime-power ceiling: a certified interval for an odd prime power contains zero or one candidate, never two.

declarations.name: primitiveReducedDenominatorUnitGapSupply_prime_power_projection
declarations.signature: theorem primitiveReducedDenominatorUnitGapSupply_prime_power_projection (hsupply : PrimitiveReducedDenominatorUnitGapSupply) {p e a : ℕ} (hp : Nat.Prime p) (hpodd : Odd p) (he : 0 < e) : ∃ N K : ℕ, a ≤ N ∧ 0 < K ∧ ReducedDenominatorUnitGapCert (p ^ e) N K ∧ reducedDenominatorCandidateCount (p ^ e) N K ≤ 1
declarations.docstring: Every hypothetical all-denominator unit-gap supply must in particular solve the depth-growing zero-or-one-candidate problem for every odd prime power. This is the formal residual/no-go boundary, not a supply result.

declarations.name: reducedDenominatorUnitGapCert_three_window_3_5
declarations.docstring: The first strict strengthening over the ordinary empty-gap certificate: at `(u,N,K)=(3,3,5)` the dyadic interval contains the single integer `3`, which is forbidden because it is a nonunit modulo the reduced denominator.

declarations.name: not_ordinary_gap_certificate_three_window_3_5
declarations.docstring: The same row is not an ordinary empty-gap certificate: its scaled residue plus tail height is `38`, while the dyadic modulus is `32`.

declarations.name: primitiveCarry_three_window_3_5
declarations.docstring: Exact arithmetic in the strict-strengthening regression row.

declarations.name: sum_powerset_singleton
declarations.signature: private theorem sum_powerset_singleton {α β : Type*} [DecidableEq α] [AddCommMonoid β] (a : α) (f : Finset α → β) : ∑ t ∈ ({a} : Finset α).powerset, f t = f ∅ + f {a}

declarations.name: mersenne
declarations.docstring: The Mersenne denominator at exponent `n`.

declarations.name: squarefreeKernel
declarations.docstring: The squarefree kernel used by the numeric shadow: the product of the distinct prime factors of `n`. For `n = 0` this convention gives `1`; all development-facing scaling theorems assume `0 < n`.

declarations.name: squarefreeKernel_idem
declarations.signature: theorem squarefreeKernel_idem (n : ℕ) : squarefreeKernel (squarefreeKernel n) = squarefreeKernel n

declarations.name: squarefreeKernel_dvd
declarations.signature: theorem squarefreeKernel_dvd (n : ℕ) : squarefreeKernel n ∣ n

declarations.name: squarefreeKernel_squarefree
declarations.docstring: The canonical kernel is squarefree, including at the convention `squarefreeKernel 0 = 1`.

declarations.name: prime_dvd_squarefreeKernel_iff
declarations.signature: theorem prime_dvd_squarefreeKernel_iff {n p : ℕ} (hn : n ≠ 0) (hp : p.Prime) : p ∣ squarefreeKernel n ↔ p ∣ n
declarations.docstring: Away from the exceptional zero convention, taking the squarefree kernel preserves exactly the prime divisors.

declarations.name: mobiusNumerator
declarations.docstring: The integral numerator, written as its squarefree-divisor expansion. For `s ⊆ primeFactors(r)`, put `d = ∏ p ∈ s, p`. Then the summand is `(-1)^|s| (r/d) ((2^r-1)/(2^d-1))`. This is exactly the nonzero part of `Σ_{d ∣ r} μ(d) (r/d) ((2^r-1)/(2^d-1))`: nonsquarefree divisors have Möbius coefficient zero. The subset form makes that finite support explicit and keeps the definition executable without factoring irrelevant divisors.

declarations.name: baseMobiusShadow
declarations.docstring: The unscaled radical shadow `B(r) = M_r / (2^r - 1)`.

declarations.name: numericMobiusShadow
declarations.docstring: The numeric shadow at an arbitrary scale. By construction it only sees the distinct prime factors of `H`.

declarations.name: numericMobiusShadow_radical
declarations.signature: theorem numericMobiusShadow_radical (H : ℕ) : numericMobiusShadow H = numericMobiusShadow (squarefreeKernel H)
declarations.docstring: Exact radical reduction: repeated prime powers do not change the numeric Möbius shadow.

declarations.name: scaledMobiusShadow_eq_radicalBase
declarations.signature: theorem scaledMobiusShadow_eq_radicalBase (H : ℕ) (_hH : 0 < H) : (H : ℚ) * numericMobiusShadow H = ((H / squarefreeKernel H : ℕ) : ℚ) * baseMobiusShadow (squarefreeKernel H)
declarations.docstring: Scaling by `H` is exactly scaling the unscaled radical shadow by `H / rad(H)`. This theorem deliberately leaves `B(r)` reduced separately; the factor `H / r` may cause further cancellation.

declarations.name: scaledMobiusShadow_eq_radicalNumerator
declarations.signature: theorem scaledMobiusShadow_eq_radicalNumerator (H : ℕ) (hH : 0 < H) : (H : ℚ) * numericMobiusShadow H = ((H / squarefreeKernel H : ℕ) : ℚ) * Rat.divInt (mobiusNumerator (squarefreeKernel H)) (mersenne (squarefreeKernel H) : ℤ)
declarations.docstring: The development's scaled formula with the numerator and Mersenne denominator displayed explicitly.

declarations.name: baseMobiusShadow_den
declarations.signature: theorem baseMobiusShadow_den (r : ℕ) (hr : 0 < r) : (baseMobiusShadow r).den = mersenne r / (mobiusNumerator r).natAbs.gcd (mersenne r)
declarations.docstring: Exact reduced denominator of the unscaled shadow. This is the generic gcd formula in T6; no coprimality or channel-survival hypothesis is hidden.

declarations.name: baseMobiusShadow_den_of_gcd_eq
declarations.signature: theorem baseMobiusShadow_den_of_gcd_eq (r g : ℕ) (hr : 0 < r) (hgcd : (mobiusNumerator r).natAbs.gcd (mersenne r) = g) : (baseMobiusShadow r).den = mersenne r / g
declarations.docstring: A reusable corollary: a kernel-checked gcd certificate immediately gives the displayed reduced denominator.

declarations.name: mobiusNumerator_prime
declarations.signature: theorem mobiusNumerator_prime {p : ℕ} (hp : p.Prime) : mobiusNumerator p = (p : ℤ) * (mersenne p : ℤ) - 1
declarations.docstring: For a prime radical there are only the empty and singleton subsets, so the Möbius numerator is `p(2^p-1)-1`.

declarations.name: mobiusNumerator_prime_gcd
declarations.signature: theorem mobiusNumerator_prime_gcd {p : ℕ} (hp : p.Prime) : (mobiusNumerator p).natAbs.gcd (mersenne p) = 1
declarations.docstring: The prime numerator is coprime to its Mersenne denominator.

declarations.name: baseMobiusShadow_den_prime
declarations.signature: theorem baseMobiusShadow_den_prime {p : ℕ} (hp : p.Prime) : (baseMobiusShadow p).den = mersenne p
declarations.docstring: Exact prime-radical denominator from the generic gcd formula.

declarations.name: mobiusNumerator_twicePrime
declarations.signature: theorem mobiusNumerator_twicePrime {p : ℕ} (hp : p.Prime) (hp5 : 5 ≤ p) : mobiusNumerator (2 * p) = ((2 * p : ℕ) : ℤ) * (mersenne (2 * p) : ℤ) - (p : ℤ) * ((mersenne (2 * p) / 3 : ℕ) : ℤ) - 2 * ((mersenne (2 * p) / mersenne p : ℕ) : ℤ) + 1
declarations.docstring: The four-term numerator expansion for a twice-prime radical. This is the exact algebraic fixture used before proving the gcd is `3`; the natural quotients are exact because `3 = 2^2-1` and `2^p-1` divide `2^(2p)-1`.

declarations.name: prime_mod_six_eq_one_or_five
declarations.signature: private theorem prime_mod_six_eq_one_or_five {p : ℕ} (hp : p.Prime) (hp5 : 5 ≤ p) : p % 6 = 1 ∨ p % 6 = 5

declarations.name: prime_pow_two_mod_nine_cases
declarations.signature: private theorem prime_pow_two_mod_nine_cases {p : ℕ} (hp : p.Prime) (hp5 : 5 ≤ p) : (p % 6 = 1 ∧ 2 ^ p % 9 = 2) ∨ (p % 6 = 5 ∧ 2 ^ p % 9 = 5)

declarations.name: mobiusNumerator_twicePrime_gcd
declarations.signature: theorem mobiusNumerator_twicePrime_gcd {p : ℕ} (hp : p.Prime) (hp5 : 5 ≤ p) : (mobiusNumerator (2 * p)).natAbs.gcd (mersenne (2 * p)) = 3
declarations.docstring: For every prime `p ≥ 5`, the twice-prime numerator has exactly the factor `3` in common with its Mersenne denominator.

declarations.name: baseMobiusShadow_den_twicePrime
declarations.signature: theorem baseMobiusShadow_den_twicePrime {p : ℕ} (hp : p.Prime) (hp5 : 5 ≤ p) : (baseMobiusShadow (2 * p)).den = mersenne (2 * p) / 3
declarations.docstring: Exact reduced denominator for a twice-prime radical.

declarations.name: prime5_numerator_fixture
declarations.docstring: Exact reduced denominator for a twice-prime radical. -/ theorem baseMobiusShadow_den_twicePrime {p : ℕ} (hp : p.Prime) (hp5 : 5 ≤ p) : (baseMobiusShadow (2 * p)).den = mersenne (2 * p) / 3 := by exact baseMobiusShadow_den_of_gcd_eq (2 * p) 3 (by positivity) (mobiusNumerator_twicePrime_gcd hp hp5) /-! ## Exact regression fixtures The first two fixtures use the smallest development-relevant prime `p = 5` and its double. The `210` fixture checks the full denominator claimed in T6 without materialising any later large-LCM object.

declarations.name: divisor_dvd_divInt_den
declarations.signature: theorem divisor_dvd_divInt_den {a : ℤ} {D m : ℕ} (hD : 0 < D) (hmD : m ∣ D) (hcop : Nat.Coprime m a.natAbs) : m ∣ (Rat.divInt a (D : ℤ)).den
declarations.docstring: A divisor of a displayed denominator survives rational reduction when it is coprime to the displayed numerator.

declarations.name: survivingDivisor_dvd_scaled_divInt_den
declarations.signature: theorem survivingDivisor_dvd_scaled_divInt_den {a : ℤ} {D C h : ℕ} (hD : 0 < D) (hCD : C ∣ D) (hcop : Nat.Coprime C a.natAbs) : C / Nat.gcd C h ∣ (Rat.divInt ((h : ℤ) * a) (D : ℤ)).den
declarations.docstring: Scaling can cancel the part `gcd C h`, but no further part of `C` when `C` was coprime to the unscaled numerator.

declarations.name: doublingResidue
declarations.signature: def doublingResidue (p v n : ℕ) : ℕ
declarations.docstring: Least nonnegative residue of `p*2^n` modulo `v`. Under the T9 coprimality hypotheses it is automatically the least *positive* residue.

declarations.name: doublingWrapDigit
declarations.signature: def doublingWrapDigit (p v n : ℕ) : ℕ
declarations.docstring: The quotient expelled when a least residue is doubled. Since the residue is below `v`, this quotient is always zero or one.

declarations.name: doublingWrapCount
declarations.signature: def doublingWrapCount (p v h : ℕ) : ℕ
declarations.docstring: Number of binary wraps in the first `h` transitions.

declarations.name: IsDoublingPeriod
declarations.docstring: The formal development.s period hypothesis, separated from minimality: `h>0` and `2^h = 1 (mod v)`. Substituting the multiplicative order supplies this.

declarations.name: doublingResidue_lt
declarations.signature: theorem doublingResidue_lt (p : ℕ) {v : ℕ} (hv : 0 < v) (n : ℕ) : doublingResidue p v n < v

declarations.name: doublingWrapDigit_le_one
declarations.signature: theorem doublingWrapDigit_le_one (p : ℕ) {v : ℕ} (hv : 0 < v) (n : ℕ) : doublingWrapDigit p v n ≤ 1

declarations.name: doublingWrapDigit_eq_zero_or_one
declarations.signature: theorem doublingWrapDigit_eq_zero_or_one (p : ℕ) {v : ℕ} (hv : 0 < v) (n : ℕ) : doublingWrapDigit p v n = 0 ∨ doublingWrapDigit p v n = 1

declarations.name: doublingResidue_pos
declarations.signature: theorem doublingResidue_pos (p : ℕ) {v : ℕ} (hv : 1 < v) (hvodd : Odd v) (hpv : p.Coprime v) (j : ℕ) : 0 < doublingResidue p v j
declarations.docstring: For an odd modulus and reduced numerator, every canonical residue is strictly positive.

declarations.name: doublingResidue_succ
