declarations.signature: theorem doublingResidue_succ (p v n : ℕ) : doublingResidue p v (n + 1) = (2 * doublingResidue p v n) % v
declarations.docstring: Doubling and reducing advances the canonical residue cycle by one.

declarations.name: doublingResidue_carry
declarations.signature: theorem doublingResidue_carry (p v n : ℕ) : 2 * doublingResidue p v n = v * doublingWrapDigit p v n + doublingResidue p v (n + 1)
declarations.docstring: Exact binary carry recurrence for canonical residues.

declarations.name: doublingResidue_add_period
declarations.signature: theorem doublingResidue_add_period (p : ℕ) {v h : ℕ} (hperiod : IsDoublingPeriod v h) (n : ℕ) : doublingResidue p v (n + h) = doublingResidue p v n
declarations.docstring: A doubling period closes every numerator residue cycle.

declarations.name: doublingResidue_add
declarations.signature: theorem doublingResidue_add (p v N j : ℕ) : doublingResidue p v (N + j) = doublingResidue (p * 2 ^ N) v j
declarations.docstring: Shifting the tail index multiplies the starting numerator by the same power of two.

declarations.name: sum_range_shift_one
declarations.signature: theorem sum_range_shift_one (r : ℕ → ℕ) (h : ℕ) : (∑ j ∈ Finset.range h, r (j + 1)) + r 0 = (∑ j ∈ Finset.range h, r j) + r h
declarations.docstring: Summing a shifted finite sequence differs only by its two endpoints.

declarations.name: sum_range_add_eq_sum_range_of_periodic
declarations.signature: theorem sum_range_add_eq_sum_range_of_periodic (r : ℕ → ℕ) (h : ℕ) (hperiod : ∀ N : ℕ, r (N + h) = r N) : ∀ N : ℕ, ∑ j ∈ Finset.range h, r (N + j) = ∑ j ∈ Finset.range h, r j
declarations.docstring: Every complete block of a periodic natural sequence has the same sum.

declarations.name: sum_eq_modulus_mul_wrapCount_of_closed_cycle
declarations.signature: theorem sum_eq_modulus_mul_wrapCount_of_closed_cycle (v h : ℕ) (r k : ℕ → ℕ) (hcarry : ∀ j : ℕ, 2 * r j = v * k j + r (j + 1)) (hclosed : r h = r 0) : ∑ j ∈ Finset.range h, r j = v * ∑ j ∈ Finset.range h, k j
declarations.docstring: **Wrap identity for a closed binary residue cycle.**

declarations.name: sum_doublingResidue_eq_mul_wrapCount
declarations.signature: theorem sum_doublingResidue_eq_mul_wrapCount (p : ℕ) {v h : ℕ} (hperiod : IsDoublingPeriod v h) : ∑ j ∈ Finset.range h, doublingResidue p v j = v * doublingWrapCount p v h
declarations.docstring: Canonical form of the wrap identity.

declarations.name: doublingWrapCount_pos
declarations.signature: theorem doublingWrapCount_pos (p : ℕ) {v h : ℕ} (hv : 1 < v) (hvodd : Odd v) (hpv : p.Coprime v) (hperiod : IsDoublingPeriod v h) : 0 < doublingWrapCount p v h
declarations.docstring: A nonempty positive closed residue cycle has at least one wrap.

declarations.name: doublingWrapCount_mul_pow_eq
declarations.signature: theorem doublingWrapCount_mul_pow_eq (p : ℕ) {v h : ℕ} (hv : 0 < v) (hperiod : IsDoublingPeriod v h) (N : ℕ) : doublingWrapCount (p * 2 ^ N) v h = doublingWrapCount p v h
declarations.docstring: The wrap count is invariant under rotation of a complete residue cycle.

declarations.name: exists_unique_wrap_of_sum_eq_one
declarations.signature: theorem exists_unique_wrap_of_sum_eq_one (h : ℕ) (wrap : ℕ → ℕ) (hbit : ∀ j < h, wrap j ≤ 1) (hone : ∑ j ∈ Finset.range h, wrap j = 1) : ∃ t < h, wrap t = 1 ∧ ∀ j < h, j ≠ t → wrap j = 0
declarations.docstring: A bit-valued family whose sum is one has one and only one nonzero position in the displayed range.

declarations.name: accumulatedWrap
declarations.signature: def accumulatedWrap (wrap : ℕ → ℕ) : ℕ → ℕ | 0 => 0 | n + 1 => 2 * accumulatedWrap wrap n + wrap n /-- Unroll the binary carry recurrence through a finite prefix. -/ theorem recurrence_unroll (v n : ℕ) (r wrap : ℕ → ℕ) (hrec : ∀ j < n, 2 * r j = v * wrap j + r (j + 1)) : 2 ^ n * r 0 = r n + v * accumulatedWrap wrap n
declarations.docstring: Binary value represented by a prefix of wrap digits, with the most recent digit least significant in `E(n+1)=2E(n)+wrap(n)`.

declarations.name: recurrence_unroll
declarations.signature: theorem recurrence_unroll (v n : ℕ) (r wrap : ℕ → ℕ) (hrec : ∀ j < n, 2 * r j = v * wrap j + r (j + 1)) : 2 ^ n * r 0 = r n + v * accumulatedWrap wrap n
declarations.docstring: Unroll the binary carry recurrence through a finite prefix.

declarations.name: accumulatedWrap_eq_zero_of_zero
declarations.signature: private theorem accumulatedWrap_eq_zero_of_zero (wrap : ℕ → ℕ) (n : ℕ) (hzero : ∀ j < n, wrap j = 0) : accumulatedWrap wrap n = 0

declarations.name: accumulatedWrap_eq_pow_of_unique
declarations.signature: theorem accumulatedWrap_eq_pow_of_unique (wrap : ℕ → ℕ) {h t : ℕ} (ht : t < h) (htone : wrap t = 1) (hother : ∀ j < h, j ≠ t → wrap j = 0) : accumulatedWrap wrap h = 2 ^ (h - 1 - t)

declarations.name: two_pow_sub_one_mod_two
declarations.signature: private theorem two_pow_sub_one_mod_two {h : ℕ} (hh : 0 < h) : (2 ^ h - 1) % 2 = 1

declarations.name: one_wrap_cycle_classification
declarations.signature: theorem one_wrap_cycle_classification (v h : ℕ) (r wrap : ℕ → ℕ) (hh : 0 < h) (hrcop : (r 0).Coprime v) (hclosed : r h = r 0) (hrec : ∀ j < h, 2 * r j = v * wrap j + r (j + 1)) (hbit : ∀ j < h, wrap j ≤ 1) (hone : ∑ j ∈ Finset.range h, wrap j = 1) : v = 2 ^ h - 1 ∧ ∃ a < h, r 0 = 2 ^ a
declarations.docstring: Full algebraic classification of a one-wrap positive closed cycle. Its starting residue is a power of two and its modulus is Mersenne.

declarations.name: one_doublingWrap_classification
declarations.signature: theorem one_doublingWrap_classification (p : ℕ) {v h : ℕ} (hv : 1 < v) (_hvodd : Odd v) (hpv : p.Coprime v) (hperiod : IsDoublingPeriod v h) (hone : doublingWrapCount p v h = 1) : v = 2 ^ h - 1 ∧ ∃ a < h, doublingResidue p v 0 = 2 ^ a
declarations.docstring: Canonical one-wrap theorem: the odd denominator is `2^h-1`, and the numerator residue lies in the power-of-two orbit.

declarations.name: oddDoublingOrder
declarations.signature: noncomputable def oddDoublingOrder (v : ℕ) (hvodd : Odd v) : ℕ
declarations.docstring: Mathlib realization of `ord_v(2)` for an odd modulus.

declarations.name: pow_oddDoublingOrder_mod_eq_one
declarations.signature: theorem pow_oddDoublingOrder_mod_eq_one {v : ℕ} (hv : 1 < v) (hvodd : Odd v) : 2 ^ oddDoublingOrder v hvodd % v = 1

declarations.name: oddDoublingOrder_pos
declarations.signature: theorem oddDoublingOrder_pos {v : ℕ} (hvodd : Odd v) : 0 < oddDoublingOrder v hvodd

declarations.name: oddDoublingOrder_isPeriod
declarations.signature: theorem oddDoublingOrder_isPeriod {v : ℕ} (hv : 1 < v) (hvodd : Odd v) : IsDoublingPeriod v (oddDoublingOrder v hvodd)
declarations.docstring: The actual multiplicative order closes the canonical residue cycle.

declarations.name: oddOrder_doublingWrapCount_pos
declarations.signature: theorem oddOrder_doublingWrapCount_pos (p : ℕ) {v : ℕ} (hv : 1 < v) (hvodd : Odd v) (hpv : p.Coprime v) : 0 < doublingWrapCount p v (oddDoublingOrder v hvodd)

declarations.name: one_oddOrder_doublingWrap_classification
declarations.signature: theorem one_oddOrder_doublingWrap_classification (p : ℕ) {v : ℕ} (hv : 1 < v) (hvodd : Odd v) (hpv : p.Coprime v) (hone : doublingWrapCount p v (oddDoublingOrder v hvodd) = 1) : v = 2 ^ oddDoublingOrder v hvodd - 1 ∧ ∃ a < oddDoublingOrder v hvodd, doublingResidue p v 0 = 2 ^ a
declarations.docstring: Order-instantiated one-wrap classification in the notation of T9.

declarations.name: IsOddSupportTailState
declarations.signature: def IsOddSupportTailState (A : Set ℕ) (p v : ℕ) (u : ℕ → ℕ) : Prop
declarations.docstring: A natural state which is simultaneously the odd-denominator scaling of the true support tail and the canonical doubling residue of a numerator. The rational-fraction bridge below constructs this state after removing the power of two from a displayed denominator.

declarations.name: oddTailExcess
declarations.signature: def oddTailExcess (u : ℕ → ℕ) (v N : ℕ) : ℕ
declarations.docstring: Integral excess of an odd tail state above its least residue.

declarations.name: oddSupportTail_eq_residue_div_add_excess
declarations.signature: theorem oddSupportTail_eq_residue_div_add_excess (A : Set ℕ) (p : ℕ) {v : ℕ} (hv : 0 < v) (u : ℕ → ℕ) (hstate : IsOddSupportTailState A p v u) (N : ℕ) : binaryCoeffTail (supportCoeff A) N = (doublingResidue p v N : ℝ) / (v : ℝ) + (oddTailExcess u v N : ℝ)
declarations.docstring: Exact pointwise decomposition of a genuine odd tail state into its least residue and a nonnegative integral excess.

declarations.name: sum_supportTail_block_eq_wrapCount_add_excess
declarations.signature: theorem sum_supportTail_block_eq_wrapCount_add_excess (A : Set ℕ) (p : ℕ) {v h : ℕ} (hv : 0 < v) (hperiod : IsDoublingPeriod v h) (u : ℕ → ℕ) (hstate : IsOddSupportTailState A p v u) (N : ℕ) : ∑ j ∈ Finset.range h, binaryCoeffTail (supportCoeff A) (N + j) = (doublingWrapCount (p * 2 ^ N) v h : ℝ) + ∑ j ∈ Finset.range h, (oddTailExcess u v (N + j) : ℝ)
declarations.docstring: Exact block identity: a complete residue cycle contributes its wrap count, and every additional lift of a tail state contributes one nonnegative integer. This is the finite algebra behind both the order lower bound and the formal development.s exact excess-mean identity.

declarations.name: wrapCount_le_sum_supportTail_block
declarations.signature: theorem wrapCount_le_sum_supportTail_block (A : Set ℕ) (p : ℕ) {v h : ℕ} (hv : 0 < v) (hperiod : IsDoublingPeriod v h) (u : ℕ → ℕ) (hstate : IsOddSupportTailState A p v u) (N : ℕ) : (doublingWrapCount (p * 2 ^ N) v h : ℝ) ≤ ∑ j ∈ Finset.range h, binaryCoeffTail (supportCoeff A) (N + j)
declarations.docstring: Dropping the nonnegative excess terms gives the order-sensitive local block lower bound.

declarations.name: numerator_pos_of_support_fraction
declarations.signature: theorem numerator_pos_of_support_fraction (A : Set ℕ) (hA : ∃ a : ℕ, 0 < a ∧ a ∈ A) (p : ℤ) (c v : ℕ) (hv : 0 < v) (hvalue : erdosSupportSeries 2 A = (p : ℝ) / ((2 ^ c * v : ℕ) : ℝ)) : 0 < p
declarations.docstring: A nonempty positive support forces the numerator of every displayed positive-denominator fraction to be positive.

declarations.name: exists_shifted_odd_tail_states_of_support_fraction
declarations.signature: theorem exists_shifted_odd_tail_states_of_support_fraction (A : Set ℕ) (hA : ∃ a : ℕ, 0 < a ∧ a ∈ A) (p : ℤ) (c v : ℕ) (hv : 0 < v) (hvalue : erdosSupportSeries 2 A = (p : ℝ) / ((2 ^ c * v : ℕ) : ℝ)) : ∃ u : ℕ → ℤ, (∀ n : ℕ, (u n : ℝ) = (v : ℝ) * binaryCoeffTail (supportCoeff A) (c + n)) ∧ (∀ n : ℕ, 0 < u n) ∧ (∀ n : ℕ, u (n + 1) = 2 * u n - (v : ℤ) * (supportCoeff A (c + n + 1) : ℤ)) ∧ (∀ n : ℕ, u n ≡ (2 : ℤ) ^ n * p [ZMOD (v : ℤ)])
declarations.docstring: Removing the power-of-two part of a displayed denominator gives a positive integer state equal to `v` times the shifted true tail. The state also carries the exact additive recurrence and its numerator residue class.

declarations.name: exists_shifted_odd_tail_nat_states_of_support_fraction
declarations.signature: theorem exists_shifted_odd_tail_nat_states_of_support_fraction (A : Set ℕ) (hA : ∃ a : ℕ, 0 < a ∧ a ∈ A) (p : ℤ) (c v : ℕ) (hv : 0 < v) (hvalue : erdosSupportSeries 2 A = (p : ℝ) / ((2 ^ c * v : ℕ) : ℝ)) : ∃ u : ℕ → ℕ, (∀ n : ℕ, (u n : ℝ) = (v : ℝ) * binaryCoeffTail (supportCoeff A) (c + n)) ∧ (∀ n : ℕ, 0 < u n) ∧ (∀ n : ℕ, u (n + 1) + v * supportCoeff A (c + n + 1) = 2 * u n) ∧ (∀ n : ℕ, u n ≡ p.toNat * 2 ^ n [MOD v])
declarations.docstring: Natural-number form of the shifted odd tail state.

declarations.name: wrapCount_le_sum_tail_of_modEq
declarations.signature: theorem wrapCount_le_sum_tail_of_modEq (coeff : ℕ → ℕ) (shift p : ℕ) {v h : ℕ} (hv : 0 < v) (hperiod : IsDoublingPeriod v h) (u : ℕ → ℕ) (htail : ∀ n : ℕ, (u n : ℝ) = (v : ℝ) * binaryCoeffTail coeff (shift + n)) (hmod : ∀ n : ℕ, u n ≡ p * 2 ^ n [MOD v]) (N : ℕ) : (doublingWrapCount (p * 2 ^ N) v h : ℝ) ≤ ∑ j ∈ Finset.range h, binaryCoeffTail coeff (shift + (N + j))
declarations.docstring: Any shifted natural tail state with the correct numerator congruence inherits the canonical wrap-count lower bound on every complete block.

declarations.name: support_fraction_wrapCount_le_tail_block
declarations.signature: theorem support_fraction_wrapCount_le_tail_block (A : Set ℕ) (hA : ∃ a : ℕ, 0 < a ∧ a ∈ A) (p : ℤ) (c : ℕ) {v h : ℕ} (hv : 0 < v) (hvalue : erdosSupportSeries 2 A = (p : ℝ) / ((2 ^ c * v : ℕ) : ℝ)) (hperiod : IsDoublingPeriod v h) (N : ℕ) : (doublingWrapCount (p.toNat * 2 ^ N) v h : ℝ) ≤ ∑ j ∈ Finset.range h, binaryCoeffTail (supportCoeff A) (c + (N + j))
declarations.docstring: Fraction-facing local order-wrap theorem. The only normalization here is the explicit denominator `2^c*v`; reducedness and oddness enter the order corollary below.

declarations.name: reciprocalSupportTerm
declarations.signature: noncomputable def reciprocalSupportTerm (A : Set ℕ) (a : ℕ) : ℝ
declarations.docstring: The reciprocal summand of a support, with exponent zero harmlessly normalized to zero by real division.

declarations.name: reciprocalMass
declarations.signature: noncomputable def reciprocalMass (A : Set ℕ) : ℝ
declarations.docstring: The reciprocal mass `ρ(A) = ∑_{a∈A} 1/a`.

declarations.name: ReciprocalMassDivergesOrAtLeast
declarations.signature: def ReciprocalMassDivergesOrAtLeast (A : Set ℕ) (r : ℝ) : Prop
declarations.docstring: Extended-real-free statement of a reciprocal-mass lower bound: either the reciprocal family diverges, in which case every finite lower bound is automatic, or its real tsum is at least `r`.

declarations.name: divisorFrequency
declarations.signature: noncomputable def divisorFrequency (A : Set ℕ) (N a : ℕ) : ℝ
declarations.docstring: The finite divisor frequency of `a` in the first `N` positive integers.

declarations.name: sum_supportCoeff_range_eq_sum_div
declarations.signature: theorem sum_supportCoeff_range_eq_sum_div (A : Set ℕ) (N : ℕ) : ∑ n ∈ Finset.range N, supportCoeff A (n + 1) = ∑ a ∈ Finset.range (N + 1), Set.indicator A (fun a : ℕ => N / a) a
declarations.docstring: Exact finite double counting: summing the support coefficient through `N` is the sum of the counts `⌊N/a⌋` over the support.

declarations.name: tendsto_nat_div_frequency
declarations.signature: theorem tendsto_nat_div_frequency (a : ℕ) (ha : 0 < a) : Tendsto (fun N : ℕ => ((N / a : ℕ) : ℝ) / (N : ℝ)) atTop (nhds ((1 : ℝ) / (a : ℝ)))

declarations.name: tendsto_divisorFrequency
declarations.signature: theorem tendsto_divisorFrequency (A : Set ℕ) (a : ℕ) : Tendsto (fun N : ℕ => divisorFrequency A N a) atTop (nhds (reciprocalSupportTerm A a))

declarations.name: norm_divisorFrequency_le_reciprocalSupportTerm
declarations.signature: theorem norm_divisorFrequency_le_reciprocalSupportTerm (A : Set ℕ) (N a : ℕ) : ‖divisorFrequency A N a‖ ≤ reciprocalSupportTerm A a

declarations.name: tsum_divisorFrequency_eq_supportCoeff_mean
declarations.signature: theorem tsum_divisorFrequency_eq_supportCoeff_mean (A : Set ℕ) (N : ℕ) : ∑' a : ℕ, divisorFrequency A N a = ((∑ n ∈ Finset.range N, supportCoeff A (n + 1) : ℕ) : ℝ) / (N : ℝ)
declarations.docstring: The frequency tsum is exactly the Cesàro mean of `supportCoeff`.

declarations.name: tendsto_supportCoeff_mean_reciprocalMass
declarations.signature: theorem tendsto_supportCoeff_mean_reciprocalMass (A : Set ℕ) (hsum : Summable (reciprocalSupportTerm A)) : Tendsto (fun N : ℕ => ((∑ n ∈ Finset.range N, supportCoeff A (n + 1) : ℕ) : ℝ) / (N : ℝ)) atTop (nhds (reciprocalMass A))
declarations.docstring: **Analytic T9 core.** For a support with summable reciprocals, the Cesàro mean of its divisor-count coefficient tends exactly to `ρ(A)`.

declarations.name: le_reciprocalMass_of_eventually_le_supportCoeff_mean
declarations.signature: theorem le_reciprocalMass_of_eventually_le_supportCoeff_mean (A : Set ℕ) (hsum : Summable (reciprocalSupportTerm A)) (r : ℝ) (hr : ∀ᶠ N : ℕ in atTop, r ≤ ((∑ n ∈ Finset.range N, supportCoeff A (n + 1) : ℕ) : ℝ) / (N : ℝ)) : r ≤ reciprocalMass A
declarations.docstring: A ready-to-use lower-bound corollary: any eventual lower bound on the support-coefficient Cesàro means is inherited by reciprocal mass.

declarations.name: cesaroMean
declarations.signature: noncomputable def cesaroMean (x : ℕ → ℝ) (N : ℕ) : ℝ
declarations.docstring: Cesàro mean of a real sequence over indices `0,...,N-1`.

declarations.name: sum_supportCoeff_cast_eq_sum_binaryCoeffTail_add_boundary
declarations.signature: theorem sum_supportCoeff_cast_eq_sum_binaryCoeffTail_add_boundary (A : Set ℕ) (N : ℕ) : ((∑ n ∈ Finset.range N, supportCoeff A (n + 1) : ℕ) : ℝ) = (∑ n ∈ Finset.range N, binaryCoeffTail (supportCoeff A) n) + binaryCoeffTail (supportCoeff A) 0 - binaryCoeffTail (supportCoeff A) N
declarations.docstring: Finite telescoping of the binary-tail recurrence.

declarations.name: tendsto_sqrt_nat_div_nat_zero
declarations.signature: theorem tendsto_sqrt_nat_div_nat_zero : Tendsto (fun N : ℕ => Real.sqrt (N : ℝ) / (N : ℝ)) atTop (nhds 0)

declarations.name: tendsto_binaryCoeffTail_supportCoeff_div_nat_zero
declarations.signature: theorem tendsto_binaryCoeffTail_supportCoeff_div_nat_zero (A : Set ℕ) : Tendsto (fun N : ℕ => binaryCoeffTail (supportCoeff A) N / (N : ℝ)) atTop (nhds 0)

declarations.name: tendsto_binaryCoeffTail_supportCoeff_mean_reciprocalMass
declarations.signature: theorem tendsto_binaryCoeffTail_supportCoeff_mean_reciprocalMass (A : Set ℕ) (hsum : Summable (reciprocalSupportTerm A)) : Tendsto (cesaroMean (binaryCoeffTail (supportCoeff A))) atTop (nhds (reciprocalMass A))
declarations.docstring: The Cesàro mean of scaled binary support tails has the same limit as the coefficient mean, namely reciprocal mass. This is the direct T9 interface because a rational carry decomposes each tail into a periodic residue fraction plus a nonnegative integral excess.

declarations.name: le_reciprocalMass_of_eventually_le_binaryCoeffTail_mean
declarations.signature: theorem le_reciprocalMass_of_eventually_le_binaryCoeffTail_mean (A : Set ℕ) (hsum : Summable (reciprocalSupportTerm A)) (r : ℝ) (hr : ∀ᶠ N : ℕ in atTop, r ≤ cesaroMean (binaryCoeffTail (supportCoeff A)) N) : r ≤ reciprocalMass A

declarations.name: sum_range_mul_eq_sum_blocks
declarations.signature: theorem sum_range_mul_eq_sum_blocks (x : ℕ → ℝ) (M h : ℕ) : ∑ n ∈ Finset.range (M * h), x n = ∑ q ∈ Finset.range M, ∑ j ∈ Finset.range h, x (q * h + j)
declarations.docstring: Exact partition of a prefix of length `M*h` into `M` consecutive blocks of length `h`.

declarations.name: div_le_of_tendsto_cesaroMean_of_block_lower_bound
declarations.signature: theorem div_le_of_tendsto_cesaroMean_of_block_lower_bound (x : ℕ → ℝ) (limit : ℝ) (h w : ℕ) (hh : 0 < h) (hlimit : Tendsto (cesaroMean x) atTop (nhds limit)) (hblock : ∀ q : ℕ, (w : ℝ) ≤ ∑ j ∈ Finset.range h, x (q * h + j)) : (w : ℝ) / (h : ℝ) ≤ limit
declarations.docstring: A uniform lower bound `w` on every length-`h` block forces the Cesàro limit to be at least `w/h`.

declarations.name: wrapRatio_le_reciprocalMass_of_binaryCoeffTail_blocks
declarations.signature: theorem wrapRatio_le_reciprocalMass_of_binaryCoeffTail_blocks (A : Set ℕ) (hsum : Summable (reciprocalSupportTerm A)) (h w : ℕ) (hh : 0 < h) (hblock : ∀ q : ℕ, (w : ℝ) ≤ ∑ j ∈ Finset.range h, binaryCoeffTail (supportCoeff A) (q * h + j)) : (w : ℝ) / (h : ℝ) ≤ reciprocalMass A
declarations.docstring: Direct T9 handoff: a length-`h` block lower bound `w` on genuine support tails yields `ρ(A) ≥ w/h`.

declarations.name: blockRemainder
declarations.signature: noncomputable def blockRemainder (x : ℕ → ℝ) (h N : ℕ) : ℝ
declarations.docstring: The incomplete final block after the `N / h` complete aligned blocks.

declarations.name: sum_range_eq_mul_blockSum_add_blockRemainder
declarations.signature: theorem sum_range_eq_mul_blockSum_add_blockRemainder (x : ℕ → ℝ) (h : ℕ) (blockSum : ℝ) (hblock : ∀ q : ℕ, ∑ j ∈ Finset.range h, x (q * h + j) = blockSum) (N : ℕ) : ∑ n ∈ Finset.range N, x n = ((N / h : ℕ) : ℝ) * blockSum + blockRemainder x h N

declarations.name: blockRemainder_nonneg
declarations.signature: theorem blockRemainder_nonneg (x : ℕ → ℝ) (hx0 : ∀ n : ℕ, 0 ≤ x n) (h N : ℕ) : 0 ≤ blockRemainder x h N

declarations.name: blockRemainder_le_period
declarations.signature: theorem blockRemainder_le_period (x : ℕ → ℝ) (hx1 : ∀ n : ℕ, x n ≤ 1) {h : ℕ} (hh : 0 < h) (N : ℕ) : blockRemainder x h N ≤ (h : ℝ)

declarations.name: tendsto_cesaroMean_of_constant_blocks
declarations.signature: theorem tendsto_cesaroMean_of_constant_blocks (x : ℕ → ℝ) (h : ℕ) (blockSum : ℝ) (hh : 0 < h) (hx0 : ∀ n : ℕ, 0 ≤ x n) (hx1 : ∀ n : ℕ, x n ≤ 1) (hblock : ∀ q : ℕ, ∑ j ∈ Finset.range h, x (q * h + j) = blockSum) : Tendsto (cesaroMean x) atTop (nhds (blockSum / (h : ℝ)))
declarations.docstring: A nonnegative sequence bounded by one and having the same exact sum on every aligned length-`h` block has Cesàro mean `blockSum / h`.

declarations.name: tendsto_cesaroMean_excess_of_binaryCoeffTail_decomposition
declarations.signature: theorem tendsto_cesaroMean_excess_of_binaryCoeffTail_decomposition (A : Set ℕ) (hsum : Summable (reciprocalSupportTerm A)) (residue excess : ℕ → ℝ) (residueMean : ℝ) (hdecomp : ∀ N : ℕ, binaryCoeffTail (supportCoeff A) N = residue N + excess N) (hresidue : Tendsto (cesaroMean residue) atTop (nhds residueMean)) : Tendsto (cesaroMean excess) atTop (nhds (reciprocalMass A - residueMean))
declarations.docstring: If each tail is a periodic residue contribution plus an excess, the excess Cesàro mean converges automatically to `ρ(A) - residueMean`.

declarations.name: residueMean_le_reciprocalMass_of_binaryCoeffTail_decomposition
declarations.signature: theorem residueMean_le_reciprocalMass_of_binaryCoeffTail_decomposition (A : Set ℕ) (hsum : Summable (reciprocalSupportTerm A)) (residue excess : ℕ → ℝ) (residueMean : ℝ) (hdecomp : ∀ N : ℕ, binaryCoeffTail (supportCoeff A) N = residue N + excess N) (hexcess : ∀ N : ℕ, 0 ≤ excess N) (hresidue : Tendsto (cesaroMean residue) atTop (nhds residueMean)) : residueMean ≤ reciprocalMass A
declarations.docstring: Direct wrap lower bound: a nonnegative tail excess over a residue word of Cesàro mean `r` forces `ρ(A) ≥ r`.

declarations.name: reciprocalMass_eq_residueMean_add_excessMean
declarations.signature: theorem reciprocalMass_eq_residueMean_add_excessMean (A : Set ℕ) (hsum : Summable (reciprocalSupportTerm A)) (residue excess : ℕ → ℝ) (residueMean excessMean : ℝ) (hdecomp : ∀ N : ℕ, binaryCoeffTail (supportCoeff A) N = residue N + excess N) (hresidue : Tendsto (cesaroMean residue) atTop (nhds residueMean)) (hexcess : Tendsto (cesaroMean excess) atTop (nhds excessMean)) : reciprocalMass A = residueMean + excessMean
declarations.docstring: Exact `ρ = residueMean + lim-mean(excess)` identity when the excess mean is named explicitly.

declarations.name: doublingResidue_mul_period
declarations.signature: theorem doublingResidue_mul_period (p : ℕ) {v h : ℕ} (hperiod : IsDoublingPeriod v h) (q n : ℕ) : doublingResidue (p * 2 ^ (q * h)) v n = doublingResidue p v n

declarations.name: doublingWrapCount_mul_period
declarations.signature: theorem doublingWrapCount_mul_period (p : ℕ) {v h : ℕ} (hperiod : IsDoublingPeriod v h) (q : ℕ) : doublingWrapCount (p * 2 ^ (q * h)) v h = doublingWrapCount p v h

declarations.name: tendsto_cesaroMean_normalizedDoublingResidue
declarations.signature: theorem tendsto_cesaroMean_normalizedDoublingResidue (p : ℕ) {v h : ℕ} (hv : 0 < v) (hperiod : IsDoublingPeriod v h) : Tendsto (cesaroMean (fun N : ℕ => (doublingResidue p v N : ℝ) / (v : ℝ))) atTop (nhds ((doublingWrapCount p v h : ℝ) / (h : ℝ)))

declarations.name: tendsto_oddTailExcess_mean
declarations.signature: theorem tendsto_oddTailExcess_mean (A : Set ℕ) (hsum : Summable (reciprocalSupportTerm A)) (p : ℕ) {v h : ℕ} (hv : 0 < v) (hperiod : IsDoublingPeriod v h) (u : ℕ → ℕ) (hstate : IsOddSupportTailState A p v u) : Tendsto (cesaroMean (fun N : ℕ => (oddTailExcess u v N : ℝ))) atTop (nhds (reciprocalMass A - (doublingWrapCount p v h : ℝ) / (h : ℝ)))
declarations.docstring: The formal development.s exact mean-excess statement in its strongest canonical form: the mean excess exists automatically and its limit is `ρ(A) - w/h`.

declarations.name: reciprocalMass_eq_wrapRatio_add_oddTailExcessMean
declarations.signature: theorem reciprocalMass_eq_wrapRatio_add_oddTailExcessMean (A : Set ℕ) (hsum : Summable (reciprocalSupportTerm A)) (p : ℕ) {v h : ℕ} (hv : 0 < v) (hperiod : IsDoublingPeriod v h) (u : ℕ → ℕ) (hstate : IsOddSupportTailState A p v u) (excessMean : ℝ) (hexcess : Tendsto (cesaroMean (fun N : ℕ => (oddTailExcess u v N : ℝ))) atTop (nhds excessMean)) : reciprocalMass A = (doublingWrapCount p v h : ℝ) / (h : ℝ) + excessMean
declarations.docstring: Explicit named-limit form of `ρ = w/h + lim mean(excess)`.

declarations.name: doublingWrapRatio_le_reciprocalMass
declarations.signature: theorem doublingWrapRatio_le_reciprocalMass (A : Set ℕ) (hsum : Summable (reciprocalSupportTerm A)) (p : ℕ) {v h : ℕ} (hv : 0 < v) (hperiod : IsDoublingPeriod v h) (u : ℕ → ℕ) (hstate : IsOddSupportTailState A p v u) : (doublingWrapCount p v h : ℝ) / (h : ℝ) ≤ reciprocalMass A
declarations.docstring: Fully instantiated wrap lower bound for a genuine odd support-tail state.

declarations.name: doublingWrapRatio_reciprocalMassDivergesOrAtLeast
declarations.signature: theorem doublingWrapRatio_reciprocalMassDivergesOrAtLeast (A : Set ℕ) (p : ℕ) {v h : ℕ} (hv : 0 < v) (hperiod : IsDoublingPeriod v h) (u : ℕ → ℕ) (hstate : IsOddSupportTailState A p v u) : ReciprocalMassDivergesOrAtLeast A ((doublingWrapCount p v h : ℝ) / (h : ℝ))

declarations.name: one_div_period_reciprocalMassDivergesOrAtLeast
declarations.signature: theorem one_div_period_reciprocalMassDivergesOrAtLeast (A : Set ℕ) (p : ℕ) {v h : ℕ} (hv : 1 < v) (hvodd : Odd v) (hpv : p.Coprime v) (hperiod : IsDoublingPeriod v h) (u : ℕ → ℕ) (hstate : IsOddSupportTailState A p v u) : ReciprocalMassDivergesOrAtLeast A ((1 : ℝ) / (h : ℝ))
declarations.docstring: The positive-wrap corollary `1/h ≤ ρ(A)`, with divergence handled honestly rather than relying on the nonsummable convention for `tsum`.

declarations.name: tendsto_shifted_binaryCoeffTail_supportCoeff_mean_reciprocalMass

declarations.name: booleanCollisionSurplus
declarations.docstring: The natural-number realization of `ceil ((m - 1) / 2)` for positive cardinalities. For `m > 0`, this is exactly `m / 2`.

declarations.name: card_le_supportCoeff_of_forced_divisors
declarations.signature: theorem card_le_supportCoeff_of_forced_divisors (A : Set ℕ) (F : Finset ℕ) (n : ℕ) (hn : n ≠ 0) (hFA : ∀ a ∈ F, a ∈ A) (hFdvd : ∀ a ∈ F, a ∣ n) : F.card ≤ supportCoeff A n

declarations.name: booleanCollisionSurplus_le_of_carry
declarations.signature: theorem booleanCollisionSurplus_le_of_carry (m f epsilon kPrev kNext : ℕ) (hmf : m ≤ f) (hepsilon : epsilon ≤ 1) (hcarry : f + kNext = epsilon + 2 * kPrev) : booleanCollisionSurplus m ≤ kPrev
declarations.docstring: Pure carry arithmetic behind the Boolean collision gain.

declarations.name: booleanCollisionSurplus_le_of_supportCoeff_carry

declarations.docstring: Support-specialized form of the same carry arithmetic.

declarations.name: supportCoeff_add_oddTailExcess_succ_eq_wrap_add_two_excess

declarations.name: booleanCollisionSurplus_le_oddTailExcess

declarations.docstring: A periodic arithmetic progression of excess spikes contributes at least `q / L` to every Cesaro limit. The residue `r` handles a finite denominator shift.

declarations.name: tendsto_cesaroMean_shift

declarations.docstring: Removing or adding a fixed finite prefix does not change a Cesaro limit.

declarations.name: periodic_spike_div_le_of_tendsto_cesaroMean_offset

declarations.docstring: Odd tail state after removing the power-of-two part of a displayed denominator.

declarations.name: shiftedBooleanCollision_wrap_bound_of_common_multiple

declarations.name: wrapRatio_add_collisionSurplus_div_le_reciprocalMass
declarations.signature: theorem wrapRatio_add_collisionSurplus_div_le_reciprocalMass (A : Set ℕ) (hsum : Summable (reciprocalSupportTerm A)) (p : ℕ) {v h : ℕ} (hv : 0 < v) (hperiod : IsDoublingPeriod v h) (u : ℕ → ℕ) (hstate : IsOddSupportTailState A p v u) (L q r : ℕ) (hL : 0 < L) (hr : r < L) (hspike : ∀ j : ℕ, q ≤ oddTailExcess u v (r + j * L)) : (doublingWrapCount p v h : ℝ) / (h : ℝ) + (q : ℝ) / (L : ℝ) ≤ reciprocalMass A
declarations.docstring: Analytic fan-in matching the exact T10 inequality once a carry excess has a spike on one residue class modulo `L`.

declarations.name: booleanCollision_wrap_bound_of_common_multiple
declarations.signature: theorem booleanCollision_wrap_bound_of_common_multiple (A : Set ℕ) (F : Finset ℕ) (p : ℕ) (hsum : Summable (reciprocalSupportTerm A)) {v h L : ℕ} (hv : 0 < v) (hperiod : IsDoublingPeriod v h) (hL : 0 < L) (hFA : ∀ a ∈ F, a ∈ A) (hFL : ∀ a ∈ F, a ∣ L) (u : ℕ → ℕ) (hstate : IsOddSupportTailState A p v u) : (doublingWrapCount p v h : ℝ) / (h : ℝ) + (booleanCollisionSurplus F.card : ℝ) / (L : ℝ) ≤ reciprocalMass A

declarations.name: one_lt_of_tendsto_cesaroMean_of_positive_two_spikes

declarations.name: one_lt_reciprocalMass_of_dyadic_support_fraction_of_two_pos_mem
declarations.signature: theorem one_lt_reciprocalMass_of_dyadic_support_fraction_of_two_pos_mem (A : Set ℕ) (hsum : Summable (reciprocalSupportTerm A)) (p : ℤ) (c : ℕ) {a b : ℕ} (ha : 0 < a) (hb : 0 < b) (hab : a ≠ b) (haA : a ∈ A) (hbA : b ∈ A) (hvalue : erdosSupportSeries 2 A = (p : ℝ) / ((2 ^ c : ℕ) : ℝ)) : 1 < reciprocalMass A
declarations.docstring: A dyadic support fraction with two distinct positive support elements has reciprocal mass strictly greater than one whenever that mass is summable.

declarations.name: dyadic_support_fraction_reciprocalMass_diverges_or_gt_one
declarations.signature: theorem dyadic_support_fraction_reciprocalMass_diverges_or_gt_one (A : Set ℕ) (hAinf : A.Infinite) (p : ℤ) (c : ℕ) (hvalue : erdosSupportSeries 2 A = (p : ℝ) / ((2 ^ c : ℕ) : ℝ)) : ¬ Summable (reciprocalSupportTerm A) ∨ 1 < reciprocalMass A

declarations.name: one_add_mul_card_le_two_mul_shifted_state
declarations.signature: theorem one_add_mul_card_le_two_mul_shifted_state (A : Set ℕ) (F : Finset ℕ) (c v L : ℕ) (u : ℕ → ℕ) (hcL : c < L) (hpos : ∀ n : ℕ, 0 < u n) (hrec : ∀ n : ℕ, u (n + 1) + v * supportCoeff A (c + n + 1) = 2 * u n) (hFA : ∀ a ∈ F, a ∈ A) (hFdvd : ∀ a ∈ F, a ∣ L) : 1 + v * F.card ≤ 2 * u (L - c - 1)

declarations.name: one_add_mul_card_le_two_mul_state

declarations.name: one_add_mul_card_ceil_half_le_shifted_state
declarations.signature: theorem one_add_mul_card_ceil_half_le_shifted_state (A : Set ℕ) (F : Finset ℕ) (c v L : ℕ) (u : ℕ → ℕ) (hcL : c < L) (hpos : ∀ n : ℕ, 0 < u n) (hrec : ∀ n : ℕ, u (n + 1) + v * supportCoeff A (c + n + 1) = 2 * u n) (hFA : ∀ a ∈ F, a ∈ A) (hFdvd : ∀ a ∈ F, a ∣ L) : (1 + v * F.card + 1) / 2 ≤ u (L - c - 1)

declarations.name: one_add_mul_card_half_le_shifted_state
declarations.signature: theorem one_add_mul_card_half_le_shifted_state (A : Set ℕ) (F : Finset ℕ) (c v L : ℕ) (u : ℕ → ℕ) (hcL : c < L) (hpos : ∀ n : ℕ, 0 < u n) (hrec : ∀ n : ℕ, u (n + 1) + v * supportCoeff A (c + n + 1) = 2 * u n) (hFA : ∀ a ∈ F, a ∈ A) (hFdvd : ∀ a ∈ F, a ∣ L) : ((1 + v * F.card : ℕ) : ℝ) / 2 ≤ (u (L - c - 1) : ℝ)

declarations.docstring: Fraction-facing local T13 wrapper. It preserves every clause of the existing shifted natural-state constructor and adds the raw common-multiple bound without changing that constructor's API.

declarations.name: shifted_state_unbounded_of_infinite_support
declarations.signature: theorem shifted_state_unbounded_of_infinite_support (A : Set ℕ) (hAinf : A.Infinite) (c v : ℕ) (hv : 0 < v) (u : ℕ → ℕ) (hpos : ∀ n : ℕ, 0 < u n) (hrec : ∀ n : ℕ, u (n + 1) + v * supportCoeff A (c + n + 1) = 2 * u n) : ∀ B : ℕ, ∃ n : ℕ, B < u n
declarations.docstring: The complete T13 core: every positive shifted recurrence over an infinite support is globally unbounded. The proof selects `2B+1` positive support elements and uses their product, multiplied by `c+1`, as an explicit common multiple.

declarations.name: exists_unbounded_shifted_odd_tail_nat_state_of_support_fraction
declarations.signature: theorem exists_unbounded_shifted_odd_tail_nat_state_of_support_fraction (A : Set ℕ) (hAinf : A.Infinite) (p : ℤ) (c v : ℕ) (hv : 0 < v) (hvalue : erdosSupportSeries 2 A = (p : ℝ) / ((2 ^ c * v : ℕ) : ℝ)) : ∃ u : ℕ → ℕ, (∀ n : ℕ, (u n : ℝ) = (v : ℝ) * binaryCoeffTail (supportCoeff A) (c + n)) ∧ (∀ n : ℕ, 0 < u n) ∧ (∀ n : ℕ, u (n + 1) + v * supportCoeff A (c + n + 1) = 2 * u n) ∧ (∀ n : ℕ, u n ≡ p.toNat * 2 ^ n [MOD v]) ∧ (∀ B : ℕ, ∃ n : ℕ, B < u n)

declarations.name: carryOrbit23_common_multiple_bound_validation66
declarations.signature: theorem carryOrbit23_common_multiple_bound_validation66 : ∀ k ∈ Finset.range 66, (1 : ℤ) + 21 * ({2, 3} : Finset ℕ).card ≤ 2 * carryOrbit23 (6 * k + 5)
declarations.docstring: Every tested common multiple in the formal development.s `{2,3}` regression family hits the exact preceding carry value `26`. The range `0 ≤ k < 66` corresponds to the 66 checked indices `6(k+1)-1`.

declarations.name: coprimeResidueStarts
declarations.docstring: Reduced numerator starts modulo `v`, represented as a computable list for the native finite certificate.

declarations.name: isPowerOfTwoOrbitStart
declarations.signature: def isPowerOfTwoOrbitStart (p v h : ℕ) : Bool
declarations.docstring: Does the starting residue lie in the displayed power-of-two orbit?

declarations.name: orderWrapValidationRow
declarations.docstring: Computable validation of positivity and both directions of the one-wrap classification on one finite modulus/order row. This is validation evidence, not the source of the universal theorem above.

declarations.name: orderWrapValidationRows
declarations.docstring: The twelve modulus/order rows in the 446-orbit validation.

declarations.name: wrapMinimumCertificate
declarations.signature: def wrapMinimumCertificate (v h minimum : ℕ) : Bool
declarations.docstring: Exact minimum-wrap certificate on a single finite row.

declarations.name: orderWrap_minima_table_passes
declarations.signature: theorem orderWrap_minima_table_passes : wrapMinimumCertificate 3 2 1 = true ∧ wrapMinimumCertificate 5 4 2 = true ∧ wrapMinimumCertificate 7 3 1 = true ∧ wrapMinimumCertificate 15 4 1 = true ∧ wrapMinimumCertificate 31 5 1 = true
declarations.docstring: The formal development.s minimum ratios are respectively `1/2, 2/4, 1/3, 1/4, 1/5`.

declarations.name: spacedRepunit
declarations.signature: noncomputable def spacedRepunit (d q : ℕ) : ℤ[X]

declarations.name: mobiusNumeratorPolynomial
declarations.docstring: The divisor-signed polynomial numerator. For positive `r`, evaluation at `X = 2` is the common-denominator Möbius numerator; the public bridge below is stated only on the formal development.s squarefree boundary.

declarations.name: gcdWordCoeff
declarations.docstring: The natural coefficient in the gcd-word presentation.

declarations.name: gcdWord
declarations.docstring: The positive gcd word, represented by its first `r` coefficients.

declarations.name: coeff_spacedRepunit_of_lt
declarations.signature: private theorem coeff_spacedRepunit_of_lt {r d k : ℕ} (hr : 0 < r) (hd : d ∣ r) (hk : k < r) : (spacedRepunit d (r / d)).coeff k = if d ∣ k then 1 else 0

declarations.name: coeff_spacedRepunit_eq_zero_of_le
declarations.signature: private theorem coeff_spacedRepunit_eq_zero_of_le {r d k : ℕ} (hr : 0 < r) (hd : d ∣ r) (hk : r ≤ k) : (spacedRepunit d (r / d)).coeff k = 0

declarations.name: moebius_divisor_sum_eq_totient
declarations.signature: private theorem moebius_divisor_sum_eq_totient (n : ℕ) (hn : 0 < n) : ∑ d ∈ n.divisors, ArithmeticFunction.moebius d * (((n / d : ℕ) : ℤ)) = (n.totient : ℤ)

declarations.name: scaled_moebius_divisor_sum_eq
declarations.signature: private theorem scaled_moebius_divisor_sum_eq (r g : ℕ) (hgpos : 0 < g) (hgr : g ∣ r) : ∑ d ∈ g.divisors, ArithmeticFunction.moebius d * (((r / d : ℕ) : ℤ)) = ((r / g : ℕ) : ℤ) * (g.totient : ℤ)

declarations.name: mobiusNumeratorPolynomial_coeff_of_lt
declarations.signature: private theorem mobiusNumeratorPolynomial_coeff_of_lt {r k : ℕ} (hr : 0 < r) (hk : k < r) : (mobiusNumeratorPolynomial r).coeff k = (gcdWordCoeff r k : ℤ)

declarations.name: mobiusNumeratorPolynomial_coeff_eq_zero_of_le
declarations.signature: private theorem mobiusNumeratorPolynomial_coeff_eq_zero_of_le {r k : ℕ} (hr : 0 < r) (hk : r ≤ k) : (mobiusNumeratorPolynomial r).coeff k = 0

declarations.name: gcdWord_coeff_of_lt
declarations.signature: theorem gcdWord_coeff_of_lt {r k : ℕ} (hk : k < r) : (gcdWord r).coeff k = (gcdWordCoeff r k : ℤ)

declarations.name: gcdWord_coeff_eq_zero_of_le
declarations.signature: theorem gcdWord_coeff_eq_zero_of_le {r k : ℕ} (hk : r ≤ k) : (gcdWord r).coeff k = 0

declarations.name: mobiusNumeratorPolynomial_eq_gcdWord
declarations.signature: theorem mobiusNumeratorPolynomial_eq_gcdWord {r : ℕ} (hr : Squarefree r) : mobiusNumeratorPolynomial r = gcdWord r
declarations.docstring: T1: on the squarefree-positive stated boundary, the signed repunit numerator is exactly the positive gcd word.

declarations.name: mobiusNumeratorPolynomial_coeff
declarations.signature: theorem mobiusNumeratorPolynomial_coeff {r k : ℕ} (hr : Squarefree r) : (mobiusNumeratorPolynomial r).coeff k = if k < r then (gcdWordCoeff r k : ℤ) else 0
declarations.docstring: Coefficient form of T1, including the exact zero tail.

declarations.name: gcdWordCoeff_pos
declarations.signature: theorem gcdWordCoeff_pos {r k : ℕ} (hr : 0 < r) : 0 < gcdWordCoeff r k

declarations.name: mobiusNumeratorPolynomial_coeff_pos
declarations.signature: theorem mobiusNumeratorPolynomial_coeff_pos {r k : ℕ} (hr : Squarefree r) (hk : k < r) : 0 < (mobiusNumeratorPolynomial r).coeff k
declarations.docstring: Every coefficient in the stated range is strictly positive.

declarations.name: mobiusNumeratorPolynomial_coeff_nonneg
declarations.signature: theorem mobiusNumeratorPolynomial_coeff_nonneg {r k : ℕ} (hr : Squarefree r) : 0 ≤ (mobiusNumeratorPolynomial r).coeff k
declarations.docstring: There are no negative coefficients; beyond degree `r - 1` they are exactly zero.

declarations.name: eval_spacedRepunit_two
declarations.signature: private theorem eval_spacedRepunit_two {r d : ℕ} (hr : 0 < r) (hd : d ∣ r) : (spacedRepunit d (r / d)).eval 2 = (((RadicalMobiusShadow.mersenne r / RadicalMobiusShadow.mersenne d : ℕ) : ℤ))

declarations.name: moebius_prod_subset_primeFactors
declarations.signature: private theorem moebius_prod_subset_primeFactors {r : ℕ} (hr : Squarefree r) {s : Finset ℕ} (hs : s ⊆ r.primeFactors) : ArithmeticFunction.moebius (s.prod id) = (-1 : ℤ) ^ s.card

declarations.name: divisor_mobiusNumerator_eq_subset
declarations.signature: private theorem divisor_mobiusNumerator_eq_subset {r : ℕ} (hr : Squarefree r) : (∑ d ∈ r.divisors, ArithmeticFunction.moebius d * (((r / d : ℕ) : ℤ)) * (((RadicalMobiusShadow.mersenne r / RadicalMobiusShadow.mersenne d : ℕ) : ℤ))) = RadicalMobiusShadow.mobiusNumerator r

declarations.name: mobiusNumeratorPolynomial_eval_two
declarations.signature: theorem mobiusNumeratorPolynomial_eval_two {r : ℕ} (hr : Squarefree r) : (mobiusNumeratorPolynomial r).eval 2 = RadicalMobiusShadow.mobiusNumerator r
declarations.docstring: Evaluation at `X = 2` recovers the integral numerator already owned by `RadicalMobiusShadow`. The theorem deliberately retains the squarefree boundary of T1.

declarations.name: phasePowerMatrix
declarations.signature: def phasePowerMatrix {d : ℕ} (e : Fin d → ℕ) (z : Fin d → ℂ) : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) ℂ
declarations.docstring: The coefficient-side monomial matrix before residual column scaling.

declarations.name: residualMonomialMatrix
declarations.signature: def residualMonomialMatrix {d : ℕ} (e : Fin d → ℕ) (W z : Fin d → ℂ) : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) ℂ
declarations.docstring: A common residual weight applied to every row of a supplier column.

declarations.name: residualMonomialMatrix_eq_mul_diagonal
declarations.signature: theorem residualMonomialMatrix_eq_mul_diagonal {d : ℕ} (e : Fin d → ℕ) (W z : Fin d → ℂ) : residualMonomialMatrix e W z = phasePowerMatrix e z * Matrix.diagonal W
declarations.docstring: Common residual weights are exactly a right diagonal gauge action.

declarations.name: det_residualMonomialMatrix
declarations.signature: theorem det_residualMonomialMatrix {d : ℕ} (e : Fin d → ℕ) (W z : Fin d → ℂ) : Matrix.det (residualMonomialMatrix e W z) = Matrix.det (phasePowerMatrix e z) * ∏ j, W j
declarations.docstring: Determinants factor into the coefficient-side determinant and the product of residual column gauges.

declarations.name: det_residualMonomialMatrix_ne_zero_iff
declarations.signature: theorem det_residualMonomialMatrix_ne_zero_iff {d : ℕ} (e : Fin d → ℕ) (W z : Fin d → ℂ) (hW : ∏ j, W j ≠ 0) : Matrix.det (residualMonomialMatrix e W z) ≠ 0 ↔ Matrix.det (phasePowerMatrix e z) ≠ 0
declarations.docstring: Nonzero column gauges preserve determinant nonvanishing at every rank.

declarations.name: norm_det_residualMonomialMatrix
declarations.signature: theorem norm_det_residualMonomialMatrix {d : ℕ} (e : Fin d → ℕ) (W z : Fin d → ℂ) (hW : ∀ j, ‖W j‖ = 1) : ‖Matrix.det (residualMonomialMatrix e W z)‖ = ‖Matrix.det (phasePowerMatrix e z)‖
declarations.docstring: Unit residual gauges preserve determinant magnitude.

declarations.name: lockedResidual
declarations.signature: noncomputable def lockedResidual {d : ℕ} (z : Fin d → ℂ) : Fin d → ℂ
declarations.docstring: The locked reconstruction gauge is the inverse pivot phase.

declarations.name: norm_lockedResidual
declarations.signature: theorem norm_lockedResidual {d : ℕ} (z : Fin d → ℂ) (hz : ∀ j, ‖z j‖ = 1) : ∀ j, ‖lockedResidual z j‖ = 1
declarations.docstring: Unit pivot phases give unit locked residuals.

declarations.name: residualMonomialMatrix_locked_row_one
declarations.signature: theorem residualMonomialMatrix_locked_row_one {d : ℕ} (e : Fin d → ℕ) (z : Fin d → ℂ) (i : Fin d) (hi : e i = 1) (hz : ∀ j, z j ≠ 0) : ∀ j, residualMonomialMatrix e (lockedResidual z) z i j = 1
declarations.docstring: Under the locked gauge, any exponent-one row is identically the bad target phase `1`, while the other rows remain available to carry rank.

declarations.name: locked_reconstruction_preserves_nonzero_minor
declarations.signature: theorem locked_reconstruction_preserves_nonzero_minor {d : ℕ} (e : Fin d → ℕ) (z : Fin d → ℂ) (i : Fin d) (hi : e i = 1) (hz : ∀ j, z j ≠ 0) (hdet : Matrix.det (phasePowerMatrix e z) ≠ 0) : Matrix.det (residualMonomialMatrix e (lockedResidual z) z) ≠ 0 ∧ ∀ j, residualMonomialMatrix e (lockedResidual z) z i j = 1
declarations.docstring: The locked bad row and a nonzero coefficient-side determinant coexist. This is the all-rank countermodel to a residual-blind minor certificate.

declarations.name: rowResidualMonomialMatrix
declarations.signature: def rowResidualMonomialMatrix {d : ℕ} (e : Fin d → ℕ) (W : Fin d → Fin d → ℂ) (z : Fin d → ℂ) : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) ℂ
declarations.docstring: Monomial rows with a residual allowed to vary across both rows and columns.

declarations.name: rowReconstructingResidual
declarations.signature: noncomputable def rowReconstructingResidual {d : ℕ} (e : Fin d → ℕ) (z : Fin d → ℂ) : Fin d → Fin d → ℂ
declarations.docstring: A row-dependent residual can reconstruct the ordinary consecutive-power matrix for arbitrary requested exponents.

declarations.name: rowResidualMonomialMatrix_reconstructs_phasePowerMatrix
declarations.signature: theorem rowResidualMonomialMatrix_reconstructs_phasePowerMatrix {d : ℕ} (e : Fin d → ℕ) (z : Fin d → ℂ) (hz : ∀ j, z j ≠ 0) : rowResidualMonomialMatrix e (rowReconstructingResidual e z) z = phasePowerMatrix (fun i : Fin d ↦ i.1) z
declarations.docstring: The explicit row-dependent residual relaxation reconstructs an ordinary Vandermonde-shaped consecutive-power matrix exactly.

declarations.name: norm_rowReconstructingResidual
declarations.signature: theorem norm_rowReconstructingResidual {d : ℕ} (e : Fin d → ℕ) (z : Fin d → ℂ) (hz : ∀ j, ‖z j‖ = 1) : ∀ i j, ‖rowReconstructingResidual e z i j‖ = 1
declarations.docstring: When the pivot phases are unit, every entry of the reconstructing row-dependent residual is also unit.

declarations.name: rowReconstruction_zero_row_one
declarations.signature: theorem rowReconstruction_zero_row_one {d : ℕ} (e : Fin d → ℕ) (z : Fin d → ℂ) (hz : ∀ j, z j ≠ 0) (i : Fin d) (hi : i.1 = 0) : ∀ j, rowResidualMonomialMatrix e (rowReconstructingResidual e z) z i j = 1
declarations.docstring: The reconstructed zeroth row is identically `1`. Thus even allowing row-dependent unit residuals does not let a fixed-rank nonzero minor exclude the locked affine target without an additional arithmetic coupling law.

declarations.name: det_mul_rectangular
declarations.signature: theorem det_mul_rectangular {R ι κ : Type*} [CommRing R] [Fintype ι] [DecidableEq ι] [Fintype κ] (M : Matrix ι κ R) (N : Matrix κ ι R) : Matrix.det (M * N) = ∑ p : ι → κ, Matrix.det (fun i j ↦ M i (p j)) * ∏ i, N (p i) i

declarations.name: finiteSignedMomentMatrix
declarations.signature: def finiteSignedMomentMatrix {R κ : Type*} [CommRing R] [Fintype κ] (n : ℕ) (w x : κ → R) : Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) R
declarations.docstring: A finite signed moment matrix on an arbitrary finite atom type. Here `w` is the full atom mass (for the target application, `w N = a N * x N`).

declarations.name: even_finset_sum
declarations.signature: private theorem even_finset_sum {α : Type*} (s : Finset α) (f : α → ℤ) (h : ∀ i ∈ s, Even (f i)) : Even (∑ i ∈ s, f i)
declarations.docstring: A finite signed moment matrix on an arbitrary finite atom type. Here `w` is the full atom mass (for the target application, `w N = a N * x N`). -/ def finiteSignedMomentMatrix {R κ : Type*} [CommRing R] [Fintype κ] (n : ℕ) (w x : κ → R) : Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) R := fun i j ↦ ∑ a : κ, w a * x a ^ (i.1 + j.1) /-! ## Unique-terminal dyadic parity

declarations.name: not_even_finset_sum_of_unique_not_even
declarations.signature: private theorem not_even_finset_sum_of_unique_not_even {α : Type*} (s : Finset α) (f : α → ℤ) (m : α) (hm : m ∈ s) (hodd : ¬ Even (f m)) (heven : ∀ i ∈ s, i ≠ m → Even (f i)) : ¬ Even (∑ i ∈ s, f i)

declarations.name: scaled_dyadic_sum_odd
declarations.signature: theorem scaled_dyadic_sum_odd {α : Type*} (s : Finset α) (u : α → ℤ) (e : α → ℕ) (m : α) (hm : m ∈ s) (hu : ¬ Even (u m)) (hmax : ∀ i ∈ s, i ≠ m → e i < e m) : (∑ i ∈ s, u i * (2 : ℤ) ^ (e m - e i)) % 2 = 1
declarations.docstring: If one dyadic denominator exponent is uniquely largest and its numerator is odd, the common numerator obtained by clearing that denominator is odd.

declarations.name: scaled_dyadic_sum_ne_zero
declarations.signature: theorem scaled_dyadic_sum_ne_zero {α : Type*} (s : Finset α) (u : α → ℤ) (e : α → ℕ) (m : α) (hm : m ∈ s) (hu : ¬ Even (u m)) (hmax : ∀ i ∈ s, i ≠ m → e i < e m) : (∑ i ∈ s, u i * (2 : ℤ) ^ (e m - e i)) ≠ 0
declarations.docstring: The same unique-terminal hypothesis gives non-vanishing after common dyadic denominator clearing.

declarations.name: truncatedMoment
declarations.signature: def truncatedMoment (a : ℕ → ℤ) (Y k : ℕ) : ℚ
declarations.docstring: The finite signed `q = 1/2` moment used by the Möbius Hankel lane.

declarations.name: hankelMatrix
declarations.signature: def hankelMatrix (m : ℕ → ℚ) (n : ℕ) : Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) ℚ
declarations.docstring: Hankel matrix associated to a moment sequence.

declarations.name: hankelDet
declarations.signature: def hankelDet (m : ℕ → ℚ) (n : ℕ) : ℚ
declarations.docstring: Order-`n` Hankel determinant associated to a moment sequence.

declarations.name: totient_mul_prime_cocycle
declarations.signature: theorem totient_mul_prime_cocycle {p m : ℕ} (hp : p.Prime) : Nat.totient (p * m) = (p - 1) * Nat.totient m + if p ∣ m then Nat.totient m else 0
declarations.docstring: Exact prime-dilation cocycle for Euler's totient. The second summand is the weighted correction which square localization removes on a finite horizon.

declarations.name: totient_dilation_clean_of_square_residue
declarations.signature: theorem totient_dilation_clean_of_square_residue {p A a t h : ℕ} (hp : p.Prime) (hclean : ¬p ∣ a + h) : Nat.totient (A + p * (a + p * t) + p * h - A) = (p - 1) * Nat.totient (a + p * t + h)
declarations.docstring: General square-witness form. The equality `n = A + p * (a + p*t)` is the quotient-bearing form of `n ≡ A + p*a [MOD p^2]`. If `p ∤ a+h`, the cofactor at shift `h` is clean.

declarations.name: totient_dilation_clean_of_square_witness
declarations.signature: theorem totient_dilation_clean_of_square_witness {p A t h : ℕ} (hp : p.Prime) (hh : 0 < h) (hhp : h < p) : Nat.totient (A + p ^ 2 * t + p * h - A) = (p - 1) * Nat.totient (p * t + h)
declarations.docstring: Canonical anchor residue `a = 0`. The hypotheses `0 < h < p` make the whole horizon `1 ≤ h ≤ J < p` clean after imposing `n = A + p^2*t`.

declarations.name: exists_square_witness_of_modEq
declarations.signature: theorem exists_square_witness_of_modEq {p r n : ℕ} (hrn : r ≤ n) (hmod : n ≡ r [MOD p ^ 2]) : ∃ t : ℕ, n = r + p ^ 2 * t
declarations.docstring: An ordered square congruence has the quotient witness used by the clean dilation theorem.

declarations.name: totient_dilation_clean_of_square_modEq
declarations.signature: theorem totient_dilation_clean_of_square_modEq {p A a n h : ℕ} (hp : p.Prime) (hresidue : A + p * a ≤ n) (hmod : n ≡ A + p * a [MOD p ^ 2]) (hclean : ¬p ∣ a + h) : ∃ t : ℕ, n = A + p * (a + p * t) ∧ Nat.totient (n + p * h - A) = (p - 1) * Nat.totient (a + p * t + h)
declarations.docstring: Direct `Nat.ModEq` adapter: once the CRT representative is at least its target residue, the congruence produces a cofactor and the correction-free totient identity at every clean shift.

declarations.name: pairwise_coprime_prime_squares
declarations.signature: theorem pairwise_coprime_prime_squares {ι : Type*} {E : Finset ι} {p : ι → ℕ} (hprime : ∀ i ∈ E, (p i).Prime) (hinj : Set.InjOn p (E : Set ι)) : Set.Pairwise (E : Set ι) (fun i j => Nat.Coprime (p i ^ 2) (p j ^ 2))
declarations.docstring: Distinct prime labels have pairwise-coprime square moduli.

declarations.name: exists_squareCRT_base
declarations.signature: theorem exists_squareCRT_base {ι : Type*} [DecidableEq ι] (E : Finset ι) (p A a : ι → ℕ) (hmod : ∀ i ∈ E, p i ^ 2 ≠ 0) (hcop : Set.Pairwise (E : Set ι) (fun i j => Nat.Coprime (p i ^ 2) (p j ^ 2))) : ∃ n : ℕ, n < ∏ i ∈ E, p i ^ 2 ∧ ∀ i ∈ E, n ≡ A i + p i * a i [MOD p i ^ 2]
declarations.docstring: A finite family of pairwise-coprime square congruences has a simultaneous representative strictly below the product of the square moduli.

declarations.name: exists_squareCRT_base_of_distinct_primes
declarations.signature: theorem exists_squareCRT_base_of_distinct_primes {ι : Type*} [DecidableEq ι] (E : Finset ι) (p A a : ι → ℕ) (hprime : ∀ i ∈ E, (p i).Prime) (hinj : Set.InjOn p (E : Set ι)) : ∃ n : ℕ, n < ∏ i ∈ E, p i ^ 2 ∧ ∀ i ∈ E, n ≡ A i + p i * a i [MOD p i ^ 2]
declarations.docstring: Prime-specialized square CRT, including the least-base bound.

declarations.name: exists_large_squareCRT_base
declarations.signature: theorem exists_large_squareCRT_base {ι : Type*} [DecidableEq ι] (E : Finset ι) (p A a : ι → ℕ) (B : ℕ) (hmod : ∀ i ∈ E, p i ^ 2 ≠ 0) (hcop : Set.Pairwise (E : Set ι) (fun i j => Nat.Coprime (p i ^ 2) (p j ^ 2))) : ∃ n : ℕ, B < n ∧ n < (∏ i ∈ E, p i ^ 2) * (B + 2) ∧ ∀ i ∈ E, n ≡ A i + p i * a i [MOD p i ^ 2]
declarations.docstring: The least CRT representative can be lifted above any prescribed anchor floor at a cost of less than one additional block of `B+1` square moduli. This is the exact finite-family version of the least-admissible-base ledger.

declarations.name: exists_squareCRT_clean_totient_family
declarations.signature: theorem exists_squareCRT_clean_totient_family {ι : Type*} [DecidableEq ι] (E : Finset ι) (p A a : ι → ℕ) (hprime : ∀ i ∈ E, (p i).Prime) (hinj : Set.InjOn p (E : Set ι)) : ∃ n : ℕ, n < (∏ i ∈ E, p i ^ 2) * (E.sup (fun i => A i + p i * a i) + 2) ∧ ∀ i ∈ E, ∀ h : ℕ, ¬p i ∣ a i + h → ∃ t : ℕ, n = A i + p i * (a i + p i * t) ∧ Nat.totient (n + p i * h - A i) = (p i - 1) * Nat.totient (a i + p i * t + h)
declarations.docstring: Simultaneous square-CRT correction suppression on a finite family. One common base works at every label and every shift whose chosen cofactor residue avoids zero modulo the corresponding prime.

declarations.name: exists_squareCRT_clean_horizon
declarations.signature: theorem exists_squareCRT_clean_horizon {ι : Type*} [DecidableEq ι] (E : Finset ι) (p A : ι → ℕ) (J : ℕ) (hprime : ∀ i ∈ E, (p i).Prime) (hinj : Set.InjOn p (E : Set ι)) (horizon : ∀ i ∈ E, J < p i) : ∃ n : ℕ, n < (∏ i ∈ E, p i ^ 2) * (E.sup A + 2) ∧ ∀ i ∈ E, ∀ h : ℕ, 0 < h → h ≤ J → ∃ t : ℕ, n = A i + p i ^ 2 * t ∧ Nat.totient (n + p i * h - A i) = (p i - 1) * Nat.totient (p i * t + h)
declarations.docstring: Canonical finite-horizon corollary. If every distinct construction prime exceeds `J`, the residue choice `a = 0` removes every totient dilation correction simultaneously for all `1 ≤ h ≤ J`.

declarations.name: alternating_powerset_sum_eq_zero_of_insert_invariant
declarations.signature: theorem alternating_powerset_sum_eq_zero_of_insert_invariant {ι : Type*} [DecidableEq ι] {s : Finset ι} {i : ι} (hi : i ∉ s) (f : Finset ι → ℤ) (hinv : ∀ t ∈ s.powerset, f (insert i t) = f t) : ∑ t ∈ (insert i s).powerset, (-1 : ℤ) ^ t.card * f t = 0
declarations.docstring: Alternating Boolean-cube terms cancel exactly when insertion of one coordinate does not change the underlying term. This is the algebraic core of the first-`k` Hilbert-cube cancellation, separated from all totient and CRT hypotheses.

declarations.name: actualOneCubeCoeff
declarations.signature: def actualOneCubeCoeff (n r0 r1 : ℕ) : ℤ
declarations.docstring: One-dimensional actual-totient cube coefficient.

declarations.name: squareCRT_clean_block_can_vanish
declarations.signature: theorem squareCRT_clean_block_can_vanish : (13 : ℕ).Prime ∧ (5 : ℕ).Prime ∧ 52 ≡ 52 [MOD 13 ^ 2] ∧ 52 ≡ 2 [MOD 5 ^ 2] ∧ actualOneCubeCoeff 52 13 13 = 0 ∧ actualOneCubeCoeff 52 26 18 = 0
declarations.docstring: A clean square-localized cube need not separate: the returned minimal `k = 1`, `J = 2` model has both finite coefficients equal to zero.

declarations.name: squareCRT_vanishing_countermodel_is_clean
declarations.signature: theorem squareCRT_vanishing_countermodel_is_clean : Nat.totient (13 * (4 + 1)) = (13 - 1) * Nat.totient (4 + 1) ∧ Nat.totient (5 * (12 + 1)) = (5 - 1) * Nat.totient (12 + 1) ∧ Nat.totient (13 * (4 + 2)) = (13 - 1) * Nat.totient (4 + 2) ∧ Nat.totient (5 * (12 + 2)) = (5 - 1) * Nat.totient (12 + 2)
declarations.docstring: The vanishing witness is genuinely correction-free at both vertices and both controlled shifts, not merely an equality of unrelated totient values.

declarations.name: squareCRT_clean_block_can_be_nonzero
declarations.signature: theorem squareCRT_clean_block_can_be_nonzero : (3 : ℕ).Prime ∧ (5 : ℕ).Prime ∧ 27 ≡ 0 [MOD 3 ^ 2] ∧ 27 ≡ 2 [MOD 5 ^ 2] ∧ actualOneCubeCoeff 27 3 3 = 0 ∧ actualOneCubeCoeff 27 6 8 = -4
declarations.docstring: Correction suppression is not identically trivial: a nearby clean cube has a nonzero second coefficient.

declarations.name: squareCRT_nonzero_countermodel_is_clean
declarations.signature: theorem squareCRT_nonzero_countermodel_is_clean : Nat.totient (3 * (9 + 1)) = (3 - 1) * Nat.totient (9 + 1) ∧ Nat.totient (5 * (5 + 1)) = (5 - 1) * Nat.totient (5 + 1) ∧ Nat.totient (3 * (9 + 2)) = (3 - 1) * Nat.totient (9 + 2) ∧ Nat.totient (5 * (5 + 2)) = (5 - 1) * Nat.totient (5 + 2)
declarations.docstring: The nonzero witness is also clean at both vertices and both shifts.

declarations.name: mobiusSquarePartialRat
declarations.docstring: Executable rational partial state `1/2 + ∑_{1 ≤ d ≤ D} μ(d)/(2^d-1)^2`.

declarations.name: lambertProjectedDiagonalRat
declarations.docstring: Rational centre for the actual diagonal.

declarations.name: lambertProjectedDiagonal
declarations.signature: noncomputable def lambertProjectedDiagonal (H D : ℕ) : ℝ
declarations.docstring: Real display of the exact rational centre.

declarations.name: mobiusSquareTail
declarations.docstring: The omitted signed squared-Mersenne tail, indexed from `D+1`.

declarations.name: lambertSquareComplementBound
declarations.signature: noncomputable def lambertSquareComplementBound (H D : ℕ) : ℝ
declarations.docstring: Sharp elementary radius obtained by comparing every later Mersenne denominator with `2^k (2^(D+1)-1)`.

declarations.name: totientSeries_eq_pnat_half_pow
declarations.signature: theorem totientSeries_eq_pnat_half_pow : totientSeries = ∑' n : ℕ+, (Nat.totient (n : ℕ) : ℝ) * ((1 : ℝ) / 2) ^ (n : ℕ)
declarations.docstring: The local `ℕ` series convention agrees with the ladder's positive-index convention.

declarations.name: mobius_square_series_eq_partial_add_tail
declarations.signature: theorem mobius_square_series_eq_partial_add_tail (D : ℕ) : 1 / 2 + ∑' d : ℕ+, ((ArithmeticFunction.moebius (d : ℕ) : ℤ) : ℝ) / (((2 : ℝ) ^ (d : ℕ) - 1) ^ 2) = (mobiusSquarePartialRat D : ℝ) + mobiusSquareTail D
declarations.docstring: The full Möbius-square series splits into the exact rational prefix and the shifted tail.

declarations.name: scaleDiagonalTailDifference_sub_lambertProjectedDiagonal
declarations.signature: theorem scaleDiagonalTailDifference_sub_lambertProjectedDiagonal (H D : ℕ) : scaleDiagonalTailDifference H - lambertProjectedDiagonal H D = (diagonalCoefficient H : ℝ) * mobiusSquareTail D
declarations.docstring: Exact direct projection: no residue split and no `2H ≤ D` condition.

declarations.name: mersenne_shift_lower_bound
declarations.signature: theorem mersenne_shift_lower_bound (D k : ℕ) : (2 : ℝ) ^ k * ((2 : ℝ) ^ (D + 1) - 1) ≤ (2 : ℝ) ^ (D + 1 + k) - 1
declarations.docstring: Later Mersenne denominators grow at least geometrically from the first omitted denominator.

declarations.name: abs_mobiusSquareTail_le
declarations.docstring: The signed square tail is bounded by the exact first-denominator geometric majorant.

declarations.name: abs_scaleDiagonalTailDifference_sub_lambertProjectedDiagonal_le
declarations.signature: theorem abs_scaleDiagonalTailDifference_sub_lambertProjectedDiagonal_le (H D : ℕ) : |scaleDiagonalTailDifference H - lambertProjectedDiagonal H D| ≤ lambertSquareComplementBound H D
declarations.docstring: The actual diagonal lies in the sharp squared-Mersenne enclosure around the exact rational centre.

declarations.name: scaleFullTarget_miss_of_lambert_projected_separation
declarations.signature: theorem scaleFullTarget_miss_of_lambert_projected_separation {H D : ℕ} (hsep : ∀ z : ℤ, lambertSquareComplementBound H D < |lambertProjectedDiagonal H D - (z : ℝ)|) : ¬ScaleFullTargetHit H
declarations.docstring: Exact rational separation of the squared-Mersenne centre from the integer lattice forces a genuine miss of the actual full target.

declarations.name: LR
declarations.signature: inductive LR | left | right deriving DecidableEq, Repr /-- Exchange the two parabolic directions. -/ def LR.flip : LR → LR | .left => .right | .right => .left @[simp] theorem LR.flip_left : LR.left.flip = LR.right
declarations.docstring: The two Stern--Brocot mediant moves.

declarations.name: LR.flip
declarations.signature: def LR.flip : LR → LR | .left => .right | .right => .left @[simp] theorem LR.flip_left : LR.left.flip = LR.right
declarations.docstring: Exchange the two parabolic directions.

declarations.name: step
declarations.signature: def step : LR → ℕ × ℕ → ℕ × ℕ | .left, (a, b) => (a + b, b) | .right, (a, b) => (a, a + b) /-- The pair reached from `(1,1)`. The list head is the newest move and acts last; this agrees with the newest-run-first convention used below. -/ def sbPair : List LR → ℕ × ℕ | [] => (1, 1) | d :: w => step d (sbPair w) /-- Arithmetic height of a Stern--Brocot word. -/ def sbHeight (w : List LR) : ℕ
declarations.docstring: One mediant step on a positive lattice pair.

declarations.name: sbPair
declarations.signature: def sbPair : List LR → ℕ × ℕ | [] => (1, 1) | d :: w => step d (sbPair w) /-- Arithmetic height of a Stern--Brocot word. -/ def sbHeight (w : List LR) : ℕ
declarations.docstring: The pair reached from `(1,1)`. The list head is the newest move and acts last; this agrees with the newest-run-first convention used below.

declarations.name: sbHeight
declarations.docstring: Arithmetic height of a Stern--Brocot word.

declarations.name: sbPair_coprime
declarations.signature: theorem sbPair_coprime (w : List LR) : Nat.Coprime (sbPair w).1 (sbPair w).2
declarations.docstring: Every word reaches a primitive pair.

declarations.name: sbPair_pos
declarations.signature: theorem sbPair_pos (w : List LR) : 0 < (sbPair w).1 ∧ 0 < (sbPair w).2
declarations.docstring: Both coordinates reached from `(1,1)` stay positive.

declarations.name: runWord
declarations.signature: def runWord : LR → List ℕ → List LR | _, [] => [] | newest, n :: ns => List.replicate n newest ++ runWord newest.flip ns /-- The direction-free continuant state at run boundaries. Its first coordinate is the coordinate changed
declarations.docstring: A word with alternating directions and the supplied run lengths. The first length is the newest run and its direction is `newest`.

declarations.name: runBoundaryPair
declarations.signature: def runBoundaryPair : List ℕ → ℕ × ℕ | [] => (1, 1) | n :: ns => let p
declarations.docstring: The direction-free continuant state at run boundaries. Its first coordinate is the coordinate changed by the newest run; the second is the other coordinate.

declarations.name: runHeight
declarations.docstring: Height in run coordinates.

declarations.name: sbPair_replicate_left_append
declarations.signature: private theorem sbPair_replicate_left_append (n : ℕ) (w : List LR) : sbPair (List.replicate n LR.left ++ w) = ((sbPair w).1 + n * (sbPair w).2, (sbPair w).2)

declarations.name: sbPair_replicate_right_append
declarations.signature: private theorem sbPair_replicate_right_append (n : ℕ) (w : List LR) : sbPair (List.replicate n LR.right ++ w) = ((sbPair w).1, (sbPair w).2 + n * (sbPair w).1)

declarations.name: sbPair_runWord
declarations.signature: theorem sbPair_runWord (newest : LR) (ns : List ℕ) : sbPair (runWord newest ns) = match newest with | .left => runBoundaryPair ns | .right => ((runBoundaryPair ns).2, (runBoundaryPair ns).1)
declarations.docstring: The run continuant is not a new coordinate detached from the tree: it is exactly the pair reached by the corresponding alternating Stern--Brocot word, up to the expected coordinate swap for a newest right run.

declarations.name: sbHeight_runWord
declarations.signature: theorem sbHeight_runWord (newest : LR) (ns : List ℕ) : sbHeight (runWord newest ns) = runHeight ns
declarations.docstring: Run height is the literal arithmetic height on the mediant tree.

declarations.name: runBoundaryPair_coprime
declarations.signature: theorem runBoundaryPair_coprime (ns : List ℕ) : Nat.Coprime (runBoundaryPair ns).1 (runBoundaryPair ns).2
declarations.docstring: The run-boundary pair remains primitive.

declarations.name: runBoundaryPair_fib_lower
declarations.signature: theorem runBoundaryPair_fib_lower (ns : List ℕ) (hpos : ∀ n ∈ ns, 0 < n) : Nat.fib (ns.length + 2) ≤ (runBoundaryPair ns).1 ∧ Nat.fib (ns.length + 1) ≤ (runBoundaryPair ns).2
declarations.docstring: Coordinatewise Fibonacci floor for positive run lengths.

declarations.name: runHeight_fib_lower
declarations.signature: theorem runHeight_fib_lower (ns : List ℕ) (hpos : ∀ n ∈ ns, 0 < n) : Nat.fib (ns.length + 3) ≤ runHeight ns
declarations.docstring: **Fibonacci minimum height.** Every alternating word with `r` nonempty runs has height at least `F_{r+3}`.

declarations.name: sbHeight_runWord_fib_lower
declarations.signature: theorem sbHeight_runWord_fib_lower (newest : LR) (ns : List ℕ) (hpos : ∀ n ∈ ns, 0 < n) : Nat.fib (ns.length + 3) ≤ sbHeight (runWord newest ns)
declarations.docstring: Tree-facing form of the Fibonacci height floor.

declarations.name: runBoundaryPair_replicate_one
declarations.signature: theorem runBoundaryPair_replicate_one (r : ℕ) : runBoundaryPair (List.replicate r 1) = (Nat.fib (r + 2), Nat.fib (r + 1))
declarations.docstring: The all-unit alternating spine is exactly Fibonacci.

declarations.name: runHeight_replicate_one
declarations.signature: theorem runHeight_replicate_one (r : ℕ) : runHeight (List.replicate r 1) = Nat.fib (r + 3)
declarations.docstring: Equality case for the Fibonacci height floor.

declarations.name: defectRunLengths
declarations.signature: def defectRunLengths (e : List ℕ) : List ℕ
declarations.docstring: Replace every nonnegative defect `eᵢ` by the positive run length `1 + eᵢ`.

declarations.name: exactDefectPair
declarations.signature: def exactDefectPair : List ℕ → ℕ × ℕ | [] => (0, 0) | e :: es => let d
declarations.docstring: The exact defect of the two run-boundary coordinates from their Fibonacci values. The term `e * d.1` is the first mixed monomial created at the newest defect; recursively expanding it gives the full positive multiaffine continuant-defect polynomial.

declarations.name: linearDefectPair
declarations.signature: def linearDefectPair : List ℕ → ℕ × ℕ | [] => (0, 0) | e :: es => let d
declarations.docstring: The degree-one part of `exactDefectPair`. It is obtained by deleting the mixed term `e * d.1` at every recursive step.

declarations.name: runBoundaryPair_defectRunLengths
declarations.signature: theorem runBoundaryPair_defectRunLengths (e : List ℕ) : runBoundaryPair (defectRunLengths e) = (Nat.fib (e.length + 2) + (exactDefectPair e).1, Nat.fib (e.length + 1) + (exactDefectPair e).2)
declarations.docstring: Exact coordinate expansion around the all-unit Fibonacci spine.

declarations.name: runHeight_defectRunLengths
declarations.signature: theorem runHeight_defectRunLengths (e : List ℕ) : runHeight (defectRunLengths e) = Nat.fib (e.length + 3) + (exactDefectPair e).1 + (exactDefectPair e).2
declarations.docstring: Exact height expansion. In particular, `exactDefectPair` is not an estimate: it owns every mixed coefficient of the continuant defect.

declarations.name: linearDefectPair_le_exactDefectPair
declarations.signature: theorem linearDefectPair_le_exactDefectPair (e : List ℕ) : (linearDefectPair e).1 ≤ (exactDefectPair e).1 ∧ (linearDefectPair e).2 ≤ (exactDefectPair e).2
declarations.docstring: Every coefficient discarded by the linearization is nonnegative.

declarations.name: runHeight_defect_linear_lower
declarations.signature: theorem runHeight_defect_linear_lower (e : List ℕ) : Nat.fib (e.length + 3) + (linearDefectPair e).1 + (linearDefectPair e).2 ≤ runHeight (defectRunLengths e)
declarations.docstring: Structural linear floor for the full continuant defect. This statement retains the recursively ordered one-site coefficients; the next theorem identifies each of them with a closed Fibonacci product.

declarations.name: linearDefectPair_fib_sum_lower
declarations.signature: theorem linearDefectPair_fib_sum_lower (e : List ℕ) : Nat.fib e.length * e.sum ≤ (linearDefectPair e).1 ∧ Nat.fib (e.length + 1) * e.sum ≤ (linearDefectPair e).1 + (linearDefectPair e).2
declarations.docstring: The recursively ordered coefficients admit the uniform sharp floor: the first-coordinate coefficients are at least `F_r`, and the height coefficients are at least `F_{r+1}`.

declarations.name: runHeight_defect_fib_sum_lower
declarations.signature: theorem runHeight_defect_fib_sum_lower (e : List ℕ) : Nat.fib (e.length + 3) + Nat.fib (e.length + 1) * e.sum ≤ runHeight (defectRunLengths e)
declarations.docstring: **Sharp global stability floor.** If the positive run lengths are `1 + eᵢ`, their height gains at least `F_{r+1}` for every unit of total defect. Boundary one-site rays attain this coefficient exactly.

declarations.name: oneSiteWeight
declarations.docstring: Closed coefficient of a defect at position `i`, counted from the newest run boundary. The hypothesis `i < r` keeps the coefficient inside an `r`-run word without introducing a second indexing type.

declarations.name: oneSiteWeight_eq_base_add
declarations.signature: theorem oneSiteWeight_eq_base_add {r i : ℕ} (hi : i < r) : oneSiteWeight r i = Nat.fib (r + 1) + Nat.fib i * Nat.fib (r - i - 1)
declarations.docstring: The one-site coefficient is the boundary value `F_{r+1}` plus an explicit interior surplus.

declarations.name: linearDefectPair_first_lt_exact_of_head_tail_pos
declarations.signature: theorem linearDefectPair_first_lt_exact_of_head_tail_pos {e : ℕ} (he : 0 < e) {es : List ℕ} (htail : 0 < (exactDefectPair es).1) : (linearDefectPair (e :: es)).1 < (exactDefectPair (e :: es)).1
declarations.docstring: The recursive expansion exposes a strict mixed term as soon as the newest defect and an older defect are both present.

declarations.name: runHeight_one_cons
declarations.signature: private theorem runHeight_one_cons (ns : List ℕ) : runHeight (1 :: ns) = runHeight ns + (runBoundaryPair ns).1

declarations.name: runBoundaryPair_replicate_one_append
declarations.signature: private theorem runBoundaryPair_replicate_one_append (i : ℕ) (ns : List ℕ) : let p

declarations.name: runHeight_oneSiteDefect
declarations.signature: theorem runHeight_oneSiteDefect {r i : ℕ} (hi : i < r) (e : ℕ) : runHeight (List.replicate i 1 ++ (e + 1) :: List.replicate (r - i - 1) 1) = Nat.fib (r + 3) + oneSiteWeight r i * e
declarations.docstring: A single defect is exactly linear, with coefficient `F_{i+2} F_{r-i+1}`. This is the sharp coefficient formula and includes the arbitrary defect magnitude `e`, not only the derivative at `e = 0`.

declarations.name: runHeight_single
declarations.docstring: Exact height of one parabolic run.

declarations.name: runHeight_pair
declarations.signature: @[simp] theorem runHeight_pair (m n : ℕ) : runHeight [m, n] = (m + 1) * (n + 1) + 1
declarations.docstring: Exact height of two alternating parabolic runs. After putting `u = m+1`, `v = n+1`, the two orientations together contribute the weight `2 * 2^{-(uv+1)} = 2^{-uv}` used by the second induced layer.

declarations.name: zeroRunMass
declarations.docstring: Root contribution, before any nonempty parabolic run.

declarations.name: oneRunMass
declarations.docstring: Contribution of the two orientations with one positive run. The natural number index `n` represents run length `n+1`.

declarations.name: oneRunMass_eq_half
declarations.docstring: Exact first induced layer: the two parabolic cusps together carry mass `1/2`.

declarations.name: naturalRunDenominatorExponent
declarations.docstring: Sum of the invariant-coordinate exponents encountered on the unit-run spine. Clearing the corresponding Mersenne resolvents introduces factors on this exponent scale.

declarations.name: naturalRunDenominatorExponent_add_two
declarations.signature: theorem naturalRunDenominatorExponent_add_two (r : ℕ) : naturalRunDenominatorExponent r + 2 = Nat.fib (r + 3)
declarations.docstring: The natural exponent is exactly two below the minimum run height scale: `F₂ + ... + F_{r+1} + 2 = F_{r+3}`.

declarations.name: nat_add_one_pow_le_self_pow_mul_two_pow
declarations.signature: theorem nat_add_one_pow_le_self_pow_mul_two_pow (k a : ℕ) (hk : 1 ≤ k) : (a + 1) ^ k ≤ k ^ k * 2 ^ a
declarations.docstring: A single explicit penalty `k^k` controls every exponent at every prime at least two.

declarations.name: factorization_add_one_pow_le_penalty_mul
declarations.signature: theorem factorization_add_one_pow_le_penalty_mul (n p k : ℕ) (hk : 1 ≤ k) (hp : p ∈ n.primeFactors) : (n.factorization p + 1) ^ k ≤ (if p < 2 ^ k then k ^ k else 1) * p ^ n.factorization p
declarations.docstring: Pointwise prime-factor estimate: only primes below `2^k` pay the explicit `k^k` penalty.

declarations.name: card_small_primeFactors_le_two_pow
declarations.signature: theorem card_small_primeFactors_le_two_pow (n k : ℕ) : (n.primeFactors.filter fun p => p < 2 ^ k).card ≤ 2 ^ k
declarations.docstring: There are at most `2^k` prime factors below the cutoff `2^k`.

declarations.name: divisorSubpowerConst
declarations.docstring: Completely explicit constant for the fixed-`k` divisor bound.

declarations.name: card_divisors_pow_le_divisorSubpowerConst_pow_mul
declarations.signature: theorem card_divisors_pow_le_divisorSubpowerConst_pow_mul (n k : ℕ) (hn : 0 < n) (hk : 1 ≤ k) : n.divisors.card ^ k ≤ divisorSubpowerConst k ^ k * n
declarations.docstring: Fixed-natural-power divisor theorem. All prime-factor bookkeeping is in `ℕ`; the only loss is the explicit penalty from primes below `2^k`.

declarations.name: card_divisors_le_divisorSubpowerConst_mul_rpow
declarations.signature: theorem card_divisors_le_divisorSubpowerConst_mul_rpow (n k : ℕ) (hk : 1 ≤ k) : (n.divisors.card : ℝ) ≤ (divisorSubpowerConst k : ℝ) * (n : ℝ) ^ ((k : ℝ)⁻¹)
declarations.docstring: Take the real `k`-th root once, after the finite prime-factor product has been closed in `ℕ`. This version includes `n = 0`.

declarations.name: supportCoeff_pow_le_divisorSubpowerConst_pow_mul
declarations.signature: theorem supportCoeff_pow_le_divisorSubpowerConst_pow_mul (A : Set ℕ) (n k : ℕ) (hn : 0 < n) (hk : 1 ≤ k) : supportCoeff A n ^ k ≤ divisorSubpowerConst k ^ k * n
declarations.docstring: The support coefficient inherits the fixed-power divisor theorem.

declarations.name: supportCoeff_le_divisorSubpowerConst_mul_rpow
declarations.signature: theorem supportCoeff_le_divisorSubpowerConst_mul_rpow (A : Set ℕ) (n k : ℕ) (hk : 1 ≤ k) : (supportCoeff A n : ℝ) ≤ (divisorSubpowerConst k : ℝ) * (n : ℝ) ^ ((k : ℝ)⁻¹)
declarations.docstring: Real subpower form inherited by `supportCoeff`.

declarations.name: SupportCoeffZeroWindow
declarations.signature: def SupportCoeffZeroWindow (A : Set ℕ) (N h : ℕ) : Prop
declarations.docstring: `supportCoeff A` vanishes on the `h` positive indices immediately after `N`, namely `N + 1, ..., N + h`.

declarations.name: shiftedTailState_eq_pow_mul_of_supportCoeffZeroWindow
declarations.signature: theorem shiftedTailState_eq_pow_mul_of_supportCoeffZeroWindow (A : Set ℕ) (c v N h : ℕ) (u : ℕ → ℕ) (hrec : ∀ n : ℕ, u (n + 1) + v * supportCoeff A (c + n + 1) = 2 * u n) (hzero : SupportCoeffZeroWindow A (c + N) h) : u (N + h) = 2 ^ h * u N
declarations.docstring: A shifted natural tail state doubles exactly across a support-coefficient zero window. This is the recurrence-only core of T11; it uses no analytic growth estimate.

declarations.name: pow_le_shiftedTailState_of_supportCoeffZeroWindow
declarations.signature: theorem pow_le_shiftedTailState_of_supportCoeffZeroWindow (A : Set ℕ) (c v N h : ℕ) (u : ℕ → ℕ) (hpos : ∀ n : ℕ, 0 < u n) (hrec : ∀ n : ℕ, u (n + 1) + v * supportCoeff A (c + n + 1) = 2 * u n) (hzero : SupportCoeffZeroWindow A (c + N) h) : 2 ^ h ≤ u (N + h)
declarations.docstring: Positivity upgrades exact doubling to the natural-number lower bound `2^h ≤ u(N+h)`.

declarations.name: pow_le_mul_binaryCoeffTail_of_support_fraction_zeroWindow
declarations.signature: theorem pow_le_mul_binaryCoeffTail_of_support_fraction_zeroWindow (A : Set ℕ) (hA : ∃ a : ℕ, 0 < a ∧ a ∈ A) (p : ℤ) (c v N h : ℕ) (hv : 0 < v) (hvalue : erdosSupportSeries 2 A = (p : ℝ) / ((2 ^ c * v : ℕ) : ℝ)) (hzero : SupportCoeffZeroWindow A (c + N) h) : (2 : ℝ) ^ h ≤ (v : ℝ) * binaryCoeffTail (supportCoeff A) (c + N + h)
declarations.docstring: Fraction-facing algebraic T11 boundary. For a displayed denominator `2^c * v`, every zero window forces the odd tail state, hence the scaled true tail, to be at least `2^h`.

declarations.name: SupportCoeffTailEnvelope
declarations.signature: def SupportCoeffTailEnvelope (A : Set ℕ) (envelope : ℕ → ℝ) : Prop
declarations.docstring: An explicit upper envelope for the scaled support-coefficient tail.

declarations.name: supportCoeffTailEnvelope_two_mul_rpow_of_card_divisors_bound
declarations.signature: theorem supportCoeffTailEnvelope_two_mul_rpow_of_card_divisors_bound (A : Set ℕ) (C δ : ℝ) (hδ0 : 0 ≤ δ) (hδ1 : δ ≤ 1) (hdiv : ∀ n : ℕ, (n.divisors.card : ℝ) ≤ C * (n : ℝ) ^ δ) : SupportCoeffTailEnvelope A (fun M : ℕ => 2 * C * (((M + 1 : ℕ) : ℝ) ^ δ))
declarations.docstring: Package a pointwise divisor-card bound into the exact tail-envelope interface consumed by the rational carry theorem below.

declarations.name: supportCoeffTailEnvelope_divisorSubpower
declarations.signature: theorem supportCoeffTailEnvelope_divisorSubpower (A : Set ℕ) (k : ℕ) (hk : 1 ≤ k) : SupportCoeffTailEnvelope A (fun M : ℕ => 2 * (divisorSubpowerConst k : ℝ) * (((M + 1 : ℕ) : ℝ) ^ ((k : ℝ)⁻¹)))
declarations.docstring: Unconditional support-tail envelope with exponent exactly `1/k`.

declarations.name: pow_le_mul_tailEnvelope_of_support_fraction_zeroWindow
declarations.signature: theorem pow_le_mul_tailEnvelope_of_support_fraction_zeroWindow (A : Set ℕ) (hA : ∃ a : ℕ, 0 < a ∧ a ∈ A) (p : ℤ) (c v N h : ℕ) (hv : 0 < v) (hvalue : erdosSupportSeries 2 A = (p : ℝ) / ((2 ^ c * v : ℕ) : ℝ)) (hzero : SupportCoeffZeroWindow A (c + N) h) (envelope : ℕ → ℝ) (henvelope : SupportCoeffTailEnvelope A envelope) : (2 : ℝ) ^ h ≤ (v : ℝ) * envelope (c + N + h)
declarations.docstring: Consumer-shaped conditional T11 theorem. Once a subpower tail envelope is supplied, a zero window is bounded by that envelope at its right endpoint. No divisor-growth implementation detail leaks into the rational-support argument.

declarations.name: pow_le_mul_two_mul_rpow_of_card_divisors_bound_zeroWindow
declarations.signature: theorem pow_le_mul_two_mul_rpow_of_card_divisors_bound_zeroWindow (A : Set ℕ) (hA : ∃ a : ℕ, 0 < a ∧ a ∈ A) (p : ℤ) (c v N h : ℕ) (hv : 0 < v) (hvalue : erdosSupportSeries 2 A = (p : ℝ) / ((2 ^ c * v : ℕ) : ℝ)) (hzero : SupportCoeffZeroWindow A (c + N) h) (C δ : ℝ) (hδ0 : 0 ≤ δ) (hδ1 : δ ≤ 1) (hdiv : ∀ n : ℕ, (n.divisors.card : ℝ) ≤ C * (n : ℝ) ^ δ) : (2 : ℝ) ^ h ≤ (v : ℝ) * (2 * C * ((((c + N + h) + 1 : ℕ) : ℝ) ^ δ))
declarations.docstring: Direct consumer bridge from any pointwise divisor subpower bound to a zero-window power inequality.

declarations.name: pow_le_divisorSubpower_of_support_fraction_zeroWindow
declarations.signature: theorem pow_le_divisorSubpower_of_support_fraction_zeroWindow (A : Set ℕ) (hA : ∃ a : ℕ, 0 < a ∧ a ∈ A) (p : ℤ) (c v N h k : ℕ) (hv : 0 < v) (hk : 1 ≤ k) (hvalue : erdosSupportSeries 2 A = (p : ℝ) / ((2 ^ c * v : ℕ) : ℝ)) (hzero : SupportCoeffZeroWindow A (c + N) h) : (2 : ℝ) ^ h ≤ (2 * (v : ℝ) * (divisorSubpowerConst k : ℝ)) * (((c + N + 1 + h : ℕ) : ℝ) ^ ((k : ℝ)⁻¹))
declarations.docstring: The unconditional divisor-subpower envelope composed with the verified rational carry mechanism.

declarations.name: supportCoeffZeroWindow_length_le_logb_divisorSubpower
declarations.signature: theorem supportCoeffZeroWindow_length_le_logb_divisorSubpower (A : Set ℕ) (hA : ∃ a : ℕ, 0 < a ∧ a ∈ A) (p : ℤ) (c v N h k : ℕ) (hv : 0 < v) (hk : 2 ≤ k) (hvalue : erdosSupportSeries 2 A = (p : ℝ) / ((2 ^ c * v : ℕ) : ℝ)) (hzero : SupportCoeffZeroWindow A (c + N) h) : (h : ℝ) ≤ (Real.logb 2 (c + N + 1 : ℝ) + Real.logb 2 ((2 * (v : ℝ) * (divisorSubpowerConst k : ℝ)) ^ k)) / ((k - 1 : ℕ) : ℝ)
declarations.docstring: Parameterized fixed-`k` T11 theorem. This is the load-bearing form from which the every-`ε` statement follows.

declarations.name: supportCoeffZeroWindow_length_le_eps_logb_add
declarations.signature: theorem supportCoeffZeroWindow_length_le_eps_logb_add (A : Set ℕ) (hA : ∃ a : ℕ, 0 < a ∧ a ∈ A) (p : ℤ) (c v : ℕ) (hv : 0 < v) (hvalue : erdosSupportSeries 2 A = (p : ℝ) / ((2 ^ c * v : ℕ) : ℝ)) (ε : ℝ) (hε : 0 < ε) : ∃ B : ℝ, 0 ≤ B ∧ ∀ N h : ℕ, SupportCoeffZeroWindow A (c + N) h → (h : ℝ) ≤ ε * Real.logb 2 (N + 1 : ℝ) + B
declarations.docstring: **Unconditional T11.** For every positive `ε`, a rational support-series value forces every support-coefficient zero window to have length at most `ε * log₂(N+1)` plus a constant depending only on `ε`, `v`, and `c`. The bound is uniform in the support `A`, numerator `p`, starting index `N`, and window length `h`.
