declarations.name: supportCoeffZeroWindow_length_le_eps_logb
declarations.signature: theorem supportCoeffZeroWindow_length_le_eps_logb (A : Set ℕ) (hA : ∃ a : ℕ, 0 < a ∧ a ∈ A) (p : ℤ) (c v : ℕ) (hv : 0 < v) (hvalue : erdosSupportSeries 2 A = (p : ℝ) / ((2 ^ c * v : ℕ) : ℝ)) (ε : ℝ) (hε : 0 < ε) : ∃ B : ℝ, 0 ≤ B ∧ ∀ N h : ℕ, 1 ≤ N → SupportCoeffZeroWindow A (c + N) h → (h : ℝ) ≤ ε * Real.logb 2 (N : ℝ) + B
declarations.docstring: Exact `ε * log₂ N + O(1)` form of unconditional T11 for every positive starting index.

declarations.name: supportPullback
declarations.signature: def supportPullback (p : ℕ) (A : Set ℕ) : Set ℕ
declarations.docstring: Pull a support back through multiplication by `p`.

declarations.name: iteratedSupportPullback
declarations.signature: def iteratedSupportPullback : List ℕ → Set ℕ → Set ℕ | [], A => A | p :: ps, A => supportPullback p (iteratedSupportPullback ps A) /-- Integer-valued packaging of the support divisor-count coefficient. -/ noncomputable def supportCoeffInt (A : Set ℕ) (n : ℕ) : ℤ
declarations.docstring: Successive support pullback. Its membership condition is multiplication by the product of the listed dilation factors.

declarations.name: supportCoeffInt
declarations.signature: noncomputable def supportCoeffInt (A : Set ℕ) (n : ℕ) : ℤ
declarations.docstring: Integer-valued packaging of the support divisor-count coefficient.

declarations.name: dilationDifference
declarations.signature: def dilationDifference (p : ℕ) (f : ℕ → ℤ) (n : ℕ) : ℤ
declarations.docstring: The forward dilation difference `f(pn)-f(n)`.

declarations.name: iteratedDilationDifference
declarations.signature: def iteratedDilationDifference : List ℕ → (ℕ → ℤ) → ℕ → ℤ | [], f => f | p :: ps, f => dilationDifference p (iteratedDilationDifference ps f) @[simp] theorem mem_supportPullback {p a : ℕ} {A : Set ℕ} : a ∈ supportPullback p A ↔ p * a ∈ A
declarations.docstring: Compose dilation differences in list order.

declarations.name: mem_supportPullback
declarations.signature: theorem mem_supportPullback {p a : ℕ} {A : Set ℕ} : a ∈ supportPullback p A ↔ p * a ∈ A

declarations.name: mem_iteratedSupportPullback
declarations.signature: theorem mem_iteratedSupportPullback {ps : List ℕ} {a : ℕ} {A : Set ℕ} : a ∈ iteratedSupportPullback ps A ↔ ps.prod * a ∈ A

declarations.name: divisors_mul_prime_split
declarations.signature: private theorem divisors_mul_prime_split {n p : ℕ} (hp : p.Prime) : (n * p).divisors = n.divisors ∪ n.divisors.image (fun d => d * p)

declarations.name: divisors_disjoint_prime_multiples
declarations.signature: private theorem divisors_disjoint_prime_multiples {n p : ℕ} (hp : p.Prime) (hnp : n.Coprime p) : Disjoint n.divisors (n.divisors.image fun d => d * p)

declarations.name: filtered_prime_multiple_card
declarations.signature: private theorem filtered_prime_multiple_card {n p : ℕ} (hp : p.Prime) (A : Set ℕ) [DecidablePred (· ∈ A)] [DecidablePred (· ∈ supportPullback p A)] : ((n.divisors.image fun d => d * p).filter fun d => d ∈ A).card = (n.divisors.filter fun d => d ∈ supportPullback p A).card

declarations.name: supportCoeff_mul_prime
declarations.signature: theorem supportCoeff_mul_prime (A : Set ℕ) {n p : ℕ} (hp : p.Prime) (hnp : n.Coprime p) : supportCoeff A (p * n) = supportCoeff A n + supportCoeff (supportPullback p A) n
declarations.docstring: The exact one-step divisor split behind T14.

declarations.name: dilationDifference_supportCoeffInt
declarations.signature: theorem dilationDifference_supportCoeffInt (A : Set ℕ) {n p : ℕ} (hp : p.Prime) (hnp : n.Coprime p) : dilationDifference p (supportCoeffInt A) n = supportCoeffInt (supportPullback p A) n
declarations.docstring: A single forward dilation difference is the coefficient of the pulled-back support at every argument coprime to the prime dilation.

declarations.name: pairwise_coprime_of_nodup_primes
declarations.signature: theorem pairwise_coprime_of_nodup_primes {ps : List ℕ} (hprime : ∀ p ∈ ps, p.Prime) (hnodup : ps.Nodup) : ps.Pairwise Nat.Coprime
declarations.docstring: Distinct primes are pairwise coprime, packaged in the list form consumed by the iterated dilation theorem.

declarations.name: iteratedDilationDifference_supportCoeffInt
declarations.signature: theorem iteratedDilationDifference_supportCoeffInt (A : Set ℕ) {ps : List ℕ} {n : ℕ} (hprime : ∀ p ∈ ps, p.Prime) (hpair : ps.Pairwise Nat.Coprime) (hn : n.Coprime ps.prod) : iteratedDilationDifference ps (supportCoeffInt A) n = supportCoeffInt (iteratedSupportPullback ps A) n
declarations.docstring: T14's iterated identity. Pairwise coprimality makes every intermediate argument legal for the next one-step split.

declarations.name: iteratedDilationDifference_supportCoeffInt_of_nodup_primes
declarations.signature: theorem iteratedDilationDifference_supportCoeffInt_of_nodup_primes (A : Set ℕ) {ps : List ℕ} {n : ℕ} (hprime : ∀ p ∈ ps, p.Prime) (hnodup : ps.Nodup) (hn : n.Coprime ps.prod) : iteratedDilationDifference ps (supportCoeffInt A) n = supportCoeffInt (iteratedSupportPullback ps A) n
declarations.docstring: Interface distinct-prime form of the iterated identity.

declarations.name: cardFactors_list_prod_of_primes
declarations.signature: theorem cardFactors_list_prod_of_primes {ps : List ℕ} (hprime : ∀ p ∈ ps, p.Prime) : ArithmeticFunction.cardFactors ps.prod = ps.length
declarations.docstring: A product of prime dilation factors has exactly one `Ω` contribution per list entry, including repeated primes.

declarations.name: supportCoeff_iteratedSupportPullback_eq_zero_of_cardFactors_le
declarations.signature: theorem supportCoeff_iteratedSupportPullback_eq_zero_of_cardFactors_le (A : Set ℕ) {ps : List ℕ} {K n : ℕ} (hprime : ∀ p ∈ ps, p.Prime) (hbound : ∀ a ∈ A, ArithmeticFunction.cardFactors a ≤ K) (hlen : K < ps.length) : supportCoeff (iteratedSupportPullback ps A) n = 0
declarations.docstring: More than `K` prime pullbacks annihilate every divisor coefficient of a support whose elements have `Ω` at most `K`. No pairwise-coprimality hypothesis is needed for this support-side extinction statement.

declarations.name: iteratedDilationDifference_eq_zero_of_cardFactors_le
declarations.signature: theorem iteratedDilationDifference_eq_zero_of_cardFactors_le (A : Set ℕ) {ps : List ℕ} {K n : ℕ} (hprime : ∀ p ∈ ps, p.Prime) (hpair : ps.Pairwise Nat.Coprime) (hn : n.Coprime ps.prod) (hbound : ∀ a ∈ A, ArithmeticFunction.cardFactors a ≤ K) (hlen : K < ps.length) : iteratedDilationDifference ps (supportCoeffInt A) n = 0
declarations.docstring: The finite bounded-`Ω` annihilation theorem: a pairwise-coprime prime list longer than the support's `Ω` ceiling makes the actual iterated dilation difference vanish at arguments coprime to its product.

declarations.name: supportTwoThree
declarations.docstring: The finite bounded-`Ω` annihilation theorem: a pairwise-coprime prime list longer than the support's `Ω` ceiling makes the actual iterated dilation difference vanish at arguments coprime to its product. -/ theorem iteratedDilationDifference_eq_zero_of_cardFactors_le (A : Set ℕ) {ps : List ℕ} {K n : ℕ} (hprime : ∀ p ∈ ps, p.Prime) (hpair : ps.Pairwise Nat.Coprime) (hn : n.Coprime ps.prod) (hbound : ∀ a ∈ A, ArithmeticFunction.cardFactors a ≤ K) (hlen : K < ps.length) : iteratedDilationDifference ps (supportCoeffInt A) n = 0 := by rw [iteratedDilationDifference_supportCoeffInt A hprime hpair hn, supportCoeffInt, supportCoeff_iteratedSupportPullback_eq_zero_of_cardFactors_le A hprime hbound hlen] norm_num /-! Kernel fixtures: one difference extracts multiples of `2`, while two pairwise-coprime prime differences annihilate the support `{2,3}` because its elements have `Ω = 1`.

declarations.name: OrthogonalPetalBouquet
declarations.docstring: A finite-core orthogonal petal bouquet. Every exceptional element and every ray core is absorbed by one fixed positive integer `Q`; the residual petals are nontrivial, coprime to `Q`, globally pairwise coprime, and have summable reciprocal mass. The support equation is deliberately set-valued, so duplicate bookkeeping cannot change the Boolean divisor transform.

declarations.name: ray
declarations.signature: def ray {A : Set ℕ} (hB : OrthogonalPetalBouquet A) (i : ℕ) : ℕ
declarations.docstring: The support ray attached to an index.

declarations.name: ray_pos
declarations.signature: theorem ray_pos {A : Set ℕ} (hB : OrthogonalPetalBouquet A) (i : ℕ) : 0 < hB.ray i

declarations.name: ray_mem
declarations.signature: theorem ray_mem {A : Set ℕ} (hB : OrthogonalPetalBouquet A) (i : ℕ) : hB.ray i ∈ A

declarations.name: exceptional_ne_ray
declarations.signature: theorem exceptional_ne_ray {A : Set ℕ} (hB : OrthogonalPetalBouquet A) {d : ℕ} (hd : d ∈ hB.exceptional) (i : ℕ) : d ≠ hB.ray i
declarations.docstring: Exceptional frame elements cannot coincide with a ray. Thus the support equation is automatically a disjoint decomposition, rather than requiring a redundant field in the structure.

declarations.name: ray_injective
declarations.signature: theorem ray_injective {A : Set ℕ} (hB : OrthogonalPetalBouquet A) : Function.Injective hB.ray
declarations.docstring: Orthogonal petals force distinct support rays even when their cores can repeat.

declarations.name: petal_injective
declarations.signature: theorem petal_injective {A : Set ℕ} (hB : OrthogonalPetalBouquet A) : Function.Injective hB.petal

declarations.name: rayIndex
declarations.signature: noncomputable def rayIndex {A : Set ℕ} (hB : OrthogonalPetalBouquet A) (d : ℕ) : ℕ
declarations.docstring: Canonical inverse index on the ray range; values away from the range are irrelevant.

declarations.name: ray_rayIndex_eq
declarations.signature: theorem ray_rayIndex_eq {A : Set ℕ} (hB : OrthogonalPetalBouquet A) {d : ℕ} (hd : d ∈ Set.range hB.ray) : hB.ray (hB.rayIndex d) = d

declarations.name: petal_dvd_of_ray_dvd
declarations.signature: theorem petal_dvd_of_ray_dvd {A : Set ℕ} (hB : OrthogonalPetalBouquet A) {i j x : ℕ} (hji : j ≠ i) (hdiv : hB.ray j ∣ hB.ray i * x) : hB.petal j ∣ x
declarations.docstring: A foreign ray that appears after dilation by a selected ray pays its own petal locally: orthogonality removes the selected ray, leaving a divisor of the undilated argument.

declarations.name: infinite
declarations.signature: theorem infinite {A : Set ℕ} (hB : OrthogonalPetalBouquet A) : A.Infinite
declarations.docstring: The ray part, hence the whole bouquet support, is infinite.

declarations.name: frameStep
declarations.signature: def frameStep {A : Set ℕ} (hB : OrthogonalPetalBouquet A) (F : Finset ℕ) : ℕ
declarations.docstring: The selected frame step. It absorbs the fixed core and the petals at the finitely many promoted ray indices.

declarations.name: core_dvd_frameStep
declarations.signature: theorem core_dvd_frameStep {A : Set ℕ} (hB : OrthogonalPetalBouquet A) (F : Finset ℕ) (i : ℕ) : hB.core i ∣ hB.frameStep F

declarations.name: petal_coprime_frameStep
declarations.signature: theorem petal_coprime_frameStep {A : Set ℕ} (hB : OrthogonalPetalBouquet A) (F : Finset ℕ) {i : ℕ} (hi : i ∉ F) : Nat.Coprime (hB.petal i) (hB.frameStep F)
declarations.docstring: An unselected petal stays coprime to the entire enlarged frame.

declarations.name: gcd_ray_frameStep
declarations.signature: theorem gcd_ray_frameStep {A : Set ℕ} (hB : OrthogonalPetalBouquet A) (F : Finset ℕ) {i : ℕ} (hi : i ∉ F) : Nat.gcd (hB.ray i) (hB.frameStep F) = hB.core i
declarations.docstring: Persistent reduced modulus: after any finite frame is assembled, every unselected ray has gcd exactly its core.

declarations.name: ray_div_gcd_frameStep
declarations.signature: theorem ray_div_gcd_frameStep {A : Set ℕ} (hB : OrthogonalPetalBouquet A) (F : Finset ℕ) {i : ℕ} (hi : i ∉ F) : hB.ray i / Nat.gcd (hB.ray i) (hB.frameStep F) = hB.petal i
declarations.docstring: Consequently the exact reduced modulus of an unselected ray is its petal, independently of the size of the selected frame.

declarations.name: exists_pos_mem
declarations.signature: theorem exists_pos_mem {A : Set ℕ} (hB : OrthogonalPetalBouquet A) : ∃ a : ℕ, 0 < a ∧ a ∈ A
declarations.docstring: Every bouquet contains a positive support element, as needed by the far-tail positivity clause of the carry consumer.

declarations.name: cardFactors_le_of_dvd
declarations.signature: private theorem cardFactors_le_of_dvd {a Q : ℕ} (ha : 0 < a) (hQ : 0 < Q) (had : a ∣ Q) : ArithmeticFunction.cardFactors a ≤ ArithmeticFunction.cardFactors Q

declarations.name: cardFactors_le_of_mem
declarations.signature: theorem cardFactors_le_of_mem {A : Set ℕ} (hB : OrthogonalPetalBouquet A) {R a : ℕ} (hpetal : ∀ i, ArithmeticFunction.cardFactors (hB.petal i) ≤ R) (haA : a ∈ A) : ArithmeticFunction.cardFactors a ≤ ArithmeticFunction.cardFactors hB.Q + R
declarations.docstring: A uniform petal-rank ceiling gives a genuine bounded-`Ω` support. The fixed contribution is paid once by `Ω(Q)`, while each ray pays only its own petal rank.

declarations.name: alternatingPrimeCore
declarations.docstring: Alternate between the two fixed cores `2` and `3`.

declarations.name: primeSquarePetal
declarations.docstring: The square of the `(i + 2)`-nd prime. Starting at index two excludes both fixed core primes.

declarations.name: alternatingPrimeSquareSupport
declarations.docstring: An explicit infinite support with genuinely different fixed cores.

declarations.name: alternatingPrimeCore_pos
declarations.signature: private theorem alternatingPrimeCore_pos (i : ℕ) : 0 < alternatingPrimeCore i

declarations.name: alternatingPrimeCore_dvd_six
declarations.signature: private theorem alternatingPrimeCore_dvd_six (i : ℕ) : alternatingPrimeCore i ∣ 6

declarations.name: primeSquarePetal_one_lt
declarations.signature: private theorem primeSquarePetal_one_lt (i : ℕ) : 1 < primeSquarePetal i

declarations.name: primeSquarePetal_coprime_six
declarations.signature: private theorem primeSquarePetal_coprime_six (i : ℕ) : Nat.Coprime (primeSquarePetal i) 6

declarations.name: primeSquarePetal_pairwise
declarations.signature: private theorem primeSquarePetal_pairwise (i j : ℕ) (hij : i ≠ j) : Nat.Coprime (primeSquarePetal i) (primeSquarePetal j)

declarations.name: summable_inv_primeSquarePetal
declarations.signature: private theorem summable_inv_primeSquarePetal : Summable (fun i => (1 : ℝ) / (primeSquarePetal i : ℝ))

declarations.name: alternatingPrimeSquareBouquet
declarations.docstring: Concrete witness for the orthogonal-petal interface. It has no exceptional part, frame `Q = 6`, alternating cores `2,3`, and squared-prime petals.

declarations.name: alternatingPrimeSquareSupport_infinite
declarations.docstring: The explicit alternating-core support is infinite.

declarations.name: alternatingPrimeSquareSupport_cardFactors_eq_three
declarations.signature: theorem alternatingPrimeSquareSupport_cardFactors_eq_three {a : ℕ} (ha : a ∈ alternatingPrimeSquareSupport) : ArithmeticFunction.cardFactors a = 3
declarations.docstring: Every member of the explicit support has exactly three prime factors with multiplicity: one fixed core prime and a squared petal prime.

declarations.name: alternatingPrimeSquareBouquet_uses_two_cores
declarations.docstring: A small non-vacuity receipt: both fixed cores actually occur.

declarations.name: SunflowerForcedCarrySupply
declarations.docstring: The unresolved analytic supply in the bounded-`Ω`, reciprocal-summable sunflower route. Its conclusion is exactly the carry-aware certificate data consumed by the landed arbitrary-support irrationality theorem.

declarations.name: irrational_erdosSupportSeries_of_sunflower_forcedCarry_supply
declarations.signature: theorem irrational_erdosSupportSeries_of_sunflower_forcedCarry_supply (A : Set ℕ) (hsupply : SunflowerForcedCarrySupply A) : Irrational (erdosSupportSeries 2 A)
declarations.docstring: Closing the named forced-carry socket would prove irrationality for that support by the existing carry-aware certificate kernel.

declarations.name: SunflowerForcedSlotCarrySelection
declarations.docstring: The exact finite selector produced by the persistent-reduced-modulus argument. The first clause fixes the undamped carry slot. The second clause is the finite, natural-number form of a uniform binary-tail bound: every middle window beginning after that slot has normalized cost at most `16`. Keeping this predicate separate from `SunflowerForcedCarrySupply` records the real remaining mathematical seam. It contains no rationality hypothesis and refers directly to the genuine Boolean divisor transform `supportCoeff A`.

declarations.name: SunflowerForcedSlotTailSelection
declarations.docstring: Analytic form of the forced-slot selector. This is the statement proved directly by the orthogonal-bouquet averaging argument.

declarations.name: forcedSlotCarrySelection_of_tailSelection
declarations.signature: theorem forcedSlotCarrySelection_of_tailSelection (A : Set ℕ) (htail : SunflowerForcedSlotTailSelection A) : SunflowerForcedSlotCarrySelection A
declarations.docstring: A uniform real binary-tail bound pays every finite natural middle-window budget. This bridge keeps the analytic bouquet proof and the exact carry consumer cleanly separated.

declarations.name: sunflowerForcedCarrySupply_of_forcedSlotCarrySelection
declarations.signature: theorem sunflowerForcedCarrySupply_of_forcedSlotCarrySelection (A : Set ℕ) (hA : ∃ a : ℕ, 0 < a ∧ a ∈ A) (hselection : SunflowerForcedSlotCarrySelection A) : SunflowerForcedCarrySupply A
declarations.docstring: A forced-slot selector composes unconditionally into the landed carry supply as soon as the support has one positive member. This is the exact consumer bridge needed by the orthogonal-petal bouquet argument; in particular, no multiplicative decimation or layer-rationality transport is used. The proof chooses an explicit later scale. `param_linear_le_two_pow` absorbs the linear far-tail term, while the selector's `16 * 2^(L-K)` budget is paid by choosing `K = 16*q + 1`.

declarations.name: sunflowerForcedCarrySupply_of_orthogonalPetalBouquet
declarations.signature: theorem sunflowerForcedCarrySupply_of_orthogonalPetalBouquet {A : Set ℕ} (hB : OrthogonalPetalBouquet A) (hselection : SunflowerForcedSlotTailSelection A) : SunflowerForcedCarrySupply A
declarations.docstring: Exact bouquet composition endpoint. The sole remaining hypothesis is the analytic selector proved by the persistent-reduced-modulus averaging argument; all carry arithmetic and support positivity are discharged here.

declarations.name: irrational_erdosSupportSeries_of_orthogonalPetalBouquet
declarations.signature: theorem irrational_erdosSupportSeries_of_orthogonalPetalBouquet {A : Set ℕ} (hB : OrthogonalPetalBouquet A) (hselection : SunflowerForcedSlotTailSelection A) : Irrational (erdosSupportSeries 2 A)
declarations.docstring: Conditional headline for the bouquet lane, with the remaining selector named at exactly its mathematical boundary.

declarations.name: card_filter_dvd_progression_le_reduced
declarations.signature: theorem card_filter_dvd_progression_le_reduced (M c a Y : ℕ) (ha : 0 < a) : ((Finset.range Y).filter (fun y => a ∣ M * y + c)).card ≤ Y / (a / Nat.gcd a M) + 1
declarations.docstring: Divisibility along `M * y + c` occupies at most one residue class modulo the reduced modulus `a / gcd a M`. This is the valid replacement for applying the coprime progression bound when support strays share factors with the CRT frame.

declarations.name: gcd_dvd_offset_sub_of_dvd_progression
declarations.signature: theorem gcd_dvd_offset_sub_of_dvd_progression (M x a y r s : ℕ) (hframe : M ∣ x + r) (hhit : a ∣ x + y * M + s) : Nat.gcd a M ∣ s - r
declarations.docstring: If the frame step divides a distinguished slot, every later hit by a support divisor is spaced from that slot by `gcd a M`. This is the exact arithmetic source of the geometric damping in the repaired middle-window estimate.

declarations.name: gcd_le_offset_sub_of_dvd_progression
declarations.signature: theorem gcd_le_offset_sub_of_dvd_progression (M x a y r s : ℕ) (hframe : M ∣ x + r) (hhit : a ∣ x + y * M + s) (hrs : r < s) : Nat.gcd a M ≤ s - r
declarations.docstring: A nontrivial later hit cannot occur before the gcd spacing.

declarations.name: exactPrimePowerPullback
declarations.signature: def exactPrimePowerPullback (p e : ℕ) (A : Set ℕ) : Set ℕ
declarations.docstring: Support elements with exact `p`-adic exponent `e`, after removing the prime-power layer.

declarations.name: filter_range_succ_card
declarations.signature: private theorem filter_range_succ_card (P : ℕ → Prop) [DecidablePred P] (e : ℕ) : ((Finset.range (e + 1)).filter P).card = ((Finset.range e).filter P).card + if P e then 1 else 0

declarations.name: supportCoeff_prime_power_layer
declarations.signature: theorem supportCoeff_prime_power_layer (A : Set ℕ) {p e n : ℕ} (hp : p.Prime) (he : 0 < e) (hnp : n.Coprime p) : supportCoeff A (p ^ e * n) = supportCoeff A (p ^ (e - 1) * n) + supportCoeff (exactPrimePowerPullback p e A) n
declarations.docstring: The new divisor layer between `p^(e-1) * n` and `p^e * n` is exactly the prescribed valuation pullback, provided the residual argument is coprime to `p`.

declarations.name: supportCoeffInt_prime_power_layer
declarations.signature: theorem supportCoeffInt_prime_power_layer (A : Set ℕ) {p e n : ℕ} (hp : p.Prime) (he : 0 < e) (hnp : n.Coprime p) : supportCoeffInt A (p ^ e * n) - supportCoeffInt A (p ^ (e - 1) * n) = supportCoeffInt (exactPrimePowerPullback p e A) n
declarations.docstring: Integer-difference form consumed by valuation-signature operators.

declarations.name: supportCoeffInt_prime_power_layer_fixture
declarations.signature: theorem supportCoeffInt_prime_power_layer_fixture : supportCoeffInt ({8} : Set ℕ) (2 ^ 3 * 1) - supportCoeffInt ({8} : Set ℕ) (2 ^ (3 - 1) * 1) = 1
declarations.docstring: Moving-prime-power regression: the exact third `2`-adic layer of the singleton support `{8}` contributes one new divisor at residual argument `1`.

declarations.name: threeTransportTail
declarations.docstring: The balanced four-ray tail word on multipliers `(1,2,3,6)`.

declarations.name: threeTransportDrive
declarations.docstring: The exact integer letter at boundary offset `s`.

declarations.name: threeTransportWindow
declarations.docstring: The dyadic depth-`L` boundary window.

declarations.name: threeTransportShiftedTail
declarations.signature: noncomputable def threeTransportShiftedTail (H L : ℕ) : ℝ
declarations.docstring: The shifted tail left after truncating at depth `L`.

declarations.name: threeTransportRadius
declarations.docstring: The symmetric edge radius forced by the positive and negative stencil masses: both sides have multiplier mass `4` and first moment `9`.

declarations.name: abs_threeTransportDrive_le
declarations.signature: theorem abs_threeTransportDrive_le (H s : ℕ) : |threeTransportDrive H s| ≤ (9 * H + 4 * s : ℤ)
declarations.docstring: The boundary letter itself already has the sharp elementary pointwise bound obtained by grouping the positive and negative stencil parts.

declarations.name: two_pow_mul_threeTransport_eq_window_add_shifted
declarations.signature: theorem two_pow_mul_threeTransport_eq_window_add_shifted (H L : ℕ) : (2 : ℝ) ^ L * threeTransportTail H = (threeTransportWindow H L : ℝ) + threeTransportShiftedTail H L
declarations.docstring: Exact prefix/tail split for the balanced four-ray word.

declarations.name: abs_threeTransportShiftedTail_le
declarations.signature: theorem abs_threeTransportShiftedTail_le (H L : ℕ) (hH : 1 ≤ H) : |threeTransportShiftedTail H L| ≤ (threeTransportRadius H L : ℝ)
declarations.docstring: The shifted tail lies in the sharp symmetric strip.

declarations.name: threeTransportWindow_succ
declarations.signature: theorem threeTransportWindow_succ (H L : ℕ) : threeTransportWindow H (L + 1) = 2 * threeTransportWindow H L + threeTransportDrive H (L + 1)
declarations.docstring: The boundary word is an exact input-driven doubling recurrence.

declarations.name: sharpThreeTransportCert
declarations.docstring: The exact decidable lattice-separation certificate.

declarations.name: sharpThreeTransportCert_depth_floor
declarations.signature: theorem sharpThreeTransportCert_depth_floor {H L : ℕ} (hcert : sharpThreeTransportCert H L) : (2 * threeTransportRadius H L : ℤ) < 2 ^ L
declarations.docstring: Every certificate has enough dyadic room for both excluded boundary arcs.

declarations.name: threeTransport_notMem_int_of_cert
declarations.signature: theorem threeTransport_notMem_int_of_cert {H L : ℕ} (hH : 1 ≤ H) (hcert : sharpThreeTransportCert H L) : threeTransportTail H ∉ Set.range ((↑) : ℤ → ℝ)
declarations.docstring: A sharp residue gap excludes integral three-transport curvature.

declarations.name: threeTransportTail_eq_commutator
declarations.signature: theorem threeTransportTail_eq_commutator (H : ℕ) : threeTransportTail H = ExponentOnlyTransport.threeTransportTailCommutator H
declarations.docstring: The new boundary word agrees definitionally with the earlier exponent-only transport commutator.

declarations.name: RoughnessBoundaryLatticeSupply
declarations.docstring: Arbitrarily large exact boundary certificates. This is the sole open producer socket in this module.

declarations.name: irrational_totientSeries_of_roughnessBoundaryLatticeSupply
declarations.signature: theorem irrational_totientSeries_of_roughnessBoundaryLatticeSupply (hsupply : RoughnessBoundaryLatticeSupply) : Irrational (∑' n : ℕ, (Nat.totient n : ℝ) / 2 ^ n)
declarations.docstring: The boundary-certificate supply contradicts eventual LCM-cone flatness.

declarations.name: threeTransportWindow_60_12
declarations.docstring: An exact balanced boundary word at the lcm height `H = lcm(1,…,6)`.

declarations.name: sharpThreeTransportCert_60_12
declarations.docstring: Kernel-checked finite lattice separation at `t = 6`, `H = 60`.

declarations.name: carryDerivative
declarations.signature: def carryDerivative (u : ℕ → ℤ) (n : ℕ) : ℤ
declarations.docstring: The discrete derivative naturally recovered from a binary carry orbit.

declarations.name: carryDerivative_eq_scaledCoeff_of_recurrence
declarations.signature: theorem carryDerivative_eq_scaledCoeff_of_recurrence {c : ℕ → ℕ} {v : ℕ} {u : ℕ → ℤ} (hrec : ∀ N : ℕ, u (N + 1) = 2 * u N - ((v * c (N + 1) : ℕ) : ℤ)) {n : ℕ} (hn : 0 < n) : carryDerivative u n = ((v * c n : ℕ) : ℤ)
declarations.docstring: Any exact binary-orbit recurrence recovers its coefficient at every positive index through `carryDerivative`. Temperedness is not needed.

declarations.name: totient_temperedOrbit_derivative
declarations.signature: theorem totient_temperedOrbit_derivative {v : ℕ} {u : ℕ → ℤ} (hu : IsTemperedBinaryOrbit Nat.totient v u) {n : ℕ} (hn : 0 < n) : carryDerivative u n = ((v * Nat.totient n : ℕ) : ℤ)
declarations.docstring: A tempered totient orbit recovers the scaled totient sequence through its discrete carry derivative.

declarations.name: totient_carryDerivative_coprime_mul
declarations.signature: theorem totient_carryDerivative_coprime_mul {v : ℕ} {u : ℕ → ℤ} (hu : IsTemperedBinaryOrbit Nat.totient v u) {m n : ℕ} (hm : 0 < m) (hn : 0 < n) (hcop : Nat.Coprime m n) : (v : ℤ) * carryDerivative u (m * n) = carryDerivative u m * carryDerivative u n
declarations.docstring: The carry derivative inherits Euler totient's multiplicativity on coprime positive arguments.

declarations.name: totient_carryDerivative_gcd_mul
declarations.signature: theorem totient_carryDerivative_gcd_mul {v : ℕ} {u : ℕ → ℤ} (hu : IsTemperedBinaryOrbit Nat.totient v u) {m n : ℕ} (hm : 0 < m) (hn : 0 < n) : carryDerivative u (m * n) * carryDerivative u (m.gcd n) = (m.gcd n : ℤ) * carryDerivative u m * carryDerivative u n
declarations.docstring: GCD-corrected multiplicativity of the carry derivative. Unlike the coprime specialization, this identity retains the exact non-coprime defect.

declarations.name: carryKernelSeq
declarations.signature: def carryKernelSeq (u : ℕ → ℤ) (j r : ℕ) : ℕ → ℚ
declarations.docstring: A dyadic section of an integer carry orbit, viewed over `ℚ`.

declarations.name: totient_carryKernel_diff
declarations.signature: theorem totient_carryKernel_diff {v : ℕ} {u : ℕ → ℤ} (hu : IsTemperedBinaryOrbit Nat.totient v u) {j r : ℕ} (hr : 0 < r) : (fun n => (v : ℚ) * totientKernelSeq j r n) = fun n => 2 * carryKernelSeq u j (r - 1) n - carryKernelSeq u j r n
declarations.docstring: Every positive-residue totient-kernel channel is a two-term difference of adjacent carry-kernel channels. This is the exact coefficient-to-carry bridge used by rank-transfer arguments.

declarations.name: TotientOddIndex
declarations.docstring: The odd canonical dyadic channels through levels `1,...,e`.

declarations.name: TotientCarryIndex
declarations.docstring: The full carry-section family through levels `1,...,e`.

declarations.name: canonicalTotientOddKernelFamily
declarations.signature: def canonicalTotientOddKernelFamily (e : ℕ) : TotientOddIndex e → ℕ → ℚ | ⟨j, r⟩ => totientKernelSeq (j.val + 1) (2 * r.val + 1) /-- Every carry section through levels `1,...,e`, without quotienting or identifying residue channels. -/ def canonicalCarryKernelFamily (u : ℕ → ℤ) (e : ℕ) : TotientCarryIndex e → ℕ → ℚ | ⟨j, r⟩ => carryKernelSeq u (j.val + 1) r.val /-- Embed an odd-channel index as its even predecessor carry section. -/ def carryEvenIndex {e : ℕ} : TotientOddIndex e → TotientCarryIndex e | ⟨j, r⟩ => ⟨j, ⟨2 * r.val,
declarations.docstring: The odd-residue part of the canonical totient family.

declarations.name: canonicalCarryKernelFamily
declarations.signature: def canonicalCarryKernelFamily (u : ℕ → ℤ) (e : ℕ) : TotientCarryIndex e → ℕ → ℚ | ⟨j, r⟩ => carryKernelSeq u (j.val + 1) r.val /-- Embed an odd-channel index as its even predecessor carry section. -/ def carryEvenIndex {e : ℕ} : TotientOddIndex e → TotientCarryIndex e | ⟨j, r⟩ => ⟨j, ⟨2 * r.val,
declarations.docstring: Every carry section through levels `1,...,e`, without quotienting or identifying residue channels.

declarations.name: carryEvenIndex
declarations.signature: def carryEvenIndex {e : ℕ} : TotientOddIndex e → TotientCarryIndex e | ⟨j, r⟩ => ⟨j, ⟨2 * r.val,
declarations.docstring: Embed an odd-channel index as its even predecessor carry section.

declarations.name: carryOddIndex
declarations.signature: def carryOddIndex {e : ℕ} : TotientOddIndex e → TotientCarryIndex e | ⟨j, r⟩ => ⟨j, ⟨2 * r.val + 1,
declarations.docstring: Embed an odd-channel index as the corresponding odd carry section.

declarations.name: canonicalCarryDifferenceFamily
declarations.signature: def canonicalCarryDifferenceFamily (u : ℕ → ℤ) (e : ℕ) : TotientOddIndex e → ℕ → ℚ
declarations.docstring: The carry differences that recover the odd canonical totient channels.

declarations.name: card_totientOddIndex
declarations.signature: theorem card_totientOddIndex (e : ℕ) : Fintype.card (TotientOddIndex e) = 2 ^ e - 1
declarations.docstring: There are exactly `2^e-1` odd canonical channels through level `e`.

declarations.name: linearIndependent_totientOddKernelFamily
declarations.signature: theorem linearIndependent_totientOddKernelFamily (e : ℕ) (hcanon : LinearIndependent ℚ (canonicalTotientKernelFamily e)) : LinearIndependent ℚ (canonicalTotientOddKernelFamily e)
declarations.docstring: Linear independence of the full canonical family restricts to the odd canonical channels.

declarations.name: linearIndependent_totientOddKernelFamily_of_certificate
declarations.signature: theorem linearIndependent_totientOddKernelFamily_of_certificate (e : ℕ) (cert : SeparatedMinorCertificate (canonicalTotientKernelFamily e)) : LinearIndependent ℚ (canonicalTotientOddKernelFamily e)
declarations.docstring: A separated minor for the canonical family already certifies independence of all its odd channels.

declarations.name: canonicalCarryDifferenceFamily_eq_smul_totient
declarations.signature: theorem canonicalCarryDifferenceFamily_eq_smul_totient {v : ℕ} {u : ℕ → ℤ} (hu : IsTemperedBinaryOrbit Nat.totient v u) (e : ℕ) : canonicalCarryDifferenceFamily u e = fun i => (v : ℚ) • canonicalTotientOddKernelFamily e i
declarations.docstring: The recurrence identifies the canonical carry differences with the odd totient channels, scaled by the orbit multiplier.

declarations.name: canonicalCarryDifferenceFamily_mem_span
declarations.signature: theorem canonicalCarryDifferenceFamily_mem_span (u : ℕ → ℤ) (e : ℕ) (i : TotientOddIndex e) : canonicalCarryDifferenceFamily u e i ∈ Submodule.span ℚ (Set.range (canonicalCarryKernelFamily u e))
declarations.docstring: Every canonical carry difference lies in the span of the full finite-level carry family.

declarations.name: finrank_canonicalCarryKernel_ge_of_linearIndependent
declarations.signature: theorem finrank_canonicalCarryKernel_ge_of_linearIndependent {v : ℕ} {u : ℕ → ℤ} (hv : 0 < v) (hu : IsTemperedBinaryOrbit Nat.totient v u) (e : ℕ) (hcanon : LinearIndependent ℚ (canonicalTotientKernelFamily e)) : 2 ^ e - 1 ≤ finrank ℚ (Submodule.span ℚ (Set.range (canonicalCarryKernelFamily u e)))
declarations.docstring: **Finite-level carry anti-compression.** A nonzero-multiplier orbit whose canonical totient family is linearly independent must retain at least `2^e-1` dimensions among its carry sections through level `e`. Temperedness is not used beyond supplying the exact recurrence through the existing orbit interface.

declarations.name: finrank_canonicalCarryKernel_ge_of_certificate
declarations.signature: theorem finrank_canonicalCarryKernel_ge_of_certificate {v : ℕ} {u : ℕ → ℤ} (hv : 0 < v) (hu : IsTemperedBinaryOrbit Nat.totient v u) (e : ℕ) (cert : SeparatedMinorCertificate (canonicalTotientKernelFamily e)) : 2 ^ e - 1 ≤ finrank ℚ (Submodule.span ℚ (Set.range (canonicalCarryKernelFamily u e)))
declarations.docstring: Certificate-facing form of `finrank_canonicalCarryKernel_ge_of_linearIndependent`. This is immediately usable with the existing finite separated-minor owner.

declarations.name: not_irrational_totientSeries_implies_unbounded_carryRank
declarations.signature: theorem not_irrational_totientSeries_implies_unbounded_carryRank (hcanon : ∀ e : ℕ, LinearIndependent ℚ (canonicalTotientKernelFamily e)) (hirr : ¬ Irrational (binaryCoeffSeries Nat.totient)) : ∃ v : ℕ, 0 < v ∧ ∃ u : ℕ → ℤ, IsTemperedBinaryOrbit Nat.totient v u ∧ ∀ e : ℕ, 2 ^ e - 1 ≤ finrank ℚ (Submodule.span ℚ (Set.range (canonicalCarryKernelFamily u e)))
declarations.docstring: Rationality of the totient coefficient series, together with an all-level canonical independence producer, yields one tempered integral carry whose finite-level section ranks grow at least as `2^e-1`. This theorem records the exact endpoint composition without asserting that the still-open adaptive-row producer has already been formalized.

declarations.name: totientKernelSeq
declarations.signature: def totientKernelSeq (j r : ℕ) : ℕ → ℚ
declarations.docstring: The `(j,r)` dyadic-kernel channel of Euler's totient, viewed over `ℚ`.

declarations.name: TotientCanonicalIndex
declarations.docstring: The canonical channels through level `e`: the two zero-residue base channels, followed by every odd residue at levels `1,...,e`.

declarations.name: canonicalTotientKernelFamily
declarations.signature: def canonicalTotientKernelFamily (e : ℕ) : TotientCanonicalIndex e → ℕ → ℚ | Sum.inl i => totientKernelSeq i.val 0 | Sum.inr ⟨j, r⟩ => totientKernelSeq (j.val + 1) (2 * r.val + 1) /-- The canonical family has exactly `2^e+1` channels. -/ theorem card_totientCanonicalIndex (e : ℕ) : Fintype.card (TotientCanonicalIndex e) = 2 ^ e + 1
declarations.docstring: The canonical family indexed without duplicate even-residue channels.

declarations.name: card_totientCanonicalIndex
declarations.signature: theorem card_totientCanonicalIndex (e : ℕ) : Fintype.card (TotientCanonicalIndex e) = 2 ^ e + 1
declarations.docstring: The canonical family has exactly `2^e+1` channels.

declarations.name: evaluationLinearMap
declarations.signature: def evaluationLinearMap {ι : Type*} [Fintype ι] (rowIndex : ι → ℕ) : (ℕ → ℚ) →ₗ[ℚ] (ι → ℚ)
declarations.docstring: Pull a finite family of sequences back along a finite family of evaluation points.

declarations.name: SeparatedMinorCertificate
declarations.signature: structure SeparatedMinorCertificate {ι : Type*} [Fintype ι] [DecidableEq ι] (family : ι → ℕ → ℚ)
declarations.docstring: A square nonzero evaluation minor. This is the exact finite object needed to turn number-theoretic row construction into linear independence.

declarations.name: linearIndependent_of_separatedMinorCertificate
declarations.signature: theorem linearIndependent_of_separatedMinorCertificate {ι : Type*} [Fintype ι] [DecidableEq ι] (family : ι → ℕ → ℚ) (cert : SeparatedMinorCertificate family) : LinearIndependent ℚ family
declarations.docstring: A nonzero square evaluation minor proves that the underlying functions are linearly independent.

declarations.name: finrank_canonicalTotientKernel_eq_of_linearIndependent
declarations.signature: theorem finrank_canonicalTotientKernel_eq_of_linearIndependent (e : ℕ) (hcanon : LinearIndependent ℚ (canonicalTotientKernelFamily e)) : finrank ℚ (Submodule.span ℚ (Set.range (canonicalTotientKernelFamily e))) = 2 ^ e + 1
declarations.docstring: Linear independence of the canonical family gives its exact finite-level dimension. This is the producer-neutral endpoint consumed both by explicit minors and by a future adaptive CRT--Dirichlet proof.

declarations.name: finrank_canonicalTotientKernel_eq_of_certificate
declarations.signature: theorem finrank_canonicalTotientKernel_eq_of_certificate (e : ℕ) (cert : SeparatedMinorCertificate (canonicalTotientKernelFamily e)) : finrank ℚ (Submodule.span ℚ (Set.range (canonicalTotientKernelFamily e))) = 2 ^ e + 1
declarations.docstring: A separated minor for the canonical family gives the exact finite-level dimension promised by the dyadic rank route. The remaining all-level task is to construct this certificate by CRT and Dirichlet for each `e`.

declarations.name: totient_two_pow_succ_mul_of_odd
declarations.signature: theorem totient_two_pow_succ_mul_of_odd (t m : ℕ) (hm : Odd m) : Nat.totient (2 ^ (t + 1) * m) = 2 ^ t * Nat.totient m
declarations.docstring: Totient of an odd number multiplied by a positive power of two.

declarations.name: totient_two_pow_mul_two
declarations.signature: theorem totient_two_pow_mul_two (j n : ℕ) : Nat.totient (2 ^ j * (2 * n)) = 2 ^ j * Nat.totient (2 * n)
declarations.docstring: Repeated doubling scales the totient once the argument is already even.

declarations.name: totientKernel_zero_residue
declarations.signature: theorem totientKernel_zero_residue (j n : ℕ) : totientKernelSeq (j + 1) 0 n = (2 ^ j : ℚ) * totientKernelSeq 1 0 n
declarations.docstring: Every positive zero-residue channel is a scalar multiple of the first zero-residue channel.

declarations.name: totientKernel_even_residue_reduce
declarations.signature: theorem totientKernel_even_residue_reduce (j t s n : ℕ) (ht : t + 1 < j) (hs : Odd s) : totientKernelSeq j (2 ^ (t + 1) * s) n = (2 ^ t : ℚ) * totientKernelSeq (j - (t + 1)) s n
declarations.docstring: Exact reduction of an even residue to its odd-core channel.

declarations.name: CompressedAdjointCertificate
declarations.docstring: The exact proof object required from a future totient-specific compressed adjoint construction.

declarations.name: false_of_compressedAdjointCertificate
declarations.signature: theorem false_of_compressedAdjointCertificate {v : ℕ} (cert : CompressedAdjointCertificate v) : False
declarations.docstring: A compressed-adjoint certificate is impossible: its boundary is both a nonzero multiple of `Q*v` and strictly smaller than `Q*v`.

declarations.name: totientTail
declarations.docstring: The local totient tail `R_N = ∑_{j≥0} φ(N+1+j)/2^{j+1} = ∑_{m≥1} φ(N+m)/2^m`: the fractional layer of `2^N · S`.

declarations.name: totientPrefix
declarations.docstring: The integer prefix `Φ_N = ∑_{n=0}^{N} φ(n)·2^{N-n}` of `2^N · S`.

declarations.name: windowDiscrepancy
declarations.signature: def windowDiscrepancy (h N L : ℕ) : ℤ
declarations.docstring: The window discrepancy `A_{h,N,L} = ∑_{j=0}^{L-1} (φ(N+h+1+j) - φ(N+1+j))·2^{L-1-j}`: the depth-`L` truncation of `2^L·(R_{N+h} - R_N)`.

declarations.name: certifiedKill
declarations.signature: def certifiedKill (h N L : ℕ) : Prop
declarations.docstring: The decidable period-killer certificate: the residue of `A_{h,N,L}` modulo `2^L` avoids the radius-`(N+h+L+2)` neighbourhood of `0`.

declarations.name: certifiedKill_depth_floor
declarations.signature: theorem certifiedKill_depth_floor {h N L : ℕ} (hcert : certifiedKill h N L) : (2 * (N + h + L + 2) : ℤ) < 2 ^ L
declarations.docstring: Every endpoint certificate has enough dyadic room for both excluded boundary arcs. In particular, certificate depth cannot remain fixed while `N+h` tends to infinity.

declarations.name: div_two_pow_eq
declarations.signature: private lemma div_two_pow_eq (a : ℝ) (n : ℕ) : a / 2 ^ n = a * (1 / 2 : ℝ) ^ n
declarations.docstring: Every endpoint certificate has enough dyadic room for both excluded boundary arcs. In particular, certificate depth cannot remain fixed while `N+h` tends to infinity. -/ theorem certifiedKill_depth_floor {h N L : ℕ} (hcert : certifiedKill h N L) : (2 * (N + h + L + 2) : ℤ) < 2 ^ L := by rcases hcert with ⟨hleft, hright⟩ linarith instance (h N L : ℕ) : Decidable (certifiedKill h N L) := inferInstanceAs (Decidable (_ ∧ _)) /-! ## Geometric-linear summability spine

declarations.name: summable_linear_half
declarations.signature: private lemma summable_linear_half : Summable (fun j : ℕ => (j : ℝ) * (1 / 2 : ℝ) ^ j)

declarations.name: summable_const_add_mul_half
declarations.signature: private lemma summable_const_add_mul_half (C : ℝ) : Summable (fun j : ℕ => (C + (j : ℝ)) * (1 / 2 : ℝ) ^ j)

declarations.name: tsum_const_add_mul_half
declarations.signature: private lemma tsum_const_add_mul_half (C : ℝ) : ∑' j : ℕ, (C + (j : ℝ)) * (1 / 2 : ℝ) ^ j = 2 * C + 2

declarations.name: summable_totientTail_terms
declarations.signature: lemma summable_totientTail_terms (M : ℕ) : Summable (fun j : ℕ => (Nat.totient (M + 1 + j) : ℝ) / 2 ^ (j + 1))
declarations.docstring: The tail terms of `totientTail` are summable.

declarations.name: summable_totient_div_two_pow
declarations.signature: lemma summable_totient_div_two_pow : Summable (fun n : ℕ => (Nat.totient n : ℝ) / 2 ^ n)
declarations.docstring: The full totient series is summable.

declarations.name: two_pow_mul_totient_series_eq
declarations.signature: theorem two_pow_mul_totient_series_eq (N : ℕ) : (2 : ℝ) ^ N * (∑' n : ℕ, (Nat.totient n : ℝ) / 2 ^ n) = (totientPrefix N : ℝ) + totientTail N
declarations.docstring: **Shift identity.** `2^N` times the #249 constant splits as integer prefix plus the local totient tail. This is what makes the binary expansion of `S` a statement about `R_N`.

declarations.name: totientTail_eq_partial_add_tail
declarations.signature: lemma totientTail_eq_partial_add_tail (M L : ℕ) : totientTail M = (∑ j ∈ Finset.range L, (Nat.totient (M + 1 + j) : ℝ) / 2 ^ (j + 1)) + ∑' j : ℕ, (Nat.totient (M + 1 + (j + L)) : ℝ) / 2 ^ ((j + L) + 1)
declarations.docstring: Exact decomposition of the tail at window depth `L`.

declarations.name: tail_after_nonneg
declarations.signature: lemma tail_after_nonneg (M L : ℕ) : 0 ≤ ∑' j : ℕ, (Nat.totient (M + 1 + (j + L)) : ℝ) / 2 ^ ((j + L) + 1)
declarations.docstring: The deep tail is nonnegative.

declarations.name: tail_after_le
declarations.signature: lemma tail_after_le (M L : ℕ) : ∑' j : ℕ, (Nat.totient (M + 1 + (j + L)) : ℝ) / 2 ^ ((j + L) + 1) ≤ ((M : ℝ) + L + 2) / 2 ^ L
declarations.docstring: The deep tail after `L` window terms is at most `(M+L+2)/2^L` — the only analytic input is `φ(n) ≤ n` against a geometric-linear majorant.

declarations.name: windowDiscrepancy_div_eq
declarations.signature: lemma windowDiscrepancy_div_eq (h N L : ℕ) : ((windowDiscrepancy h N L : ℤ) : ℝ) / 2 ^ L = (∑ j ∈ Finset.range L, (Nat.totient (N + h + 1 + j) : ℝ) / 2 ^ (j + 1)) - ∑ j ∈ Finset.range L, (Nat.totient (N + 1 + j) : ℝ) / 2 ^ (j + 1)
declarations.docstring: The window discrepancy over `2^L` is the difference of the two partial windows.

declarations.name: tail_diff_notMem_int_of_certifiedKill
declarations.signature: theorem tail_diff_notMem_int_of_certifiedKill {h N L : ℕ} (hcert : certifiedKill h N L) : totientTail (N + h) - totientTail N ∉ Set.range ((↑) : ℤ → ℝ)
declarations.docstring: **Certificate soundness.** If the residue of `A_{h,N,L}` modulo `2^L` avoids the radius-`(N+h+L+2)` neighbourhood of `0`, then `R_{N+h} - R_N` is not an integer: period `h` is dead at position `N`.

declarations.name: tail_diff_int_of_den_dvd
declarations.signature: theorem tail_diff_int_of_den_dvd (r : ℚ) (hS : (∑' n : ℕ, (Nat.totient n : ℝ) / 2 ^ n) = (r : ℝ)) (h N : ℕ) (hdvd : (r.den : ℕ) ∣ 2 ^ N * (2 ^ h - 1)) : totientTail (N + h) - totientTail N ∈ Set.range ((↑) : ℤ → ℝ)
declarations.docstring: If `S = r ∈ ℚ` and `r.den ∣ 2^N·(2^h - 1)`, the `(h, N)` tail difference is an integer. This is the sharp form: any `h` whose Mersenne number kills the odd part of the denominator works, with pre-period exactly `v₂(den)`.

declarations.name: eventual_period_of_not_irrational
declarations.signature: theorem eventual_period_of_not_irrational (hrat : ¬ Irrational (∑' n : ℕ, (Nat.totient n : ℝ) / 2 ^ n)) : ∃ h : ℕ, 0 < h ∧ ∃ N₀ : ℕ, ∀ N, N₀ ≤ N → totientTail (N + h) - totientTail N ∈ Set.range ((↑) : ℤ → ℝ)
declarations.docstring: **Tail-period law.** If the totient series is rational, some period `h ≥ 1` makes every shifted tail difference an integer from the pre-period `v₂(den)` on.

declarations.name: irrational_totient_series_of_certificate_supply
declarations.signature: theorem irrational_totient_series_of_certificate_supply (hsupply : ∀ h : ℕ, 0 < h → ∀ N₀ : ℕ, ∃ N, N₀ ≤ N ∧ ∃ L, certifiedKill h N L) : Irrational (∑' n : ℕ, (Nat.totient n : ℝ) / 2 ^ n)
declarations.docstring: **The wave-21 reduction (the throat of #249).** A certified period-kill supply — for every period `h ≥ 1` and every threshold `N₀`, a certified kill at some `(N ≥ N₀, L)` — proves the totient series irrational. Each individual certificate is a finite, decidable residue computation.

declarations.name: certifiedKill_all_small
declarations.signature: theorem certifiedKill_all_small : ∀ h ∈ Finset.Icc 1 8, certifiedKill h 12 16
declarations.docstring: Machine-checked kills: for every period `h ∈ [1,8]` the residue certificate holds at `(N, L) = (12, 16)`. Kernel-decidable: 256 totient values below 37.

declarations.name: tail_diff_not_int_small
declarations.signature: theorem tail_diff_not_int_small (h : ℕ) (h1 : 1 ≤ h) (h8 : h ≤ 8) : totientTail (12 + h) - totientTail 12 ∉ Set.range ((↑) : ℤ → ℝ)
declarations.docstring: No shifted tail difference with period `h ≤ 8` is an integer at `N = 12`.

declarations.name: totient_series_ne_rat_of_den_dvd
declarations.signature: theorem totient_series_ne_rat_of_den_dvd (r : ℚ) (h : ℕ) (h1 : 1 ≤ h) (h8 : h ≤ 8) (hdvd : (r.den : ℕ) ∣ 2 ^ 12 * (2 ^ h - 1)) : (∑' n : ℕ, (Nat.totient n : ℝ) / 2 ^ n) ≠ (r : ℝ)
declarations.docstring: **Concrete denominator exclusion.** The totient series equals no rational whose denominator divides `2¹²·(2ʰ-1)` for some `1 ≤ h ≤ 8`: no dyadic rational with denominator up to `2¹²`, and no `k/(2ᵃ·m)` with `a ≤ 12` and the multiplicative order of `2` mod `m` at most `8`.

declarations.name: VUSymbol
declarations.docstring: A nonzero 2-adic valuation--unit symbol. `valuation` records the power of two and `unit` is an odd representative of the retained unit bits.

declarations.name: VUCompatible
declarations.signature: def VUCompatible (u : ℕ) (σ : VUSymbol) (c : ℤ) : Prop
declarations.docstring: Integers carrying the fixed-precision symbol `σ` are obtained by varying an unrestricted high quotient `z`.

declarations.name: vuModulus
declarations.docstring: The modulus left by retaining `u` unit bits at valuation `σ.valuation`.

declarations.name: vuRadius
declarations.docstring: Half of `vuModulus`, available when at least one unit bit is retained.

declarations.name: vuBaseDigit
declarations.docstring: The least representative of the valuation--unit cylinder.

declarations.name: vuCentredStep
declarations.signature: def vuCentredStep (u : ℕ) (σ : VUSymbol) (e : ℤ) : ℤ
declarations.docstring: Recenter `2e + vuBaseDigit σ` in the symmetric interval of radius `vuRadius u σ`.

declarations.name: vuModulus_eq_two_mul_radius
declarations.signature: lemma vuModulus_eq_two_mul_radius (u : ℕ) (σ : VUSymbol) (hu : 0 < u) : vuModulus u σ = 2 * vuRadius u σ

declarations.name: vu_step_has_centred_completion
declarations.signature: theorem vu_step_has_centred_completion (u : ℕ) (σ : VUSymbol) (hu : 0 < u) (hodd : Odd σ.unit) (e : ℤ) : ∃ c e' : ℤ, VUCompatible u σ c ∧ e' = 2 * e + c ∧ |e'| ≤ vuRadius u σ
declarations.docstring: Every one-step fixed-precision symbol has a compatible digit that recentres an arbitrary incoming carry state.

declarations.name: VUOrbit
declarations.signature: inductive VUOrbit (u : ℕ) : ℤ → List VUSymbol → List ℤ → Prop | nil (e : ℤ) : VUOrbit u e [] [] | cons (e c e' : ℤ) (σ : VUSymbol) (symbols : List VUSymbol) (states : List ℤ) (hcompat : VUCompatible u σ c) (hstep : e' = 2 * e + c) (htail : VUOrbit u e' symbols states) : VUOrbit u e (σ :: symbols) (e' :: states) /-- Every finite word of odd valuation--unit symbols has a compatible carry orbit centred at every prefix. The initial state is arbitrary. -/ theorem vu_word_has_prefix_locked_completion (u : ℕ) (hu : 0 < u) (symbols : List VUSymbol) (hodd : ∀ σ ∈ symbols, Odd σ.unit) (e : ℤ) : ∃ states : List ℤ, VUOrbit u e symbols states ∧ List.Forall₂ (fun σ e' => |e'| ≤ vuRadius u σ) symbols states
declarations.docstring: An exact finite carry orbit whose successive input digits realise the listed valuation--unit symbols.

declarations.name: vu_word_has_prefix_locked_completion
declarations.signature: theorem vu_word_has_prefix_locked_completion (u : ℕ) (hu : 0 < u) (symbols : List VUSymbol) (hodd : ∀ σ ∈ symbols, Odd σ.unit) (e : ℤ) : ∃ states : List ℤ, VUOrbit u e symbols states ∧ List.Forall₂ (fun σ e' => |e'| ≤ vuRadius u σ) symbols states
declarations.docstring: Every finite word of odd valuation--unit symbols has a compatible carry orbit centred at every prefix. The initial state is arbitrary.

declarations.name: fixedPrecisionTropicalNoGo
declarations.signature: theorem fixedPrecisionTropicalNoGo (u : ℕ) (hu : 0 < u) (symbols : List VUSymbol) (hodd : ∀ σ ∈ symbols, Odd σ.unit) (e : ℤ) : ∃ states : List ℤ, VUOrbit u e symbols states ∧ List.Forall₂ (fun σ e' => |e'| ≤ vuRadius u σ) symbols states
declarations.docstring: Formal fixed-precision tropical no-go: bounded local valuation--unit data alone never excludes all finite centred carry completions.
